title: writev scatter-gather for pipelined write path
description:
Pipelined throughput is 33-47% of Redis at pipeline=16. The bottleneck is per-reply write() syscalls instead of batched writev().

**Current state:**
- fr-server coalesces writes per poll-cycle
- Uses single write() syscall for accumulated buffer
- Pipeline=1 throughput is 71-83% of Redis (acceptable)
- Pipeline=16 throughput is 33-47% of Redis (gap)

**What Redis does:**
- Uses writev() scatter-gather to combine multiple buffers
- Batches more aggressively with vectored I/O

**Implementation path:**
1. Collect IoSlice references to pending reply buffers
2. Use writev() via libc or nix crate
3. Handle partial writes across multiple buffers
notes: WRITEV CLOSEOUT 2026-06-08 OrangeMouse: current-main writev premise rejected with fresh proof at artifacts/optimization/frankenredis-1cbca/closeout-20260608/PROOF.md. RCH build fb9a8f245: GET P16/300k 529.2ms +/-21.0ms; GET P16/1M 710915 ops/sec p99 1827us. Server strace SET P16/100k: 6250 sendto calls = one coalesced send per 16-command pipeline batch, not per-reply writes. Prior write_vectored response segments regressed: baseline 1.660s +/-0.028 vs candidate 1.772s +/-0.049. Golden output sha256 6a4fff4d2af35aef8a0d06970dffb1e4f12afc4b0f5fe0880be4fc727ed26acd. No production hunk retained; route next to a different profile-backed P16 CPU/allocation primitive.
close_reason: Rejected: span argv scratch was golden-equal but neutral, candidate 504.5 ms +/- 12.1 vs baseline 510.0 ms +/- 11.4, 1.01 +/- 0.03x; Score < 2.0

title: Lazy expiry on access does not propagate DEL/UNLINK (wqrb6 follow-up; needs in-dispatch hook)
description: Follow-up to wqrb6 (active-expire DEL propagation shipped in commit 6f9ca7ad7). The LAZY expiry path — drop_if_expired (fr-store ~11094), called when a command accesses a key past its TTL — removes the key and fires the 'expired' keyspace event but still does NOT propagate a DEL/UNLINK to replicas/AOF. Needs an in-dispatch hook that, on a master, captures DEL <key> for each lazily-evicted key ordered BEFORE the triggering command's own propagation. Ordering is critical: a command like SET on an expired key recreates it, so a late/after-command stale DEL would wrongly delete the new value on the replica. Suggested: store records lazily-evicted (db,key) into a drain-queue; the runtime drains it in the dispatch propagation arm just before capturing the command record, gated on ReplicationRoleState::Master, reusing propagate_expired_key_deletions. Also handle the read-only-command case (a GET that lazy-expires must propagate ONLY the DEL, not the GET). In practice less impactful than active-cycle since the ~10Hz active cycle reaps most keys before they're accessed.
close_reason: Fixed + shipped (commit 99c170780). Lazy expiry on access now propagates DEL/UNLINK before the triggering command, with the read-no-propagate guard (delta>lazy_count) and SET-recreate ordering (DEL before SET). Covers regular commands AND EVAL/FCALL (they flow through the dispatch arm which drains the lazy queue before capturing script effects). Verified unit + e2e (DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE 0: master GET on expired key -> replica gets DEL ek, not GET). The MULTI/EXEC queued-command sub-case (queued cmd lazily expires a key that a later queued cmd recreates) is tracked as a separate P4 follow-up; astronomically rare.

title: fr-command FUNCTION LIST emits flat Array per library + per function — upstream functions.c uses addReplyMapLen for both
description:
REVIEW MODE finding extending the RESP3-Map-shape audit cluster (now ten fixes deep).

Source: crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:8682-8744 — function_cmd LIST branch.

Per library (lib.rs:8683-8742): builds flat Array of {library_name, engine, functions, [library_code]} alternating k/v entries.

Per function inside the library (lib.rs:8718-8734): builds flat Array of {name, description, flags} alternating k/v entries.

Upstream functions.c::functionListCommand (line 540+):

  addReplyMapLen(c, with_code ? 4 : 3);   // per-library Map
  addReplyBulkCString(c, "library_name"); ...
  addReplyBulkCString(c, "engine"); ...
  addReplyBulkCString(c, "functions");
  addReplyArrayLen(c, dictSize(li->functions));
  while (...)  {
      addReplyMapLen(c, 3);              // per-function Map
      addReplyBulkCString(c, "name"); ...
      addReplyBulkCString(c, "description"); ...
      addReplyBulkCString(c, "flags"); ...
  }
  if (with_code) addReplyBulkCString(c, "library_code"); ...

So under RESP3 each library is a 3- or 4-entry Map, and each function within is a 3-entry Map.

Reachable from any client running 'FUNCTION LIST [WITHCODE]' under HELLO 3.

Fix: branch on store.dispatch_client_ctx.resp_protocol_version. Per-library RESP3 → 3- or 4-entry Map; per-function RESP3 → 3-entry Map. RESP2 stays the existing flat Array shape on both levels.
close_reason: fr-command FUNCTION LIST now branches on store.dispatch_client_ctx.resp_protocol_version. Per-library RESP3 → 3- or 4-entry Map (library_name/engine/functions/[library_code]); per-function RESP3 → 3-entry Map (name/description/flags). RESP2 keeps the existing flat alternating-k/v Array on both levels. Mirrors upstream functions.c::functionListCommand's addReplyMapLen(c, with_code ? 4 : 3) for libraries and addReplyMapLen(c, 3) for functions. New pin test function_list_under_resp3_returns_nested_map_per_library_and_function loads a library + asserts library Map with 3 entries → functions Array → function Map with 3 entries. Existing 2 function_list tests still pass; clippy clean. Eleventh in the RESP3-Map-shape audit cluster (i40x2/e4njz/udl3y/asvh1/5d04d/ndz0j/5h86s/cz712/ec2rf/q11am/1dfox).

title: [perf] fr-runtime::execute_frame_internal:5596 — compute cmd_keys lazily (skip command_keys alloc on no-tracking reads)
description:
Profiling follow-up to ryfqt. In execute_frame_internal (fr-runtime/src/lib.rs ~5596) 'let cmd_keys = fr_command::command_keys(&argv)' runs on EVERY command (allocates a Vec of cloned keys), but is only used: (a) on the write path when dirty_after>dirty_before, and (b) on the read path by record_client_tracking_keys, which no-ops unless should_record_client_tracking_keys() (O(1), false when the client isn't tracking). So common reads (e.g. GET) and no-op writes pay a wasted alloc.

CHANGE (one lever, ready, proven isomorphic): move the cmd_keys computation INTO the 'if dirty_after>dirty_before' block, and on the read else-if add '&& self.should_record_client_tracking_keys()' then compute cmd_keys inside that branch. 

PROOF DONE: golden RESP wire sha256 identical (7e92abc5...); cargo test -p fr-runtime -p fr-server = 391+43 (+others) passed, 0 failed.

STATUS: NOT shipped — marginal wall-clock impact was BELOW the noise floor on the contended profiling host (load~47): opt2-isolation interleaved paired A/B GET p1 n=6 median -0.3% / mean +2.3% / 2-6 favor new (inconclusive). Needs confirmation on a QUIET host (see frankenredis-vibu6) before landing. Mechanically it strictly reduces allocations (zero risk). Diff saved at /tmp/frperf/runtime_opt2.rs during session; re-derive from the description. Profiler context: allocator = 58% of on-CPU samples (tests/artifacts/perf/run_20260601T232249Z_58605c812/).
close_reason: Rejected: fresh rch-built lazy command_keys candidate was slower on the profiled no-tracking GET path. GET p1 baseline 1.699s +/- 0.047s vs candidate 1.719s +/- 0.058s; GET p16 baseline 429.0ms +/- 13.6ms vs candidate 440.7ms +/- 11.7ms. No code kept.

title: fr advertises redis_version='7.2.0-frankenredis' instead of '7.2.4' — HELLO and INFO server diverge from vendored 7.2.4
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 16380:
  HELLO 3
    vendored: ... version 7.2.4 ...
    fr      : ... version 7.2.0 ...
  INFO server | grep redis_version:
    vendored: redis_version:7.2.4
    fr      : redis_version:7.2.0-frankenredis

Source: crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs:9: pub const REDIS_COMPAT_VERSION: &str = '7.2.0';
Used in: crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:13037 (INFO server), and HELLO is also routed through this constant.

Likely intentional design choice (the '-frankenredis' suffix makes the port identification visible). But for clients that parse the version and gate features on Redis 7.2.x, the mismatch matters:
  - 7.2.0 vs 7.2.4 — clients that require minimum 7.2.4 (for some 7.2.4-specific bugfix or feature) will reject fr.
  - The '-frankenredis' suffix breaks strict semver parsers.

Decision needed:
  Option A: Bump REDIS_COMPAT_VERSION to '7.2.4' and drop the '-frankenredis' suffix → strict parity, transparent to clients.
  Option B: Bump to '7.2.4' and keep the suffix as '7.2.4-frankenredis' → compromise (matches major.minor.patch but identifies the port).
  Option C: Leave as-is → status quo divergence.

Lower priority because version-string changes touch external observability (monitoring dashboards, client compatibility matrices). Filed as P3 for product judgment.
close_reason: Aligned HELLO, INFO server, LOLWUT fixtures, and live-oracle version matching with vendored Redis 7.2.4

title: DEBUG CLUSTERLINK KILL <direction> <node-id> not implemented; falls through to unknown subcommand

File contains argc=5 specifically; out-of-arity calls correctly fall through to the same generic error, matching upstream. Mirror the cluster-disabled error path (most-likely user impact) and stub direction parsing for parity.

title: [perf] ZDIFFSTORE/ZINTERCARD accumulator gaps after streaming ZINTERSTORE
description: Follow-up after frankenredis-jjg2q kept the streaming ZINTERSTORE lever. Current pass85 matrix on 20k-member zsets shows ZINTERSTORE closed and faster than Redis, but remaining zset algebra gaps still need a separate one-lever pass: ZDIFFSTORE d 2 za zb baseline 3841.991 us/op, candidate/current 3837.932 us/op, Redis 3167.708 us/op (fr still ~1.21x slower than Redis); ZINTERCARD 2 za zb current 1244.245 us/op, Redis 847.612 us/op (fr still ~1.47x slower in this matrix; previous bead scan recorded 1.31x). Profile-backed candidate route from jjg2q: replace default-hash accumulators in fr-command ZDIFFSTORE/ZINTERCARD with foldhash::quality::RandomState or a streaming/no-materialization primitive, one lever only, preserve reply order/tie/floating-point/RNG/keyspace stats semantics, prove via zset_algebra_bench.py golden SHA and same-worker paired/reversed timing. Do not modify Store::zinterstore again unless fresh profile points there.
close_reason: Kept ZDIFFSTORE survivor-pairs accumulator: 3844.412 -> 2769.177 us/op (1.388x), golden cf52308d9c843e2ca35b0dea182415de5f4da2ddeb94511e8240e70563baca9e equal true; residual ZINTERCARD filed as frankenredis-u2r0c.

title: fr-store: SREM downgrades set encoding from listpack back to intset, diverges from upstream stability
description:
Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4:

  SADD s 0..9              → both: intset
  SADD s alpha             → both: listpack
  SREM s alpha             → upstream: listpack    fr: intset

Upstream Redis t_set.c keeps the encoding STABLE once a set has been promoted out of intset; SREM removing the last non-integer does not trigger a downgrade. fr-store's object_encoding instead recomputes encoding live from the current membership, so it walks back to intset whenever the membership becomes pure-integer again.

Acceptance: a set that was promoted to listpack stays at listpack across any combination of SADD/SREM that doesn't push it past set_max_listpack_entries to hashtable. (Hashtable is also a one-way state — SREM down to <=128 entries should NOT return to listpack.)

Fix sketch: track a `force_listpack` flag on Value::Set entries that flips true the first time the set is promoted out of intset (and stays true even after the offending element is removed). Mirrors the existing `force_raw_encoding` flag for strings.

Reference: differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 (port 16701) on 2026-05-04 while building set encoding metamorphic tests.
close_reason: Preserved one-way Redis set encoding promotions across SREM and added store plus core_object conformance coverage.

title: perf(protocol): exact borrowed GET parser fast lane
description: Profile-backed pass 59 after frankenredis-get-store-fast-read-5rpzl rejected active-expire zero-key fast return. Fresh GET P16/1M profile on main showed Store::get 10.62%, Runtime::refresh_store_runtime_info_context 9.12%, foldhash hash_one 4.79%, execute_plain_get_borrowed 4.51%, and parse/borrowed-dispatch residuals. Prior micro-levers on Store::get lookup fusion, context-refresh skip, and active-expire zero-key fast return were rejected. Alien/no-gaps primitive: zero-copy framing and parser-produced command specialization. Candidate: parse the exact hot GET key multibulk shape directly in fr-protocol, returning a borrowed key slice and consumed offset before fr-server allocates/materializes borrowed argv; non-GET, fallback states, and runtime gate refusals fall back to the existing borrowed argv path. Must preserve RESP parsing, command ordering, arity/error precedence, ACL/COMMAND metadata semantics, unknown-command fallback, case-insensitivity, and ambiguous/non-plain frames. Acceptance: established rch/hyperfine baseline first; one lever only; golden TCP sha256 equal vs baseline for pipelined GET plus lowercase/uppercase/unknown/arity/error/fallback cases; isomorphism proof for ordering/tie/floating/RNG; rch crate-scoped check/clippy/tests; paired and reversed GET P16 win with Score>=2.0; commit before/after numbers or remove source and reject.
close_reason: Rejected: exact borrowed GET parser failed Score>=2.0; paired 300k only 1.02x +/- 0.04, paired 1M only 1.05x +/- 0.04, reversed 300k tied, source removed.

title: AOF rewrite FUNCTION LOAD must omit REPLACE like Redis
description: Redis 7.2.4 legacy_redis_code/redis/src/aof.c::rewriteFunctions emits a 3-argument FUNCTION LOAD <code> command during AOF rewrite. fr-store's to_aof_commands currently emits FUNCTION LOAD REPLACE <code>, adding an upstream-divergent option in rewritten function-library commands.

title: Lua sandbox exposes 'print' and 'os' globals that vendored blocks
description: fr's Lua sandbox includes 'print' as a top-level RustFunction global and an 'os' table with os.clock. Redis 7.2.4's script_lua.c sandbox blocks both: scripts referencing print/os get 'Script attempted to access nonexistent global variable'. Live differential probe: fr 'print("hi") return 1' returns 1 (executes print as no-op) but vendored errors; fr type(os) returns 'table' but vendored errors. Fix: remove 'print' from the global list at fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:1821; remove the os table registration at lib.rs:2057-2063. The 'print' RustFunction dispatch at line 3507 should remain (it's harmless but unreachable after the global is dropped). Acceptance: 'return type(os)', 'return type(print)', 'print("x")' all produce the same 'Script attempted to access nonexistent global variable' error byte-exact with vendored.
close_reason: Lua sandbox now matches Redis 7.2.4's exposed-globals surface: (1) removed 'print' from the top-level RustFunction globals registration (~lib.rs:1821) so scripts that reference it now get the standard 'user_script:1: Script attempted to access nonexistent global variable print' error. The print RustFunction dispatch handler at line 3507 remains for internal callers, but the global is not bound. (2) removed the 'os' table registration (~lib.rs:2057-2063) which had exposed os.clock; upstream's script_lua.c blocks 'os' entirely. (3) added the 'user_script:1: ' source-location prefix to the 'Script attempted to access nonexistent global variable' error (line 2515) and to both 'Attempt to modify a readonly table' errors (lines 2332, 2424), matching the prefix luaL_error wraps around every C-side script error. Live differential probe: 11 cases byte-exact -- print('hi'), type(os), type(io), type(dofile), type(print), x=42 (readonly write rejected), type(string/math/table/redis/bit) (still exposed correctly). Tests: fr-command 910 pass.

title: ACL command-denial NOPERM message wrong: 'this user'+UPPERCASE vs upstream 'User <name>'+lowercase
description: fr's ACL command-denial reply is 'NOPERM this user has no permissions to run the '\''DEL'\'' command' (no username, command echoed as-typed/uppercase). Vendored redis 7.2.4 replies 'NOPERM User <name> has no permissions to run the '\''del'\'' command' (includes the authenticated username, command as canonical lowercase fullname). Pre-existing; affects EVERY ACL-restricted user (verified with a plain non-selector user). Sites: fr-command dispatch gate (~lib.rs:2150), fr-runtime gates (~5542, ~14149). Username is available via store.dispatch_client_ctx.authenticated_user (fr-command) and self.session.current_user_name() (fr-runtime). Key/channel denial messages already match. Also update 6 core_acl.json fixtures that currently encode the wrong format + unit tests asserting it. Found via ACL selector differential probe (d919b).
close_reason: Completed: command-level ACL NOPERM replies now include authenticated username and canonical lowercase selector/fullname; updated runtime dispatch, direct command dispatch, unit assertions, and core_acl fixture. Validated via rch focused fr-command/fr-runtime tests, core_acl conformance fixture, crate-scoped check/clippy.

title: Fix unused CLIENT CACHING warning-clean gate
description: fr-command emits unused CLIENT_CACHING_REQUIRES_TRACKING dead_code/unused-import warnings during focused cargo tests; remove stale constant/import so warning-clean gates can pass.

title: Implement command propagation from primary to connected replicas
description:
# Command Propagation to Replicas

## What
When FrankenRedis is acting as a primary and replicas are connected, write commands must be propagated to all connected replicas in real-time.

## Current State
The fr-server has `replication_follow_up_bytes()` which serializes commands for replication. Replica connections are tracked. The gap is that write commands are not actually pushed to replica write buffers after execution, and the backlog buffer is not populated.

## Implementation
1. After each write command execution in the main event loop, check if there are connected replicas
2. Serialize the command as RESP (already done by replication_follow_up_bytes)
3. Push the serialized bytes into each replica connection write_buf AND the backlog buffer
4. Advance replication offset by the number of bytes pushed
5. Handle slow replicas: if write_buf exceeds output_buffer_limit, disconnect replica with warning
6. Ensure read-only commands (GET, KEYS, etc.) are NOT propagated

## Unit Tests (in fr-runtime)
- Test 1: Write command increments replication offset
- Test 2: Read command does NOT increment replication offset
- Test 3: Offset advances by exact RESP-serialized byte count
- Test 4: MULTI/EXEC propagates all commands in the transaction

## Integration Tests
- Test 5: Start primary, connect replica, SET 1000 keys on primary, verify all 1000 appear on replica
- Test 6: Slow replica (inject delay) — verify primary disconnects it after buffer overflow
- Test 7: Multiple replicas — verify all receive the same commands
- Test 8: INFO replication on primary shows connected_slaves count

## Logging
- Log propagation: "REPL: propagating SET key (42 bytes) to 2 replicas"
- Log offset advancement
- Log slow replica disconnection with reason

## Depends On
- REPLICAOF TCP client (1v8b) — need a connection to propagate to
- Backlog buffer (iz1j) — propagated bytes go here for partial resync
- RDB snapshot streaming (de0t) — replica must have initial state before streaming

## Files
- crates/fr-server/src/main.rs (propagation after command dispatch)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (offset tracking)
close_reason: Already implemented: propagate_writes_to_replicas() at main.rs:2255 pushes AOF-serialized writes to all connected replica write buffers. Called in main event loop at line 534 after every command batch. Unit test at line 3564 verifies the flow.

title: [code-review] ACL SETUSER rejects generated subcommand selectors
description: Recent ACL CAT metadata generation added subcommand-form names such as acl|cat, client|info, and command|docs, but ACL SETUSER +command/-command validation still accepts only top-level command-table names. Users cannot grant or deny generated subcommand selectors, and permission checks still evaluate only the top-level argv[0]. Acceptance: ACL SETUSER accepts known generated subcommand selector names and runtime ACL checks honor them for commands such as ACL CAT while still rejecting unrelated ACL subcommands.
close_reason: Verified subcommand selector ACL support is implemented and covered by acl_subcommand/acl_per_subcommand runtime tests

title: Lua table.foreach / table.foreachi missing from sandbox (Lua 5.1 stdlib)
description:
Lua 5.1 ltablib.c exposes table.foreach(t, f) and table.foreachi(t, f)
which iterate over the table and call f(k,v) (or f(i,v) for the
array part) for each entry. Vendored Redis 7.2.4 exposes both — they
were deprecated and removed in Lua 5.2+ but Redis pins Lua 5.1.

Repro:
  EVAL "local t={'a','b'}; local r=''; table.foreach(t,function(k,v) r=r..k..v end); return r" 0
    vendored: 1a2b
    fr:       (error) ERR ... attempt to call field 'foreach' (a nil value)

  EVAL "local t={'a','b'}; local r=''; table.foreachi(t,function(i,v) r=r..i..v end); return r" 0
    vendored: 1a2b
    fr:       (error) ERR ... attempt to call field 'foreachi' (a nil value)

table.foreach iterates all keys via pairs() ordering (array part
1..#t in order, then hash part). If f returns a non-nil value, the
iteration stops and that value is returned. Otherwise returns nil.
table.foreachi iterates 1..#t (array part only) in order.

Both are pure Lua 5.1 contracts — no Redis-specific semantics — and
should be one short builtin arm each plus a table-lib registration.
close_reason: completed

title: Expand stable core_errors live Redis coverage

title: [HIGH] DEBUG DIGEST/DIGEST-VALUE/OBJECT parity — 48 divergences across digest-computation + DEBUG OBJECT encoding/addr/serialize fields
description: Surfaced by live_redis_core_debug_matches_runtime (48 cases after enable-debug-command yes): (a) DEBUG DIGEST / DIGEST-VALUE — fr-runtime doesn't implement the xor-SHA1-of-per-key-digest algorithm upstream uses, so every digest case diverges. (b) DEBUG OBJECT — upstream reply is 'Value at:0x... refcount:1 encoding:<enc> serializedlength:N lru:... lru_seconds_idle:N type:<type>'; ours emits a shorter 'refcount:1 encoding:<enc>' without the pointer/lru/serialized fields. (c) DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE invalid-value / wrong-arity wording differs. (d) Case-insensitive DEBUG subcommand dispatch — upstream normalises, ours may not. Fix: wire DEBUG DIGEST / DIGEST-VALUE in fr-runtime + fill in the full DEBUG OBJECT reply + align SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE error wording + lowercase dispatch.
close_reason: Already resolved: live_redis_core_debug_matches_runtime passes 100% under STRICT mode. DEBUG OBJECT + DIGEST/DIGEST-VALUE canonicalizers + SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE permissive parse + upstream-wording fixes all landed.

title: [perf] INCR existing-key in-place integer rewrite avoids generic key reinsert
description: Profile-backed target from pass146: current vs-redis dashboard on main b24685c5a shows INCR P16/C50 at redis/fr 1.42x slower. Remote perf on vmi1152480 under INCR P16 shows Store::drop_if_expired 8.73%, Store::internal_entries_insert 5.18%, foldhash hashing about 5.5%, Store::incr 2.83%, parse_command_args_borrowed_into 1.25%. Candidate: for existing live string/integer INCR/INCRBY keys, rewrite the entry in place to Value::Integer(next) instead of key.clone()+internal_entries_insert, while preserving Redis-visible and internal contracts: TTL unchanged, LRU/LFU reset like Entry::new, modification_count bumped, force flags cleared, int_copy_not_shared cleared, HLL cache invalidated, dirty incremented, digest marked stale, WRONGTYPE/parse/overflow behavior unchanged. Baseline and proof artifacts under artifacts/optimization/frankenredis-incr-inplace/pass146/.
close_reason: Kept pass146 INCR existing-key in-place rewrite: paired hyperfine 673.7ms -> 609.8ms (1.10x +/-0.05), golden SHA256 unchanged 4acdf6b733ca8d92c32b75ce310bfe2386ac9d1c0609d7162da4ffd3d1fd69fc

title: COMMAND DOCS metadata gaps for acl and cluster subcommands
description:
COMMAND DOCS metadata for `acl` and `cluster` subcommands diverges from vendored Redis 7.2.4:

ACL:
  fr has extra subcommand-argument fields: `category`, `optional`
  fr is missing some subcommand DOCS entries (e.g. `acl|list`, `acl|save`, `acl|load`) that exist in vendored
  Subcommand ordering also differs

CLUSTER:
  fr lacks deprecated/history metadata fields (`deprecated`, `deprecated_since`, `doc_flags`, `history`, `replaced_by`) that vendored emits for deprecated subcommands (ADDSLOTS, DELSLOTS, GETSLOTSINNODE...)
  fr has extra fields `block`, `range` that don't appear in vendored's metadata

Sample comparison:
  vendored COMMAND DOCS acl arg keys = acl,arguments,complexity,display_text,flags,group,multiple,name,server,since,string,subcommands,summary,type,username
  fr       COMMAND DOCS acl arg keys = acl,arguments,category,complexity,display_text,flags,group,multiple,name,optional,server,since,string,subcommands,summary,type,username

The actual subcommand IMPLEMENTATIONS are correct on fr (ACL LIST/SAVE/LOAD/etc. work) — only the COMMAND DOCS metadata is out of sync.

Fix plan (scope is substantial):
1. Audit per-subcommand argument metadata across ACL and CLUSTER subcommands.
2. Update each metadata block to match upstream's commands/*.json schema.
3. Add the missing subcommand DOCS entries for ACL (list/save/load).
4. Remove the fr-specific `category`/`optional` ACL arg fields.
5. Add deprecated/history metadata for CLUSTER's deprecated subcommands.

This is multi-day work and likely won't fit in a single probe iteration. Other commands in COMMAND DOCS may have similar issues — a broader audit should cover all of them.

Probe sweep #76 (COMMAND DOCS shape probe, inline).
close_reason: completed

title: Implement real DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE, RELOAD, and OBJECT subcommands
description:
# Implement real DEBUG subcommands

## What
Three DEBUG subcommands currently return OK without doing anything:
1. DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE 0/1: should toggle the active-expire cycle on/off
2. DEBUG RELOAD: should save + reload the dataset (equivalent to SAVE + restart load)
3. DEBUG OBJECT <key>: should return encoding, refcount, serializedlength, lru info

## Implementation

### DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE
- Add pub active_expire_enabled: bool to Store (default true)
- DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE 0 sets it to false, 1 sets it to true
- The runtime's active_expire_cycle checks this flag before running
- dispatch_argv needs &mut Store access (already has it)

### DEBUG RELOAD
- This is a runtime-level operation (needs to call persist_snapshot_to_disk then load_aof)
- In fr-command, we can't do this. Two options:
  a) Add a flag to Store (debug_reload_requested) that the runtime checks after dispatch
  b) Accept that DEBUG RELOAD only works from the runtime path (not from Lua)
- Recommendation: option (a) — add the flag, runtime checks it after each command batch

### DEBUG OBJECT <key>
- dispatch_argv has access to Store, which has object_encoding()
- Return format: \"Value at:0x... refcount:1 encoding:<encoding> serializedlength:<len> lru:<idle> lru_seconds_idle:<idle_s>\"
- encoding: from store.object_encoding()
- serializedlength: from store.memory_usage_for_key()
- lru/idle: from store.object_idletime()
- refcount: always 1 (same as Redis for non-shared objects)

### DEBUG JMAP
- Keep as no-op — JVM thread dump is N/A for Rust. Return OK.

## Testing
- Unit test: DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE 0, verify active_expire_enabled is false
- Unit test: DEBUG OBJECT <key> returns correct encoding string
- Conformance: add DEBUG OBJECT cases

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs (debug_cmd)
- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs (active_expire_enabled field)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (check active_expire_enabled, check debug_reload_requested)
close_reason: Completed

Fixed in fr-command bitcount argc-5 arm by adding the negative-reversed early-return before the unit check. Found via option fuzz; 0 diffs/8000 after fix; golden sha256 byte-exact. BITPOS/LPOS/GETEX/EXPIRE-family fuzzed clean.

title: Repair stale Beads SQLite database from JSONL
description: br sync --flush-only refuses to export because .beads/beads.db has fewer issues than issues.jsonl. Snapshot .beads, rebuild an isolated DB from JSONL, verify doctor/status/show, then promote without deleting old DB artifacts.
close_reason: DB recovery shipped via hs8w sister bead. Procedure documented + executed: (1) cp .beads/beads.db backup; (2) br doctor --repair (rebuilds blocked_issues_cache); (3) when 'br sync --import-only' fails with UNIQUE constraint due to duplicate-row corruption, mv the canonical .beads/beads.db{,-wal} aside; (4) br init --prefix frankenredis; (5) br sync --import-only --rebuild (imports JSONL→fresh DB cleanly). Current state verified: 'br doctor' reports sqlite.integrity_check OK, counts.db_vs_jsonl OK (806/806), sync.metadata in sync, schema.tables OK, schema.columns OK, write_probe OK. Snapshots preserved at .beads/beads.db.backup_orphan_recovery_1777347889 and .beads/beads.db.corrupt_orphan_recovery_1777347912 (gitignored). The 'snapshot, rebuild isolated, verify, promote' acceptance criterion has been met end-to-end.

title: Main-loop propagate_writes_to_replicas scans ALL clients every iteration (O(N), even with 0 replicas)
description: fr-server main loop calls propagate_writes_to_replicas (main.rs:1465) every event-loop iteration; it does clients.iter_mut() over the WHOLE client table to find replicas (conn.replication_sent_offset.is_some()). On a read-only or idle workload, or any server with few/zero replicas + many connections, that is an O(connected-clients) scan per iteration for nothing. Redis keeps an explicit server.replicas list. SAFE-LOOKING GUARD: track last_propagated_offset; skip the call when runtime.replication_primary_offset() is unchanged since the last main-loop propagate — a static offset means no replica is behind, because (a) propagate queues all pending bytes into each replica's write_buf and sets sent_offset=primary_offset (the async write_buf/writer-pool/WRITABLE machinery delivers them independently), and (b) a freshly-attached replica's sent_offset is set to the CURRENT primary_offset at attach (main.rs:10006/10104/10148), its initial data coming from the PSYNC snapshot, not propagate. CAVEAT/RISK (why deferred): a PARTIAL PSYNC resync (replica_psync_request, main.rs:5422) could set sent_offset BACKWARD (< primary_offset, to resend backlog) with primary_offset static -> the offset-guard would skip and stall the resync. Must confirm fr's resync path (full vs partial) before guarding, OR guard on an explicit replica-count (replication_runtime_state.replicas.is_empty(), main.rs:7139/24184) instead — but that must be proven in-lockstep with conn.replication_sent_offset. REPLICATION-CRITICAL: verify with the replication parity gates (replication_cross_compat_gate, replication_digest_fuzz) which are sandbox-144-blocked this session. Alternative low-risk: maintain a replica_tokens:HashSet<Token> updated at every sent_offset set/clear and iterate only that. Not benchmarked (env 144); value is at high connection counts.
close_reason: Shipped a691b60d1: early-return propagate_writes_to_replicas when !has_connected_replicas() (registry authoritative; offset set only when is_replica). Verified replication_cross_compat_gate + replication_digest_fuzz (3x500) PASS — propagation unchanged with replicas, O(N) sweep skipped without.

title: [HIGH] r73v sub-task (c): LZF compression on RDB save
description: Follow-up to br-frankenredis-r73v: implement upstream-compatible LZF compression on the fr-persist RDB write side. Today fr-persist::rdb_encode_string emits raw length-prefixed strings; upstream prefers LZF (encoding byte 0xC3 with the 0x40 special-encoding flag) for strings >= 16 bytes (configurable via rdbcompression). fr-persist already DEcompresses LZF on read (see lzf_decompress in fr-persist/src/lib.rs:1212). Implement the matching LZF compressor (~80 lines, mirrors upstream's lzf_c.c::lzf_compress). Wire into rdb_encode_string with a heuristic threshold + 'compressed-fits' check. Acceptance: encode/decode round-trip is identity AND fr-persist's emitted dump.rdb loads cleanly under upstream redis-server when long strings are present.

title: INCRBYFLOAT/HINCRBYFLOAT (and verify SPOP) not rewritten to deterministic form for AOF/replication propagation
description: Upstream rewrites non-deterministic write commands before propagation: t_string.c incrbyfloatCommand rewrites INCRBYFLOAT -> SET key <result>; t_hash.c hincrbyfloatCommand rewrites HINCRBYFLOAT -> HSET key field <result>; t_set.c spopCommand rewrites SPOP -> SREM key <members> (or DEL when emptied). This guarantees replicas/AOF-replay reproduce the EXACT value/members regardless of float-impl or PRNG differences. fr's propagation arm (fr-runtime ~5625) only calls rewrite_relative_expire_for_propagation (expire-family + SET-EX + GETEX); everything else propagates VERBATIM. Confirmed via oracle AOF (port 17541, appendonly): INCRBYFLOAT->SET, HINCRBYFLOAT->HSET, SPOP->SREM all present as rewritten forms; fr emits the verbatim command. INCRBYFLOAT/HINCRBYFLOAT are unambiguous determinism rewrites (high value, low risk). SPOP needs care: the mdrk8 note claims fr relies on master/replica PRNG lockstep (fragile vs redis-replica / AOF-restart with a fresh seed) — verify whether fr should rewrite SPOP->SREM like upstream. Fix lives in fr-runtime's propagation arm (extend rewrite_for_propagation to use argv + the command reply, e.g. INCRBYFLOAT's new value). BLOCKED: fr-runtime currently has uncommitted peer (ACL) work; land when free.
close_reason: Fixed across commits edbd4c060 (INCRBYFLOAT->SET, HINCRBYFLOAT->HSET) and 065916be0 (SPOP->SREM/DEL). All three non-deterministic write commands now propagate as their deterministic equivalent for AOF/replication, matching upstream. Verified end-to-end via fr master->replica MONITOR: INCRBYFLOAT->SET, HINCRBYFLOAT->HSET, SPOP partial->SREM, SPOP full-drain->DEL; values/membership byte-identical on master and replica. Unit tests incrbyfloat_family_rewritten_to_deterministic_set_for_propagation + spop_rewritten_to_srem_or_del_for_propagation. fr-runtime 540 pass.

title: COMMAND GETKEYS gates should cover script/function read-only variants

The COMMAND GETKEYS/GETKEYSANDFLAGS differential gates cover EVAL/EVALSHA/FCALL but miss Redis' read-only variants EVAL_RO, EVALSHA_RO, and FCALL_RO. Add live Redis-oracle probes so script/function key extraction and flags stay byte-exact across read-only command metadata refactors.

title: Implement real REPLICAOF TCP client that connects to primary and completes PSYNC handshake
description:
# REPLICAOF TCP Client

## What
When `REPLICAOF host port` is executed (or --replicaof CLI flag), FrankenRedis should actually connect to the specified primary via TCP, complete the PSYNC handshake, and enter replication mode.

## Current State
The existing `drive_replica_sync()` in fr-server/src/main.rs has the connection scaffolding including PING, AUTH, REPLCONF, and PSYNC handshake steps. The `ReplicaPrimaryConnection` struct exists. The `replication_follow_up_bytes()` function exists. The issue is that REPLICAOF command in fr-runtime returns OK but does not trigger the actual connection — it only sets config state.

## Implementation
1. In fr-runtime, REPLICAOF should set `server.replicaof_host` and `server.replicaof_port` fields (add if missing)
2. In fr-server main loop, check if replicaof is configured but no ReplicaPrimaryConnection exists → initiate connection
3. The existing `drive_replica_sync()` already handles the handshake FSM — verify it works with a real Redis primary
4. After FULLRESYNC: receive RDB snapshot bytes, decode with fr_persist::decode_rdb, apply via apply_rdb_entries_to_store
5. After CONTINUE: receive backlog bytes, parse as RESP commands, execute through runtime
6. Handle connection failure: set retry timer, log error, try again after backoff

## Unit Tests (in fr-runtime)
- Test 1: REPLICAOF host port sets config fields correctly
- Test 2: REPLICAOF NO ONE clears config and stops replication
- Test 3: INFO replication shows role:slave when replicaof is set
- Test 4: INFO replication shows master_link_status:down before connection succeeds

## Integration Tests (in fr-server tests)
- Test 5: Start legacy redis-server, start frankenredis with --replicaof, verify PING/REPLCONF/PSYNC handshake completes (check INFO for master_link_status:up)
- Test 6: After handshake, SET on primary → GET on replica returns the value
- Test 7: Connection drops → replica detects and schedules retry

## Logging
- Log each handshake step with timestamp: "REPL: sending PING to primary", "REPL: received PONG", etc.
- Log FULLRESYNC/CONTINUE decision and offset
- Log snapshot size and apply time
- Log connection failures with retry schedule

## Files
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (REPLICAOF command handler, config fields)
- crates/fr-server/src/main.rs (connection initiation, snapshot apply)
- crates/fr-server/tests/replication_e2e.rs (integration tests)
close_reason: Completed

title: SMOVE should refresh touched set metadata on no-op paths like Redis
description: Upstream Redis 7.2.4 t_set.c::smoveCommand calls lookupKeyWrite on source and destination before checking same-set and missing-member no-op paths, and lookupKeyWrite refreshes LRU/LFU metadata. fr-store Store::smove currently returns from source==destination and missing-member paths without touching source/destination entries, so OBJECT IDLETIME can diverge even when SMOVE returns 0/1 without moving an element.
close_reason: Completed fr-store SMOVE no-op metadata parity fix

title: Sentinel voting for another leader should delay local failover
description: Upstream Redis 7.2.4 sentinelVoteLeader sets master->failover_start_time = mstime()+rand()%SENTINEL_MAX_DESYNC when the Sentinel votes for a runid other than its own myid. This forces a delay before this Sentinel can start a failover for the same master. fr-sentinel records the leader vote but leaves failover_start_time unchanged, so a Sentinel that just voted for another leader can immediately consider starting its own automatic failover. Acceptance: SENTINEL IS-MASTER-DOWN-BY-ADDR with a non-self runid records the vote and updates failover_start_time to at least the command time; self votes do not apply that delay.
close_reason: Implemented in 9c2f074

title: fr-runtime: HELLO option errors use generic 'syntax error' instead of upstream-specific 'Syntax error in HELLO option <opt>' wording
description: HELLO 3 AUTH (missing args) and HELLO 3 BADKW (unknown option) emit 'ERR syntax error'. Upstream networking.c::helloCommand emits 'ERR Syntax error in HELLO option <OPT>' with the (case-preserved) option name. Caught by ad-hoc differential probe.

title: Add PFDEBUG conformance fixtures
close_reason: PFDEBUG conformance fixtures added (27 cases) in core_pfdebug.json

title: AOF rewrite must preserve empty streams with Redis XADD MAXLEN 0 form
description: Redis 7.2.4 aof.c::rewriteStreamObject emits an XADD MAXLEN 0 0-1 x y command for empty streams, then always appends XSETID with ENTRIESADDED and MAXDELETEDID. FrankenRedis Store::to_aof_commands currently emits no XADD for an empty stream and only conditionally emits XSETID, so an empty stream is lost, and empty streams with consumer groups replay XGROUP CREATE against a missing key. Fix fr-store AOF generation to preserve empty stream keys and metadata like upstream.
close_reason: Completed fr-store empty stream AOF rewrite parity fix

title: Add benchmark regression gate to CI/scripts pipeline
description:
# Benchmark Regression Gate

## What
A script that runs the benchmark suite, compares against checked-in baselines, and fails if p99 degrades by more than 10% or throughput drops by more than 15%.

## Why
Performance regressions are silent killers. The Performance Doctrine in AGENTS.md requires "gate persistence overhead and replication-lag regressions" but no gate exists.

## Implementation
1. scripts/benchmark_gate.sh:
   - Start frankenredis in background
   - Run fr_benchmark with standard workloads
   - Compare against baselines/*.json
   - Report deltas
   - Exit non-zero if regression detected
2. Configurable thresholds via env vars
3. Output: delta report artifact in artifacts/benchmark/

## Depends On
- Benchmark harness (fr_benchmark binary)
- Initial baselines recorded
close_reason: Implemented benchmark regression gate script, docs, and guarded workflow integration

title: perf(store): inline integer value encoding (OBJ_ENCODING_INT) — avoid heap Vec for integer string values
description: Redis stores string values that parse as integers as the integer itself (OBJ_ENCODING_INT), no heap alloc. fr-store stores ALL string values as Value::String(Vec<u8>), so every INCR/counter/small-int value heap-allocates a Vec. LEVER: add Value::Int(i64) (or a tagged small-object variant) so integer values avoid the heap Vec — the huge INCR/counter/integer-key workload goes alloc->0 for the value. Blast radius: every Value::String match site in fr-store + fr-command handlers must handle the Int variant (materialize bytes lazily via a Cow/accessor for read paths). Big memory-layout lever (addendum's 'inline small objects'). PARITY: OBJECT ENCODING must report 'int'; all string ops must behave identically. Target: per-value heap alloc->0 on integer workloads + lower RSS.
close_reason: Completed: inline integer string Value storage; paired 10M hyperfine 1.450s +/-0.030s -> 1.209s +/-0.043s (1.20x +/-0.05x), golden sha256 efd2f0d222b340ce440d36eafaf86af53ab2023c943a636c597ea30645ed729e

title: Lua redis.* sandbox: error_reply/status_reply/log/sha1hex arity & type-check gaps
description:
Differential probe against vendored Redis 7.2.4 found four real semantic divergences in fr's Lua redis.* sandbox surface:

1. redis.error_reply with 2+ args:
   vendored: ERR wrong number or type of arguments
   fr:       ERR a   (silently uses first arg, ignores extras)

2. redis.status_reply with 2+ args:
   vendored: ERR wrong number or type of arguments
   fr:       a   (silently uses first arg, ignores extras)

3. redis.log with negative level:
   vendored: ERR Invalid debug level.
   fr:       returns 1 (no rejection — invalid level silently accepted)

4. redis.sha1hex(true):
   vendored: da39a3ee5e6b4b0d3255bfef95601890afd80709  (sha1 of empty — boolean coerced to empty)
   fr:       5ffe533b830f08a0326348a9160afafc8ada44db  (different — hashing "true" repr)

Lower-priority wording divergences from same sweep (filed for separate follow-up):
- redis.call too-many-args: fr emits "Wrong number of args calling Redis command from script" vs vendored's "wrong number of arguments for '<cmd>' command".
- redis.pcall no-args: fr wraps the error in `script: <sha>, on @user_script:1.` envelope while vendored emits the bare error.

Fix locations: likely in crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs near the redis.* RustFunction handlers. Each handler needs argument count validation and (for sha1hex) explicit type coercion matching upstream scripting.c::luaRedisSha1hexCommand (which converts the arg via lua_tolstring — booleans yield empty string, not "true"/"false").

Test plan:
  - Pin redis.error_reply()/redis.status_reply() with 0, 1, 2, 3 args.
  - Pin redis.log(-1, "msg") rejected with the "Invalid debug level." text.
  - Pin redis.log(redis.LOG_DEBUG, "msg") still accepted.
  - Pin redis.sha1hex(true), redis.sha1hex(false), redis.sha1hex(nil) all return SHA1 of empty string (matching upstream lua_tolstring coercion).

title: EVAL/script WAIT returns 'requires runtime replication context' error; vendored returns 0
description:
Vendored Redis 7.2.4's waitCommand returns the current ack count immediately when CLIENT_DENY_BLOCKING is set (scripts), regardless of the requested numreplicas. With no replicas attached the count is 0, so EVAL 'return redis.call("WAIT", 0, 0)' returns 0.

fr's wait_cmd (fr-command/src/lib.rs:19013) acknowledges this in its comment but unconditionally errors:
  'ERR WAIT requires runtime replication context'

Probe:
  EVAL 'return redis.call("WAIT", 0, 0)' 0
    vendored: 0
    fr:       ERR WAIT requires runtime replication context

Fix: return Ok(RespFrame::Integer(0)) from wait_cmd after validation passes — matches vendored's script-context behavior (no replicas → 0 acks). The runtime path Runtime::handle_wait_command stays unchanged; it's the one with real state and can return non-zero ack counts.

Update the two existing tests that pin the error wording:
  - tests::wait_validates_numreplicas_timeout_in_script_context (around line 50450)
  - tests::wait_rejects_extra_args_and_does_not_fake_script_ack_counts (around line 50505)

Both currently assert the error; flip to assert Ok(Integer(0)).

Probe sweep #128.
close_reason: completed

title: Use exact Lua number equality in script evaluator
close_reason: Completed

title: Lua concat error lacks accessor context (local/upvalue/field NAME)
description: fr's BinOp::Concat error 'attempt to concatenate a TYPE value' lacks the accessor-context prefix vendored emits. Probe: 'local t={}; t..\'x\'' → vendored 'attempt to concatenate upvalue \'t\' (a table value)' (since t is captured by the function passed to pcall), fr 'attempt to concatenate a table value'. Same pattern as frankenredis-md71j for call errors. Requires plumbing the operand AST expression through eval_binop or wrapping the concat error formation at the eval_expr BinOp call site so it can derive the accessor label from the operand Expr::Name/Field/Index. Discovered while implementing frankenredis-hqevr __concat metamethod.
close_reason: Reused callee_label() (added for md71j) in eval_expr's BinOp::Concat branch. After the __concat metamethod check fails, derive the accessor label from the first non-string-non-number operand's AST Expr (LHS preferred when both are bad) and emit Lua 5.1's 'attempt to concatenate <label> (a TYPE value)' wording instead of the bare 'attempt to concatenate a TYPE value'. Anonymous call sites (function call result, parenthesized expression) still fall back to the bare form. Verified live diff vs vendored 7.2.4 (port 16380): 10/10 cases match including local-LHS, local-RHS, upvalue, field by literal name, numeric-index ('field ?'), string-key index, method-call-result anonymous, paren-nil anonymous, function-call-result anonymous, and global access. Pinned by lua_concat_error_carries_accessor_context covering 7 sub-cases. All 926 fr-command lib tests pass.

title: CLIENT INFO emits rbs=0 rbp=0 — vendored emits rbs=16384 rbp=16384 (PROTO_REPLY_CHUNK_BYTES default)
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4: CLIENT INFO output matches vendored byte-for-byte EXCEPT the rbs and rbp fields. Per upstream networking.c::catClientInfoString, rbs = c->buf_usable_size (initial reply buffer = PROTO_REPLY_CHUNK_BYTES = 16384) and rbp = c->buf_peak (peak observed). For a fresh connection both equal 16384. fr hardcodes both to 0 in two places (fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:5871 client_info_line_for_session, fr-command/src/lib.rs:15401 client_info_line).

Fix: emit rbs=16384 rbp=16384 to match vendored idle-state baseline. Acceptance: CLIENT INFO byte-equal vendored under sed-canonicalisation of dynamic fields.
close_reason: Pinned rbs/rbp to 16384 (PROTO_REPLY_CHUNK_BYTES) in both client_info_line_for_session (fr-runtime) and client_info_line (fr-command) to mirror upstream's createClient default-allocation baseline. CLIENT INFO byte-equal vendored under sed-canonicalisation.

title: Make smoke_harness_finds_oracle test conditional on oracle presence
close_reason: Made oracle assertion conditional on oracle directory existence; conformance now 142/142 on remote workers

title: Track per-client peer address for CLIENT LIST/KILL accuracy
description:
## Problem

CLIENT LIST always reports addr=127.0.0.1:0. Redis reports the actual peer address (e.g., addr=10.0.0.5:54321). This breaks:
- Client monitoring tools that parse CLIENT LIST
- CLIENT KILL by addr (which needs the real address)
- Debugging connection issues in production

## Implementation Plan

1. In fr-server accept_connections, after accepting a TcpStream, call stream.peer_addr()
2. Store the SocketAddr in ClientConnection (new field: peer_addr: SocketAddr)
3. Add peer_addr to ClientSession so it's available during execute_frame
4. In fr-runtime CLIENT LIST handler, use session.peer_addr instead of hardcoded value
5. Also store the local listen address for the laddr field

## Files to Modify

- crates/fr-server/src/main.rs: Store peer_addr on accept, propagate to session
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs: ClientSession peer_addr field, CLIENT LIST format
close_reason: ClientSession.peer_addr stores real peer address from accept(), CLIENT LIST uses it instead of hardcoded 127.0.0.1:0

title: Sentinel INFO parser should honor replica_announced like Redis
description: Upstream Redis 7.2.4 sentinelRefreshInstanceInfo parses replica_announced:<announcement> inside the role == SRI_SLAVE branch and updates ri->replica_announced via atoi. FrankenRedis exposes replica-announced and filters SENTINEL REPLICAS on SentinelRedisInstance::replica_announced, but fr-sentinel::health::parse_info_response never parses replica_announced from INFO, so a live replica advertising replica_announced:0 remains visible. Acceptance: replica_announced is captured only after role:slave has been observed, applies to SentinelRedisInstance::replica_announced, treats 0/malformed as false and nonzero as true, master-role INFO cannot hide a replica, and existing normal slave parsing still works.
close_reason: Completed Sentinel INFO replica_announced parser parity

title: fr-command SETRANGE rejects empty value with large offset; upstream short-circuits before size check
description:
REVIEW MODE Tick 14 finding via /testing-fuzzing on SETRANGE arg combinations vs vendored t_string.c::setrangeCommand.

UPSTREAM BEHAVIOR (t_string.c lines 442-500):
  1. parse offset; offset < 0 → 'offset is out of range'
  2. lookupKeyWrite
     - missing key + EMPTY value → addReply shared.czero (NO size check, NO key create)
     - missing key + non-empty value → checkStringLength → create
     - existing string + EMPTY value → addReplyLongLong(olen) (return existing length, NO size check)
     - existing string + non-empty value → checkStringLength → grow + write

FR BEHAVIOR (crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs::setrange lines 9396-9420):
  After parsing offset, fr ALWAYS runs the size check: 'offset_u64 + added_len > 512 MiB → ERR string exceeds maximum allowed size'.
  This fires even when added_len == 0, which upstream never gates on size.

REPRO:
  SETRANGE notexist 600000000 ""
    upstream → Integer(0)
    fr      → 'ERR string exceeds maximum allowed size (proto-max-bulk-len)'

  SETRANGE existing 600000000 ""
    upstream → Integer(strlen) (existing length)
    fr      → 'ERR string exceeds maximum allowed size (proto-max-bulk-len)'

Severity: [LOW]. Wire-format conformance gap. Empty-value SETRANGE is a niche pattern (no-op probe for current length without modifying), but mishandling produces a spurious error reply.

FIX:
  Short-circuit when argv[3] is empty BEFORE the size check:
    - if key missing → Integer(0)
    - if key existing string → Integer(strlen)
    - if key existing non-string → WRONGTYPE
  This matches upstream's order: lookup (and empty-value short-circuit) precedes the size check.
close_reason:
Added empty-value short-circuit in setrange() before the size check, mirroring upstream t_string.c lines 458-461 and 478-481.

CHANGE (crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs):
After the offset parse and negative-offset check, when argv[3] is empty fr now calls store.strlen(key) and returns its value (0 for missing keys, current strlen for existing strings, WRONGTYPE for existing non-strings) — without running the 'string exceeds maximum allowed size' guard. Previously fr fired that guard whenever offset > 512 MiB regardless of value length, producing spurious errors for `SETRANGE k 600000000 ""`.

Regression test setrange_empty_value_short_circuits_before_size_check pins all three branches (missing, existing-string, existing-non-string).

Verification:
- cargo test -p fr-command --lib setrange → 3/3 pass (setrange_command, adversarial_setrange_empty, setrange_empty_value_short_circuits_before_size_check).
- cargo clippy -p fr-command --lib --no-deps -- -D warnings → clean.
- The 4 pre-existing failures (xreadgroup_*, xinfo_stream_reports_live_deleted_id, migrate_stream_dump_payload) reproduce on git stash baseline — unrelated.

[LOW] severity. Wire-format conformance gap, only visible when SETRANGE is invoked with an empty value and an offset above 512 MiB.

title: ACL GETUSER/LIST drops explicit +cmd after +@all (early-return ignores allowed_commands)
description:
After `ACL SETUSER u on '+@all' '-DEBUG' '+debug'` (or similar), vendored Redis 7.2.4 reports the user's commands as `+@all +debug` — preserving the explicit `+debug` modifier on top of `+@all`. fr collapses this to just `+@all` because `commands_string()` short-circuits as soon as `all_commands` is true and the deny sets are empty, ignoring whatever explicit allows accumulated.

Reproduction:
  ACL SETUSER u on '+@all' '+set' -> v: commands = "+@all +set"
                                     f: commands = "+@all"
  ACL SETUSER u on '+@all' '-set' '+set'  -> v: "+@all +set"
                                             f: "+@all"
  ACL SETUSER u on '+@all' '-DEBUG' '+debug' -> v: "+@all +debug"
                                                f: "+@all"

Both `ACL GETUSER` (`commands` field) and `ACL LIST` show the dropped modifier — same code path through `AclUser::commands_string`.

Root cause: fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:791-795 early-returns `+@all` whenever `all_commands && denied_commands.is_empty() && denied_categories.is_empty()`, but doesn't check the *allowed* sets. So a non-empty `allowed_commands` (which `+cmd` after `+@all` populates at line 1345) gets dropped.

Fix plan:
- Extend the early-return guard to also require `allowed_commands.is_empty() && allowed_categories.is_empty()`. Only collapse to the bare `+@all` shortcut when there's nothing else to emit.
- Falling through into the existing parts builder emits `+@all` plus the sorted explicit grants, matching upstream's order.
- Regression test pins all three cases above plus the simple `+@all` (still collapses) plus `+@all -DEBUG` (still emits "-debug").

Probe sweep #77 (probe155.sh + inline follow-up).
close_reason: completed

title: CLUSTER SETSLOT invalid action uses syntax/arity instead of Redis help error
description: Redis cluster.c routes CLUSTER SETSLOT requests with argc >= 4 into the SETSLOT handler, validates slot, then returns 'ERR Invalid CLUSTER SETSLOT action or number of arguments. Try CLUSTER HELP' for invalid actions or per-action arity mismatches. fr-command currently returns generic syntax for bogus actions and cluster wrong-arity for enabled-cluster extra/missing action arguments. Preserve the Redis SETSLOT handler error wording and pin invalid action plus STABLE/NODE shape cases.

title: fr-command SET defers NX/XX conflict to post-loop, lets a malformed EX/PX integer surface its parse error first
description:
Tick finding via /porting-to-rust differential probe vs vendored t_string.c::parseExtendedStringArgumentsOrReply.

UPSTREAM (t_string.c lines 216-225):
  The NX option is only accepted when '!(*flags & OBJ_SET_XX)' — a second NX after XX (or vice versa) is rejected EAGERLY at parse time, returning shared.syntaxerr from parseExtendedStringArgumentsOrReply BEFORE any later option (including EX/PX with their integer arg) is processed.

FR CURRENT (crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs::set lines 2471-2484):
  NX and XX flags are accumulated unconditionally, and `if nx && xx { return SyntaxError }` only fires AFTER the full option loop. This means a malformed integer for a downstream EX/PX/EXAT/PXAT surfaces its 'value is not an integer or out of range' error before the NX+XX conflict is detected.

REPRO:
  SET k v NX XX EX BAD
    upstream → 'ERR syntax error' (XX rejected on NX flag set)
    fr      → 'ERR value is not an integer or out of range'

  SET k v XX NX 5  (where the trailing 5 is just a position)
    upstream → 'ERR syntax error'
    fr      → 'ERR syntax error' (post-loop check still catches it; both agree here)

Severity: [LOW]. Wire-format conformance gap. Visible only when both NX/XX conflict AND a malformed integer follow.

FIX:
  Move the `if nx { return SyntaxError }` and `if xx { return SyntaxError }` guards INSIDE the NX/XX branches of the option loop, so the conflict surfaces before later options are parsed. Drop the now-dead post-loop `if nx && xx` check.
close_reason:
Moved the SET NX/XX conflict check INSIDE the option loop, mirroring upstream parseExtendedStringArgumentsOrReply at t_string.c lines 216-225 which gates each flag on the absence of its opposite.

CHANGE (crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs::set):
- NX branch now returns SyntaxError if xx is already set.
- XX branch now returns SyntaxError if nx is already set.
- Removed the now-dead post-loop `if nx && xx` check.

Effect: `SET k v NX XX EX BAD` now returns 'ERR syntax error' (matching upstream) instead of 'ERR value is not an integer or out of range' that fr previously surfaced after parsing the EX argument.

Regression test set_nx_xx_conflict_fires_before_trailing_integer_parse pins three cases:
  - SET k v NX XX EX NOTANINT → SyntaxError
  - SET k v XX NX PX NOTANINT → SyntaxError
  - SET k v NX EX 5 → still succeeds (sanity check that the eager-reject didn't break the legitimate path)

Verification:
- cargo test -p fr-command --lib set_ → 42/42 pass.
- cargo clippy -p fr-command --lib --no-deps -- -D warnings → clean.

[LOW] severity. Wire-format conformance gap. The deeper deferred-parse case (PX/EX with a malformed integer when followed by another expiry kind) is left for a separate bead.

Default DB 0 key namespacing is byte-identical to the original argv, but the current path still clones argv and key buffers before dispatch.
