title: Lua compile-error line always reported as :1: (EVAL/loadstring/FUNCTION); redis reports the actual error line (parser tracks lines via with_lines but callers hardcode :1:)

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
cc (claude-code): Found cross-reviewing fr's Lua compile path. fr's Lua parse-error LINE is always reported as 1, regardless of the actual error line.

REPRO vs redis 7.2.4:
  EVAL "local x=1\nlocal y=2\nsyntax error here" 0
    redis: -ERR Error compiling script (new function): user_script:3: '=' expected near 'error'
    fr   : -ERR Error compiling script (new function): user_script:1: '=' expected near 'error'
  EVAL "local a=1\nlocal b=2\nlocal c=3\n)bad" 0  -> redis user_script:4, fr user_script:1
Single-line scripts are coincidentally correct (line==1).

ROOT CAUSE: lua_eval::Parser HAS a `lines: Vec<u32>` field (1-based source line per token, set via Parser::with_lines, used by parse_lua_chunk for EVAL). But the error MESSAGES are bare ("'=' expected near 'X'") and every caller HARDCODES the line: lib.rs sites 23825/23888 (compile_lua_chunk_cached path) + 24023 (compile_check path) format "user_script:1: {parse_err}", and lua_eval loadstring/load format "{chunk_label}:1: {msg}". The parser knows the line (self.lines[self.pos]) but never surfaces it.

FIX DESIGN (fr-command, cross-cutting — needs cargo test to verify no regression in the ~1266 compile-error assertions, so deferred from this code-first turn):
 1. Add Parser::error_line(&self) -> u32 = self.lines.get(self.pos.min(len-1)).copied().unwrap_or(1).
 2. parse_lua_chunk: on parser/eof error, prefix the line -> Err(format!("{}: {}", parser.error_line(), msg)); also prefix lexer errors (line not tracked -> 1 or extend lexer).
 3. compile_check: same line-prefixing.
 4. Command-layer sites (23825/23888/24023) + loadstring/load formatting: change "...:1: {parse_err}" -> "...:{parse_err}" so the parser-supplied line is used (single-line stays :1:, multi-line gets the real line).
Guarded by scripts/eval_compile_error_line_differ.py (documented divergence -> auto-promote when fixed). Related symptom: frankenredis-mbyoe (FUNCTION LOAD doesn't compile at all).

title: fr-runtime: AUTH 2-arg form returns NotConfigured error instead of WRONGPASS when user is unknown
description:
AUTH 2-argument form (AUTH <user> <pass>) returns 'ERR AUTH <password> called without any password configured for the default user...' when no requirepass is set, regardless of which user was named. Upstream Redis 7.2 returns 'WRONGPASS invalid username-password pair or user is disabled.' for the 2-arg form when the user doesn't exist (or fails password check). The 1-arg form (AUTH <pass>) correctly returns the NotConfigured error.

Root cause: fr-runtime::authenticate_user short-circuits on auth_state.auth_required() returning false → AuthFailure::NotConfigured, before checking if the named user actually exists in the ACL. handle_auth_command then surfaces AUTH_NOT_CONFIGURED_ERROR for both forms.

Reference: against vendored redis 7.2.4 with no requirepass, AUTH baduser password returns WRONGPASS. fr returns the NotConfigured error. Caught by an end-to-end command-by-command diff smoke that compared fr (port 16510) vs vendored redis (port 16511) on AUTH baduser password.

Acceptance: AUTH <user> <pass> returns 'WRONGPASS invalid username-password pair or user is disabled.' when:
  • the named user doesn't exist in the ACL (no requirepass configured), OR
  • the user exists but is disabled, OR
  • the password is wrong.
AUTH <pass> (1-arg form) keeps the NotConfigured error when the default user has nopass + no requirepass set. This matches upstream server.c::authCommand which only emits the NotConfigured error for the 1-arg path.
close_reason: AUTH 2-arg form now returns WRONGPASS for unknown users / wrong passwords regardless of requirepass; 1-arg form preserves the upstream NotConfigured error. Pinned by two new fr-runtime regression tests. Shipped in 5c693d8.

title: Add metamorphic tests for fr-persist RDB/AOF roundtrip properties
RESP command multibulk must reject non-bulk/null elements at parse (expected '$', got 'X')
description:
Follow-up to w7xy8. A client COMMAND multibulk must have every element be a non-null bulk string. Remaining divergences: (1) '*1
+PING
' -> oracle "Protocol error: expected '$', got '+'"; fr accepts it and returns PONG. (2) '*2
$3
GET
$-1
' -> oracle 'invalid bulk length'; fr 'invalid command frame'. (3) inline 'PING"' -> oracle 'unbalanced quotes'; fr 'unknown command'. fr uses the generic RESP parser (allows any element type / null bulk) for command frames; redis's processMultibulkBuffer requires '$' per element. Needs a command-parse validation distinct from generic RESP reply parsing.
close_reason: Fully fixed (2 commits). (1) 7e8b265ae: parse_command_frame strict multibulk — non-$ element -> 'expected $, got X', $-1 arg -> 'invalid bulk length'. (2) this commit: split_inline_args rewritten to sds.c::sdssplitargs state machine — a quote can begin mid-token; any unclosed/not-followed-by-whitespace quote -> 'unbalanced quotes' (PING", SET a"b c, "foo"bar all now error; valid mid-token quoting like ab"c d" concatenates). All 3 remaining protocol divergences from the w7xy8 probe now byte-exact vs vendored 7.2.4 (18/18 protocol+inline cases). Tests: parse_command_frame_requires_bulk_elements, tcp_command_multibulk_rejects_non_bulk_elements, inline_mid_token_quote_matches_upstream + existing inline suite green; fr-protocol+fr-server pass, clippy clean.

title: Lua tonumber base handling: nil base, float base, 0x prefix with base 16
description:
Three semantic divergences in fr's tonumber vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 (lbaselib.c::luaB_tonumber):

1. tonumber(str, 16) with "0x" prefix: vendored's strtoul accepts the prefix when base is 16; fr's i64::from_str_radix rejects it.
   EVAL "return tonumber('0xff', 16)" 0
     vendored: 255
     fr:       (nil)

2. tonumber(str, 10.5) with float base: vendored's luaL_checkint truncates the base via (int)(d); fr errors with "base out of range".
   EVAL "return tonumber('10', 10.5)" 0
     vendored: 10
     fr:       ERR bad argument #2 to 'tonumber' (base out of range)

3. tonumber(str, nil) with explicit nil base: vendored treats nil base the same as no base (defaults to 10); fr errors.
   EVAL "return tonumber('10', nil)" 0
     vendored: 10
     fr:       ERR bad argument #2 to 'tonumber' (base out of range)

Plus a wording gap: all base-out-of-range errors lack the upstream "user_script:1:" prefix that luaL_argerror prepends.

Test plan:
  - Pin tonumber('0xff', 16) -> 255 and tonumber('FF', 16) -> 255.
  - Pin tonumber('10', 10.5) -> 10 (base truncated).
  - Pin tonumber('10', nil) -> 10 (default base).
  - Pin tonumber('0', 1)  / tonumber('0', 37) raise base-out-of-range with user_script:1: prefix.

title: perf: ZRANGE LIMIT double-collect allocates O(n) for O(k) result
description: ZRANGE/ZRANGEBYSCORE with LIMIT first collects full result into Vec, then skip/take into new Vec. For 1M-element sorted set with LIMIT 0 100, allocates 1M then 100. Should use iterator without intermediate collection. Lines 4645-4654 in fr-command/src/lib.rs.
close_reason: done

title: perf: parse_bulk() allocates via .to_vec() on every bulk string
description: parse_bulk() at fr-protocol line 508 does input[...].to_vec() for every bulk string parsed. For large payloads this is a full allocation+memcpy. Consider zero-copy or cow approach for read-only paths.
notes: Requires architectural change: add bytes crate or lifetime parameters to RespFrame. ~1350 BulkString usages need updating.

title: XREADGROUP returns generic ERR instead of WRONGTYPE/NOGROUP when stream ID is also malformed (validation order)
description: fr's xreadgroup parsed the stream ID BEFORE the per-key type/group check, so 'XREADGROUP GROUP g STREAMS <key> <bad-id>' returned 'ERR Invalid stream ID' where redis 7.2.4 returns WRONGTYPE (key is non-stream) or NOGROUP (missing key/group). Upstream t_stream.c::xreadCommand does lookupKeyRead (counting the keyspace hit/miss) + checkType + group lookup BEFORE parsing the ID. Also fixed the matching keyspace_hits/misses under-count on those error paths. Found via new seeded stream differential fuzzer (15 diffs/600 cmds). Fix in fr-command xreadgroup.
close_reason: Fixed in adfe15af1 (fr-command xreadgroup): on stream-ID parse failure, route through key_type+group check to surface WRONGTYPE/NOGROUP in upstream precedence (valid-ID path untouched -> no double keyspace count). Fuzzer 15->0 diffs/600; keyspace hit/miss deltas match redis on all error paths. unit test xrgord; 1134 tests pass.

title: Lua: arg-type errors from string.format etc lack 'user_script:N:' line prefix
description: Lua 5.1 wraps arg-type errors from C-side check_arg/luaL_argerror with the standard '<source>:<line>: ' prefix before the error message. fr's Lua interpreter emits 'bad argument #2 to format (string expected, got nil)' but vendored emits 'user_script:1: bad argument #2 to format (string expected, got nil)'. This affects every C-side error in stdlib functions (format, sub, etc). Fix is generic: wrap Err returns from RustFunction match cases with the current script source line. Low priority because it's cosmetic and doesn't break parsing; filing for tracking.
close_reason: Lua string.format arg-type errors now carry the standard 'user_script:1: ' prefix matching upstream Lua 5.1's luaL_argerror behavior (which wraps every C-side argument error with '<source>:<line>: '). Inlined the prefix at the format() %s error site in fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:5497. Live differential probe: string.format('%s', nil) and string.format('%s', {}) error messages now byte-exact with vendored. Tests: fr-command 910 pass. Note: this is a narrow fix for the format %s site; other future stdlib type errors would need the same prefix if/when they get tightened.

title: SRANDMEMBER should bump LFU access frequency like Redis
description: Upstream Redis 7.2.4 t_set.c::srandmemberCommand and srandmemberWithCountCommand route through lookupKeyReadOrReply, and db.c::lookupKey updates LFU metadata under LFU maxmemory policies. fr-store Store::srandmember and Store::srandmember_count currently touch LRU metadata but do not bump lfu_freq, so OBJECT FREQ after SRANDMEMBER diverges for set keys.
close_reason: Completed fr-store SRANDMEMBER LFU parity fix

title: [perf] Pass66 profile output/syscall batching or metadata amortization after GET store rejections
description: After pass65 rejected hot-key store certificates/sidecars, fresh clean GET P16 profile points away from store lookup and toward output/syscall, runtime metadata refresh, parser, and GET frame cost. Re-profile current main after NobleRiver's q0qym APPEND fast-path lands, then test exactly one non-store primitive: output/syscall batching, reply slab/writev, or runtime metadata amortization. Do not touch NobleRiver's q0qym runtime/server surface while it is active. Requires rch baseline, golden SHA/isomorphism proof, same-worker rebench, Score>=2.0.
close_reason: Completed pass67 with profile-backed metadata amortization rejection. Parent GET P16/300k 497.8 ms +/- 16.2 ms; candidate aggregate 502.1 ms +/- 16.7 ms. Paired GET P16/1M candidate only 1.01x +/- 0.03, below Score>=2.0. Behavior proof baseline/candidate tracking INFO transcript SHA-256 d4a9346460558d9cf4137cee8d76445677fb9de87b74475f5008600e46c8f17e, parity true. Production code reverted; no source lever kept. Follow-up filed as frankenredis-jdiiv for deeper output-buffer/syscall batching.

title: Fix Lua table nil-value semantics and other evaluator edge cases
close_reason: done

title: COMMAND INFO READONLY missing flags + EVALSHA/EVALSHA_RO missing keyspec
description: (1) READONLY at fr-command/src/lib.rs:13620 has empty flags string but upstream declares CMD_FAST|CMD_LOADING|CMD_STALE (verified in commands.def: readonlyCommand,1,CMD_FAST|CMD_LOADING|CMD_STALE,...). (2) EVALSHA and EVALSHA_RO emit empty keyspec arrays. Upstream commands.def: EVALSHA_Keyspecs declares RW|ACCESS|UPDATE + KSPEC_BS_INDEX(2) + KSPEC_FK_KEYNUM{0,1,1}; EVALSHA_RO_Keyspecs is RO|ACCESS + same begin_search/find_keys. fr's command_info_keynum_keyspec at lib.rs:14947 only fires for eval/eval_ro/fcall/fcall_ro -- need to extend the match arm to evalsha/evalsha_ro plus add them to the keyspec_override notes lookup. Acceptance: COMMAND INFO READONLY/EVALSHA/EVALSHA_RO byte-exact with vendored.
close_reason: READONLY now emits 'loading stale fast' flags matching upstream commands.def (CMD_FAST|CMD_LOADING|CMD_STALE; fr's COMMAND_TABLE entry at lib.rs:13620 had empty flags). EVALSHA and EVALSHA_RO now emit a single keyspec each, matching upstream's EVALSHA_Keyspecs (RW|ACCESS|UPDATE + KSPEC_BS_INDEX(2) + KSPEC_FK_KEYNUM{0,1,1}) and EVALSHA_RO_Keyspecs (RO|ACCESS + same begin_search/find_keys). Implementation: (1) extended the eval/fcall match arm in command_info_keyspec_override (lib.rs:14808) to include evalsha/evalsha_ro, (2) extended command_info_keynum_keyspec's whitelist (lib.rs:14947) to fire for evalsha/evalsha_ro, (3) refactored that function to omit the 'notes' field for evalsha/evalsha_ro since upstream commands.def has NULL notes for the hash-id variants but a 'We cannot tell...' string for the source-string variants. Updated the expected-flags test case for READONLY to reflect the now-correct emission. Live differential probe: all 7 commands (READONLY, EVALSHA, EVALSHA_RO, EVAL, EVAL_RO, FCALL, FCALL_RO) byte-exact with vendored 7.2.4. Tests: fr-command 910 pass.

title: Add metamorphic tests for Set operations (SINTER, SUNION, SDIFF) in fr-store
Implement stateful CLIENT TRACKING / TRACKINGINFO parity
close_reason: Completed

title: INFO cluster section hardcodes cluster_enabled:0; should reflect store.cluster_enabled
description: Upstream server.c::genRedisInfoString emits 'cluster_enabled:%d' from server.cluster_enabled. fr's info_cmd at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:12763 hardcodes 'cluster_enabled:0' so even when store.cluster_enabled is true (e.g. CLUSTER ADDSLOTS has been called), the INFO 'cluster' section still reports 0 — diverging from upstream. Replace the hardcoded literal with a conditional that reads store.cluster_enabled.
close_reason: fixed

title: ZPOPMIN <wrongtype-key> 0 returns empty instead of WRONGTYPE (parallel to cf9z1)
description:
fr's ZPOPMIN with count=0 returns an empty array even when the key is a wrong type, while vendored Redis 7.2.4 returns WRONGTYPE. Same bug class as frankenredis-cf9z1 (SPOP count=0 wrongtype).

Root cause: fr-command/src/lib.rs:4618-4620 in zpopmin() has an early return that bypasses store.zpopmin_count when count==0:

  let count = parse_zpop_count(&argv[2])?;
  if count == 0 {
      return Ok(RespFrame::Array(Some(vec![])));   // <-- no type check
  }
  let pairs = store.zpopmin_count(&argv[1], count, now_ms)?;

store.zpopmin_count() DOES validate the type (returns WrongType for non-SortedSet), but the early-return skips that path. Upstream t_zset.c::genericZpopCommand validates type before serving any reply.

ZPOPMAX is correct: zpopmax() has no such early-return and always routes through zpopmax_count (which does the type check).

Probe sweep #64 (probe127.sh):
  vendored SET strkey hello -> OK
  vendored ZPOPMIN strkey 0 -> WRONGTYPE Operation against a key holding the wrong kind of value
  fr        ZPOPMIN strkey 0 -> (empty array)
  vendored ZPOPMAX strkey 0 -> WRONGTYPE (already correct in fr)
  vendored LPOP strkey 0    -> WRONGTYPE (already correct in fr)
  vendored RPOP strkey 0    -> WRONGTYPE (already correct in fr)

Fix plan:
- Remove the count==0 early-return in zpopmin(). zpopmin_count's existing for-loop iterates 0..count which is a no-op when count==0, and the type check at lib.rs:7303 fires first regardless.
- Add a regression test pinning ZPOPMIN wrongtype-key 0 -> WRONGTYPE.
close_reason: Fixed in iteration 97: removed the count==0 early-return in zpopmin() that bypassed store.zpopmin_count's type check. zpopmin_count's existing for-loop is a no-op at count==0, so removing the short-circuit is functionally equivalent for sorted-set keys and now surfaces WRONGTYPE on wrongtype keys (matching ZPOPMAX, LPOP, RPOP, SPOP-count-zero behavior). Live-diff: ZPOPMIN strkey 0 -> WRONGTYPE (matches vendored), ZPOPMIN nokey 0 -> empty (matches vendored). Regression test zpop_count_zero_on_wrongtype_key_reports_wrongtype_5w1d3 pins both ZPOPMIN and ZPOPMAX wrongtype/nokey count=0 cases. Full 1022-test suite passes.

title: Keyspace: *STORE commands (SINTERSTORE/SUNIONSTORE/SDIFFSTORE/ZINTER/ZUNION/ZDIFF/ZRANGESTORE/SORT/GEO) fire on all keys with shared names; should be dest-only + per-command name + del-on-empty-result
notes:
EXACT spec (verified vs 7.2.4). All *STORE fire ONE event on the DESTINATION only (like COPY's B7 fix), with a per-command name; on EMPTY result they fire 'del' on dest instead (dest was deleted — reaching the dirty-gated block means dest existed). fr currently fires the shared name on ALL cmd_keys.
Per-command names: SINTERSTORE->sinterstore, SUNIONSTORE->sunionstore, SDIFFSTORE->sdiffstore, ZINTERSTORE->zinterstore, ZUNIONSTORE->zunionstore, ZDIFFSTORE->zdiffstore, ZRANGESTORE->zrangestore, SORT...STORE->sortstore, GEOSEARCHSTORE->geosearchstore, GEORADIUS/GEORADIUSBYMEMBER...STORE->georadiusstore.
fr bug 1: command_to_keyspace_event maps SINTERSTORE/SUNIONSTORE/SDIFFSTORE->'sinterstore' (shared) and ZUNIONSTORE/ZINTERSTORE/ZDIFFSTORE/ZRANGESTORE->'zrangestore' (shared). Fix to per-command.
fr bug 2: generic loop fires on all cmd_keys; need dest-only + del-on-empty branch.
DEST POSITION: dest=cmd_keys[0]=argv[1] for SINTER/SUNION/SDIFFSTORE, ZINTER/ZUNION/ZDIFFSTORE, ZRANGESTORE, GEOSEARCHSTORE (clean subset). For SORT key ... STORE dest and GEORADIUS key ... STORE dest, the dest is the arg after STORE (NOT argv[1]) — handle via command_key_indexes or a store-key scan. IMPL: add an is_store_command branch: dest = key_is_present? fire per-name on dest : fire 'del' on dest. Reuse the COPY-style single-key emission. NOTE bhd3u (ZRANGESTORE dirty) already fixed separately; now its event fires but with wrong shape.
close_reason: Fixed: *STORE commands now fire ONE per-command-named keyevent on the DESTINATION only (or 'del' on dest when the result is empty and dest was deleted), not the shared name on all keys. Per-command names fixed in command_to_keyspace_event; added is_store_command + store_command_dest_is_last (dest=cmd_keys.first() for set/zset/geosearch-store, .last() for SORT/GEORADIUS) + a dedicated emission branch. Byte-exact vs vendored 7.2.4 across all 10 *STORE commands incl. empty-result del and empty-no-dest (no event).

title: TYPE/OBJECT ENCODING update keyspace stats/LRU
description: Store::value_type/object_encoding bypassed record_keyspace_lookup and did not touch LRU, so TYPE/OBJECT ENCODING calls failed to update keyspace hit/miss stats and last_access_ms. Route through keyspace lookup and touch entry; add tests.
close_reason: TYPE/object encoding now record keyspace stats + LRU touch

title: XCLAIM FORCE-create seeds PEL delivery_count at 0 not 1 (streamCreateNACK)
description: fr Store::xclaim FORCE-create path inserted a new StreamPendingEntry with deliveries:0, then the shared claim path applies delivery_count++ (default) → 1. Upstream xclaimCommand FORCE-creates via streamCreateNACK(NULL) which sets delivery_count=1, THEN the shared path runs (++ default / =retrycount / no-op JUSTID). So a default XCLAIM FORCE that creates the entry must land at 2 (fr gave 1) and JUSTID at 1 (fr gave 1 — coincidentally ok via no-op but from wrong base); RETRYCOUNT overrides so unaffected. Found via scripts/stream_xinfo_differ.py (XCLAIM/XAUTOCLAIM ops added this session, seed 1234 iter 2155). Verified vs vendored redis 7.2.4: default FORCE-create deliv 2, JUSTID 1.
notes: Fixed: FORCE-create initial deliveries 0 -> 1 (matches streamCreateNACK). default FORCE-create now 2, JUSTID 1, RETRYCOUNT n (overridden). Extended stream_xinfo_differ.py with XCLAIM (default/JUSTID/RETRYCOUNT/FORCE) + XAUTOCLAIM; byte-exact after fix: seed 1234 x4000 + seeds 3/11/77/555/8888 x2500, zero divergence.

title: Expand live Redis parity coverage for stable PFDEBUG surface
description: Add a stable core_pfdebug live differential subset for deterministic PFSELFTEST/PFDEBUG admin behavior against vendored Redis, covering selftest, missing-key errors, wrong-arity handling, case-insensitive dispatch, and a narrow existing-HLL inspection slice without overlapping the FUNCTION lane.
close_reason: Expanded stable live Redis PFDEBUG coverage in standalone mode

title: XTRIM LIMIT 0 without strategy emits wrong wording vs vendored 7.2.4
description:
fr's XTRIM emits `ERR syntax error, LIMIT cannot be used without specifying a trimming strategy` for `XTRIM xs LIMIT 0`, but vendored Redis 7.2.4 emits `ERR syntax error, XTRIM must be called with a trimming strategy`.

Root cause is the same C-truthy-falsy quirk that drove frankenredis-kgxl7 (XADD case) plus an additional XTRIM-specific check.

Upstream t_stream.c::streamParseAddOrTrimArgsOrReply parses LIMIT eagerly but only stores the value — the "without strategy" diagnostic fires in a SECOND post-parse pass at line 998:

  if (args->limit && args->trim_strategy == TRIM_STRATEGY_NONE) {
      addReplyError(c, "syntax error, LIMIT cannot be used without specifying a trimming strategy");
      return -1;
  }
  if (!xadd && args->trim_strategy == TRIM_STRATEGY_NONE) {
      addReplyError(c, "syntax error, XTRIM must be called with a trimming strategy");
      return -1;
  }

For `XTRIM xs LIMIT 0`: args->limit=0 (falsy in C) so line 998 skips. Line 1003 (!xadd && no_strategy) fires and emits the XTRIM-specific "must be called with a trimming strategy" wording.

fr's xtrim() in lib.rs:6310-6316 eagerly errors at LIMIT-parse time when strategy==None — regardless of the LIMIT value — and always reports the "without specifying a trimming strategy" wording. This breaks the LIMIT 0 case and also doesn't surface the XTRIM-specific wording.

Probe sweep #60 (probe120.sh):
  vendored XTRIM xs LIMIT 0       -> ERR syntax error, XTRIM must be called with a trimming strategy
  fr        XTRIM xs LIMIT 0       -> ERR syntax error, LIMIT cannot be used without specifying a trimming strategy

Other cases to preserve:
  vendored XTRIM xs LIMIT 5       -> ERR syntax error, LIMIT cannot be used without specifying a trimming strategy  (also fr-correct)
  vendored XTRIM xs               -> ERR wrong number of arguments  (fr-correct)
  vendored XTRIM xs MAXLEN 5 LIMIT 0 -> ERR syntax error, LIMIT cannot be used without the special ~ option  (fr-correct)

Fix plan:
- In xtrim() at lib.rs:6310, drop the eager 'strategy == None' rejection. Parse LIMIT, store value, set limit_given.
- After the parse loop, run the post-parse checks in vendored order:
  1. limit_given && limit_value > 0 && strategy==None -> 'LIMIT cannot be used without specifying a trimming strategy'
  2. strategy==None -> 'XTRIM must be called with a trimming strategy'
  3. limit_given && !approx -> 'LIMIT cannot be used without the special ~ option'
- Add regression test pinning all four combinations.
close_reason: Fixed in iteration 93: refactored XTRIM's LIMIT-handling to defer the 'without strategy' diagnostics until after the parse loop, mirroring vendored t_stream.c's structure. The first check now gates on limit_value > 0 (mirroring the C truthy-falsy quirk for args->limit), then the XTRIM-specific 'must be called with a trimming strategy' fires, then the 'without ~' check. Live-diff: all 4 cases (LIMIT 0 no strat -> 'XTRIM must be called', LIMIT 5 no strat -> 'without strategy', MAXLEN 5 LIMIT 0 -> 'without ~', no args -> wrong arity) match vendored. Regression test xtrim_limit_zero_without_strategy_returns_xtrim_specific_wording_5zr5y pins all three diagnostic combos. Full 1020-test suite passes.

title: redis parity: CLIENT REPLY should reject script context
description: Vendored Redis marks CLIENT REPLY as NOSCRIPT in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/commands/client-reply.json, but the local CLIENT REPLY branch at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:11272 validates ON/OFF/SKIP and returns OK without any script_nesting_level guard. Add Redis-compatible NOSCRIPT rejection after arity/mode validation and cover redis.call("CLIENT","REPLY",...) from Lua.
close_reason: Completed

title: fr-command: CLIENT TRACKING checks OPTIN/OPTOUT mutual-exclusion before BCAST conflict, reversing upstream order
description:
Upstream networking.c::clientCommand checks the BCAST+(OPTIN|OPTOUT) conflict BEFORE the mutual-exclusion of OPTIN/OPTOUT, so when all three are specified the user-visible error is 'OPTIN and OPTOUT are not compatible with BCAST'.

fr-command's parse_tracking_options checked optin&&optout first and returned 'You can't specify both OPTIN mode and OPTOUT mode' when BCAST was also set.

Differential probe vs vendored redis 7.2.4 (CLIENT TRACKING ON BCAST OPTIN OPTOUT):
  fr:    ERR You can't specify both OPTIN mode and OPTOUT mode
  redis: ERR OPTIN and OPTOUT are not compatible with BCAST

Acceptance: byte-match upstream for the all-three-set case while preserving the existing isolated-conflict cases.

title: Wire SENTINEL command to fr-sentinel dispatcher
description: Mock-code-finder sweep found fr-command advertises SENTINEL but sentinel_cmd always returns UnknownCommand even though fr-sentinel has a stateful dispatcher. Wire fr-sentinel state through Store and dispatch SENTINEL subcommands, then add coverage for state persistence.
close_reason: Completed: wired SENTINEL through fr-sentinel dispatcher with Store-owned state and arity coverage.

title: OBJECT ENCODING of a near-8KB list reports listpack vs redis quicklist after DEBUG RELOAD
description: REPRO (found by scripts/list_chunk_seal_gate.py 2026-06-16): a list whose single-listpack size lands JUST OVER list-max-listpack-size (-2 = 8192 B) — e.g. ~590 short string elems (~9.4 KB packed) — is 'quicklist' LIVE (correct, both fr+redis), but after DEBUG RELOAD fr reports OBJECT ENCODING 'listpack' while redis reports 'quicklist'. Content is byte-exact (LLEN/LRANGE/DEBUG DIGEST-VALUE all match post-RELOAD); only the encoding LABEL diverges. PRE-EXISTING: reproduces on the old pre-seal binary fr_probe_v2 (commit 46aadf2f8), so NOT introduced by the 99fwc seal (8c2421045) or stream grouping. NOT a boundary artifact: a clearly-large list (300 x 50B = 15KB) loads as quicklist correctly on both. ROOT CAUSE: fr's RDB list SAVE consolidates the near-8KB list into a SINGLE listpack node (so LOAD rebuilds a single-chunk list -> 'listpack') where redis's save splits at the 8KB list-max-listpack-size limit into multiple quicklist nodes. Same node-boundary family as frankenredis-99fwc / s36di (ChunkedList node-split predicate vs redis SIZE_SAFETY_LIMIT). FIX likely in the RDB list save/load path: enforce list-max-listpack-size when deciding single-listpack vs quicklist on reconstruction. fr-store list domain (CoralOx active — coordinate).
close_reason: Fixed by RDB SAVE packed quicklist-node reuse in bbc6daeaa; verified near-8KB list live and post-DEBUG-RELOAD OBJECT ENCODING quicklist on redis+fr, LLEN :590, LRANGE sha256 64b9e9b9cce352b806721e849b26c53cee2b8a492f2818ecdbc7beac2426898e.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: CoralOx is ACTIVELY FIXING this (uncommitted WIP in packed_set.rs + fr-persist as of 2026-06-16): quicklist_packed_node_blobs(fill) + encode_quicklist2_packed_payload split the RDB list save into multiple quicklist nodes by list-max-listpack-size, so a near-8KB list reloads as quicklist (matching redis) instead of one oversized listpack. DO NOT duplicate — leave to CoralOx. title: Direct CLIENT KILL LADDR dispatcher ignores reported local address
description: The runtime CLIENT KILL path now matches LADDR against the same 127.0.0.1:<server_port> address reported by CLIENT INFO/LIST, but fr-command's direct dispatch_argv CLIENT KILL handler still short-circuits any LADDR filter to Integer(0). This leaves command-layer tests and non-runtime dispatch paths inconsistent with the advertised laddr surface. Parse LADDR like ADDR, include it in the current-client predicate, and pin SKIPME/no-match behavior.

title: Remove hide-user-data-from-log (Redis 7.4 config not in 7.2.4 baseline)
description:
fr exposes `hide-user-data-from-log` in CONFIG GET output, but vendored Redis 7.2.4 doesn't have this config (it was added in Redis 7.4). The extra field breaks CONFIG GET pattern parity for any pattern matching `*log*` (and similar) and surfaces in monitoring tools that compare config dumps.

Reproduction:
  CONFIG GET '*memory*' '*log*' (both servers):
    vendored: 21 unique keys (no hide-user-data-from-log)
    fr:       22 unique keys (includes hide-user-data-from-log)

Config registration site: fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:521.

Fix plan:
- Remove the `("hide-user-data-from-log", "no")` entry from the default config table at fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:521. fr targets 7.2.4 wire parity per the project memory.
- Verify CONFIG GET '*log*' returns the same key set as vendored.

Note: INFO commandstats' line count also differs but the difference is "the two servers have processed different commands" — not a shape divergence (the cmdstat_ prefix encodes the command name; missing entries just mean that command hasn't been called on that server). No fix needed there.

Probe sweep #75 (probe151.sh).
close_reason: completed

title: [perf] Borrowed fast path for OBJECT IDLETIME/FREQ
description: Split from frankenredis-8ap9a after plain ZRANGE was shipped. Profile-backed deep-pipeline row: OBJECT IDLETIME/FREQ 0.298x vs Redis, missing borrowed OBJECT subcommand path. Scope to OBJECT IDLETIME key and OBJECT FREQ key only; preserve LFU/LRU config-dependent replies, no-side-effect read semantics, subcommand arity/case behavior, and golden transcript SHA proof.

Commit evidence: IDLETIME 0.298x -> 0.95x and FREQ ~0.30x -> 0.90x vs Redis deep-pipeline, golden sha256 ac90297e9f326fa70f43290cc066b41dfed35f2619cf3d5e37ba161af26bca21, focused isomorphism tests.

title: perf(fr-store): PFCOUNT multi-key needs register-resident HLL primitive after direct-merge rejection
description: Pass142 on frankenredis-fbj62 found the single-key PFCOUNT cache is already implemented in current main (Store::pfcount uses hll_cache_read/write; store test pfcount_single_key_caches_and_dirties_like_upstream pins it). Current baseline harness: single-cached 13977.6 ops/s, multi-key 8228.6 ops/s on two 50k HLLs. Tried one safe-Rust direct merge of encoded HLL payloads into the temporary merged register array, avoiding per-source Vec allocation and preserving tolerant sparse corruption behavior. Paired hyperfine rejected it: multi baseline 2.229s +/- 0.017s, candidate 2.280s +/- 0.008s; inner loop 9499.2 -> 9269.5 ops/s. Behavior SHA matched: 79613588c2e15445010c474022011dc73c1fe207e2ee1c8300b15dc1eb544e55. Next primitive should be algorithmically different: store/cache a register-resident HLL representation or merged-register workspace that removes repeated dense decode without regressing PFADD/PFMERGE invalidation, RDB/AOF bytes, OBJECT ENCODING raw, and Redis sparse corruption semantics. Artifacts: artifacts/optimization/frankenredis-fbj62/.
close_reason: Completed: register-resident HLL sidecar cache for multi-key PFCOUNT. Baseline multi 2.253468483945s / 9733.674 ops/s; candidate 1.871917184785s / 11409.413 ops/s; speedup 1.203829x hyperfine, 1.172159x internal; golden SHA c619bac67d64829d28058a8f77f6b2ef1a46167f5cca849fdda169b10c4a3dcf unchanged; Score 2.10.

title: [perf] Incremental memory-cache delta for list pushes
description: Post-cms7p profile on hz1 at f818387cc (LPUSH P16/C50/n3M, 3304 perf samples) shifted userspace cost to Store::lpush<&[u8]> 7.27% self and fr_store::estimate_value_memory_usage_bytes 5.43% self. The memory estimator is hit via record_ops_sec_sample -> estimate_memory_usage_bytes, which recomputes the keyspace every ~64 mutations; LPUSH/RPUSH mutate lists every command, so sustained list pushes repeatedly rescan many entries even though list value memory can be updated in O(1) from pre/post listpack/quicklist byte estimates. One lever only: when cached_memory_usage_bytes is initialized and cached_memory_usage_dirty exactly matches the pre-mutation counter, update the cached memory total by the LPUSH/RPUSH entry/list delta and advance cached_memory_usage_dirty instead of waiting for a full recompute. Fall back to existing recompute behavior whenever the cache is stale, key expires, wrongtype, encoding transition cannot be proven, or removal/new-key accounting is not exact. Preserve MEMORY USAGE/INFO memory semantics, eviction pressure accounting, dirty counters, list order, integer replies, lazy-expiry propagation, LFU/touch behavior, RNG/tie-breaking/FP. Baseline current main first with rch-built fr-server/fr-bench; prove raw golden SHA unchanged; keep only if Score>=2.0. Do not combine with parser/output changes.
close_reason: Rejected: incremental LPUSH/RPUSH memory-cache delta did not clear Score>=2.0. Golden raw output SHA matched (8169647db39fbfe1951fc2291fd7332a77911becbf0427640146b796adb343e1), but candidate independent n1M was slower (1.408s vs 1.353s), paired n1M was order-sensitive, and paired n5M only reached 1.04x mean with overlapping variance plus last-run throughput regression (534694 -> 515683 ops/sec). Score 0.6. Source hunk removed; evidence in artifacts/optimization/frankenredis-63c8x/. Next route: re-profile and attack list payload allocation/layout, listpack entry construction, or memory sampling architecture instead of per-command cache deltas.

title: SET encoding downgrades hashtable->listpack across DEBUG RELOAD / RDB load (hash/zset survive)
description:
REPRO (verified, byte-exact vs redis 7.2.4 oracle): with set-max-listpack-entries=4 and set-max-intset-entries=4, SADD 8 generic members -> OBJECT ENCODING 'hashtable' (correct). After DEBUG RELOAD (or any whole-DB RDB load): fr reports 'listpack', redis keeps 'hashtable'. SCARD stays 8; adding a 9th member re-triggers 'hashtable'. HASH (8 fields) and ZSET (8 members) survive correctly under the same config — ONLY sets regress. DUMP+RESTORE of the same set is CORRECT (hashtable) — the bug is specific to the whole-DB RDB save/load path, not fr-store's dump_key/decode_value.

ROOT CAUSE: the whole-DB RDB load (fr-runtime load_rdb ~4423) rebuilds each value into a fresh replacement Store via store.sadd(&key, members) (fr-runtime ~20370), and preserve_store_load_context (which restores the runtime encoding configs incl set_max_listpack_entries=4) runs AFTER the rebuild. So the sadd runs under the fresh store's COMPILED-DEFAULT config (set_max_listpack_entries=128). Building the 8-member set: the first non-int member converts the (default-intset) set to listpack and sets the sticky entry.force_set_listpack_encoding=true; under config=128 the 8-member set never crosses the listpack->hashtable threshold (8<128), so force_set_hashtable is never set. The encoding reporter's `else if force_set_listpack_encoding => "listpack"` branch is UNCONDITIONAL, so even after preserve restores config=4, OBJECT ENCODING short-circuits to listpack and ignores that 8>4. HASH/ZSET have no such sticky-listpack flag (their encoding is computed at report time), so they self-correct once preserve restores config=4.

Compounding: the whole-DB save also uses CompactRdbThresholds::default() (set_max_listpack_entries=128) via encode_rdb_with_functions instead of the runtime config, so the 8-member set is serialized as RDB_TYPE_SET_LISTPACK (8<=128) where redis (config=4) saves plain RDB_TYPE_SET; a plain-SET load forces hashtable and would be correct.

FIX OPTIONS (fr-runtime, peer-owned hot file): (A) apply preserve_store_load_context's encoding configs to the replacement store BEFORE rebuilding values via sadd/hset/zadd, so the rebuild uses the runtime config; OR (B) make the encoding reporter's force_set_listpack branch re-evaluate listpack-vs-hashtable by current size (set_fits_listpack) instead of unconditional listpack — but a naive version regressed runtime cases, needs care re: value-length and the all-int sticky case; OR (C) thread the runtime config thresholds into the whole-DB encode_rdb call so sets serialize by the live config. NOTE: server state during this investigation was messy (asymmetric configs/stale binaries) — there were also apparent RUNTIME-only set-encoding divergences in a config-change-mid-life fuzz that need clean re-verification (may be artifact). Verify on FRESH symmetric servers. Probe: scripts metamorphic DEBUG RELOAD (needs --enable-debug-command on both).
notes:
INVESTIGATION UPDATE (CobaltLynx): Fix option (B) [make the encoding reporter's `else if force_set_listpack_encoding` branch re-evaluate set_fits_listpack instead of unconditional "listpack"] is UNSAFE — it REGRESSES SUNIONSTORE: scripts/store_encoding_differ.py fails "SUNIONSTORE mixed" (oracle hashtable, fr listpack) with (B), passes without. The force_set_listpack flag is load-bearing for store-op result encodings, so a naive reporter re-eval breaks them. Reverted. 

Verified clean: RUNTIME set encoding is byte-exact vs redis (the apparent runtime divergences in my first fuzz were a HARNESS BUG — a shared RNG advanced between the oracle-build and fr-build loops, so the two servers got DIFFERENT members; with identical commands to both, runtime div=0). With a correct fuzz, ONLY the RELOAD path diverges (and 1 residual value-length+reload edge even with B). DUMP/RESTORE + encoding_config_boundary are byte-exact.

RECOMMENDED FIX: option (A) — in fr-runtime load_rdb, restore the runtime encoding configs (preserve_store_load_context's set_max_* fields) to the replacement store BEFORE rebuilding values via sadd/hset/zadd, so the set rebuild's intset->listpack->hashtable decisions use the live config (config=4), not the fresh-store compiled default (128). Hash/zset already self-correct because they have no sticky-listpack flag; the SET needs the rebuild to happen under the right config so it sets force_set_hashtable (not a stuck force_set_listpack). VERIFY with scripts metamorphic DEBUG RELOAD (needs --enable-debug-command on BOTH servers) + store_encoding_differ (must still pass SUNIONSTORE) + correct-fuzz identical-data-both-servers.
close_reason: FIXED: copy_encoding_thresholds(replacement, original) now applies the live encoding configs (hash/list/set/zset max_* + set_max_intset + hll_sparse) to the fresh replacement Store BEFORE apply_rdb_entries_to_store at all 4 RDB-rebuild sites (load_rdb, DEBUG RELOAD, replica full-sync x2), so the sadd/hset/zadd/rpush replay makes encoding decisions under the runtime config instead of the fresh store's compiled defaults. An 8-member set under set-max-listpack-entries=4 now reloads as hashtable (was listpack). Deliberately excludes notify_keyspace_events (would wrongly fire events during replay). Verified: bug repro fixed; correct-fuzz (all types, identical data) runtime div=0, reload 1/400 (residual = a separate narrower value-length+mixed-members+sort-order edge that was already broken pre-fix, NOT a regression); 585 fr-store + 506 fr-runtime tests pass; store_encoding_differ SUNIONSTORE PASSES (unlike the rejected reporter-reeval approach); dump_restore + encoding_config_boundary byte-exact. Regression test load_rdb_set_encoding_honors_live_config_thresholds. Residual value-length edge tracked separately if it recurs.

title: fix(set/zset): SDIFF/SDIFFSTORE/ZDIFF/ZDIFFSTORE skip type-check of later sources when first is missing
description: Found via randomized differential fuzz vs vendored 7.2.4. When the FIRST source key is missing/empty, fr short-circuits the diff to empty WITHOUT type-checking the remaining source keys, so a wrong-type later source (e.g. SDIFFSTORE dst nomiss listkey) returned 0/empty instead of WRONGTYPE. Redis lookupKeyRead+checkType's EVERY source first. (SINTER/SUNION/ZINTER/ZUNION were already correct.) Fix: Store::sdiff missing-first-key arm starts with an empty set and falls into the existing type-checking loop instead of early-returning; ZDIFF/ZDIFFSTORE (fr-command) validate all sources up front via new no-stat Store::ensure_zset_or_set_source. Verified vs oracle + 10-seed fuzz (remaining 'roots' were fuzzer set-member-order digest artifacts, sets equal) + regression test + 1101/535 lib tests.
close_reason: Fixed in 10049a0d4. SDIFF missing-first-key arm starts empty + falls into the type-checking loop; ZDIFF/ZDIFFSTORE validate all sources via new no-stat Store::ensure_zset_or_set_source. Verified vs oracle + fuzz + regression test + 1101/535 lib tests.

Source key RW/access/delete unchanged. Regression assertion added to command_getkeysandflags_distinguishes_delete_and_rename_roles. Verified 0 divergences RESP2+RESP3 vs vendored 7.2.4.

title: ZADD INCR/ZINCRBY of -0 onto a new member must reply -0 (sign-preserved)
description: Found via ZADD-flag differential fuzz vs redis 7.2.4. ZADD ... INCR ... -0 <newmember> and ZINCRBY key -0 <newmember> reply '-0' in redis (increment used verbatim for a new member) but fr replied '0'. Root cause fr-store zincrby_with_options: new_score = old_score.unwrap_or(0.0)+delta; 0.0+(-0.0)=+0.0 collapses the sign. Redis sets a new member's score to the increment directly. Stored score stays +0 (ZSCORE '0') via insert_with_limits canonicalization. Fix: new member -> score=delta verbatim.
close_reason: Fixed 8959a7945: zincrby_with_options new-member score = delta verbatim (not 0.0+delta). ZADD INCR/ZINCRBY -0 onto new member now replies -0 byte-exact vs redis 7.2.4; stored score stays +0 (ZSCORE 0). ZADD-flag fuzz 0 diffs/22k ops. Regression test added.

title: SENTINEL MASTER replies should always include runid field
description: Redis 7.2.4 sentinel.c::addReplySentinelRedisInstance always emits the runid key for Sentinel instance maps, with an empty string when ri->runid is NULL. fr-sentinel instance_to_info_array only appends runid when instance.runid is Some, so new monitored masters omit the field entirely in SENTINEL MASTER/MASTERS replies. title: Add concurrent WATCH/MULTI/EXEC transaction isolation integration test
description:
# Transaction Isolation Under Concurrency

## What
Test that WATCH + MULTI/EXEC provides proper optimistic locking when multiple clients race on the same key. This is a core Redis guarantee.

## Why
The existing tcp_e2e tests only test single-client transactions. Real deployments depend on WATCH to detect conflicts. If this is broken, data corruption occurs silently.

## Implementation
1. Add to crates/fr-server/tests/concurrent_e2e.rs
2. Test scenario:
   - Client A: WATCH key, GET key → "0"
   - Client B: SET key "1" (modifies the watched key)  
   - Client A: MULTI, SET key "2", EXEC
   - Client A EXEC should return nil (transaction aborted due to WATCH)
   - GET key should be "1" (Client B value, not Client A)
3. Repeat 100 times with random timing to stress-test

## Logging
- Log each client operation with timestamp and thread ID
- Log EXEC result (success vs abort) for each iteration
- Summary: "N/100 transactions correctly aborted"

## Files
- crates/fr-server/tests/concurrent_e2e.rs
close_reason: Added 2 WATCH/MULTI/EXEC E2E tests: tcp_watch_exec_aborts_on_concurrent_modification (proves WATCH detects cross-client modification and aborts EXEC with null array) and tcp_watch_exec_succeeds_without_concurrent_modification (proves clean EXEC returns results). Both pass.

title: fr-command/tests: no metamorphic invariants pin MOVE cross-DB semantics — TTL preservation, round-trip identity, target collision, and arity edge cases are all unverified
description:
/testing-metamorphic gap audit:

  find crates/fr-command/tests -name 'metamorphic*' → empty
  grep -rn 'mr_move\|move_cross_db\|MR.*MOVE' crates/ → only the 3 dispatch_argv tests just added in w9yzb

w9yzb fixed fr-command::move_cmd to actually transfer keys cross-DB but only added 3 unit tests (same-db rejection, simple cross-db transfer, out-of-range wording). The harder invariants are unverified:

- MR1 (identity): MOVE k 0→1 then MOVE k 1→0 must restore the original (k, v) pair in db 0 with the SAME absolute TTL deadline (not reset to no-TTL).
- MR2 (TTL preservation): MOVE k from a db where it has expiry T must leave k with expiry T in the target db.
- MR3 (target collision): SET k in both source AND target db. MOVE k → must return 0 and leave both unchanged (no overwrite).
- MR4 (missing source): MOVE missing k 1 → returns 0; no key created in target.
- MR5 (cross-encoding): MOVE on a hash-encoded key must preserve hashtable/listpack encoding in target.

Without these, a future refactor that changes Store::copy semantics (e.g. drops TTL during copy, or changes overwrite policy) silently breaks MOVE without a regression test catching it.

Fix: add crates/fr-command/tests/metamorphic_move.rs with 5+ invariants exercising dispatch_argv MOVE.
close_reason: Added crates/fr-command/tests/metamorphic_move.rs with 7 invariants: round-trip identity preserves TTL, no-TTL key stays no-TTL across MOVE, target collision is non-destructive (matches upstream db.c), missing source returns 0 with no spurious target row, hash-encoded value preserves type after MOVE, same-db rejection emits the upstream error wording without mutation, out-of-range db likewise. MR8 (SELECT-then-MOVE composed) intentionally deferred — surfaced a SELECT stub filed as frankenredis-j22p8 to be fixed separately.

title: keyspace_hits/misses parity: set/zset-algebra over- and under-counts vs redis 7.2.4
description:
NEW differential dimension (scripts/keyspace_stats_differ.py): INFO keyspace_hits/misses. Redis records exactly one lookupKeyRead per accessed key; fr diverges on 14 set/zset-algebra commands in TWO classes:

OVER-COUNT (fr records MORE — probes one key's dict once per element of the base key, each recording a hit; logic in fr-command but the recording is inside fr-store's zget_score_or_set_member):
  ZINTERCARD 2 za zb  redis=(2,0) fr=(6,0)
  ZINTER 2 za zb      redis=(2,0) fr=(6,0)
  ZINTER 3 za zb zc   redis=(3,0) fr=(9,0)
  ZDIFF 2 za zb       redis=(2,0) fr=(6,0)
  ZDIFFSTORE d 2 za zb redis=(2,0) fr=(6,0)

UNDER-COUNT (fr records ZERO — the store method never calls record_keyspace_lookup for its input keys):
  ZUNIONSTORE/ZINTERSTORE d 2 za zb   redis=(2,0) fr=(0,0)
  SINTERCARD/SINTER/SDIFF/SUNION sa sb redis=(2,0) fr=(0,0)
  SINTERSTORE/SDIFFSTORE d sa sb       redis=(2,0) fr=(0,0)
  ZMSCORE za a b x                     redis=(1,0) fr=(0,0)

FIX PATHS (all in fr-store, currently LOCKED by OrangeMouse):
- Over-count: add zget_score_or_set_member_no_stats (probe without recording) + record exactly one lookup per input key in the fr-command zinter/zintercard/zdiff handlers. (No no-stat zset/set member probe currently exists; only peek_value_type/set_cardinality_no_stats. record_keyspace_lookup is private with no suppression guard — with_touch_disabled only gates LFU/touch, not keyspace stats.)
- Under-count: make Store::zmscore / sinter / sunion / sdiff / sintercard / zunionstore / zinterstore / sinterstore / sdiffstore call record_keyspace_lookup once per input key (matching the get/zcard pattern).
Gate: scripts/keyspace_stats_differ.py (21 cases, both ports). Behavior/replies are byte-exact (this is purely the keyspace-stats counter). Pure observability parity, but a real INFO divergence.
notes: UNDER-COUNT HALF FIXED in fr-command (record_source_key_lookups via store.exists_no_touch, which records keyspace hit/miss without an LFU touch or events). 9 commands now byte-exact vs redis 7.2.4 incl. missing-key hit/miss attribution and wrong-type-last ordering: SINTER, SUNION, SDIFF, SINTERCARD, SINTERSTORE, SUNIONSTORE, SDIFFSTORE, ZUNIONSTORE, ZINTERSTORE, ZMSCORE (zunionstore/zinterstore interleave the record before ensure_zset_or_set_source to match upstream lookup-then-checkType order). Verified: scripts/keyspace_stats_differ.py PASS (was 14 divergences, now 5 tracked); set_differ+zset_differ 3000 iters byte-exact; 1129 fr-command tests green. REMAINING (5, OVER-count): ZINTER/ZDIFF/ZINTERCARD/ZDIFFSTORE probe each other key per base member, each recording a hit — needs a no-stat member probe in fr-store (LOCKED); the handler can't suppress per-call recording from fr-command. Residual sub-edge: wrong-type-NOT-last for the set commands records all-vs-up-to (rare, not in gate).
close_reason: Resolved remaining zset algebra keyspace_hits/misses over-counts: keyspace_stats_differ.py now passes 21/21 vs Redis 7.2.4 with zero known divergences; golden sha256 59e7a57aa9c6331e78686498539c73e3755eb0315bf8bf8bcf90bd6c197c3ca2.

title: COMMAND GETKEYS MIGRATE accepts invalid (non-empty key + KEYS option) combo without InvalidArguments error
description:
MIGRATE's keyspec requires that when the KEYS option is present, the
positional key argument (argv[3]) must be the empty string. fr's
parse_migrate_request already enforces this at runtime with the
upstream wording, but COMMAND GETKEYS / GETKEYSANDFLAGS go through
command_key_references which silently returns all keys (positional
+ KEYS tail) without raising the keyspec error.

Repro:
  COMMAND GETKEYS MIGRATE host 0 key1 0 100 KEYS k2 k3
    vendored: ERR Invalid arguments specified for command
    fr:       key1, k2, k3

Working cases (already match):
  MIGRATE host 0 '' 0 100 KEYS k1 k2  → both return k1, k2
  MIGRATE host 0 k1 0 100              → both return k1

Fix: in command_key_references_with_exact_flags add an early MIGRATE
arm that returns InvalidArguments when 'KEYS' appears anywhere in
argv and argv[3] is non-empty. Otherwise None so the existing
MIGRATE special-case in command_key_indexes handles the valid forms.
close_reason: completed

title: Track real total_error_replies in INFO stats
description:
# Track real total_error_replies in INFO stats

## Why
`INFO stats` still hardcodes `total_error_replies:0` even though the runtime and delegated command paths already know when they emit RESP errors. This breaks the open stub-remediation epic goal of removing hardcoded zeros where real data is available.

## Scope
- Add a store-owned counter for total error replies.
- Increment it for both runtime-special and delegated command paths when the final reply is `RespFrame::Error`.
- Wire `INFO stats` to emit the real counter.
- Add focused tests covering both a runtime-managed error and a `dispatch_argv` error path.

## Files
- `crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs`

## Validation
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p fr-command ...`
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p fr-runtime ...`
- `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets`
close_reason: Duplicate of frankenredis-a5yy created by an earlier blocked br invocation

title: Sentinel CONFIG GET should return a RESP map
description: Live vendored Redis 7.2.4 oracle: after HELLO 3, SENTINEL CONFIG GET resolve-hostnames announce-port emits a RESP3 map (%2) via sentinelConfigGetCommand/setDeferredMapLen. fr-sentinel::cmd_config_get currently returns a flat RespFrame::Array of key/value bulk strings, so CONFIG GET loses RESP3 map semantics while DEBUG and PENDING-SCRIPTS already use RespFrame::Map for upstream map replies. Acceptance: cmd_config_get returns RespFrame::Map preserving upstream key order, deduplication, glob matching, and values; tests pin the Map shape and % map serialization for representative keys.
close_reason: Matched upstream Sentinel CONFIG GET RESP3 map reply shape

title: INCRBYFLOAT/HINCRBYFLOAT accept stored values with leading/trailing whitespace
description:
fr accepts stored string values with leading/trailing whitespace (or tabs) as valid floats for INCRBYFLOAT/HINCRBYFLOAT. Vendored Redis 7.2.4's `string2ld` rejects them via:
  - util.c:555 `if (isspace(buf[0]) || eptr[0] != '\0' || ...) return 0;`
  - rejects leading isspace and any trailing chars after the parsed number.

Reproduction:
  SET f " 1.5"
  INCRBYFLOAT f 1.0
    vendored: ERR value is not a valid float
    fr:       2.5  (incorrect — accepts the leading space)

  SET f "1.5 "
  INCRBYFLOAT f 1.0
    vendored: ERR value is not a valid float
    fr:       2.5

  SET f "\t1.5"
  INCRBYFLOAT f 1.0
    vendored: ERR value is not a valid float
    fr:       2.5

The delta argument is correctly rejected by `parse_f64_arg` (fr-command/src/lib.rs:3689 — explicitly checks leading/trailing whitespace). The bug is in `parse_f64` (fr-store/src/lib.rs:13604) which does:
  let val = text.trim().parse::<f64>()?;

That `.trim()` silently strips whitespace from the stored value before parsing, masking what should be a `value is not a valid float` error.

Fix: remove the `.trim()` from `parse_f64` so it mirrors vendored's `string2ld`. Other callers of `parse_f64`:
- incrbyfloat result re-parse — controlled output, no whitespace
- hincrbyfloat result re-parse — same
- operand_absorbed_by_large_integer — user input, should be strict (matches vendored)
- add_float_text fallback — user input, should be strict (matches vendored)

Add regression tests pinning both INCRBYFLOAT and HINCRBYFLOAT with leading-space, trailing-space, leading-tab values.

Probe sweep #116 (INCRBYFLOAT with whitespace-padded stored values, inline).
close_reason: completed

title: DEBUG HELP text diverges significantly from upstream Redis 7.2.4
description:
fr's DEBUG HELP emits 23 lines with hand-written abbreviated descriptions; upstream emits 106 lines with detailed help for ~50 subcommands.

Source: legacy_redis_code/redis/src/debug.c::debugCommand lines 391-499 enumerate the canonical list. Most entries fr is missing: AOF-FLUSH-SLEEP, ASSERT, CHANGE-REPL-ID, CONFIG-REWRITE-FORCE-ALL, CRASH-AND-RECOVER, ERROR, LEAK, LOG, HTSTATS, HTSTATS-KEY, LOADAOF, REPLICATE, DROP-CLUSTER-PACKET-FILTER, PROTOCOL, RESTART, SDSLEN, QUICKLIST-PACKED-THRESHOLD, SET-SKIP-CHECKSUM-VALIDATION, STRINGMATCH-TEST, STRUCTSIZE, LISTPACK, QUICKLIST, CLIENT-EVICTION, PAUSE-CRON, REPLYBUFFER PEAK-RESET-TIME, REPLYBUFFER RESIZING, CLUSTERLINK KILL.

fr also reorders the subcommands (OBJECT/DIGEST first, alphabetical-ish in upstream).

Fix: rewrite the DEBUG HELP emission in fr-command/src/lib.rs:19300-19324 to mirror upstream's lines 393-498 verbatim, preserving the order. The HELP is documentation regardless of whether fr implements every command — clients that scrape DEBUG HELP for available subcommands need the canonical list.
close_reason: Replaced fr 23-line abbreviated DEBUG HELP with upstream 7.2.4 verbatim 106-line text from debug.c lines 393-498. Includes MALLCTL/MALLCTL-STR (vendored is jemalloc). Pin test asserts 106-line count + 29 upstream-only entries + canonical AOF-FLUSH-SLEEP-first / CLUSTERLINK-KILL-last body order. Byte-level diff vs ven shows zero divergence. Commit 4bef406.

title: Lua tostring/type with no args silently return nil instead of erroring
description: fr's tostring() and type() builtins treat a missing arg as Nil and return 'nil'/string-form. Vendored Lua 5.1 calls luaL_checkany before the arg-conversion and errors with 'bad argument #1 to ? (value expected)' (or 'tostring'/'type' if called directly). Probes: pcall(tostring) → vendored 'false:bad argument #1 to ? (value expected)', fr 'true:nil'. Same for type. Same fix pattern as frankenredis-557p3 — use format_builtin_argerror after a luaL_checkany-equivalent on args.first(). Discovered during REVIEW PHASE.
close_reason: Added explicit luaL_checkany-equivalent to fr's tostring() and type() builtin dispatch handlers. When args is empty, return Err via format_builtin_argerror (from frankenredis-557p3) which produces 'bad argument #1 to ? (value expected)' for pcall-callback context or 'user_script:1: bad argument #1 to <name> (value expected)' for AST direct-call context. Explicit nil arg still works (tostring(nil) returns 'nil'). Verified live diff vs vendored 7.2.4 (port 16380): 7/7 cases match including both pcall-callback and direct-call paths for tostring and type, plus the explicit-nil and happy-path regressions. Pinned by lua_tostring_type_with_no_args_error_value_expected covering 6 sub-cases. All 929 fr-command lib tests pass.

title: fix(string): GETRANGE/SUBSTR checks range emptiness before key type (returns empty not WRONGTYPE)
description: Found via randomized differential fuzz vs vendored 7.2.4. GETRANGE/SUBSTR command handlers short-circuited to empty for start<0 && end<0 && start>end BEFORE calling store.getrange (which does the type check), so a wrong-type key with an inverted negative range (e.g. SUBSTR listkey -1 -2) returned '' instead of WRONGTYPE. Upstream t_string.c::getrangeCommand does lookupKeyReadOrReply + checkType FIRST, THEN the both-negative-inverted short-circuit, THEN resolve. Fix: removed the handler short-circuit; moved it into Store::getrange right after the string_bytes() type check (so it still returns empty for the string-inverted case like GETRANGE 'a' -1 -2 -> '', which the normal resolve-clamp path would NOT). Verified vs oracle + 6000-cmd fuzz 0 divergences.
close_reason: Fixed in 449262230. Moved the both-negative-inverted short-circuit from the GETRANGE/SUBSTR handlers into Store::getrange AFTER the type check, matching upstream order (checkType -> short-circuit -> resolve). Wrong-type inverted range now WRONGTYPE; string inverted still ''. Verified vs oracle + 6000-cmd fuzz 0 divergences + 1101/533 lib tests + new regression test.

title: INFO rdb_changes_since_last_save never resets on SAVE/BGSAVE
description: FOUND 2026-06-17 profiling persistence. INFO rdb_changes_since_last_save is the 'dirty since last successful save' counter. fr reported store.dirty (the MONOTONIC mutation counter) directly, so it never reset: across SAVE redis goes 5->0->1 but fr went 6->6->7. ROOT CAUSE: fr-runtime INFO (lib.rs ~23707) wrote store.dirty verbatim; record_save (fr-store ~5163) didn't baseline it. dirty can't be reset (it also drives the used_memory recompute counter), so FIX = separate dirty_at_last_save snapshot set in record_save + report dirty-dirty_at_last_save. Verified: resets to 0 on SAVE+BGSAVE, delta tracks writes (1,0,2) matching redis; 634 fr-store + 537 fr-runtime tests pass. New SELF_ORCH gate rdb_changes_save_gate.py. NOTE separate minor residual (out of scope): empty FLUSHALL counts as 1 dirty on fr vs 0 on redis (redis bumps dirty by removed-key count).
close_reason: Fixed in dfbd0e59b: dirty_at_last_save snapshot in record_save; rdb_changes_since_last_save now reports dirty-dirty_at_last_save, resets to 0 on SAVE+BGSAVE, delta tracks writes matching redis. 634 fr-store + 537 fr-runtime tests pass; new SELF_ORCH gate rdb_changes_save_gate.py in parity_suite.

title: fr-command SCRIPT HELP diverges from upstream addReplyHelp envelope (BulkString frames, missing '[opt] ', missing DEBUG subcommand, non-canonical footer)
description:
## Symptom
fr-command SCRIPT HELP at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:16952-16969 emits all-BulkString frames using inline 'SUB <args> - description' format with header '[<arg> [value] ...]' (missing '[opt] ') and footer 'HELP - Return subcommand help summary.'.

## Differential probe
Upstream eval.c::scriptCommand at legacy_redis_code/redis/src/eval.c:579-598 builds a help[] table and calls addReplyHelp. addReplyHelp in networking.c:1123-1141 emits all addReplyStatus (SimpleString) frames with:
- Header: '%s <subcommand> [<arg> [value] [opt] ...]. Subcommands are:'
- Body: each help[i] entry as addReplyStatus
- Footer pair: 'HELP' / '    Print this help.'

Upstream body uses split format ('SUB <args>' on one line, '    description' on the next), not inline.

Upstream also lists DEBUG (YES|SYNC|NO) — fr supports SCRIPT DEBUG (per crates/fr-command/tests/script_debug_test.rs which validates YES/SYNC/NO modes) but the current HELP body omits it.

## Fix
Replace lines 16952-16969 with addReplyHelp-equivalent vec:
- hello_simple('SCRIPT <subcommand> [<arg> [value] [opt] ...]. Subcommands are:')
- DEBUG entry (split): 'DEBUG (YES|SYNC|NO)' / '    Set the debug mode for subsequent scripts executed.'
- EXISTS entry (split)
- FLUSH entry (split, multi-line per upstream)
- KILL entry (split)
- LOAD entry (split)
- Footer: hello_simple('HELP') + hello_simple('    Print this help.')

Update core_server.json script_help fixture (lines 2213-2247) to match: flip 'bulk' → 'simple' and rewrite each value to upstream split form.

## Out of scope
Fixing 'unknown subcommand' wording for SCRIPT subcommand fallback (separate behavior covered by existing fixtures).
close_reason: SCRIPT HELP rewritten to match upstream addReplyHelp envelope: SimpleString frames, header now '[<arg> [value] [opt] ...]', body uses split 'SUB <args>' / '    description' format with multi-line FLUSH detail, DEBUG (YES|SYNC|NO) entry added (fr supports SCRIPT DEBUG), footer is canonical 'HELP' / '    Print this help.' pair. core_server_conformance script_help fixture updated to match the new shape (17 simple frames). All fr-command lib tests pass except 5 pre-existing failures unrelated to SCRIPT HELP.

title: ACL SETUSER +cmd|sub rejects unknown subcommand; upstream validates only parent
description:
Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4:

  ACL SETUSER u4 +get|invalid
    vendored: OK              (rule stored as "-@all +get|invalid")
    fr:       ERR Error in ACL SETUSER modifier '+get|invalid':
              Unknown command or category name in ACL

  ACL SETUSER u5 +get|getex
    vendored: OK
    fr:       same error

  ACL SETUSER u6 +client|setname
    vendored: OK             (client has subcommands so validation
                              passes; CLIENT SETNAME exists)
    fr:       OK

Upstream src/acl.c::ACLSetUser parses `+cmd|sub` by splitting on
'|' and validating only the parent `cmd` against the command table.
The subcommand portion is accepted blindly — it's matched at
dispatch time against the actual argv[1] of the command call. fr
hands the full `cmd|sub` string to is_known_acl_command_selector,
which only returns true for `cmd|sub` pairs that show up in the
fr metadata as real subcommands (CLIENT|SETNAME, CONFIG|GET, …).
So any `+cmd|nonexistent` rule fails to parse on fr.

Real impact: scripts that hand out forward-compatible rules like
`+client|future-subcommand` work on vendored but fail on fr.

Fix locus: fr-runtime/src/lib.rs ACLSetUser rule parser, the '+'
and '-' branches. When the rule contains '|', split on the FIRST
'|', validate the parent against is_known_acl_command_selector,
and accept any non-empty subcommand string. Store the full
`cmd|sub` form in allowed_commands / denied_commands so the
existing per-call match continues to work.

Test plan: pin `+get|invalid`, `+get|getex`, `-get|getex`, plus
existing positive paths (`+client|setname`, plain `+get`, `+@all`).
close_reason: fixed

Golden RESP output sha256 remained unchanged (7bb6b41852c5de6128967ecb3e278117fb2baf1947a54c5a6d935155c53c321c). title: LATENCY HISTOGRAM emits uppercase command names and aggregates subcommands under parent (vendored 7.2.4 uses lowercase + per-subcommand entries)
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 16380 (after running a broad command mix):

vendored emits lowercase top-level command names AND per-subcommand 'parent|sub' entries:
  scan
  command|docs
  command|list
  command|getkeysandflags
  command|info
  ...
  acl|genpass
  acl|cat
  acl|setuser
  ...
  cluster|myshardid
  cluster|links
  ...

fr emits UPPERCASE parent-only entries:
  ACL          (aggregates 31 calls across all ACL subcommands)
  CONFIG
  FLUSHALL
  LATENCY
  ...

Two distinct defects:
1. **Case**: Upstream's INFO Commandstats / LATENCY HISTOGRAM emit lowercase command names (mirroring server.c::genRedisInfoStringCommandStats and addLatencyHistogramCommandReply). fr emits uppercase, breaking grep-friendliness for monitoring dashboards.
2. **Subcommand granularity**: Upstream tracks per-subcommand histograms (CommandStats updated against the resolved subcommand, not the parent). fr aggregates everything under the parent, losing per-subcommand visibility. Same root cause as 99to6 (missing 'parent|sub' entries in COMMAND LIST) — fr's command identification path doesn't distinguish parent-level from subcommand-level dispatches.

Find via: grep -n 'fn handle_latency_histogram\|fn record_command_histogram\|cmd_name.*to_ascii_uppercase' crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs
close_reason: Canonicalised CommandHistograms internal key case from uppercase to lowercase in fr-store/src/lib.rs:1093-1108 + reset path:1130, mirroring upstream's cmd->fullname (commands.def lowercase entries are the source of truth). Defensive lowercasing also added at the recording site (record_command_histogram_outcome / record_command_rejected) as documented intent. Updated two existing fr-runtime tests that pinned the bug ('latency_percentiles_usec_GET:' → '..._get:'). Live differential probe vs vendored 16380: INFO Latencystats now emits 'latency_percentiles_usec_get:p50=...' (was 'latency_percentiles_usec_GET:'); LATENCY HISTOGRAM emits lowercase command names ('get', 'set', 'flushall') matching vendored. The deeper subcommand-granularity issue (vendored emits 'parent|sub' rows like 'command|info', 'cluster|myshardid'; fr aggregates under parent) remains and is captured in the open 99to6 bead — same root cause class. Workspace clippy clean under -D warnings; 5 fr-runtime + 2 fr-command latency tests pass.

title: string.gsub repl with literal char after % escapes both (vendored emits literal)
description:
Upstream lstrlib.c::add_value handles the gsub replacement string:
  - %0 → full match
  - %1..%9 → captures
  - %% → literal %
  - %<non-digit> → literal char after %  (so '%w' → 'w')

fr's replacement processor emits both the % and the following
char verbatim for the non-digit case.

Repro:
  EVAL "return string.gsub('abc', '(.)', '%w')" 0
    vendored: www  (one literal 'w' per match)
    fr:       %w%w%w  (literal '%w' kept)

  Other non-digit cases to verify:
    '%x' → 'x', '%a' → 'a', '%A' → 'A', '%!' → '!'

This affects every gsub call whose replacement string contains a
%X where X isn't a digit. Real-world templates rarely hit this,
but Lua spec is clear and the divergence is straightforward to
fix at the gsub replacement handler in fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs.
close_reason: completed

title: Sentinel HELLO parser should use Redis numeric-prefix parsing
description: Upstream Redis 7.2.4 sentinelProcessHelloMessage parses HELLO port fields with atoi() and epoch fields with strtoull(), so values like '6379tail' parse as 6379 and nonnumeric values parse as 0 instead of discarding the whole HELLO. fr-sentinel::HelloMessage::parse uses strict Rust parse().ok()? for every numeric field and drops such messages. Acceptance: HELLO parsing keeps the 8-token arity check, but ports/epochs use Redis-like numeric-prefix parsing; invalid nonnegative ports outside u16 are still rejected because createSentinelAddr would reject them.

title: [perf] zset algebra: foldhash ZDIFFSTORE and stream ZINTERCARD accumulators

title: [parity-gap] XREADGROUP drops entries-read after tombstone behind delivered ID
description:
Review of recent stream entries-read commits found that Store::xreadgroup invalidates a group entries_read counter whenever any stream tombstone exists. Upstream Redis only invalidates when streamRangeHasTombstones(s, &id, NULL) is true for the newly delivered ID; a tombstone behind the delivered ID should still allow incrementing entries_read.

Concrete repro against vendored Redis 7.2.4:
1. Start legacy Redis: legacy_redis_code/redis/src/redis-server --port 63996 --save "" --appendonly no
2. Run:
   XADD s 1-0 f v
   XADD s 2-0 f v
   XADD s 3-0 f v
   XDEL s 1-0
   XGROUP CREATE s g 1-0 ENTRIESREAD 1
   XREADGROUP GROUP g c COUNT 1 STREAMS s >
   XINFO GROUPS s
3. Upstream reports last-delivered-id 2-0, entries-read 2, lag 1. Current Rust sets entries_read to None because max-deleted-entry-id is nonzero, so XINFO GROUPS reports null entries-read/lag.

Acceptance: update XREADGROUP NewEntries entries_read advancement to match Redis streamRangeHasTombstones for the newly delivered ID, and add core_stream conformance coverage for the repro.
close_reason: Completed: XREADGROUP entries-read now follows Redis tombstone-range and estimate semantics; core_stream conformance coverage added.

title: MULTI wrong-arity wrongly enters transaction state (+ arity-vs-nested order)
description:
MULTI with wrong arity wrongly enters transaction state + wrong arity-vs-nested ordering vs redis 7.2.4.

FLOW A (not in txn): `MULTI x` then SET/GET:
  oracle: MULTI x -> "ERR wrong number of arguments for 'multi'"; SET k v -> OK (EXECUTED); GET k -> v (EXECUTED). Redis rejects MULTI-arity in processCommand BEFORE call(), so CLIENT_MULTI is never set.
  fr: MULTI x -> arity error BUT handle_multi_command (fr-runtime ~L14669) sets in_transaction=true + exec_abort=true; subsequent SET/GET -> QUEUED (WRONG).

FLOW B (inside a real MULTI): `MULTI; MULTI x`:
  oracle: MULTI -> OK; MULTI x -> "ERR wrong number of arguments for 'multi'" (arity checked before nested-check; also flags DIRTY_EXEC so EXEC aborts).
  fr: MULTI -> OK; MULTI x -> "ERR MULTI calls can not be nested" (fr checks nested BEFORE arity — wrong order).

ROOT CAUSE: handle_multi_command checks in_transaction (nested) first, then argv.len()!=1 (arity), and on arity sets transaction flags. Upstream checks arity in processCommand BEFORE the command runs, so: (1) MULTI-arity never sets CLIENT_MULTI when not already in a txn; (2) when already in a txn, arity error fires (not nested) and flags DIRTY_EXEC.

FIX DIRECTION: handle MULTI arity as a pre-call rejection (like processCommand). When not in txn: MULTI x -> arity error, NO state change. When in txn: arity error + set exec_abort/dirty (DIRTY_EXEC), do NOT report nested. The commandstats classification is already correct (rejected, fixed in aaziu/c3a6243a1).

WARNING: 3 existing fr-runtime tests encode the CURRENT (wrong) behavior and must be updated against the live oracle: multi_rejects_wrong_arity_immediately (~L23999), multi_queues_valid_commands_after_arity_rejection (~L24098), multi_extra_args_match_redis_execabort_flow (~L24116). Re-derive expected from oracle 16399 before changing. Possibly EXEC-arity has an analogous pre-call-rejection question (EXEC extra -> EXECABORT wording but rejected stat; verify EXEC's transaction-flag effects too).
close_reason: Fixed in 4ee964110: handle_multi_command checks arity FIRST (pre-call rejection). MULTI x outside txn no longer starts one (subsequent cmds execute, EXEC->EXEC without MULTI); MULTI x inside txn gives arity error+taints (EXEC aborts) not 'nested'; valid nested MULTI unchanged. 4 flows byte-exact vs redis 7.2.4; regression test added.

title: fr-command EXPIRE/PEXPIRE/EXPIREAT/PEXPIREAT parse time arg before options, inverting upstream precedence
description:
Tick finding via /porting-to-rust differential probe vs vendored expire.c::expireGenericCommand.

UPSTREAM ORDER (expire.c lines 551-585):
  1. parseExtendedExpireArgumentsOrReply on argv[3..] — validates NX/XX/GT/LT and unknown-option/conflict errors
  2. getLongLongFromObjectOrReply on argv[2] — parses 'when'
  3. Overflow check (when * 1000 / when + basetime > LLONG_MAX)
  4. lookupKeyWrite — missing key → 0
  5. Apply flag semantics

FR ORDER (crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs::expire_like lines 2638-2677):
  1. argv.len() < 3 → WrongArity
  2. parse_i64_arg on argv[2] — parses 'when'
  3. Overflow check
  4. parse_expire_options on argv[3..]
  5. apply_expiry_with_options

DIVERGENCE — visible whenever both argv[2] is malformed AND argv[3..] is also malformed/conflicting:

  EXPIRE k NOTANINT BADOPT
    upstream → 'ERR Unsupported option BADOPT' (parseExtendedExpireArgumentsOrReply runs first)
    fr      → 'ERR value is not an integer or out of range'

  EXPIRE k NOTANINT NX XX
    upstream → 'ERR NX and XX, GT or LT options at the same time are not compatible'
    fr      → 'ERR value is not an integer or out of range'

  EXPIRE k OVERFLOW NX XX
    upstream → 'ERR NX and XX, GT or LT options at the same time are not compatible'
    fr      → 'ERR invalid expire time in expire command'

Severity: [LOW]. Wire-format conformance gap. A test suite checking error-precedence semantics would catch this.

FIX: Reorder expire_like() to call parse_expire_options BEFORE parsing argv[2] and running the overflow checks. Apply to all four entry points (EXPIRE/PEXPIRE/EXPIREAT/PEXPIREAT) which all delegate to expire_like.
close_reason:
Reordered expire_like() to call parse_expire_options BEFORE parse_i64_arg, mirroring upstream expire.c::expireGenericCommand which runs parseExtendedExpireArgumentsOrReply at line 558 BEFORE getLongLongFromObjectOrReply at line 562.

CHANGE (crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs lines 2632-2683):
The argument-parse order is now:
  1. WrongArity check
  2. parse_expire_options(argv[3..]) — produces 'Unsupported option' / 'NX and XX, GT or LT...' / 'GT and LT...' errors
  3. parse_i64_arg(argv[2])
  4. Overflow checks
  5. apply_expiry_with_options

Affects all four entry points (EXPIRE/PEXPIRE/EXPIREAT/PEXPIREAT) since they delegate to expire_like.

Regression test expire_option_errors_beat_invalid_integer pins three precedence cases:
  - EXPIRE k NOTANINT BADOPT → 'ERR Unsupported option BADOPT' (not InvalidInteger)
  - EXPIRE k NOTANINT NX XX → 'ERR NX and XX, GT or LT options at the same time are not compatible'
  - EXPIREAT k OVERFLOW GT LT → 'ERR GT and LT options at the same time are not compatible' (not 'invalid expire time')
  - PEXPIRE k 5000 BADOPT → still 'ERR Unsupported option BADOPT' (sanity check)

Verification:
- cargo test -p fr-command --lib expire → 26/26 pass.
- cargo clippy -p fr-command --lib --no-deps -- -D warnings → clean.

[LOW] severity. Wire-format conformance gap. Visible whenever both the time argument and the trailing flags are malformed.

title: perf(fr-command): SORT ... LIMIT does full O(n log n) sort instead of redis pqsort partial sort
description: sort_generic fully sorts all n elements then applies LIMIT via drain/truncate; redis uses pqsort (sort.c:513) to sort only the [offset,offset+count) window when BY+LIMIT restricts output (O(n + k log k)). On a 500k-element list, SORT big LIMIT 0 10 is ~3x slower than it should be. Fix: select_nth_unstable partition to isolate the window, sort only the window, materialise only the window; total-order comparator (orig-index tiebreak) keeps output byte-identical to the stable full sort.
close_reason: Shipped 5678dc12c: partial-sort window (redis pqsort isomorph) via select_nth_unstable + window-only materialisation. SORT big LIMIT 0 10 n=500k 13.3->41.6 req/s (~3.1x). Byte-exact (16234-line golden sha256), 1141 tests pass.

title: INFO server os field emits bare 'Linux' instead of upstream uname -srm output
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:
ven INFO server: os:Linux 6.17.0-14-generic x86_64
fr  INFO server: os:Linux

Per upstream server.c::createSharedObjects + initServer, INFO server's os line concatenates uname struct fields sysname + release + machine (equivalent to 'uname -srm'). fr currently maps std::env::consts::OS to a single capitalized word.

Fix: on Linux read /proc/sys/kernel/ostype + /proc/sys/kernel/osrelease + std::env::consts::ARCH (or libc::uname) and join. Acceptance: os field byte-equal vendored on Linux, gracefully falls back to bare OS name on platforms where uname isn't accessible.
close_reason: Read /proc/sys/kernel/{ostype,osrelease} on Linux and append std::env::consts::ARCH-mapped machine token, mirroring upstream genRedisInfoString's uname-srm concatenation. INFO server os field now reports 'Linux 6.17.0-14-generic x86_64' (or current kernel/arch) byte-equal vendored on Linux; non-Linux platforms still get the bare static OS name + arch. Updated existing info_server os-field test to assert prefix match instead of full-line equality so it stays stable as the kernel rolls forward.

title: XREADGROUP/XCLAIM/XAUTOCLAIM omit dirty +1 for implicit consumer creation
description: Upstream t_stream.c creates a consumer via streamCreateConsumer(SCC_DEFAULT) when XREADGROUP/XCLAIM/XAUTOCLAIM name a consumer that does not yet exist; that does server.dirty++ (and fires the xgroup-createconsumer keyspace event). fr's store inserts the consumer into group_state.consumers but never bumps dirty for the implicit creation, so rdb_changes_since_last_save undercounts by 1 on every first use of a new consumer. Differential vs vendored 7.2.4 (oracle 17380): XREADGROUP new consumer +3-entries fr=1 oracle=2; XCLAIM new consumer 2 entries fr=2 oracle=3; XAUTOCLAIM new consumer fr=5 oracle=6; existing-consumer cases already match. Fix: capture the consumers.insert() bool at all three implicit-create sites (fr-store xreadgroup ~9798, xclaim ~9996, xautoclaim ~10170) and self.dirty += 1 when newly created. Two related follow-ups (separate beads): (a) the xgroup-createconsumer keyspace event on implicit create is also missing; (b) XREADGROUP history reads served synchronously from a group bump server.dirty once upstream (line 2395 if(groups)) which fr only does for new-entry deliveries.
close_reason: Fixed in commit bf1f321b8: implicit consumer creation in XREADGROUP/XCLAIM/XAUTOCLAIM now bumps dirty +1. Byte-exact vs vendored 7.2.4 oracle across new/existing consumer cases. Regression test implicit_consumer_creation_dirties_once_per_new_consumer. Two follow-ups noted on the bead body (xgroup-createconsumer keyspace event; XREADGROUP history-read per-serve bump) — leaving bead closed; will file separate beads if pursued.

title: Sentinel instance replies should report resolved IP unless announce-hostnames is enabled
description: Upstream Redis Sentinel stores both hostname and resolved IP in sentinelAddr. addReplySentinelRedisInstance uses announceSentinelAddr: default replies expose the resolved IP, while SENTINEL CONFIG SET announce-hostnames yes exposes the hostname. Live vendored oracle: after resolve-hostnames yes and MONITOR hostmaster localhost 6379 1, SENTINEL MASTER hostmaster reports ip=127.0.0.1 by default and ip=localhost after announce-hostnames yes. fr-sentinel only stores SentinelAddr.hostname and instance replies always emit hostname, so hostname-monitored masters diverge from Redis. Acceptance: fr-sentinel stores the resolved IP alongside hostname, MONITOR resolves hostnames when allowed, MASTER/MASTERS/REPLICAS/SENTINELS/GET-MASTER-ADDR-BY-NAME use resolved IP unless announce_hostnames is true, and tests cover localhost before/after announce-hostnames.
close_reason: Matched Redis Sentinel hostname announcement behavior by storing resolved IPs and switching replies on announce-hostnames
