title: DEBUG DIGEST and DIGEST-VALUE produce different SHA1 hashes than vendored 7.2.4 — algorithm divergence
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 16380:

After SET k1 v1:
  vendored DIGEST       : f501b7e652f1e4874b8a6890245c152b5b89fe96
  fr       DIGEST       : 63297d5114c459c1d1c621bca1080828cd71425a
  vendored DIGEST-VALUE k1: 1b39f6ecf00cfce8fa56c4c62c40126162d878fe
  fr       DIGEST-VALUE k1: 63297d5114c459c1d1c621bca1080828cd71425a

After also SET k2 v2:
  vendored DIGEST       : 91720c7142b6dfec5df4d6409a6f52e2da2a829f
  fr       DIGEST       : 7f9143b0cfbeb29ead0d93c2f3c8eda4ffe37062
  vendored DIGEST-VALUE k2: becefaf9bf124b8996f547b6b4876ef6a6b60c37
  fr       DIGEST-VALUE k2: 1cb83ee1db7aeb5f7ccbb27e52c0e58c32923238

Empty DB returns 40 zeros on both — the zero-state matches. After even a single key, the algorithms diverge.

Upstream debug.c (mixDigest/xorObjectDigest):
- xorObjectDigest mixes htonl(o->type) FIRST as a 4-byte aux header.
- mixDigest = xorDigest(digest, ptr, len) then SHA1(digest) -> digest.
- For OBJ_STRING: mixStringObjectDigest(digest, o).
- For OBJ_LIST: iterate listTypeNext, mixStringObjectDigest per entry.
- For OBJ_SET: iterate setTypeNext, xorDigest (NOT mixDigest) per entry.
- For OBJ_ZSET: per-entry build eledigest via mixDigest(value) + mixDigest(score-as-fpconv-dtoa-string), then xorDigest into outer digest.
- For OBJ_HASH: mixDigest each field-value pair.
- For OBJ_STREAM: includes stream entries + groups + consumer state + last-id.
- Plus expire-time mixed in if set (via mixDigest of long-long expiretime as 16-char ASCII).

fr's compute_debug_digest implementation in crates/fr-command needs auditing per-type to match this exact sequence — wrong order, missing aux byte, wrong score-formatting, or skipped expiretime mixing all cause hash divergence.

Lower priority since DEBUG DIGEST is mostly used for replication-consistency gating (which fr's replication doesn't fully exercise yet) and benchmarking; production paths don't depend on it. Filed at P3 for backlog when stream parity (project_rdb_stream_incompatibility) is being addressed — same code-path neighborhood.
close_reason: Replaced DEBUG DIGEST/DIGEST-VALUE key+dump hash with Redis SHA1 xor/mix object digest flow and pinned string-object hashes to vendored 7.2.4

title: fr-command DEBUG missing HTSTATS / HTSTATS-KEY subcommands; tooling that probes hash-table stats can't inspect the keyspace or specific keys
description:
Final entry in the DEBUG-subcommand-missing family (l2zqy / r2l7c / 53n6u / byt3l / wcedk / eejfm). Two more subcommands upstream debug.c::debugCommand exposes:

- DEBUG HTSTATS <dbid> [full] at debug.c:889-916 — argc>=3; returns a Verbatim-formatted block:
    [Dictionary HT]
    <dictGetStats output for db.dict>
    [Expires HT]
    <dictGetStats output for db.expires>
  Invalid dbid → 'ERR Out of range database'. The optional 'full' arg toggles full-detail bucket info.

- DEBUG HTSTATS-KEY <key> [full] at debug.c:917-949 — argc>=3; lookup key. Returns:
    ERR no such key                                                       if missing
    ERR The value stored at the specified key is not represented using an hash table
                                                                           if encoding != ht / skiplist
    Verbatim-formatted dictGetStats output                                 otherwise

fr stores entries via Rust HashMap rather than upstream's dict implementation, so we can't surface real bucket-distribution stats. Synthetic minimal output that's syntactically valid keeps tooling parsing, while making the format obvious to inspectors that fr lacks dict instrumentation.

For HTSTATS-KEY, the encoding gate maps cleanly: only 'hashtable' (Hash) and 'skiplist' (SortedSet) qualify. The hashtable_ex variant (7.4 hash field-TTL) also qualifies since fr's encoding helper can return it. Other encodings (listpack, listpack_ex, quicklist, intset, embstr, raw, int, stream) trigger the 'not represented using an hash table' error.

Acceptance:
  - HTSTATS 0 → BulkString with '[Dictionary HT]\n...\n[Expires HT]\n...' (synthetic, but parseable).
  - HTSTATS -1 / out-of-range dbid → 'ERR Out of range database'
  - HTSTATS-KEY missing → 'ERR no such key'
  - HTSTATS-KEY string_key → 'ERR The value stored at the specified key is not represented using an hash table'
  - HTSTATS-KEY hashtable_key → BulkString with synthetic dict stats
  - Wrong-arity → canonical 'unknown subcommand or wrong number of arguments for X. Try DEBUG HELP.'
  - Tests stay green; clippy clean.
close_reason: done

title: REVIEW MODE Tick 14 audit: RESP3 wire-shape cluster mostly exhausted; deeper architectural gaps remain (Double/Set/Verbatim/Bool/Null variants, addReplyAttribute support)
description:
REVIEW MODE Tick 14 audit summary. Across many cycles I shipped 14 RESP3 wire-format fixes (i40x2/e4njz/udl3y/asvh1/5d04d/ndz0j/5h86s/cz712/ec2rf/q11am/1dfox/cgjlc/jnf53/1g3ao + ljrt6 OBJECT REFCOUNT shared-int). The high-yield Map / nested-2-Array sweep is now mostly exhausted on the fr-command surface. This bead documents what's covered and what remains, so the next agent doesn't re-traverse the same surface.

COVERED (RESP3 Map / nested-2-Array shape):
  - CLIENT TRACKINGINFO (i40x2)
  - ACL GETUSER (e4njz)
  - MEMORY STATS outer + db.<n> sub-map (udl3y, ndz0j)
  - FUNCTION STATS (asvh1)
  - ACL LOG entries (5d04d)
  - CONFIG REWRITE consumer (5h86s)
  - LCS IDX (cz712)
  - CLUSTER SHARDS shard + node (ec2rf)
  - COMMAND DOCS outer + per-cmd (q11am)
  - FUNCTION LIST library + function (1dfox)
  - LATENCY HISTOGRAM 3-deep (cgjlc)
  - ZRANGE/ZRANGEBYSCORE/ZREVRANGE/ZRANDMEMBER WITHSCORES → Array<[m,s]> (jnf53)
  - ZPOPMIN/ZPOPMAX with COUNT → Array<[m,s]> (1g3ao)
  - OBJECT REFCOUNT shared integers → INT_MAX (ljrt6)
  - XINFO STREAM/GROUPS/CONSUMERS Maps (already covered pre-cluster, br-frankenredis-f6z6)
  - HGETALL Map (already covered, br-frankenredis-9itc)

DEEPER ARCHITECTURAL GAPS — these can't be fixed in a single tick, they require a new RespFrame variant + encoder + parser + thread the protocol version into the wire-out path:

  1. RespFrame::Double(f64) missing. Upstream addReplyDouble emits ',<value>\r\n' (RESP3 Double type) for ZSCORE, ZINCRBY, ZADD INCR, ZMSCORE per-member, ZRANGE WITHSCORES per-score, ZPOP*-with-COUNT per-score, GEODIST, GEOPOS each-pair, etc. fr emits BulkString(score.to_string()) for all scores under both protocols. 26+ call sites in upstream networking.c.

  2. RespFrame::Set missing. Upstream addReplySetLen emits '~<N>\r\n' (RESP3 Set type) under RESP3 / '*<N>\r\n' under RESP2. SMEMBERS, SINTER, SUNION, SDIFF, SRANDMEMBER (no count) all use it. fr emits Array regardless. ~10 call sites.

  3. RespFrame::Verbatim missing. Upstream addReplyVerbatim emits '=<N>\r\nxxx:<body>\r\n' under RESP3 (Verbatim string with content-type prefix) / bulk string under RESP2. Used for INFO output sections, LATENCY DOCTOR/GRAPH, lolwut, MEMORY DOCTOR, MEMORY MALLOC-STATS, MEMORY PURGE.

  4. RespFrame::Bool missing. Upstream addReplyBool emits '#t\r\n' / '#f\r\n' under RESP3 / ':1\r\n' / ':0\r\n' under RESP2. Used in CONFIG GET (boolean configs), Lua boolean returns, DEBUG SLEEP, DEBUG STRINGMATCH-LEN, MODULE-style replies.

  5. RespFrame::Null missing. Upstream emits '_\r\n' (RESP3 Null) under RESP3, '569JNRXghkl1\r\n' / '*-1\r\n' under RESP2. fr's BulkString(None) / Array(None) always emit RESP2 nil regardless of protocol. Affects every nil reply from every command.

  6. addReplyAttribute (b'|' prefix) is parsed but never emitted. Per-reply metadata can't be attached. Upstream uses it sparingly (DEBUG, MODULE) but RedisInsight and some clients gate on the presence of attributes.

  7. RESP3 Push frames are partially supported (RespFrame::Push exists) but not all pubsub/keyspace-notification paths use them. Worth auditing whether fr emits 'message' / 'pmessage' replies as Push under RESP3 and as Array under RESP2.

  8. WAIT/WAITAOF stubs always return 0. Documented at project_wait_commands_stub. Multi-day epic to implement replica ACK tracking.

ROTATION: per REVIEW-MODE.md, the next ticks should rotate to /testing-* skills (metamorphic, fuzzing, conformance harnesses, golden artifacts). This tick's concrete fix extends the metamorphic harness at fr-command/tests/metamorphic_resp3_map_shape.rs to cover the cluster fixes that landed since the file was originally written (currently it pins only i40x2/e4njz/udl3y/asvh1; everything from 5d04d onward is unpinned at the family invariant level).
close_reason: Audit complete + concrete defensive harness extension shipped. Extended fr-command/tests/metamorphic_resp3_map_shape.rs with 4 new family-level invariants (zrange WITHSCORES, zpop with COUNT, LCS IDX, LATENCY HISTOGRAM) covering the cluster fixes that landed since the file was first written. Also fixed 2 pre-existing stale assertions (mr_memory_stats_resp2_array_matches_resp3_map_shape, mr_protocol_switch_does_not_alter_data) that were written before frankenredis-ndz0j introduced the MEMORY STATS db.<n> sub-Map shape — they were comparing nested values strictly without normalizing Map↔flat-Array divergence at deeper layers. New canonicalize_to_resp2_shape helper recursively rewrites RESP3 Maps into their RESP2-equivalent flat-Array form so the structural comparison is correct at every nesting depth. All 8 metamorphic tests now pass; clippy clean. Audit also documented (in the bead body) the deeper architectural gaps that can't be fixed in a single tick — RespFrame::Double/Set/Verbatim/Bool/Null variants missing, addReplyAttribute support, WAIT/WAITAOF stub. Future ticks should focus on either (a) /testing-* skills with the harness now extended, or (b) one of the architectural epics (Double variant rollout is the highest-impact).

title: Lua string.gsub broken for table and function replacement args
description: fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:4035 string.gsub only handles string replacement -- it calls to_display_string() on the third arg, which turns tables/functions into their tostring representations ('table: 0x...' / 'function: 0x...') and uses that as the literal substitution string. Lua spec: (string, integer) gsub(s, pattern, repl[, n]) where repl can be: (a) string (with %0/%1/.. capture refs - works), (b) table indexed by the 1st capture or whole match (broken in fr), (c) function called with captures and using its return value (broken in fr). Live differential probe: string.gsub('abc', '[a-z]', {a='1', b='2'}) returns '12c' in vendored but '<table-addr><table-addr><table-addr>' in fr; string.gsub('hello', '%a', function(c) return string.upper(c) end) returns 'HELLO' in vendored but a function-addr string in fr. Fix: extend the replacement path in the gsub handler to type-dispatch on the LuaValue: pass match captures to the function/table lookup and convert the result back to a string (with nil/false meaning 'leave unchanged' per Lua spec). Acceptance: both probe cases byte-exact with vendored.
close_reason: string.gsub now supports the three replacement-value types per Lua 5.1 spec instead of always coercing to string. Implementation in fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:4035: kept the 3rd arg as a LuaValue (not pre-stringified), dispatch on its type each match: (a) Str -> existing lua_gsub_replace (capture-ref aware), (b) Table -> lookup t[key] where key is the 1st capture or whole match (lua_gsub_capture_key helper), normalise via lua_gsub_normalise_repl, (c) Function/RustFunction/WrappedCoroutine -> self.call_function with captures as args (lua_gsub_capture_args helper), normalise the first return, (d) Number -> legacy string coercion, (e) other types -> 'bad argument #3 to gsub (string/function/table expected, got <type>)'. lua_gsub_normalise_repl encodes the spec: nil/false leaves the match unchanged, strings/numbers are coerced, true is 'invalid replacement value (a boolean)', other types raise the standard error. Live differential probe: string.gsub('abc','[a-z]',{a='1',b='2'}) byte-exact ('12c'), string.gsub('hello','%a',upper-fn) byte-exact ('HELLO'), string.gsub('hello world','(%a+)',bracket-fn) byte-exact ('[hello] [world]'), missing-key table case byte-exact, %1 capture-ref string repl regression-free. Tests: fr-command 910 pass.

title: Wire LATENCY subcommands to real LatencyTracker data
description:
# Wire LATENCY subcommands to real LatencyTracker data

## What
Replace the stub latency_cmd in fr-command/src/lib.rs (line ~11387) with real implementations that read from Store's LatencyTracker.

## Changes needed
- latency_cmd must accept &Store parameter (change dispatch_argv call site)
- LATENCY LATEST: return store.latency_tracker.latency_latest() formatted as [[event, timestamp, latest_ms, max_ms], ...]
- LATENCY HISTORY event: return store.latency_tracker.latency_history(event) as [[timestamp, duration_ms], ...]
- LATENCY RESET [event ...]: call store.latency_tracker.latency_reset() and return count of reset events
- LATENCY GRAPH event: generate ASCII art from history samples (implementation detail: use Redis's exact format with ± indicators and time axis)
- LATENCY DOCTOR: analyze samples and generate diagnostic text (e.g., "I have no latency reports to analyze" when empty, or "Command latency spikes detected..." when samples exist)

## LATENCY GRAPH format (from Redis)
```
event - min: Xms, max: Yms
+---+
|   |
|  #|
|###|
+---+
HH:MM
```
Each column is one sample. Height proportional to duration. Time labels at bottom.

## LATENCY DOCTOR format
Redis produces multi-paragraph analysis. For initial implementation:
- If no samples: "I have no latency reports to analyze. Be happy!"
- If samples exist: "I have reports for the following events:\n- {event}: {count} samples, max {max}ms\n..."

## Depends on
- frankenredis-a4sf (LatencyTracker infrastructure)

## Testing
- Conformance: update core_server.json LATENCY cases to expect real data after CONFIG SET latency-monitor-threshold + command execution
- Unit test: record_latency_sample then verify latency_latest output format

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs (latency_cmd, dispatch_argv call site)
close_reason: Completed

title: Per-command Instant::now timing = ~6% of GET CPU; chain batch timestamps (N+1 clock reads not 2N)
description: PROFILE (redis-benchmark GET -P16 -c50 saturation + perf -g on fr, AFTER d5nez): execute_plain_get_borrowed_into_with_default_read_gate -> Timespec::now -> clock_gettime = 6.1% CPU. Source: each command handler does Instant::now() at start + start.elapsed() at end (2 vDSO CLOCK_MONOTONIC reads/command) for cmdstat usec + slowlog + latency duration. At ~1M cmd/s that's ~2M clock reads/s (~25ns each via vDSO) = ~5-6% of one core. redis pays the same 2 reads but via a cheaper RDTSC-calibrated getMonotonicUs(); fr can't use RDTSC (forbid-unsafe) and CLOCK_MONOTONIC_COARSE (~5ns) is too coarse (1-4ms) for sub-us command timing. LEVER: hoist timing into the batch loop (process_buffered_frames) and CHAIN timestamps — one Instant::now() between commands serves as end-of-cmd-i AND start-of-cmd-(i+1), so N commands need N+1 reads instead of 2N (~halves the timing CPU, ~3% on GET). RISK/COST: (a) big cross-cutting refactor — every execute_plain_*_borrowed handler currently captures its own start; must thread a shared timer through the batch loop. (b) chained duration includes inter-command parse/dispatch time, slightly inflating cmdstat usec (NOT parity-checked per cmdstat_keyspace_parity_gate which ignores usec) and theoretically slowlog/latency (negligible: ns inflation vs the 10ms slowlog default). SYSCALL-BOUND CAVEAT: GET is 51% __send, so the freed CPU only raises throughput under CPU contention (like d5nez: ~3-5% at -c120), neutral single-client. Verify byte-exact + cmdstat/slowlog parity via individual gates + a forced-load bench. NOTE: full parity_suite runner is sandbox-144-blocked this session; individual 2-server gates run.
close_reason: Chained command-timing clock shipped (N+1 clock reads/batch); fr-runtime infra + fr-server batch reset; byte-exact verified (cmdstat parity PASS, fuzz 0 diffs 16k ops, builds clean)

title: Lua cmsgpack stdlib not exposed (sandbox surface)
description:
Vendored Redis 7.2.4 exposes cmsgpack.pack/cmsgpack.unpack to EVAL scripts via the bundled lua-cmsgpack library. fr's Lua environment treats cmsgpack as a nonexistent global.

Reproduction:
  EVAL "return tostring(cmsgpack.pack({1,2,3}) ~= '')" 0
    vendored: true
    fr:       ERR user_script:1: Script attempted to access nonexistent global variable 'cmsgpack' script: <sha>, on @user_script:1.

Impact: low-medium — cmsgpack is used by some real Redis-based libraries (e.g., resque-bus, lua-resty-* msgpack pipelines) for binary-safe RESP-passable encoding. Pure-EVAL Lua workloads that don't need msgpack are unaffected.

Implementation: register a cmsgpack RustFunction table with .pack and .unpack handlers. Pack must support nil/boolean/number/string/table per upstream lua_cmsgpack.c. Should reuse fr's existing Lua-to-bytes converters where possible.

Out of scope for current sweep — file as parity-tracking bead. Realistic users encountering this gap typically rewrite the script to use cjson, or run vendored.
close_reason: done

title: fr-command/tests: COPY cross-DB invariants unverified after op84s fix — TTL preservation, idempotency, REPLACE flag, type fidelity, SELECT composition
description:
/testing-metamorphic gap audit:

  find crates/fr-command/tests -name 'metamorphic_copy.rs' → empty
  grep -rn 'copy_via_dispatch' crates/ → only the 4 dispatch_argv unit tests just added in op84s

op84s fixed fr-command::copy_cmd to actually namespace keys per-DB and accept any valid target db, but only added 4 primitive unit tests (cross-db transfer, out-of-range rejection, cross-db same-name, same-key/same-db error). The richer cross-cutting invariants are unverified:

- MR1 (TTL preservation): COPY of a key with absolute expiry T must materialise the dest with the SAME T (cross-db too).
- MR2 (idempotency without REPLACE): COPY src dst when dst already exists returns 0 and leaves both untouched.
- MR3 (REPLACE overwrite): COPY src dst REPLACE always succeeds, dest takes source value.
- MR4 (source preservation): COPY never deletes source, regardless of target db or REPLACE.
- MR5 (type fidelity): COPY of a hash key produces a hash key in the target.
- MR6 (round trip): COPY src dst DB N; COPY dst src DB 0 REPLACE — final state matches initial source contents.
- MR7 (SELECT + COPY composition): SELECT 1 then COPY foo bar in dispatch session must operate on db 1 keys (composes with j22p8's SELECT fix).

Without these, future Store::copy or dispatch_argv refactors can silently regress cross-DB COPY and only show up via real-world workloads (replication, AOF replay).

Fix: add crates/fr-command/tests/metamorphic_copy.rs with 7+ invariants exercising dispatch_argv COPY.
close_reason: Added crates/fr-command/tests/metamorphic_copy.rs with 8 invariants exercising the dispatch_argv COPY path: TTL preservation across cross-DB COPY, no-TTL stays no-TTL, target-collision is non-destructive without REPLACE, REPLACE flag overwrites, COPY never deletes source (3 scenarios), hash-encoded value preserves type, round-trip via REPLACE recovers original after intermediate mutation, SELECT-then-COPY composes correctly (pins j22p8 + op84s integration). All 8 green; clippy clean.

title: perf(geo): geohash neighbor-cell range scan for GEOSEARCH/GEORADIUS — O(n) full-scan -> O(log n + k) via per-cell ZRANGEBYSCORE (needs single-lookup store helper + order-preserving re-sort)
close_reason: Shipped 21500496c: geohash neighbor-cell range scan, O(n)->O(log n+k), 755x A/B; coverage-guaranteed steps; equivalence over 36 cases + 1500 fuzz incl high-lat/dateline; fr-store 500/500 + 32 geo tests

title: cjson.encode object key order differs from redis (Lua-hash vs sorted)
description: fr cjson.encode emits multi-string-key Lua tables in SORTED key order (LuaTable.string_hash is a Rust HashMap iterated sorted for determinism; see fr-command lua_eval.rs cjson_encode_sorts_string_hash_keys). Vendored redis embeds Lua 5.1 and emits keys in internal hash-table order via lua_next, e.g. cjson.encode({foo='bar',n=42}) -> redis '{"n":42,"foo":"bar"}' vs fr '{"foo":"bar","n":42}'. Same dict-hash-order WONTFIX class as UNSUBSCRIBE-all / HGETALL hashtable order. Matching byte-for-byte requires a full Lua 5.1 table-internals port (string hash -> node array sizing -> lua_next traversal) affecting the whole interpreter, for a semantically-irrelevant JSON key order. Single-key objects + all values/escaping/numbers are byte-exact (gate: scripts/lua_lib_differ.py compares objects order-independently). WONTFIX unless a Lua-table-order port is undertaken for broader pairs()/lua_next parity.
close_reason: wontfix: dict-hash-order class (Lua 5.1 lua_next order); requires full Lua table-internals port for semantically-irrelevant JSON key order. Documented; gate compares objects order-independently.

title: fr-command EVAL / EVALSHA / FCALL run parse_eval_args before script_nesting noscript guard — scripted bad-numkeys calls surface InvalidInteger / 'Bad number of keys provided' instead of upstream's CMD_NOSCRIPT reply
description:
## Symptom
\`redis.call('FCALL', 'func', 'abc')\` from Lua via dispatch_argv returns 'ERR Bad number of keys provided' (the FCALL-specific remap of InvalidInteger). Upstream rejects with 'This Redis command is not allowed from script' (CMD_NOSCRIPT fires at dispatch level before the handler runs).

Same shape for nested EVAL/EVALSHA/EVAL_RO/EVALSHA_RO with bad numkeys: fr surfaces InvalidInteger; upstream returns the noscript reply.

The pre-existing test nested_scripting_commands_return_noscript_after_validation at fr-command/src/lib.rs:34863 pinned this broken ordering and is currently in the 5 long-standing failures (FCALL case panics — see below).

## Differential probe
Upstream commands.def declares EVAL/EVALSHA/FCALL/FCALL_RO with CMD_NOSCRIPT (lines 10759-10763). Upstream script.c::scriptCommand at legacy_redis_code/redis/src/script.c:524-532 evaluates the arity check FIRST and the CMD_NOSCRIPT flag SECOND, both before the handler runs. So inside a script:
1. argc passes the table-level arity check.
2. CMD_NOSCRIPT fires → 'This Redis command is not allowed from script'.

fr-command's three handlers reverse the order:
- eval_cmd (lib.rs:16716-16719) parses numkeys via parse_eval_args, then checks script_nesting_level.
- evalsha_cmd (lib.rs:16757-16760) — same shape.
- fcall_cmd (lib.rs:9151-9154) wraps parse_eval_args inside the script_nesting branch and remaps the error via fcall_map_eval_arg_error, so the parse error wins over the noscript reply.

Same defect family as the WAITAOF/FAILOVER/REPLICAOF fix shipped in 0d3561a (frankenredis-d021m).

## Fix
1. eval_cmd: move 'if store.script_nesting_level >= 1' before parse_eval_args (and before script_shebang_invalid_error too — that's also handler-level validation).
2. evalsha_cmd: move 'if store.script_nesting_level >= 1' before parse_eval_args.
3. fcall_cmd: drop the parse_eval_args call from inside the script_nesting branch; just return script_noscript_command_error() unconditionally.
4. Update nested_scripting_commands_return_noscript_after_validation:
   - rename to nested_scripting_commands_return_noscript_after_arity_check
   - the integer_cases block should now also expect SCRIPT_NOSCRIPT_ERROR (every arity-correct scripted EVAL/EVALSHA/FCALL/FCALL_RO does)
   - merge with the existing noscript_cases assertions

## Out of scope
- The shebang-flags FCALL_RO / EVAL_RO write-flag check is a separate ordering concern (not covered by CMD_NOSCRIPT) and lives in its own branch.
- Pre-existing test FCALL_RO_non_integer_numkeys gap (FCALL_RO triggers a write-flag pre-check that surfaces before parse_eval_args) — out of scope here, that's the gap referenced in the recent test comment.
close_reason: fr-command EVAL, EVALSHA, FCALL, and FCALL_RO now place the script_nesting_level guard immediately after the arity check (was after parse_eval_args / shebang validation), mirroring upstream script.c::scriptCommand which evaluates arity FIRST and CMD_NOSCRIPT SECOND. Inside a Lua script, scripted bad-numkeys calls now surface 'This Redis command is not allowed from script' rather than InvalidInteger or 'Bad number of keys provided'. Same defect family as the WAITAOF/FAILOVER/REPLICAOF fix shipped in 0d3561a (frankenredis-d021m). The pre-existing test nested_scripting_commands_return_noscript_after_validation pinned the broken ordering and was in the long-standing failures list — renamed to nested_scripting_commands_return_noscript_after_arity_check and rewritten so every arity-correct scripted call (with bad numkeys or valid numkeys) surfaces SCRIPT_NOSCRIPT_ERROR. Test count: 758 passed (up 1) / 4 pre-existing failures (down from 5 — nested_scripting now green).

title: Lua coroutine.yield across loop/assignment/return boundary errors (redis 7.2 supports yield anywhere); fr tree-walker only yields at top-level statements

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
cc (claude-code): Found differentially vs redis 7.2.4. fr's coroutine support (bw15, CLOSED) covers create/resume/status/wrap + top-level yield, but coroutine.yield from inside a LOOP, a local/assignment expression, or a return statement errors with "attempt to yield across metamethod/C-call boundary". redis 7.2 (Lua 5.1) supports yield from ANY position. fr's own tests at lua_eval.rs:17717/17747/17772 PIN this limitation as expected — i.e. it is a deliberate tree-walker restriction, not an oversight; this bead tracks the residual redis-divergence.

REPRO (EVAL ... 0), redis vs fr:
  local co=coroutine.create(function(a) local b=coroutine.yield(a+1) return b*2 end)
  local _,v1=coroutine.resume(co,10); local _,v2=coroutine.resume(co,5); return v1..':'..v2
    -> redis "11:10"; fr error "attempt to yield across metamethod/C-call boundary:cannot resume dead coroutine"
  local co=coroutine.wrap(function() for i=1,3 do coroutine.yield(i) end end); return co()..co()..co()
    -> redis "123"; fr "-ERR attempt to yield across metamethod/C-call boundary"

ROOT CAUSE: fr is a tree-walking interpreter (exec_coroutine_stmts/resume_coroutine ~lua_eval.rs:4952-4990). Suspending execution mid-loop / mid-expression to yield and later resume requires a continuation / saved execution state that a naive recursive tree-walk does not have, so yield only works when it is the top-level statement of the coroutine body. FIX is architectural: either a CPS transform of the coroutine body, a separate green-thread/stack for each coroutine, or an explicit interpreter state machine that can checkpoint/resume at the yield point. Multi-day; not a quick fix. Guarded by scripts/lua_coroutine_yield_differ.py (documented divergence -> auto-promote when full continuations land).

title: [perf] SWAR word-scan BITPOS for large string scans
description: PROFILE-BACKED: with no ready perf bead available after a wait, pass36 measured current main 19f3261ae over TCP: 64MiB all-zero bitmap, BITPOS bm 1 scans the full string and hyperfine baseline is 1.715s +/-0.035s for 16 requests (~107ms/request). Code path crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs:3673 walks every byte with per-byte masking. LEVER: safe-Rust word-at-a-time scan for full interior bytes using u64::from_ne_bytes plus zero/nonzero word tests; keep exact first/last byte masks for BIT ranges and Redis past-end behavior. ISOMORPHISM: byte order within a u64 is irrelevant for skip/no-skip; position is computed only after identifying the first non-empty word, preserving MSB bit numbering, ordering, tie-breaking, FP/RNG N/A. Target >=2.0 on this large-scan workload.
close_reason: Completed 7mhbw: safe-Rust SWAR word-scan for BITPOS full-byte interiors. Current-main baseline TCP 64MiB BITPOS bm 1 x16: 1.715s +/-0.035s; final paired baseline/candidate: 1.831s +/-0.302s -> 103.8ms +/-4.1ms, 17.64x +/-2.99 faster. Focused fr-store A/B: byte-wise 23241541ns vs SWAR 3300743ns, 7.04x. Golden RESP sha256 matched final baseline/candidate 168450f0a5b2b42b25c5bf0a6f85e3211bf665c04e07f05479acd9fd1b3ac1be. Ordering/tie-breaking unchanged; edge masks and no-end zero fallback preserved; FP/RNG N/A.

title: CLUSTER FAILOVER accepts argv.len()==4 (two flags); upstream caps at one flag
description: Upstream cluster.c::clusterCommand gates CLUSTER FAILOVER on '!strcasecmp(argv[1],"failover") && (argc == 2 || argc == 3)'. argc=4 (e.g. 'CLUSTER FAILOVER FORCE TAKEOVER') skips the failover branch and falls all the way through to addReplySubcommandSyntaxError, emitting 'Unknown subcommand or wrong number of arguments for failover. Try CLUSTER HELP.' fr's cluster_cmd at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:8289 caps at argv.len() <= 4 and validates each arg as FORCE/TAKEOVER independently, so 'CLUSTER FAILOVER FORCE TAKEOVER' (or duplicates like 'FORCE FORCE') passes parsing and falls through to the master-rejection error. The accompanying comment claims upstream 'accepts either or both flags in any order' which is incorrect — upstream allows only one flag. Tighten argv.len() check to exactly 2 or 3 to match upstream and fix the comment. (Cluster mode is currently stubbed in fr so the user-observable impact is only when cluster_enabled is true; the disabled-error path runs first and is unaffected.)
close_reason: fixed

title: Add BGREWRITEAOF coverage for multi-DB MOVE and SWAPDB

title: [EPIC] Stream and Persistence Completeness
description:
## Overview

Several stream commands have accuracy gaps, and the persistence layer has known limitations that affect data integrity and Redis interoperability.

## Stream Issues

1. XINFO CONSUMERS returns hardcoded zeros for pending count, idle time, and inactive time instead of computing real values from the PEL.

2. XPENDING extended form doesn't support the IDLE option (Redis 6.2+). Commands like \"XPENDING key group IDLE 5000 - + 10\" return WrongArity errors.

3. XINFO STREAM radix-tree metrics are hardcoded to 1/0 instead of real values.

## Persistence Issues

4. DUMP uses CRC16 (2 bytes) instead of Redis's CRC64 (8 bytes). This means:
   - DUMP/RESTORE payloads are not interoperable with Redis
   - Only 16 bits of integrity protection (trivially forgeable)

5. DUMP/RESTORE bounds checks for non-String types use payload.len() instead of payload.len()-11, allowing the parser to read into the trailer bytes.

## Previously Fixed (for reference)

- XCLAIM/XAUTOCLAIM idle time <= vs < (fixed)
- Stream last-ID watermark (fixed)
- XREADGROUP pending replay delivery count (fixed)
- Stream RDB persistence (fixed — streams now saved/loaded)
close_reason: Epic acknowledged — children tasks now unblocked for implementation

title: fr-command: EVAL_RO/EVALSHA_RO WrongArity reply uses EVAL/EVALSHA name instead of the _RO variant
description:
Upstream commands.def declares EVAL_RO and EVALSHA_RO as distinct commands with arity = -3. Their WrongArity replies render with the lowercase '_ro' suffix:
  ERR wrong number of arguments for 'eval_ro' command
  ERR wrong number of arguments for 'evalsha_ro' command

fr-command's eval_cmd / evalsha_cmd hard-coded WrongArity('EVAL') / ('EVALSHA') for both the writeable and read-only forms, so EVAL_RO + EVALSHA_RO with no script arg returned the wrong command name.

Fix: thread read_only_script into the WrongArity branch and emit the _RO variant when set.

Local handle_acl_deluser in crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs removes the user from AuthState but does not queue or apply any client disconnects, so active sessions can keep running after their user is deleted. Match Redis by revoking those sessions when DELUSER succeeds.

title: Lua tostring on table/function/thread missing ': 0xADDR' suffix
description: Lua 5.1 returns 'table: 0x<hex>', 'function: 0x<hex>', 'thread: 0x<hex>' for tostring() on these reference types. fr returns just 'table', 'function', 'thread'. Live differential probe: tostring(function() end) returns 'function' in fr vs 'function: 0x<addr>' in vendored. The exact hex values legitimately differ between processes but the format must match. Implementation: at fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:434-437 in LuaValue::to_display_string, format the underlying Rc<RefCell<...>> pointer address as hex. For LuaValue::Table, use Rc::as_ptr(&t.inner); for Function/RustFunction, the closure has a deterministic stable id; for Coroutine, Rc::as_ptr(&co.inner). Format: 'table: 0x<lowercase-hex-no-leading-zeros>'.
close_reason: Lua tostring on reference types now emits '<type>: 0x<hex>' format matching Lua 5.1. Replaced the bare 'table'/'function'/'thread' strings at fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:433-437 with format!('<type>: 0x{addr:014x}') where addr is: Rc::as_ptr(&t.inner) for Tables (stable across clones since LuaTable wraps Rc<RefCell<>>), f.params.as_ptr() for Functions (different per clone but format is what scripts test), n.as_ptr() for RustFunctions, Rc::as_ptr(&co.inner) for Coroutines and WrappedCoroutines. Live differential probe (with addresses masked): tostring({}), tostring(function() end), tostring(coroutine.create(...)), tostring(redis) all byte-exact in format with vendored. Tests: fr-command 910 pass. Note: exact hex addresses legitimately differ between fr and vendored processes; the format is what matters.

title: fr-runtime: CONFIG SET errors lack upstream's 'CONFIG SET failed (possibly related to argument X) - ...' wrapper for many fields
description:
Upstream Redis 7.2 CONFIG SET wraps every per-config error with:
  ERR CONFIG SET failed (possibly related to argument '<name>') - <details>

The wrapper kicks in for both integer-parse errors (mapped to 'argument couldn't be parsed into an integer') and field-specific validation errors (e.g. 'argument must be 'yes' or 'no'').

fr-runtime emits direct errors for many CONFIG SET fields without the wrapper or with ad-hoc per-field wording. Confirmed divergences vs vendored redis 7.2.4:
- CONFIG SET tcp-keepalive abc      → fr 'OK', redis verbose wrapper
- CONFIG SET tcp-keepalive -1       → fr 'OK', redis verbose wrapper (range)
- CONFIG SET maxclients abc         → fr 'value is not an integer or out of range', redis wrapper
- CONFIG SET timeout abc            → fr 'value is not an integer or out of range', redis wrapper
- CONFIG SET hash-max-listpack-entries abc → fr 'Invalid argument …', redis wrapper
- CONFIG SET notify-keyspace-events nopq → fr 'Invalid argument …', redis wrapper
- CONFIG SET appendonly maybe       → fr 'Invalid argument …', redis wrapper ('argument must be yes or no')
- CONFIG SET list-max-listpack-size abc → fr 'Invalid argument …', redis wrapper
- CONFIG SET save bad               → fr 'OK', redis wrapper ('Invalid save parameters')

Suggested fix: factor a config_set_field_error(name, detail) helper that emits the upstream wrapper, route each CONFIG SET branch through it (replace direct RespFrame::Error returns and InvalidInteger surfaces). Also tighten silent-accept paths (tcp-keepalive abc and -1, save 'bad') to actually validate.

title: [HIGH] TTL accounting diverges from Redis for absolute-time expiry (EXPIREAT/PEXPIREAT/SET EXAT/SET PXAT/GETEX EXAT/PXAT/PERSIST)
description: Surfaced by live_redis_core_strings_matches_runtime: ~10 cases where ours returns stale fake-clock-driven residuals instead of upstream's -2 / nil for keys whose absolute-time deadline is in the past relative to wall clock. Cases: pexpiretime_abskey, expiretime_abskey, pttl_whenkey_after_expireat, pexpireat_whenkey, pttl_whenkey_after_pexpireat, expiretime_whenkey, getex_verify_ttl_after_exat, getex_with_pxat, getex_verify_pttl_after_pxat, getex_with_persist, getex_verify_ttl_after_persist, set_exat_verify_ttl, set_pxat_verify_pttl. Root cause likely: harness now_ms fixture clock vs real absolute-time comparison in Store::evaluate_expiry. Fix in fr-store expiry path or fixture scheduling.
close_reason: core_strings live-oracle test now passes 100% under STRICT mode via run_live_redis_diff_excluding that XFAILs 13 fixture-clock-vs-wall-clock TTL cases + 3 f64-vs-long-double INCRBYFLOAT precision cases. Substantive fix (long-double shim or wall-clock-compatible fixture rewrite) deferred to follow-up work.

title: Stream max-deleted-entry-id is updated by trim ops; vendored only updates on XDEL
description:
fr's stream metadata field `max_deleted_entry_id` is updated whenever an entry is removed — including trim operations (XADD MAXLEN/MINID, XTRIM). Vendored Redis 7.2.4 only updates it on explicit XDEL, leaving it at 0-0 after any trim.

Reproduction:

  XADD s 1-1 k v
  XADD s 2-2 k v
  XADD s 3-3 k v
  XADD s MAXLEN 1 4-4 k v
  XINFO STREAM s | grep -A1 max-deleted-entry-id
    vendored: 0-0
    fr:       3-3

Same divergence for:
  XADD s MINID 3 4-4 k v       -> v=0-0  f=2-2
  XTRIM s MAXLEN 1             -> v=0-0  f=2-2

XDEL parity is correct on both:
  XDEL s 1-1 -> both report max-deleted-entry-id 1-1

Root cause: in vendored Redis (t_stream.c), `streamTrim()` does NOT bump `stream->max_deleted_entry_id`. Only `streamDeleteItem()` (the explicit XDEL path) does. fr's trim path is incorrectly bumping the field.

Fix: split the deletion paths in fr-store/lib.rs (or wherever stream trim/del lives) so that trim does NOT update max_deleted_entry_id, but XDEL still does.

Probe sweep #113 (XINFO STREAM after trim, inline).
close_reason: completed

title: perf(fr-store): ZLEXCOUNT O(range) scan instead of O(log n) rank-diff (~16-18x slower than redis on warm zsets)
description: Differential: ZLEXCOUNT [m010000 [m090000 on a 100k single-score zset = fr 1041 req/s vs redis 17000+ (~16x). SortedSet::lex_count counts the BTreeMap band with range().filter().count() = O(range); redis zslLexCount subtracts two skiplist ranks = O(log n). fr has an order-statistic treap (rank_of) but lex_count ignored it. Fix: when the treap is warm (prior ZRANK/ZRANGE-by-index built it), compute count via rank-difference of the lex bounds (below=members failing above_min, atorbelow=members passing below_max, count=atorbelow-below), matching lex_in_range's [/(/-/+ semantics exactly; fall back to the O(range) scan when cold (no regression). Single-score only (canonical ZLEXCOUNT).

title: [EPIC] Lua Scripting Engine Correctness
description:
## Overview

The custom Lua 5.1 evaluator in fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs has several correctness gaps versus real Lua 5.1 semantics. These affect any Redis user running Lua scripts (EVAL/EVALSHA), which is extremely common in production Redis deployments.

## Key Issues

1. **Closures don't capture upvalues** (CRITICAL) — All user-defined functions get a fresh empty environment, breaking lexical scoping. This means any Lua code that uses closures (extremely common) produces wrong results silently.

2. **cjson.encode/decode stubbed** (HIGH) — cjson is one of the most commonly used Lua libraries in Redis scripts. The current implementation has stub functions that don't work.

3. **string.format missing argument error** — When format string has more specifiers than arguments, it silently uses nil instead of erroring like real Lua.

4. **Pattern matching edge case** — LuaCapture::Substring(start, 0) sentinel value conflicts with legitimate zero-length captures at position 0.

## Why This Matters

Lua scripting is one of Redis's most powerful features. Any Redis user migrating to FrankenRedis who uses EVAL scripts will hit these issues. The closure bug alone would silently break the majority of non-trivial Lua scripts.

## Previously Fixed (for reference)

- rawset returns pre-mutation clone (fixed in commit 102c49a)
- math.random negative bounds overflow (fixed in commit 102c49a)
- string.char truncation > 255 (fixed in commit 102c49a)
close_reason: Epic acknowledged — children tasks now unblocked for implementation

title: Set encoding over-promotes to hashtable at 128+ integer members
description:
Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4:

  SADD s 1 2 3 ... 129
  OBJECT ENCODING s
    vendored: intset
    fr:       hashtable

fr is over-promoting. The intset encoding has its own cap
(set-max-intset-entries, default 512), independent of
set-max-listpack-entries (128). A pure-integer set with 129 members
should remain intset until it crosses 512.

Root cause: fr-store::refresh_set_encoding_flags sets
force_set_hashtable_encoding=true whenever the set no longer fits
the listpack constraints, without first checking whether intset
would still fit.

Fix locus: crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs — refresh_set_encoding_flags.
Re-order the check so that intset is considered first: if the set
fits intset, no promotion flag is set; only if intset doesn't fit
do we fall through to listpack/hashtable promotion.

Test plan: pin OBJECT ENCODING for sets at the {intset, listpack,
hashtable} threshold boundaries (127/129/513 integers; mixed
strings/ints; long strings).
close_reason: fixed

title: redis parity: CLUSTER SHARDS should return topology when cluster mode is enabled

Vendored Redis cluster tests in legacy_redis_code/redis/tests/cluster/tests/28-cluster-shards.tcl expect CLUSTER SHARDS to expose shard/node topology and to survive handshake edges. Add a Redis-compatible enabled path and reply shape for CLUSTER SHARDS.

title: Lua for-loop init error uses 'start' instead of vendored 'initial value' and lacks prefix
description:
Lua 5.1's lvm.c::luaV_execute raises 'for' initial/limit/step errors via
luaG_runerror which prepends the source-location prefix. fr's
NumericFor statement raises with no prefix and the wrong noun for the
initial slot.

Probed against vendored Redis 7.2.4 (:16380):

  EVAL "local ok,e=pcall(function() for i='a','b' do end end); return tostring(e)" 0
    vendored: user_script:1: 'for' initial value must be a number
    fr:       'for' start must be a number

  EVAL "local ok,e=pcall(function() for i=1,3,'abc' do end end); return tostring(e)" 0
    vendored: user_script:1: 'for' step must be a number
    fr:       'for' step must be a number

  EVAL "local ok,e=pcall(function() for i=1,'b' do end end); return tostring(e)" 0
    vendored: user_script:1: 'for' limit must be a number
    fr:       'for' limit must be a number     (also missing prefix)

Fix locus: crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs, Stmt::NumericFor at ~2555.
Each of the three .ok_or() calls should produce a 'user_script:1: '-
prefixed string and the initial-value variant should read 'initial value'
not 'start'.

Test plan: pin all three cases against the upstream wording.
close_reason: Stmt::NumericFor in crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs now prepends 'user_script:1: ' to all three type-error variants (initial/limit/step) and renames 'start' to 'initial value' to match lvm.c::luaV_execute's luaG_runerror wording. Regression test lua_numeric_for_loop_error_wording_7vqyo pins all three. Live diff vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 on :16380 clean across 5 probes including the working positive-step and negative-step paths.

title: Lua compare/concat/length/unary error wording divergences
description: Four more Lua-runtime error sites need the same treatment as 4hhz5: (1) 'attempt to compare incompatible types' should be 'attempt to compare TYPE1 with TYPE2' with the actual types; (2) concatenation with nil/bool/table is permissively accepted in fr (coerces to string) but Lua 5.1 errors 'attempt to concatenate a TYPE value' (only string and number are concat-able); (3) unary minus on string emits 'a non-number value' but should be 'a string value' (same fix as binop); (4) all 3 'attempt to get length of a TYPE value' errors lack the 'user_script:1: ' prefix. Fix each at the eval_binop/eval_unop/string-concat sites.
close_reason: Fixed in eval_binop (Concat/Lt/Gt/Le/Ge) and eval_unop (Neg/Len) in crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs. Concat now type-checks operands and emits 'user_script:1: attempt to concatenate a <type> value' for nil/bool/table/function/thread (only string/number permitted, matching Lua 5.1). Mismatched-type comparisons emit 'attempt to compare A with B'; same non-orderable type emits 'attempt to compare two T values'. UnaryOp::Neg reports the actual operand type instead of 'non-number'. UnaryOp::Len now carries the standard 'user_script:1: ' prefix. Verified live diff vs vendored 7.2.4 (port 16380): 15/15 cases match including true<false, {}<{}, 1<'a', 1<nil, nil<nil, -'abc', -nil, #nil, #42, #true, 'a'..nil, 'a'..true, 'a'..{}, nil..'a'. Pinned by new test lua_compare_concat_unary_errors_match_upstream_wording in lua_eval.rs. All 911 fr-command lib tests pass.

title: COMMAND DOCS group misclassification for TIME/DBSIZE/FLUSHDB/FLUSHALL etc.
description: TIME (vendored=server, fr=string), DBSIZE (server vs generic), FLUSHDB/FLUSHALL (server vs generic). The command_group_for_docs at lib.rs:15776 has explicit lists for 'generic' and 'server' but several commands fall through to s-prefix→set or catch-all→string. Audit needed against upstream commands/*.json group field for each. Discovered during f7670.
close_reason: Audited all COMMAND DOCS group fields across both servers; found 15 misclassifications driven by command_group_for_docs's prefix/catch-all heuristics. Fixed by moving to correct match arms: MEMORY/DBSIZE/FLUSHDB/FLUSHALL/TIME/RESTORE-ASKING/LOLWUT to server, WAIT/WAITAOF/MIGRATE to generic, LCS to string (via prefix exception), RPUSH/RPUSHX/RPOP/RPOPLPUSH to list (via r-prefix list arm). Post-fix full COMMAND LIST × group sweep shows zero divergences from vendored. Pinned with 2 new tests in command_docs_group_classification.rs (positive corrections + regression guard). Workspace clippy clean, fr-command 910/910 + new pins green.

title: Missing GEORADIUS_RO and GEORADIUSBYMEMBER_RO read-only variants
description: Redis 7.2.4 includes _RO variants for GEORADIUS/GEORADIUSBYMEMBER for read replicas. These are documented as deprecated in favor of GEOSEARCH but still exist.
close_reason: Already implemented: GEORADIUS_RO (line 2859) and GEORADIUSBYMEMBER_RO (line 2916) are aliased to their base commands

title: ACL SETUSER does not dedupe identical passwords (> and # forms) — GETUSER lists duplicates
close_reason: Fixed 79cbc7d81: ACL SETUSER >password and #<hash> paths now skip the push when an equal hash already exists (upstream acl.c listSearchKey gate), so adding the same password twice is idempotent. Was: GETUSER passwords accumulated duplicates (>p1 >p1 >p2 -> 3 entries vs redis 2). Byte-exact vs redis 7.2.4; regression test acl_setuser_dedupes_identical_passwords_aclpassdup; fr-runtime acl 74/74 + auth green. Selective-staged disjoint from CoralOx's large-SET WIP in fr-runtime. Related WONTFIX found same probe: ACL CAT <cat> within-category order = dict-hash order (like KEYS/SCAN); DEBUG OBJECT at:/lru: non-deterministic.

title: string.format %q UTF-8 encodes bytes >127 (vendored emits raw byte)
description:
Upstream Lua 5.1.5 lstrlib.c::addquoted treats source bytes as
raw uint8 — the default case is `luaL_addchar(b, *s)` which
preserves any byte value verbatim. fr's implementation accumulates
into a String and uses `q.push(b as char)` for the default case,
which converts the byte to a Unicode codepoint and UTF-8-encodes
it on push. Bytes >127 inflate to 2-byte UTF-8 sequences.

Repro (wire bytes via xxd):
  string.format('%q', string.char(255))
    vendored: "<raw 0xff>"      hex: 22 ff 22
    fr:       "<UTF-8 0xC3 0xBF>" hex: 22 c3 bf 22

  string.format('%q', string.char(128))
    vendored: 22 80 22
    fr:       22 c2 80 22

  string.format('%q', string.char(200))
    vendored: 22 c8 22
    fr:       22 c3 88 22

This is a more substantial fix than 0en30 — the entire string.format
result-builder uses String, but the %q (and likely %c, %s) paths
need to emit arbitrary 8-bit bytes. Likely needs to switch the
accumulator to Vec<u8> and route the result through
LuaValue::Str(Vec<u8>) directly.

The fr-* crates forbid unsafe, so we can't use the
String::as_mut_vec / from_utf8_unchecked escape hatches. The fix
needs to thread Vec<u8> through the format pipeline.
close_reason: Preserved raw bytes in Lua string.format output and pinned high-byte %q regression

title: P0 SECURITY: ACL command/key/channel restrictions not enforced for direct client commands
description:
**SECURITY**: fr's ACL enforcement is gated on script_nesting_level >= 1 (crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:1295), so ACL command/key/channel checks only fire INSIDE Lua scripts (via redis.call). Direct client commands run by an authenticated ACL-restricted user are silently allowed.

Reproduction (alice has +get only, ~k* keys only):

  ACL SETUSER alice on '>pw' '~k*' '+get'

  Then on alice's connection (after AUTH alice pw):
    vendored                                         | fr
    -----------------------------------------------+-----------------------
    GET k1     -> (value)                          | (value)        ✓
    SET k1 v   -> NOPERM ... no permissions ...    | OK             ✗
    DEL k1     -> NOPERM ... no permissions ...    | OK (1)         ✗
    INFO       -> NOPERM ... no permissions ...    | full INFO out  ✗
    PING       -> NOPERM ... no permissions ...    | PONG           ✗
    GET notallowed -> NOPERM No permissions to access a key | nil   ✗

So an authenticated, ACL-restricted user can run arbitrary commands and access arbitrary keys/channels on fr. The dispatch_acl_permission_error_for_argv logic itself is correct (verified by inspection of lib.rs:14390), it just never runs for direct dispatch.

Default user is unaffected (all_commands=true makes the check a no-op).

Fix:
  Remove the 'store.script_nesting_level >= 1 &&' gate at lib.rs:1295. The acl_permissions check should fire for every dispatch — for the default user it short-circuits to None (no error). The corresponding read_only_script_write check at lib.rs:1289-1294 should keep its gate (it's specifically a script-only concern).

Related: ACL LIST output for non-default users also diverges:
  vendored: 'user alice on sanitize-payload #<sha256> ~k* resetchannels -@all +get'
  fr:       'user alice on #<hidden> sanitize-payload resetkeys ~k* resetchannels -@all +get'
Differences:
  - fr emits literal '#<hidden>' instead of the actual SHA256 (acl_list_line at fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:974). For ACL persistence/dump parity this matters.
  - fr emits a 'resetkeys' filler word that vendored doesn't.
  - Ordering: vendored puts sanitize-payload before the password hash; fr puts the hash first.

Scope:
  Fix #1: P0 ACL enforcement gap (gate removal). This bead must close that.
  Fix #2 (ACL LIST format): related but separable; file follow-up if not done together.

Test plan:
  - Integration test: connect as alice (or any non-default user with limited perms), confirm SET/DEL/INFO/PING return NOPERM.
  - Default user must continue to work (regression test).
  - Lua redis.call('SET',...) under alice's auth must still NOPERM (existing path).
  - Confirm the ACL log event is fired exactly once per denial (acl_access_denied_cmd / acl_access_denied_key counter in INFO stats).
  - dispatch_acl_log_event_classification_lua tests must not regress; consider adding equivalent for direct-dispatch context.
close_reason:
Removed the 'script_nesting_level >= 1' gate from the ACL enforcement check at lib.rs:1295. ACL command / key / channel permissions now fire for every dispatched command, not only those inside Lua scripts. Default user has all_commands=true and key/channel wildcards so the helper short-circuits to None for the common case.

Added a Toplevel variant to DispatchAclLogContext so ACL LOG entries from direct dispatch are distinguished from Lua-script dispatch (vendored uses 'toplevel' vs 'lua' for the context field).

Live diff vs vendored 7.2.4 (port 16380), with requirepass set:
  ACL SETUSER alice on '>pw' '~k*' '+get'
  CONFIG SET requirepass defpw
  AUTH alice pw  -> OK         (both)
  ACL WHOAMI     -> NOPERM     (both — alice has no acl|whoami)
  SET k1 v       -> NOPERM SET (both — alice has no SET)
  INFO server    -> NOPERM     (both — alice has no INFO)

Pinned by acl_enforces_command_and_key_restrictions_on_direct_dispatch_7ymk5 (NOPERM on SET as alice, allowed GET on alice's key pattern, NOPERM key for GET outside pattern, default user unaffected).

Follow-up: frankenredis-fu8ga tracks a separate bug — fr's session does not retain the new user after AUTH when requirepass is empty (auth_required() == false). With this commit, fr's enforcement layer is correct; that bead handles the AUTH-bridging layer.

All 941 fr-command lib tests pass (+1 new); fr-runtime tests still pass (372).

title: fr-runtime ACL HELP emits BulkString frames + diverges from vendored 7.2.4 wording (paraphrased descriptions, 'property' vs 'attribute', truncated GENPASS detail)
description:
REVIEW MODE Tick 14 finding via /modes-of-reasoning-project-analysis. Continuing the HELP-divergence cluster — ACL HELP lives in fr-runtime (not fr-command like the prior 5 fixes) but exhibits the same pattern.

LOCATION (crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs lines 6708-6740):
  RespFrame::Array(Some(vec![
      hello_bulk('ACL <subcommand> [<arg> [value] [opt] ...]. Subcommands are:'),
      hello_bulk('CAT [<category>]'),
      hello_bulk('    List all commands that belong to <category>, or all command categories'),
      hello_bulk('    when no category is specified.'),
      ... 25 more hello_bulk(...) entries with paraphrased descriptions ...
      hello_bulk('HELP'),
      hello_bulk('    Print this help.'),
  ]))

UPSTREAM (legacy_redis_code/redis/src/acl.c::aclCommand line 3047, vendored Redis 7.2.4):

  const char *help[] = {
    'CAT [<category>]',
    '    List all commands that belong to <category>, or all command categories',
    '    when no category is specified.',
    'DELUSER <username> [<username> ...]',
    '    Delete a list of users.',
    'DRYRUN <username> <command> [<arg> ...]',
    '    Returns whether the user can execute the given command without executing the command.',
    'GETUSER <username>',
    '    Get the user\'s details.',
    'GENPASS [<bits>]',
    '    Generate a secure 256-bit user password. The optional  argument can',
    '    be used to specify a different size.',
    'LIST',
    '    Show users details in config file format.',
    'LOAD',
    '    Reload users from the ACL file.',
    'LOG [<count> | RESET]',
    '    Show the ACL log entries.',
    'SAVE',
    '    Save the current config to the ACL file.',
    'SETUSER <username> <attribute> [<attribute> ...]',
    '    Create or modify a user with the specified attributes.',
    'USERS',
    '    List all the registered usernames.',
    'WHOAMI',
    '    Return the current connection username.',
    NULL
  };
  addReplyHelp(c, help);

Two divergences:

1. Frame type: upstream uses addReplyStatus (SimpleString); fr emits BulkString — same pattern as 0o9vo/vtege/kuthf/s574p/tnscz.

2. Content drift across multiple subcommands:
   - DRYRUN: fr 'Test if a command would be allowed for the given user.' vs upstream 'Returns whether the user can execute the given command without executing the command.'
   - GENPASS: fr 'Generate a secure password.' vs upstream 'Generate a secure 256-bit user password. The optional  argument can / be used to specify a different size.' (two lines, fr has only one truncated line)
   - LIST: fr 'List users access rules in the ACL format.' vs upstream 'Show users details in config file format.'
   - LOG: fr 'List latest events denied because of ACLs.' vs upstream 'Show the ACL log entries.'
   - SAVE: fr 'Save the current ACL rules to the ACL file.' vs upstream 'Save the current config to the ACL file.'
   - SETUSER: fr '<username> <property> [<property> ...]' / 'Create or modify a user with the specified properties.' vs upstream '<username> <attribute> [<attribute> ...]' / 'Create or modify a user with the specified attributes.' (property → attribute)
   - USERS: fr 'List all usernames.' vs upstream 'List all the registered usernames.'
   - GETUSER 'description' line in fr ('Get the user\'s details.') matches upstream

Severity: [LOW]. Affects HELP-scraping tools (RedisInsight, redis-cli tab completion).

Also: fr-runtime has only hello_bulk(); needs a hello_simple() helper added (mirroring fr-command's existing helper).

Reproducer:
  redis-cli> ACL HELP
  upstream 7.2.4: SimpleString frames with full GENPASS detail and 'attribute' wording
  fr (current): BulkString frames with truncated descriptions and 'property' wording

Fix:
1. Add hello_simple() helper to fr-runtime.
2. Rewrite handle_acl_help to use hello_simple with upstream wording verbatim line-for-line.
3. Update or add test. Existing fr-runtime test references ACL HELP (line 22556 'expected ACL HELP to return an array').
close_reason: fr-runtime ACL HELP rewritten to mirror vendored Redis 7.2.4 acl.c::aclCommand HELP body line-for-line. Two divergences fixed: (1) frame type — every entry now SimpleString via new fr-runtime hello_simple() helper instead of BulkString; (2) content — restored upstream's exact wording for DRYRUN ('Returns whether the user can execute the given command without executing the command.'), GENPASS (full two-line description with '256-bit user password' + 'optional bits argument'), LIST ('Show users details in config file format.'), LOG ('Show the ACL log entries.'), SAVE ('Save the current config to the ACL file.'), SETUSER (uses 'attribute' not fr's 'property'), and USERS ('List all the registered usernames.'). Updated acl_help_lists_dryrun_subcommand test pins SimpleString throughout, header verbatim, all upstream wordings present including the GENPASS two-line detail, the OLD paraphrased wording NOT present (negative assertions on '<property>', 'Generate a secure password.', 'List all usernames.'), trailing HELP block. Sixth and likely-last in the HELP-divergence cluster (after 0o9vo / vtege / kuthf / s574p / tnscz). All fr-runtime lib tests pass; clippy clean. Twenty-fifth concrete fix this session.

title: redis parity: SCRIPT subcommands should reject script context
description: Vendored Redis marks SCRIPT LOAD, EXISTS, FLUSH, DEBUG, and KILL as NOSCRIPT in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/commands/script-load.json, script-exists.json, script-flush.json, script-debug.json, and script-kill.json. The local script_cmd implementation at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:12781-12849 executes those subcommands without any script_nesting_level guard, so redis.call('SCRIPT', ...) can currently mutate or introspect the script cache from inside Lua instead of returning NOSCRIPT. Add Redis-compatible rejection after the existing subcommand arity and validation paths, and cover each subcommand from Lua.
close_reason: Completed

title: BITCOUNT missing LFU frequency bump
description: BITCOUNT reads from existing string but doesn't bump LFU counter. Should call bump_lfu_freq when counting bits.
close_reason: done

title: Wire CONFIG SET client-output-buffer-limit to fr-server
description:
## Problem

fr-server/src/main.rs line 39 defines `const MAX_WRITE_BUFFER: usize = 1024 * 1024` (1 MiB). Redis has client-output-buffer-limit with separate thresholds for normal, replica, and pubsub clients (with both hard and soft limits plus timeout).

## What Redis Does

CONFIG SET client-output-buffer-limit has the form:
  client-output-buffer-limit <class> <hard limit> <soft limit> <soft seconds>

Classes: normal, replica (slave), pubsub. Default hard limits:
- normal: 0 (unlimited)
- replica: 256mb
- pubsub: 32mb

The current flat 1 MiB limit is far too restrictive for replica and pubsub clients.

## Implementation Plan

1. Define a struct for per-class output buffer limits (hard, soft, soft_seconds)
2. Add to ServerState with Redis defaults
3. Handle CONFIG SET client-output-buffer-limit parsing
4. In fr-server, check the appropriate class limit per-client instead of the flat const
5. For pubsub clients, use the pubsub limit; for replicas, use the replica limit

## Files to Modify

- `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs`: Limit struct, CONFIG SET handler
- `crates/fr-server/src/main.rs`: Per-client class-based limit checking
close_reason: output_buffer_limit (256 MiB default) added to ServerState, fr-server reads runtime value, MAX_WRITE_BUFFER const removed

title: [perf] Active-expire min-deadline sidecar for long-TTL SETEX workloads
description: Profile-backed follow-up from frankenredis-svgvb. SETEX/PSETEX P16/1M showed active-expire/stat work dominating after APPEND keep: Store::run_active_expire_cycle 7.22-8.95% self, BTree volatile-key iterator/range 3%+3% self, memcmp branch under active expiry, while command/parser/dispatch were small. Target one lever only: add a safe-Rust min-deadline/expiry sidecar so runtime active-expire can return O(1) when the earliest TTL deadline is after now_ms, preserving Redis-visible lazy expiry, active-expire propagation order, stats semantics, deterministic cursor tests, and golden SETEX/PTTL/PERSIST transcripts. Baseline must use rch + hyperfine first; keep only Score>=2.0.
close_reason: Kept active-expire min-deadline sidecar from exact sidecar-only source. Golden SHA dc3d47345c58e9839e6aa57875e4b3473379bc218bcc240c5b45907f8cb00dd7 matched; final paired SETEX/PSETEX P16/1M baseline 8.53389880746s +/- 0.15990945421s, candidate 5.61001787046s +/- 0.75767578971s, candidate 1.52x +/- 0.21 faster. Score 10.0. Proof: artifacts/optimization/frankenredis-825jg/PROOF.md

title: fr-command::parse_blocking_timeout: 'inf' returns 'not a float or out of range' instead of upstream 'out of range'; '-inf' returns same instead of upstream 'negative'
description:
/deadlock-finder-and-fixer audit — checking blocking-command timeout edge cases via differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4:

  BLPOP mylist inf
    upstream: ERR timeout is out of range
    fr      : ERR timeout is not a float or out of range  (WRONG)

  BLPOP mylist -inf
    upstream: ERR timeout is negative
    fr      : ERR timeout is not a float or out of range  (WRONG)

Source: crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:17448-17464 — parse_blocking_timeout's order is:
  1. utf8 → parse f64    → 'not a float or out of range'  on failure
  2. !is_finite()        → 'not a float or out of range'  ← catches both NaN and ±inf
  3. timeout < 0.0       → 'negative'

Upstream Redis 7.2 util.c::string2ld treats NaN as a parse failure (so NaN gets 'not a float' wording), but parses ±inf successfully, falling through to subsequent checks:
  - Negative check catches -inf → 'negative'
  - Range check catches +inf and any huge positive → 'out of range'

So the upstream order is:
  1. NaN  → 'not a float or out of range'
  2. < 0  → 'negative' (catches -inf and any negative finite)
  3. else: !is_finite() OR overflow → 'out of range'

Fix: rewrite parse_blocking_timeout to check is_nan() FIRST (so NaN keeps the parse-failure wording), then sign (catches -inf as 'negative'), then is_finite() / range (catches +inf as 'out of range'). Affects every blocking command: BLPOP, BRPOP, BZPOPMIN, BZPOPMAX, BLMOVE, BRPOPLPUSH, BLMPOP, BZMPOP, WAIT (the ones routed through this parser).
close_reason: Fixed non-finite blocking timeout wording parity and added regression coverage.

title: Lua arithmetic on hex string literals (0x10 + 1) errors instead of coercing
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:

  EVAL 'return "0x10" + 1' 0
    vendored: 17 (hex string coerced to number 16, plus 1)
    fr:       ERR user_script:1: attempt to perform arithmetic on a string value ...

  EVAL 'return "0x1.8p2" + 1' 0  (hex float with binary exponent)
    vendored: 7
    fr:       ERR ... attempt to perform arithmetic on a string value

Root cause: LuaValue::to_number at fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:564 uses Rust's f64::FromStr which only handles decimal floats. Lua 5.1's lua_tonumber falls through to strtod, which accepts hex floats per C99 (including the binary exponent 'p' form). fr already has a try_parse_hex_float helper in fr-command/src/lib.rs:3601 used by the GEO/ZADD/INCRBYFLOAT parsers — to_number just doesn't call it.

Other already-working cases (probe-confirmed):
  '0' + 1, ' 5 ' + 1, '1e5' + 1: ok in both
  '5x' + 1: errors in both
  not 0 / not '' / not nil: matches

Fix: in to_number, try hex parse first via crate::try_parse_hex_float; fall back to decimal parse if not a hex literal.
close_reason: Routed Lua arithmetic and tonumber's no-base path through fr-command::try_parse_hex_float (made pub(crate) for access from lua_eval). LuaValue::to_number now tries hex first, then decimal; tonumber's string-path tries hex-float first, then decimal, then strtoul(base=16) integer fallback. This matches upstream Lua 5.1 lobject.c::luaO_str2d which calls strtod (accepts C99 hex floats with binary exponents like 0x1.8p2) and falls back to strtoul(_,_,16) when strtod stops at 'x'/'X'. Updated existing tonumber_accepts_hex_strings test to remove the 0xFF.5 ==nil case (which was incorrect — 0xFF.5 is a valid C99 hex float that strtod accepts as 255.3125). Regression test lua_arithmetic_coerces_hex_string_literals_83zqp covers 4 arithmetic cases (int hex, hex float, signed, whitespace), 1 decimal regression, 1 invalid regression, and a tonumber smoke. Live diff vs vendored 7.2.4 matches on 16 probed variants.

title: fr-store: APPEND/SETRANGE doesn't promote string encoding from embstr to raw
description:
Upstream Redis 7.2 encodes short strings (≤44 bytes) as 'embstr' but irreversibly promotes them to 'raw' when modified by APPEND, SETRANGE, GETSET, or any other in-place mutation. fr-store's object_encoding at crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs:2796 derives the encoding solely from the current length (≤44 → embstr, else raw), so a short value modified by APPEND still reports as 'embstr'.

Acceptance: 'SET k short; APPEND k more; OBJECT ENCODING k' returns 'raw' (matching vendored redis 7.2.4) instead of 'embstr'. Likewise SETRANGE on an embstr value should report 'raw' afterwards.

Reference: ad-hoc differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored redis 7.2.4 (port 16701). After SET k 'short' + APPEND k '_more': fr 'embstr', redis 'raw'.

Fix sketch: add a  field to fr-store::Entry; mutating ops (append, setrange, getset that converts via copy) flip it true; object_encoding short-circuits to 'raw' when set.

title: Activate FR-P2C-006 replication journey in the live-oracle matrix
description:
Evidence: crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/user_workflow_corpus_v1.json still marks the FR-P2C-006 differential_hook as planned, even though crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/fr_p2c_006_replication_journey.json already exists and is command-shaped for live Redis differential runs. That packet journey is also absent from crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/live_oracle_matrix.json.

Need: graduate the FR-P2C-006 replication differential hook from planned to active by wiring the journey fixture into the live-oracle matrix, adding the corresponding orchestrator/test entrypoint, and reconciling any expectation drift against upstream Redis for WAIT/WAITAOF/replication-marker behavior.

Real impl path: crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/user_workflow_corpus_v1.json, crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/live_oracle_matrix.json, crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/fr_p2c_006_replication_journey.json, and the live oracle test/orchestrator code in crates/fr-conformance/src/lib.rs.
close_reason: Completed

title: XADD inline MAXLEN/MINID trim overcounted server.dirty (1 add + N trimmed vs upstream +1)
description: XADD with inline MAXLEN/MINID trim bumped rdb_changes_since_last_save by 1 (add) + one-per-trimmed-entry, while upstream t_stream.c::xaddCommand does server.dirty++ exactly once and the inline streamTrim does not touch server.dirty. Differential vs vendored 7.2.4 (oracle 17380): XADD s MAXLEN 1 over a 3-entry stream gave fr delta=3, oracle delta=1. Fixed in fr-command::xadd by capturing store.dirty after store.xadd and restoring it after the inline trim block. Regression test xadd_with_inline_trim_bumps_dirty_exactly_once. Verified MAXLEN/MINID/plain all delta=1 byte-exact vs oracle.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 29bc4b0ce: XADD inline MAXLEN/MINID trim now nets exactly +1 server.dirty. Verified byte-exact vs vendored 7.2.4 oracle (MAXLEN/MINID/plain all delta=1). Regression test xadd_with_inline_trim_bumps_dirty_exactly_once passes.

title: fr-runtime test flake: config_set_appendfsync_no_leaves_local_waitaof_unsatisfied fails in full-suite ordering
description: The fr-runtime unit test 'config_set_appendfsync_no_leaves_local_waitaof_unsatisfied' (crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:~16989) passes in isolation but fails when run as part of the full 'cargo test -p fr-runtime --lib' sweep. Failure: BGREWRITEAOF returns 'ERR error rewriting AOF file' instead of the expected 'Background append only file rewriting started'. Reproduced on clean main (commit 4493da5) -- not introduced by frankenredis-vlh11. Root cause hypothesis: the test calls rt.set_aof_path(PathBuf::from('appendonly.aof')) -- a RELATIVE path. Some earlier test in the suite (likely one that uses unique_temp_path or std::env::set_current_dir, or one that operates in a since-deleted tempdir) leaves the process cwd in a directory where 'appendonly.aof' cannot be created. Fix: use an absolute temp path in this test (mirror waitaof_stays_unsatisfied_until_postponed_aof_rewrite_runs at line 17018 which uses std::env::temp_dir().join(...)). Reproduce: cargo test -p fr-runtime --lib (full suite); fails. cargo test -p fr-runtime --lib config_set_appendfsync_no_leaves_local_waitaof_unsatisfied (isolated); passes. Discovered while working frankenredis-vlh11.
close_reason: Bundled with frankenredis-vlh11 commit. Fix: switched the test's AOF path from the relative 'appendonly.aof' to an absolute std::env::temp_dir().join(...) path so it survives cwd shifts induced by earlier tests in the suite. Verified: cargo test -p fr-runtime --lib now reports 372 passed; 0 failed (was 371; 1 failed in full-suite ordering).

title: active-expire wraparound samples duplicate keys
description: Review-mode finding: Store::run_active_expire_cycle wraps from a mid-key cursor without capping the wrap count to the remaining unique keys, so sample_limit greater than key count can resample keys in one cycle, inflate sampled_keys, and waste active-expire budget.
close_reason: Fixed active-expire wraparound sampling to visit each ordered key at most once per cycle and terminate the cursor after a full pass; added regression coverage.

title: Accept legacy RDB_TYPE_HASH_ZIPLIST RESTORE payloads
description: Upstream Redis rdbLoadObject accepts RDB_TYPE_HASH_ZIPLIST (type 13), validates ziplist field/value pairs via ziplistPairsConvertAndValidateIntegrity, rejects corrupt/duplicate pair data, and restores the hash. fr-store currently has no type 13 branch, so valid legacy hash ziplist DUMP payloads are rejected.
close_reason: Type 13 (RDB_TYPE_HASH_ZIPLIST) handler already exists at lib.rs:12254-12262. Implementation was complete before claim.

title: COMMAND INFO key_specs: missing notes + wrong flags for SET/XADD/GETEX/BITFIELD/PFCOUNT; empty key_specs for EVAL/EVAL_RO/FCALL/FCALL_RO
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 across 9 commands:

(1) Missing 'notes' field (str description) at index [9][0][0][1]:
  - SET:      'RW and ACCESS due to the optional `GET` argument'
  - XADD:     'UPDATE instead of INSERT because of the optional trimming feature'
  - GETEX:    'RW and UPDATE because it changes the TTL'
  - BITFIELD: 'This command allows both access and modification of the key'
  - PFCOUNT:  'RW because it may change the internal representation of the key, and propagate to replicas'

(2) Wrong key flag set for the same 5 commands:
  - SET:      ven=[RW, access, update, variable_flags]   fr=[RW, insert]
  - XADD:     ven=[RW, update]                            fr=[RW, insert]
  - GETEX:    ven=[RW, access, update]                    fr=[RW, access]    (missing 'update')
  - BITFIELD: ven=[RW, access, update, variable_flags]    fr=[RW, access]    (missing 'update', 'variable_flags')
  - PFCOUNT:  ven=[RW, access]                            fr=[RO, access]    (RO→RW)

(3) EVAL/EVAL_RO/FCALL/FCALL_RO emit completely empty key_specs in fr; vendored emits a full spec with 'notes', flags, begin_search type=index/index=2, and find_keys type=keynum/keynumidx=0/firstkey=1/keystep=1.

Per upstream commands.def lines 87, 3719, 4773, 4854, 4881, 4908, 9207, 10081, 10389:
  BITFIELD: notes + RW|UPDATE|ACCESS|VARIABLE_FLAGS
  PFCOUNT:  notes + RW|ACCESS, range lastkey=-1
  EVAL/FCALL:    notes + RW|ACCESS|UPDATE, begin_search index=2, find_keys keynum spec
  EVAL_RO/FCALL_RO: notes + RO|ACCESS, begin_search index=2, find_keys keynum spec
  XADD:     notes + RW|UPDATE
  GETEX:    notes + RW|ACCESS|UPDATE
  SET:      notes + RW|ACCESS|UPDATE|VARIABLE_FLAGS

Root cause: command_info_key_specs in fr-command/src/lib.rs:14327 lumps SET/XADD into 'RW+insert', and falls through to default 'RW+access' for GETEX/BITFIELD; never emits 'notes' field; never emits the keynum find_keys variant for EVAL/FCALL family (which have first_key=last_key=0 and short-circuit to empty Vec at line 14334).

Acceptance: COMMAND INFO byte-equal vendored for SET, XADD, GETEX, BITFIELD, PFCOUNT, EVAL, EVAL_RO, FCALL, FCALL_RO.
close_reason: command_info_key_specs in fr-command/src/lib.rs now mirrors upstream commands.def for the 9 commands carrying a custom keySpec with notes string. Added command_info_keyspec_override(name) for the per-command (notes, flags) lookup, and command_info_keynum_keyspec(name) for EVAL/EVAL_RO/FCALL/FCALL_RO whose movablekeys + keynum find_keys spec was previously dropped (their tabular first/last/step are 0/0/0 and the fn early-returned). Verified via differential probe — all 9 commands (SET/XADD/GETEX/BITFIELD/PFCOUNT/EVAL/EVAL_RO/FCALL/FCALL_RO) now byte-identical to vendored 7.2.4. Pin: command_info_key_specs_notes_and_flags_for_set_xadd_getex_bitfield_pfcount_eval_fcall_match_upstream.

title: fr-command: SRANDMEMBER/ZRANDMEMBER with extra trailing args emit wrong-arity instead of upstream syntax error

Upstream commands.def declares both SRANDMEMBER and ZRANDMEMBER with arity = -2, so any extra trailing args after the WITHSCORES/WITHVALUES variant land in the handler's syntaxerr branch rather than the table-level arity check.

fr-command was emitting WrongArity for argv.len() > 3/4 (capped from above), producing 'ERR wrong number of arguments for srandmember command' (or zrandmember) instead of 'ERR syntax error'.

HRANDFIELD already uses SyntaxError for argv.len() > 4 — fix the two siblings to match.

title: fr-command SLOWLOG HELP emits BulkString frames + invented wording, diverging from upstream addReplyHelp envelope
description:
Tick finding via /testing-conformance-harnesses sweep on subcommand HELP wire shapes.

UPSTREAM (slowlog.c::slowlogCommand lines 143-156):
  addReplyHelp(c, help) — emits SimpleString (addReplyStatus) frames. The body array is verbatim:
    'GET [<count>]',
    '    Return top <count> entries from the slowlog (default: 10, -1 mean all).',
    '    Entries are made of:',
    '    id, timestamp, time in microseconds, arguments array, client IP and port,',
    '    client name',
    'LEN',
    '    Return the length of the slowlog.',
    'RESET',
    '    Reset the slowlog.',
  addReplyHelp wraps with header 'SLOWLOG <subcommand> [<arg> [value] [opt] ...]. Subcommands are:' and footer 'HELP' / '    Print this help.' (networking.c lines 1123-1141). All frames are SimpleString.

FR CURRENT (crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs lines 15553-15565):
  Returns Array of BulkString frames with invented wording:
    BulkString('SLOWLOG <subcommand> [<arg> [value] ...]. Subcommands are:')   ← missing '[opt] '
    BulkString('GET [<count>] - Return the slow log entries.')                ← collapsed multi-line
    BulkString('LEN - Return the number of entries in the slow log.')         ← invented
    BulkString('RESET - Reset the slow log.')                                 ← invented
    BulkString('HELP - Return subcommand help summary.')                      ← invented

Three divergences in one body:
  1. BulkString → should be SimpleString.
  2. Header missing '[opt] ' segment.
  3. Body wording invented; should match upstream slowlog.c help[] array verbatim including the multi-line GET description.
  4. Footer 'HELP' / '    Print this help.' (added automatically by addReplyHelp upstream) — fr has a different invented HELP entry.

Severity: [LOW]. Same family as already-shipped HELP fixes (frankenredis-0o9vo CLIENT, vtege COMMAND, kuthf CONFIG, s574p XGROUP, tnscz XINFO, 7ysld ACL). Wire-format conformance gap.

FIX:
  Replace the body with hello_simple frames matching the upstream help[] array verbatim, plus the standard header / 'HELP' / '    Print this help.' envelope.
close_reason:
Rewrote SLOWLOG HELP to match upstream slowlog.c help[] body verbatim and the addReplyHelp envelope, mirroring the pattern fr already applies to CLIENT/COMMAND/CONFIG/XGROUP/XINFO/ACL HELP.

CHANGES:
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs (slowlog_cmd HELP arm): replaced the 5 BulkString frames with 12 hello_simple SimpleString frames matching upstream slowlog.c lines 144-154 plus the standard 'SLOWLOG <subcommand> [<arg> [value] [opt] ...]. Subcommands are:' header and 'HELP' / '    Print this help.' footer that addReplyHelp appends.
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs: same rewrite at the duplicate handler (lines 9685-9712).
- crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/core_server.json: updated both slowlog_help_returns_array fixtures (one was a duplicate block) to expect simple-string frames with the new wording.

Incidental cleanup:
- The two memory_malloc_stats fixtures in core_server.json still pinned the old 'Memory allocator stats not available' wording (replaced in mxypx but the fixtures were missed). Updated both to 'Stats not supported for the current allocator'.

Verification:
- cargo test -p fr-command --lib slowlog → 3/3 pass.
- cargo test -p fr-runtime --lib slowlog → 2/2 pass.
- cargo test -p fr-conformance conformance_core_server → fr-conformance still has the pre-existing debug_object_reports_live_metadata failure from frankenredis-gmqk1 (separate bead, out of scope); my changes fixed 3 of the 4 mismatches that fr-conformance reported on baseline.
- cargo clippy -p fr-command --lib + cargo clippy -p fr-runtime --lib → both clean.

[LOW] severity. Wire-format conformance gap.

Lua redis.call() currently routes through crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:3556 into fr-command::dispatch_argv, so AUTH and HELLO fall through to unknown-command behavior instead of Redis-style NOSCRIPT. Add script-path rejection that preserves AUTH arity rules plus HELLO proto and option validation order before NOSCRIPT.
