title: DEBUG RELOAD without configured persistence returns 'requires configured appendonly or RDB persistence' — vendored 7.2.4 always succeeds via tempfile RDB round-trip
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 16380 (default config, no --rdb / --aof):
  DEBUG RELOAD
    vendored: OK
    fr      : ERR DEBUG RELOAD requires configured appendonly or RDB persistence

Same divergence for DEBUG RELOAD NOSAVE / DEBUG RELOAD NOSAVE NOFLUSH MERGE.

Upstream debug.c::DEBUG RELOAD path:
  1. rdbSave(server.rdb_filename, ...)  — writes to a tempfile (default 'dump.rdb' in CWD), regardless of whether persistence is configured at startup.
  2. emptyDb(...)
  3. rdbLoad(server.rdb_filename, ...)
  4. (the file is left in place — same as a manual SAVE)

So vendored DEBUG RELOAD is a self-contained round-trip test of the RDB encoder/decoder, not gated on a persistence config.

fr's handle_debug_reload_requested at crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:2898-2929 short-circuits with 'requires configured appendonly or RDB persistence' when neither rdb_path nor aof_path is set.

Fix: when neither path is configured, fall back to an in-memory RDB round trip — encode_rdb(store) → decode_rdb → apply_rdb_entries_to_store. This matches the semantic of vendored's DEBUG RELOAD (validates the RDB encode/decode pair) without requiring a real disk file. The persist_snapshot_to_disk + read_rdb_file paths stay for the configured-rdb branch.

NOSAVE/NOFLUSH/MERGE flags are already accepted; they need no behavior change for the default RELOAD path.
close_reason: Vendored Redis's debug.c::DEBUG RELOAD does not require persistence to be configured — it always round-trips the dataset through an RDB tempfile. fr's handle_debug_reload_requested at fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:2898-2929 short-circuited with 'requires configured appendonly or RDB persistence' when neither --rdb nor --aof was supplied. Restructured the function so the configured-rdb-path branch returns inline (was a Some()/None match), and added an in-memory fallback at the end: store_to_rdb_entries → encode_rdb → decode_rdb → apply_rdb_entries_to_store. This validates the encoder/decoder pair (the actual semantic intent of vendored's command) without needing a real disk file. NOSAVE/NOFLUSH/MERGE keywords still pass through to the same path. Pinned via debug_reload_no_persistence_round_trips_in_memory_per_upstream which seeds string/list/set/hash/zset values and asserts each round-trips through the in-memory reload. Live differential probe vs vendored 16380 confirms identical 'OK' replies and post-reload data preservation. The 3 prior debug_reload tests still pass. Workspace clippy clean under -D warnings.

title: Sentinel quorum should wrap oversized values like Redis unsigned int
description: Live vendored Redis 7.2.4 oracle for SENTINEL SET alpha quorum: values are parsed as positive long long, then assigned to unsigned int. 2147483648 returns OK and SENTINEL MASTER reports quorum=2147483648; 4294967295 returns OK and reports 4294967295; 4294967296 returns OK and reports 0; 9223372036854775807 returns OK and reports 4294967295; only values outside signed long long parsing fail with Invalid argument. fr-sentinel currently parses quorum as u32 and rejects oversized positive values, so it diverges from Redis wire-visible state. Acceptance: fr-sentinel accepts positive i64 values for quorum, stores the u32 wraparound result, and SENTINEL MASTER / CKQUORUM use the wrapped unsigned value while invalid/out-of-range integer text still returns the upstream invalid-argument error.
close_reason: Matched Redis Sentinel quorum signed parse and unsigned-int wraparound

title: RESP3 COMMAND DOCS arguments should be Map per-arg, not flat Array
description: Differential probe (resp3_types corpus, --hello3) vs vendored 7.2.4: COMMAND DOCS <cmd> in RESP3 returns each entry under 'arguments' as a Map (%) of name/type/display_text/key_spec_index (upstream server.c::addReplyCommandArgList uses addReplyMapLen per arg). fr returns each argument as a flat Array of alternating k/v. Also the field order differs (oracle: display_text,key_spec_index,name,type — map keys; fr: name,key,type,display_text). Larger structural fix than the trackinginfo one; deferred. Reproduce: python3 scripts/resp_differ.py --corpus artifacts/probe_corpus_resp3_types.json --hello3 (gauntlet workspace).
close_reason: Fixed in dd373d82e: COMMAND DOCS arguments now emit as per-arg Maps under RESP3 (recursive for oneof/block subargs) via command_docs_arg_to_resp3(); RESP2 path unchanged. Verified by new metamorphic test (Map shape + structural equivalence) and differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 resp3_types --hello3 corpus going 1→0 divergences.

title: PFCOUNT does not propagate the HLL cache update to replicas/AOF (PROPAGATE_REPL)
bug(fr-command): Lua library tables (math/string/table/struct/bit/cjson/cmsgpack/redis) are mutable — redis protects them readonly
description: Differential vs redis 7.2.4: EVAL 'math.newfield=1 return math.newfield' returns 1 in fr (mutation succeeds) but errors in redis: 'ERR user_script:1: Attempt to modify a readonly table'. Same for string/table/struct/bit/cjson/cmsgpack/redis tables, and via rawset() ('Attempt to modify a readonly table', no line prefix) and setmetatable() (blocked in redis, 'ok' in fr). Redis runs luaSetTableProtectionRecursively after env init so the entire global env (incl all library tables) is readonly during script execution; fr only locks the TOP-LEVEL globals map (globals_locked), not the nested library tables. Fix: recursively mark library tables readonly and reject assignment/rawset/setmetatable with redis's exact error strings. SIDE BENEFIT: once immutable, the per-call env rebuild (~200 allocs/EVAL, the 1.84x trivial-EVAL gap) can be replaced by a shared thread-local template — redis's actual architecture (persistent lua_State). File: crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs.
close_reason: Fixed 54bc6bda9: library tables (math/string/table/struct/bit/cjson/cmsgpack/redis) now readonly via LuaTableInner.readonly flag + checks in assignment/rawset/setmetatable/table.insert. Byte-exact vs redis 7.2.4 (28-probe differential, sha256 9ed36cbb...), 1141 tests pass.

title: Stream CG dump/restore: PEL delivery_count off-by-one (restored=4 vs original=3)
description: Surfaced immediately when fuzz_store_stream_groups was restored to compile (f29f96b31) — the broken target had hidden it. Assertion fuzz_store_stream_groups.rs:644 'pending entries': a stream consumer-group pending entry's delivery_count differs after dump/restore — left(restored)=[((1,0),'consumer',0,4)] vs right(original)=[((1,0),'consumer',0,3)]. Restore produced a HIGHER delivery_count (+1). Repro: cargo +nightly fuzz run fuzz_store_stream_groups fuzz/artifacts/fuzz_store_stream_groups/crash-a0b5b1fe2972457b6dd5141ec1641f2cd5431715 (also /tmp/scg_crash_repro.bin). HYPOTHESES: (1) DOUBLE-RESTORE test artifact: the target restores the CG twice — restore_key(payload) [stream DUMP includes CG via dump_stream_consumer_groups, so restore_key restores the PEL] THEN restore_stream_group(group_state) — and the second restore bumps/double-counts delivery_count; if so, fix is to drop the now-redundant restore_stream_group loop OR confirm restore_stream_group insert overwrites the PEL cleanly. (2) REAL fr bug: restore_key (or restore_stream_group) increments PEL delivery_count by 1 on restore (delivery_count is observable via XPENDING, so a real off-by-one is a parity bug vs redis 7.2.4). INVESTIGATE: does restore_key restore CG with exact delivery_count, and does the test's restore_stream_group double-apply? delivery_count bump semantics are at fr-store lib.rs ~12578-12824 (XREADGROUP/XCLAIM bump). Found via running the restored fuzz target.
notes: Capped static+print investigation (gdb-level from here). PROVEN: dump/load preserves raw delivery_count (raw stays 3 in BOTH stores across all post-load reads; DBG A=B=C=3). group_snapshots verified READ-ONLY (calling it does not change raw). Yet its xpending-derived snapshot reports deliveries=4 for the reconstructed stream vs 3 for the raw-identical original — an irreconcilable read-vs-raw contradiction by inspection (xpending_entries returns pending_entry.deliveries verbatim, 12714-12730; group_snapshots = xinfo_groups/xinfo_consumers/xpending_summary/xpending_entries, all read-only). NOT a storage parity bug (storage correct). Downgraded P2->P3: likely harness/read-order oddity confined to the fuzz comparison, not client-observable. NEXT (gdb): break in xpending_entries on the reconstructed key, inspect the exact pending_entry it reads vs stream_consumer_groups().
close_reason: Harness bug fixed in 649a8da05. Root cause: assert_dump_restore_round_trip compared restored.pending vs expected_groups (from the original, un-replayed store) AFTER assert_stream_group_invariants ran on restored. That invariant check replays the consumer PEL via XREADGROUP id=0 which — like upstream streamReplyWithRangeFromConsumerPEL — does delivery_count++ for every still-existing entry served (verified: entry (1,0) exists, upstream takes the bump branch; fr's 3->4 is CORRECT vs redis 7.2.4). Replay ran only on restored, inflating its count. NOT an fr storage/read bug. Fix: snapshot restored_groups for the cross-store equality BEFORE the invariant replay mutates it. Crash artifact passes; 99,648-run fuzz clean.

title: SELECT N (N != 0) in cluster mode silently switches DB; upstream rejects with 'SELECT is not allowed in cluster mode'
description: Upstream db.c::selectCommand:756-770 rejects SELECT N when cluster_enabled is true and N != 0 with 'ERR SELECT is not allowed in cluster mode' (cluster mode only uses DB 0). fr's handle_select_command in fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:10359 and select() in fr-command/src/lib.rs:11980 both skip this check and unconditionally accept any in-range DB index — exposing a parity gap when cluster_enabled is true. Add the check in both paths after argument parsing and before the db_index assignment, mirroring upstream's wording.

title: Lua tonumber('-N', base) should wrap via strtoul, not preserve signed value
description:
Edge case discovered during frankenredis-s99a4 verification. Lua 5.1's
luaB_tonumber dispatches to strtoul(s1, _, base) when base is explicit;
strtoul handles a leading '-' by parsing the absolute digits and then
applying unsigned negation (wrap-around) to the result. Lua pushes the
unsigned-long as a lua_Number (f64), and Redis casts f64→i64 for the
reply, which saturates to INT64_MIN for the very large positive value.

  EVAL "return tonumber('-5', 16)" 0
    vendored: -9223372036854775808     (i.e. INT64_MIN from saturation)
    fr:       -5                       (signed parse, more sensible but
                                       not bit-compatible)

This is a vendored quirk — fr's behavior is arguably more sensible.
Pinning parity requires reproducing the unsigned wrap-around: parse
the digits as u64, wrapping_neg, cast to f64. Then the lua_to_resp
boundary will saturate to INT64_MIN.

Fix locus: crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs — the 'tonumber' arm,
LuaValue::Str path inside the `if let Some(base)` branch. The handling
currently does i64::from_str_radix(stripped, base) which faithfully
parses signed ints; replace with a u64-and-wrap variant when a leading
'-' was consumed.

Test plan: pin tonumber("-5", 16) → INT64_MIN as a number. Confirm
that tonumber("-0xFF") (no explicit base) keeps the existing signed
luaO_str2d fallback.

Priority 4 — extreme edge case, unlikely to bite real scripts.
close_reason: fixed

title: perf(fr-store): O(1) SREM/HDEL on hashtable-encoded sets and hashes
description: SREM/HDEL of many members/fields from a large (hashtable-encoded) set/hash was O(k*n): each removal used the order-preserving shift_remove (O(n) on the insertion-ordered IndexSet/IndexMap), so SREM/HDEL of k=500 on a 100k collection took ~100-125ms vs redis ~0.2-0.3ms (200-396x slower). redis's dict deletes are O(1). Fix (same pattern as SPOP y9xql): SetValue::swap_remove / GenericSet::swap_remove / HashFieldMap::swap_remove use IndexSet/IndexMap swap_remove (O(1)) for the Hash encoding (order unspecified, like redis dict), order-preserving remove for Packed (listpack, matches redis), shift_remove for intset (bounded <=512). SREM-500 on 100k: 100579->207us (486x); HDEL-500: 120167->311us (386x); both now ~parity with redis. Parity: random_command_differ 0 div; SREM/HDEL membership+encoding+values identical to redis; 585 fr-store tests + clippy clean.
close_reason: Shipped: SREM/HDEL O(k*n)->O(k) on hashtable sets/hashes via swap_remove; SREM-500 100579->207us (486x), HDEL-500 120167->311us (386x), both ~parity redis; differ 0 div + membership identical + 585 tests + clippy clean

title: ZSET listpack encodes integer-valued scores as ASCII strings instead of compact listpack int encoding
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:
ZADD z3 1 a 2 b
DEBUG OBJECT z3 → serializedlength: ven=18, fr=20

DUMP z3 hex bytes:
ven: 11 11 11 00 00 00 04 00 81 61 02 01 01 81 62 02 02 01 ff ...
fr:  11 13 13 00 00 00 04 00 81 61 02 81 31 02 81 62 02 81 32 02 ff ...

Both use RDB_TYPE_ZSET_2 (0x11). The listpack body diverges: vendored encodes integer scores 1 and 2 as compact listpack int entries (0x01 0x01 / 0x02 0x01 = uint7+backlen), while fr encodes them as listpack STRING entries (0x81 '1' 0x02 / 0x81 '2' 0x02 = string with 1-byte payload). Each integer score wastes 1 byte.

Per upstream listpack.c::lpInsert + lpEncodeGetType, when storing a score that round-trips through long long (i.e. the float is integer-valued), the listpack writer picks the compact uint7/uint13/sint16/etc. encoding instead of the string-with-int-content encoding.

fr-store/fr-persist's listpack writer for zsets needs to detect integer-valued scores and use the compact int encoding. Affects: DEBUG OBJECT serializedlength, MEMORY USAGE for zsets, RDB DUMP wire shape (related to project_rdb_stream_incompatibility memory note).

Acceptance: ZADD z 1 a 2 b → DEBUG OBJECT serializedlength byte-equal vendored.
close_reason: fr-store::encode_listpack_entry now mirrors upstream listpack.c lpEncodeGetType — integer-looking strings round-trip through parse_listpack_integer and emit the compact uint7/uint13/sint16/sint24/sint32/sint64 encoding instead of the 0x80|len string form. ZADD z 1 a 2 b → DEBUG OBJECT serializedlength now reports 18 (== vendored), DUMP bytes are byte-identical to vendored 7.2.4. Same fix lights up integer-looking HASH values and LIST elements (verified via DUMP h1/l1 byte-identical). Pin: zset_listpack_encodes_integer_scores_as_compact_int_per_upstream.

title: CONFIG GET client-output-buffer-limit returns bare number, not per-class spec
notes: SCOPE (bigger than it looks — do as a focused session): fr models a SINGLE output_buffer_limit:usize (fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:2606, default 256MiB) and CONFIG GET returns it bare (line ~9741); CONFIG SET parses a bare number (~10980). Redis models PER-CLASS limits: normal/slave/pubsub each (hard,soft,soft-seconds), CONFIG GET returns 'normal 0 0 0 slave 268435456 67108864 60 pubsub 33554432 8388608 60', CONFIG SET takes repeatable <class> <hard> <soft> <soft-sec> triples with human-size parsing (256mb). FAITHFUL FIX REQUIRES: (1) per-class state [(hard,soft,soft_sec);3] w/ redis defaults; (2) CONFIG GET format; (3) CONFIG SET reparse (bare-number form becomes a SYNTAX ERROR like redis — UPDATE test config_set_client_output_buffer_limit_updates_live_runtime_state @27977 which sends '1024'); (4) enforcement in fr-server uses per-class hard limit. SAFETY TRADEOFF: redis NORMAL default is 0=UNLIMITED; fr's 256MiB is a deliberate OOM guard (comment @2605). Matching redis removes that guard for normal clients — flag/confirm before flipping. fr-runtime currently free (was locked many turns by cod). NOT rushed end-of-turn.
close_reason: Per-class client-output-buffer-limit shipped fc26829c0: ClientOutputBufferLimits (normal/slave/pubsub × hard/soft/soft-sec), CONFIG GET per-class format + CONFIG SET quadruple parse (replica alias, empty no-op, byte-exact error wording), enforcement via effective_output_hard_limit with 0=unlimited flip, mode-split (strict=redis-exact normal unlimited / hardened=256MiB OOM guard). Verified byte-exact vs redis 7.2.4 across 15 cases.

title: fr-command INFO Memory section hardcodes number_of_cached_scripts/functions/libraries as 0 — real counters exist in Store
description:
REVIEW MODE Tick 14 finding via /mock-code-finder skill. fr's INFO command emits 0 for three counters that have real data sources, leaving observability tools blind to script/function/library state.

LOCATION (crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs lines 11920-11922):

  info.push_str('number_of_cached_scripts:0\r\n');
  info.push_str('number_of_functions:0\r\n');
  info.push_str('number_of_libraries:0\r\n');

SOURCES THAT EXIST:
  - Store::function_stats() returns (lib_count, func_count) — used correctly by FUNCTION STATS but NOT by INFO.
  - Store::script_cache: HashMap<String, Vec<u8>> — has .len() but no public accessor.
  - SCRIPT LOAD inserts into script_cache; SCRIPT FLUSH clears it; SCRIPT EXISTS uses it.
  - FUNCTION LOAD populates function_libraries; FUNCTION STATS reports correctly.

UPSTREAM (legacy_redis_code/redis/src/server.c info section):
  number_of_cached_scripts:N    where N = dictSize(server.lua_scripts)
  number_of_functions:N          where N = functionsLibCount() (sum across libs)
  number_of_libraries:N          where N = engineLibrariesNum()

Reproducer:
  redis-cli> SCRIPT LOAD 'return 1'
  '<sha1>'
  redis-cli> FUNCTION LOAD '#!lua name=lib1\nredis.register_function("f1", function() return 1 end)'
  'lib1'
  redis-cli> INFO memory | grep number_of
  upstream:
    number_of_cached_scripts:1
    number_of_functions:1
    number_of_libraries:1
  fr (current):
    number_of_cached_scripts:0
    number_of_functions:0
    number_of_libraries:0

Severity: [LOW]. Observability divergence on hot-path INFO. Affects monitoring tools that scrape these counters (RedisInsight, datadog-redis-integration, prometheus_redis_exporter, redis-exporter).

Fix:
1. Add Store::script_count() -> usize.
2. Wire all 3 INFO lines to real values using script_count() + function_stats().
3. Add regression test via fr-command lib tests.

Discovered via /mock-code-finder skill — grep for 'placeholder|fake|stub' in production code paths, narrowed to the INFO emit which had a comment explicitly noting the placeholder ('Allocator-derived stats (placeholders since fr uses the Rust system allocator)') and inferred the script/function counters were lumped in even though they have real backing data.
close_reason: fr-command INFO memory now reports real values for number_of_cached_scripts (Store::script_count() = script_cache.len()), number_of_functions and number_of_libraries (Store::function_stats() = (lib_count, func_count)). New helper Store::script_count() exposes the existing private script_cache HashMap. Wired the three INFO emit lines from hardcoded ':0' to format!() with live values. New test info_memory_reports_real_script_function_library_counts pins both the empty-store baseline (all 0) and the post-SCRIPT LOAD + FUNCTION LOAD live state (1, 2, 1 for a single script + a library with 2 functions). Discovered via /mock-code-finder skill (grep for placeholder/stub/fake/0-stub patterns in production code paths). All fr-command lib tests pass; clippy clean. The 4 unrelated pre-existing failures (xreadgroup/xinfo stream/migrate-vendored) reproduce on a clean stash and are untouched.

title: Lua string.gmatch iterator loses state when called outside for-in loop
description:
Lua 5.1's string.gmatch returns an iterator function whose internal
closure carries the source string, pattern, and position. When called
with no args, it advances and returns the next match. fr's gmatch
implementation returns a (RustFunction, state_table, nil) triple
where the iterator function reads state from its first arg — which
works for the `for ... in gmatch(s, p) do` form (the for-in loop
passes state explicitly) but breaks for direct calls:

  EVAL "return string.gmatch('abc def', '%a+')()" 0
    vendored: abc
    fr:       (nil)

  EVAL "local g=string.gmatch('a b c', '%a'); return g()..g()..g()" 0
    vendored: abc
    fr:       ERR attempt to concatenate a nil value

The fix requires giving the iterator stateful closure semantics. Options:

1. Add a LuaValue variant for "rust closure with captured args" or
   reuse a Function with captured upvalues. Heavyweight.
2. Stash the gmatch state on LuaState keyed by an opaque cookie (a
   string id), have __gmatch_iter look up state from current
   invocation. Risk: leaks state across scripts unless cleared.
3. Use a LuaValue::Table with an embedded callable metatable (__call)
   that delegates to __gmatch_iter with the table as state. Lighter
   weight but introduces a new __call dispatch pattern.

Option 3 is probably the cleanest. The for-in path continues to work
(state arg is the table); the no-arg call goes through __call which
prepends the table as the first arg.

Test plan: pin gmatch(s, p)() returns first match, three successive
calls return 1st/2nd/3rd matches then nil, and the existing for-in
regression continues to work.

title: Lua 'attempt to call a nil/thread/X value' errors lack 'user_script:1:' prefix
description: fr's call_function emits 'attempt to call a nil value' (and similar for thread/non-callable types) without the standard 'user_script:1: ' source-location prefix that upstream Lua 5.1 wraps around every runtime error. Live differential probe: 'redis.register_function' inside EVAL (where it's nil) gives 'ERR attempt to call a nil value' in fr but 'ERR user_script:1: attempt to call field "register_function" (a nil value)' in vendored. Two divergences: missing prefix AND missing 'field NAME' context. The field-name context requires plumbing through the call site -- larger refactor. The prefix is the cheap win. Fix: prepend 'user_script:1: ' to the three call_function error literals at fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:2973-2975.
close_reason: Added the 'user_script:1: ' source-location prefix to the three call_function 'attempt to call' errors (nil/thread/other-type) at fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:2973+. Live differential probe: 'local x = nil x()' and 'local x = 42 x()' now carry the prefix matching vendored. Remaining 'local NAME (a TYPE value)' context detail (vendored emits 'attempt to call local x (a nil value)') requires plumbing the call-site name through to call_function -- filed as follow-up.

title: INFO commandstats collapses container subcommands — fr emits cmdstat_memory not cmdstat_memory|usage
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 INFO commandstats:

ven emits per-subcommand entries (server.c::genRedisInfoStringCommandStats keys by cmd->fullname which is 'parent|sub' for containers):
  cmdstat_memory|usage:calls=12,...
  cmdstat_memory|help:calls=1,...
  cmdstat_object|encoding:calls=4,...
  cmdstat_object|refcount:calls=3,...
  cmdstat_pubsub|channels:calls=1,...
  cmdstat_client|info:calls=1,...
  cmdstat_command|info:calls=23,...
  cmdstat_function|stats:calls=2,...

fr collapses every container call into the parent:
  cmdstat_client:calls=1,...
  cmdstat_memory:calls=12,...
  cmdstat_object:calls=6,...
  cmdstat_pubsub:calls=2,...

Per fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:2484 the histogram key is just argv[0].to_ascii_lowercase(). The container/subcommand namespacing already exists for the subscribe-context error wording (Self::pubsub_blocked_command_name at line 3985). Extract that into a shared helper and reuse it in record_command_histogram_outcome + record_command_rejected so the histogram key matches cmd->fullname.

Acceptance: INFO commandstats emits cmdstat_memory|usage / cmdstat_object|encoding / cmdstat_client|info etc. for container subcommands, while non-container commands (GET, SET, EVAL) still key as bare parent.
close_reason: Extracted canonical_command_fullname helper from pubsub_blocked_command_name and reused it in record_command_histogram_outcome + record_command_rejected so commandstats/latencystats/latency-histogram bucket container subcommands as parent|sub. Pinned via info_commandstats_namespaces_container_subcommands_per_upstream.

title: [perf] Direct borrowed GET bulk reply encoding
description: Fresh pass62 pushed-main profile for GET P16/1M shows Value::string_owned at 7.39% self after read-buffer cursor rejection. Target one lever: keep Runtime GET fast-path bookkeeping unchanged but encode the GET bulk reply directly from borrowed store value bytes, eliminating the owned Vec/RespFrame clone on the default borrowed GET path. Baseline/benchmark via rch; golden sha256 and isomorphism proof required; keep only Score>=2.0.
close_reason: Rejected: direct borrowed GET bulk encoding failed Score>=2.0. Baseline GET P16/300k 388.8ms +/- 9.8; paired GET P16/300k baseline 393.2ms +/- 13.7 vs candidate 402.4ms +/- 27.5 (baseline 1.02x +/- 0.08); reversed GET P16/1M candidate 1.257s +/- 0.038 vs baseline 1.288s +/- 0.061 (candidate 1.03x +/- 0.06, inside noise). Golden sha256 matched b79d023e586718789382bba51c903392bb6ac4aed9dfa1405cd97bc151fe87ef; source hunk removed. Do not retry direct GET bulk encoding; reprofile and attack key/value layout, batch dispatch/output fusion, or persistent reply fragments/writev.

title: perf(store): skip discarded O(value) entry-digest hashing on stale-digest mutations (EXPIRE/GETEX/DEL on large values)
close_reason: Shipped 403b71b2c: with_mutated_entry + internal_entries_remove skip the discarded O(value) entry_state_digest hashing when digest_stale (~always at runtime). Isomorphic (running_digest/digest_stale untouched; DEBUG DIGEST full-scans when stale). A/B: 186ms->~0 (2946x) for EXPIRE/PERSIST on 100KB. fr-store 513/513 incl debug digest invariant.

title: CONFIG GET <pattern> <pattern>... returns duplicate entries when patterns overlap; upstream dedupes
description: Upstream config.c::configGetCommand uses a 'matches' dict to dedupe entries across multiple patterns: each name is only added once even if multiple patterns (or repeats of the same pattern) match it. fr's handle_config_get at crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:6945 calls collect_config_entries once per pattern and just appends to a flat Vec, so 'CONFIG GET maxmemory maxmemory' returns the maxmemory pair twice, and 'CONFIG GET maxmemory maxmemory*' returns maxmemory in both passes. Dedupe entries by key (case-insensitive) before constructing the RESP2 alternating-array / RESP3 Map reply.
close_reason: fixed

title: Downgrade FEATURE_PARITY.md replication status to honest assessment
description:
# Honest Replication Status

## What
Change FEATURE_PARITY.md "Replication baseline | parity_green" to "parity_gap" or "in_progress" until cross-process replication actually works.

## Why
The current "parity_green" rating for replication is dishonest. The state machine logic works in isolation but no bytes flow between processes. Honesty about project status is a prerequisite for good engineering.

## Implementation
1. Update FEATURE_PARITY.md: change replication row status to "in_progress"
2. Update notes to honestly describe what works (FSM logic, PSYNC decision math, offset tracking) and what does not (actual TCP replication)
3. After replication epic is complete, upgrade back to parity_green with evidence

## No Dependencies
This should be done immediately, before any replication implementation work.
close_reason: Updated FEATURE_PARITY.md replication row from parity_green to in_progress with honest assessment of what works (TCP replication, PSYNC, FULLRESYNC) and what does not (runtime REPLICAOF command, replica-of-replica chains, FR-to-FR E2E, diskless replication)

title: Lua lexer 'escape sequence too large' missing 'near <token>' suffix
description:
After frankenredis-whyor, fr correctly rejects \d+ escapes >255 but emits just "escape sequence too large". Vendored Lua 5.1.5's luaX_lexerror appends " near '<token>'" where <token> is the open/close quote rendering.

Probe:
  EVAL "return string.byte('\\256')" 0
    vendored: "escape sequence too large near '''"
    fr:       "escape sequence too large"

Same near-suffix pattern as the existing 'unfinished string near <eof>' in fr; just extend to escape-too-large. Probe sweep #122.
close_reason: completed

title: mr_getrange_bounds metamorphic oracle missing redis both-negative-inverted empty short-circuit
description: fr-store tests/metamorphic_string.rs mr_getrange_bounds computed expected GETRANGE output WITHOUT upstream's 'start<0 && end<0 && start>end -> empty' early-return (t_string.c getrangeCommand applies it BEFORE resolving negatives vs strlen). Proptest counterexample key=[0] value=[0] start=-1 end=-2: oracle expected [0] but redis 7.2.4 AND fr both correctly return [] (verified vs vendored redis-cli :17580 + source). fr getrange is CORRECT (has the short-circuit, bead getrangewt); the TEST oracle was wrong. Fix: model the short-circuit in expected-value computation. Pinned via proptest-regressions seed.
notes: Fixed test oracle in crates/fr-store/tests/metamorphic_string.rs (mr_getrange_bounds): added the start<0 && end<0 && start>end -> empty short-circuit before negative resolution, matching redis/fr. Regression seed committed. No production change — fr getrange was already correct.

title: Zero-copy argv: eliminate per-request double-allocation in command dispatch hot path
description:
PROFILE-BACKED (in-code gdb note at fr-server/main.rs:1442-1445): frame_to_argv is ~58% of on-CPU allocator samples. Root cause: per command the parser allocates one Vec<u8> per arg inside RespFrame::Array(BulkString), then execute_frame_internal calls frame_to_argv which CLONES every arg's bytes again into argv:Vec<Vec<u8>>, because fr-server retains the owned frame for 7 post-dispatch helpers (is_quit_frame, waitaof_should_block, wait_should_block, try_build_blocked_state, suppress_client_network_reply, replication_follow_up_bytes, check_subscription_mode_gate) — each of which ALSO rebuilds argv via frame_to_argv. Net: every arg's bytes are heap-copied at least twice per request.

LEVER (algorithmic, not micro): build argv ONCE by MOVING bytes out of the parsed frame (fr-command::argv_from_frame(frame: RespFrame) consuming variant), dispatch on that single argv, and run all post-dispatch helpers on &[Vec<u8>] (the 7 helpers already operate on argv internally — converting their signatures from &RespFrame to &[Vec<u8>] is a simplification). execute_frame_internal/preflight_gate take argv:&[Vec<u8>]; the cold preflight/threat-digest paths reconstruct a byte-identical RespFrame::Array(BulkString) from argv on demand so ThreatInputDigestSource::Frame hashing stays byte-exact (commands are always multibulk-of-bulkstrings -> reconstruction is identity -> parity absolute). Hot success path never materializes a frame.

TARGET: >=2.0 on alloc-bound GET/SET microbench (halve per-arg allocations: 2 copies -> 1; remove RespFrame enum alloc). Keep frame_to_argv(&RespFrame) for conformance/owned callers.

PARITY OBLIGATIONS: (1) threat-digest bytes identical via frame reconstruction; (2) inline-command path produces same argv; (3) *0/*-1 no-op multibulk handled before consume; (4) full conformance suite green via rch.

Scope: fr-command/src/lib.rs (argv_from_frame), fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (execute_argv path + preflight_gate + execute_frame_internal argv signature + reconstruct helper), fr-server/src/main.rs (hot loop + 7 helpers).
close_reason: Rejected: controlled rch/hyperfine showed zero-copy argv candidate regressed SET p16 from 1.769406955615s +/- 0.091487035662s to 1.829823308845s +/- 0.066405038881s (0.96698x, -3.414%); Score 0 < 2.0. Do not ship this lever; next primitive is request-scoped slab/zero-copy RESP token views.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: PARITY GATE PASSED (cc-opus, read-only validation of the in-flight uncommitted lever): fr-command 1087/1087, fr-runtime 410/410 lib, fr-server 9/9 lib + 65/65 bin = 1571 tests, 0 failures. Covers converted post-dispatch helpers (try_build_blocked_state/is_quit_frame/waitaof_should_block/wait_should_block/replication_follow_up_bytes) + frame_to_argv/argv_from_frame/argv_to_command_frame. Remaining gate before landing: full fr-conformance suite (byte-exact vs vendored 7.2.4) since the lever rewrites the dispatch hot path. cc-opus did NOT commit — files owned by concurrent agent.

title: CLIENT TRACKING non-BCAST invalidation batches multiple keys into one push; upstream sends one invalidate push PER key
description:
Differential probe (ct_probe2.py) vs vendored redis 7.2.4: when a single command modifies multiple tracked keys, a DEFAULT/OPTIN/OPTOUT (non-BCAST) tracking client must receive ONE invalidate push PER key (upstream trackingInvalidateKey fires per-key as each key is modified). fr batches all keys from the command into a SINGLE invalidate push.

Repro (RESP3 tracker conn, CLIENT TRACKING ON; GET m1; GET m2; second conn MSET m1 9 m2 8):
  oracle: >invalidate[m1] then >invalidate[m2]  (two pushes)
  fr    : >invalidate[m1,m2]                    (one push)
Same for RENAME src dst (oracle: invalidate[rsrc] + invalidate[rdst]; fr: one push).
BCAST mode correctly batches multiple keys into one push (verified both match) — fix must preserve BCAST batching and only split the non-BCAST path to per-key messages, in key-modification order.

LOCATION: fr-runtime/src/lib.rs queue_client_tracking_invalidations (~4617): BCAST + non-BCAST both feed one BTreeMap<target,Vec<keys>> emitted as one message/target. Split: keep BCAST batched; emit non-BCAST as one Invalidate{keys:[k]} per observed key per caching client, preserving key order.
close_reason: done

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Fixed in 13f288945. Split queue_client_tracking_invalidations: BCAST keeps batching all command keys into one push (upstream also batches BCAST — verified); non-BCAST now emits one Invalidate{keys:[k]} per observed key per caching client, in key-modification order. Verified byte-exact vs vendored redis 7.2.4 across 24 tracking differential scenarios (MSET/RENAME now two pushes); full fr-runtime 411 tests pass; clippy clean. Added 2 regression tests.

title: Align XREAD BLOCK timeout validation with Redis integer-ms semantics
description: Redis requires XREAD/XREADGROUP BLOCK to be an integer millisecond timeout and rejects out-of-range values. FrankenRedis currently validates BLOCK as a float in fr-command and uses saturating_add in fr-server, which can accept fractional or oversized inputs incorrectly.
close_reason: Completed

title: [HIGH] r73v sub-task (a-ext): byte-identical re-emit (compact-type encoder selection)
description: Stretch acceptance for br-frankenredis-r73v: 're-emit byte-identical on save'. The 9c460ed live_redis_rdb_reemit_loadrdb_matches_upstream gate proves STATE-equivalence cross-impl, but the bytes differ because fr-persist::encode_rdb always emits canonical type tags (RDB_TYPE_LIST=1, RDB_TYPE_SET=2, RDB_TYPE_HASH=4, RDB_TYPE_ZSET_2=5) where upstream picks the COMPACT form (LIST_QUICKLIST_2=18, SET_INTSET=11, SET_LISTPACK=20, HASH_LISTPACK=16, ZSET_LISTPACK=17) for shapes that fit its size thresholds. Wire fr-persist::encode_rdb to mirror upstream's compact-type selection (re-using the encoder helpers that already exist in fr-store::dump_key for DUMP/RESTORE). Acceptance: encode_rdb(decode_rdb(upstream_dump)) == upstream_dump for all shapes within compact thresholds.
close_reason: Implemented compact RDB default re-emit with Redis zset ordering and conformance coverage

title: Lua __index chain depth-exhaustion returns nil instead of 'loop in gettable' error
description:
Upstream Lua 5.1 lvm.c::luaV_gettable detects __index chain cycles
via MAXTAGLOOP (2000) iterations; when exhausted it raises
'loop in gettable'. fr's table_lookup_with_index_meta caps at 16
hops and silently returns nil on exhaustion, masking what should
be a runtime error.

Repro:
  EVAL "local t = {}; setmetatable(t, {__index=t}); return tostring(t.x)" 0
    vendored: (error) ERR user_script:1: loop in gettable ...
    fr:       nil

The 16-hop cap is a fr-only safety limit; vendored uses 2000. For
parity, fr should:
  1. Raise 'loop in gettable' (with the user_script:1: prefix from
     the runtime, dropped under pcall/anonymous-C) when the limit
     is hit.
  2. Mirror the same behavior in get_with_index_depth (raw table
     lookup) if any callers go through that path.

Fix at fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:4429 — change the depth-exhausted
arm from `Ok(LuaValue::Nil)` to
`Err("user_script:1: loop in gettable".to_string())`.
close_reason: completed

title: SETRANGE missing LFU frequency bump for existing strings
description: SETRANGE modifies existing string but doesn't bump LFU counter. Should call bump_lfu_freq when modifying existing string with setrange.
close_reason: done

title: keyspace_hits/misses miscounted for ~11 commands vs redis 7.2.4
description:
INFO stats keyspace_hits/keyspace_misses diverge from vendored 7.2.4. Root cause: fr routes EVERY key lookup through Store::record_keyspace_lookup (drop_if_expired + always count), but upstream only counts in lookupKeyRead*; lookupKeyWrite* does NOT touch keyspace_hits/misses. So write commands over-count and some reads under-count. Measured deltas (oracle vs fr) on existing key unless noted:

OVER-COUNT (write cmds: fr +1 hit, redis 0):
- SETRANGE, APPEND, INCRBYFLOAT, EXPIRE, PEXPIRE, LSET, SPOP

UNDER-COUNT (read cmds: redis counts, fr 0):
- DUMP on existing key (redis +1 hit; fr 0)
- DUMP on missing key (redis +1 miss; fr 0)
- GETSET (redis +1 hit for the read of old value; fr 0)

WRONG-COUNT:
- OBJECT IDLETIME on existing key: redis +1 hit, fr +2 (double-counts)

FIX DIRECTION: write-command Store methods should use a non-counting lookup (drop_if_expired directly, which record_keyspace_lookup already wraps) instead of record_keyspace_lookup; DUMP/GETSET-read need a counting read lookup; OBJECT IDLETIME must count exactly once. fr-store record_keyspace_lookup is on IcyWolf's ohsk5 hot path (frankenredis-shewy) — coordinate. Repro: scripts probe in session; FLUSHALL; CONFIG RESETSTAT; <cmd>; INFO stats. Precedent: e350e7ef7 (plain SET keyspace-lookup count fix).
notes:
PROGRESS 2 (NobleRiver, ffd88ee2e): fixed SETRANGE+INCRBYFLOAT (key_type to peek_value_type precheck), GETSET (store drop_if_expired to record_keyspace_lookup, counts the read), OBJECT IDLETIME (store object_idletime made non-counting; single count stays in object_cmd exists_no_touch; also fixed DEBUG OBJECT which shares object_idletime). 7 of ~11 done (prior: SPOP/SPOP-COUNT/LSET/DUMP). 545 fr-store + 1109 fr-command pass.

REMAINING 2 sources PRECISELY traced (both need a small additive NON-COUNTING fr-store read helper):
- APPEND: fr-command append() calls store.strlen (counts) for the proto-max-bulk-len size precheck + WRONGTYPE. Fix: WRONGTYPE via peek_value_type (non-counting) THEN a non-counting length read (add Store::string_len_no_stats) for the size check; redis appendCommand = lookupKeyWrite (no count) then checkStringLength.
- EXPIRE/PEXPIRE/EXPIREAT/PEXPIREAT: fr-command apply_expiry_with_options (lib.rs ~13996) calls store.pttl (counts) to get current_remaining_ms for NX/XX/GT/LT — fetched even for plain EXPIRE. Fix: add Store::pttl_no_stats (non-counting) and use it here; redis reads TTL off the object after lookupKeyWrite (no count). Verify EXPIRE NX/GT/LT variants too.
close_reason: All ~11 keyspace_hits/misses divergences fixed across a7e6d2ada + ffd88ee2e + 276de99cd. 28-command differential sweep vs redis 7.2.4 = 0 divergences. SPOP/SPOP-COUNT(all branches)/DUMP/LSET/SETRANGE/INCRBYFLOAT/GETSET/OBJECT-IDLETIME/DEBUG-OBJECT/APPEND/EXPIRE-family byte-exact.

title: AOF stream group rewrite must emit ENTRIESREAD -1 like Redis
description: Upstream Redis 7.2.4 aof.c::rewriteStreamObject always emits XGROUP CREATE with the ENTRIESREAD trailer and writes group->entries_read, whose default invalid value is -1. fr-store::to_aof_commands currently omits ENTRIESREAD when StreamGroup.entries_read is None, so default groups replay through a different AOF command shape than Redis. Acceptance: stream group AOF rewrite always emits ENTRIESREAD; None/default entries_read serializes as -1 and replay restores None/default semantics.
close_reason: Completed fr-store stream AOF ENTRIESREAD -1 parity fix

title: SETBIT promotes encoding to raw
description: Upstream bitops.c::setbitCommand calls dbUnshareStringValue (or dbAdd for new keys) which always uses raw encoding regardless of length. fr was leaving int-encoded values as int (when SETBIT didn't extend) or as embstr (when length stayed ≤44). Now SETBIT sets force_raw_encoding on both the existing-entry mutation path and the new-entry creation path

title: redis parity: fix WAITAOF without appendonly parity
description: WAITAOF returns 1 0 instead of 0 0 when appendonly is disabled and min replicas are unmet.
close_reason: Completed

title: COMMAND INFO for container commands omits subcommand metadata
description:
Vendored Redis 7.2.4 emits subcommand metadata as the tenth field of every COMMAND INFO entry whose command is a container. fr currently emits the subcommand entries for only four containers (command, object, xgroup, xinfo) and an empty array for the rest, even though SUBCOMMAND_TABLE has rows for all the subcommands.

Line counts (vendored vs fr):
  acl      : 194 vs  10
  client   : 262 vs  10
  cluster  : 357 vs  10
  config   :  83 vs  10
  function : 128 vs  10
  latency  : 121 vs  10
  memory   :  95 vs  10
  module   :  75 vs  10
  pubsub   :  85 vs  10
  script   :  82 vs  10
  slowlog  :  66 vs  10
  debug    :  15 vs  15  (vendored intentionally omits — admin-flagged subcommands)
  command  :  95 vs  95  (already correct)
  object   : 137 vs 137  (already correct)
  xgroup   : 173 vs 173  (already correct)
  xinfo    : 111 vs 111  (already correct)

Impact: client tooling that parses COMMAND INFO to enumerate available subcommands (RedisInsight, monitoring agents, library autoconfig) sees container commands as opaque. ACL category attribution, fast/slow classification, write/read attribution all derived from per-subcommand metadata become unavailable.

Likely fix: extend `SUBCOMMAND_PARENTS_WITH_INFO` in crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs (line ~15563) from the current four entries to include acl, client, cluster, config, function, latency, memory, module, pubsub, script, slowlog. Excluding `debug` mirrors vendored's behavior (it suppresses debug subcommand fan-out, likely because the admin-flagged subcommands shouldn't be enumerated in COMMAND INFO).

Test plan:
  - Pin the line counts above in a fr-command integration test (or rely on byte-for-byte fixture comparison against legacy_redis_code outputs).
  - Verify SUBCOMMAND_TABLE entries match vendored for each affected subcommand's flags/arity/keyspec by spot-checking client|kill, cluster|getkeysinslot, memory|usage.
  - Add fr-command unit test: COMMAND INFO CLUSTER returns at least the canonical cluster|count-failure-reports entry with arity=3, admin/stale flags, @admin/@slow/@dangerous categories.

title: string.format accepts width/precision >=100 (vendored caps at 99 via scanformat)
description:
Upstream Lua 5.1.5 lstrlib.c::scanformat hardcodes a 2-digit cap
on both width and precision; a third digit raises 'invalid format
(width or precision too long)'. fr's format spec parser accepts
arbitrary-length numeric runs, producing huge formatted output for
specs like '%.999d' or '%999d' that vendored rejects up front.

Repro:
  EVAL "local ok,e = pcall(string.format, '%.100d', 1); return tostring(ok)..':'..tostring(e):sub(1,80)" 0
    vendored: false:invalid format (width or precision too long)
    fr:       true:000000...000 (100 zeros)

  EVAL "local ok,e = pcall(string.format, '%100d', 1); ..."
    same divergence

  Boundary:
    precision=99 → both succeed
    precision=100 → vendored errors, fr succeeds
    width=99 → both succeed
    width=100 → vendored errors, fr succeeds

Fix: at the format spec parser (crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs
near the %s/%d/%g handling), reject any width or precision whose
decimal value exceeds 99 with the upstream wording. The simplest
place is wherever the format spec is extracted before dispatch on
conv type.
close_reason: completed

title: coroutine.create/wrap/resume/status hardcode user_script:1: and 'name' in errors
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:

  pcall(coroutine.create)        v=bad argument #1 to '?' (Lua function expected)
                                  fr=user_script:1: bad argument #1 to 'create' (Lua function expected)
  pcall(coroutine.resume)        v=bad argument #1 to '?' (coroutine expected)
                                  fr=user_script:1: bad argument #1 to 'resume' (coroutine expected)
  pcall(coroutine.status,1)      v=bad argument #1 to '?' (coroutine expected)
                                  fr=user_script:1: bad argument #1 to 'status' (coroutine expected)
  pcall(coroutine.wrap,'x')      v=bad argument #1 to '?' (Lua function expected)
                                  fr=user_script:1: bad argument #1 to 'wrap' (Lua function expected)

Four coroutine.* handlers at fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs lines ~5864-5901 hardcode the named/prefixed wording. Route through lua_format_argerror with the per-function name so pcall(coroutine.X,…) renders the anonymous shape (`'?'` name, no user_script:1: prefix).

NOTE: existing test lua_coroutine_arg_validation_rejects_non_function_creation pins the direct-call shape ("user_script:1: bad argument #1 to 'create' ..."). That stays the same; only the pcall path changes.
close_reason: Routed all 4 coroutine.* argerror sites (create/wrap/resume/status) through lua_format_argerror. Regression test coroutine_argerror_pcall_shape_96j2u pins 9 pcall cases + 4 direct-call regressions. Existing 11 coroutine tests still pass. Live-diff vs vendored 16380: 7/7 scripted cases match.

title: Reject CONFIG SET databases as immutable
description: Redis marks databases as IMMUTABLE_CONFIG in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/config.c. FrankenRedis currently accepts CONFIG SET databases via static CONFIG fallback; freeze parity by rejecting runtime mutation while preserving CONFIG GET visibility.
close_reason: Completed

title: remove unused runtime_fixture_config_path dead code
description: Targeted rch clippy for fr-conformance is red only because crates/fr-conformance/src/lib.rs still defines runtime_fixture_config_path without callers. title: OBJECT IDLETIME stale after LFU->LRU policy switch + re-access: non-LFU read (touch) does not clear ENTRY_REDIS_LFU_CLOCK_FIELD
close_reason: Implemented policy-aware read access touch for common Store read paths: LFU accesses still bump/preserve LFU clock metadata, while non-LFU LRU touches clear stale Redis LFU clock marker unless touch is disabled. Added regression lru_read_after_lfu_policy_switch_clears_stale_lfu_clock_marker_97wc2. Verified via rch exec cargo test -p fr-store lru_read_after_lfu_policy_switch_clears_stale_lfu_clock_marker_97wc2, rch exec cargo fmt -p fr-store -- --check, rch exec cargo check -p fr-store; UBS ran on crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs and .beads/issues.jsonl with pre-existing whole-file findings only.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
cc (claude-code): Cross-review of nro98 (4256cd56d). The fix correctly reinterprets stale LFU bits as an LRU clock for OBJECT IDLETIME after a LFU->LRU policy switch (redis_lfu_as_idle_seconds mirrors estimateObjectIdleTime), BUT the ENTRY_REDIS_LFU_CLOCK_FIELD flag is only CLEARED in refresh_db_add_metadata (new key) -- never on a normal non-LFU READ access. Entry::touch() (the LRU read-access path, fr-store lib.rs ~3360) updates last_access_ms but leaves the flag set.

REPRO (vs redis 7.2.4, both --enable-debug-command):
  config set maxmemory-policy allkeys-lfu; set k v; access k; config set maxmemory-policy noeviction; GET k; OBJECT IDLETIME k
  -> redis ~0 (non-LFU read sets robj.lru = LRU_CLOCK()), fr large bogus value (still reinterprets stale LFU bits). nro98 only handled the no-re-access case.

redis db.c lookupKey: if (policy and MAXMEMORY_FLAG_LFU) updateLFU(val); else val->lru = LRU_CLOCK(); -- the non-LFU branch overwrites the LFU bits.

ROOT CAUSE: read-access dispatch is inlined ~126x as: if lfu_tracking_enabled { entry.bump_lfu_freq(...) } entry.touch(now_ms). bump sets the flag; touch never clears; touch runs in BOTH branches so it cannot unconditionally clear (would break LFU + the nro98 no-reaccess case).

RECOMMENDED FIX (fr-store, CoralOx domain): funnel access-update through ONE policy-aware helper -- restructure to: if lfu_tracking_enabled { bump_lfu_freq(); touch(); } else { touch_lru(); } where touch_lru = touch() + clear_redis_lfu_clock_field(), guarded by !touch_disabled() so CLIENT NO-TOUCH reads (like redis LOOKUP_NOTOUCH) do NOT clear. One helper avoids hand-editing 126 sites. Guarded by differential harness scripts/lfu_idletime_policy_differ.py (this turn).

title: List DUMP/RESTORE structural: ChunkedList Owned chunks re-synthesize listpacks (target ~3x DUMP)
description:
PROFILE-BACKED (2026-06-16, default config, DEBUG-less DUMP cmd, fresh oracle): list DUMP 10k elements redis vs fr = list-int 2.67x slower, list-str 1.73x slower (last session saw 5.34x when redis had few large default-size nodes — gap scales with redis node size). list RESTORE 2.2x (lojpt, prior). hash/set/zset DUMP are PARITY-OR-FASTER (0.46-0.85x) — list is the SOLE remaining catastrophic DUMP gap.

ROOT CAUSE: fr stores list elements as ListChunk::Owned { Arc<Vec<Vec<u8>>> } (packed_set.rs ~1317) = scattered per-element heap allocs. DUMP (encode_dump_quicklist2, fr-store lib.rs ~20907) must RE-SYNTHESIZE a listpack per node (parse_listpack_integer + encode_listpack_entry per element), while redis just memcpys its existing quicklist listpack nodes (~0 encode). Same root => list RAM ~comparable but iteration cache-unfriendly.

INFRASTRUCTURE ALREADY EXISTS: ListChunk::Listpack { Arc<Vec<u8>>, spans } variant + ChunkedList retained_listpack_chunks() + the encode_dump_quicklist2 retained fast path (memcpy, byte-exact) + quicklist_packed_node_accepts node-split predicate (q5ody) + make_mut (Listpack->Owned on mutation).

LEVER: make push_back/push_front BUILD/EXTEND Listpack chunks whose boundaries satisfy retained_quicklist2_chunks_match_dump_rules (redis quicklist node-split). Then append-only / pop-at-ends lists (queues, logs, the common case) DUMP via the retained fast path => byte-exact + ~3x faster, lower RAM, cache-friendly LRANGE. Mutations (LSET/LINSERT/LREM/middle-pop) convert the touched chunk to Owned via make_mut (DUMP synthesizes for those only, unchanged).

SCOPE/RISK: multi-day; byte-exact verification needed across ALL list cmds (RPUSH/LPUSH/RPOP/LPOP[count]/LINSERT/LSET/LREM/LTRIM/LRANGE/LINDEX/LPOS/LMOVE/RPOPLPUSH/LMPOP) + OBJECT ENCODING (listpack<->quicklist transitions) + DUMP/RESTORE/DEBUG OBJECT ql_nodes + RDB save/load. fr-store = CoralOx domain (coordinate). This is the alien-graveyard 'different memory layout / succinct packed structure' primitive for the list type. golden: candidate DUMP==redis DUMP byte-for-byte across append-only list shapes; DEBUG DIGEST-VALUE unchanged.
close_reason: Completed pass222 DUMP-path quicklist-boundary listpack sealing. Conservative focused DUMP rerun: int 1.74x, str 2.13x, wide 1.91x with unchanged DUMP sha256; retained hyperfine end-to-end artifacts 1.39x-1.44x; Redis DUMP/RESTORE oracle passed both directions. RDB snapshot/full-sync continuation filed as frankenredis-x1mmu.

comments.author: cc
comments.text:
INVESTIGATED 2026-06-16 (cc): the dump_key-only seal approach is INSUFFICIENT — implemented + proved byte-exact (re-chunk Owned->Listpack at DUMP time so 2nd+ DUMP hits the retained memcpy path; 6x->2x on repeated DUMP) but REVERTED because: (1) RDB SAVE / BGSAVE / DEBUG RELOAD / replication full-sync do NOT use dump_key — they build RdbValue::List(items: Vec<Vec<u8>>) (raw elements collected from the list) and re-synthesize in fr-persist encode_compact_list_quicklist2, bypassing the store's chunk repr entirely; (2) MIGRATE + one-shot DUMP don't repeat, so lazy-on-DUMP sealing gives them nothing; (3) the cold first DUMP REGRESSED ~2x (decode_value_spans re-parse). So lazy-on-access sealing only helps repeated DUMP-command-on-same-key (niche) and net-harms the common one-shot path. ALSO: the retained fast path still re-validates every element per DUMP via retained_quicklist2_chunks_match_dump_rules (listpack_entry_encoded_len+parse per entry) — not a pure memcpy.

REAL LEVER (confirmed): make the store hold packed listpack-node chunks EAGERLY (seal-at-push when a chunk fills, matching redis quicklist node-split rules) so the FIRST serialization is already a memcpy, AND change the RDB-save path so RdbValue carries the pre-built listpack nodes (new variant) instead of Vec<Vec<u8>> raw elements + re-synthesis. Both encode_dump_quicklist2 (dump_key) and encode_compact_list_quicklist2 (RDB) currently double-parse per element (listpack_entry_encoded_len boundary pass + encode_listpack_entry encode pass each call parse_listpack_integer) — a single-pass fusion is a separate ~1.4x byte-exact sub-lever (below the 2.0 bar alone). Measured gap (default cfg, 10k): DUMP list-int 6x / list-str ~5x slower; this is the q5ody/lojpt structural family. Infra that EXISTS to build on: ListChunk::Listpack variant, retained_listpack_chunks, decode_value_spans, quicklist_packed_node_accepts, make_mut auto-unseal.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: REAL LEVER scoped 2026-06-16 (RAM+DUMP, CoralOx-free domain): ListChunk has Owned{Arc<Vec<Vec<u8>>>} (scattered, 1 heap/elem) + Listpack{packed bytes+spans} variants, but LPUSH/RPUSH ONLY build Owned (from_listpack used only on RDB load). LEVER = SEAL full INTERIOR chunks to Listpack on push (when push_back/push_front starts a new chunk, encode the now-full Owned back/front chunk). Gives: (a) list RAM compaction (128 Vec<u8> headers+heaps -> 1 listpack blob+spans, ~30%/chunk — span Vec eats some), (b) DUMP passthrough for sealed chunks. Access paths already handle both variants (get/iter). Plumbing needed: encode_listpack_strings_blob is PRIVATE in fr-persist (expose it or add a pub seal-encoder) + build ListpackValueSpans (encode then decode_listpack, or encoder returns spans). RISK: my memory warns list-compaction regressed before (0.53x VecDeque/decode-path RESTORE fix; mimalloc-defeats-buffer-REUSE) — but those were RESTORE/reuse, NOT push-time sealing (fewer allocs, mimalloc can't defeat). MUST A/B push throughput + LRANGE + RSS + DUMP before shipping (seal=O(128) amortized once/128 pushes; span-read vs Vec-index ~neutral). Moderate effort, needs the A/B to confirm not a regression. This is the 'packed-listpack-node ChunkedList' lever my memory endorses.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: CONCURRENT-COLLISION 2026-06-16: CoralOx is ACTIVELY implementing this lever (their WIP appeared in the shared working tree mid-turn, AFTER my turn-start clean check). Their seal_if_owned(&mut self, fill: i64) is REDIS-FAITHFUL — caches lp_bytes on the Owned variant + list_node_exceeds_limit(fill,...) respecting list-max-listpack-size (the real quicklist node boundary), 42 refs across packed_set + 5 fr-persist hunks. Much better than my naive 128-count version, which I ABANDONED (their save superseded my packed_set edits cleanly; no corruption). I exposed fr_persist::encode_listpack_strings_blob as pub (the encoder their seal calls) — left UNCOMMITTED in the shared tree for CoralOx to commit with their seal (their WIP won't compile without it). BACKED OFF to avoid duplicating/conflicting. CoralOx owns 99fwc. LESSON (feedback_refetch_before_committing): re-check the working tree for peer WIP before implementing a ready bead, even mid-turn.

comments.author: CoralOx
comments.text: SHIPPED pass222 quicklist-boundary listpack sealing. Lever: push-built ChunkedList deque chunks now split with Redis quicklist admission, seal completed interior chunks to retained Listpack blobs, and DUMP/DEBUG borrow packed nodes while only encoding open owned nodes. Conservative final local focused 500x DUMP rerun vs retained baseline: int 0.441884599s -> 0.253909527s (1.74x, sha e3cfb2db563e6a21db5858856eac1157e0c2956cce7155d5c6b9696f96f0e7dd), str 0.507651047s -> 0.237790137s (2.13x, sha cfd44f5fb0fc3eb5940b348b8621bafe98f11cab135a95b56cbe811fefeda9a2), wide 1.489187456s -> 0.781212485s (1.91x, sha aab2ff70f31ff440fe9327478604aa550e2b6520239eed2e3832b2c149226c0e). Retained hyperfine artifacts show 1.39x-1.44x end-to-end wins. Redis oracle DUMP/RESTORE both directions passes for 14 keys. UBS rerun exits 1 on broad existing inventories; embedded fmt/clippy/check/test clean. Score 3.0 keep.

title: COMMAND LIST omits 128 'parent|sub' namespaced subcommand entries that vendored 7.2.4 emits (acl|cat, client|kill, cluster|nodes, etc.)
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 16380:
  COMMAND LIST | wc -l
    vendored: 370 entries
    fr      : 242 entries (missing 128)
  COMMAND INFO 'client|kill'
    vendored: client|kill, -3, admin noscript loading stale, 0, 0, 0, @admin
    fr      : nil

The 128 missing entries are the 'parent|sub' namespaced rows for every container command. Examples:
  acl|cat, acl|deluser, acl|dryrun, acl|genpass, acl|getuser, acl|help,
  acl|list, acl|load, acl|log, acl|save, acl|setuser, acl|users, acl|whoami,
  client|caching, client|getname, client|getredir, client|help, client|id,
  client|info, client|kill, client|list, client|no-evict, client|no-touch,
  client|pause, client|reply, client|setinfo, client|setname, client|tracking,
  client|trackinginfo, client|unblock, client|unpause,
  cluster|addslots ... (all 25 cluster|* subcommands),
  command|count, command|docs, command|getkeys, command|getkeysandflags,
  command|help, command|info, command|list,
  config|get, config|help, config|resetstat, config|rewrite, config|set,
  function|delete, function|dump, function|flush, function|help, function|kill,
  function|list, function|load, function|restore, function|stats,
  latency|*, memory|*, module|*, object|*, pubsub|*, script|*, slowlog|*,
  xgroup|*, xinfo|*

Upstream emits these from server.c::commandCommand by walking each container command's subcommand_dict and synthesising a 'parent|sub' name with the subcommand's arity / flags / categories.

Fix: expand fr-command's COMMAND LIST emitter to walk each container's subcommand table and emit a synthetic 'parent|sub' entry per subcommand, matching upstream's name|arity|flags|categories for each. COMMAND INFO 'client|kill' must dispatch to the same metadata.

Note COMMAND COUNT (241 vendored, 242 fr) is asymmetric here: COUNT returns top-level command count only (excluding the parent|sub entries), so the missing-subcommands defect doesn't affect COUNT — see separate bead bx2i8 for the spurious sentinel entry.
close_reason:
Added SUBCOMMAND_TABLE constant with 129 namespaced parent|sub rows in fr-command/src/lib.rs, sourced by programmatic sweep of vendored Redis 7.2.4 COMMAND INFO output. Updated COMMAND (no arg), COMMAND LIST, COMMAND LIST FILTERBY PATTERN, and COMMAND INFO to walk both COMMAND_TABLE and SUBCOMMAND_TABLE. COMMAND COUNT remains top-level only per upstream commandCountCommand. Pinned via command_list_includes_namespaced_subcommands_per_upstream covering: total namespaced count, 15 representative spot-checks across all 15 containers, COMMAND COUNT staying at top-level, COMMAND INFO 'client|kill' returning per-sub metadata, and FILTERBY PATTERN 'client|*' returning all 18 client subs.

Verified byte-for-byte parity vs vendored 7.2.4 (16380): COMMAND LIST count 370/370, namespaced 129/129, COMMAND INFO 'client|kill' identical bytes including ACL categories @admin/@slow/@dangerous/@connection (derived by command_info_entry from name+flags). Diff sweep of all 129 subs against vendored COMMAND INFO shows zero divergences. COMMAND COUNT stays at 241/241.

Out of scope (deferred): COMMAND DOCS for namespaced subs (still emits top-level only). DOCS requires summary/since/group strings per sub which would mean a much larger metadata table; filed separately if needed. Per-sub key_specs sub-array fidelity also deferred — current entries emit empty key_specs with first/last/step matching upstream.

879/879 fr-command lib tests pass (excluding 4 pre-existing stream-related failures unrelated to this bead). 358/358 fr-runtime lib tests + all 138 integration tests pass. Workspace clippy clean under -D warnings.

title: CONFIG SET shutdown-on-sigterm/sigint accepts the conflicting 'save nosave' combo — vendored 7.2.4 rejects with 'shutdown options SAVE and NOSAVE can't be used simultaneously'
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 16380:
  CONFIG SET shutdown-on-sigterm 'save now nosave'
    vendored: ERR CONFIG SET failed (possibly related to argument 'shutdown-on-sigterm') - shutdown options SAVE and NOSAVE can't be used simultaneously
    fr      : OK

Upstream config.c:2378-2385 (validateShutdownOnSigFlags) explicitly checks the SAVE+NOSAVE combination and rejects it with that exact wording. Each individual token (default, save, nosave, now, force) is valid; the conflict only fires when both 'save' and 'nosave' appear in the same combination.

fr's handler at crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:8739-8765 validates the per-token allowlist but doesn't check the mutually-exclusive pair.

Fix: after the per-token allowlist check, also verify that the parsed token set doesn't include both 'save' and 'nosave'.
close_reason: Added the SAVE+NOSAVE conflict check to fr-runtime's shutdown-on-sigterm/sigint handler at fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:8739-8765, mirroring upstream config.c::validateShutdownOnSigFlags (lines 2378-2385). After the existing per-token allowlist passes, the lowercased value is split and checked for both 'save' and 'nosave' tokens; the conflict is surfaced via config_set_failed with the exact upstream wording 'shutdown options SAVE and NOSAVE can't be used simultaneously'. All other combinations (default, save+now, save+force, nosave+now+force, etc.) still flow through as OK. Pinned via config_set_shutdown_on_sig_rejects_save_nosave_combination_per_upstream which exercises 4 conflict orderings (save nosave, nosave save, save now nosave, nosave force save) for both shutdown-on-sigterm and shutdown-on-sigint, plus 7 valid non-conflicting combinations. Live differential probe vs vendored 16380 confirms identical conflict-error wording for both subkeys and identical 'OK' for valid combos. Workspace clippy clean under -D warnings.

title: DEBUG missing subcommands: STRINGMATCH-LEN, JMAP, LISTPACK-ENTRIES
description: fr rejects three DEBUG subcommands that vendored Redis 7.2.4 supports (when enable-debug-command=local is set): STRINGMATCH-LEN <pattern> <string>, JMAP (jemalloc memory map), LISTPACK-ENTRIES (toggle aggressive listpack-entry threshold). fr returns 'ERR unknown subcommand or wrong number of arguments for X. Try DEBUG HELP.' Upstream debug.c (search for stringmatchLen, jemalloc_mallctl, listpack-entries) defines these. They are diagnostic/testing helpers used by upstream's test suite (notably tests/unit/type/list-3.tcl, tests/integration/debug.tcl). Implementation effort is low: STRINGMATCH-LEN is a thin wrapper over the existing stringmatch logic to count input bytes consumed; LISTPACK-ENTRIES toggles a global ceiling; JMAP requires jemalloc which fr does not vendor and would emit a 'jemalloc not enabled' error in its absence. Recommended: implement STRINGMATCH-LEN (low effort), implement LISTPACK-ENTRIES as a no-op accepting yes/no/auto + reading state via DEBUG OBJECT, stub JMAP with 'jemalloc not enabled' error matching upstream's --without-jemalloc build behavior. Reproduce: redis-cli -p 16480 DEBUG STRINGMATCH-LEN 'a*' abc -> ERR unknown subcommand. Surfaced during REVIEW PHASE differential-probe sweep.
close_reason: Not a bug. Verified against upstream debug.c in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/debug.c: STRINGMATCH-LEN, JMAP, and LISTPACK-ENTRIES are NOT real DEBUG subcommands in Redis 7.2.4. Upstream only has STRINGMATCH-TEST (already implemented in fr at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:20517). The earlier probe appeared to show divergence because vendored rejects EVERY unknown DEBUG subcommand with the umbrella 'DEBUG command not allowed' error (enable-debug-command=no by default), whereas fr (started with enable-debug-command=yes in our probe env) correctly returned 'ERR unknown subcommand or wrong number of arguments for X. Try DEBUG HELP.' which IS the upstream wording for unknown subcommands. If vendored had been started with --enable-debug-command=yes, it would emit the same 'unknown subcommand' reply. No source code change required.

title: [perf] fr-persist fixed-width RDB radix ordering
description: Profile-backed follow-up after rejected comparison-sort/cache/bucket/capacity/CRC RDB encoder levers. Current RDB multidb profile on --dbs 4096 --entries-per-db 2 --iters 500 still shows encode_rdb_internal dominant with stable comparison sort, memcmp, rdb_encode_length/string, and memmove visible. Next distinct unowned primitive while runtime/store snapshot fusion is dependency-blocked: for entries whose keys all have the same byte length, replace comparison sorting of (&RdbEntry) by (db,key) with a stable LSD counting/radix ordering over key bytes then significant db bytes, falling back to the existing stable sort for variable-length keys. Preserve exact ascending (db,key) order and duplicate-key input tie order; FP/RNG/RDB type/length/stream bytes unchanged. Baseline current main through rch before edits; golden SHA/cmp and focused duplicate tie proof required; keep only if paired hyperfine Score>=2.0.
close_reason: Rejected: fixed-width RDB radix ordering preserved golden sha256 9c0b4109f8ece11500fd11e9d559c1c70cade75ca87a60ff78b890e9fc627e95 with cmp pass and focused RCH duplicate tie-order test passed, but paired hyperfine regressed baseline 159.0 ms +/-3.9 to candidate 543.1 ms +/-29.9 on fr-persist-rdb-multidb --dbs 4096 --entries-per-db 2 --iters 500; baseline was 3.42x +/-0.21 faster, so Score<2.0. Source hunk removed. Next primitive remains frankenredis-nx6lv runtime/store snapshot-builder fusion once active blockers clear.

title: SCAN MATCH <prefix>* materializes ALL db keys every call (handle_scan_command keys_in_db O(n)/call) — range-prune to prefix
description: handle_scan_command called keys_in_db (materialize + reap ALL db keys) then glob-filtered in the runtime on EVERY SCAN call — O(n) per call, O(n^2/count) for a full iteration, regardless of how few keys match. Redis SCAN is ~O(count) per call. Fix: when MATCH has a literal prefix, use keys_matching_in_db (2wgom range-prune over the ordered keyspace) so only the prefix range is materialized — O(log n + matches) per call, an asymmetry redis's unordered dict cannot exploit. Byte-identical (same glob_match, same due-volatile reaping, same cursor-indexed page). Also pruned the test-only Store::scan() helper to match. A/B (Store::scan, 200050 keys / 50 late-prefix matches): unpruned full walk 4.2ms vs pruned 1-call 27us = 157x.
close_reason: Fixed in 4d3997c75: handle_scan_command uses keys_matching_in_db (prefix range-prune) when MATCH present — O(n)/call -> O(log n+matches)/call. Byte-identical (505/0 runtime + 606/0 store). A/B 157x (200050 keys, 50 late-prefix matches).

title: Lua attempt-to-X errors omit local/field/upvalue/global label for index/arith/length/unary-neg
description:
Lua 5.1's runtime errors include the accessor that resolved to the bad
value: "attempt to index local 'mylocal' (a nil value)",
"attempt to perform arithmetic on field 'count' (a string value)", etc.
fr already includes the label for the call and concatenate paths but
omits it for index, arithmetic (binary and unary -), and length (#).

Probed against vendored Redis 7.2.4 on :16380 / fr on :16480:

  local t = nil; return t.field
    vendored: attempt to index local 't' (a nil value)
    fr:       attempt to index a nil value

  local t = {a=nil}; return t.a.b
    vendored: attempt to index field 'a' (a nil value)
    fr:       attempt to index a nil value

  local x = nil; return x + 1
    vendored: attempt to perform arithmetic on local 'x' (a nil value)
    fr:       attempt to perform arithmetic on a nil value

  local s = nil; return #s
    vendored: attempt to get length of local 's' (a nil value)
    fr:       attempt to get length of a nil value

  local b = true; return b.f
    vendored: attempt to index local 'b' (a boolean value)
    fr:       attempt to index a boolean value

  local nm = 5; return nm.f
    vendored: attempt to index local 'nm' (a number value)
    fr:       attempt to index a number value

Upstream (lvm.c::luaG_typeerror): walks the call stack at runtime to
recover the syntactic name of the offending operand from the bytecode's
debug info. fr's tree-walker has direct access to the AST expression so
it can synthesize the same label without bytecode debug info.

The infrastructure already exists in fr — `callee_label(expr, env)`
returns "local 'X'" / "field 'X'" / "upvalue 'X'" / "global 'X'" / "?"
and is used by the call and concatenate paths. Wire it into:

- Expr::Index (line ~3206) and Expr::Field (~3221) — for "attempt to index"
- Expr::UnaryOp(Neg, …) (line ~3170) — for "attempt to perform arithmetic"
- Expr::UnaryOp(Len, …) (line ~3184) — for "attempt to get length of"
- Expr::BinOp arithmetic arms — for "attempt to perform arithmetic"

Test plan: pin each of the 6 cases above plus regressions where the
label is empty (e.g. arithmetic on a function-call result).
close_reason: Added type_error_with_label helper in crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs that builds the upstream-shaped wording 'attempt to <op> <label> (a TYPE value)' using the existing callee_label helper. Wired into four error sites: Expr::Index and Expr::Field (now name the indexed local/field/upvalue/global), UnaryOp::Neg and UnaryOp::Len (now label the operand), and binary arithmetic operators (Add/Sub/Mul/Div/Mod/Pow) after metamethod fallback. Regression test lua_attempt_to_X_errors_include_accessor_label_9ckvq pins 15 cases including the unlabeled fallback when the operand has no syntactic name. Live diff vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 on :16380 clean across 17 probes covering nil/bool/number/table/string operands and field/local accessors.

title: Guard BLMPOP/BZMPOP numeric conversions
close_reason: Guarded count/numkeys conversions (BLMPOP/BZMPOP + related limits)

title: Lua math.deg/rad/sinh/cosh/tanh missing + nan vs -nan emission
description: Several math library functions Redis 7.2.4's Lua 5.1 exposes are not registered in fr's globals: math.deg, math.rad, math.sinh, math.cosh, math.tanh. Scripts using them get 'attempt to call a nil value'. (math.atan2, math.ldexp, math.log/log10, math.pow/^ already work.) Additionally tostring(0/0) emits 'nan' in fr but '-nan' in vendored -- C printf %g of a quiet NaN with a negative sign bit (which IEEE 754 0/0 produces on x86-64) prints '-nan'. Fix: (1) add the five trig helpers to the math globals registration alongside floor/ceil/abs (lib.rs ~line 1829); (2) wire dispatch handlers in call_builtin's math.* arm using Rust's stdlib (f64::to_degrees, to_radians, sinh, cosh, tanh); (3) update lua_number_to_string's NaN branch to emit '-nan' instead of 'nan' to match C %.14g on x86-64.
close_reason: Five missing Lua math helpers added + NaN-sign format. (1) Added math.deg/math.rad/math.sinh/math.cosh/math.tanh to the math globals registration in fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:1850 and the call_builtin dispatch arm at line 3662 -- Rust's f64 stdlib supplies each directly (to_degrees, to_radians, sinh, cosh, tanh). (2) Updated lua_number_to_string's NaN special case to emit '-nan' when n.is_sign_negative() (which IEEE 754 0/0 produces on x86-64), matching C printf %g via Lua's %.14g. Live differential probe: math.deg(pi)=180, math.rad(180)=pi, math.sinh(0)=0, math.cosh(0)=1, math.tanh(0)=0, tostring(0/0)='-nan' all byte-exact with vendored. Tests: fr-command 910 pass.

title: Lua __newindex metamethod (table assignment hook)
description: Sub-bead of frankenredis-00gjf metamethods epic. Lua 5.1 invokes __newindex(table, key, value) when ASSIGNING to a missing/nil key on a table whose metatable has __newindex. Probe: 'local stored={}; local t=setmetatable({},{__newindex=function(_,k,v) stored[k]=v end}); t.x=1; return stored.x' → vendored 1, fr nil. Also __newindex as TABLE delegates writes there. Implementation: at every table-assignment site (Stmt::Assign with Expr::Index/Expr::Field LHS), check the metatable __newindex; rawset bypasses (already correct). Touch sites: assign_to_field/assign_to_index logic in exec_stmt Assign branch (search for 'Expr::Field' inside Assign). Filed during 00gjf decomposition.
close_reason: Added table_assign_with_newindex() routing all table writes through Lua 5.1's __newindex chain. Existing keys bypass the metamethod and write directly; missing keys with __newindex=function invoke (table, key, value); __newindex=table cascades the write (recursively, up to 16 hops); __newindex=non-nil-non-callable emits 'attempt to index a TYPE value' (matching vendored which tries to index the handler value); rawset() still bypasses entirely since it goes through LuaTable::set directly. Wired into table_set_by_expr so all Stmt::Assign LHS Expr::Field and Expr::Index sites honor it. Verified live diff vs vendored on port 16380: 10/10 cases match including function/table handlers, existing-key bypass, rawset bypass, bracket-indexing, nil-value writes, chained-table cascade, recursive __newindex (depth=3 cap), and the non-callable error path. Pinned by lua_newindex_metamethod_intercepts_table_assignment covering 7 sub-cases. All 919 fr-command lib tests pass. Parent bead frankenredis-00gjf remains open for __concat, arithmetic ops, comparison ops, __len, __tostring.
