title: fr-command: RESP3 map-typed replies emit as Array instead of Map (HGETALL, CONFIG GET, XINFO STREAM, CLUSTER SHARDS, CLIENT INFO, etc.)
description:
Multiple commands return map-typed replies in RESP3 mode upstream (Redis 7.2 networking.c::addReplyMap*) but fr's command implementations always emit RespFrame::Array regardless of session.resp_protocol_version. Affected commands include:

  - HGETALL h
  - CONFIG GET <param> (returns key/value map)
  - HRANDFIELD h N WITHVALUES
  - CLIENT INFO (single client info as RESP3 map)
  - XINFO STREAM/GROUPS/CONSUMERS

When redis-cli connects with -3, fr replies with array form (display: 'k\nv\nk\nv\n...'); upstream replies with map form (display: 'k v\nk v\n...').

Acceptance: when session.resp_protocol_version == 3, the listed commands emit RespFrame::Map(Some(pairs)) where each pair is (key, value). For fr-command this means routing the protocol_version through the dispatch path or providing a render-time override. fr-protocol already has the Map variant (crates/fr-protocol/src/lib.rs:13).

Reference: ad-hoc differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored redis 7.2.4 (port 16701) with -3 flag.

title: v8s5 part a: document upstream DUMP/RESTORE payload format (fr-store reference)
description:
Preparation sub-bead for br-frankenredis-v8s5. Extract the upstream DUMP payload layout from legacy_redis_code/redis/src/cluster.c::dumpCommand + rdb.c::rdbSaveObject + restoreCommand + rdb.c::rdbLoadObject and write it up as crates/fr-store/docs/dump-format.md.

Scope:
  * Overall payload: type_byte + rdbSaveObject + version (u16 LE) + CRC64 (u64 LE).
  * Version field semantics (currently upstream is version 11 for 7.2.x, 12 for 7.4.x).
  * Per-type rdbSaveObject layout for STRING/LIST_QUICKLIST_2/SET_INTSET/SET_LISTPACK/HASH_LISTPACK/HASH_METADATA/ZSET_LISTPACK/STREAM_LISTPACKS_3.
  * CRC64 polynomial (Redis CRC64 with the Jones polynomial, already implemented at crates/fr-persist/src/lib.rs:848).
  * A comparison table: current frankenredis-internal layout vs upstream expectation, field-by-field.

No code changes.
close_reason: Reference doc crates/fr-store/docs/dump-format.md already exists (260 lines) covering envelope/footer, RDB length + string encodings, all upstream type tags (0/2/4/5/11/16/17/18/20/21 + Redis 7.4 24/25), per-type body layouts, fr-store delta vs upstream, and decoder/encoder checklist. No further work needed for the docs-only sub-bead.

title: CONFIG GET missing hidden parameters: use-exit-on-panic, aof-disable-auto-gc, rdb-key-save-delay, key-load-delay, watchdog-period, cluster-ping-interval, loading-process-events-interval-bytes
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:

  127.0.0.1:16380> CONFIG GET aof-disable-auto-gc
  1) "aof-disable-auto-gc"
  2) "no"
  127.0.0.1:16480> CONFIG GET aof-disable-auto-gc
  (empty array)

Same divergence for: use-exit-on-panic (no), aof-disable-auto-gc (no), rdb-key-save-delay (0), key-load-delay (0), watchdog-period (0), cluster-ping-interval (0), loading-process-events-interval-bytes (2097152).

Per upstream config.c lines 3091, 3096, 3122, 3177, 3178, 3183, 3192, 3203, 3232 — these are HIDDEN_CONFIG entries (don't appear in CONFIG GET '*' wildcard sweep but DO respond when explicitly named). Defaults from createBoolConfig/createIntConfig/createOffTConfig third-from-last argument.

Root cause: CONFIG_STATIC_PARAMS in fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (line 351) does not list these 7 hidden parameters. canonical_static_config_param returns None and CONFIG GET resolves to empty array.

Fix: add the 7 entries to CONFIG_STATIC_PARAMS with their upstream default values. They should NOT be exposed via CONFIG GET '*' wildcard (already hidden via the existing 'don't list' machinery if any; otherwise add a hidden tag), but should respond to direct CONFIG GET <name>.

Acceptance: CONFIG GET <name> for each of the 7 returns the upstream default value.
close_reason: Added CONFIG_STATIC_HIDDEN_PARAMS table (use-exit-on-panic, aof-disable-auto-gc, rdb-key-save-delay, key-load-delay, watchdog-period, cluster-ping-interval, loading-process-events-interval-bytes) with upstream defaults from config.c lines 3091/3096/3177/3178/3183/3203/3232. handle_config_get only consults this table for literal (non-glob) patterns, mirroring upstream config.c::configGetCommand line 998 'if (config->flags & HIDDEN_CONFIG) continue;' which skips hidden configs in wildcard sweeps. canonical_static_config_param now chains both tables. Verified via differential probe: all 7 names respond to exact CONFIG GET (matches vendored), zero appear in CONFIG GET '*' (also matches vendored). Pin: config_get_returns_hidden_params_only_for_exact_name_per_upstream.

title: perf: packed listpack-style encoding for SMALL generic hash/set/zset/list (succinct DS, ~2x mem + cache locality)
description:
NEXT BIG STRUCTURAL SWING (alien-graveyard: succinct/compressed data structures + inline small objects). fr stores small collections in IndexMap/IndexSet/BTreeMap/VecDeque = one heap alloc PER ELEMENT + container overhead. Redis packs small collections (<= hash/set/zset-max-listpack-entries, list <8KB node) into a single contiguous LISTPACK byte buffer (1 alloc, cache-friendly linear scan), only promoting to the hashtable/skiplist/quicklist at the threshold. fr already does this for INT sets (SetValue::Int = sorted Vec<i64>, 8B/member, project_intset_setvalue) — extend the same idea to GENERIC small collections: a SmallStr-packed listpack (len-prefixed entries in one Vec<u8> + an offsets index, or scan-on-access) behind the existing OBJECT ENCODING 'listpack' state.

WHY: matches redis's actual encoding (fr currently REPORTS listpack via OBJECT ENCODING but stores IndexMap — a memory parity GAP). For a hash/set of N small entries: fr ~N*(24B Vec hdr + heap block + hashtable slot) vs listpack ~sum(entry bytes)+headers in 1 alloc. TARGET: ~2x memory on small-collection-heavy keyspaces + cache-locality win on HGETALL/SMEMBERS/ZRANGE/LRANGE iteration (1 contiguous walk vs N pointer chases).

SCOPE: large, multi-firing. Per-type: add a Packed variant to Value::Hash/Set(Generic)/SortedSet/List that holds a listpack buffer; ops (HSET/HGET/HDEL/SADD/.../LPUSH/LRANGE) operate on the packed form below threshold, convert to the current rich structure at the threshold (the conversion point already exists for OBJECT ENCODING). Isomorphism: insertion-order + byte-output identical (listpack preserves insertion order = IndexMap order); int-set precedent (hjob8) shows the pattern is byte-exact-achievable. Start with ONE type (set Generic, simplest, mirrors SetValue::Int) and prove before expanding.
close_reason: Completed packed zset wiring: SortedSet now uses PackedZSet below zset listpack thresholds and promotes one-way to full storage; harness 8192x48 mean 478.4ms -> 290.6ms (1.65x), insert/read/pop phase wins, state_digest 324091d9da416741 and checksum 17527362544575379395 unchanged; RCH fr-store zset/check/clippy plus cargo fmt check passed.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: STEP 1 DONE + SHIPPED (11df49fc4): the PackedStrSet primitive — crates/fr-store/src/packed_set.rs. Members packed as [varint len][bytes] in one Vec<u8>, insertion order. insert(dedup)/contains/remove(order-preserving)/iter/len/byte_len/FromIterator. Proven IDENTICAL to insertion-ordered IndexSet via proptest (arbitrary insert/remove stream: insert/remove return-value + len + contains + full iter order all match at every step) + unit tests (dedup, mid/head/tail removal order, empty member, varint boundary 127/128/1000). #[allow(dead_code)] (mod decl selectively staged so it didn't touch BlackThrush's concurrent qchm7 fr-store WIP). STEP 2 (wiring, blocked on fr-store going quiet): add SetValue::Packed variant, route SADD/SREM/SISMEMBER/SMEMBERS/SCARD/SPOP through it below set-max-listpack-entries(128)/-value(64), promote to IndexSet::Generic at threshold. The packed form already matches IndexSet iteration order so SMEMBERS/SSCAN/SPOP stay byte-exact.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: STEP 2 DONE + SHIPPED (eb1687131): wired PackedStrSet into generic sets. GenericSet is now an enum {Packed(PackedStrSet) | Hash(IndexSet)} replacing the IndexSet type alias — packs small string sets in one buffer, promotes to IndexSet hashtable past 128 entries/64-byte values. Drop-in (same insert/contains/shift_remove/get_index/retain/iter/FromIterator/IntoIterator), so all SADD/SREM/SMEMBERS/SPOP/SINTER/SUNION/SDIFF + the encoding-flag + DUMP paths compile and behave unchanged (encoding=sticky size flag, DUMP=RdbValue::Set(Vec) — both member-iteration-based = transparent to the variant). fr-store 523 tests pass; clippy clean; live differential vs 7.2.4 byte-exact across SMEMBERS/SCARD/SISMEMBER/SREM/SORT/OBJECT-ENCODING(generic/intset/>128-promotion/>64-value/grow-then-srem-stickiness)/SINTER/SDIFF/SINTERCARD. (SUNION order = fr's pre-existing v.sort() @8281, unspecified set-op order, NOT this change.) Committed under BlackThrush's concurrent fr-store qchm7 WIP via selective staging (5 hunks <line 10000 via git apply --cached; their hunks @10114+ left unstaged). STEP 3: replicate for small hashes (HSET/HGETALL) + lists; optionally honour configured set-max-listpack-entries>128 for storage promotion.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: STEP 3 DONE + SHIPPED (60f876462): wired packed encoding into HASHES. HashFieldMap is now enum {Packed(PackedStrMap) | Hash(IndexMap)} — small hashes in one [vint klen][k][vint vlen][v] buffer, promote to IndexMap past 128 entries/64-byte fields-or-values. PackedStrMap: insert keeps field position + splices value in place (matches IndexMap::insert return-old-value + order), proptest-proven equivalent to IndexMap. HSETNX entry() rewritten to contains_key/insert. CROSS-CRATE RIPPLE (vs the set, which Cow-iter hid): the field-map iter now borrows &[u8] not &Vec<u8>, so ~12 .clone()->.to_vec() fixes in fr-store + 2 in fr-runtime's store_to_rdb_entries RDB serializer (the ONLY dependent-crate breakage — full workspace check confirmed). fr-store 525 tests + workspace build + clippy clean; live differential vs 7.2.4 byte-exact across 25 hash cases (HGET/HGETALL/HKEYS/HVALS/HSET-update-keeps-position/HDEL/HSETNX/HMGET/HSTRLEN/HINCRBY/encoding incl >128+>64 promotion/DUMP/TYPE). Committed under BlackThrush's qchm7 fr-store WIP + another agent's fr-runtime WIP via CONTENT-based selective staging (awk-exclude their hunks by @@ -line / content; git apply --cached; git diff --cached verified). STEP 4: LISTS (VecDeque->packed).

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
STEP 4a DONE + SHIPPED (df22f2376): PackedList primitive — list analogue of PackedStrSet/PackedStrMap. Packs small lists into one varint-prefixed buffer; VecDeque-compatible API (push_back/front, pop_back/front, get, set for LSET which splices in place, insert for LINSERT before-index, remove, retain, iter, FromIterator). PROPTEST-PROVEN byte-exact equivalent to VecDeque<Vec<u8>> over arbitrary push/pop-both-ends/get/set/insert/remove/retain streams. Marked allow(dead_code) until wired, so committed packed_set.rs alone with ZERO contention (no lib.rs touch).

STEP 4b (WIRING — needs a quiet fr-store; 47 Value::List sites in fr-store lib.rs):
1. Value::List(VecDeque) is INLINE 24B. A ListValue newtype enum {Packed|Deque} is ~32-40B and would GROW Value past 32B, so it must be BOXED: Value::List(Box<ListValue>). (Set/Hash were already boxed so their inner-type swap was free; List is the exception — this is the extra complexity vs steps 2/3.) Construction sites need Box::new.
2. Rewrite LSET IndexMut (l index-assign) to a set() call (packed cannot return &mut Vec).
3. ListValue exposes the VecDeque API the ops use: len/is_empty/push_back/push_front/pop_front/pop_back/get/set/insert/remove/retain/iter.
4. iter yields slice not &Vec, so the ONE dependent-crate ripple is fr-runtime store_to_rdb_entries (l.iter().cloned() -> .map(to_vec)) — same class as hash step 3.
5. Update the value_size test (stays 32 via boxing). Promote Packed->Deque past 128 entries / 64-byte elements.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
STEP 4b DONE + SHIPPED (544d2f2af): LISTS now use the packed encoding. Value::List is now Box<ListValue> (was inline VecDeque); ListValue = enum {Packed(PackedList) | Deque}, small lists in one buffer, promote to VecDeque past 128 entries/64-byte elements. Boxing was needed (unlike Set/Hash, already boxed) to keep Value at 32B. Two awkward spots handled: LSET index-assign -> set(), LPOS reverse scan -> indexed (packed iter is forward-only). Cross-crate ripple: 1 site each in fr-command (DEBUG OBJECT digest) + fr-runtime (RDB). 527 fr-store tests + workspace build + clippy + 33-case live list differential vs 7.2.4 byte-exact (LRANGE/LINDEX/LSET/LINSERT/LPOS-rev/LREM/LTRIM/encoding+promotion/RPOPLPUSH).

REMAINING: the 4th type — ZSET (SortedSet) — is NOT yet packed. It is the HARDEST (SortedSet = BTreeMap + order-statistic treap for O(log n) ZRANK; a packed small-zset needs (member,score) pairs with ZSCORE/ZADD/ZRANK/ZRANGE/ZRANGEBYSCORE/ZRANGEBYLEX/ZINCRBY) AND the MOST contended (BlackThrush's stale qchm7 WIP lives in the zset zrangebylex code). The set/hash/list win is shipped; zset is a dedicated future swing. DONE so far: set (eb1687131), hash (60f876462), list (544d2f2af). The commit message's "9mh3o COMPLETE" referred to the set/hash/list trio; zset is the open remainder.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
STEP 5a DONE + SHIPPED (98a0068c9): PackedZSet primitive — the LAST of the four packed primitives. Packs a small zset into one [vint mlen][member][f64 score] buffer kept in (score,member) sorted order. Reuses ScoreMember's EXACT comparator (canon-zero score total_cmp, then member-bytes asc) so packed iteration == SortedSet.ordered iteration -> ZRANGE/ZRANK byte-exact once wired. API: insert(ZADD, repositions on score change), get_score, contains, remove, rank(0-based ZRANK), iter(asc), len. PROPTEST-PROVEN: arbitrary ZADD/ZREM/ZSCORE stream asserts iter-order + per-member rank + score + len match a reference sorted by the same comparator; unit test covers tie-break, score-update reposition, +0.0/-0.0 equivalence. Committed packed_set.rs alone (no lib.rs) = zero contention.

PROGRESS: all 4 packed primitives now exist (PackedStrSet/Map/List/ZSet). WIRED: set (eb1687131), hash (60f876462), list (544d2f2af). NOT wired: ZSET (step 5b).

STEP 5b (zset wiring — the HARDEST of the four, multi-firing): SortedSet has 3 fields (dict member->score HashMap, ordered BTreeMap<ScoreMember>, lazy ZRankTreap) + ~20 methods (insert/remove/get_score/iter_asc/iter_desc/for_each_in_score_range/index_slice_asc/desc/pop_min/pop_max/rank/rev_rank). A packed small-zset must reimplement all of these (mostly O(n) scans on the sorted buffer), promoting to the full BTreeMap+treap past zset-max-listpack-entries(128)/value(64). Best on a QUIET fr-store: BlackThrush's stale qchm7 WIP (zrangebylex_withscores_limited @fr-store:10104) sits in the zset code. The cross-crate iter ripple (like list/hash) will hit fr-runtime RDB + maybe fr-command digest. Likely needs SortedSet boxed already (it is: Value::SortedSet(Box<SortedSet>)) so the inner swap is free (no Value-size growth, unlike list).

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
STEP 5a+ DONE + SHIPPED (b0b53b94b): extended PackedZSet to the FULL SortedSet read API. Added iter_desc, index_slice_asc/desc (ZRANGE-by-rank), for_each_in_score_range (ZRANGEBYSCORE/ZCOUNT, inclusive canon-score filter matching min_for_score(lo)..=max_for_score(hi)), pop_min/pop_max (ZPOPMIN/MAX). All O(n) scans, each mirroring the exact SortedSet method semantics. Proptest-proven: extended the equivalence harness (index_slice/iter_desc/for_each_in_score_range vs reference after every op) + a pop_min/max alternating-drain proptest vs a reference VecDeque. Committed packed_set.rs alone = zero contention.

PackedZSet now supports EVERY SortedSet read op: insert/get_score/contains/remove/rank/iter/iter_desc/index_slice_asc/desc/for_each_in_score_range/pop_min/pop_max/len. So step 5b is now PURE DELEGATION (no new algorithm work):
1. SortedSet struct -> enum {Packed(PackedZSet) | Full{dict, ordered, rank_tree}}.
2. ~18 SortedSet methods -> match arms (Packed delegates to PackedZSet; Full keeps current BTreeMap+treap logic). Promotion Packed->Full past zset-max-listpack-entries(128)/value(64).
3. iter_asc/iter_desc item type (&Vec<u8>,&f64) -> (&[u8],f64): ~22 caller adaptations (.clone()->.to_vec()), scattered 8938-15144 in fr-store, INTERLEAVED with BlackThrush's stale qchm7 hunk @fr-store:10104.
4. 3 external field-access sites: 13177 (zs.dict.get -> zs.get_score, exists); 9361/9371 (zrangebyscore_withscores_limited zs.ordered.range with ScoreBound inclusivity+rev+skip/take -> needs a PackedZSet score_bound_range method OR handle in the Full arm only initially). NOTE: this ONE site (the ScoreBound range) is the only remaining non-trivial bit; everything else is mechanical.
5. NO cross-crate ripple (zset is NOT iterated in fr-runtime/fr-command/fr-persist — verified). SortedSet is already Box<>, so no Value-size/boxing churn.
Best on a QUIET fr-store. Estimated ~1.5h mechanical pass.

title: Canonicalize zero in sorted-set BTree ordering

title: XSETID MAXDELETEDID 0-0 must not clear stream tombstone watermark
description: Redis 7.2.4 xsetidCommand only assigns s->max_deleted_entry_id when the provided MAXDELETEDID is non-zero. FrankenRedis currently removes stream_max_deleted_ids when xsetid_with_metadata receives Some((0,0)), so a command sequence that first XDELs an entry and then runs XSETID key <higher-id> MAXDELETEDID 0-0 loses the upstream-visible max-deleted-entry-id in XINFO STREAM. Fix fr-store to preserve the existing tombstone watermark for the zero option.
close_reason: Completed fr-store XSETID MAXDELETEDID 0-0 parity fix

title: Lua cjson.decode error message wording differs from upstream
description:
Vendored Redis 7.2.4 uses the bundled cjson decoder which produces precise error messages like:

  user_script:1: Expected value but found invalid token at character 6

fr emits a generic wrapper instead:

  invalid JSON: [1,2,not_json

Reproduction:
  EVAL "return cjson.decode('[1,2,not_json')" 0
    vendored: ERR user_script:1: Expected value but found invalid token at character 6 script: <sha>, on @user_script:1.
    fr:       ERR invalid JSON: [1,2,not_json script: <sha>, on @user_script:1.

Impact: low — error message wording only. Real applications generally pcall around cjson.decode and inspect the result, not the message text, so this is mainly a parity-pinning issue.

Implementation: fr's cjson.decode handler (likely in crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs) needs to produce upstream-shaped messages keyed by the character offset and the kind of parse failure. Vendored cjson messages include:
  - Expected value but found invalid token at character N
  - Expected colon but found T at character N
  - Expected comma or end of array but found T at character N
  - Unterminated string at character N
  - Invalid string character at character N
  - etc.

Test plan: pin three or four canonical messages (invalid token, unterminated string, expected colon) under EVAL and run them against both servers.
close_reason: completed

title: [perf] Borrow list-push bytes into PackedList
description: Post-cms7p LPUSH profile still points at Store::lpush/ListValue work. Two smaller levers failed Score>=2.0: per-command memory-cache delta (frankenredis-63c8x) and PackedList front in-place encode (frankenredis-yjedr). Deeper primitive: Store::lpush/rpush currently converts every pushed value to Vec<u8> before ListValue::push_front/push_back, even when the list remains in PackedList and immediately copies the bytes into the packed buffer, dropping the temporary allocation. One lever only: add borrowed ListValue push methods that feed &[u8] directly to PackedList and allocate Vec<u8> only if maybe_promote/promoted Deque ownership requires it; then use those methods from Store::lpush/rpush. Preserve packed record bytes, list order, promotion thresholds, object encoding/listpack accounting, dirty counters, integer replies, wrongtype, lazy expiry, LFU/touch, RNG/tie/FP. Baseline current main with rch-built fr-server/fr-bench first; prove raw golden SHA unchanged; keep only Score>=2.0.
close_reason: Rejected: borrowed PackedList list-push bytes produced no credible LPUSH win (paired n1M 1.00 +/- 0.07x, Score 0.12 < 2.0); source hunk removed, evidence retained.

title: SENTINEL MONITOR should match upstream port parse errors
description: Redis 7.2.4 sentinel.c parses SENTINEL MONITOR <port> with getLongFromObjectOrReply(..., 'Invalid port'), so non-integer ports return ERR Invalid port. Numeric values are then passed to createSentinelRedisInstance; ports outside 0..65535 fail through sentinelCheckCreateInstanceErrors as ERR Invalid port number. fr-sentinel parses directly as u16 and returns ERR Invalid port number for both malformed and out-of-range inputs, without the upstream period. Scope: crates/fr-sentinel only; parse as signed integer, split malformed vs range errors, and add regression coverage.
close_reason: Split malformed vs out-of-range port errors to match upstream wording

title: fr-command has 6 pre-existing clippy errors blocking '--all-targets -D warnings' CI
description:
`cargo clippy -p fr-command --lib -- -D warnings` fails with 6 pre-existing errors that block any swarm agent's clean-clippy step:

  16644:31 fn wait_cmd unused variable `store`
  19659:4  fn bitfield_clamp is never used (dead code)
  3882:58  manual char comparison `|c| c == 'p' || c == 'P'` -> `['p','P']`
  3905:20  redundant guard `Some(s) if s.is_empty()`
  9961:24  collapsible `if let ... if ident == 'callback'`
  lua_eval.rs:5127  collapsible `if let LuaValue::Table` + `if let LuaValue::Str`

All mechanical lint cleanups. The bitfield_clamp dead code requires a judgment call (delete vs prefix _ vs wire it up); rest are pure refactors with no behavior change.
close_reason: Removed unused store binding (wait_cmd → _store), deleted dead bitfield_clamp wrapper, replaced manual char comparison with array, replaced redundant guard with literal pattern, collapsed two if-let nests, removed duplicated #[test] attribute on swapdb_via_dispatch_rejected_in_cluster_mode (was causing double registration). fr-command --all-targets clippy now green under -D warnings. fr-command 892 / fr-runtime 348 / fr-store 332 lib tests pass.

title: perf: adaptive int-set difference (linear-merge for similar sizes) — 30.8x SDIFF of two large intsets
description: Follow-up to uwj3o. SetValue::retain_diff int-int path used a.retain(|n| b.binary_search(n).is_err()) = O(|a|.log|b|). Added diff_sorted_i64 with ratio-adaptive dispatch (GALLOP_RATIO=32): linear merge O(|a|+|b|) when sizes similar (30.85x on 100k SDIFF 100k), cache-warm full-array binary search when b dwarfs a (1.09x, no regression). Powers SDIFF/SDIFFSTORE; a (first set) is the accumulator, never reordered. Byte-identical output (6000 random + edge cases vs binary-search reference, disjoint-from-b checked, golden fnv 0xa92b1154f1373a89). Graveyard primitive: adaptive set difference.
close_reason: Shipped 4c6589df6: adaptive int-set difference. retain_diff int-int now dispatches by size ratio (GALLOP_RATIO=32) — linear merge O(|a|+|b|) for similar sizes (30.85x on 100k SDIFF 100k), cache-warm full-array binary search when b dwarfs a (1.09x, no regression). Byte-identical output proven (6000 random + edge cases, kept-members-absent-from-b checked, golden FNV 0xa92b1154f1373a89); 572 fr-store tests pass.

title: BITCOUNT present-key double-counts keyspace_hits (key_type precheck + store.bitcount both record)
close_reason: FIXED f130a7e20: store.bitcount uses no-stat drop_if_expired instead of record_keyspace_lookup (both prod callers precheck via counting key_type). Present-key keyspace_hits now +1 (was +2) matching redis; values + missing/wrongtype unchanged. cmdstat_keyspace_parity_gate re-includes BITCOUNT present and PASSES (35 rows byte-exact).

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Found by new scripts/cmdstat_keyspace_parity_gate.py. BITCOUNT <present-string-key> records keyspace_hits+2 vs redis +1. Root: bitcount() command does store.key_type() (record_keyspace_lookup -> +1) for the upstream WRONGTYPE-before-argv-parse precedence (oss8i), THEN store.bitcount() calls record_keyspace_lookup AGAIN (+1). redis bitcountCommand does ONE lookupKeyReadOrReply. Affects BOTH generic (fr-command bitcount ~13070) AND the borrow fast path (fr-runtime execute_plain_bitcount_borrowed ~10524), which both precheck via key_type then call store.bitcount. Missing-key form is CORRECT (key_type miss, store.bitcount not reached). FIX (multi-crate, needs focused turn): give store.bitcount a no-stat variant (or drop its record_keyspace_lookup since both prod callers precheck with the counting key_type) and update fr-store unit tests at ~29625/37353 that call store.bitcount directly. Excluded from the gate as KNOWN until fixed.

title: EXPIRE-family/SETEX/GETEX inside Lua scripts and MULTI/EXEC propagate verbatim -> replica TTL drift (mdrk8/dt3v0 gap)
description: The relative-time->absolute propagation rewrite (mdrk8 EXPIRE->PEXPIREAT, dt3v0 SETEX/SET-EX/GETEX->PXAT) lives in Runtime::rewrite_relative_expire_for_propagation and is only called on the DIRECT-dispatch arm. The Lua/FCALL path (lua_eval redis_call) and MULTI/EXEC path (handle_exec_command) only call the reply-driven canonical fr_command::rewrite_effect_command_for_propagation, which does NOT include the expire rewrite. So EXPIRE/PEXPIRE/SETEX/GETEX run inside a script or transaction propagate VERBATIM and the replica recomputes the relative deadline at its later receive time -> PEXPIRETIME drift. Confirmed via fr master->replica: direct EXPIRE k3 -> PEXPIREAT (drift 0); script EXPIRE k2 -> verbatim EXPIRE (master 1780479434935 vs replica 1780479434942, ~7ms); transaction EXPIRE k1 verbatim (~9ms drift). Fix: fold the expire rewrite into the canonical fn (add now_ms param) so all three propagation entry points apply it; then the direct arm can drop its separate expire branch too (completes the myegu unification). Same class as mdrk8/dt3v0.
close_reason: Fixed + shipped (commit c2d760f9e). The relative-expire propagation rewrite (mdrk8/dt3v0) was only on the direct-dispatch arm; EXPIRE/SETEX/GETEX inside Lua scripts and MULTI/EXEC propagated verbatim and drifted the replica TTL (script ~7ms, tx ~9ms). Moved the rewrite into fr-command and folded it into the canonical rewrite_effect_command_for_propagation (now takes now_ms); all 3 propagation entry points apply it. Runtime's separate expire methods + direct-arm branch removed (completes the myegu unification). Verified end-to-end master to replica: EXPIRE in tx, EXPIRE+SETEX in script, direct EXPIRE all propagate absolute form with PEXPIRETIME drift = 0. fr-runtime 538 + fr-command 1197 pass.

Remaining digest gates are parity_suite-protected (it launches both servers with --enable-debug-command), so the phantom-divergence only affects manual runs — lower priority; reopen if a specific gate bites.

title: EPIC: Harden security beyond RESP parsing — persistence integrity, connection auth, and adversarial resilience
description:
# Security Hardening Epic

## Why
AGENTS.md Security Doctrine requires: "Defend against malformed protocol frames, replay/order attacks, and persistence tampering." The reality check found:
- RESP parser correctly rejects malformed frames ✓
- CRC64 checksums on RDB files ✓  
- No evidence of replay attack defense beyond basic auth
- No evidence of persistence tampering detection beyond CRC64
- No adversarial fuzzing of the RESP parser or command handlers
- No rate limiting or connection throttling

## What Success Looks Like
- Fuzz test for RESP parser (catches crashes on malformed input)
- Fuzz test for RDB decoder (catches crashes on corrupted files)
- AOF integrity verification (detect truncation/corruption on load)
- Documentation of threat model per AGENTS.md requirements
close_reason: All sub-tasks complete: RESP parser fuzz (29c7, 40K inputs), RDB/AOF decoder fuzz (9zk5, 30K inputs), threat model documentation (fap1, 6 subsystems). Zero panics found in 70K total random inputs.

title: [perf] Clone-free client tracking invalidation session scan
description: Profile-backed pass 3 under no-gaps request-path allocation campaign. Current HEAD 39bd0c2ba SET p16 baseline via rch-built fr-server/fr-bench: FrankenRedis hyperfine 0.829600s +/-0.026143 vs vendored Redis 0.135742s +/-0.008860; direct last run 117,745.24 ops/s p95 12,863us p99 13,503us vs Redis 742,543.98 ops/s p95 1,526us p99 2,051us. GDB SET long-run samples: queue_client_tracking_invalidations/client_list_sessions/BTreeMap<ClientSession>::clone in 11/35 samples; clone_subtree in 21 frames; allocation/memcpy frames present. Alien primitive: allocation-churn elimination from region/request-scoped memory (§5.10) and allocator/slab fast-path discipline (§7.9), applied as a safer borrowed-session scan rather than new allocator. Recommendation card: Change = replace client_list_sessions clone in invalidation dispatch with borrowed iteration and explicit current-session lookup; Hotspot evidence = artifacts/optimization/icywolf-perf-20260602-pass3/set-gap-*; EV = Impact 4 * Confidence 3 * Reuse 3 / (Effort 2 * Friction 1) = 18; Priority = A; Fallback = revert to client_list_sessions clone if tracking redirect/isomorphism trace differs or hyperfine win < Score 2.0; Proof obligations = preserve invalidation target existence checks, BCAST/OPTIN/OPTOUT semantics, NOLOOP, redirect, key ordering/duplicate suppression; FP/RNG N/A; golden sha256 must match.
close_reason: Completed: clone-free client tracking invalidation session scan. Removed per-write client_list_sessions clone in queue_client_tracking_invalidations by iterating borrowed stored sessions plus explicit current-session lookup. Baseline/candidate rch hyperfine SET p16 890.8ms +/-60.1 -> 497.0ms +/-29.0 (1.79x); direct last-run 101771.29 -> 193650.25 ops/s. Golden client-tracking trace sha256 unchanged: 14913c8bb40cd747ed0de4e114f4c8b0dcae0c7166ed6fe121cf623defd5c019. Validation: fmt, rch check -p fr-runtime --all-targets, rch test -p fr-runtime client_tracking (8 passed), rch clippy -p fr-runtime --all-targets -- -D warnings, UBS touched-file scan with existing inventory only.

title: fr-command: 4 xgroup_* inline tests broken on main since peer a6af191 (XINFO FULL group lag now Integer(1) instead of BulkString(None))
description:
On main since commit a6af191 ("fix: compute XINFO FULL group lag [frankenredis-mut9]"), four #[test]s in crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs panic at the `lag` field assertion because they pin BulkString(None) while the new (correct, upstream-aligned) implementation returns Integer(N) for groups with a backlog:

  tests::xgroup_create_and_xinfo_groups_report_created_group       L26468
  tests::xgroup_validation_missing_mkstream_wrongtype_and_syntax   L26569
  tests::xgroup_setid_updates_group_cursor_and_supports_dollar     L26849
  tests::xgroup_createconsumer_and_delconsumer_update_group_consumer_counts  L27093

Failure shape (one example):

  left:  ... last-delivered-id 0-0 entries-read None lag Integer(1)  (actual, correct)
  right: ... last-delivered-id 0-0 entries-read None lag BulkString(None)  (stale)

a6af191's behavior matches upstream Redis 7.2 t_stream.c::xinfoReplyWithStreamInfo which always emits the integer lag value when the group has a positive offset behind the stream tail — BulkString(None) was the pre-implementation placeholder.

Fix: align the four tests with the new lag emission. No production code change.

Acceptance: cargo test -p fr-command -- xgroup passes 8 / 8.

Reference: /testing-golden-artifacts cleanup of stale XINFO GROUPS pin on 2026-05-04.
notes: Aligned 5 lag-pin sites across 4 inline tests with the post-mut9 lag emission (Integer(N) where N = entries between group cursor and stream tail; was BulkString(None) placeholder). Sites: xgroup_create_and_xinfo_groups_report_created_group lag=0, xgroup_validation_missing_mkstream_wrongtype_and_syntax lag=0, xgroup_setid_updates_group_cursor_and_supports_dollar lag=1 then lag=0 after SETID $, xgroup_createconsumer_and_delconsumer_update_group_consumer_counts lag=1 (stays 1 after delconsumer). cargo test -p fr-command -- xgroup now 8/8; clippy clean. No production code change.

title: [perf] Borrowed SmallStr canonicalization for plain SET overwrite
description: Pass106 current-head profile on e921b0878 SET P16/1M still ranks <fr_store::Store>::set_plain_borrowed at 9.67% self and fr_store::canonical_string_value at 9.06% self after c4u8o rejected the parse fast-gate. Fresh strace shows output is batch-shaped (sendto 18,750 for 300k SET P16) and reply encode is below noise (RespFrame::encode_into ~0.26% self), so the profile-backed next lever is store value layout. One lever only: in the borrowed plain SET overwrite path, canonicalize from &[u8] directly and construct inline SmallStr without an intermediate value.to_vec allocation for <=15B strings, preserving strict parse_i64 semantics and all TTL/LFU/stream/digest sidecars. Acceptance: focused fr-store equivalence tests for integer/string/leading-zero/expired/volatile/LFU/stream overwrite, rch crate-scoped check/test/clippy, golden RESP sha, paired/reversed SET P16 benchmark plus 1M confirmation Score>=2.0.
close_reason: Rejected under Score>=2.0: borrowed SmallStr canonicalization preserved behavior but did not improve SET P16. Paired 300k baseline 365.2ms +/-14.0 vs candidate 369.8ms +/-11.5 (baseline 1.01x +/-0.05 faster); reversed candidate 359.0ms +/-14.6 vs baseline 355.2ms +/-8.4 (baseline 1.01x +/-0.05 faster). Golden RESP sha256 bdc37b9aa720451455c0cc174c31917de36121619a176b5d1087c69a57d4c61d matched; focused fr-store test and release-perf build passed via rch. Source hunk not shipped.

title: Add metamorphic tests for Redis Streams in fr-store
Add RDB decoder fuzz test for crash resilience on corrupted files
close_reason: Added 3 proptest fuzz tests to fr-persist: decode_rdb_never_panics, decode_aof_stream_never_panics, decode_rdb_with_valid_header_never_panics. Each runs 10,000 random cases. All pass — zero panics found. Added proptest dev-dependency.

title: DEBUG OBJECT serializedlength still off for LZF-compressible strings, hash, zset (smaller divergences after xp6mu fix)
description:
After frankenredis-xp6mu landed (DEBUG OBJECT now uses dump_key payload bytes instead of memory_usage_for_key), the fr ↔ vendored divergence dropped by orders of magnitude. Three smaller gaps remain:

  type=str x*100 (100 bytes):  fr=102  vendored=13  (fr-store's encode_rdb_string doesn't apply LZF compression — fr-persist's rdb_encode_string at fr-persist/src/lib.rs:936 does)
  type=hash 2 fields (4 bytes each):  fr=24  vendored=13  (different listpack encoding or threshold)
  type=zset 3 members:  fr=26  vendored=23  (different zset listpack encoding, off by 3)

For STRING, the fix is to mirror fr-persist's rdb_encode_string LZF path into fr-store's encode_rdb_string (or refactor both to share one implementation).

For HASH/ZSET listpack divergences, need to compare fr-store's encode_listpack_strings byte layout vs upstream listpack.c — likely a count or backlen byte that differs.

Filed P3 as a follow-up since the orders-of-magnitude bug is resolved; these are byte-precision parity issues that mostly matter for tools that diff RDB sizes.
close_reason:
Added LZF compression to fr-store's encode_rdb_string by exposing fr-persist::lzf_compress and mirroring rdbSaveLzfStringObject's policy: try LZF for inputs > 20 bytes, emit the 0xC3 [comp_len][orig_len][payload] special encoding when the compressed wire form is strictly smaller than the raw form. Closes the largest residual divergence after xp6mu — DEBUG OBJECT serializedlength for repetitive 100-byte 'x' strings dropped from 102 → 9 bytes (vendored: 13). The remaining 4-byte underrun vs vendored is because fr's LZF implementation is slightly more aggressive than upstream on highly-repetitive input; both produce valid 0xC3-encoded LZF streams.

Verified vs vendored 7.2.4 (16480 vs 16380):
  len=25   fr=8   ven=12
  len=50   fr=8   ven=12
  len=100  fr=9   ven=13
  len=200  fr=9   ven=13
  len=500  fr=12  ven=16
  non-compressible 25b: fr=32  ven=32 (raw path, identical)

Pinned via debug_object_serializedlength_compresses_repetitive_strings_via_lzf_per_upstream covering both the LZF path (100x compresses to <30 bytes) and the raw fallback (25-byte non-compressible string emits exactly 26 bytes = 1 length-prefix + 25 raw).

Out of scope (deferred): hash listpack (fr=24 vs vendored=13) and zset listpack (fr=26 vs vendored=23) divergences require deeper changes to fr-store's encode_listpack_strings — separate beads if pursued.

885/885 fr-command lib + 332/332 fr-store lib pass. Workspace clippy clean under -D warnings.

title: OBJECT ENCODING converts on CONFIG SET threshold-lower without a write (should be lazy/forward-only)
description:
fr's Store::object_encoding (crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs ~7088) RE-DERIVES listpack-vs-converted from the CURRENT config + CURRENT size on every read (e.g. hash: `!force_hash_hashtable_encoding && m.len() <= self.hash_max_listpack_entries && ...`), with sticky force_*_encoding flags only for the ALREADY-converted direction. Redis instead STORES o->encoding and converts FORWARD-ONLY at WRITE time (hashTypeTryConversion etc.); CONFIG SET <type>-max-listpack-* never iterates existing keys. So lowering a threshold below an existing key's size, with NO subsequent write, must NOT change its encoding.

REPRO (scripts/encoding_config_lower_differ.py, FAILs today): build a collection of 100 elems under threshold=128 (stays listpack/intset on both servers, no write crosses), then CONFIG SET <param> 50, then OBJECT ENCODING with no write:
  hash   redis listpack->listpack    fr listpack->HASHTABLE   <- bug
  list   redis listpack->listpack    fr listpack->QUICKLIST   <- bug
  zset   redis listpack->listpack    fr listpack->SKIPLIST    <- bug
  set-intset redis intset->intset    fr intset->HASHTABLE     <- bug
  set-listpack: parity (no diverge)

FIX DIRECTION: object_encoding must return the STORED encoding, updated forward-only at write time, NOT re-derived from current config on read. fr already has the force_*_encoding flags for the converted direction; the gap is the unset-flag fallback that applies the current-config size check. Making conversion purely write-triggered requires every value-CREATION path (incremental writes already do via note_command_grow; plus RESTORE, RDB load [post-hpfey], COPY, SUNIONSTORE/SINTERSTORE/ZUNIONSTORE-STORE, SORT STORE, SETRANGE-to-large) to set the force flag at creation under the then-current threshold. Audit each path before removing the read-time size check, else bulk-created large values would wrongly report listpack. Isomorphism: re-run encoding_config_lower_differ.py (must PASS) + object_encoding_boundary_gate.py + encoding_differ.py + reload_encoding_survival_gate.py (must stay PASS — don't regress yp503/rc49s/lsetql sticky-converted cases).
close_reason: config-lower facet fixed all 4 types (42cbcefc0/bb272c79f); reload-replay 'facet' proven a phantom — redis 7.2.4 also converts to hashtable on reload (rdbLoadObject length check), fr at parity

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Same root cause ALSO manifests through DEBUG RELOAD (distinct from the closed hpfey, which fixed the UNDER-conversion quicklist->listpack direction): build a 200-field hash under hash-max-listpack-entries=300 (stays listpack, no write crosses), lower to 128, then DEBUG RELOAD with NO write -> redis keeps 'listpack' (RDB save emits the listpack type byte = the live encoding, load reconstructs it; redis does NOT re-derive from current config on load), fr reports 'hashtable'. Confirms the fix is: encoding is STORED state set forward-only at write/creation/load time and preserved through RDB save/load via the type byte — never re-derived from current config on OBJECT ENCODING read. Repro: scripts/encoding_config_lower_differ.py (config-lower facet).

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
SCOPE/diligence (BlackThrush, re-verified vs redis 7.2.4): the fix is architectural — re-derivation happens in THREE places, all must switch to stored encoding:
1. Store::object_encoding READ (the headline repro).
2. RDB-SAVE encoding selection AND/OR 3. RDB-LOAD. Proof: build 200-field hash under hash-max-listpack-entries=300 (listpack), lower to 128, DEBUG RELOAD, no write -> redis stays listpack, fr becomes hashtable. So fixing only object_encoding is INSUFFICIENT; the RDB round-trip must preserve the live (possibly over-current-threshold) encoding via the type byte, not re-derive from current config.

GOOD NEWS narrowing the fix: fr's sticky force_*_encoding flags ARE reliably set on the CONVERTED direction across creation paths — verified hashtable stays sticky after RAISING the threshold above the size for: incremental write-cross, RDB load (survives reload+raise), COPY, SUNIONSTORE. So the converted-direction state is sound; the gap is the absence of a POSITIVE stored 'listpack'/'intset' state, so object_encoding/RDB fall back to current-config re-derivation when no force flag is set.

HARDEST sub-case: sets are THREE-way (intset -> listpack -> hashtable) with two thresholds (set-max-intset-entries, set-max-listpack-entries) and NO force_intset flag — a small all-int set is 'intset' purely by derivation today. Lowering set-max-intset-entries with no write wrongly drops intset. Needs a positive stored encoding enum for sets (and arguably all types) updated forward-only at write/create/load time.

RECOMMENDED design: add a per-value stored Encoding enum (Listpack/Intset/Quicklist/Hashtable/Skiplist), set at creation under then-current thresholds, advanced forward-only on writes that cross (reuse existing note_command_grow / *TryConversion sites), preserved verbatim through RDB save (emit matching type byte) and load (reconstruct from type byte, no re-check). object_encoding returns it directly. Regression gates that MUST stay green: encoding_config_lower_differ.py (new, must flip to PASS), object_encoding_boundary_gate.py, encoding_differ.py, reload_encoding_survival_gate.py, rdb_cross_load_probe.sh, and the yp503/rc49s/lsetql sticky cases. Not a safe single-session change; needs the enum refactor + per-path audit + full reload/RDB re-verification.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
PARTIAL FIX SHIPPED (commit 42cbcefc0): object_encoding hash/zset/set now read the sticky force_*_encoding markers instead of re-deriving from current config. Verified byte-exact + zero regression: encoding_config_lower_differ hash/zset/set PASS, object_encoding_boundary_gate (23 shapes), encoding_differ (4000), reload_encoding_survival_gate (18), rdb_cross_load_probe (19), random_command_differ (56k) all PASS.

REMAINING (bead stays OPEN):
1. LIST non-default list-max-listpack-size byte budget: object_encoding still re-derives via !list_fits_legacy_listpack_size in the non-default branch. The default -2 budget is already sticky-correct (reports_quicklist_default). Fix needs forced_for_fill/rebuild_growth_state to seed self.fill = live config on bulk loads (RESTORE/RDB replay) so the sticky flag can be trusted under a non-default budget without the read-time fallback. (Removing the fallback naively would regress bulk-loaded large lists under a non-default budget.)
2. RDB-reload-replay re-derivation: DEBUG RELOAD / full-sync rebuild replays values through hset/sadd/etc under CURRENT config (preserve_store_load_context/copy_encoding_thresholds, 63p1s), so a listpack hash built under a higher threshold, lowered, then reloaded re-converts. Upstream preserves the saved encoding via the RDB type byte. Fix: seed the force flag from the loaded RDB type byte rather than from replay-under-current-config.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
LIST facet FIXED (bb272c79f): added ListValue.decided_by_write (set by note_command_grow/note_lset_grow, false for bulk From/FromIterator). object_encoding LIST non-default-budget branch now trusts the sticky forced_quicklist for write-decided lists regardless of current fill (upstream converts forward-only at write time), and only bulk-built lists keep the re-derive fallback (RDB-load path unchanged). encoding_config_lower_differ list case DIVERGE->PASS; object_encoding_boundary(23)/encoding_differ(4000)/reload_encoding_survival(18)/rdb_cross_load(19)/list_differ(5000)/random_command_differ(56k) all PASS; lsetql sticky preserved.

So the NO-WRITE config-lower facet is now fixed for ALL FOUR types (hash/zset/set 42cbcefc0, list bb272c79f). REMAINING: the RDB-RELOAD-REPLAY facet — DEBUG RELOAD/full-sync rebuild replays values through hset/sadd/zadd/rpush under the CURRENT config, so a collection built under a higher threshold, lowered, then reloaded re-derives (upstream preserves the saved encoding via the RDB type byte). That facet is architectural (seed encoding from the loaded RDB type byte instead of replay-under-current-config) and stays open.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
RELOAD-REPLAY FACET IS A PHANTOM (differential proof vs redis 7.2.4, both servers same host now). The bead claimed 'redis keeps listpack after DEBUG RELOAD' — that oracle read was WRONG. Verified directly:
  redis 7.2.4: hash 200 fields @hash-max-listpack-entries=300 -> listpack; lower to 128 (no write) -> listpack; DEBUG RELOAD -> HASHTABLE.
  fr:          identical at every step -> HASHTABLE after reload.
redis's rdbLoadObject for RDB_TYPE_HASH_LISTPACK converts to HT when length > current hash_max_listpack_entries, so reloading a 200-field listpack under a lowered 128 CONVERTS — exactly like fr's replay-under-current-config. Same for set-listpack (both -> hashtable). So fr is at PARITY on reload; there is NO reload-replay divergence to fix. The genuinely-divergent facet was config-lower-WITHOUT-reload, fixed for all 4 types (42cbcefc0 hash/zset/set, bb272c79f list). Closing: real bug fixed + verified, remaining 'facet' was a misread oracle.

title: [HIGH] HINCRBYFLOAT Infinity error wording + HRANDFIELD arity error drift
description: Surfaced by live_redis_core_hash_matches_runtime: 5 HINCRBYFLOAT inf/-inf/inf-minus-inf cases emit 'ERR increment would produce NaN or Infinity' where upstream emits 'ERR value is NaN or Infinity'. Plus hrandfield_too_many_args returns 'ERR wrong number of arguments' where upstream returns 'ERR syntax error'. Fix in fr-command HINCRBYFLOAT + HRANDFIELD.

title: AOF rewrite stream pending XCLAIM must include JUSTID
description: Redis 7.2.4 legacy_redis_code/redis/src/aof.c::rioWriteStreamPendingEntry rewrites stream pending entries as XCLAIM <key> <group> <consumer> 0 <id> TIME <ms> RETRYCOUNT <count> JUSTID FORCE. fr-store's to_aof_commands currently emits FORCE without JUSTID, diverging from upstream AOF rewrite output for consumer-group PEL restore commands.
close_reason: Completed fr-store AOF XCLAIM JUSTID parity fix

title: Generate real run_id and report real process_id in INFO server
close_reason: INFO server now reports real run_id (40-char hex from LCG), process_id (std::process::id), and tcp_port (from CLI args)

title: Lua table.concat accepts non-string separator and emits garbage
description:
fr's table.concat handler accepts ANY LuaValue as the separator (arg #2) and routes it through to_display_string, producing garbage output like "1table:0x...2table:0x...3" when a table is passed as the separator. Upstream Redis 7.2.4 (ltablib.c::tconcat) uses luaL_optlstring, which requires the separator to be a string or number (or absent/nil), and otherwise raises:

  bad argument #2 to 'concat' (string expected, got <type>)

Reproduction (port 16380 vendored vs 16480 fr):
  EVAL "return table.concat({1,2,3}, {})" 0
    vendored: ERR user_script:1: bad argument #2 to 'concat' (string expected, got table)
    fr:       1table:0x006238999006f02table:0x006238999006f03

Impact: a script that passes a non-string separator (often via a bug or attacker-controlled value) produces meaningless output on fr but errors on vendored. Detection-as-an-error semantics matter.

Fix location: crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs line ~5729 — the sep extraction block of "table.concat". Reject non-string/non-number separators with the upstream wording (with the user_script:1: prefix per luaL_argerror).

Test plan:
  - Pin EVAL "return table.concat({1,2,3}, {})" -> error with vendored wording.
  - Pin EVAL "return table.concat({1,2,3}, function() end)" -> error.
  - Pin EVAL "return table.concat({1,2,3}, true)" -> error.
  - Pin EVAL "return table.concat({1,2,3}, 5)" -> "1525253" (number coerces to its string repr).
  - Pin EVAL "return table.concat({1,2,3}, ', ')" -> "1, 2, 3".

title: Implement LATENCY sample tracking infrastructure in Store
description:
# Implement LATENCY sample tracking infrastructure

## What
Redis LATENCY monitoring tracks execution time samples per named event type. When a command exceeds the latency-monitor-threshold (configurable via CONFIG SET), a sample is recorded. LATENCY LATEST/HISTORY/RESET/GRAPH/DOCTOR then query these samples.

## Design
Add a LatencyTracker struct to Store (or a new pub field):

```rust
pub struct LatencySample {
    pub timestamp_sec: u64,
    pub duration_ms: u64,
}

pub struct LatencyTracker {
    /// Per-event-type sample ring buffers. Max 160 samples per event (Redis default).
    events: HashMap<String, VecDeque<LatencySample>>,
    /// Threshold in milliseconds. 0 = disabled.
    pub threshold_ms: u64,
}
```

Event types in Redis: "command", "fast-command", "fork", "expire-cycle", "eviction-cycle", "aof-write", etc. For FrankenRedis, start with "command" and "fast-command".

## Implementation plan
1. Add LatencyTracker to Store
2. Add pub fn record_latency_sample(&mut self, event: &str, duration_ms: u64, now_sec: u64)
3. Add pub fn latency_latest(&self) -> Vec<(String, LatencySample)>
4. Add pub fn latency_history(&self, event: &str) -> &[LatencySample]
5. Add pub fn latency_reset(&mut self, events: &[&str]) -> usize
6. Wire CONFIG SET latency-monitor-threshold to update tracker.threshold_ms
7. In fr-runtime's command execution path, after measuring duration, call record_latency_sample if duration > threshold

## Why in Store (not Runtime)
So that both the runtime's command handler AND fr-command's latency_cmd can access the data. The runtime records samples; fr-command reads them for LATENCY LATEST/HISTORY.

## Key details
- Ring buffer: max 160 samples per event (matches Redis)
- LATENCY RESET with no args resets ALL events; with args resets named events
- LATENCY GRAPH: generates ASCII art timeline (see Redis source for format)
- LATENCY DOCTOR: generates human-readable analysis text
- threshold_ms=0 means disabled (no samples collected) — this is the default

## Testing
- Unit test: record samples, verify LATEST returns most recent per event
- Unit test: verify ring buffer overflow (161st sample evicts 1st)
- Conformance: CONFIG SET latency-monitor-threshold 1, run slow commands, verify LATENCY LATEST non-empty

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs (LatencyTracker struct + Store field)
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs (latency_cmd reads from store)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (record samples after command execution)
close_reason: Completed

title: Track real total_error_replies in INFO stats
description:
# Track real total_error_replies in INFO stats

## Why
`INFO stats` still hardcodes `total_error_replies:0` even though the runtime already knows when it emits RESP errors. This is a real-data-available stub under the open remediation epic.

## Scope
- Add a store-owned counter for total error replies.
- Increment it when the final client-visible reply is `RespFrame::Error`.
- Wire `INFO stats` to emit the real counter.
- Cover both runtime-managed and delegated command errors with tests.

## Files
- `crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs`

## Validation
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p fr-command info_stats_reports_expire_and_evict_counters -- --exact --nocapture`
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p fr-runtime --lib total_error_replies_counts_runtime_and_delegated_errors -- --exact --nocapture`
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p fr-runtime --lib`
close_reason: Implemented store-backed total_error_replies counter, INFO stats wiring, runtime counting, and focused tests

title: audit XGROUP/XSETID/stream subcommands for id-parse-before-existence (sibling of b7jra)
description: b7jra (2be53bffb) fixed XGROUP SETID parsing the id before key/group existence checks. Audit siblings for the same class: XSETID (key existence before id parse?), XGROUP CREATE (id parse vs key/MKSTREAM), XADD (id vs key/NOMKSTREAM), XCLAIM/XAUTOCLAIM (ids vs group existence), XACK, XGROUP CREATECONSUMER/DELCONSUMER already use key_type precheck. Method: live-oracle diff each in a clean state where the key/group is missing AND the id is malformed; redis resolves existence first. Reuse Store::stream_group_precheck for the no-stat existence check. fr-command/lib.rs.
close_reason: Audit done + fix shipped (5d99f9a04). Differentially probed all stream subcommands with id args vs vendored 7.2.4: XGROUP CREATE shared the b7jra id-parse-before-existence bug (missing key -> 'requires key to exist', wrong-type -> WRONGTYPE, even MKSTREAM waives only missing-key not wrong-type; id still parses before BUSYGROUP). Fixed by reusing the no-stat Store::stream_group_precheck. XSETID, XADD (NOMKSTREAM), XCLAIM, XAUTOCLAIM, XACK all ALREADY match upstream existence-before-id ordering — no fix needed. Verified: 9-case differential, 14 xgroup unit tests (+1 regression test), live-oracle core_stream 217/217.

title: Lua math.min/math.max wrong-type wording
description:
fr's math.min and math.max emit a generic 'bad argument to math.min' / 'bad argument to math.max' when an argument is not coercible to a number. Upstream lmathlib.c::math_min/math_max use luaL_checknumber on each arg starting at index 1 and emit the upstream template with the source-location prefix and arg index.

Reproduction:
  EVAL "return math.min(1, 'a')" 0
    vendored: ERR user_script:1: bad argument #2 to 'min' (number expected, got string)
    fr:       ERR bad argument to 'math.min'

Affected handlers: crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs lines ~5046 and ~5062.

Fix: iterate with enumerate(), call lua_check_number(args, idx, "min") to surface upstream wording with prefix and (idx+1)-arg-position.

Test plan:
  - Pin math.min(1, 'a') / math.max(1, 'a') with arg #2 wording.
  - Pin math.min({}, 1) / math.max(nil, 1) with arg #1 wording.
  - Pin math.min() / math.max() unchanged (already correctly emits "got no value").

title: [perf] Large SET direct-read chunk sizing for 256KiB/1MiB residual
description: Fresh origin/main large_value_perf_gate on 2026-06-15 after m562t closeout: SET 262144B fr=16014 op/s vs redis=20335 op/s (0.79x), SET 1048576B fr=3986 op/s vs redis=4638 op/s (0.86x); GET is now faster than Redis at every size. Profile row points at the fr-server large SET direct-read state: DIRECT_OWNED_SET_CHUNK and LARGE_CHUNK are 64KiB, causing 4 reads for 256KiB and 16 reads for 1MiB after header. Test exactly one safe-Rust lever: larger direct-read chunk size to reduce read-loop/syscall/validation overhead while preserving owned buffer transfer, trailer validation, query-buffer limits, ordering, and reply bytes. Evidence bundle: artifacts/optimization/coralox-m562t/pass208-large-value-refresh/.
close_reason: Rejected: 256KiB direct-read chunk 64KiB->256KiB measured neutral/noisy (baseline 355.2ms +/-20.4 vs candidate 348.8ms +/-52.5, 1.02x +/-0.16) and 1MiB regressed within noise (baseline 416.0ms +/-16.1 vs candidate 422.0ms +/-29.0, baseline 1.01x +/-0.08). No source kept; route deeper to structural large SET copy/allocation primitive.

title: [perf] Route exact RESP packet parsers by arity header before HSET probes
description: Profile-backed after frankenredis-tbmu1 keep and frankenredis-zweth/frankenredis-xcgry rejections: HSET remains the measured residual (postkeep Redis/fr 1.028x; p99 4819us vs Redis 1975us), while value-slice allocation and consecutive-HSET batching failed confirmation. Alien-graveyard reroute: branchless/frame-scanning data-plane primitive, not another store micro-tweak. Candidate: in process_buffered_frames, inspect the canonical RESP array/bulk command header once and route directly to the existing exact GET/SET/HSET packet parser for the matching arity/command shape, avoiding wrong-parser probes while preserving the existing fallback parser and runtime semantics. Baseline via rch-built hyperfine before edit; golden HSET/HGET/HGETALL SHA256; keep only Score>=2.0.

title: EVAL Lua {double=x} returns bulk string instead of RESP3 Double; uses Rust Display not d2string
description: Differential probe: EVAL 'return {double=3.14}' under RESP3 (HELLO 3) returns oracle=,3.14 (RESP3 Double) but fr=$3.14 (bulk string). Upstream script_lua.c::luaReplyToRedisReply emits the {double=} hint via addReplyDouble -> RESP3 Double frame (d2string-formatted), downconverted to a bulk string under RESP2. fr's lua_to_resp (fr-command/lua_eval.rs ~9341) emits RespFrame::BulkString with format!("{n}") (Rust Display) in BOTH modes -> wrong frame type in RESP3 AND wrong formatting for large/small magnitudes (1e20 expands instead of 1e+20). Fix: emit RespFrame::double_from_f64(n) (now uses fr-protocol::format_redis_double from sk4ss) and add a Double->BulkString arm to downconvert_lua_reply_to_resp2 so RESP2 stays a bulk string. Found by cc-opus.
close_reason: EVAL {double=x} now emits a RESP3 Double frame (d2string-formatted via fr-protocol::format_redis_double) and downconverts to a bulk string under RESP2. Verified byte-exact vs redis 7.2.4 across 8 magnitudes in both protocols (0 diffs). fr-command 1197 green. Commit eda9e4c6d.

title: MethodCall on non-table receiver wrongly emits 'attempt to call method' instead of 'attempt to index'
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:

  local x = 1; x:foo()
    vendored: user_script:1: attempt to index local 'x' (a number value)
    fr:       user_script:1: attempt to call method 'foo' (a nil value)

  local x = nil; x:foo()
    vendored: user_script:1: attempt to index local 'x' (a nil value)
    fr:       user_script:1: attempt to call method 'foo' (a nil value)

Upstream Lua 5.1 grammar: `x:foo(...)` is sugar for `x.foo(x, ...)`. The first step is indexing x with "foo" — if x is not a table (and has no __index meta), indexing fails BEFORE the call is attempted, so the runtime emits the indexing error with accessor context.

fr has two MethodCall sites:
1. Expr::MethodCall direct handler at lua_eval.rs ~line 3638: hits `_ =>` at 3657 emitting bare "attempt to index a TYPE value" (no accessor).
2. eval_call_args last-arg MethodCall handler at ~line 3814: hits `_ =>` at 3828 silently returning LuaValue::Nil — which then bubbles into call_function_with_callee and emits "attempt to call method 'foo' (a nil value)" with the WRONG semantics.

Fix both sites to route through type_error_with_label(obj_expr, &obj, env, "index") for accessor-aware "attempt to index local/field/etc. (a TYPE value)" wording.

NOTE: Table receivers with missing method key continue to emit "attempt to call method 'm' (a nil value)" — that's the correct path because indexing the table succeeds (returns nil), and the call step is what fails. Existing test lua_attempt_to_call_errors_carry_accessor_context covers that case.
close_reason: Fixed both MethodCall handlers to route non-table receivers through type_error_with_label with accessor context: (1) Expr::MethodCall direct handler at lua_eval.rs ~line 3657, and (2) eval_call_args last-arg MethodCall handler at ~line 3828 (which previously silently returned LuaValue::Nil → wrong 'attempt to call method' error). Regression test method_call_non_table_receiver_errors_at_index_step_aaudb pins 4 invalid-receiver cases + 1 table-receiver-with-missing-key sanity (unchanged) + 1 string-receiver sanity. Full release test suite (1013 tests) still passes. Live-diff vs vendored 16380: 6/6 cases match byte-for-byte.

title: commandstats: special-command wrong-arity counted as failed not rejected
description:
INFO commandstats: special/runtime-command dispatch path miscounts wrong-arity as calls+failed_calls instead of rejected_calls. The regular data-command path was fixed in b5a7dc89a (Err(WrongArity) -> record_command_rejected). The special-command path (fr-runtime execute_argv ~L6230 special_reply block: SUBSCRIBE/PSUBSCRIBE/CLIENT/CONFIG/etc.) returns a RespFrame::Error for arity and records via record_command_histogram (failed+call). Repro vs redis 7.2.4: SUBSCRIBE (no args) -> oracle cmdstat_subscribe calls=0/rejected=1/failed=0, fr 1/0/1.

FIX DIRECTION: classify the special-command arity rejection too. CAUTION: cannot blanket pre-dispatch arity-check special commands because EXEC has custom EXECABORT-arity wording (br-frankenredis-execary) and must NOT be turned into the generic 'ERR wrong number of arguments' nor reclassified blindly (verify EXEC's rejected/failed counting vs redis separately). Likely need: detect the canonical wrong-arity / WrongSubcommandArity reply in the special path (or pre-check arity for the subset of special cmds without custom wording) and route to record_command_rejected, skipping slowlog/latency. Reuse fr_command::check_command_arity. Verify subcommand-arity (cmdstat_parent|sub) cases too (CLIENT/OBJECT/CONFIG/XINFO/...).
close_reason: Fixed in c3a6243a1: special_reply dispatch path classifies wrong-arity via check_command_arity -> record_command_rejected. SUBSCRIBE/CLIENT/CONFIG/PUBSUB/WAIT/SELECT/WATCH/MULTI/EXEC arity now 0/1/0 = redis 7.2.4 (EXECABORT reply preserved). Regression test added.

FrankenRedis currently exposes rdbchecksum as a static CONFIG parameter and accepts CONFIG SET through the static fallback. Add runtime and fixture coverage that rejects mutation while preserving CONFIG GET visibility.

title: fr-command BITPOS argc==6 parses end before BIT|BYTE check, inverts upstream precedence
close_reason:
Reordered bitpos() argc==6 trailing-arg parse to start → unit → end, mirroring upstream bitops.c lines 905-927.

CHANGE (crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs):
The unit (BIT|BYTE) check now runs immediately after start is parsed, BEFORE end is parsed. Previously fr parsed start → end → unit, which meant a malformed end would surface 'ERR value is not an integer or out of range' even when the unit token was also bogus — upstream surfaces the syntax error on the bad unit token.

Regression test bitpos_argc6_unit_check_beats_end_parse pins the new precedence: BITPOS bm 1 0 NOTANINT BADUNIT now returns SyntaxError.

Verification:
- cargo test -p fr-command --lib bitpos → 3/3 pass.
- cargo clippy -p fr-command --lib --no-deps -- -D warnings → clean.

[LOW] severity. Wire-format conformance gap, only visible when both end and unit are malformed.

This is unrelated to frankenredis-zhphm.5, which passes cargo clippy -p fr-server --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings.

title: ACL DRYRUN 'Command not found' uppercases input; upstream preserves caller's case
description: Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4: `ACL DRYRUN default unknownCmd` returns `ERR Command 'unknownCmd' not found`. Upstream acl.c::aclCommand DRYRUN passes `c->argv[3]->ptr` verbatim into the `Command '%s' not found` format string, so the reply mirrors whatever case the caller supplied. fr's handle_acl DRYRUN was calling `cmd_name.to_ascii_uppercase()` before formatting, so the same input echoed back as `'UNKNOWNCMD'`.
acceptance_criteria: ACL DRYRUN's command-not-found reply must echo argv[3] verbatim, preserving the caller's exact input case.
close_reason: Removed the to_ascii_uppercase() cast in fr-runtime/src/lib.rs::handle_acl_command DRYRUN branch and pinned with acl_dryrun_command_not_found_preserves_input_case covering mixed-case inputs (`unknownCmd`, `BoGuS`, `abcDEF`).

title: fr-runtime: ACL LOG -1 emits 'value is not an integer or out of range' instead of upstream silent clamp to 0
description: Upstream acl.c::aclCommand 'log' branch parses count via getLongFromObjectOrReply (NULL msg → standard 'value is not an integer or out of range') and silently clamps negative counts to 0. fr-runtime's handle_acl_log rejected negative counts as InvalidInteger. Switch to the silent clamp.
close_reason: Fixed

title: fr-runtime: ACL SETUSER #hash and +/-cmd modifiers accept invalid input silently or use generic 'Syntax error'
description:
Two ACL SETUSER modifier wording/validation gaps:

1. #hash modifier: upstream emits 'The password hash must be exactly 64 characters and contain only lowercase hexadecimal characters' for malformed hashes. fr emitted the generic 'Syntax error'.

2. +cmd / -cmd modifiers: upstream rejects unknown command names with 'Unknown command or category name in ACL'. fr silently accepted any command name (e.g. '+ALL', '-nosuchcmd') as a fictional rule.

Fix:
- Rewrite the #/! hash error to the upstream verbose form.
- Reject unknown command names (via fr_command::is_known_command) in +cmd / -cmd parsers.

Differential probe vs vendored redis 7.2.4 byte-matches all four cases after fix.
close_reason: Fixed

title: Adding any password-protected user globally locks out fresh connections; default-nopass auto-auth ignored once any user has a password
description:
Upstream src/networking.c::createClient assigns DefaultUser to every new client and sets c->authenticated = 1 only when DefaultUser carries USER_FLAG_NOPASS. So a fresh connection auto-authenticates iff the *default* user is nopass — adding other password-protected users (alice, bob, ...) does NOT change the default's auth requirement.

fr's auth_required was instead returning true if ANY ACL user had a password set:
  self.requirepass.is_some() || self.acl_users.values().any(|u| !u.passwords.is_empty())
so `ACL SETUSER alice on '>secret'` would lock out every subsequent fresh connection — even though default remained nopass — until alice was deleted again.

This is a P1-severity defect because:
  - Every operator who adds a per-user ACL with a password (the documented Redis pattern for least-privilege accounts) silently breaks all existing default-nopass clients.
  - The break is total: every command including PING returns NOAUTH on fresh connections.
  - Recovery requires identifying and deleting the password-protected user, since AUTH default '' on the new connection appears to succeed but the next connection still NOAUTHs.

Repro vs vendored 7.2.4 (server with default = on nopass +@all):
  > ACL SETUSER alice on '>secret'
  > PING (fresh connection)
    16380> PONG
    16381 (pre-fix)> NOAUTH Authentication required.
  > ACL SETUSER bob on '#<sha256-hash>'
  > PING (fresh connection)
    16380> PONG
    16381 (pre-fix)> NOAUTH Authentication required.
  > ACL SETUSER charlie on nopass
  > PING
    Both: PONG (the bug only fires on password-protected adds)

Fix: rewrite auth_required to short-circuit on requirepass and then look at the default user's nopass flag. Defensive fallback if default is missing returns true. Four pre-existing fr-runtime tests that pinned the BUG behavior were updated to use set_requirepass (which legitimately deauths default by setting default.nopass=false) for the gate cases.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Fresh default-nopass session auto-authenticates without AUTH. 2. Adding a non-default password-protected user does NOT deauth existing or new sessions — PING / GET still succeed. 3. Adding multiple password-protected users (alice, bob, ...) keeps default auto-auth. 4. set_requirepass(Some(...)) DOES gate (default.nopass becomes false). 5. set_requirepass(None) restores nopass on default. 6. Adding a 'on nopass' user (charlie) doesn't trigger the gate (already correct, no regression).

title: COMMAND GETKEYSANDFLAGS per-key flags wrong for stream CG subcommands + MEMORY USAGE/MIGRATE/XACK family
description:
Differential probe (fr strict :17841 vs vendored redis 7.2.4 :17811) found 12 commands whose COMMAND GETKEYSANDFLAGS per-key FLAGS diverge from upstream. Key extraction is correct; only the flag set is wrong (generic 'access' fallback instead of the command's real key-spec flags). Oracle is ground truth. Each line = fr_flags -> upstream_flags:

  MEMORY USAGE k          : access            -> RO
  XGROUP CREATE s g 0     : access            -> RW,insert
  XGROUP CREATECONSUMER   : access            -> RW,insert
  XGROUP DELCONSUMER      : access            -> RW,delete
  XGROUP SETID            : access            -> RW,update
  XGROUP DESTROY          : access            -> RW,delete
  XINFO STREAM s          : access            -> RO,access
  XINFO GROUPS s          : access            -> RO,access
  XACK s g 0-0            : RW,access,update  -> RW,update      (spurious access)
  XCLAIM s g c 0 0-0      : RW,access,update  -> RW,update      (spurious access)
  XAUTOCLAIM s g c 0 0    : RW,access,update  -> RW,delete      (spurious access + wrong op: delete not update)
  MIGRATE host 6379 k 0 100: RW,access,update -> RW,access,delete (delete not update)

Root cause class: fr's per-keyspec flag derivation in fr-command lacks correct key-spec flags for stream consumer-group container subcommands (XGROUP */XINFO *) and for a few top-level commands, falling back to a generic write/access flag. Upstream defines these in commands.def key_specs (flags = RO/RW/OW/RM access-type + access/update/insert/delete op). Fix: add correct key-spec flag tables for these commands/subcommands so GETKEYSANDFLAGS is byte-exact. Same fertile area as project_getkeysandflags_keyspec_fidelity memory note. 40+ other probed commands already match. NOTE: fr-command is reserved by IcyWolf — coordinate before editing. Found via differential probe by cc-opus.
close_reason: Added exact key_specs for 12 commands (XGROUP CREATE/CREATECONSUMER=RW,insert; DELCONSUMER/DESTROY=RW,delete; SETID=RW,update; XINFO STREAM/GROUPS/CONSUMERS=RO,access; XACK/XCLAIM=RW,update; XAUTOCLAIM=RW,delete; MEMORY USAGE=RO; MIGRATE=RW,access,delete) in command_key_references_with_exact_flags, mirroring vendored commands/*.json. Verified byte-exact vs redis 7.2.4 (0/19 diffs incl sanity). fr-command 1197 tests green. Commit 46695e3e8.

title: RESP3 reply-type fidelity: scores not Double, XREAD/XREADGROUP not Map, Set-cmds not Set under HELLO 3
description: Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 under HELLO 3 (gauntlet round-1 finding B4). Under RESP3 the oracle emits native types where fr falls back to RESP2 encodings: (1) ZSET scores as RESP3 Double ,2.5 / ,inf vs fr bulk string -- affects ZSCORE, ZRANGE WITHSCORES, ZPOPMIN/ZPOPMAX, ZINCRBY, ZRANDMEMBER WITHSCORES; (2) XREAD/XREADGROUP as RESP3 Map (stream->entries) vs fr array-of-pairs; (3) SMEMBERS/SPOP-count/SINTER/SUNION/SDIFF as RESP3 Set (~) vs fr array (*). README acknowledges only CONFIG GET/HGETALL/XINFO emit RESP3 Map/Set today, so known partial. Fix: thread protocol_version=3 through the score/stream/set reply builders. Evidence: /data/projects/frankenredis__gauntlet_workspace/artifacts/probe_report_resp3.json. Reusable probe: that workspace's scripts/resp_differ.py --hello3.
close_reason: done

title: Add golden and metamorphic tests for fr-store key encoding and glob matching
notes: Metamorphic tests exist in fr-store/tests/metamorphic.rs and fr-store/src/lib.rs (mod metamorphic at line 14406)

title: XGROUP DESTROY must preserve stream last-id watermark
description: Upstream Redis keeps stream->last_id independent of consumer groups. In fr-store::xgroup_destroy, when the last group is removed it also removes stream_last_ids for the key. If a stream was emptied by XDEL but retained a high last-id watermark, destroying the last group makes xlast_id return None/older state, allowing later XADD IDs that upstream would reject as <= the target stream top item. Acceptance: XGROUP DESTROY removes group metadata only and preserves stream_last_ids for the stream, with focused fr-store regression coverage.
close_reason: Preserve stream last-id watermark when destroying groups

title: Add live Redis conformance tests for OBJECT commands
notes: core_object_live_redis_matches_runtime exists

title: Implement real XINFO CONSUMERS pending count and idle/inactive time
description:
## Problem

fr-store/src/lib.rs line 5796: xinfo_consumers returns Vec<Vec<u8>> (just consumer names).
fr-command/src/lib.rs lines 4169-4179: stream_consumer_info_to_frame hardcodes pending=0, idle=0, inactive=0.

## What Redis Returns

XINFO CONSUMERS key group returns per-consumer:
- name: consumer name
- pending: number of PEL entries owned by this consumer
- idle: milliseconds since last interaction (XREADGROUP/XCLAIM/XACK)
- inactive: milliseconds since last successful XREADGROUP (Redis 7.2+)

## Implementation Plan

1. Change Store::xinfo_consumers to return Vec<(Vec<u8>, usize, u64, u64)> — (name, pending_count, last_interaction_ms, last_read_ms)
2. Compute pending_count by scanning the PEL and counting entries where consumer matches
3. Track last_interaction_ms per consumer in StreamGroupState (update on XREADGROUP, XCLAIM, XACK)
4. Track last_read_ms per consumer (update only on XREADGROUP with >)
5. Update stream_consumer_info_to_frame to use real values

## Files to Modify

- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs: StreamGroupState consumer tracking, xinfo_consumers
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs: stream_consumer_info_to_frame
close_reason: XINFO CONSUMERS now returns real pending count and idle time computed from PEL, not hardcoded zeros

title: [code-review] CONFIG SET accepts invalid newly-added static keys
description: Review of 3313cbe found that adding upstream CONFIG GET-only static keys also made the generic CONFIG SET static-override fallback accept them. Repro on current main: CONFIG SET tls-cluster maybe, CONFIG SET cluster-port 1, or CONFIG SET syslog-enabled yes can return OK even though upstream Redis declares tls-cluster as bool yes/no and cluster-port/syslog-enabled as immutable. Acceptance: newly-added static keys retain CONFIG GET shape parity but CONFIG SET rejects immutable keys and validates Redis bool/int/enum types before storing overrides.

title: fr-conformance: 2 stale memory_unknown_subcmd fixtures pin pre-wlwb verbose wording, mask byte-equal upstream parity
description:
core_server.json has TWO duplicate cases pinning the OLD verbose MEMORY unknown-subcommand wording:

  memory_unknown_subcmd_nosuch (line 2286):
    expects: ERR Unknown subcommand or wrong number of arguments for 'nosuch'. Try MEMORY HELP.
  memory_unknown_subcmd_duplicate_block (line 2736):
    expects: ERR Unknown subcommand or wrong number of arguments for 'nosuch'. Try MEMORY HELP.

But fr now emits the upstream-aligned shorter wording (verified by frankenredis-wlwb migration):

  upstream + fr both: ERR unknown subcommand 'NOSUCH'. Try MEMORY HELP.

Differences vs the stale expected:
  - lowercase 'unknown' (was 'Unknown')
  - uppercase 'NOSUCH' preserves user-typed casing (was lowercase 'nosuch')
  - shorter form drops 'or wrong number of arguments for'

These two fixture cases have been red on main since the wlwb migration landed; my 2e40e29 conformance run surfaced them as part of a 7-case pre-existing failure block.

Acceptance: both fixture cases reflect the upstream wording, the
core_server_conformance suite shrinks the pre-existing failure list by 2.

Reference: /testing-conformance-harnesses fixture cleanup on 2026-05-04.
notes: Updated both stale fixture cases (memory_unknown_subcmd_nosuch + memory_unknown_subcmd_duplicate_block) in core_server.json to expect the upstream-aligned wording 'ERR unknown subcommand NOSUCH. Try MEMORY HELP.' (lowercase 'unknown', preserves user-typed 'NOSUCH' casing, drops 'or wrong number of arguments for'). Conformance pass count goes 275 → 277 (the 2 corrected cases now pass).

title: COMMAND DOCS subcommand iteration order is alphabetical, vendored uses Redis siphash dict order
description: fr's command_docs_subcommands iterates SUBCOMMAND_TABLE entries in alphabetical order (table is sorted alphabetically), while vendored Redis 7.2.4 emits subcommands in dictGetIterator order, which is determined by Redis's siphash hashing. Achieving byte-equivalent ordering would require either: (a) replicating siphash with the same seed Redis uses, or (b) hardcoding the observed order per parent at build time. Discovered while working frankenredis-bpf4q. Cosmetic only — structure is correct, only ordering differs.
close_reason: Wontfix — verified that vendored's subcommand iteration order is NON-DETERMINISTIC across restarts. Redis 7.2.4 initializes the dict siphash seed from random at startup (dictSetHashFunctionSeed in dict.c). Captured two CLUSTER subcommand orderings from two consecutive vendored restarts: run 1 started 'cluster|saveconfig/reset/forget/help/setslot...', run 2 started 'cluster|links/slots/set-config-epoch/shards/delslots...'. Neither is canonical, so there is no stable byte-equivalent target to match. fr's alphabetical order is at least deterministic. No further action needed.

Promote the G/H probes to hard failures so future regressions in lookupKeyWrite-style metadata refresh are caught by the no-mock Redis-oracle gate.
close_reason: Completed: promoted qwqln APPEND/SETRANGE LFU->LRU write-reaccess cases from notes to hard differential checks.

With clean release-perf fr-server at 10381b377, GETRANGE bigstr 0 -1 hyperfine over 200 ops measured Redis 57.6 ms +/- 6.9 ms vs fr 52.2 ms +/- 4.5 ms; fr is 1.10x +/- 0.16 faster. perf sampling unavailable (perf_event_paranoid=4).
