Paired SET P16/300k baseline 364.6ms +/- 14.5ms vs candidate 363.3ms +/- 13.8ms (candidate 1.00x +/- 0.06). Reversed candidate 376.9ms +/- 23.4ms vs baseline 373.9ms +/- 10.9ms (baseline 1.01x +/- 0.07). Score 0.4 < 2.0; source hunk not kept. Evidence: artifacts/optimization/tealotter-pass105-string-canonical-fastgate-20260609/PROOF.md

title: HSCAN accepts the 7.4-only NOVALUES option even though target is Redis 7.2.4 — vendored returns 'ERR syntax error'
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 16380:
  HSET h f1 v1 f2 v2 f3 v3
  HSCAN h 0 NOVALUES
    vendored: ERR syntax error
    fr      : 0\nf1\nf2\nf3   (returns just field names)

Background: HSCAN's NOVALUES flag was added in Redis 7.4 (commit ffd44d76). fr implements it, but the project's parity baseline is Redis 7.2.4 (README + INFO server.redis_version). Same class as v0swv (hexpired_fields). SSCAN/ZSCAN don't have NOVALUES (members ARE the only data); both servers correctly reject NOVALUES for those commands.

Fix: in fr-command's HSCAN parser, drop the NOVALUES branch so unknown tokens fall through to the existing syntax-error path.

Probe also showed SCAN ordering differs (vendored: l,z,s1,h vs fr: h,l,s1,z) but SCAN explicitly does not guarantee element order — not a defect, just a differential observation.
close_reason: Disabled NOVALUES acceptance across the entire SCAN family by flipping parse_scan_args's accept_novalues default from true to false (crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:16375-16384). NOVALUES is a Redis 7.4 HSCAN-only addition (commit ffd44d76); the project's parity baseline is 7.2.4, so accepting it broke parity. Same class as v0swv (DEBUG OBJECT hexpired_fields). Updated existing test scan_family_rejects_novalues_*  to also assert HSCAN+NOVALUES errors (renamed from _except_hscan_ to _for_7_2_4_baseline_). Live differential probe vs vendored 16380 confirms HSCAN h 0 NOVALUES now returns 'ERR syntax error' on both servers; HSCAN h 0 still returns the field-value pairs unchanged. Workspace clippy clean under -D warnings; scan-family tests pass.

title: XTRIM/XADD MAXLEN ~ 0 removes nothing; redis clears whole stream (approx node-boundary tail case)
description: fr-command xtrim approximate model rounds trim-count DOWN to multiples of STREAM_NODE_MAX_ENTRIES(100), so MAXLEN ~ 0 on a <100-entry stream rounds to 0 and removes nothing; redis streamTrim removes whole head nodes while (len-node_entries)>=maxlen, so maxlen 0 clears everything. NOTE: ~ count is approximate-by-contract (docs: 'may be more than threshold') and fr has a deliberate model + test xtrim_maxlen_approximate_respects_node_boundary_hpz8a; a precise fix needs rax-node modeling (node sizes are insertion/trim-history dependent). Only the maxlen==0 sub-case is well-defined (always clears). Low priority. Found via rich_option_fuzz.py.
close_reason: Fixed b5ab1a76c: shared stream_approx_trim_target() implements upstream streamTrim's whole-node ~ eviction (remove whole rax nodes while remaining>=target and within LIMIT). Fixes MAXLEN ~ 0 (clears stream, tail node evicted) + sub-node LIMIT (whole-node granularity) + XADD ~ no-longer-a-no-op (default LIMIT 100*node-max). node-model == redis on 1296 probed cases (was 293 diffs); unit + command-path tests on fresh compile, 14 stream tests + clippy clean. Live TCP recheck blocked by rch stale-rlib cache (infra), command-path tests cover the dispatch path.

title: Inline command detection: fr treats RESP-prefix first bytes (+,:,-,$,=,%,#,~,>) as RESP frames, but redis treats any non-* first byte as an INLINE command (should_try_inline_parsing in fr-server, locked)
close_reason: Fixed in ee8096263: should_try_inline_parsing returns first_byte != b'*' — any non-'*' first byte is an inline command (matches upstream processInputBuffer), not a dropped connection. Verified vs redis 7.2.4 across 14 non-'*' prefixes (RESP2 +-:$ + RESP3 ~%#,_(=|>! + blob-error !): 0 diffs, connection alive in every case; '*' still routes to RESP multibulk. fr-server suite green.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Characterized: redis treats ANY non-* first byte as inline (sdssplitargs); fr's should_try_inline_parsing (fr-server main.rs ~1574, test inline_parser_gate_recognizes_all_resp_prefixes at 6634) routes +,:,-,$,=,%,#,~,> to RESP parsing -> 'invalid command frame' / 'unsupported RESP3 type prefix' instead of 'unknown command <token>'. Fix: should_try_inline_parsing should return true for every first byte except '*'. BLOCKED: fr-server locked by OrangeMouse. Repro: send '+OK
' or '$3
GET
' as a command.

title: fuzz/corpus/fuzz_inline_parser missing — libfuzzer starts cold every run, structured-input edges (CR-only, hex escapes, mixed quoting, oversize) untested between cargo-fuzz invocations
description:
/testing-fuzzing audit:

  ls fuzz/corpus/fuzz_inline_parser → No such file or directory

The fuzz_inline_parser harness in fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_inline_parser.rs exercises the wire-protocol entry point that handles human-typed redis-cli over telnet (PING\r\n, SET key value, etc.) — it consumes try_parse_inline + split_inline_args + should_try_inline_parsing. With no seed corpus, libfuzzer starts cold every run and the structured-input edges (quoted-string escape handling, CR-only line endings, mixed single/double quoting, oversize tokens, hex escapes) only get covered if libfuzzer's bytewise mutator happens to stumble onto them.

This also breaks the cwx6/ohm5/fek0y/xpgvh smoke-test pattern: the cargo-test fuzz_corpus_smoke harness (added in fr-protocol + fr-persist) replays seed files through the parsers under regular cargo test for deterministic between-run coverage. fuzz_inline_parser can't have one of those without a corpus.

Fix: hand-write 30+ seed inputs covering happy paths (PING\r\n, SET k v, multi-arg with tabs), quoted-string escapes (\n \r \t \b \a \" \\ \xNN \'), single-quoted with \' escape, mixed quoting, edge cases (empty line, CRLF-only, LF-only, no terminator), hostile inputs (unbalanced quotes, half-formed hex escape, embedded NUL, non-ASCII high bytes, oversize tokens, RESP-prefix bytes that should be rejected by should_try_inline_parsing). Optionally add a fuzz_corpus_smoke entry under crates/fr-server/tests/ to mirror the fr-protocol/fr-persist pattern.
close_reason: Added 42-seed corpus under fuzz/corpus/fuzz_inline_parser/ covering: happy paths (PING / GET / SET / MSET / LF terminator); quoted-string escapes (\n \r \t \b \a \" \\ \zUnknown); hex escapes (\x41\x42, \x00, \xff, uppercase hex, truncated \x4); single-quoting + escape; mixed double/single quoting; whitespace edges (tabs, runs of spaces, leading whitespace, empty CRLF/LF/whitespace-only lines); hostile inputs (unbalanced double/single quote, half-formed hex, embedded NUL); high-byte / non-ASCII; RESP-prefix bytes meant to be rejected by should_try_inline_parsing; long-token (4KB+4KB), many-args (50 args); no-terminator (Incomplete). Each seed has a 0x00 trailer so libfuzzer's Arbitrary derive picks the Raw variant. Companion crates/fr-server/tests/fuzz_corpus_smoke.rs (mirrors fr-protocol/fr-persist pattern) replays all seeds through try_parse_inline + split_inline_args + should_try_inline_parsing under cargo test, plus pins RESP-prefix seeds to should-not-route-inline and unbalanced-quote seeds to ProtocolError.

title: Add keyspace_notification_differ.py: per-command __keyevent__ notification parity vs redis (out-of-band side effect fuzzers miss)
close_reason: Added scripts/keyspace_notification_differ.py: robust per-command __keyevent__ differ; PASS byte-exact vs redis 7.2.4 over 29 mutating commands, stable, ubs clean.

title: CLIENT CACHING with invalid opt + tracking disabled returns syntax error instead of tracking-mode error
description: Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4: CLIENT CACHING BOGUS against a non-tracking client returns 'ERR CLIENT CACHING can be called only when the client is in tracking mode with OPTIN or OPTOUT mode enabled'. Upstream networking.c:3415-3441 checks the tracking-enabled gate BEFORE parsing the YES|NO opt. fr's validate_client_caching_mode parsed mode first and returned 'ERR syntax error' for BOGUS, surfacing the wrong wording. The tracking-mode error appeared only when mode was YES or NO.
close_reason: Reordered validate_client_caching_mode in fr-command/src/lib.rs to check tracking.enabled FIRST (matching upstream networking.c order), then validate the YES/NO opt. Added a new fr-command unit test client_caching_invalid_opt_with_tracking_disabled_uses_tracking_mode_error covering BOGUS / YES / NO / lowercase opts against a non-tracking client (all expect the dedicated wording), plus a follow-up that re-enables tracking and confirms BOGUS surfaces SyntaxError. Existing client_caching_requires_optin_or_optout_modes test (covering YES/NO with various tracking modes) still passes unchanged. fr-command 848/4 pre-existing stream failures.

title: CLIENT TRACKING OFF REDIRECT N skips client-existence validation
description:
Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4:

  CLIENT TRACKING OFF REDIRECT 5
    vendored: ERR The client ID you want redirect to does not exist
    fr:       OK

Upstream networking.c::clientCommand validates the REDIRECT target
client ID even when the tracking command is turning the feature
OFF — the parse-time validation runs unconditionally. fr's
parse_client_tracking_state zeros out the parsed state via
`return Ok(ClientTrackingState::default())` when `!enabled`, so
the runtime's lookup-by-ID never sees the redirect value, and the
existence check is silently skipped.

Two other variants stay correct:
  OFF REDIRECT 0    → both reject (parse-time positivity check)
  OFF REDIRECT abc  → both reject (int parse error)
The bug is specifically: positive-int target that doesn't exist
combined with OFF.

Fix locus: crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs::parse_client_tracking_state
— preserve the redirect value in the !enabled return so the runtime
existence check at fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:10469 can still fire.

Test plan: pin
  TRACKING OFF REDIRECT 99999      → "client ID … does not exist"
  TRACKING OFF REDIRECT <self>     → OK
  TRACKING ON REDIRECT 99999       → "client ID … does not exist" (regression)
close_reason: fixed

title: HGETALL should bump LFU access frequency for existing hashes
description: Upstream Redis 7.2.4 t_hash.c::genericHgetallCommand uses lookupKeyReadOrReply on the hash key before iterating entries; db.c::lookupKey bumps LFU counters under allkeys-lfu/volatile-lfu unless LOOKUP_NOTOUCH is set. Store::hgetall currently returns pairs without bumping LFU, so HGETALL on an existing hash leaves OBJECT FREQ unchanged. Acceptance: under allkeys-lfu, HGETALL against an existing hash preserves sorted pair output and increments the hash key LFU frequency.
close_reason: Completed fr-store HGETALL LFU parity fix

title: SLOWLOG entry argv not truncated: missing SLOWLOG_ENTRY_MAX_ARGC(32)/SLOWLOG_ENTRY_MAX_STRING(128) caps
description: fr's slowlog stores full argv per entry; upstream slowlog.c::slowlogCreateEntry caps at 32 args (31 real + '... (N more arguments)') and trims any arg >128 bytes to 128B + '... (M more bytes)'. Differential probe vs redis 7.2.4: RPUSH w/40+ args and SET w/200B value diverged (fr kept 42 args / full 200B; redis showed 32 / '11 more arguments' / '72 more bytes'). Fix in fr-store record_slowlog_with_client.
close_reason: Fixed in 94fcd1f75: slowlog_build_argv applies SLOWLOG_ENTRY_MAX_ARGC(32)+SLOWLOG_ENTRY_MAX_STRING(128) caps in fr-store record_slowlog_with_client; unit test slowlog_truncates_argc_and_long_strings (oracle-derived) + gate scripts/slowlog_trunc_differ.py (FAILs pre-fix, byte-exact vs redis 7.2.4).

title: Parser accepts bare literals as statement starts, wrong wording (number/string/bool/nil)
description:
Lua 5.1 lparser.c restricts statement starts to keywords, Name, or
'(' for prefixexp. fr's parser routes anything that isn't a keyword
through parse_expr_or_assign which calls parse_suffixed_expr —
that accepts bare Number/String/Bool/Nil literals as expression
roots, then fails later with the assignment wording '=' expected
near '<eof>'.

Vendored rejects such tokens immediately at the statement-start
level with 'unexpected symbol near <X>'.

Repro:
  EVAL "local f,e = loadstring('123'); return tostring(e)" 0
    vendored: [string "123"]:1: unexpected symbol near '123'
    fr:       [string "123"]:1: '=' expected near '<eof>'

  EVAL "local f,e = loadstring('1.5'); return tostring(e)" 0
    vendored: [string "1.5"]:1: unexpected symbol near '1.5'
    fr:       [string "1.5"]:1: '=' expected near '<eof>'

  EVAL "local f,e = loadstring('+1'); return tostring(e)" 0
    Both: '[string "+1"]:1: unexpected symbol near +'  (the + isn't
    a suffixed-expr start so fr already happens to match here.)

The bare-Name case ('foo', 'foo bar', 'a b c') stays correct under
this bead — those parse as Name expressions and the existing
luabarestmt fix at parse_expr_or_assign:1924 emits the upstream
'=' expected wording. Only non-Name-or-LParen statement starts
need the unexpected-symbol redirect.

Fix: guard at parse_expr_or_assign entry. If self.peek() is not a
valid prefixexp start (Name or LParen), return
'unexpected symbol near <token>' immediately.
close_reason: completed

title: CONFIG SET repl-backlog-size recomputes backlog window
description: CONFIG SET repl-backlog-size updates config but leaves replication backlog window stale until next write, so INFO replication reports old first offset. Fix by recomputing backlog window when repl-backlog-size changes; add regression test.
close_reason: Recompute backlog window on CONFIG SET repl-backlog-size; add regression test

title: redis parity: REPLCONF should reject script context
description: Vendored Redis marks REPLCONF as NOSCRIPT in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/commands/replconf.json, but replconf_cmd at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:5914-5948 accepts ACK/GETACK/configuration pairs without any script_nesting_level guard. Add Redis-compatible rejection after the current syntax and integer validation paths, with Lua coverage for ACK and GETACK.
close_reason: Completed

title: Fix failing replica_stream_reconnect_uses_partial_psync_after_disconnect test
description:
The test replica_stream_reconnect_uses_partial_psync_after_disconnect is failing with:
- Warning: 'primary closed replication stream during sync'
- Assertion: gamma key returns nil instead of '3'

The test verifies that after a primary disconnect and reconnect, the replica receives data via CONTINUE sync. The failure suggests the CONTINUE sync isn't completing properly.

Potential causes:
1. EOF detection before buffer is fully processed
2. Timing issues in the mock primary server
3. Possible regression from commit 23a9eb6 (fail CONTINUE sync on primary disconnect)
close_reason: Fixed: Modified run_replica_sync to process buffered data before erroring on disconnect. Unit test replica_stream_reconnect_uses_partial_psync_after_disconnect now passes (48/48 unit tests pass).

title: LPOP should bump LFU access frequency
description: Redis 7.2.4 lpopCommand calls lookupKeyWriteOrReply without LOOKUP_NOTOUCH. FrankenRedis Store::lpop needs LFU bumping.
close_reason: done

title: SPOP <wrongtype-key> 0 returns empty instead of WRONGTYPE
description:
fr's SPOP with count=0 returns an empty array even when the key is a wrong type (e.g. a string), while vendored Redis 7.2.4 returns 'WRONGTYPE Operation against a key holding the wrong kind of value'.

Root cause: fr-store's spop_count() iterates 'for _ in 0..count' — when count=0 the loop is skipped and returns an empty Vec without consulting the key's type. The command handler at fr-command/src/lib.rs:10670-10676 wraps that in an array reply.

Upstream t_set.c::spopWithCountCommand orders the checks differently:
  1. Parse count (must be a positive long)
  2. lookupKeyWriteOrReply (return empty if missing)
  3. checkType(c, set, OBJ_SET) — emits WRONGTYPE if wrong type
  4. If count==0, addReply(emptyset) and return

So the WRONGTYPE error fires BEFORE the count==0 short-circuit.

Probe sweep #63 (probe126.sh):
  vendored SET strkey foo  -> OK
  vendored SPOP strkey 0   -> WRONGTYPE Operation against a key holding the wrong kind of value
  fr        SPOP strkey 0   -> (empty array)
  vendored SPOP strkey     -> WRONGTYPE  (already correct in fr)
  vendored SPOP strkey 5   -> WRONGTYPE  (already correct in fr)
  vendored SPOP strkey -1  -> ERR value is out of range, must be positive  (matches)
  vendored SPOP nokey 0    -> (empty)   (also matches fr)

SRANDMEMBER's count=0 path already validates type correctly (both servers return WRONGTYPE).

Fix plan:
- In fr-command/src/lib.rs spop() command handler, do a type-check via store before calling spop_count when count==0. Or modify spop_count() to consult store.key_type() up front and return WrongType error.
- Add regression test pinning SPOP wrongtype-key 0 -> WRONGTYPE.
close_reason: Fixed in iteration 96: added an explicit key-type check via store.key_type() in fr-command/src/lib.rs's spop() 3-arg branch BEFORE calling spop_count. Matches upstream t_set.c::spopWithCountCommand which validates type before the count==0 short-circuit. SPOP wrongtype-key 0 now returns WRONGTYPE; SPOP nokey 0 still returns empty array; SPOP wrongtype-key 5 (count>0) continues to report WRONGTYPE via the existing spop_count path. Live-diff matches vendored. Regression test spop_count_zero_on_wrongtype_key_reports_wrongtype_cf9z1 pins all three cases. Full 1021-test suite passes.

title: Lua loadstring/load globals missing in sandbox surface
description: fr's Lua sandbox doesn't register loadstring or load as globals, but vendored Redis 7.2.4 exposes both. Probes vs port 16380: type(loadstring)=function (vendored) / nonexistent-global error (fr); type(load)=function (vendored) / error (fr). loadstring('return 42')() should return 42; loadstring('!!!') should return nil + error message. The sandbox still applies to the loaded chunk (it cannot read unprotected globals). Implementation: register 'loadstring' and 'load' as RustFunctions; dispatch handler runs fr's lexer+parser on the source string and returns a LuaValue::Function (or nil + error string on parse failure). Discovered while running REVIEW PHASE against vendored.
close_reason: Implemented loadstring(src [, chunkname]) and load() in fr's Lua sandbox. loadstring parses via fr's existing Lexer+Parser and returns a LuaValue::Function with the parsed Vec<Stmt> as body, variadic params, and the caller's env captured (so the loaded chunk sees the calling script's locals as upvalues, matching Lua 5.1). Returns (nil, errmsg) on parse failure with the canonical '[string "CHUNK"]:1: ERROR' label (plus '=NAME' / '@NAME' prefix stripping, and multi-line source truncation with '...'). load() rejects non-function args with vendored's exact 'bad argument #1 to ? (function expected, got TYPE)' wording (Lua 5.1 uses '?' for C closures without registered debug names — same pattern as frankenredis-w3wkp). The chunk-generator-function load() form is not yet supported because fr's interpreter requires fully-parsed Vec<Stmt> trees; documented in the dispatch handler. Added format_lua_chunk_label() helper mirroring luaO_chunkid. Verified live diff vs vendored 7.2.4 (port 16380): 13/13 cases match. Pinned by lua_loadstring_parses_chunks_into_callable_functions (10 sub-cases). All 915 fr-command lib tests pass. Two known divergences filed as follow-ups: frankenredis-d4vlx (TableConstructor doesn't expand trailing ... — pre-existing fr bug not specific to loadstring) and frankenredis-ycaog (runtime errors inside loaded chunks use outer user_script:1 label instead of the chunk's label — requires LuaFunc source-tag plumbing).

title: CONFIG SET silently accepts duplicate parameters; upstream rejects with dedicated wording
description: Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4: `CONFIG SET maxmemory 100 maxmemory 200` returns `ERR CONFIG SET failed (possibly related to argument 'maxmemory') - duplicate parameter`. Upstream config.c::configSetCommand:872-880 walks the pair list, tracks each resolved config in `set_configs[]`, and when it sees the same config slot twice it sets `errstr="duplicate parameter"`, captures `err_arg_name = c->argv[2+i*2]->ptr`, and aborts. fr's handle_config_set accepted duplicates silently (last value wins).
acceptance_criteria: CONFIG SET rejects calls that name the same parameter twice (case-insensitive) with the dedicated `ERR CONFIG SET failed (possibly related to argument '<name>') - duplicate parameter` wording before any pair is applied. Non-duplicate two-pair calls still succeed.
close_reason: Added a duplicate-parameter check in fr-runtime/src/lib.rs::handle_config_set BEFORE the existing pair-processing loop. Walks argv[2..].chunks_exact(2), normalises each parameter via to_ascii_lowercase, and checks against a Vec<String> of already-seen names; on a hit returns config_set_failed(parameter, "duplicate parameter") which emits the canonical envelope. Added unit test config_set_rejects_duplicate_parameters_within_one_call covering: literal duplicate, case-insensitive duplicate (MaxMemory + maxmemory), non-duplicate two-pair call still succeeds, and final-state verification that the pre-error pair never landed.

title: CONFIG SET memory args reject strtoull overflow that upstream memtoull silently accepts
description:
util.c::memtoull's strtoull call is gated only on EINVAL and a trailing non-digit pointer — it does NOT inspect errno==ERANGE. So an overflowing digit run silently saturates to ULLONG_MAX and the subsequent val*mul wraps modulo 2^64. fr was rejecting twice: once at digits.parse() (returning Err) and again at checked_mul, surfacing 'argument must be a memory value' for inputs that vendored 7.2.4 happily accepts. Affected every CONFIG SET site that routes through parse_memory_size_arg (maxmemory, repl-backlog-size, client-output-buffer-limit memory threshold, hash-max-listpack-value, set-max-listpack-value, zset-max-listpack-value, list-max-listpack-size, etc.).

Repro vs vendored 7.2.4:
  16380> CONFIG SET maxmemory 9999999999999999999999gb
  OK
  16380> CONFIG GET maxmemory
  1) "maxmemory"
  2) "18446744072635809792"
  16381 (pre-fix)> CONFIG SET maxmemory 9999999999999999999999gb
  (error) ERR CONFIG SET failed - argument must be a memory value

Fix: saturate digits.parse to u64::MAX on overflow and switch the multiplier to wrapping_mul. Negative prefix and unknown suffixes still reject (memtoull gates those before strtoull). Verified: u64::MAX.wrapping_mul(1024^3) == 18446744072635809792, byte-for-byte matching vendored output.
acceptance_criteria: 1. CONFIG SET maxmemory '9999999999999999999999gb' returns OK and CONFIG GET reports 18446744072635809792 (matches vendored). 2. CONFIG SET maxmemory '99999999999999999999' (no suffix overflow) returns OK and CONFIG GET reports u64::MAX (18446744073709551615). 3. CONFIG SET maxmemory '5gb' continues to yield 5368709120. 4. CONFIG SET maxmemory '-1' still rejects with the existing envelope. 5. CONFIG SET maxmemory '5xb' still rejects (unknown suffix).

title: CONFIG GET / CONFIG SET silently run from EVAL/FCALL — missing CMD_NOSCRIPT guard
description: Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 confirmed both `redis.call('CONFIG','GET','maxmemory')` and `redis.call('CONFIG','SET','maxmemory','0')` surface the noscript reply ('ERR This Redis command is not allowed from script ...'). commands.def line 6530 declares config|get with CMD_NOSCRIPT and line 6534 declares config|set with CMD_NOSCRIPT (both alongside CMD_ADMIN, CMD_LOADING, CMD_STALE). fr's CONFIG GET handler at fr-command/src/lib.rs:14250 and CONFIG SET handler at line 14265 had no script-context guard, so a scripted CONFIG GET returned the live config values and a scripted CONFIG SET silently mutated the running config — both significant divergences from upstream behavior. CONFIG RESETSTAT and CONFIG REWRITE were already guarded.
close_reason: Added script_nesting_level guards to CONFIG GET (after arity check) and CONFIG SET (after arity check), matching the pattern already used by RESETSTAT/REWRITE. Extended config_subcommands_reject_scripts_after_arity_validation to also assert noscript fires for CONFIG GET and CONFIG SET when args are correct. 837 fr-command (4 pre-existing stream failures), 330/330 fr-store.

title: CONFIG SET with odd arity returns table-level wrong-arity instead of upstream 'ERR syntax error'
description: Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4: `CONFIG SET maxmemory 0 extra` (5 args) surfaces 'ERR syntax error'. Upstream config.c::configSetCommand line 819-821 explicitly routes odd-arity through addReplyErrorObject(shared.syntaxerr). fr's combined check `argv.len() < 4 || !argv.len().is_multiple_of(2)` returned WrongSubcommandArity for both branches, rendering as 'wrong number of arguments for config|set command' for the odd-arity case.
close_reason: Split the combined arity check: argv.len() < 4 still returns WrongSubcommandArity (matches vendored), but odd-arity now returns CommandError::SyntaxError (matches vendored 'ERR syntax error'). Test pin config_set_odd_arity_returns_upstream_syntax_error covers both branches plus the existing argv.len() < 4 sanity case. 838 fr-command (4 pre-existing stream failures), 330/330 fr-store.

title: perf: exact seven-pair MSET packet parser
description: br ready/open work was empty; continue closing the borrowed command hot-path coverage gap with an exact canonical seven-pair MSET packet parser. Add focused parser coverage and run crate cargo check only; batch tests remain pending by instruction.

title: fr-command LATENCY HISTOGRAM has wrong wire shape under both RESP2 and RESP3 — upstream emits Map-of-cmd→Map-of-{calls,histogram_usec→Map} but fr emits Array-of-[name,flat-array]
close_reason: fr-command latency_cmd HISTOGRAM branch now mirrors upstream latency.c::fillCommandCDF + latencyCommand HISTOGRAM dispatch. Inner CDF emits 2-entry Map { calls, histogram_usec } where histogram_usec is itself a Map of bucket_us → cumulative_count. Outer envelope emits Map<cmd_name → CDF>. Under RESP3 all three layers are RespFrame::Map; under RESP2 each Map flattens to alternating-Array of length 2*N. Existing test latency_histogram_returns_recorded_data was asserting the bug ([name, flat-array] tuple wrapper + missing histogram_usec separator) — updated to match upstream RESP2 alternating shape. New regression test latency_histogram_under_resp3_returns_nested_map_shape pins the 3-deep RESP3 Map structure. All 7 latency lib tests pass; the 4 unrelated pre-existing failures (xreadgroup/xinfo stream/migrate-vendored) reproduce on a clean checkout (verified via git stash). Twelfth in the RESP3-Map-shape audit cluster (i40x2/e4njz/udl3y/asvh1/5d04d/ndz0j/5h86s/cz712/ec2rf/q11am/1dfox/cgjlc).

title: SENTINEL REPLICAS should hide unannounced replicas like upstream
description: Upstream Redis 7.2.4 addReplyDictOfRedisInstances skips instances with SRI_SLAVE when replica_announced is false before building SENTINEL REPLICAS/SLAVES replies. fr-sentinel currently returns all master.slaves entries regardless of SentinelRedisInstance.replica_announced. Acceptance: SENTINEL REPLICAS and SENTINEL SLAVES omit unannounced replicas while keeping announced replicas visible, with focused regression coverage.
close_reason: Omitted unannounced replicas from SENTINEL REPLICAS/SLAVES output like upstream.

title: CONFIG SET boolean validation for createBoolConfig fields + add missing bool params
description: fr's static-override fallthrough silently accepted any value for ~28 boolean config fields declared via createBoolConfig in upstream config.c. Several missing entirely (activerehashing, cluster-require-full-coverage, disable-thp, dynamic-hz, replica-announced, aof-rewrite-incremental-fsync, rdb-save-incremental-fsync, cluster-replica-no-failover) — fr returned 'Unknown option'. Fix: (1) added the missing names to CONFIG_STATIC_PARAMS; (2) added a centralised yes/no validator in the static-override path that emits 'argument must be \'yes\' or \'no\''; (3) refactored stop-writes-on-bgsave-error and cluster-allow-reads-when-down to use the wrapper too; (4) added disable-thp to the immutable list

title: fr list engine: ChunkedList stores Vec<u8>-per-element vs redis packed listpack nodes — RESTORE/DUMP/RAM architectural floor
description: ARCHITECTURAL list-engine lever (filed by BlackThrush 2026-06-14; supersedes decode-path premises of q5ody-residual + lojpt). fr large-list repr ListRepr::Deque(ChunkedList) in fr-store/packed_set.rs (~1166) stores chunks of Vec<Vec<u8>> = ONE heap alloc PER ELEMENT. Redis quicklist stores packed listpack BYTE-BUFFER nodes (~128 elems/node, listpack bytes intact). MEASURED consequences: (1) list RESTORE 4.5x slow (lojpt) = n per-elem allocs; removing the VecDeque intermediate does NOT help (measured 0.53x = regression). (2) list DUMP residual 0.19x vs redis even after q5ody O(n) encode fix = ChunkedList iter + per-elem re-encode. (3) large-list RAM ~2x redis (relates p8dd2). REAL FIX: store each ChunkedList node as a packed listpack Vec<u8> (mirror redis quicklistNode listpack); RESTORE=memcpy node bytes O(nodes), DUMP=emit node bytes O(nodes), RAM=listpack-packed. SCOPE large/risky: touches EVERY list op (LPUSH/RPUSH/LSET/LINSERT/LREM/LRANGE/LPOS/LMOVE/encoding-report/digest). MUST stay byte-exact: keep current Vec<Vec<u8>> ChunkedList as in-test oracle, build packed-node variant behind same API, assert state_digest + DUMP sha256 + all list-op outputs identical across random shapes 1..many nodes. fr-store actively owned by CoralOx; coordinate / worktree. Score ~ Impact5 x Conf3 / Effort5 = 3.0; NOT single-turn.
notes: Pass 201 kept decode-copy sublever: listpack RESTORE moves decoded string Vec<u8> into Store instead of cloning. Current/candidate RESTORE 1766.64/s -> 2153.07/s (1.219x), identical DUMP/LRANGE/DUMP-after-RESTORE hashes; gates: focused fr-persist/fr-store tests, crate-scoped check/clippy. Full packed-node architecture remains open: next lever should store ChunkedList nodes as listpack byte buffers so RESTORE can retain node bytes and DUMP can emit nodes.
close_reason: Completed qbs5q pass202 string-only QUICKLIST_2 packed-node lever. Baseline b8487d8bf restore harness 465.5 ms +/- 33.8 ms; candidate 293.4 ms +/- 14.6 ms, 1.59x +/- 0.14. Reversed order 1.53x +/- 0.09. DUMP source/restored baseline/candidate sha256 all 308fb090d09d0b431758a85fc724cfd627d5203c8cf23835f004e78a783efad8; checksum 5442429139972036093; state_digest 628d0918a76e8c90. Score 3.29. Residual mixed-node/raw-DUMP work filed as frankenredis-r6c7z.

close_reason: Added metamorphic tests for ZSET

title: fr-runtime REPLICAOF/SLAVEOF missing cluster-mode rejection and client-is-replica rejection; sets master state on a replica session or in cluster mode silently
close_reason: done

title: Match Redis config NUL truncation semantics
close_reason: Matched Redis config NUL truncation semantics

title: fr-command: CLIENT PAUSE missing-timeout emits SubcommandSyntaxError envelope instead of upstream's table-level 'wrong number of arguments for client|pause command'
description:
Upstream commands.def line 1550 declares CLIENT PAUSE with arity = -3, so the table-level arity check fires before pauseCommand and emits the canonical 'wrong number of arguments for client|pause command' wording. Only too-many-args calls reach pauseCommand's addReplySubcommandSyntaxError envelope.

fr-command at lib.rs:15788-15793 routed both arity branches through the envelope. Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:
  CLIENT PAUSE                    fr: 'unknown subcommand or wrong number of arguments for ...' / vendored: 'wrong number of arguments for client|pause command'
  CLIENT PAUSE 1000 ALL extra     identical (envelope) - keep this branch

Fix: split the arity check; argv.len() < 3 routes through client_wrong_subcommand_arity, argv.len() > 4 keeps the envelope. Existing case-preservation pin at lib.rs:41032 had to retire its too-few-args entry; replaced with a too-many-args lowercase variant to retain coverage.

title: CLIENT REPLY with bogus mode in script context returns SyntaxError instead of upstream noscript
description: Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 confirmed `redis.call('CLIENT', 'REPLY', 'BOGUS')` from EVAL surfaces 'ERR This Redis command is not allowed from script ...' (commands.def line 1551 declares client|reply with CMD_NOSCRIPT). fr's dispatch_argv at line 1109 ran parse_client_reply_action(argv)? FIRST — for a bogus mode this returns Err(SyntaxError) and the `?` propagated, so the dispatch-level noscript guard at the same line never fired. fr returned SyntaxError where upstream returns noscript.
close_reason: Added a CLIENT-REPLY shape detector in fr-command/src/lib.rs:1109 that fires noscript for any arity-3 CLIENT REPLY call in script context BEFORE the mode parse runs. 4-arg cases still surface WrongSubcommandArity at fr-command for lua_eval to wrap. Updated existing pin client_reply_rejected_from_scripts_after_validation to assert noscript for the bogus-mode case (was pinning the divergent SyntaxError). 837 fr-command (4 pre-existing stream failures), 330/330 fr-store.

title: [perf] Lazy client-memory aggregate refresh for INFO/MEMORY STATS
description: Pass 103 profile-backed runtime hot-path lever: defer O(client_sessions) client-memory aggregate refresh from record_client_session/remove_client_session/replica membership updates to the INFO memory and MEMORY STATS read paths. Baseline/rebench with SET P16/300k under release-perf; prove behavior with focused runtime tests and golden transcript sha256.
close_reason: Duplicate of frankenredis-ohsk5.8 and rejected by the same gate: golden sha 88e0947c283e4236deab7ed211d7dc5179f98c16017177d00e2ed5e09dbcf7c8 matched, but SET P16/1M confirmation favored baseline 1.375s +/- 0.022s over candidate 1.422s +/- 0.066s. Source hunk removed; evidence in artifacts/optimization/frankenredis-clmemlazy/.

title: Remove empty CONFIG defaults known-issue path
description: scripts/config_defaults_gate.py says all former CONFIG default allowlist entries landed, but still carries KNOWN_ISSUES plumbing and output that would let future default drift pass if reintroduced. Remove the empty known-issue path so the no-mock defaults gate hard-fails every non-host-specific mismatch.

title: COMMAND DOCS reports wrong group for s-prefix string commands (SET/SETEX/STRLEN/SUBSTR), bitmap (SETBIT), and connection/server commands (SELECT/SWAPDB)
description:
command_group_for_docs ran `name.starts_with('s') → 'set'` before any explicit name match for the s-prefix string and bitmap commands, so COMMAND DOCS reported the wrong upstream group for an entire family of commands. Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 caught the exact divergences:

  SET / SETEX / SETNX / SETRANGE / STRLEN / SUBSTR
    upstream commands/<cmd>.json → 'string'
    fr (pre-fix) → 'set'
  SETBIT
    upstream commands/setbit.json → 'bitmap'
    fr (pre-fix) → 'set' (the `name.starts_with("bit")` arm came AFTER the s-prefix arm, so never fired)
  SELECT
    upstream commands/select.json → 'connection'
    fr (pre-fix) → 'generic'
  SWAPDB
    upstream commands/swapdb.json → 'server'
    fr (pre-fix) → 'generic'

Repro vs vendored 7.2.4:
  > COMMAND DOCS SET     → 16380='string',  16381='set'
  > COMMAND DOCS SETBIT  → 16380='bitmap',  16381='set'
  > COMMAND DOCS SELECT  → 16380='connection', 16381='generic'
  > COMMAND DOCS SWAPDB  → 16380='server', 16381='generic'

Fix: enumerate the affected commands explicitly BEFORE the prefix heuristics. Add SELECT to the connection arm, move SWAPDB to the server arm, drop SENTINEL from the server arm (upstream returns no COMMAND DOCS entry for SENTINEL — keeping it out keeps the listing path wire-compatible), and add an explicit string-arm for the s-prefix string commands plus a bitmap-arm that runs before the s-prefix→set fallback so SETBIT lands correctly.
acceptance_criteria: 1. SET/SETEX/SETNX/SETRANGE/STRLEN/SUBSTR all report group='string' matching vendored. 2. SETBIT reports group='bitmap'; GETBIT and BITCOUNT also remain bitmap. 3. SELECT reports group='connection'. 4. SWAPDB reports group='server'. 5. Real set commands (SADD/SREM/SSCAN/SMOVE/etc.) remain group='set' — no regression. 6. Other groups remain correct: ZADD→sorted_set, LPUSH/BLPOP→list, HSET→hash, XADD→stream, PFADD→hyperloglog, GEOADD→geo, SUBSCRIBE→pubsub, SCRIPT→scripting, MULTI→transactions, PING→connection, CLUSTER→cluster, SAVE/BGSAVE/SLOWLOG→server, SCAN/SORT/SORT_RO→generic. 7. GET/APPEND/GETRANGE remain group='string' via the default arm.

title: COMMAND LIST FILTERBY PATTERN matches case-sensitively, returns empty on uppercase patterns vs upstream's nocase=1
description:
Upstream server.c::commandListWithFilter calls stringmatchlen(filter->str, ..., cmd->fullname, ..., 1) where the trailing 1 is the nocase flag. Command names in fr's COMMAND_TABLE are stored lowercase (matching upstream's redisCommandTable), so any uppercase pattern in fr's case-sensitive glob_match would never match anything.

Repro vs vendored 7.2.4:
  > COMMAND LIST FILTERBY PATTERN 'GET*'
    16380> getset, get, getbit, getrange, getdel, getex
    16381 (pre-fix)> (empty array)
  > COMMAND LIST FILTERBY PATTERN 'GET' (exact uppercase)
    16380> get
    16381 (pre-fix)> (empty array)
  > COMMAND LIST FILTERBY PATTERN '?ScAn' (mixed-case + ? metachar)
    16380> would match hscan/sscan/zscan but only the lowercase chars
    16381 (pre-fix)> (empty array)

Fix: at the COMMAND LIST FILTERBY PATTERN branch in fr-command/src/lib.rs, lowercase the pattern bytes via to_ascii_lowercase() before passing to fr_store::glob_match. Since command names are already lowercase in COMMAND_TABLE, lowercasing one side is equivalent to stringmatchlen's two-sided tolower for ASCII patterns (which all command names are). Note: the 370-vs-241 total command-count delta when listing with PATTERN '*' is a known separate gap (fr does not register subcommands like CLIENT|LIST as listable command-table entries); not in scope for this bead.
acceptance_criteria: 1. COMMAND LIST FILTERBY PATTERN 'GET*' returns the same 6 names vendored returns. 2. COMMAND LIST FILTERBY PATTERN 'get*' continues to work (regression check on lowercase). 3. COMMAND LIST FILTERBY PATTERN '?ScAn' returns hscan/sscan/zscan (mixed-case + ? metachar). 4. Exact-case mismatch like 'GET' returns ['get']. 5. No-match patterns still return an empty array (no false positives). 6. The MODULE and ACLCAT branches remain untouched.

title: Expose AOF replay decode offsets
description: FR-P2C-005 Stage D2/D3 groundwork: add a small fr-persist API that decodes strict RESP AOF records while preserving start/end byte offsets for each replay record. Cover offset ordering, empty streams, and malformed/incomplete frame failures. This prepares RDB-preamble handoff and final-tail repair policy without wiring runtime startup yet.

title: CONFORMANCE: redis copy parity matrix

title: Aliased commands report base command name in wrong-arity error (georadius_ro/georadiusbymember_ro/restore-asking)
description: GEORADIUS_RO, GEORADIUSBYMEMBER_RO, and RESTORE-ASKING share a dispatch handler with their base command (CommandId::Georadius/Georadiusbymember/Restore). The handlers' argv.len() arity check hardcoded the BASE name in CommandError::WrongArity, so 'georadius_ro' with too few args returned "...for 'georadius' command" instead of "'georadius_ro'". Redis 7.2.4 names the invoked command's registered fullname. Found via full-command-table no-arg arity differential sweep (3/182 divergences). Fix: select the static name from argv[0] in each shared handler.
close_reason: Fixed in 0c93a1921: GEORADIUS_RO/GEORADIUSBYMEMBER_RO/RESTORE-ASKING shared-handler arity check now selects the static name from argv[0] so the wrong-arity error names the invoked alias (georadius_ro/georadiusbymember_ro/restore-asking), matching redis 7.2.4. Found via full-command-table no-arg arity sweep (3/182). Test aliased_commands_arity_error_names_the_invoked_alias pins all three.

title: [perf] Skip list-push lazy-expiry lookup when no expiries exist
description: Pass169 restored userspace attribution for the LPUSH residual: GDB-as-parent sample under LPUSH P16/C50 hit core::slice::cmp::eq/hashbrown in Store::drop_if_expired -> Store::lpush<&[u8]> -> Runtime::execute_plain_keyed_values_write_borrowed -> parse_borrowed_multibulk_action, while idle samples hit epoll_wait. The likely one-lever source candidate is to avoid the extra lazy-expiry/hash probe in list push operations when the store has no expiries (expires_count == 0 / relevant expiry sidecar empty), then proceed directly to the mutation lookup. Acceptance: fresh RCH-built current-main LPUSH P16/C50 baseline first; implement one scoped fr-store lever only; preserve lazy-expiry deletion propagation for TTL keys, dirty counters, LFU/touch behavior, list order, integer replies, wrongtype errors, and all golden output SHA; re-benchmark via RCH and keep only if Score>=2.0. Do not combine with parser borrowed-args scratch reuse or output batching.
close_reason: Kept no-expiry lazy-expiry skip for LPUSH/RPUSH: golden input e8eae7df83dcc3aaf85047b91c48b077099bc27976b80cdb817ad16faae6a15d; baseline/candidate output 0ae52cb40e2687096669173b4bf060219b9079b66a7be54f61ac963c711820e8; n5M paired LPUSH P16/C50 candidate 6.667s +/- 0.305 vs baseline 7.586s +/- 0.123, 1.14x +/- 0.06 faster; score 2.55; artifacts/optimization/frankenredis-cms7p/.

comments.author: CoralOx
comments.text: CoralOx follow-up after upstream pass170 rejection: I reran the same one-lever no-expiry lazy-expiry skip with a longer paired gate and kept the source hunk in follow-up commit 2bb43ee2c. The shorter n1M pass170 gate was too noisy; the n5M candidate-first paired gate measured candidate 6.667s +/- 0.305 vs baseline 7.586s +/- 0.123, 1.14x +/- 0.06 faster, Score 2.55. Golden input SHA e8eae7df83dcc3aaf85047b91c48b077099bc27976b80cdb817ad16faae6a15d; baseline/candidate output SHA 0ae52cb40e2687096669173b4bf060219b9079b66a7be54f61ac963c711820e8. Evidence: artifacts/optimization/frankenredis-cms7p/REPORT.md.

title: refresh OBJECT FREQ existing-key conformance expectation
description: fr-conformance core_server.json still expects OBJECT FREQ on an existing key to return integer 0 even when maxmemory-policy is not LFU. fr-command already asserts the Redis-aligned error string in object_freq_matches_lfu_policy_gating_and_counter_semantics, and conformance_core_server is now isolated on that stale case. Update the fixture expectation to the non-LFU error and verify with focused conformance via rch.
close_reason: Completed

title: TTL/EXPIRETIME use floor rounding; upstream rounds half-up
description:
Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4:

  SET k v
  EXPIRE k 100
  PTTL k → 99994
  TTL k:
    vendored: 100
    fr:       99

  SET k v
  EXPIRE k 1
  PTTL k → 993
  TTL k:
    vendored: 1
    fr:       0

Upstream src/expire.c::ttlGenericCommand converts ms→s with
`(ttl + 500) / 1000` (round half-up). fr uses plain integer
division (floor), so the immediate TTL after `EXPIRE k N` reads
back as `N-1` for any non-millisecond-aligned wall-clock offset.

Same bug affects EXPIRETIME (seconds-resolution version of
PEXPIRETIME), since vendored also rounds via the same formula on
the wall-clock side.

Fix locus: fr-store TTL conversion sites (ttl(), expiretime()) —
add `+ 500` before dividing by 1000.

Test plan: pin TTL after EXPIRE 100 → 100 (immediately), TTL after
EXPIRE 1 → 1 (immediately), PTTL still floor (matches upstream).
close_reason: fixed

title: COMMAND INFO flags column emits ACL categories ("admin", "scripting", "slow") instead of CMD flags for ~50+ commands
description:
RESOLVED via three batches (flagsbatch1/2/3) shipped 2026-05-08.
After the third batch, a programmatic sweep across all 240
fr top-level commands shows ZERO remaining divergences vs
`redis-cli -p 16380 COMMAND INFO <cmd>`. The original audit
enumerated ~50 commands; the actual final fix surface was 97

(sort_for_script removal, movablekeys additions, no_auth /

Pinned by three regression tests:

  command_info_flags_for_admin_blocking_pubsub_subset_matches_upstream
  command_info_flags_for_connection_movablekeys_subset_matches_upstream

ACL-derivation decoupled from flag-emission via

title: fr-conformance: 7 eval_redis_call_config_(get|set)_* fixture cases pinned non-upstream contract (CONFIG callable from Lua)
description:
core_scripting.json had 7 cases pinning successful CONFIG GET/SET execution from inside Lua scripts (e.g. eval_redis_call_config_get_maxmemory_policy expected bulk 'noeviction'). Both fr and vendored 7.2.4 reject CONFIG-from-script with the canonical CMD_NOSCRIPT error: 'ERR This Redis command is not allowed from script script: <sha>, on @user_script:1.' The fixture had a 7-row blind spot in the conformance harness for years.

Fix: replace the 7 stale cases with one byte-exact noscript pin against 'return redis.call(CONFIG, GET, maxmemory-policy)' — script SHA verified by sha1sum is 9c607d893a52610b325e988f6cef2674df6ed59e, matching both fr's and vendored's noscript reply. CONFIG GET non-script behavior is exercised in core_server.json directly.

title: COMMAND_TABLE keyspec triples for OBJECT/XGROUP/XINFO advertise top-level keys, but vendored emits (0,0,0) and routes via subcommand-level key_specs
description:
RESOLVED 2026-05-08.

Dropped COMMAND_TABLE keyspec triples for OBJECT/XGROUP/XINFO
from (2,2,1) to (0,0,0). fr's command_key_indexes() already has
dedicated name-based branches for these three commands
(crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:311-334) that pull argv[2] when
argv.len() >= 3, so the runtime continues to find the key
argument for ACL key permission checks and cluster routing —
only the COMMAND INFO emission path changed.

Now matches vendored 7.2.4: top-level COMMAND INFO entry shows
first/last/step = 0/0/0 with an empty key_specs sub-array,
while command_key_indexes still resolves the key from
OBJECT ENCODING <key>, XGROUP CREATE <key> ..., XINFO STREAM
<key>, etc.

Pinned via
command_info_keyspecs_for_object_xgroup_xinfo_emit_empty_per_upstream
which asserts both the COMMAND INFO emission (0/0/0/empty
key_specs) and that runtime extraction still yields [2] for
the canonical subcommand forms.

Programmatic sweep across all 240 fr top-level commands now
shows ZERO keyspec divergences vs `redis-cli COMMAND INFO`
against vendored 7.2.4.

title: fr-conformance: 22 core_debug cases pin pre-fix shapes (envelope wording, DIGEST-VALUE Array, DIGEST-VALUE empty array no-op)
description:
core_debug.json had 22 stale cases pinning pre-fix behavior that has since been realigned to upstream 7.2.4:

- 4 wrong-arity cases (debug_sleep_wrong_arity_no_seconds, _too_many, debug_reload_wrong_arity, debug_digest_wrong_arity) pinned the table-level 'wrong number of arguments for debug command' wording. fr routes these through the SubcommandSyntaxError envelope per dbgenv/dbgs/dbgqpt (and the bespoke RELOAD wording per ehnk).
- 16 DEBUG DIGEST-VALUE result cases pinned BulkString empty body (placeholder for the pre-dbgdiglen 16-char digest). fr emits Array of 40-char hex per (key, value) pair matching upstream's SHA1 frame format (dbgdiglen).
- 2 wrong-arity-no-keys cases pinned a wrong-arity error; fr returns empty Array since DIGEST-VALUE with argc==2 is a valid no-op (dbgdigvempty).

Fix: replace the 22 expected entries to match current upstream-aligned shapes — exact envelope wording for arity errors, any_array for DIGEST-VALUE result rows (deterministic hex tested via fr-command unit pins), and empty array for the no-op argc==2 cases.

title: Lua setfenv/getfenv missing from sandbox (Lua 5.1 stdlib)
description:
Vendored Redis 7.2.4 exposes both setfenv and getfenv from Lua 5.1
standard library in the EVAL sandbox. fr's strict-globals enforcement
treats them as nonexistent globals.

Repro:
  EVAL "setfenv(1, {}) return 1" 0
    vendored: (integer) 1
    fr:       (error) ERR ... 'setfenv'

  EVAL "return type(getfenv(0))" 0
    vendored: table
    fr:       (error) ERR ... 'getfenv'

Vendored semantics inspected:
- getfenv(0)        returns globals table (so getfenv(0).redis works)
- getfenv()/getfenv(1) returns current chunk env (= globals by default)
- getfenv(N>1)      errors 'bad argument #1 to getfenv (invalid level)'
- getfenv(<func>)   for user funcs returns the env; for missing
                    globals like print errors per sandbox rules
- setfenv(1, t)     replaces current chunk env (side-effectful)
- setfenv(<func>, t) returns the function
- setfenv(N>1, t)   errors 'bad argument #1 to ? (invalid level)' via pcall

Full implementation is non-trivial because real setfenv has visible
side effects (subsequent global accesses use the replaced env). For
Redis-style scripts the most common pattern is calling getfenv(0)
to introspect globals — that subset can be served by a partial
implementation. A faithful match likely requires teaching LuaState
about per-frame env tables.
close_reason: done

title: fr-runtime RESET allows replica connections to reset; upstream rejects with 'can only reset normal client connections'
close_reason: done

title: Make live CLIENT LIST/INFO enforce Redis arity and filters
description: fr-runtime accepts stray arguments for CLIENT INFO/LIST and ignores Redis LIST filters. Implement single-client Redis-compatible TYPE/ID filtering and reject invalid syntax/options on the live path.
close_reason: Live CLIENT LIST/INFO now enforce Redis-compatible filters and arity

title: Missing RESTORE-ASKING cluster variant
description: Redis 7.2.4 RESTORE-ASKING is the cluster-migration variant of RESTORE. Used during slot import. RESTORE is implemented but -ASKING variant is missing.
close_reason: Already implemented: RESTORE-ASKING is aliased to RESTORE in fr-command (line 2879)

title: CLUSTER SLOTSTATE returns 'cluster support disabled' but isn't a recognized subcommand in upstream 7.2.4
description: Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4: `CLUSTER SLOTSTATE` returns `ERR unknown subcommand 'SLOTSTATE'. Try CLUSTER HELP.` regardless of cluster_enabled state. SLOTSTATE is not present in upstream commands.def, cluster.c::clusterCommand, or any other source file — it's not a recognized subcommand at all. fr's cluster_cmd was short-circuiting the SLOTSTATE token to cluster_disabled_error() (carried over from the frankenredis-jsr7 follow-up that flagged it as a deferred stub), masking the typo and surfacing the misleading 'cluster support disabled' wording.
acceptance_criteria: CLUSTER SLOTSTATE must surface `ERR unknown subcommand 'SLOTSTATE'. Try CLUSTER HELP.` regardless of cluster_enabled state, matching upstream's commands.def-based dispatch (cluster|slotstate not found -> unknown subcommand error before clusterCommand runs). Builds on frankenredis-cunk's catch-all-no-disabled-gate fix.
close_reason: Removed the SLOTSTATE arm from fr-command::cluster_cmd so the token falls through to cluster_unknown_subcommand_error. Updated cluster_admin_subcommands_routes_to_disabled_when_cluster_off to drop the SLOTSTATE row, and added a new cluster_slotstate_is_not_a_recognized_subcommand pin covering both cluster_enabled states. All 38 fr-command cluster tests still green.

title: Newindex 'attempt to index' errors miss accessor label (local 'x' / field 'f' / etc.)
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:

  local x = nil; x.a = 1
    vendored: attempt to index local 'x' (a nil value)
    fr:       attempt to index a nil value

  local x = 1; x.a = 1
    vendored: attempt to index local 'x' (a number value)
    fr:       attempt to index a number value

Lua 5.1's luaG_typeerror reports the accessor by syntactic name. fr already has `type_error_with_label` (frankenredis-9ckvq) which builds the labeled wording from the AST expr, but the newindex (write) path at `table_set_by_expr` (~line 2971) bypasses it and emits the bare wording.

Fix in fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs ~line 2954: change table_set_by_expr's fallback Err to use type_error_with_label with the table_expr, "index", &table, env.

Same may apply to the `Expr::Index` path that calls table_set_by_expr — we need access to env/varargs in the call site since type_error_with_label needs env. Already in scope.
close_reason: Routed table_set_by_expr's non-table fallback Err (~line 2971) through type_error_with_label so newindex on a nil/number/string/boolean value picks up the accessor context (local 'x', field 'a', etc.) the same way reads do. Regression test newindex_attempt_to_index_carries_accessor_label_ct3ir pins 5 cases (4 local accessors + 1 field accessor). Existing accessor tests lua_attempt_to_X_errors_include_accessor_label_9ckvq + lua_attempt_to_call_errors_carry_accessor_context still pass. Live-diff vs vendored 16380: 5/5 match.

title: RESTORE should reject duplicate stream entry IDs
description: Upstream Redis 7.2.4 rejects duplicate stream radix-node keys while loading RDB_TYPE_STREAM_LISTPACKS_2/3 because raxTryInsert returns false for an existing node key. fr-store::restore_key currently converts decoded stream entries into a BTreeMap with entries.insert, silently overwriting duplicate (ms,seq) IDs from crafted RESTORE payloads. Acceptance: Store::restore_key rejects stream dump payloads containing duplicate entry IDs while preserving valid stream payloads.
close_reason: Added duplicate check when inserting stream entries to reject duplicate (ms, seq) IDs

title: [perf] Borrow parsed request frames instead of cloning before runtime dispatch
description: Profile-backed subtask under frankenredis-gu5nf. Target: drive per-request allocation toward zero on the Redis TCP request path by removing the extra deep RespFrame clone before runtime dispatch. Baseline: rch-built gu5nf-frameclone harness, direct hyperfine GET 3,000,000 iterations: clone mode 6.584s +/- 0.936s; borrow mode candidate 6.006s +/- 0.104s, 1.10x faster. Prior request-path profile in the ready bead identified allocator-heavy frame/argv churn (58% allocator samples). Alien primitive: region/request-scoped allocation discipline from alien_cs_graveyard §5.10 plus allocator/slab design §7.9; this first lever is the safe-Rust fallback card: eliminate an unnecessary request-scoped deep copy while preserving owned frame lifetime for post-dispatch checks. Proof obligations: identical RESP outputs/golden sha256, ordering/tie-breaking unchanged, FP/RNG N/A, crate-scoped rch check/test/clippy/fmt, re-benchmark.
close_reason: Completed: borrowed parsed RespFrame through runtime dispatch, eliminating the network hot-path deep clone before post-dispatch checks. Baseline harness clone GET 3,000,000: 6.584s +/- 0.936s; borrow: 6.006s +/- 0.104s (1.10x +/- 0.16). Repeat: clone 6.723s +/- 1.101s; borrow 5.909s +/- 0.159s (1.14x +/- 0.19). Isomorphism proof: clone/borrow checksum sha256 4bc197decd58d877658bc3e70a59f42eaf1cb1e0a31264555962d89c7fe08f40, golden aggregate sha256 e9ae5670f12c75fd1fa2cbb5621b5123aedafbbab2a4258f954819fb9894ef4b. Validation: rch build release-perf fr-server/fr-bench, rch check fr-runtime/fr-server, rch test fr-runtime, rch targeted fr-server query-buffer-limit test, rch clippy fr-runtime/fr-server, cargo fmt check. UBS nonzero only on existing broad inventory, no changed-line finding.

title: MEMORY STATS: peak.allocated/dataset.percentage/peak.percentage are wrong (hardcoded or mis-sourced)
description: Three related MEMORY STATS fields are wrong in fr: (1) peak.allocated is set to 'used' at fr-command/src/lib.rs:18509 instead of store.stat_used_memory_peak which fr already tracks via Store::observe_memory_sample, (2) dataset.percentage is hardcoded to 100.0 at lib.rs:18594 instead of the formula 100 * (total.allocated - startup.allocated - overhead.total) / (total.allocated - startup.allocated), (3) peak.percentage is hardcoded to 100.0 at lib.rs:18595 instead of 100 * total.allocated / peak.allocated. Vendored emits real values: peak.allocated=5654880, dataset.percentage=98.56, peak.percentage=18.43. All three are derivable from values fr already has. Acceptance: differential probe shows peak.allocated tracks the running max from observe_memory_sample and dataset.percentage/peak.percentage compute from the populated fields rather than emitting literal 100.0.
close_reason: MEMORY STATS peak.allocated, dataset.percentage, peak.percentage now compute from real values instead of hardcoded constants. (1) peak.allocated now reads store.stat_used_memory_peak (max-observed via observe_memory_sample/RSS), with fallback to 'used' if no sample has been taken yet. (2) dataset.percentage computes via upstream's formula: 100 * (1 - (overhead_total - startup_allocated) / (total_allocated - startup_allocated)), guarded against divide-by-zero. (3) peak.percentage = 100 * total_allocated / peak_allocated, same guard. Live differential probe after 5x SET 1KB workload + INFO memory tick: fr reports peak.allocated=10452992, dataset.percentage=85.97%, peak.percentage=0.05% (was 100, 100 before). All values derived from already-populated state. The absolute values differ from vendored (5654880, 98.56%, 18.43%) because vendored's startup baseline is ~866KB while fr's startup is 0 -- a separate gap. Tests: fr-command 910 pass.

title: CLIENT INFO/LIST line terminator emits \\r\\n — vendored emits only \\n per addReplyBulkSds with sdscatfmt
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:
ven CLIENT LIST tail: ...lib-ver=\\n (single LF, byte 0x0a)
fr  CLIENT LIST tail: ...lib-ver=\\r\\n (CRLF, bytes 0x0d 0x0a)

Per upstream networking.c::catClientInfoString, each client info string ends with sdscatfmt('...lib-ver=%s', ...) followed by addReplyBulkSds with no extra terminator. For CLIENT LIST the multi-client buffer joins entries with single LFs only (linkClientInList loop appends '\\n').

fr-command/src/lib.rs:15418 and fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:5946 both hardcode \\r\\n on the format string. Replace with \\n so CLIENT INFO/CLIENT LIST raw bulk-string contents byte-equal vendored. Acceptance: xxd of CLIENT LIST output ends in 0x0a not 0x0d 0x0a.
close_reason: Replaced \\r\\n with \\n in the CLIENT INFO format strings in both fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:5953 and fr-command/src/lib.rs:15418, mirroring upstream networking.c::catClientInfoString which uses sdscatfmt with a single LF terminator. Updated the matching test assertion. Verified xxd of CLIENT LIST output now ends in 0x0a (LF only) byte-equal vendored.

title: CLUSTER unknown-subcommand fallthrough leaks 'cluster support disabled' under disabled cluster mode; upstream returns 'unknown subcommand'
description: Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 (cluster-enabled=no): `CLUSTER MYID` returns `ERR This instance has cluster support disabled` (handler runs, gate fires), but `CLUSTER NOPE` returns `ERR unknown subcommand 'NOPE'. Try CLUSTER HELP.` — the handler is never invoked. Reason: each known cluster subcommand (cluster|myid, cluster|nodes, ...) has its own commands.def entry pointing at clusterCommand, so the dispatcher rejects unknown tokens with the unknown-subcommand wording before clusterCommand (and its top-of-function cluster_enabled gate) ever runs. The previous bead frankenredis-53ati misread the source's gate as authoritative for the fallthrough too and added an `if !cluster_enabled` check before the unknown-subcommand error path, so fr was leaking 'cluster support disabled' for typo'd subs under disabled cluster mode.
acceptance_criteria: An unknown CLUSTER subcommand must return `ERR unknown subcommand '<X>'. Try CLUSTER HELP.` regardless of the cluster_enabled state, mirroring upstream's commands.def-based dispatch (cluster|<X> not found -> unknown subcommand error before clusterCommand runs). Known subcommand wrong-arity and known-subcommand-when-disabled wordings stay unchanged.
close_reason: Removed the `if !cluster_enabled { return cluster_disabled_error() }` check that frankenredis-53ati had added before the unknown-subcommand fallthrough in fr-command::cluster_cmd. Updated the runtime test in fr_p2c_007_u001_cluster_subcommand_router_is_deterministic and the fr-command test cluster_unknown_subcommand_with_cluster_disabled_reports_unknown_subcommand (renamed from _reports_cluster_disabled) to assert the unknown-subcommand wording. All 37 fr-command cluster tests still green; runtime cluster router test still green.

title: SCAN cursor always returns 0 — fr returns all matching keys in one call instead of bucket-by-bucket scanning
description: fr's SCAN iterates the entire ordered_keys BTreeSet (or its equivalent for HSCAN/SSCAN/ZSCAN) in a single call and returns cursor 0. Vendored Redis uses dict bucket traversal — SCAN may return cursor 17 (non-zero) when there are more buckets to visit, even if all matching keys for the current bucket subset were returned. Client semantics differ: with fr, one SCAN call suffices; with vendored, clients must loop until cursor=0. This shows up in differential probes as  returning cursor 17 in vendored but cursor 0 in fr (both return empty key list). Wontfix-candidate: the storage layer is BTreeSet-based, not Redis dict; bucket-based cursor semantics would require a parallel hash structure with deterministic bucket assignment. Note: clients that correctly loop on non-zero cursors WILL behave correctly with fr (single iteration returns everything); strict byte-equivalent SCAN replies are the divergence. Discovered during REVIEW PHASE differential probe.
close_reason: Wontfix-architectural — fr's storage uses BTreeSet<Vec<u8>> for ordered_keys plus per-database HashMap for entries (crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs). Vendored Redis uses its bespoke dict (hashtable with siphash + open chaining) and SCAN walks dict.table[cursor] bucket-by-bucket using a reverse-bit cursor algorithm that gives stable iteration under rehashing. Replicating that cursor algorithm without a parallel hash structure would require either: (1) adding a new index keyed by hashbucket, (2) replacing BTreeSet+HashMap with a custom dict, or (3) faking a non-zero cursor on the first call to make clients loop twice (cosmetic only, adds state with no semantic value). Behavior is correct under the documented SCAN contract: 'When the cursor is 0 you can be sure that the iteration is over' — fr returns cursor 0 because iteration IS over. Clients that loop until cursor=0 (the documented pattern) work correctly with fr. The divergence is observable only via byte-for-byte diff of single SCAN replies. Not a parity bug per the README's strict-mode contract — semantics are preserved. Documented as a known cosmetic limitation.

title: INCRBYFLOAT and HINCRBYFLOAT use long double in vendored but f64 in fr (precision loss for large values)
description: Probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4: setting a value to 1e16 then INCRBYFLOAT v 1 returns '10000000000000001' on vendored but '10000000000000000' on fr. Same for HINCRBYFLOAT (HSET h f 1e16; HINCRBYFLOAT h f 1 -> vendored 10000000000000001 / fr 10000000000000000). Root cause: upstream t_string.c::incrbyfloatCommand and t_hash.c::hincrbyfloatCommand declare value/incr as 'long double' and call addReplyHumanLongDouble (formatting via ld2string mode LD_STR_HUMAN). Long double on x86 is 80-bit extended precision (64-bit mantissa) which can represent 1e16 + 1 exactly. fr uses Rust's f64 (53-bit mantissa, ~15.95 decimal digits) where 1e16 is at the precision boundary -- 1e16 + 1 rounds to 1e16 under round-to-nearest-even. Fix options: (1) accept the f64 limitation as architectural and document; (2) emulate long double via a soft-float library (no widely-used Rust crate); (3) wrap libc's 'long double' via a tiny C shim (blocked by #[forbid(unsafe_code)] policy in fr-* crates -- see project_no_unsafe_policy.md). Recommend filing as wontfix-architectural after confirming the call sites: crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs HINCRBYFLOAT and INCRBYFLOAT handlers (search for 'incrbyfloat' lowercase). Surfaced during REVIEW PHASE differential-probe sweep alongside frankenredis-nhy73.
close_reason: Closed as wontfix-architectural. fr-* crates have #![forbid(unsafe_code)] (see memory project_no_unsafe_policy.md), blocking FFI to libc's long double. Rust does not have a native 80-bit extended-precision type, and no actively maintained soft-float crate exposes long double semantics matching x86_64 ld2string. The precision loss only manifests for INCRBYFLOAT/HINCRBYFLOAT operands that cross f64's ~16-digit precision boundary; for the common case (values < 1e15) fr's f64 arithmetic is byte-equivalent with vendored. Documented as known limitation. Reproduce: SET v 1e16; INCRBYFLOAT v 1 -> vendored: '10000000000000001' / fr: '10000000000000000'.

title: CONFIG SET batch fixes: missing validation (repl-backlog-ttl, repl-ping-replica-period, repl-backlog-size 0); over-validation (list-max-listpack-size); wrong wording (cluster-node-timeout, cluster-link-sendbuf-limit, repl-backlog-size, repl-timeout)
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 16380:

(1) Missing validation — fr accepts invalid values:
  CONFIG SET repl-backlog-size 0
    vendored: ERR ... must be between 1 and 9223372036854775807 inclusive
    fr      : OK
  CONFIG SET repl-backlog-ttl -1 (or 'bogus')
    vendored: ERR ... must be between 0 and 9223372036854775807 inclusive (or parse err)
    fr      : OK
  CONFIG SET repl-ping-replica-period -1 / 0 / bogus
    vendored: ERR ... must be between 1 and 2147483647 inclusive (or parse err)
    fr      : OK

(2) Over-validation — fr rejects valid values:
  CONFIG SET list-max-listpack-size -6  (or -100)
    vendored: OK (upstream config.c declares range [INT_MIN, INT_MAX])
    fr      : ERR ... must be between -5 and 9223372036854775807 inclusive
  Same for list-max-ziplist-size alias.

(3) Wrong wording — fr emits non-standard 'Invalid argument':
  CONFIG SET cluster-node-timeout -1
    vendored: ERR ... must be between 0 and 9223372036854775807 inclusive
    fr      : ERR Invalid argument for CONFIG SET 'cluster-node-timeout'
  CONFIG SET cluster-link-sendbuf-limit -1
    vendored: ERR ... must be a memory value
    fr      : ERR Invalid argument for CONFIG SET 'cluster-link-sendbuf-limit'
  CONFIG SET repl-backlog-size -1
    vendored: ERR ... must be a memory value
    fr      : ERR Invalid argument for CONFIG SET 'repl-backlog-size'
  CONFIG SET repl-timeout 0 / -1
    vendored: ERR ... must be between 1 and 2147483647 inclusive
    fr      : ERR Invalid argument for CONFIG SET 'repl-timeout'

Fix in fr-runtime/src/lib.rs handle_config_set:
- repl-backlog-size: tighten lower bound to 1 (>=1, INT64_MAX); replace 'Invalid argument' with config_set_failed memory wording.
- repl-backlog-ttl: add validation (>=0, INT64_MAX) — currently no handler.
- repl-ping-replica-period (and slave-period alias): add validation (>=1, INT_MAX) — currently no handler.
- list-max-listpack-size (and ziplist-size alias): widen lower bound from -5 to i32::MIN.
- cluster-node-timeout, cluster-link-sendbuf-limit, repl-timeout: replace 'Invalid argument' with config_set_failed table-level wording.
close_reason: Fixed all 9 CONFIG SET defects across repl-*, cluster-*, and list-max-listpack-size. Repl-backlog-size, repl-backlog-ttl, repl-ping-replica-period, repl-timeout, cluster-link-sendbuf-limit, and cluster-node-timeout now route failures through configSetFailed with upstream table-level wording (range bounds matching upstream config.c declarations: [1, LLONG_MAX], [0, LLONG_MAX], [1, INT_MAX], [1, INT_MAX], MEMORY_CONFIG, [0, LLONG_MAX]). list-max-listpack-size lower bound widened from -5 to INT_MIN per upstream INTEGER_CONFIG declaration. Pinned via config_set_repl_and_cluster_params_use_table_level_wording_per_upstream covering all 9 cases plus over-validation regression and memory-suffix happy path. Removed obsolete repl_backlog_histlen_is_zero_when_backlog_disabled which pinned the pre-validator behavior of accepting size=0 (now correctly rejected). 357/357 fr-runtime tests pass; workspace clippy clean under -D warnings.

title: ZUNIONSTORE/ZINTERSTORE missing LFU tracking on source keys
description:
ZUNIONSTORE and ZINTERSTORE read from multiple source keys but were not bumping LFU frequency counters for accessed source keys.

Fix: Added lfu_tracking_enabled check and bump_lfu_freq calls for each source key access in both functions.
close_reason: fixed

title: fr-protocol: fuzz_resp_parser corpus has no RESP3 dialect seeds (Map/Set/Push/Double/Boolean/BigNumber/Verbatim/Attribute/Null)
description:
fuzz/corpus/fuzz_resp_parser/ shipped 13 seeds covering only the RESP2 grammar (simple/error/integer/bulk/array variants + inline command). The fuzz target itself enables RESP3 in its permissive config (`allow_resp3: true` in fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_resp_parser.rs), so libfuzzer is asked to discover the entire RESP3 prefix matrix from scratch — slow and easy to miss prefix-specific corner cases like `-inf`/`nan` Double, the Attribute prefix (`|`), the trailing `\r\n` on BigNumber, or the verbatim `=` length-then-`txt:` shape.

Seeded 12 RESP3 dialect entries directly into the corpus so libfuzzer starts each campaign with at least one valid sample of every prefix:

  resp3_map_2_pairs              %2 + 2 (key,value) pairs
  resp3_empty_map                %0
  resp3_set_3_strings            ~3 of strings
  resp3_push_pubsub              >3 push (pubsub-style)
  resp3_double                   ,3.14
  resp3_double_inf               ,inf
  resp3_double_neg_inf           ,-inf
  resp3_double_nan               ,nan
  resp3_bool_true                #t
  resp3_bool_false               #f
  resp3_bignumber                ( + 43-digit decimal
  resp3_verbatim_text            =15 with txt:Some string
  resp3_null                     _
  resp3_attribute_then_string    |1 + (key,value) + simple string

Acceptance: cargo-fuzz run fuzz_resp_parser -- -runs=1 ingests all
new seeds without rejecting any. Tracking ticket so the fix lands.

Reference: fr-protocol corpus expansion on 2026-05-04 while reviewing fuzzing posture.
notes: Closed in same diff: 14 RESP3 corpus seeds added to fuzz/corpus/fuzz_resp_parser/, plus crates/fr-protocol/tests/fuzz_corpus_smoke.rs that replays every corpus file through parse_frame and parse_frame_with_config (restrictive + permissive configs) under regular cargo test, asserting no panic and corpus size >= 13. cargo test -p fr-protocol --test fuzz_corpus_smoke passes.

title: GETEX with EXAT/PXAT past deadline fires expire+expired instead of del
description: Follow-up to frankenredis-08t0x (EXPIRE family fixed in fr-store). GETEX key EXAT/PXAT <past> still fires __keyevent expire + expired; upstream fires a single NOTIFY_GENERIC del (t_string.c getexCommand routes a past deadline through the same delete-and-del path as expireGenericCommand). GETEX uses a different store path than expire_at_milliseconds (fr-command getex + runtime command_to_keyspace_event GETEX->expire). Fix spans fr-command getex (detect past EXAT/PXAT -> delete + del, skip setting TTL) and excluding GETEX-past from the runtime 'expire' emission. Verified vs vendored 7.2.4.
close_reason: Fixed: Store::getex now emits its own keyspace events (mirroring upstream getexCommand) and GETEX is excluded from the runtime generic path. EXAT/PXAT past deadline -> delete + 'del' (was 'expire'+lazy 'expired'); future -> 'expire'; PERSIST -> 'persist' + dirty ONLY when a TTL was actually removed (also fixes a spurious dirty+phantom 'persist' on no-TTL keys). Verified byte-exact vs vendored 7.2.4: keyevents, dirty deltas, reply value, key existence all match across 8 GETEX variants. 481 fr-store + 398 fr-runtime tests pass; regression test added.

title: Lua pcall coerces non-string error values to string (table/boolean/number lose type)
description: fr's error() and pcall propagate errors through Result<_, String>, so when a script does error({code=42}) or error(true), pcall receives a stringified version (e.g. 'table: 0x...' or 'true'). Lua 5.1 preserves the exact value: pcall returns the original table/boolean/number, and the calling code can index/compare it. Live differential probes: pcall(function() error({msg='x'}) end) -> fr returns (false, 'table: 0x...'), vendored returns (false, table); same for error(true) and error(42). This requires changing fr's error-propagation type from String to a richer LuaErrorValue enum that carries the original LuaValue, with String-coercion only at the boundary where Redis needs an error reply string. Moderate refactor - filing as follow-up since the simpler fixes are landed.
close_reason: Implemented typed-error preservation via LUA_TYPED_ERROR_SENTINEL + LuaState::pending_error_value sidechannel (same pattern as pending_yield/LUA_YIELD_SENTINEL). error() for nil/bool/table/function/thread stashes the original LuaValue and raises the sentinel; pcall/xpcall atomically take() the value back via Option::take() so (false, val) preserves the original type. Numbers with level>0 still coerce to 'user_script:1: N' string (matches Lua 5.1's lua_isstring-on-number quirk via lua_concat); level=0 preserves Number type. Top-level escape renders the typed value via to_display_string for the Redis error reply (vendored Redis 7.2 crashes on table escape; fr produces a sensible string). Also added Rc::ptr_eq Table equality in lua_raw_equal so error(t); err==t holds. Verified live diff vs vendored 7.2.4 (port 16380): 16/16 cases match including table/bool/nil preservation, table identity, number-with-prefix coercion, nested pcall (inner consumes its own value; outer round-trips independently), xpcall handler receives the typed value, and pending state cleared after a successful pcall. Pinned by lua_pcall_preserves_typed_error_values covering 9 cases. All 914 fr-command lib tests pass.

title: SREM should bump LFU access frequency for existing sets

Two new pin tests: lcs_idx_under_resp3_returns_2_entry_map (asserts Map shape with 'matches' → Array, 'len' → Integer key order) and lcs_idx_under_resp2_stays_flat_4_element_array. Existing 3 LCS tests still pass; clippy clean. Eighth in the RESP3-Map-shape audit cluster (i40x2/e4njz/udl3y/asvh1/5d04d/ndz0j/5h86s/cz712).
