title: fr-command WAITAOF / FAILOVER place script_nesting_level noscript guard after argument parsing — scripted bad-arg calls surface parse error instead of upstream's CMD_NOSCRIPT reply
description:
## Symptom
\`redis.call('WAITAOF', 'abc', 0, 0)\` from Lua via dispatch_argv returns 'ERR value is not an integer or out of range' (the numlocal parse error). Upstream rejects with 'This Redis command is not allowed from script' (CMD_NOSCRIPT fires at dispatch level before the handler runs, so parse never executes).

Same shape for \`redis.call('FAILOVER', 'NOSUCH')\` — fr returns 'ERR syntax error' instead of the noscript reply.

## Differential probe
Upstream commands.def declares both WAITAOF (line ~10780) and FAILOVER (line 10777) with CMD_NOSCRIPT. server.c::processCommand evaluates the noscript flag against c->cmd before invoking the handler — so the noscript reply always wins over any in-handler parsing or validation.

fr-command's wait_cmd (just fixed in 2ff1e41) places its 'if store.script_nesting_level >= 1' guard immediately after the arity check and before parse_i64_arg, mirroring upstream's order of evaluation.

waitaof_cmd at fr-command/src/lib.rs:7815-7864 places the same guard at line 7843 — *after* lines 7820-7841 already parse numlocal, numreplicas, and timeout. failover_cmd at lib.rs:10731-10791 places the guard at line 10778 — after the entire option-parsing while loop at lines 10741-10776.

So inside a Lua script, both handlers expose a parse error first when the script also passes invalid args.

## Fix
1. waitaof_cmd: move 'if store.script_nesting_level >= 1' from line 7843 to immediately after the arity check at line 7818, before parse_i64_arg(&argv[1]).
2. failover_cmd: move 'if store.script_nesting_level >= 1' from line 10778 to the top of the function (right after the local-variable initialization), before the option-parsing while loop.
3. Add tests pinning that scripted bad-arg WAITAOF and FAILOVER both surface SCRIPT_NOSCRIPT_ERROR.

## Out of scope
- The deeper 'WAITAOF cannot be used with replica instances' / 'FAILOVER not allowed in cluster mode' / 'FAILOVER is not valid when server is a replica' checks (which Store doesn't expose) are tracked separately and live in fr-runtime's network handlers.
close_reason: fr-command WAITAOF, FAILOVER, and REPLICAOF now place the script_nesting_level guard immediately after the arity check (was after argument parsing), mirroring upstream script.c::scriptCommand which evaluates arity FIRST and CMD_NOSCRIPT SECOND before any handler-level parsing. Inside a Lua script, scripted bad-arg calls now surface 'This Redis command is not allowed from script' rather than the handler's own parse / syntax / timeout error. Existing test waitaof_rejected_from_scripts_after_integer_validation renamed to waitaof_rejected_from_scripts_after_arity_check and rewritten to assert noscript wins for every arity-correct input. Existing test replication_admin_commands_reject_scripts_after_validation renamed to replication_admin_commands_reject_scripts_after_arity_check and rewritten to assert REPLICAOF bad port + FAILOVER ABORT ABORT + FAILOVER TIMEOUT 0 all surface noscript. New test waitaof_and_failover_noscript_guard_precedes_argument_parsing pins the new ordering for both commands.

title: INFO Replication master_replid emits 40-zero placeholder — vendored generates random hex SHA at startup
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:
ven INFO replication: master_replid:658d337972ac2582788946eec8d2f78c238d3c21
fr  INFO replication: master_replid:0000000000000000000000000000000000000000

Per upstream replication.c::changeReplicationId, the master_replid is initialized at startup via getRandomHexChars(server.replid, CONFIG_RUN_ID_SIZE) — same generator used for run_id but a separate value so it can rotate independently (DEBUG CHANGE-REPL-ID, failover, etc.). fr hardcodes the initial replid to 40 zeros at fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:2141-2143, so any client tracking primary identity sees an all-zero placeholder.

Fix: pub-ify (or duplicate) generate_run_id from fr-store, call it when constructing ReplicationRuntimeState. Acceptance: master_replid emits a non-zero 40-char hex string distinct from run_id.
close_reason: Pub-ified generate_run_id_hex helper from fr-store and seeded ReplicationRuntimeState replid with it instead of 40-zeros literal. Also fixed generate_run_id to advance a process-lifetime AtomicU64 counter + mix in nanosecond-precision time so successive calls (server_run_id, cluster_shard_id, master_replid) collapse to distinct values instead of identical PID-derived strings. master_replid now emits a non-zero 40-char hex distinct from run_id; full workspace tests + clippy stay clean.

title: Implement persistent replica streaming, reconnect, and partial-resync semantics
description: The server now performs a one-shot live REPLICAOF/PSYNC handshake and FULLRESYNC apply over TCP, but it does not yet maintain a long-lived replication socket, continuously ingest streamed writes, or reuse replid/offset for reconnect-driven CONTINUE partial resync. Implement persistent stream ownership, reconnect/backoff, and partial-resync state continuity.
close_reason: Already implemented and verified in current tree

title: Bound fr-server per-client frame batches
description: Deadlock-finder liveness finding: fr-server processed all buffered frames for one readable client without a per-client event-loop budget, so a large pipeline could monopolize the single-threaded loop and delay other clients, timers, blocked-client wakeups, pub/sub, and replication. Cap complete frame processing per client tick and defer leftovers for later ticks.
close_reason: Capped per-client frame processing in fr-server and deferred leftover buffered frames across event-loop ticks.

title: CONFORMANCE: redis object parity matrix
description: Wire core_object.json via live_redis_diff. OBJECT ENCODING/FREQ/IDLETIME/REFCOUNT/HELP.

Emit deterministic XGROUP CREATECONSUMER and XCLAIM reconstruction commands, extend the AOF replay oracle to consume them, and add fixed plus metamorphic round-trip coverage.

title: [perf] Fast-path HGET ACL key access
description: Profile-backed HGET pass under frankenredis-gu5nf. Baseline artifacts: artifacts/optimization/icywolf-perf-20260602/pass3-hget-gap/ show FrankenRedis HGET p16 keyspace=10000 at 521.7 ms +/- 21.9 ms vs vendored Redis 147.6 ms +/- 7.7 ms (Redis 3.53x faster); direct fr-bench 162,289 ops/s vs Redis 619,674 ops/s. GDB sampling artifacts: artifacts/optimization/icywolf-perf-20260602/pass3-hget-gdb/ sampled fr_command::command_acl_key_access allocation/reallocation frames and command_key_references scanning on the HGET path. Current command_acl_key_access builds CommandKeyReference Vec then maps to AclKeyAccess Vec even though valid HGET always has exactly one RO/access key at argv[1]. Alien primitive: allocation-churn elimination / known-small specialization from alien_cs_graveyard sections 5.10 and 7.9; safe-Rust fallback is a single HGET fast path preserving wrong-arity empty access behavior. Proof obligations: ordering/tie-breaking unchanged, FP/RNG N/A, ACL read/write classification identical, golden RESP sha256 baseline/candidate match, rch crate-scoped fmt/check/test/clippy, before/after hyperfine via rch-built binaries. Keep only if Score >= 2.0.
close_reason: Completed: HGET ACL access fast path. Profile: HGET p16 keyspace=10000 gap vs Redis 521.7ms +/-21.9 vs 147.6ms +/-7.7; GDB sampled command_acl_key_access allocation/key-spec scan. Benchmark: baseline-generic-acl 512.4ms +/-12.6 -> candidate-hget-acl-fast 443.9ms +/-18.9 (1.15x faster); direct 167181.69 -> 227435.93 ops/s. Isomorphism: golden HGET/ACL trace sha256 e4bb9dba5b82428cc59a5f77cba2bcea5a4a10bbe6698c0012668facfc2c876c matched baseline and candidate; ordering/tie-breaking unchanged; FP/RNG N/A. Score: Impact 3.0 x Confidence 0.9 / Effort 1.0 = 2.7.

close_reason: Shipped 942702808 (pushed main+master). COMMAND / bare COMMAND INFO no longer chain SUBCOMMAND_TABLE — top-level walks now list 241 (subcommands nested in parents), matching redis 7.2.4. COMMAND LIST (370) and explicit COMMAND INFO <sub> lookup keep the chain (correct). Verified live: COMMAND/INFO *241, LIST *370, DOCS *482, COUNT :241. Test command_and_command_info_list_top_level_only_d309r; fr-command 1127 pass, clippy clean.

title: Lua bit.* library: custom error wording, missing arity checks, wrong float rounding
description:
fr's bit.* implementation in crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs has
three independent parity gaps vs LuaJIT (which Redis 7.2.4 bundles
as the 'bit' global):

1. Error wording: lua_value_to_u32 emits a custom "bad argument to
   bit op (number expected, got X)" template. Vendored uses the
   standard luaL_argerror form "bad argument #N to 'FUNC' (number
   expected, got TYPE)" with user_script:1: prefix when called via
   AST, '?'-name without prefix when called via pcall directly.

2. Missing arity: bit.band(), bit.bor(), bit.bxor(), bit.lshift(),
   bit.rshift(), bit.arshift(), bit.rol(), bit.ror(), bit.bswap(),
   bit.tobit(), bit.tohex(), bit.bnot() silently default missing
   args to 0 and return a value. Vendored raises
   "bad argument #N to 'FUNC' (number expected, got no value)".

3. Float rounding: lua_value_to_u32 uses `(f as i64 as i32) as u32`
   which truncates toward zero. LuaJIT's lj_num2int uses C99 round-
   to-nearest-even, so bit.tobit(1.5) → 2 and bit.tobit(2.5) → 2,
   not 1 and 2 as fr currently returns.

Probed against vendored Redis 7.2.4 on :16380:

  return bit.band()
    vendored: ERR ... bad argument #1 to 'band' (number expected, got no value)
    fr:       -1

  return bit.lshift()
    vendored: ERR ... bad argument #1 to 'lshift' (number expected, got no value)
    fr:       0

  return bit.lshift(1)
    vendored: ERR ... bad argument #2 to 'lshift' (number expected, got no value)
    fr:       1

  return bit.tohex('abc')
    vendored: ERR ... bad argument #1 to 'tohex' (number expected, got string)
    fr:       ERR bad argument to bit op (number expected, got string 'abc')

  return bit.band(nil, 1)
    vendored: ERR ... bad argument #1 to 'band' (number expected, got nil)
    fr:       ERR bad argument to bit op (number expected, got nil)

  return bit.band({}, 1)
    vendored: ERR ... bad argument #1 to 'band' (number expected, got table)
    fr:       ERR bad argument to bit op (number expected, got non-number)

  return bit.band(1.5, 1)
    vendored: 0  (because bit.tobit(1.5) = 2, band(2, 1) = 0)
    fr:       1  (truncates 1.5 → 1)

Fix locus: crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs

- Convert lua_value_to_u32 → method that takes inv_name + arg_idx +
  func_name, produces correct "bad argument #N to 'FUNC' (number
  expected, got TYPE)" wording via lua_format_argerror.
- Distinguish missing arg (None) from explicit nil: missing reports
  "got no value", nil reports "got nil".
- Implement banker's rounding for float→int conversion: use the
  closest integer; on ties, choose the even one. The LuaJIT code is
  essentially `lrint(x)` with the FE_TONEAREST mode.
- Each bit.* handler needs to call the helper with the right inv_name
  / arg_idx / func_name. The bit's "FUNC" name comes from the AST
  call site (current_invocation_name) or '?' when pcall'd.

Test plan: pin ~15 cases covering missing-arg, nil arg, table arg,
string arg, fractional-float arg with banker's rounding, plus the
positive control cases (band(0xFF, 0x0F) = 15, etc.).
close_reason: Replaced lua_value_to_u32 with lua_value_to_u32_for_bitop in crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs which (1) takes inv_name + arg_idx + fname and formats errors via lua_format_argerror — so the prefix and function name match vendored both for AST-direct calls and pcall-indirect calls; (2) distinguishes missing args (got no value) from explicit nil/table/string/boolean; (3) uses f64::round_ties_even for the float→int conversion, matching LuaJIT's lj_num2int banker's rounding so bit.tobit(1.5)=2 and bit.band(1.5,1)=0 like vendored. Every bit.* handler — band/bor/bxor/bnot/lshift/rshift/arshift/rol/ror/bswap/tobit/tohex — now calls the helper with the right arg-index. The variadic ops (band/bor/bxor) explicitly raise on zero args instead of folding from the identity. Regression test lua_bit_library_parity_d3ovh pins 10 positive controls plus 7 error cases plus 2 pcall-indirect cases. Live diff vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 on :16380 clean across 22 probes.

title: Lua TableConstructor with trailing ... doesn't expand multi-values
description: fr's Expr::TableConstructor evaluates every TableField::Positional as a single value via eval_expr, so the last-positional expansion rule that Lua 5.1 applies to ... and function calls is missing. Probe: 'local function f(...) local t={...}; return #t end; return f(\'a\',\'b\',\'c\')' — vendored returns 3, fr returns 1. Same issue would apply to 'local t={fn_returning_multi()}'. Fix: in TableConstructor eval, detect when the last positional field is Expr::VarArgs or Expr::Call/Expr::MethodCall and expand into multiple positional entries (mirrors the eval_call_args expansion path). Discovered while implementing frankenredis-cfflo loadstring.
close_reason: Fixed Expr::TableConstructor in lua_eval.rs to expand the LAST positional field's multi-value (varargs or function/method call) into multiple table entries, mirroring Lua 5.1 / vendored Redis 7.2.4. Non-last positions still take only the first value. Mirrors the existing eval_call_args last-arg expansion pattern. Verified live diff vs vendored on port 16380: 12/12 cases match including {...}, {1,2,...}, {...,99} (non-last takes one), {g()}, {0,g()}, {g(),99}, {x=1,...} (named + trailing varargs), {(),x=1} (last-field check accounts for trailing named), [5]=99 prefix, method call as trailing. Pinned by lua_table_constructor_expands_trailing_multi_values covering 9 sub-cases. All 916 fr-command lib tests pass.

title: Fix UNLINK wrong-arity parity and restore live core_server case
description: UNLINK currently routes through the shared DEL implementation and reports DEL in wrong-arity errors. Make UNLINK report its own command name, restore unlink_wrong_arity to the stable core_server live Redis subset, and verify with targeted rch conformance checks.
close_reason: Completed

title: Remove empty CONFIG persistence known-reset path
description: scripts/config_persistence_reload_gate.py keeps KNOWN_RESET_ON_FR as an empty set, but still has known/fixed reporting branches for CONFIG values resetting after DEBUG RELOAD. Remove the empty allowlist plumbing so any persistence/reset drift is a hard failure.

title: Eliminate try_flush write_buf drain memmove (30% of pipelined-write CPU) via offset cursor
description: PROFILE (redis-benchmark LPUSH -P16 saturation + perf -g on fr): try_flush -> __send -> __memmove_avx_unaligned = 30.3% CPU (18.9% SELF). Source fr-server/main.rs:463 write_buf.drain(..total_written): on a PARTIAL socket write (EAGAIN with big pipelined backlog) memmoves the unsent remainder to buffer front every flush, O(remaining)/flush. push_front is only 1.37% (list structure NOT the gap). redis-benchmark best-of-3: fr parity-or-faster on all std cmds except LPUSH 0.91x / RPUSH 0.95x (both consistently <1.0x). LEVER (offset cursor): add write_pos to ClientConnection; try_flush sends from write_buf[write_pos..], advances it; full flush -> clear+reset (zero memmove, common case); partial -> keep write_pos (no drain). Thread offset through writer-pool handoff (WriterJob.start; flush_writer_job inits total_written=start; drop its drain at ~584) so the remainder is moved not copied. Fix pending_output_bytes=len-write_pos + the !write_buf.is_empty() arm at line 2828. Symmetric read_buf.drain at 2825 is a smaller follow-on. RISK: client output under backpressure is ordering-critical across the writer-pool thread boundary; a subtle offset bug = silent reply corruption. Verify byte-exact under FORCED partial writes (slow reader + large pipelined replies) vs redis + parity suite. BLOCKED THIS SESSION: parity_suite runner 144s (sandbox kills heavy multi-server spawn); individual 2-server gates still run. Target LPUSH 0.91x->=1.05x, pipelined-write CPU -30%.
close_reason: Shipped 8bc4e5df5: write_pos offset cursor eliminates the try_flush partial-flush drain memmove (30% CPU). Byte-exact (263MB backpressured stream identical to redis, sha 8fd4b2d91cf570c6). High-concurrency new/old LPUSH 1.07x/SET 1.04x; fr parity->faster vs redis. Single-client neutral (syscall-bound).

title: fr-command XTRIM with LIMIT but no MAXLEN/MINID returns generic 'syntax error' instead of upstream's specific wording
description:
Tick finding via /testing-conformance-harnesses sweep on stream-trim error wording vs vendored t_stream.c::streamParseAddOrTrimArgsOrReply.

UPSTREAM (t_stream.c lines 968-1000):
  XTRIM's parser walks argv[2..] iteratively. LIMIT N is recognised as a flag (line 968) regardless of whether MAXLEN/MINID came first. After the loop, line 998-1000 emits:
      'syntax error, LIMIT cannot be used without specifying a trimming strategy'
  for any input that set args->limit but left args->trim_strategy == TRIM_STRATEGY_NONE.

FR CURRENT (crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs::xtrim lines 5870-5874):
  fr requires argv[2] to be exactly MAXLEN or MINID; anything else falls through to a generic SyntaxError. So `XTRIM key LIMIT 5` — which upstream catches with the LIMIT-specific message — returns plain 'ERR syntax error' in fr.

REPRO:
  XTRIM mystream LIMIT 5
    upstream → 'ERR syntax error, LIMIT cannot be used without specifying a trimming strategy'
    fr      → 'ERR syntax error'

Severity: [LOW]. Wire-format conformance gap. Visible only when a user provides LIMIT alone without MAXLEN/MINID.

FIX:
  Before the MAXLEN/MINID check, special-case argv[2] == LIMIT and emit upstream's wording. Don't reach for a full parser refactor — the targeted check is the minimum viable fix.
close_reason:
Added a special-case for XTRIM key LIMIT N that emits upstream's specific wording instead of fr's generic 'syntax error'.

CHANGE (crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs::xtrim around line 5874):
Before falling through to the generic SyntaxError on a non-MAXLEN/MINID argv[2], check whether argv[2] is exactly 'LIMIT' and surface the upstream-documented message:
  'syntax error, LIMIT cannot be used without specifying a trimming strategy'
This mirrors upstream streamParseAddOrTrimArgsOrReply at t_stream.c lines 998-1000 which emits the same wording when args->limit is set but args->trim_strategy == TRIM_STRATEGY_NONE.

The targeted check is the minimum viable fix; restructuring the whole XTRIM parser into upstream's iterative form is out of scope.

Regression test in xtrim_validation_missing_and_wrongtype pins the new precedence: XTRIM stream LIMIT 5 returns the specific Error frame.

Verification:
- cargo test -p fr-command --lib xtrim → 2/2 pass.
- cargo clippy -p fr-command --lib --no-deps -- -D warnings → clean.

[LOW] severity. Wire-format conformance gap.

title: ZMPOP/BZMPOP scores must be RESP3 Double under HELLO 3, not bulk string
close_reason: Fixed: ZMPOP/BZMPOP popped-member scores now emit RESP3 Double via zpop_score_frame; RESP2 bulk string unchanged. Byte-exact vs vendored 7.2.4.

title: SENTINEL script reconfiguration should be denied by default
description: Upstream Redis 7.2.4 initializes SENTINEL_DEFAULT_DENY_SCRIPTS_RECONFIG to 1, so SENTINEL SET <master> notification-script/client-reconfig-script rejects by default unless the global config explicitly allows script reconfiguration. fr-sentinel currently initializes deny_scripts_reconfig=false, allowing script path changes by default. Acceptance: SentinelState::new defaults deny_scripts_reconfig to true and focused command coverage proves script-path SET is rejected by default while still succeeding when the flag is explicitly disabled.
close_reason: Changed deny_scripts_reconfig default to true, matching upstream SENTINEL-DENY-SCRIPTS-RECONFIG=yes

title: ZSCORE should bump LFU access frequency
description: Redis 7.2.4 zscoreCommand calls lookupKeyReadOrReply without LOOKUP_NOTOUCH. FrankenRedis Store::zscore needs LFU bumping.
close_reason: done

title: ACL selectors unsupported: SETUSER '(+get ~a:*)' returns Syntax error (Redis 7 feature)
description: fr-runtime apply_setuser_rules rejects Redis 7 ACL selector syntax '(...)' with 'ERR Error in ACL SETUSER modifier: Syntax error'. Oracle 7.2.4 stores selectors; ACL GETUSER returns a 'selectors' array of {commands,keys,channels} maps, and fr returns nil ($-1) for users that would have selectors. ACL LIST renders selectors as '(~app1:* resetchannels -@all +get)'. Larger feature: parse '(...)' groups, store per-selector command/key/channel rules, render in GETUSER/LIST, enforce on command dispatch. Found via ACL differential probe.
close_reason: Implemented Redis 7 ACL selectors: parse/merge (...) groups, store as permission-only sub-AclUsers, render byte-exact in ACL LIST/GETUSER, enforce additively via DispatchAclPermissions plumbing + deepest-error selection. Verified byte-exact vs redis 7.2.4. Commit 65ea6617a.

title: INFO memory: used_memory_overhead/dataset_perc/mem_clients_normal/slaves hardcoded to 0
description: INFO memory has parallel divergences to MEMORY STATS (zfu61, wkglo, cucvq) that were fixed there but not here: fr-command/src/lib.rs:12872 'used_memory_overhead:0' (vendored 868408), :12874 'used_memory_dataset:{used_memory}' (should subtract overhead), :12875 'used_memory_dataset_perc:100.00%' (vendored 98.56), :12968 'mem_clients_slaves:0' (vendored 0 but trivial mirror), :12969 'mem_clients_normal:0' (vendored 1984). Same Store fields already exist from prior beads (stat_clients_normal_mem_bytes, stat_clients_replica_mem_bytes, scripts_memory_bytes, functions_memory_bytes). Implementation: replicate the overhead_total/dataset_percentage computation from MEMORY STATS into the INFO memory section. used_memory_dataset = used_memory - overhead (clamped to >= 0). Acceptance: these 5 fields move from 0/100.00 to real values that match what MEMORY STATS already emits.
close_reason: INFO memory used_memory_overhead, mem_clients_normal, mem_clients_slaves now emit real values mirrored from MEMORY STATS' fixes (wkglo/zfu61). Implementation: computed used_memory_overhead inline as the sum of stat_clients_replica_mem_bytes + stat_clients_normal_mem_bytes + scripts_memory_bytes + functions_memory_bytes + per-db (keys + expires) * 64B/dict-entry, matching the wkglo formula. mem_clients_slaves/normal now read directly from store.stat_clients_replica_mem_bytes / stat_clients_normal_mem_bytes. Reverted the dataset/dataset_perc change after live probe revealed fr's  is dataset-only (it's hand-computed by estimate_memory_usage_bytes which counts data bytes, not heap allocations like upstream's zmalloc_used_memory). Subtracting overhead from a dataset-only used_memory wrongly clamped used_memory_dataset to 0. Documented this intentional divergence in-place: fr's used_memory_dataset == used_memory and dataset_perc == 100% by definition. Live differential probe after one SET k v: fr 'used_memory_overhead:496 mem_clients_normal:432' vs vendored 'used_memory_overhead:868408 mem_clients_normal:1984'. Both fields now move with traffic instead of staying at 0. The absolute gap vs vendored is dominated by vendored's ~866KB startup baseline that fr's startup_allocated is still 0 for (separate gap). Tests: fr-command 910 pass.

title: DEBUG subcommand arity envelope hardcoded uppercase token; upstream preserves input case
description: Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4: DEBUG <sub> wrong-arity routes through addReplySubcommandSyntaxError, which echoes argv[1] verbatim — preserving input case. Probe: DEBUG sleep -> ERR ... for 'sleep' ...; DEBUG SeT-AcTive-EXPIRE -> ERR ... for 'SeT-AcTive-EXPIRE' .... fr's debug_cmd hard-coded ~28 sites with uppercase literals (SLEEP, SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE, OBJECT, DIGEST, DIGEST-VALUE, POPULATE, CHANGE-REPL-ID, ERROR, LOG, LEAK, PAUSE-CRON, SET-DISABLE-DENY-SCRIPTS, AOF-FLUSH-SLEEP, SET-SKIP-CHECKSUM-VALIDATION, SDSLEN, STRUCTSIZE, LISTPACK, QUICKLIST, CONFIG-REWRITE-FORCE-ALL, DROP-CLUSTER-PACKET-FILTER, CLIENT-EVICTION, HTSTATS, HTSTATS-KEY, REPLICATE, REPLYBUFFER (x2), STRINGMATCH-LEN, STRINGMATCH-TEST, plus the CLUSTERLINK fallthrough using sub.to_ascii_uppercase()). For lowercase / mixed-case input the wire wording diverged from upstream byte-for-byte.
close_reason: Added a debug_subcommand_envelope_error(sub) helper at the top of debug_cmd that builds the canonical ERR string with sub formatted via {sub} (preserving input case). Replaced 28 hardcoded Custom() blocks across debug_cmd with Err(debug_subcommand_envelope_error(sub)). Patched the CLUSTERLINK fallthrough to format with {sub} instead of sub.to_ascii_uppercase(). Added a new fr-command unit test debug_subcommand_envelope_preserves_input_case covering 29 input-token shapes (lowercase, mixed-case, hyphen-separated, with/without trailing extras) — all assert the envelope echoes the exact input case. fr-command 841/4 pre-existing stream failures, 43/43 fr-runtime admin, 330/330 fr-store. Existing test pins (NOSUCH, JMAP, HTSTATS, CLUSTERLINK uppercase) still pass since uppercase input -> uppercase echo.

title: DEBUG DIGEST / DEBUG DIGEST-VALUE return 16-char hex instead of upstream's 40-char SHA1-frame digest, breaking output-length-aware tooling
description:
Upstream debug.c::computeDatasetDigest hashes each (key, dump) pair with SHA1 and XORs the 20-byte outputs into a cumulative digest, then hex-encodes the 20 bytes → 40 hex chars. fr was using Rust's std::hash::DefaultHasher (SipHash) which produces a u64 → 16 hex chars. Tooling that consumes the DEBUG DIGEST reply and pins the output length (redis-cli --csv probes, replication-digest comparison scripts, etc.) breaks on the format mismatch.

Repro vs vendored 7.2.4 (with --enable-debug-command local on vendored):
  > SET k v
  > DEBUG DIGEST
    16380> 264ccf65edec66bccfa7cc659414ccca432057d2  (40 chars)
    16381 (pre-fix)> 3561cc86a6c07bcb                 (16 chars)
  > DEBUG DIGEST-VALUE k
    16380> d79ee9fe5d5ad902d7f6620efc3b07b75ddd742f  (40 chars)
    16381 (pre-fix)> 3561cc86a6c07bcb                (16 chars)

Byte-exact reproduction of upstream's hash isn't possible from fr because fr's RDB dump encoding diverges in subtle places (LFU init values, log factor, hash factor fix, etc.). But matching the 20-byte / 40-char frame width is wire-compatible — the XOR reduction stays order-independent so the digest is still deterministic for a given dataset across restarts.

Fix: add sha2 to fr-command's dependencies (already a workspace dep used by fr-runtime), rewrite compute_debug_digest to Sha256-truncate-to-20-bytes-XOR, and hex-encode the 20-byte digest as 40 chars. Updated three existing test pins from 16-char to 40-char expected length.
acceptance_criteria: 1. DEBUG DIGEST returns a 40-character lowercase-hex string. 2. DEBUG DIGEST-VALUE k returns a 40-character per-key digest. 3. Multi-key DEBUG DIGEST-VALUE k1 k2 returns N entries each 40 chars. 4. DEBUG DIGEST on empty db still returns a 40-char string (all zeros). 5. Same dataset produces same digest across calls / restarts (deterministic + order-independent via XOR). 6. DEBUG DIGEST-VALUE with no keys still returns the empty array (no regression on frankenredis-dbgdigvempty fix).

title: DEBUG DIGEST-VALUE returns single BulkString instead of upstream Array of per-key digests
description: Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4: `DEBUG DIGEST-VALUE k1 k2` returns an array with one digest per requested key. fr returned a single BulkString combining all keys into one hash via compute_debug_digest with the full key list. This diverges from upstream debug.c::debugCommand which calls computeDatasetDigest per key and emits one array entry per key. Probe confirmed: vendored shows `1) <hex-of-k1>; 2) <hex-of-k2>` for two-key DIGEST-VALUE.
close_reason: DEBUG DIGEST-VALUE handler now iterates argv[2..] and computes a per-key digest via compute_debug_digest with a single-key slice, returning an Array of BulkString entries (one per input key). fr's internal hash is still 16-hex-char DefaultHasher (vs upstream 40-char SHA1) — that's an internal implementation detail; the wire shape is now correct. Updated the existing debug_digest_value_specific_keys pin to assert the array shape, plus a new multi-key sub-case. 839 fr-command (4 pre-existing stream failures), 330/330 fr-store.

title: DEBUG DIGEST-VALUE with no keys returns subcommand-syntax envelope instead of upstream's empty array
description: Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4: `DEBUG DIGEST-VALUE` (no keys) returns an empty array. Upstream debug.c::debugCommand:754 gates the branch on `!strcasecmp(argv[1]->ptr,"digest-value") && c->argc >= 2` and emits `addReplyArrayLen(c, c->argc - 2)`, so argc==2 produces an empty array. fr's debug_cmd had `if argv.len() < 3 { return Err(debug_subcommand_envelope_error(sub)); }`, which rejected argc==2 with `ERR unknown subcommand or wrong number of arguments for 'DIGEST-VALUE'. Try DEBUG HELP.`. The earlier bead frankenredis-dbgdigval fixed the per-key reply shape but didn't widen the arity gate.
acceptance_criteria: DEBUG DIGEST-VALUE with no keys (argc == 2) must return an empty array (matching `addReplyArrayLen(c, 0)`). Existing per-key digest paths stay unchanged.
close_reason: Removed the `argv.len() < 3` envelope-error guard from the DIGEST-VALUE arm so the existing argv[2..].iter()...collect() yields an empty Vec for argc==2. Updated the debug_subcommands_use_subcommand_syntax_envelope_for_arity_errors test to drop the now-invalid `DEBUG DIGEST-VALUE` no-keys row, and extended debug_digest_value_specific_keys with a positive assertion that `DEBUG DIGEST-VALUE` returns RespFrame::Array(Some(Vec::new())). All 4 debug_digest tests still green.

title: DEBUG subcommands return table-level wrong-arity instead of upstream subcommand-syntax envelope
description: Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4: DEBUG DIGEST extra, DEBUG DIGEST-VALUE (no key), DEBUG POPULATE (no count or too many args), DEBUG CHANGE-REPL-ID extra, DEBUG STRINGMATCH-LEN extra, DEBUG STRINGMATCH-TEST extra all surface 'ERR unknown subcommand or wrong number of arguments for \'<sub>\'. Try DEBUG HELP.' — the envelope wording from addReplySubcommandSyntaxError. fr's handlers returned WrongArity(\"DEBUG\") which renders as the table-level 'wrong number of arguments for debug command' wording. Six wire-wording divergences in the same family (DEBUG OBJECT and SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE were already correctly using the envelope).
close_reason: Replaced WrongArity('DEBUG') with the subcommand-syntax envelope Custom wording for all six DEBUG subcommands (DIGEST, DIGEST-VALUE, POPULATE, CHANGE-REPL-ID, STRINGMATCH-LEN, STRINGMATCH-TEST). Test pin debug_subcommands_use_subcommand_syntax_envelope_for_arity_errors covers all six subcommands. 839 fr-command (4 pre-existing stream failures), 330/330 fr-store.

title: DEBUG HELP with extra args returns table-level wrong-arity instead of upstream's subcommand envelope
description: Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4: `DEBUG HELP extra` returns `ERR unknown subcommand or wrong number of arguments for 'HELP'. Try DEBUG HELP.`. Upstream debug.c::debugCommand:391 gates the help branch on `c->argc == 2 && !strcasecmp(c->argv[1]->ptr,"help")`; when argc>2 the help branch is skipped, no other arm matches "help", and execution falls through to addReplySubcommandSyntaxError at debug.c:1046, which emits the envelope wording. fr's debug_cmd was returning `CommandError::WrongSubcommandArity { command: "DEBUG", subcommand: "HELP" }` (formatted as `ERR wrong number of arguments for 'debug|help' command`) — same wording the rest of the container HELPs use, but DEBUG is intentionally different.
acceptance_criteria: DEBUG HELP with argc != 2 must surface `ERR unknown subcommand or wrong number of arguments for 'HELP'. Try DEBUG HELP.` (the addReplySubcommandSyntaxError envelope), not the table-level 'wrong number of arguments for debug|help' wording.
close_reason: Replaced the WrongSubcommandArity error in the DEBUG HELP arity check with debug_subcommand_envelope_error(sub) — same helper the rest of debug_cmd already uses for fall-through-to-envelope cases. Pinned with debug_help_with_extra_args_uses_subcommand_envelope_error.

title: DEBUG JMAP returns OK silently — upstream rejects with subcommand-syntax-error envelope
description: Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4: DEBUG JMAP / DEBUG jmap return 'ERR unknown subcommand or wrong number of arguments for {SUB}. Try DEBUG HELP.' with the input-case token preserved verbatim. fr's debug_cmd hard-coded a JMAP arm that returned SimpleString 'OK', diverging from upstream debug.c::debugCommand which has no JMAP subcommand and falls through to addReplySubcommandSyntaxError. Conformance fixtures (core_debug.json, core_connection.json, core_server.json) pinned the wrong OK behavior. The DEBUG fallthrough envelope also uppercased the subcommand token via sub.to_ascii_uppercase(), losing the case that upstream preserves.
close_reason: Removed the JMAP arm from debug_cmd in fr-command/src/lib.rs so JMAP falls through to the unknown-subcommand fallthrough. Updated the fallthrough to format with sub directly (no .to_ascii_uppercase()) so input case is preserved per upstream addReplySubcommandSyntaxError. Added fr-command unit-test pin debug_jmap_falls_through_to_unknown_subcommand_envelope covering JMAP, jmap, and JMAP+extra. Rewrote fr-runtime debug_jmap_returns_ok to debug_jmap_rejects_with_subcommand_envelope and tightened debug_jmap_rejects_extra_arguments to assert the exact envelope wording. Updated 5 conformance fixtures (core_debug.json: debug_jmap_ok, debug_jmap_wrong_arity, debug_jmap_case_insensitive, debug_jmap_extra_args; core_connection.json: debug_jmap; core_server.json: debug_jmap) to match upstream. fr-command 841/4 (4 pre-existing stream failures), 330/330 fr-store, 43/43 fr-runtime admin. Conformance failure count unchanged (pre-existing divergences in debug_sleep_wrong_arity / debug_reload_wrong_arity / debug_digest_value_* shape).

title: DEBUG POPULATE silently accepts negative count and unparseable arg; upstream surfaces 'value is out of range, must be positive'
description: Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4: `DEBUG POPULATE -1`, `DEBUG POPULATE abc`, `DEBUG POPULATE 5 pre: -1`, and `DEBUG POPULATE 5 pre: abc` all return `ERR value is out of range, must be positive`. Upstream debug.c::debugCommand:707-723 parses both <count> and <size> via `getPositiveLongFromObjectOrReply(c, ..., NULL)` which uses the canonical 'value is out of range, must be positive' wording for BOTH unparseable input and non-positive values. fr's POPULATE branch was using parse_i64_arg, so negative counts iterated 0 times and silently OK'd, and bad-parse cases surfaced 'value is not an integer or out of range'.
acceptance_criteria: DEBUG POPULATE must reject any <count> or <size> that's unparseable or <0 with `ERR value is out of range, must be positive`. count == 0 still succeeds (no iteration).
close_reason: Added a positive_arg helper inside fr-command::debug_cmd's POPULATE arm that parses via parse_i64_arg, remaps parse failures to the canonical 'value is out of range, must be positive' wording, and rejects negatives with the same message. Applied to both <count> (argv[2]) and <size> (argv[4]). Pinned with debug_populate_rejects_non_positive_count_and_size_with_upstream_wording covering -1, -100, abc on count and -1, abc on size, plus a count=0 success sanity check. All 43 fr-command debug tests still green.

title: DEBUG QUICKLIST-PACKED-THRESHOLD: arity wording, range bounds, and memtoull suffix semantics all diverged
description: Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 surfaced three divergences in the QUICKLIST-PACKED-THRESHOLD handler: (1) wrong-arity used the table-level 'wrong number of arguments for debug command' reply instead of addReplySubcommandSyntaxError; (2) the range check rejected sz=0 (upstream means 'restore default', accepts) and sz=1 (upstream sets to 1 byte, accepts), and accepted [4GB-1MB, 4GB) which upstream's quicklistisSetPackedThreshold rejects with the same dedicated wording; (3) the memtoull-style suffix parser used 1024-based multipliers for single-letter k/m/g, but upstream memtoull uses DECIMAL multipliers for single-letter suffixes (k=1000, m=1_000_000, g=1_000_000_000) and BINARY only for two-letter forms (kb/mb/gb). Standalone `b` = 1 byte. Empty digit run = 0. Probe captures: 4g (=4_000_000_000) is OK, 4gb (=4*1024^3=4GB) is rejected, 1k=1000, 1kb=1024.
close_reason: Rewrote the QUICKLIST-PACKED-THRESHOLD handler in fr-command/src/lib.rs to (a) use debug_subcommand_envelope_error(sub) for the arity error, (b) split the suffix into separate decimal (k/m/g) and binary (kb/mb/gb) tables matching upstream util.c memtoull, (c) accept any value <= (1<<32) - (1<<20) (= 4GB-1MB) including 0 and 1, (d) reject leading '-' explicitly. Replaced the existing 2 test pins with 3 new ones: debug_quicklist_packed_threshold_accepts_memtoull_values (14 valid inputs including empty / b / kb / 4g / boundary), debug_quicklist_packed_threshold_rejects_invalid_values (8 invalid inputs including 4gb / 5g / 4294967295 / 1KIB / -1), and debug_quicklist_packed_threshold_arity_uses_subcommand_envelope (uppercase + lowercase input). fr-command 844/4 pre-existing stream failures.

title: fr-command: DEBUG SLEEP wrong-arity returns generic WrongArity instead of upstream's subcommand-syntax-error wording
description: Upstream debug.c::debugCommand emits 'ERR unknown subcommand or wrong number of arguments for SLEEP. Try DEBUG HELP.' for any DEBUG SLEEP call with !=1 trailing arg (no-arg or extra args), via addReplySubcommandSyntaxError. fr-command at lib.rs:17402-17404 returns CommandError::WrongArity('DEBUG') instead, which renders as 'wrong number of arguments for debug command'. The sibling DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE handler at lib.rs:17414 already uses the correct dedicated wording — DEBUG SLEEP just hadn't been migrated. Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 (--enable-debug-command local) confirms.

title: DEBUG STRINGMATCH-LEN returns OK silently — upstream rejects (subcommand does not exist)
description: Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4: DEBUG STRINGMATCH-LEN is rejected with the subcommand-syntax-error envelope (input case preserved). Upstream debug.c registers only STRINGMATCH-TEST (line 954); STRINGMATCH-LEN does not exist. fr's debug_cmd had a fabricated arm that returned SimpleString OK on STRINGMATCH-LEN — same JMAP-style fabrication pattern (frankenredis-dbgjmap).
close_reason: Removed the STRINGMATCH-LEN arm from debug_cmd in fr-command/src/lib.rs so the subcommand falls through to the unknown-subcommand envelope (now case-preserving via the dbgcase fix). Updated the debug_subcommands_use_subcommand_syntax_envelope_for_arity_errors test to drop STRINGMATCH-LEN (it now returns Ok(Error) via fallthrough rather than Err(Custom)). Added a new fr-command unit test debug_stringmatch_len_falls_through_to_unknown_subcommand pinning both uppercase and lowercase input to the case-preserved envelope wording. fr-command 843/4 pre-existing stream failures.

title: Make CLUSTER epoch admin commands stateful
description: Discovery via /mock-code-finder: fr-command marks CLUSTER BUMPEPOCH / SET-CONFIG-EPOCH as no-op stubs. BUMPEPOCH always returns BUMPED 1 and SET-CONFIG-EPOCH accepts any non-negative epoch without recording node/current epoch. Redis 7.2.4 tracks currentEpoch and myself->configEpoch: repeated BUMPEPOCH returns STILL <epoch>, SET-CONFIG-EPOCH sets the fresh node epoch once, and later SET-CONFIG-EPOCH returns ERR Node config epoch is already non-zero. Fix scope: add minimal single-node cluster epoch state to Store, wire CLUSTER INFO/NODES/BUMPEPOCH/SET-CONFIG-EPOCH/RESET HARD to that state, and pin unit tests against vendored Redis semantics.
close_reason: Added minimal Store cluster epoch state and wired CLUSTER BUMPEPOCH / SET-CONFIG-EPOCH / INFO / NODES / RESET HARD to Redis 7.2-style current/my epoch semantics. Focused cluster tests pass; check/clippy pass via rch.

title: [parity-gap] XGROUP keyevent notifications always use xgroup-create
description: mock-code-finder found a runtime placeholder in crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs: keyevent_name_for_command maps every XGROUP subcommand to xgroup-create. Upstream Redis emits subcommand-specific stream keyevent names: xgroup-create, xgroup-setid, xgroup-destroy, xgroup-createconsumer, and xgroup-delconsumer. Repro: enable stream keyevent notifications, run XGROUP CREATE/SETID/DESTROY/CREATECONSUMER/DELCONSUMER, and observe FrankenRedis publishes xgroup-create for all of them. Fix in same tick: derive the XGROUP event name from argv[1] and add focused fr-runtime tests.
close_reason: Completed: XGROUP keyspace notifications now use subcommand-specific Redis event names and NOTIFY_STREAM classification; focused fr-runtime regression test added.

title: fr-runtime pubsub-mode command gate ignores RESP version; RESP3 subscribers cannot run non-pubsub commands despite upstream allowing them
description:
Differential probe vs upstream Redis 7.2.4 server.c::processCommand:4067-4082:

  if ((c->flags & CLIENT_PUBSUB && c->resp == 2) &&
      c->cmd->proc != pingCommand &&
      ... allowed list ...
  {
      rejectCommandFormat(c, 'Can't execute ...');
  }

The RESP2 qualifier means RESP3 subscribers can run ANY command (including PUBSUB CHANNELS, GET, PUBLISH, etc.) — upstream changed this in 7.0+ when RESP3 was introduced.

fr-runtime's gate at crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:4613-4619 lacks the RESP-version check:

  if command_arity_ok
      && self.is_in_subscription_mode()
      && !Self::pubsub_command_allowed_in_subscription_mode(special_command, &argv)
  {
      return Self::pubsub_context_error(...);
  }

This rejects non-pubsub commands in subscription mode regardless of RESP version. Concrete repro:
  > HELLO 3
  > SUBSCRIBE alpha
  > PUBSUB CHANNELS
  fr returns:  (error) ERR Can't execute 'pubsub|channels': only (P|S)SUBSCRIBE / (P|S)UNSUBSCRIBE / PING / QUIT / RESET are allowed in this context
  upstream:    1) 'alpha'

Same applies to GET, PUBLISH, etc. RESP3 subscribers cannot use any non-allowed-list command despite upstream supporting it.

There's also a secondary inconsistency: pubsub_command_allowed_in_subscription_mode at lib.rs:3893-3911 INCLUDES RuntimeSpecialCommand::Pubsub in its allow set, so PUBSUB-the-command would be allowed through the gate. The reason PUBSUB IS rejected today is a workaround inside handle_pubsub_command at lib.rs:10082-10084 that re-checks subscription mode. With the RESP-version fix, this duplication can be cleaned up: drop Pubsub from the gate's allowed set so the gate is the sole source of truth.

Fix:
1. Add RESP version check to the gate: skip the rejection when self.session.resp_protocol_version != 2.
2. Drop RuntimeSpecialCommand::Pubsub from pubsub_command_allowed_in_subscription_mode (it's not in upstream's allow list).
3. Remove the redundant subscription-mode check in handle_pubsub_command (lib.rs:10082-10084) — the gate handles it now, and keeping it would block PUBSUB in RESP3 too.

Acceptance: existing pubsub-rejection tests stay green (RESP2 default). New test covers (a) RESP3 + SUBSCRIBE + PUBSUB CHANNELS → ok; (b) RESP3 + SUBSCRIBE + GET → ok; (c) RESP2 + SUBSCRIBE + PUBSUB CHANNELS → still rejected.
close_reason: done

title: Reject CONFIG SET unixsocketperm as immutable
description: Redis marks unixsocketperm as IMMUTABLE_CONFIG in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/config.c. FrankenRedis exposes unixsocketperm as a static CONFIG parameter and currently accepts CONFIG SET through the static fallback. Add runtime and core_config conformance coverage that rejects mutation while preserving CONFIG GET visibility.
close_reason: Completed

title: Implement RDB snapshot streaming to new replicas on FULLRESYNC
description:
# RDB Snapshot Streaming

## What  
When a new replica connects and requests FULLRESYNC, the primary must encode its entire dataset as an RDB snapshot and stream it to the replica.

## Current State
PSYNC handler returns FULLRESYNC response. encoded_rdb_snapshot() exists and works. The gap is actually sending the snapshot bytes over the replica TCP connection.

## Implementation
1. After sending "+FULLRESYNC replid offset" to replica, generate RDB snapshot via encoded_rdb_snapshot()
2. Send "$<len>\r\n<rdb_bytes>" (bulk string protocol for snapshot delivery, matching Redis wire format)
3. After snapshot, mark replica state as ONLINE and begin streaming live commands from the backlog
4. Track replica state transitions: WAITING_BGSAVE → SEND_BULK → ONLINE
5. Handle errors: if write fails during snapshot send, disconnect replica and schedule retry

## Unit Tests (in fr-runtime)
- Test 1: encoded_rdb_snapshot() produces valid RDB (decode it back and verify entry count)
- Test 2: Snapshot includes all data types (string, list, set, hash, zset, stream with consumer groups)
- Test 3: Snapshot respects database selection (multi-db snapshot)

## Integration Tests (in fr-server)
- Test 4: New replica connects, receives FULLRESYNC, applies snapshot, has all keys from primary
- Test 5: Large dataset (10k keys) — snapshot delivery completes without timeout
- Test 6: Replica disconnects mid-snapshot — primary cleans up gracefully

## Logging
- Log snapshot generation: "REPL: generating RDB snapshot (N keys, M bytes)"
- Log send progress for large snapshots: "REPL: sent X/Y bytes to replica"
- Log completion: "REPL: snapshot delivered, replica entering ONLINE state"

## Depends On
- REPLICAOF TCP client (1v8b) — need connection before sending snapshot

## Files
- crates/fr-server/src/main.rs (snapshot send after FULLRESYNC)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (replica state tracking)
close_reason: Already implemented: encode_replication_snapshot() generates RDB bulk, PSYNC handler streams it to replicas at main.rs:1637-1640, propagate_writes_to_replicas() handles ongoing streaming. Multiple E2E tests exercise this path.

title: DEBUG OBJECT and INFO server emit wrong lru clock values (idle_secs and seconds/10 instead of upstream getLRUClock())
description:
Two intertwined LRU-clock divergences vs vendored Redis 7.2.4.

Upstream:
  evict.c::getLRUClock()  ->  (mstime() / LRU_CLOCK_RESOLUTION) & LRU_CLOCK_MAX
    LRU_CLOCK_RESOLUTION = 1000ms,  LRU_CLOCK_MAX = (1<<24)-1
  debug.c::debugCommand DEBUG OBJECT emits:
    "... lru:%d lru_seconds_idle:%llu ..."
      %d  is o->lru     (the per-object LRU clock at access time)
      %llu is estimateObjectIdleTime(o)/1000 (idle seconds)
  server.c::genRedisInfoString INFO server emits:
    "lru_clock:%u\r\n" with the same getLRUClock() value

Probe vs vendored 7.2.4:
  127.0.0.1:16380> SET k v
  127.0.0.1:16380> DEBUG OBJECT k
    Value at:0x73491ac11280 refcount:1 encoding:embstr serializedlength:6 lru:16645189 lru_seconds_idle:0
  127.0.0.1:16380> INFO server | grep lru_clock
    lru_clock:16645248
  Both fields are large (~16.6M) because the LRU clock is
  mstime/1000 modulo 2^24.

fr (pre-fix):
  crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:18278  emitted `lru:{idle_secs}
    lru_seconds_idle:{idle_secs}` — same value in both slots,
    a tiny number measured in seconds rather than the
    24-bit clock. Clients that parse the lru clock for
    LRU-aware eviction telemetry would see absurdly small
    values.
  crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:12953  computed
    `(now_ms / 10_000) & 0xFFFFFF` — used 10-second
    resolution instead of upstream's 1-second, so INFO
    server's lru_clock value was 10x smaller than vendored
    AND inconsistent with the per-object DEBUG OBJECT lru
    field even after fixing the latter.

Fix:
  - Added Store::object_lru_clock(key) -> Option<u32> that
    returns ((entry.last_access_ms / 1000) & 0xFFFFFF)
    matching upstream's getLRUClock() formula on
    last_access_ms.
  - DEBUG OBJECT now emits `lru:<lru_clock>` and
    `lru_seconds_idle:<idle_secs>` as distinct fields.
  - INFO server's lru_clock now uses /1_000.

Regression tests:
  debug_object_emits_lru_clock_and_idle_seconds_as_separate_fields
  info_server_lru_clock_uses_one_second_resolution_per_upstream_evict
  pin the new emission with synthetic now_ms timestamps so the
  test stays deterministic across CI runs.

title: function a.b.c() decl bypasses sandbox _G read; emits 'attempt to index a nil value' instead of 'nonexistent global'
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:

  function a.b.c() return 1 end
    vendored: Script attempted to access nonexistent global variable 'a'
    fr:       attempt to index a nil value

When `function NAME.X.Y()` is a multi-segment declaration, the first segment is supposed to be looked up via the sandboxed `_G` __index metamethod (which raises "nonexistent global" for missing keys). fr's set_nested_field (lua_eval.rs ~line 3004) reads self.globals.get(root_name) directly and emits "attempt to index a nil value" when the result is Nil — bypassing the sandbox layer.

Fix at line ~3014: when root_name is missing from self.globals AND self.globals_locked, emit the "Script attempted to access nonexistent global variable 'NAME'" error instead of falling through to the "attempt to index a nil value" wording.

This is the same sandbox error already emitted from Expr::Name evaluation at line 3245 — share the wording.
close_reason: Updated set_nested_field at lua_eval.rs ~line 3014 to route the root-name lookup through the sandbox layer: when globals_locked is set and root_name is missing, emit 'Script attempted to access nonexistent global variable NAME' (same wording as Expr::Name evaluation). Updated the pre-existing function_decl_errors_on_missing_table_path test to pin the new correct wording. Full release test suite (1013 tests) still passes. Live-diff vs vendored 16380: 3/3 cases match byte-for-byte.

title: fr-command standalone WAIT/WAITAOF dispatch still returns placeholder ack counts
description:
Mock-code-finder scan found the direct fr-command dispatch path still documents WAITAOF as a standalone stub and returns placeholder [0,0] counts, while WAIT returns standalone zero-replica behavior. Runtime has richer replication_ack_state/local fsync tracking and intercepts server traffic, but direct dispatch_argv callers and command-crate tests can still observe fake ack semantics. This conflicts with the README full drop-in parity contract and Phase2C WAIT/WAITAOF fixtures documenting threshold semantics.

Evidence:
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:7813-7816: WAITAOF comment says standalone stub and returns [0,0].
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:16480: WAIT comment says standalone mode returns 0 replicas.
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:11691-11802 implements WAIT/WAITAOF via replication_ack_state and evaluate_wait/evaluate_waitaof.

Acceptance:
- Decide and implement the command-crate boundary so direct fr-command dispatch cannot silently present fake WAIT/WAITAOF success semantics.
- Prefer reusing the runtime evaluator state model or an explicit fail-closed/runtime-only path over placeholder counts.
- Add focused fr-command/fr-runtime tests showing the direct dispatch behavior is no longer a fake successful ack path.
- Validate with rch cargo check/clippy and targeted WAIT/WAITAOF tests.
close_reason: Made direct fr-command WAIT/WAITAOF fail closed without runtime ack context while preserving runtime-owned real ack evaluation; landed in current main.

description: Profile-backed pass 61 target from current main e782ad8cd. Fresh GET P16/300k hyperfine: 400.0 ms +/- 9.3 ms. GET P16/1M profile run: 814052.88 ops/sec, p50 297us, p95 396us, p99 606us, p999 1004us, 714 samples, 0 lost. Top flat samples: <fr_store::Value>::string_owned 8.70%, __memmove_avx_unaligned_erms 6.02%, frankenredis::process_buffered_frames 3.48%, Runtime::refresh_store_runtime_info_context 2.23%, parse_command_args_borrowed_into only 0.98%. title: CONFIG SET active-expire-effort accepts values outside 1-10 — vendored 7.2.4 rejects with bound message
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 16380:
  CONFIG SET active-expire-effort 0
    vendored: ERR CONFIG SET failed (possibly related to argument 'active-expire-effort') - argument must be between 1 and 10 inclusive
    fr      : OK
  CONFIG SET active-expire-effort 11
    vendored: same error
    fr      : OK
  CONFIG GET active-expire-effort  (after fr SET 11):
    vendored: 1
    fr      : 11

Upstream config.c declares the active-expire-effort field with min=1, max=10 (numericConfigGenericInit's inclusive bounds enforced by the generic config setter). fr is missing the bound check, so any int passes through.

Fix: gate the SET in the active-expire-effort handler so values <1 or >10 surface the upstream bounded-error wording: 'CONFIG SET failed (possibly related to argument \'active-expire-effort\') - argument must be between 1 and 10 inclusive'.

Find via grep -n 'active-expire-effort' crates/fr-config/src and crates/fr-runtime/src.
close_reason: Added INTEGER_CONFIG range validation [1, 10] for active-expire-effort in fr-runtime's handle_config_set, mirroring upstream config.c. Out-of-range values surface 'argument must be between 1 and 10 inclusive'; unparseable values surface 'argument couldn't be parsed into an integer' — both routed through the existing config_set_failed helper. Pinned via config_set_active_expire_effort_enforces_1_to_10_inclusive_per_upstream which exercises 0/-1/11/100 (rejected with bound msg), 'abc' (rejected with parse msg), and 1/5/10 (accepted). Live differential probe vs vendored 16380 confirms identical responses for SET 0, SET 11, SET 5, GET active-expire-effort. Workspace clippy clean under -D warnings.

title: fr-command: MEMORY subcommand wrong-arity wording uses 'syntax error' instead of subcommand-specific
description: MEMORY DOCTOR/MALLOC-STATS/PURGE/STATS/HELP with extra trailing args emit 'ERR syntax error' but upstream emits 'ERR wrong number of arguments for memory|<sub> command'. Also MEMORY USAGE k SAMPLES 0 is rejected as syntax error but upstream accepts 0 (means use default sampling); only negative SAMPLES values should fail. Caught by ad-hoc differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored redis 7.2.4 (port 16701).

title: GEOSEARCH rejects duplicate FROMMEMBER/BYRADIUS/etc; vendored silently overrides
description:
fr's GEOSEARCH (and GEOSEARCHSTORE) returns 'ERR syntax error' when the same FROM/BY clause appears twice (e.g. FROMMEMBER sf ... FROMMEMBER nyc, or BYRADIUS 1000 km ... BYRADIUS 500 mi). Vendored Redis 7.2.4 silently accepts these and the LATER value wins.

Root cause: upstream geo.c::geoSearchGeneric guards FROMMEMBER with `!fromloc` (only rejects if FROMLONLAT was set) and FROMLONLAT with `!frommember`. Same pattern for BYRADIUS/BYBOX — each is guarded against the OTHER. There's no guard against re-using the SAME clause; the parser simply overwrites the stored coordinates / shape.

fr-command/src/lib.rs:5331+ uses a single `has_center` boolean for both FROMMEMBER and FROMLONLAT, and `has_shape` for both BYRADIUS and BYBOX. Setting either triggers the boolean, so a duplicate FROMMEMBER tries to set it again and hits the SyntaxError branch.

Probe sweep #67 (probe135.sh):
  vendored GEOSEARCH geo FROMMEMBER sf BYRADIUS 5000 km FROMMEMBER nyc -> sf  (success, second FROMMEMBER overrides)
  fr        GEOSEARCH geo FROMMEMBER sf BYRADIUS 5000 km FROMMEMBER nyc -> ERR syntax error
  vendored GEOSEARCH geo FROMMEMBER sf BYRADIUS 5000 km BYRADIUS 1000 mi -> sf  (success)
  fr        GEOSEARCH geo FROMMEMBER sf BYRADIUS 5000 km BYRADIUS 1000 mi -> ERR syntax error
  Both still reject mixed pairs:
    GEOSEARCH geo FROMMEMBER sf BYRADIUS 5000 km BYBOX 1 1 km -> ERR syntax error  (correct)
    GEOSEARCH geo FROMMEMBER sf BYRADIUS 5000 km FROMLONLAT 1 1 -> not tested but expected to error

Fix plan:
- Split has_center into has_frommember/has_fromlonlat. FROMMEMBER rejects only when has_fromlonlat is true; FROMLONLAT rejects only when has_frommember is true.
- Same split for has_shape: has_byradius/has_bybox.
- Allow duplicate clauses of the same kind — the parser simply overwrites center_lon/center_lat/radius_m/box_w_m,box_h_m.
- Add regression test pinning duplicate-of-same / mix-different behaviors against upstream wording.
close_reason: Fixed in iteration 100: split GEOSEARCH/GEOSEARCHSTORE's single has_center/has_shape booleans into per-kind flags (has_frommember/has_fromlonlat, has_byradius/has_bybox) so that duplicate clauses of the same kind silently overwrite (matching upstream geo.c::geoSearchGeneric's per-kind guards), while mixed FROMMEMBER+FROMLONLAT and BYRADIUS+BYBOX still error. Live-diff: 4 dup-of-same cases now succeed (GEOSEARCH dup FROMMEMBER, dup BYRADIUS, GEOSEARCHSTORE dup FROMMEMBER); 2 mixed-pair cases still emit syntax error matching vendored. Regression test geosearch_duplicate_same_clause_silently_overrides_dnguy pins dup-FROMLONLAT, dup-BYRADIUS, FROMLONLAT+FROMMEMBER mix, BYRADIUS+BYBOX mix. Full 1024-test suite passes.

title: Add fuzz target for CLIENT REPLY state transitions
close_reason: Completed

title: DEBUG SDSLEN: report jemalloc-rounded zmalloc sizes (parity with vendored)
description:
DEBUG SDSLEN's key_zmalloc / val_zmalloc fields on fr always equal the raw byte length, while vendored Redis 7.2.4 reports the jemalloc-rounded allocation size (sds header + content + null terminator, rounded to the next jemalloc small-class).

Examples (vendored vs fr) — fr always reports the raw length:
  SET s1 abc        → key:s1 (2 bytes), val:abc (3 bytes)
  vendored: key_zmalloc:8,  val_zmalloc:8
  fr:       key_zmalloc:2,  val_zmalloc:3

Per-length sweep (val side, SDS_TYPE_8 embstr header = 3 bytes, +1 null = +4):
  len 1-4   -> 8   (total 5-8 rounds to 8)
  len 5-12  -> 16  (total 9-16 rounds to 16)
  len 13-20 -> 24  (total 17-24 rounds to 24)
  len 21-28 -> 32
  ...
  len 60-76 -> 80  (jemalloc switches to 16-byte stride in [64, 128))
  len 124-156 -> 160 (32-byte stride in [128, 256))
  len 252-316 -> 320 (64-byte stride in [256, 512))

Algorithm:
1. Header size based on SDS type vendored picks for that slot:
   - Keys (sdsReqType-driven): 1 (<32), 3 (<256), 5 (<65536), 9 (<2^32), 17 else.
   - Values (embstr/raw both default to SDS_TYPE_8 for short strings via createEmbeddedStringObject): 3 (<256), 5 (<65536), 9 (<2^32), 17 else.
2. Total = header + len + 1
3. Round up to jemalloc small-class:
   - lower = largest power of 2 <= total
   - spacing = max(8, lower / 4)  (i.e. quartile spacing within each power-of-2 range, floor 8)
   - rounded = ceil(total / spacing) * spacing

Confirmed against vendored on all 26 tested lengths (1..256).

Implementation site: fr-command/src/lib.rs:21260 (the format! that currently emits `key_zmalloc: {key_len}, val_zmalloc: {val_len}`).

Fix plan:
- Add `jemalloc_small_size_class(n)` helper.
- Add `sds_key_header(len)` and `sds_value_header(len)` helpers.
- Replace `key_len` / `val_len` in the format string with `jemalloc_small_size_class(sds_*_header(len) + len + 1)`.
- Regression test pins multiple representative sizes.

Probe sweep #74 (SDSLEN sweep, inline).
close_reason: completed

title: Lua string.dump not exposed in fr sandbox
description: Vendored Redis 7.2.4 exposes string.dump (Lua 5.1 stdlib function that serializes a function to bytecode). type(string.dump) returns function in vendored but nil in fr. Fix: register dump in the string table at fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:1894 and add a dispatch handler that errors when called (since fr's interpreter has no native bytecode form to dump). Acceptance: type(string.dump) returns function byte-exact with vendored.
close_reason: string.dump now exists as a callable function in fr's Lua sandbox. Added 'dump' to the string-library globals registration so type(string.dump) returns 'function' (was 'nil' before). Added a dispatch handler at string.reverse's neighborhood that errors with 'user_script:1: unable to dump given function' since fr's tree-walking interpreter has no native bytecode form to serialise. Live differential probe: type(string.dump) returns 'function' byte-exact; string.reverse regression-clean. Tests: fr-command 910 pass.

title: SORT numeric BY <int-key> does int->string->f64 round-trip per element (~2x)
description: Profile of SORT mylist BY w_* GET d_* LIMIT 0 10 (50k list, w_* int-encoded weights): ~50% CPU in i64::to_string (SpecToString 32.8% + u64::_fmt 13% + usize::_fmt 5.9%). SORT's numeric path fetches each BY weight via store.get -> get_string_bytes -> value.string_bytes() which formats Integer-encoded values to a string, then the sort re-parses string->f64. Redis reads int-encoded objects as (double)(long) directly (getDoubleFromObject). Fix: add Store::get_sort_weight returning Integer values as f64 directly (replicating get_string_bytes side-effects: record_keyspace_lookup/LFU/touch), and use it in the numeric plain-keyed BY path (by_pattern has * and no ->). String/hash-field/no-BY keep the parse path. Byte-identical (n as f64 == format+parse for integers, == redis (double)(long)). Measured SORT BY w_* LIMIT 0 10: oracle 7632us vs fr 15379us (1.97x).
close_reason: Fixed: SORT numeric BY <plain int-key> reads Integer-encoded weights as f64 directly (Store::get_sort_weight, no int->string->f64) and reuses one key buffer (no per-element alloc). SORT mylist BY w_* GET d_* LIMIT 0 10 (50k): 14069us->7458us (1.89x), parity with redis 7167us. Byte-exact: 11 curated + 2880 sort_differ combos byte-exact; fr-command lib 1143/0. golden 30be6618.

Current HEAD parser harness baseline: FIELDS=16 VALUE_SIZE=64 ITERATIONS=200000 mean 435.7 ms +/- 14.5 ms. perf record on ITERATIONS=2000000 captured 18,211 samples; top user symbols: fr_protocol::parse_bulk 25.56%, cfree 17.23%, fr_protocol::parse_array 16.56%, drop_glue<RespFrame> 11.21%, RawVec allocation 7.32%, malloc 5.21%. title: Replication: SETEX/PSETEX/SET EX|PX|EXAT|PXAT/GETEX not rewritten to absolute for propagation -> replica TTL drift
description: Follow-up to mdrk8 (EXPIRE-family fix done). The SET-with-expiry family propagates verbatim, so a replica recomputes the relative deadline at its later receive time and its PEXPIRETIME drifts above the master's by the propagation delay (verified ~12ms for SETEX via local master/replica PEXPIRETIME delta). Upstream rewrites these on propagation: SETEX/PSETEX/SET EX|PX -> SET key val PXAT <abs>; GETEX EX|PX|EXAT|PXAT -> PEXPIREAT/PERSIST/DEL. fr should rewrite them in the dispatch propagation path like the EXPIRE family (see Runtime::rewrite_relative_expire_for_propagation). More involved than EXPIRE because SET carries the value + other options (NX/XX/GET/KEEPTTL) and GETEX has persist/del branches.
close_reason: FIXED + shipped (commit b22588820). rewrite_relative_expire_for_propagation now also handles the SET-with-expire family: SETEX/PSETEX and SET key val EX|PX|EXAT -> SET key val PXAT <abs> (other options NX/XX/GET dropped, per upstream setGenericCommand); GETEX future -> PEXPIREAT, past EXAT/PXAT -> DEL, PERSIST -> PERSIST (getexCommand); absolute PXAT/KEEPTTL/no-expire SET stay verbatim. Verified with a local master/replica: PEXPIRETIME delta 0 across SETEX/PSETEX/SET EX|PX|EXAT/SET NX EX GET/SET PXAT; GETEX EX delta 0, PERSIST clears TTL on both, past EXAT deletes on both. 399 fr-runtime tests pass; unit test extended. Together with mdrk8 this fully eliminates replica TTL drift for all relative-time commands.

title: COMMAND INFO GEORADIUS/GEORADIUSBYMEMBER missing STORE+STOREDIST keyspecs
description: Upstream commands.def declares GEORADIUS and GEORADIUSBYMEMBER each with 3 keyspecs: (1) input geo key @1 with CMD_KEY_RO|CMD_KEY_ACCESS, (2) keyword 'STORE' startfrom 6 (or 5 for GEORADIUSBYMEMBER) with CMD_KEY_OW|CMD_KEY_UPDATE, (3) keyword 'STOREDIST' startfrom 6/5 with CMD_KEY_OW|CMD_KEY_UPDATE. fr currently emits a single keyspec with flags 'RW' from the heuristic. Fix: add georadius/georadiusbymember to command_info_multi_keyspec (fr-command/src/lib.rs:15081) using make_keyspec_index_range + 2x make_keyspec_keyword_range entries. Startfrom is 6 for georadius (5 leading positional + 1 for the source key), 5 for georadiusbymember (4 + 1).

Added two entries to command_info_multi_keyspec at fr-command/src/lib.rs:15161: each command gets make_keyspec_index_range(['RO','access'],1,0,1) for the source key plus two make_keyspec_keyword_range entries for the STORE and STOREDIST optional destinations (CMD_KEY_OW|CMD_KEY_UPDATE, startfrom=6 for georadius, startfrom=5 for georadiusbymember -- the byte offsets where the keyword scan should begin per upstream's .bs.keyword struct). Measured DEBUG RELOAD (2000 sets x 120 string members): fr 2.62x SLOWER than redis 7.2.4. title: ACL LIST omits implicit '-@all' baseline for users without +@all — vendored emits 'resetchannels -@all +get' but fr emits 'resetchannels +get'
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:
ACL SETUSER bob on nopass +get
ven: user bob on nopass sanitize-payload resetchannels -@all +get
fr : user bob on nopass sanitize-payload resetchannels +get

Same pattern for any user without +@all (e.g. +@read alone, +@read +@write, +get -set). Per acl.c::ACLDescribeUserCommandRules, upstream describes the deny-all baseline by emitting '-@all' before the explicit grants whenever the user does not have allcommands set. fr-runtime's ACL LIST emitter drops the '-@all' when there are no explicit negations.

Fix: mirror upstream's 'if not allcommands then prepend -@all' rule in the ACL LIST canonicalizer. Acceptance: ACL SETUSER probe round-trip byte-equal vendored across +get / +@read / +get -set fixtures.
close_reason: Mirrored upstream acl.c::ACLDescribeUserCommandRules: ACL LIST opens with the deny-all baseline (-@all) whenever the user does not have allcommands set, even when explicit positive grants follow. fr previously emitted -@all only when zero explicit grants existed, so '+@read' round-tripped as the wrong (broader) permission. Verified live_redis_core_acl + 59 fr-runtime ACL tests pass.

title: SORT BY/GET hash-deref '->' located in substituted key instead of pattern-after-star (diverges from upstream lookupKeyByPattern)

A '->' inside an element value or before the '*' is now a plain STRING key, not a hash deref. Byte-exact vs redis 7.2.4 across 10 arrow edge cases + random_command_differ clean; SortPattern plan also hoists the '*'/'->' split + key-buffer alloc out of the per-element loop. fr-command sort suite 24/24. Commit message has the differential repro.

title: Add persistence restart integration test (write → stop → restart → verify)
description:
# Persistence Restart Test

## What
An integration test that writes data to FrankenRedis with persistence enabled, gracefully stops the server, restarts it, and verifies all data survived. Tests BOTH AOF and RDB paths.

## Why
AOF round-trip is tested at the unit level (8 tests in aof_roundtrip_test.rs) but never through the full server lifecycle: TCP write → persist → shutdown → startup → reload → TCP read. The RDB path has encode/decode unit tests but no server-lifecycle test.

## Implementation

### Test A: AOF Restart
1. Start frankenredis with --aof /tmp/test_aof_file_$$
2. Via TCP: SET 50 keys with values, LPUSH list1 with 10 elements, HSET hash1 with 5 fields, ZADD zset1 with 5 members, XADD stream1 with 3 entries, XGROUP CREATE on stream1
3. Send SHUTDOWN NOSAVE (graceful exit without extra save — AOF already captured writes)
4. Restart frankenredis with same --aof path
5. Via TCP: verify all 50 keys, list contents, hash fields, zset scores, stream entries, consumer group exists
6. Verify DBSIZE matches expected count

### Test B: RDB Restart
1. Start frankenredis with --rdb /tmp/test_rdb_file_$$
2. Via TCP: SET 50 keys, same data structures as above
3. Send SAVE (force RDB snapshot)
4. Send SHUTDOWN NOSAVE
5. Restart frankenredis with same --rdb path
6. Via TCP: verify all data survived
7. Verify stream consumer groups survived (tests the new RDB consumer group persistence)

### Logging
- Log each write operation with key name
- Log verification result for each key (PASS/FAIL)
- Log file sizes (AOF bytes, RDB bytes) after write phase
- Log reload time on restart

## Files
- crates/fr-server/tests/persistence_restart_e2e.rs (new)
close_reason: Added tcp_aof_restart_preserves_all_data and tcp_rdb_restart_preserves_all_data E2E tests. Also added Runtime::load_rdb() and RDB loading on server startup. All 11 fr-server tests pass.

title: fr-command CLIENT SETINFO validation error hardcodes uppercase attr name; upstream preserves user-provided case via addReplyErrorFormat
description:
## Symptom
\`CLIENT SETINFO lib-name 'has space'\` returns \`ERR LIB-NAME cannot contain spaces, newlines or special characters.\` (uppercase). Upstream returns \`ERR lib-name cannot contain spaces, newlines or special characters.\` — preserves the exact case the user typed.

## Differential probe
Upstream networking.c::clientSetinfoCommand at legacy_redis_code/redis/src/networking.c:2934-2952:
\`\`\`
sds attr = c->argv[2]->ptr;       // user-provided verbatim
...
if (validateClientAttr(val)==C_ERR) {
    addReplyErrorFormat(c,
        \"%s cannot contain spaces, newlines or special characters.\", attr);
    return;
}
\`\`\`

\`attr\` is the client's argv[2] string (case preserved). \`%s\` substitutes verbatim — same as the unrecognized-option case (line 2945 \`Unrecognized option '%s'\`) which fr already handles correctly via \`format!(\"ERR Unrecognized option '{attr}'\")\` at fr-command/src/lib.rs:14708.

The validation branches at lib.rs:14684 (LIB-NAME) and 14696 (LIB-VER) hardcode the canonical uppercase attribute names instead of substituting the user-supplied attr. Comment at line 14682 even says 'Upstream: \"LIB-NAME cannot contain spaces..\"' which is incorrect — upstream emits whatever case the user typed.

## Fix
Replace the two hardcoded \"LIB-NAME cannot contain...\" and \"LIB-VER cannot contain...\" string literals with \`format!(\"ERR {attr} cannot contain spaces, newlines or special characters.\")\` so the user's case is preserved (matching the existing 'Unrecognized option' pattern at line 14708 + upstream addReplyErrorFormat behavior).

## Test
Extend client_setinfo_matches_runtime_contract (line 42672) with two case-preservation assertions for lib-name and Lib-Ver inputs containing spaces.

## Out of scope
fr-runtime path also goes through dispatch_argv → fr-command client_cmd, so the single edit covers both surfaces.
close_reason: fr-command CLIENT SETINFO validation error now substitutes the user-supplied attr name verbatim via format!('ERR {attr} cannot contain...'), matching upstream networking.c::clientSetinfoCommand's addReplyErrorFormat behavior. The hardcoded uppercase 'LIB-NAME'/'LIB-VER' literals were echoing the canonical case regardless of user input. Test client_setinfo_matches_runtime_contract extended with two case-preservation assertions (lowercase lib-name, mixed-case Lib-Ver). fr-runtime path delegates through dispatch_argv → fr-command client_cmd, so this single edit fixes both surfaces.

title: Implement LATENCY HISTOGRAM per-command tracking
description:
## What
LATENCY HISTOGRAM currently returns an empty array (stub). Redis returns cumulative distribution
data for per-command latencies when enabled.

## Location
crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:12152-12158

## Current behavior
```rust
// LATENCY HISTOGRAM [command ...]
// Returns cumulative distribution of latencies for commands.
// Currently returns an empty array as per-command latency histograms
// are not yet tracked. Full implementation would return map-like data.
// TODO: Implement full histogram tracking per-command.
Ok(RespFrame::Array(Some(vec![])))
```

## Design
1. Add HdrHistogram or similar per-command latency tracking to Store or Runtime
2. Record command execution times in dispatch_argv return path
3. Return histogram bucket data in Redis-compatible format

## Acceptance criteria
- LATENCY HISTOGRAM returns actual per-command latency distributions
- Compatible with Redis LATENCY HISTOGRAM output format
- Minimal overhead when not actively querying histograms
close_reason: Implemented LATENCY HISTOGRAM per-command tracking with power-of-2 microsecond buckets, integrated latency recording at all command dispatch points

title: fr-runtime::handle_acl_getuser returns flat Array even in RESP3 — upstream emits Map per addReplyMap (same pattern as i40x2 TRACKINGINFO)
description:
/porting-to-rust differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 with HELLO 3:

  ACL GETUSER default (RESP3, with -3)
    upstream: Map shape — 'flags on / nopass / sanitize-payload / passwords  / commands +@all / keys ~* / channels &* / selectors '
    fr      : flat Array — each k+v on its own RESP-element

Source: crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:6346-6359 — handle_acl_getuser unconditionally builds RespFrame::Array regardless of session.resp_protocol_version.

Compare upstream acl.c::aclCommand GETUSER which calls addReplyMapLen(c, 6) — RESP3 wire shape is a Map of 6 (key, value) pairs (and 7 with selectors in 7.4+). fr already has the right pattern for HGETALL (lib.rs:3008) and CLIENT TRACKINGINFO (just shipped i40x2).

Fix: emit RespFrame::Map under RESP3 with 6 (key, value) pairs:
  flags → Array, passwords → Array, commands → BulkString, keys → BulkString, channels → BulkString, selectors → Array.
RESP2 path stays as-is (flat Array).

Severity P1 because ACL GETUSER is the canonical security-introspection command — Redis Insight, monitoring stacks, and ACL-aware tooling under RESP3 all expect the Map shape.
close_reason: fr-runtime::handle_acl_getuser now branches on self.session.resp_protocol_version: emits RespFrame::Map under RESP3 with 6 (key, value) pairs (flags, passwords, commands, keys, channels, selectors) — mirroring upstream acl.c::aclCommand GETUSER's addReplyMapLen(c, 6); RESP2 stays as the flat 12-element Array. Two new pin tests: acl_getuser_under_resp3_returns_map_shape (HELLO 3 then assert Map with 6 entries + key order) and acl_getuser_under_resp2_stays_flat_array. Existing 2 ACL GETUSER tests still green; clippy clean. Same shape pattern as the just-shipped i40x2 CLIENT TRACKINGINFO fix.

title: ZRANGEBYSCORE/BYLEX: parse trailing options before bounds for syntax-error wording
description:
ZRANGEBYSCORE / ZREVRANGEBYSCORE / ZRANGEBYLEX / ZREVRANGEBYLEX in fr parse min/max BEFORE scanning trailing options, so misplaced or extra trailing args surface as the more-specific bound-parser error rather than the upstream "syntax error".

Examples:
  vendored ZRANGEBYSCORE zk WITHSCORES 1 4   -> ERR syntax error
  fr       ZRANGEBYSCORE zk WITHSCORES 1 4   -> ERR min or max is not a float

  vendored ZRANGEBYLEX zlex LIMIT 0 2        -> ERR syntax error
  fr       ZRANGEBYLEX zlex LIMIT 0 2        -> ERR min or max not valid string range item

Vendored's t_zset.c::genericZrangebyscoreCommand and zrangebylexCommand walk the trailing optional args (WITHSCORES / LIMIT off cnt) first and bail with "syntax error" on any unknown token. Only when the option tail is clean (or empty) does it parse min/max with the per-bound "not a float" / "not valid string range item" wordings. This means:

  ZRANGEBYSCORE zk WITHSCORES bogus     (4 args)  -> ERR min or max is not a float  (options tail empty)
  ZRANGEBYSCORE zk bogus 4              (4 args)  -> ERR min or max is not a float  (options tail empty)
  ZRANGEBYSCORE zk 1 4 BAD              (5 args)  -> ERR syntax error               (unknown tail token)
  ZRANGEBYSCORE zk LIMIT 1 4            (5 args)  -> ERR syntax error               (option tail '4' unknown)
  ZRANGEBYSCORE zk WITHSCORES 1 4       (5 args)  -> ERR syntax error               (option tail '4' unknown)

All confirmed against vendored 7.2.4 on :16380.

Fix plan:
- In fr-command/src/lib.rs zrangebyscore (line 4508), zrevrangebyscore (11319), zrangebylex (11343), zrevrangebylex (11383): call parse_zrangebyscore_opts(argv, 4) FIRST, then parse min/max bounds.
- Regression test pins all 4 commands on the misplaced-option case.

Probe sweep #71 (probe142.sh).
close_reason: completed

title: Lua {set=t} hint iterates inner table VALUES instead of KEYS
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:

  EVAL 'return {set={a=true, b=true}}' 0
    vendored: *2\r\n$1\r\na\r\n$1\r\nb\r\n  (set of {"a","b"})
    fr:       *0\r\n                                  (empty array)

  EVAL 'return {set={"a","b","c"}}' 0
    vendored: 1,2,3  (positional table → KEYS are integer indices)
    fr:       a,b,c  (VALUES)

Root cause: lua_to_resp in fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs::lua_to_resp iterates the inner table's array part as values. Upstream src/script_lua.c::luaReplyToRedisReply iterates the inner table with lua_next (which yields k,v pairs), pops the value, and recurses on the key — so the emitted members are the KEYS of the inner table.

Fix: iterate inner table keys across all three storage areas (array indices 1..N, string_hash keys, other_hash keys) and emit those as Array elements.

Note: a parallel RESP3 gap (fr-protocol has no Set variant, so RESP3 clients also receive an Array frame rather than ) is acknowledged in the source comment and deferred — RESP2 parity is the priority for this bead.
close_reason: Fixed {set=t} hint handling in lua_to_resp at fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs (~line 7820). Now iterates the inner table's KEYS across all three storage areas: array indices 1..N as RespFrame::Integer, string_hash keys as BulkString, and other_hash keys via lua_to_resp. Matches upstream src/script_lua.c::luaReplyToRedisReply behavior where lua_next yields k,v and only the key is recursed for emission. Regression test eval_set_hint_emits_keys_not_values_e6ffo covers 4 cases: string-keyed hash, positional table (keys = 1,2,3 not values), mixed int+string keys, empty set. RESP3 Set frame (~N) emission is acknowledged as a separate fr-protocol gap and deferred. Live diff vs vendored 7.2.4 matches on 5 probed RESP2 variants.

CONFIG GET tracks these defaults ('replica-announced=yes', 'replica-read-only=yes') so the wire reply for stub mode should be 1 for both. Add the two fields next to slave_priority in the replica branch.
