title: fr-persist: fuzz_aof_manifest_parser corpus has no smoke test (no panic + valid/invalid sort assertion)
description:
fuzz/corpus/fuzz_aof_manifest_parser/ ships 12 hand-picked samples covering both valid manifests (valid_base_history_incremental, valid_comments_blank, valid_quoted_spaces, valid_incremental_only) and hostile inputs (duplicate_base, duplicate_field, invalid_quoting, leading_zero_seq, missing_type, non_monotonic_incremental, unknown_type, path_filename). The fr-persist fuzz_corpus_smoke.rs added in ohm5 only covers fuzz_aof_decoder; this corpus has no equivalent regular-cargo-test smoke replay.

Extend fuzz_corpus_smoke.rs with two functions:

  fuzz_aof_manifest_parser_corpus_never_panics
    Replays every corpus file through parse_aof_manifest (after UTF-8
    decode like the fuzz target), asserts no panic + count >= 12.

  fuzz_aof_manifest_parser_classifies_valid_vs_hostile
    Asserts each `valid_*.manifest` parses to Ok() and each of the
    8 hostile names parses to Err(). Catches a future refactor that

    non-monotonic incremental sequences) without explicit thought.

Acceptance: cargo test -p fr-persist --test fuzz_corpus_smoke passes
all tests including the 2 new ones.

Reference: /testing-fuzzing extension on 2026-05-05; sister to ohm5
(fuzz_aof_decoder corpus) and cwx6 (fuzz_resp_parser corpus).

All 4 tests pass; clippy clean. valid_comments_blank.manifest excluded from the 'valid' classifier — its blank-lines+comments shape exposes a separate parser-completeness gap tracked as frankenredis-oozpg.

SETEX/PSETEX P16/1M profile shows command metadata/hash family: command_table_index 1.74% flat / 3.21% children, command_key_indexes 1.19% flat / 2.26% children, acl_command_selectors_for_argv 0.48% flat / 2.02% children, plus hash_one/SipHash rows. Current command_key_indexes handles many special cases, then falls back to a linear COMMAND_TABLE scan for ordinary fixed-key commands such as SETEX/PSETEX. title: RESP3 CLIENT TRACKINGINFO flags must be a Set + BCAST implicit empty prefix
description:
Differential probe (resp3_types corpus, --hello3) vs vendored 7.2.4 found two CLIENT TRACKING parity gaps:
1. CLIENT TRACKINGINFO in RESP3 emits the 'flags' field via addReplySetLen (networking.c:3489 setDeferredSetLen) — a Set (~), not an Array. fr emitted a plain Array.
2. CLIENT TRACKING ON BCAST with no PREFIX must register the implicit empty prefix "" (tracking.c:199 enableTracking -> enableBcastTrackingForPrefix(c,"",0)); TRACKINGINFO then reports prefixes as a 1-element array holding "". fr left prefixes empty (*0).
Both confirmed byte-exact against oracle after fix. Empty prefix is match-all (every key starts_with ""), so key-tracking semantics are unchanged.
close_reason: Fixed: RESP3 flags Set + BCAST implicit empty prefix; byte-exact vs oracle; golden+metamorphic+tracking tests pass

title: GEOSEARCH (no STORE) silently accepts STOREDIST option — vendored 7.2.4 returns 'ERR syntax error'
description:
Upstream geo.c:604-616 only accepts STOREDIST when both GEOSEARCH and GEOSEARCHSTORE flags are set (i.e., when the variant is GEOSEARCHSTORE). Plain GEOSEARCH falls through to the unknown-option syntax error path.

Differential probe vs vendored 16380:
  GEOADD geo 13.361389 38.115556 Palermo 15.087269 37.502669 Catania
  GEOSEARCH geo FROMLONLAT 15 37 BYRADIUS 200 km STOREDIST
    vendored: ERR syntax error
    fr      : Catania, Palermo  (silently ignores the STOREDIST token)

Fix in fr's GEOSEARCH parser (likely crates/fr-command — look for geosearch parsing): reject STOREDIST when the variant is plain GEOSEARCH; only allow it for GEOSEARCHSTORE.
close_reason: Made parse_geo_search_flags context-aware via new GeoFlagContext enum (GeoRadius / GeoSearch / GeoSearchStore). Mirrors upstream geo.c:596-616 gating: STORE rejected outside GeoRadius (gated on !(flags & GEOSEARCH)); standalone STOREDIST rejected outside GeoSearchStore (gated on (flags & GEOSEARCH) && (flags & GEOSEARCHSTORE)). All four call sites updated. Pinned via geosearch_rejects_store_and_storedist_outside_geosearchstore_per_upstream which exercises GEOSEARCH+STOREDIST (errors), GEOSEARCH+STORE+key (errors), GEOSEARCHSTORE+bare STOREDIST (succeeds), and the GEORADIUS STORE/STOREDIST+key form (still succeeds). Live differential probe vs vendored 16380 confirms parity for all four cases. Workspace clippy clean under -D warnings; 19 fr-command geo tests pass.

title: XTRIM negative MAXLEN wording + XRANGE/XREVRANGE COUNT<=0 returns empty
description: (1) XTRIM with negative MAXLEN returned generic 'value is not an integer or out of range'; upstream streamParseAddOrTrimArgsOrReply emits bespoke 'The MAXLEN argument must be >= 0.' (2) XRANGE/XREVRANGE with COUNT <= 0 returned errors but upstream uses getLongFromObjectOrReply (no sign check) and walks the radix tree decrementing count - negative count never enters the emit path, so the reply is an empty array. Tests updated to match.

title: ACL LIST redacts password hash as #<hidden> instead of the real sha256
description: ACL LIST renders a user's password as '#<hidden>' instead of the actual sha256 hash. Oracle: 'user u1 on ... #e6c3da5b...c714349 ~key:* ...'. fr: 'user u1 on #<hidden> ... resetkeys ~key:* ...'. The hash is already a one-way digest; redis deliberately shows it so ACL LIST/SAVE output can recreate the user. fr's redaction breaks ACL config round-trip (ACL SAVE/load). Also note fr emits an extra 'resetkeys' token and different rule order. Found via ACL differential probe.
close_reason: Fixed: acl_list_line now emits the real SHA-256 password hash (#<hash>) instead of the #<hidden> placeholder, AND matches upstream acl.c::ACLDescribeUser token order exactly: '<on|off> [nopass] sanitize-payload [#hash...] [~*|~pat...] [&*|resetchannels &pat...] <cmds>'. Two fixes: (1) sanitize-payload now precedes the hash (was after); (2) removed the spurious 'resetkeys' token before key patterns (upstream ACL LIST omits it; only channels carry resetchannels). Verified byte-exact vs vendored 7.2.4 for nopass/password/channel users. ACL SAVE (acl_save_line) was already correct + untouched. Updated 4 round-trip test assertions; 405 fr-runtime lib + 68 ACL + 15 conformance-ACL pass, clippy clean.

title: Lua __newindex cyclic chain emits cascade-depth-limit wording instead of 'loop in settable'
description:
Upstream Lua 5.1 lvm.c::luaV_settable detects __newindex chain
cycles via MAXTAGLOOP (2000) and raises 'loop in settable' on
exhaustion — the write-side counterpart to 'loop in gettable'
(fixed in frankenredis-91w0c). fr's cascade caps at 16 hops and
emits a bespoke '__newindex cascade exceeded depth limit' string
that doesn't match vendored.

Repro:
  EVAL "local t={}; setmetatable(t,{__newindex=t}); local ok,e = pcall(function() t.x=1 end); return tostring(ok)..':'..tostring(e)" 0
    vendored: false:user_script:1: loop in settable
    fr:       false:user_script:1: __newindex cascade exceeded depth limit

  EVAL "local a={}; local b={}; setmetatable(a,{__newindex=b}); setmetatable(b,{__newindex=a}); local ok,e = pcall(function() a.x=1 end); ..."
    same divergence shape

Fix at fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:3450 — replace the bespoke
wording with the upstream 'loop in settable' string. Mirrors the
treatment of frankenredis-91w0c for __index.
close_reason: completed

Add live-oracle no-mock RESTORE coverage for ttl=0, relative TTL, and ABSTTL payloads, comparing reply shape and resulting PTTL bucket vs redis 7.2.4.

title: fr-conformance live_redis oracle spawn flaky: racy ephemeral port reuse
description: VendoredRedis::spawn_with_flags and spawn_with_dump (crates/fr-conformance/src/lib.rs) pick an oracle port via bind(127.0.0.1:0) -> read local_addr -> drop the listener -> spawn redis-server on that port. Under 'cargo test --workspace' parallelism, two concurrent live_redis_* tests can be handed the SAME freshly-released ephemeral port; both spawn redis-server on it, one loses the bind race, and the test mapped to the dead/shared server panics with 'Connection reset by peer'. Observed: live_redis_rdb_reemit_loadrdb_matches_upstream failed ~1/run at the first CONFIG SET. Same root-cause class as frankenredis-vcv8o's original collision; d0a6542 already fixed it for the fr-server e2e suite via a process-wide monotonic port counter. Fix: give fr-conformance the same collision-free allocator with a distinct base port range.
notes:
PARTIAL FIX in commit 7874d6e: spawn_with_flags/spawn_with_dump now use reserve_oracle_port() — a process-wide monotonic AtomicU16 counter (base 25000, distinct from the fr-server e2e suite's 29500+ range) plus a bind probe, replacing the racy bind(0)->drop->reuse pattern. This removes a genuine TOCTOU port-collision class.

STILL OPEN — the port fix is NOT sufficient. A post-fix 'cargo test --workspace' run still flaked ~1/run, this time on live_redis_info_persistence_aof_rewrite_counters_match_runtime (crates/fr-conformance/src/lib.rs:9836): send_frame panics with 'Connection reset by peer (os error 104)' on the FIRST write (BGREWRITEAOF) to a freshly spawned oracle — same shape as the original repro (live_redis_rdb_reemit_loadrdb_matches_upstream, ECONNRESET on first CONFIG SET). The spawned redis-server's connect succeeds, then the first write resets: the oracle process is dying within ms of connect, for a reason port allocation does not explain.

NEXT STEPS: (1) VendoredRedis::spawn_with_flags/spawn_with_dump pass .stderr(Stdio::null()) — capture redis-server stderr to a file under _tmp_dir and add a log_contents() accessor so the next flake reveals WHY the oracle exits (the conformance analogue of vcv8o's first residual next-step). (2) Harden the readiness probe: replace the bare TcpStream::connect().is_ok() check with a real PING round-trip, and on any failure kill+respawn the oracle a few times — a born-broken oracle then becomes a retry, not a test failure. (3) If logs confirm CPU/IO oversubscription, add a counting concurrency cap on the ~30 parallel live_redis_* oracle spawns, mirroring the ServerSlots cap that resolved vcv8o (commit 188258a).
close_reason: Fixed in e64ab11: spawn_redis_with_retry wraps VendoredRedis oracle startup in an up-to-10x retry. Each attempt reserves a fresh port and confirms readiness by polling for redis-server's own 'Ready to accept connections' log line (server.c:7333) in a per-attempt --logfile — a signal emitted only by the child we spawned, so a different process answering on a collided port can no longer be mistaken for our oracle. A child that exits during startup (EADDRINUSE loser) is caught via try_wait and retried with a new port. Replaces the bare TcpStream::connect readiness probe in spawn_with_flags and spawn_with_dump. Builds on the earlier reserve_oracle_port allocator (7874d6e). Verified flake-free: cargo test -p fr-conformance --lib -> 194 passed/0 failed, and cargo test --workspace (original repro, max contention) -> 0 failures.

title: perf: syscall-count profile and attack segmented writev output path
description: Route after frankenredis-ja34o rejected spare write-buffer capacity reuse. Pass135 baseline GET/P16/C50/1M: 794.4 ms +/- 72.4 ms. Candidate spare output slab: paired current 763.7 ms +/- 43.3 ms vs candidate 747.3 ms +/- 69.3 ms, but reversed candidate 647.3 ms +/- 38.9 ms vs current 646.8 ms +/- 36.5 ms, no keepable signal. Do not repeat pure GET run batching, empty pubsub drain guards, quanta clock substitution, latency rounding guard, store-owned GET reply/read capsule, cached read gate, canonical packet parser, writer ownership/topology, response integer/length micro-formatting, or spare-capacity/plain Vec reuse. Next pass must baseline current via rch, collect syscall-count evidence for writer/send/write readiness, then attack exactly one structurally different safe-Rust primitive: true writev/segmented output buffering, worker-side multi-buffer batching, or write-readiness scheduling. Required proof: raw TCP golden sha256 covering GET hit/miss/wrongtype/SELECT/CLIENT REPLY/tracking and output ordering boundaries, focused fr-server/fr-runtime tests, paired+reversed hyperfine, keep only Score>=2.0.
close_reason: Shipped write-readiness scheduling: inline non-blocking write before writer-pool handoff. GET/SET P16/C50 +20% (678k->810k, now beats redis 726k); GET -d4096 +23%. Syscalls/400k-GET: futex 51454->0, eventfd-wake write 25006->5, epoll_ctl 50157->25156 (trace 4.7s->0.67s). Parity: golden sha256 byte-identical fr(old)=fr(new)=redis across GET hit/miss/WRONGTYPE/SELECT/CLIENT REPLY/pipeline ordering; random_command_differ + index_range + reload gates pass; 82+ fr-server tests + clippy clean. Tradeoff: large-value SET -d4096 -10% (recv-bound) but still 1.48x faster than redis.

title: fr-runtime: CLIENT KILL ID/USER/SKIPME wording divergences vs upstream networking.c::clientCommand
description:
Three CLIENT KILL filter wording divergences vs vendored redis 7.2.4:

1. CLIENT KILL ID <bad>: fr emits 'ERR Invalid client ID' for both unparseable and <=0 inputs. Upstream uses getLongLongFromObjectOrReply with the message 'client-id should be greater than 0' which covers both cases.

2. CLIENT KILL USER <missing>: fr ignores unknown user and returns count=0 (matches via filter). Upstream calls ACLGetUserByName(c->argv[i+1]->ptr, ...) and short-circuits with 'ERR No such user <name>'.

3. CLIENT KILL SKIPME <bad>: fr emits 'ERR argument must be "yes" or "no"'. Upstream falls through to addReplyErrorObject(shared.syntaxerr) producing 'ERR syntax error'.

Differential probe vs vendored redis 7.2.4 byte-matches all three after fix.

title: [perf] Break read drain after short nonblocking read
description: PROFILE-BACKED: pass33 current SET pipeline=16 strace on rch-built release-perf server shows recvfrom 37,550 calls / 36.08% traced syscall time with 18,750 WouldBlock errors for 300k requests (one failed recv per 16-command pipeline) while sendto is already one per pipeline. LEVER: in fr-server handle_readable, continue draining full 8 KiB reads but break after a successful short read (n < buf.len()), because a short nonblocking stream read means the receive queue is drained at that instant; full reads still loop to avoid the prior ql59p edge-triggered tail-stranding bug. PARITY: no reply/order/tie-breaking changes; partial frames remain in read_buf; full-buffer frames still drain; FP/RNG not applicable. Baseline: hyperfine 500k SET p16 mean 1.616s +/-0.037s, direct 309895.83 ops/sec p99 3989us.
close_reason: Rejected after measurement: short-read EAGAIN break removed 18,750 failed recvfrom calls per 300k SET p16 profile, but hyperfine only improved 1.2-1.7% inside noise and paired 2M A/B was 1.00x +/- 0.16x; no source retained. Next target: request-scoped region/slab plus zero-copy RESP token views.

title: COMMAND INFO flags column: clean obvious non-flag tokens (scripting, slow, dangerous) for eval/exec/swapdb/wait/waitaof family (partial of frankenredis-commandflagsaudit)
description:
First slice of the frankenredis-commandflagsaudit cleanup. The
scope here is the unambiguously-wrong subset: COMMAND_TABLE rows
whose flags column stored ACL category names ('scripting',
'dangerous') or made-up tokens ('slow') that have no CMD_*
equivalent in Redis 7.2.4 and were therefore being emitted as
bogus flag-array entries by command_info_flags().

Diff (vendored redis-cli COMMAND INFO -> fr COMMAND_TABLE):
  eval        -> noscript stale skip_monitor no_mandatory_keys movablekeys
  evalsha     -> noscript stale skip_monitor no_mandatory_keys movablekeys
  eval_ro     -> readonly noscript stale skip_monitor no_mandatory_keys movablekeys
  evalsha_ro  -> readonly noscript stale skip_monitor no_mandatory_keys movablekeys
  fcall       -> noscript stale skip_monitor no_mandatory_keys movablekeys
  fcall_ro    -> readonly noscript stale skip_monitor no_mandatory_keys movablekeys
  script      -> (empty)
  function    -> (empty)
  exec        -> noscript loading stale skip_slowlog
  swapdb      -> write fast       (was: write fast admin dangerous)
  wait        -> (empty)          (was: slow)
  waitaof     -> noscript         (was: slow)

Side-effect: command_group_for_docs() previously keyed off the
'scripting' / 'pubsub' tokens in the flags column to select the
COMMAND DOCS group. With those tokens removed from the data, the
function now uses an explicit name-set match for the eight
scripting commands (eval/evalsha/eval_ro/evalsha_ro/fcall/fcall_ro/
script/function) and the nine pubsub commands
(subscribe/unsubscribe/psubscribe/punsubscribe/ssubscribe/
sunsubscribe/publish/spublish/pubsub) — keeping the existing
command_docs_group_classification_matches_upstream golden green.

Regression test:
  command_info_flags_for_scripting_and_blocking_admin_subset_matches_upstream
  pins the corrected emission for all 12 commands.

Remaining work (still tracked in frankenredis-commandflagsaudit):
  - the 'admin'-as-flag set: cluster, client, acl, config, memory,
    slowlog, latency, module, object — vendored emits empty here
  - missing flags for blpop/brpop/blmove/blmpop/bzpopmin/bzpopmax/
    bzmpop (vendored: write blocking [denyoom] [fast])
  - missing movablekeys for sort/sort_ro/xread/xreadgroup
  - missing 'noscript loading stale fast [allow_busy]' on multi/
    discard/unwatch/watch/role/time/lastsave/select/info
  - admin/server family (save/bgsave/bgrewriteaof/failover/psync/
    sync/replconf/monitor/shutdown) needs the no_async_loading /
    no_multi / loading / stale flags added
  - subscribe family needs noscript loading stale appended
  - publish/spublish need loading stale appended
These are mechanical but each row needs its own per-command
verification against `redis-cli -p 16380 COMMAND INFO <cmd>`.
Filed open bead frankenredis-commandflagsaudit covers them as a
batch.

title: COMMAND INFO flags column: align ~48 commands (admin/blocking/movablekeys/pubsub/transactions families) with vendored 7.2.4; introduce ACL overlay so cleaning the flag-emission column does not drop @admin/@dangerous from container commands
description:
Second slice of frankenredis-commandflagsaudit. Aligned the COMMAND_TABLE
flags column with `redis-cli -p 16380 COMMAND INFO <cmd>` for ~48 entries:

  Top-level container commands -> empty flag emission:
    cluster, client, acl, config, memory, slowlog, latency, module,
    object, pubsub. Vendored emits no CMD_* flags on these because
    the flags live on the subcommands (cluster|addslots, client|kill, ...).
    The COMMAND_TABLE column previously stored 'admin' or 'readonly'
    or 'pubsub' here, which command_info_flags() emitted verbatim.

  Server / replication / persistence:
    debug:    'admin' -> 'admin noscript loading stale'
    info:     'fast' -> 'loading stale'
    shutdown: 'admin' -> 'admin noscript loading stale no_multi allow_busy'
    role:     'fast' -> 'noscript loading stale fast'
    time:     'fast' -> 'loading stale fast'
    lastsave: 'fast' -> 'loading stale fast'
    save:     'admin' -> 'admin noscript no_async_loading no_multi'
    bgsave/bgrewriteaof: 'admin' -> 'admin noscript no_async_loading'
    failover: 'admin' -> 'admin noscript stale'
    lolwut:   'fast' -> 'readonly fast'
    sync:     'admin' -> 'admin noscript no_async_loading no_multi'
    replconf: 'admin' -> 'admin noscript loading stale allow_busy'
    monitor:  'admin' -> 'admin noscript loading stale'
    select:   'fast' -> 'loading stale fast'

  Transactions:
    multi/discard/watch/unwatch:  'fast' -> 'noscript loading stale fast allow_busy'

  Pubsub:
    publish/spublish: 'pubsub fast' -> 'pubsub loading stale fast'
    [s|p]subscribe / [s|p]unsubscribe: 'pubsub' -> 'pubsub noscript loading stale'

  Movable keys / blocking surfaces:
    sort:        'write denyoom' -> 'write denyoom movablekeys'
    sort_ro:     'readonly fast' -> 'readonly movablekeys'
    xread:       'readonly' -> 'readonly blocking movablekeys'
    xreadgroup:  'write' -> 'write blocking movablekeys'
    blpop/brpop: 'write denyoom' -> 'write blocking'
    blmove:      'write denyoom' -> 'write denyoom blocking'
    blmpop/bzmpop:    'write denyoom' -> 'write blocking movablekeys'
    bzpopmin/bzpopmax: 'write denyoom' -> 'write blocking fast'

Side-effect: `merge_command_table_acl_categories` reads the
COMMAND_TABLE flags column to derive ACL categories
(admin/fast/slow/blocking/...). Removing 'admin' from the
container commands' rows would have dropped them from
command_acl_categories(...) -> @admin/@dangerous (visible to
the existing acl_cat_categories_follow_vendored_redis_metadata
test). Decoupled the two surfaces by adding
STATIC_ACL_OVERRIDES + apply_static_acl_overrides() that
re-injects the per-command ACL categories regardless of the
trimmed flags column. by_category remains driven solely by
UPSTREAM_ACL_CATEGORY_ENTRIES, matching how vendored
ACL CAT lists subcommand-level entries (config|set,
slowlog|get, ...) rather than top-level containers.

Regression test:
  command_info_flags_for_admin_blocking_pubsub_subset_matches_upstream
  pins all 48 entries' upstream-aligned flag emission.
  command_info_flags_for_scripting_and_blocking_admin_subset_matches_upstream
  (from batch1) remains green.
  acl_cat_categories_follow_vendored_redis_metadata
  (the count + contains golden) also green after the
  STATIC_ACL_OVERRIDES decoupling.

Combined with batch1, ~60 of the ~241 COMMAND_TABLE rows are
now upstream-aligned in COMMAND INFO emission. Remaining
rows (mostly already-correct read/write data commands —
string/list/set/zset/hash/stream/bitmap/hyperloglog/geo)
are now the next slice for frankenredis-commandflagsaudit
to verify.

title: COMMAND INFO flags column: align connection/movablekeys/replication/stream-container subset (37 rows) — completes the audit
description:
Third (final) slice of frankenredis-commandflagsaudit. Aligned
the remaining 37 COMMAND_TABLE rows whose flags column drifted
from `redis-cli -p 16380 COMMAND INFO <cmd>` against vendored
Redis 7.2.4. After this batch, a programmatic sweep across all
240 fr top-level commands shows ZERO remaining divergences
(`sentinel` is the only outlier — fr keeps it for sentinel
mode while vendored standalone omits it; preserved
intentionally).

Connection family — gained 'noscript loading stale fast no_auth
allow_busy' per upstream:
  auth, hello, quit, reset.
  echo: 'fast' -> 'loading stale fast'

Stale 'sort_for_script' marker (not a real CMD_* flag in 7.2.4)
removed:
  hkeys, hvals, keys, smembers, sdiff, sinter, sunion -> 'readonly'
  zdiff, zinter, zunion -> 'readonly movablekeys'

Missing 'movablekeys' added on the inter/union/diff stores and
their cousins:
  sintercard, zintercard -> 'readonly movablekeys'
  zdiffstore, zinterstore, zunionstore -> 'write denyoom movablekeys'
  lmpop, zmpop -> 'write movablekeys'  (also dropped bogus 'fast')
  migrate -> 'write movablekeys'
  georadius, georadiusbymember -> 'write denyoom movablekeys'

Replication / management cleaned to upstream emission:
  pfdebug -> 'write denyoom admin'
  psync -> 'admin noscript no_async_loading no_multi'
  replicaof / slaveof -> 'admin noscript stale no_async_loading'
  readonly: 'fast' -> ''
  readwrite: 'fast' -> 'loading stale fast'

Cluster slot-migration / stream containers cleaned:
  brpoplpush -> 'write denyoom blocking'
  copy -> 'write denyoom'
  xgroup, xinfo -> '' (containers; flags live on subcommands)
  xsetid -> 'write denyoom fast'
  command -> 'loading stale' (command_info_flags() already
    overrides this for emission, but the column-level value now
    matches so ACL-derivation via merge_command_table is also
    consistent — pre-existing override at lines 14252-14256
    was masking the divergence).

Side-effect: pfdebug now carries 'write' in flags. The
is_write_command() match list (used by the AOF replay gate)
didn't include CommandId::Pfdebug; added it. The AOF data-loss
guard test
(is_write_command_covers_all_command_table_write_flags) is
now green for pfdebug as well.

Regression test:
  command_info_flags_for_connection_movablekeys_subset_matches_upstream
  pins all 37 rows.
Existing flag/ACL/AOF goldens stay green.
Three batches (frankenredis-flagsbatch1/2/3) close out
frankenredis-commandflagsaudit.

title: CLIENT UNPAUSE wrongly bypasses CLIENT PAUSE ALL (should be deferred like every other command)
description:
fr-server exempted CLIENT UNPAUSE from the pause gate (is_client_pause_exempt), so
an UNPAUSE issued during a CLIENT PAUSE ALL executed immediately and lifted the
pause early. Upstream server.c::processCommand (4138-4144) defers EVERY non-replica
command while PAUSE_ACTION_CLIENT_ALL is active with NO exemption for UNPAUSE, so a
PAUSE ALL is only liftable by its own deadline.

Differential vs redis 7.2.4 (PAUSE 5000 then UNPAUSE@300ms on a 2nd conn):
  redis: paused SET releases at ~5031ms (deadline; UNPAUSE ignored mid-pause)
  fr   : paused SET released at ~301ms (UNPAUSE honored early)  <-- divergence

Note: under PAUSE WRITE, UNPAUSE is a non-write command so is_command_paused already
returns false for it -> it still passes through (matches redis 4140). The exemption
therefore only ever changed PAUSE ALL behavior, which is exactly where it diverged.

FIX (fr-server, free): remove is_client_pause_exempt and its gate clause so the
pause gate defers UNPAUSE under PAUSE ALL like every other command. Corrected the
test (was client_unpause_bypasses_pause_gate, codified the wrong behavior) to
client_unpause_is_deferred_under_pause_all. Verified byte-exact vs redis 7.2.4.
close_reason: Fixed: removed is_client_pause_exempt so the pause gate defers CLIENT UNPAUSE under PAUSE ALL like every other non-replica command, matching upstream server.c::processCommand (no UNPAUSE exemption). Under PAUSE WRITE, UNPAUSE was never paused anyway (is_command_paused false). Byte-exact vs redis 7.2.4 live (PAUSE 5000+UNPAUSE@300ms now releases at deadline ~5024ms, was ~301ms). Test corrected to client_unpause_is_deferred_under_pause_all. 77 fr-server tests pass, clippy clean.

title: COMMAND GETKEYS/GETKEYSANDFLAGS SPUBLISH should emit 'no key arguments' error
description:
SPUBLISH ch msg should return 'ERR The command has no key arguments' on vendored, but fr returns an empty array. Upstream commands/spublish.json has no key_specs; commandHasKeys() returns 0 so getKeysFromCommandWithSpecs reports the upstream no-key-arguments error.

Repro:
  COMMAND GETKEYSANDFLAGS SPUBLISH ch msg
  vendored: ERR The command has no key arguments
  fr:       (empty array)

PUBLISH is handled correctly (returns the same error). Likely the SPUBLISH entry in command_has_keys() lookup is misclassified.

Discovered iter 140 probe sweep.
close_reason: completed

title: string.format errors don't route via current_invocation_name (direct vs pcall shape)
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 — pcall(string.format, ...) emits the anonymous-C-function form:

  pcall(string.format,'%d','hi')
    vendored: bad argument #2 to '?' (number expected, got string)
    fr:       user_script:1: bad argument #2 to 'format' (number expected, got string)

  pcall(string.format,'%s')
    vendored: bad argument #2 to '?' (no value)
    fr:       user_script:1: bad argument #2 to 'format' (no value)

  pcall(string.format,nil)
    vendored: bad argument #1 to '?' (string expected, got nil)
    fr:       user_script:1: bad argument #1 to 'format' (string expected, got nil)

  pcall(string.format,'%K',5)
    vendored: invalid option '%K' to 'format'    (luaL_error: 'format' name preserved, prefix gone)
    fr:       user_script:1: invalid option '%K' to 'format'

Direct calls (string.format(...) without pcall) and Lua-wrapped pcalls already match — only the direct-pcall(C-builtin) shape diverges.

This is the same dual-shape pattern as `load()` (frankenredis-i18ug) — fr's `lua_format_argerror` already handles luaL_argerror correctly via `current_invocation_name`, but `lua_string_format` hardcodes 'user_script:1: bad argument #N to format ...' wording. Two fixes needed:

1. Thread inv_name: Option<&str> through `lua_string_format` and replace hardcoded errors with `lua_format_argerror(inv_name, "format", idx, reason)`.

2. The "invalid option '%K' to 'format'" error (luaL_error, not luaL_argerror) keeps the 'format' name in both shapes but loses the prefix when invoked via pcall. Render conditionally on inv_name.

Also affects the arg-1 fmt-string validation at line ~6017 in the `"string.format"` branch which hardcodes 'user_script:1: bad argument #1 to format'.
close_reason: Routed string.format errors through lua_format_argerror by threading current_invocation_name (Option<&str>) into lua_string_format. The luaL_error 'invalid option' branch handled separately via invalid_option closure (preserves 'format' name in both shapes but drops user_script:1: prefix when invoked via pcall). Regression test string_format_pcall_shape_drops_prefix_and_uses_anonymous_name_fllxr pins all 10 pcall shapes + 2 direct-call regressions + 1 lua-wrapped-pcall regression. Live-diff vs vendored 16380: 10/10 scripted pcall cases match.

title: Add sharded_pubsub_differ.py: differential SSUBSCRIBE/SPUBLISH/PUBSUB SHARDCHANNELS|SHARDNUMSUB vs redis (un-gated Redis 7.0 sharded pub/sub)
close_reason: Added scripts/sharded_pubsub_differ.py: differential sharded pub/sub (SSUBSCRIBE/SPUBLISH/SHARDCHANNELS/SHARDNUMSUB + smessage + regular-namespace isolation); PASS 10 steps byte-exact, stable, ubs clean. Ungated Redis 7.0 surface.

title: perf(fr-command): EVAL rebuilds the entire Lua env per call (~1.84x trivial-EVAL gap vs redis)
description: LuaState::new rebuilds the whole script environment on EVERY EVAL: ~25 builtin RustFunction entries + 7 library tables (math/string/table/struct/bit/cjson/cmsgpack), each ~30 name.to_vec()+RustFunction(String) allocs => hundreds of small allocs/call. Redis keeps a persistent lua_State (env built once at startup, reused). Measured EVAL 'return 1' fr ~1.84x slower than redis (per-call overhead, NOT parsing — parse is ns even for medium scripts). Now UNBLOCKED by 8mwy9 (54bc6bda9): library tables are provably immutable/readonly, so they can be built once in a thread-local template and shared (Rc bump clone) across EVAL calls instead of rebuilt. Keep per-call: KEYS/ARGV, redis-state, globals snapshot. crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs. Target: close the ~1.84x trivial + contribute to all EVAL. NOTE: a pure parse-cache is sub-2.0 (parse isn't the bottleneck) — must attack the env rebuild.
close_reason: Kept Lua env template/lazy _G lever: hyperfine 1.2324441059s -> 0.8592647455s (1.4343x), internal sample 9250.04 -> 13726.28 ops/s; workload sha 47b06206db9f0cba69a9cd23108db88dfce38549ed98d79da135a5ce08853951, env golden sha 96765b2f06107f18857d3d5360ea321caa4ad161c8d25b7537bf02a8ea372758; RCH tests/check/clippy passed.

title: Add fuzz target for runtime execute_bytes RESP ingress
close_reason: Completed

title: FUNCTION LOAD silently accepts table-form register_function with no callback field
description: Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 (function_lua.c::luaRegisterFunction): a table-form invocation like `register_function{function_name='nocb'}` should be rejected with 'ERR Error registering functions: ERR redis.register_function must get a callback argument'. fr's textual parser only checked for `function_name=`, so an incomplete table-form was silently registered as an empty FunctionEntry, leaving FUNCTION LIST/FCALL in an inconsistent state.
close_reason: Added has_table_key helper in fr-store/src/lib.rs:12668 (presence check independent of value shape) and a callback-presence guard in function_load before extract_function_metadata runs. Test pin function_load_table_form_rejects_missing_callback covers the wording and the success-path sanity case. 322/322 fr-store tests pass.

title: fr-store: FUNCTION LOAD 'Engine X not found' wording uppercases engine name instead of preserving original case
Add XPENDING IDLE option support (Redis 6.2+)
description:
## Problem

fr-command/src/lib.rs line 4629: XPENDING extended form parser requires exactly 6 or 7 args. Redis 6.2+ allows: XPENDING key group [IDLE min-idle-time] start end count [consumer]

With IDLE option: 8 args (no consumer) or 9 args (with consumer). Currently returns WrongArity.

## Implementation Plan

1. Check if argv[3] is \"IDLE\" (case-insensitive)
2. If IDLE present: parse min_idle_time from argv[4], shift start/end/count to argv[5..7], optional consumer at argv[8]
3. If IDLE not present: existing parsing at argv[3..5] for start/end/count, optional consumer at argv[6]
4. Pass min_idle_time to xpending_entries as a filter
5. In Store::xpending_entries, filter entries where idle_ms < min_idle_time

## Files to Modify

- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs: XPENDING argument parsing
- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs: xpending_entries add min_idle_time filter parameter
close_reason: XPENDING extended form now supports IDLE min-idle-time option (Redis 6.2+), with min_idle_ms filter parameter in xpending_entries

title: FUNCTION LOAD silently accepts duplicate function names within one library
description: Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 (functions.c::functionLibCreateFunction): a library that registers the same function name twice should be rejected with 'ERR Error registering functions: ERR Function already exists in the library'. fr's function_load loop pushed a duplicate FunctionEntry, so FUNCTION LIST would emit the function twice and FCALL silently dispatched to whichever appeared first in the wrapper script.
close_reason: Fixed in fr-store/src/lib.rs:10403 (functions.iter().any duplicate-name check) with the upstream-prefixed wording. Test pin function_load_rejects_duplicate_function_name_within_library added. 321/321 fr-store tests pass.

title: FUNCTION LOAD ignores flags field validation in table-form register_function
description: Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 (function_lua.c::luaRegisterFunction): the flags field in a table-form register_function call must (1) be a table, and (2) contain only registered flag names — accepted set in 7.2.4 is no-writes / allow-oom / allow-stale / no-cluster / allow-cross-slot-keys. fr's textual parser ignored the flags field entirely, so `flags='x'`, `flags=42`, or `flags={'bogus'}` all silently loaded the library. Upstream wordings: 'flags argument to redis.register_function must be a table representing function flags' and 'unknown flag given' (both prefixed with 'ERR Error registering functions:').
close_reason: Added extract_table_value_raw and extract_flag_strings helpers in fr-store/src/lib.rs:12695-12762; wired flags validation into function_load (must-be-table check first, then per-entry flag-name check). Test pin function_load_table_form_validates_flags covers scalar/integer flags, bogus-flag, mixed valid+bogus, all five valid flags, and empty flags table. 325/325 fr-store tests pass.

title: FUNCTION LOAD discards table-form flags, breaking FCALL_RO no-writes dispatch and FUNCTION LIST flags output
description: function_load validated table-form flags (after frankenredis-fnflags) but always pushed `flags: Vec::new()` onto FunctionEntry, so the registered flag set was discarded. Two consequences: (1) FCALL_RO's no-writes lookup at fr-command/src/lib.rs:9476 always returned false, so a function correctly declared with flags={'no-writes'} could never be dispatched via FCALL_RO — every read-only call returned 'ERR Can not execute a script with write flag using *_ro command.'. (2) FUNCTION LIST surfaced an empty flags array for every function, hiding the registered flag set from clients. Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 confirmed both issues.
close_reason: function_load now captures flags via extract_table_value_raw + extract_flag_strings at the FunctionEntry push site (fr-store/src/lib.rs:10500-10520). Test pin function_load_table_form_captures_flags_into_function_entry covers single-flag, multi-flag (preserving order), and flagless cases. 326/326 fr-store tests pass.

title: Lua setmetatable/getmetatable ignore __metatable protection
close_reason: setmetatable/getmetatable in crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs now honor the __metatable protection field: (1) getmetatable returns the __metatable field value (masking the real metatable) when present, falling back to the metatable otherwise; (2) setmetatable raises 'cannot change a protected metatable' (prefix toggled by current_invocation_name to match where(1) behavior) when the target's existing metatable has a non-nil __metatable; (3) setmetatable with only one arg now raises 'bad argument #2 (nil or table expected)' — previously fr silently coerced missing → nil and cleared the metatable. Regression test lua_metatable_protection_and_setmetatable_arg_check_fnh42 pins 7 cases including direct-vs-wrapped pcall variants. Live diff vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 on :16380 clean for all 13 probed inputs.

title: FUNCTION LOAD header validation gaps: no-newline payload + duplicate name= silently accepted
description:
Two related header-validation gaps confirmed by differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 (functions.c::functionExtractLibMetaData):

1. NO-NEWLINE PAYLOAD: A single-line payload like '#!lua name=l1' (no '\n') should surface 'ERR Invalid library metadata' upstream because the shebang is unterminated. fr previously fell through to the function-extraction loop and surfaced 'ERR No functions registered'.

2. DUPLICATE name=: A header with two name= tokens should surface 'ERR Invalid metadata value, name argument was given multiple times' upstream. fr previously silently overwrote and accepted the second value.

Upstream behavior verified at /data/projects/frankenredis/legacy_redis_code/redis/src/functions.c:904-907 and 923-927.
close_reason: Fixed in fr-store/src/lib.rs:10271 (no-newline check via find('\n')) and 10286-10295 (duplicate name= rejection via name_seen flag). Two test pins added in fr-store/src/lib.rs (function_load_no_newline_payload_returns_invalid_library_metadata + function_load_rejects_duplicate_name_argument). 320/320 fr-store tests pass.

title: fr-store: FUNCTION LOAD silently ignores unknown shebang meta tokens instead of upstream 'Invalid metadata value given' rejection
description:
fr-store::function_load at lib.rs:10282-10288 only recognizes the engine (first token) and 'name=X' tokens; any other token in the shebang line is silently ignored. Upstream functions.c rejects with 'ERR Invalid metadata value given: <token>'. Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:
  '#!lua name=lib extra_token\nbody'   -> upstream 'Invalid metadata value given: extra_token', fr loads silently
  '#!lua badkey=value name=lib\nbody' -> upstream 'Invalid metadata value given: badkey=value', fr loads silently

Fix: in the meta-token loop, after recognizing index 0 (engine) and 'name=' prefix, reject any unknown token with the upstream wording. The check fires before the LUA gate to match upstream ordering.

title: FUNCTION LOAD silently accepts table-form register_function with no function_name field
description: Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 (function_lua.c::luaRegisterFunction): a table-form invocation like `register_function{callback=fn}` or `register_function{}` should fail with 'ERR Error registering functions: ERR redis.register_function must get a function name argument'. Upstream validates function_name BEFORE callback. fr's textual parser previously fell through to extract_function_metadata, returned None, and surfaced the wrong 'No functions registered' wording when no other registrations existed in the library.
close_reason: Reordered the table-form presence checks in fr-store/src/lib.rs:10384-10412 so function_name fires first (matching upstream), then callback. Added test pin function_load_table_form_rejects_missing_function_name covering callback-only, empty-table, and description-only variants. 323/323 fr-store tests pass.

title: fr-command: FUNCTION LOAD silently accepts unknown options + empty-body libraries instead of upstream rejection
description:
Two related defects in FUNCTION LOAD parsing surfaced by differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:

(1) FUNCTION LOAD <BANANA> <code> (non-REPLACE first arg with code following) is silently mis-parsed: fr's parser at lib.rs:9020-9028 sets code_idx=2 when arg2 != REPLACE, treats BANANA as the code body, and the subsequent function_load('BANANA') fails with 'ERR Missing library metadata'. Upstream emits 'ERR Unknown option given: BANANA' before parsing the code.

(2) FUNCTION LOAD '#!lua name=lib\n' (well-formed shebang, no register_function calls) is accepted by fr, returning the library name. Upstream rejects with 'ERR No functions registered'.

Both fixes are localized: (1) reject any non-REPLACE token in the option slot with the dedicated wording before passing to function_load; (2) inside store::function_load, after the function-extraction loop, verify functions.is_empty() and return the upstream wording.

title: FUNCTION LOAD positional form silently accepts a third argument
description: Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 (function_lua.c::luaRegisterFunction): a positional call with three or more arguments like `register_function('f', function() return 1 end, 'extra')` should fail with 'ERR Error registering functions: ERR wrong number of arguments to redis.register_function'. fr previously walked past the function expression and silently ignored trailing arg(s), pushing a FunctionEntry as if the call were well-formed.
close_reason: Added positional_call_has_extra_arg_after_function helper that walks past function(params) body end via paren-balanced match for params and the keyword-block depth counter for body, then checks for a trailing comma. Wired into the positional-form validation chain after the second-arg type check. Test pin function_load_positional_form_rejects_third_argument covers the failure path plus a two-arg sanity case. 330/330 fr-store tests pass.

title: FUNCTION LOAD positional form silently accepts zero-arg and single-string-arg invocations
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 (function_lua.c::luaRegisterFunction) surfaced two arity gaps in fr's positional-form parser:
  (1) `register_function()` (zero args) → upstream 'ERR wrong number of arguments to redis.register_function'
  (2) `register_function('f')` (single string arg) → upstream 'ERR calling redis.register_function with a single argument is only applicable to Lua table (representing named arguments).'
fr fell through to extract_function_metadata in both cases, surfacing the wrong 'No functions registered' wording (zero-arg) or silently loading a body-less function entry (single-arg) that would later fail at FCALL with 'attempt to call a nil value'.
close_reason: Added zero-arg and single-string-arg arity guards in the positional-form arm of function_load (fr-store/src/lib.rs:10515 and 10550), each surfacing the upstream-prefixed wording. Test pin function_load_positional_form_arity_wordings covers both cases. 329/329 fr-store tests pass.

title: FUNCTION LOAD positional form silently accepts non-string first arg and non-function callback
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 (function_lua.c::luaRegisterFunction) surfaced two silent-acceptance gaps in fr's positional-form parser:
  (1) `register_function('f', 'not_fn')` (callback as string/number/table/nil) → upstream 'ERR second argument to redis.register_function must be a function'
  (2) `register_function(true, function() ... end)` (non-string first arg) → upstream 'ERR first argument to redis.register_function must be a string'
fr's textual parser only used extract_quoted_string on the first arg; non-quoted first args returned None (skipping the line, surfacing 'No functions registered') and string callbacks were silently accepted, pushing a FunctionEntry whose body was missing from the wrapper script.
close_reason: Added positional-form arm in function_load alongside the table-form one: when register_function is followed by `(`, parse the first arg (must be a quoted string), then check the second arg starts with `function`. Test pin function_load_positional_form_type_checks_callback_and_first_arg covers string/number callbacks, non-string first arg, and the success-path sanity case. 328/328 fr-store tests pass.

title: FUNCTION LOAD: per-function register_function name-validation error missing 'ERR Error registering functions:' prefix
description: When a register_function call inside a library uses an invalid name (e.g. 'bad-name'), upstream Redis 7.2.4 returns 'ERR Error registering functions: ERR Library names ...'. fr drops the prefix, conflating per-function and header (name=) name-validation paths. Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 confirmed divergence.
close_reason: Fixed in fr-store/src/lib.rs:10359 — per-function name-validation now emits the upstream 'ERR Error registering functions: ' prefix; existing test pin at fr-store/src/lib.rs:18108 updated.

title: Fix remaining live ZSET parity gaps in ZMPOP and ZADD conflicts
close_reason: Fixed ZMPOP/BZMPOP tuple nesting and Redis-exact ZADD conflict wording

title: FCALL/FCALL_RO fails for table-form register_function libraries — wrapper script transformation only handles positional form
description: fr-command::transform_register_function only matched the positional form `register_function('name', function(...) ... end)`, returning None for table-form `register_function{function_name='n', callback=function(...) ... end}`. The dispatch loop then SKIPPED the line entirely (continue without push), so the function body was lost from the wrapper script. FCALL/FCALL_RO on a table-form library always failed with 'ERR Error running function: attempt to call a nil value'. Discovered while validating the fnflagstore fix end-to-end via dispatch_argv.
close_reason: Extended transform_register_function to detect a `{` after the call site and delegate to a new transform_register_function_table_form helper that extracts function_name (any quote style) and callback function body using brace-depth tracking and the same coarse keyword-block depth counter used in fr-store. Test pin fcall_ro_succeeds_for_table_form_function_with_no_writes_flag exercises the full LOAD → FCALL_RO path. 837 passed (4 pre-existing stream failures only), 326/326 fr-store.

title: FUNCTION LOAD silently accepts wrong-typed values for function_name/description/callback in table form
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 (function_lua.c::luaRegisterFunction) surfaced three silent-acceptance gaps in fr's table-form validation:
  (1) function_name={'a'} → upstream 'ERR function_name argument given to redis.register_function must be a string'
  (2) description={'x'} → upstream 'ERR description argument given to redis.register_function must be a string'
  (3) callback='not_fn' → upstream 'ERR callback argument given to redis.register_function must be a function'
fr's textual parser only checked PRESENCE of these keys, not the value shape; non-conforming values would either skip metadata extraction silently or load a broken library where the callback is never callable.
close_reason: Added head-byte type checks in function_load before the unknown-field scan: a `{` head for function_name/description triggers the must-be-a-string wordings; a value not starting with `function` for callback triggers the must-be-a-function wording. All three wordings are upstream-prefixed with 'ERR Error registering functions:'. Test pin function_load_table_form_type_checks_string_and_callback_fields covers all three. 327/327 fr-store tests pass.

title: FUNCTION LOAD silently accepts table-form register_function with unknown fields
description: Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 (function_lua.c::luaRegisterFunction): a table-form invocation like `register_function{function_name='f', callback=fn, bogus='x'}` should be rejected with 'ERR Error registering functions: ERR unknown argument given to redis.register_function'. The four valid fields are function_name, callback, description, flags. fr's textual parser previously ignored unknown fields silently — even typos like `function_nme` or `flgs` would slip through.
close_reason: Added find_unknown_table_key in fr-store/src/lib.rs:12695 — walks the table body skipping strings, nested braces, and `function ... end` blocks (coarse keyword-block depth counter), then flags any top-level <ident>= whose ident isn't in the allowed set. Wired into function_load. Test pin function_load_table_form_rejects_unknown_field covers AFTER-callback, BEFORE-callback, and all-four-valid-fields cases. 324/324 fr-store tests pass.

title: fr-command/lua: EVAL error wording divergences for unknown command + script compile errors
description:
Several Lua-script error wording divergences from upstream Redis 7.2 script_lua.c:

- EVAL 'return redis.call("NOPE")' 0:
    fr 'ERR Error running script: ERR unknown command NOPE, with args beginning with: '
    redis 'ERR Unknown Redis command called from script script: <sha1>, on @user_script:1.'
- EVAL 'return redis.pcall("NOPE")' 0 (in-table form):
    fr 'ERR unknown command NOPE...'
    redis 'ERR Unknown Redis command called from script'
- EVAL '@@@' 0 (script compile error):
    fr 'ERR Error running script: unexpected character ...'
    redis 'ERR Error compiling script (new function): user_script:1: unexpected symbol near ...'

Upstream uses a fixed prefix 'Error compiling script (new function):' for compile errors and 'Unknown Redis command called from script' for unknown-command paths, plus appends script SHA1 + source location for the redis.call (not pcall) variant.

All cosmetic but visible.

title: fr-native stream loses max-deleted-entry-id on RDB persist (XDEL drift)
description:
store_to_rdb_entries (fr-runtime/src/lib.rs ~15385) builds RdbValue::Stream(entries, watermark, groups, None /*metadata*/, entries_added) and never captures the stream's max-deleted-entry-id, even though fr-store tracks it (Store::stream_max_deleted_id). The type-21 synthesizer encode_upstream_stream_listpacks3 (fr-persist/src/rdb_stream.rs) hardcodes max_deleted_entry_id ms/seq to 0. So an fr-NATIVE stream (built via XADD, never loaded from an upstream RDB, hence metadata=None) that has had entries XDEL'd loses its max-deleted-entry-id watermark on SAVE/BGSAVE/DUMP -> 0 after reload. redis loading our RDB (or fr reloading it) then reports max-deleted-entry-id 0-0, which drives XINFO STREAM/GROUPS lag+tombstone logic astray (cf h3vkq, ren6y).

Note: redis's own DUMP physically retains XDEL'd entries in listpacks (DELETED flag) until node rewrite; that exact byte layout is operation-history-dependent and NOT reproducible from live state (and not required for a valid RESTORE). The achievable, correct fix is to round-trip the max-deleted-entry-id METADATA, not the tombstone bodies.

Fix sketch: add a max_deleted_id field to RdbValue::Stream (touches the enum + ~15 construction/match sites across fr-persist, fr-runtime, fr-store, fr-conformance) OR thread max_deleted_id as a parameter through encode_upstream_stream_listpacks3 + encode_stream_rdb_value and read it from Store::stream_max_deleted_id in store_to_rdb_entries and the DUMP command path. Decoder already parses max_deleted (currently discarded in stream_parts).

Proof: end-to-end — fr XADD N, XDEL some, DUMP, RESTORE into vendored redis :17580, assert XINFO STREAM max-deleted-entry-id matches; plus a fr-persist unit test (encode with nonzero max_deleted -> decode -> round-trips). Discovered via differential probe vs redis 7.2.4 while shipping the type-21 node-packing rewrite for ren6y (132 vs 168 bytes on an XDEL stream traced to max_deleted=0 + dropped tombstones).
notes: FIXED. Added a 6th field max_deleted: Option<(u64,u64)> to RdbValue::Stream and threaded the max-deleted-entry-id watermark end-to-end: type-21 encoder/decoder (fr-persist/rdb_stream.rs) now write/parse it instead of hardcoding/discarding 0-0; the private RDB_TYPE_STREAM format carries it too (encoder+decoder, +16 bytes); store_to_rdb_entries (fr-runtime) reads Store::stream_max_deleted_id; DUMP path (fr-store) passes it; RESTORE/apply_rdb_entries path restores via Store::restore_stream_max_deleted_id. So an fr-native XDEL'd stream now round-trips max-deleted-entry-id through SAVE/BGSAVE/DUMP+RESTORE instead of resetting to 0-0. Proven: golden.rs type21_round_trips_max_deleted_entry_id_fplrm (7-3 survives, None stays None) + byte-exact-vs-redis golden still green; fr-persist/fr-store/fr-runtime full suites pass (only an UNRELATED pre-existing GETRANGE metamorphic divergence remains, filed separately). Note: redis's physical XDEL tombstone bytes in listpacks are operation-history-dependent and intentionally NOT reproduced (not required for a valid RESTORE); only the max-deleted-id metadata is round-tripped. Implemented jointly with a concurrent codex pane (fr-store/fr-runtime wiring) + this agent (fr-persist enum/encoders/decoder + proofs).

title: EPIC: Eliminate all stubs, placeholders, and hardcoded zeros from production code
description:
# Stub/Placeholder Remediation Epic

## Motivation
A comprehensive audit identified 28+ stubs, placeholders, hardcoded zeros, and incomplete implementations across the FrankenRedis codebase. While many are in fr-command (the dispatch_argv path used by Lua scripts and AOF replay), some affect the runtime directly (ACL SAVE/LOAD, LATENCY tracking). Eliminating these is critical for:

1. **Lua script compatibility**: redis.call() from Lua goes through dispatch_argv, hitting fr-command stubs instead of runtime implementations for SLOWLOG, CLIENT, etc.
2. **Monitoring tool compatibility**: INFO stats with hardcoded zeros break Redis monitoring dashboards (redis-stat, RedisInsight, Grafana).
3. **Drop-in replacement fidelity**: The project's #1 goal is full behavioral parity with legacy Redis.

## Architecture Approach
Rather than duplicating runtime logic into fr-command, we use a **shared state pattern**:
- Add tracking counters and data structures to Store (which both fr-command and fr-runtime can access)
- For runtime-only state (blocked clients, maxmemory), pass context through new Store fields that the server populates each tick
- For LATENCY, add a LatencyTracker to Store that records samples from both dispatch paths

## Execution Layers (in dependency order)
1. **Foundation**: Add stat counters and tracking structures to Store
2. **LATENCY**: Build latency sample infrastructure + wire into command execution
3. **ACL persistence**: Implement real ACL SAVE/LOAD file I/O
4. **INFO accuracy**: Wire real counters into all INFO sections
5. **Stream metrics**: Track XINFO radix-tree and deletion metadata
6. **RDB LZF**: Implement LZF decompression for full RDB compatibility
7. **DEBUG commands**: Implement real behavior for DEBUG subcommands
8. **Lua-path stubs**: Forward SLOWLOG/CLIENT from fr-command to shared state

## Success Criteria
- Zero hardcoded zeros in INFO output where real data is available
- LATENCY LATEST/HISTORY returns real samples after threshold crossings
- ACL SAVE/LOAD round-trips to disk
- redis.call("SLOWLOG", "GET") from Lua returns real slow log entries
- RDB files with LZF-compressed strings can be decoded
- All existing conformance tests continue to pass
- New conformance tests validate each fix
close_reason: done

title: INFO Memory: maxmemory/maxmemory_human/maxmemory_policy emitted in wrong position — vendored emits after used_memory_scripts_human, fr emits after used_memory_dataset_perc
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 INFO memory:

ven order:
  used_memory_dataset_perc:..
  allocator_allocated:..
  allocator_active:..
  ...
  used_memory_scripts:..
  used_memory_scripts_human:..
  maxmemory:..               <-- here
  maxmemory_human:..
  maxmemory_policy:..
  allocator_frag_ratio:..

fr order:
  used_memory_dataset_perc:..
  maxmemory:..               <-- WRONG: emitted way too early
  maxmemory_human:..
  maxmemory_policy:..
  allocator_allocated:..
  ...

Per upstream server.c::genRedisInfoString memory section, the maxmemory triple is bunched with the maxmemory-related fragmentation/eviction lines later in the section, not in the middle of the dataset/allocator block. Move it from line ~12715 (right after used_memory_dataset_perc) to right after used_memory_scripts_human at line ~12774.
close_reason: Moved maxmemory/maxmemory_human/maxmemory_policy emit point from after used_memory_dataset_perc to after used_memory_scripts_human, matching upstream server.c::genRedisInfoString. Pinned via info_memory_emits_maxmemory_triple_after_scripts_human_per_upstream.

title: Tighten LATENCY admin command semantics
close_reason: Completed

title: fuzz_config_file: directive with empty name parsed
close_reason: fixed: skip directives with empty names from empty quoted strings

title: fr-command: XSETID accepts last-id smaller than current stream top, missing upstream rejection
description:
Upstream t_stream.c::xsetidCommand validates that the provided last-id is >= the current stream top entry id. If smaller, it replies 'ERR The ID specified in XSETID is smaller than the target stream top item'.

fr-command's xsetid_cmd silently accepted any last-id, allowing the stream's last_id to regress below the top entry. This breaks the invariant that XADD '*' produces monotonically increasing IDs — after a regression, subsequent XADDs would generate IDs that conflict with existing entries.

Differential probe vs vendored redis 7.2.4:
  XADD st 100-0 f v
  XSETID st 50-0 → fr OK, redis 'smaller than the target stream top item'

Add the top-id check after option parsing.

title: AUTH user/pass does not switch session.authenticated_user when default has nopass (requirepass empty)
description:
When requirepass is empty (no global password) and the default user has nopass, vendored Redis still honours an explicit `AUTH <user> <pass>` and switches the session to that user. fr's handle_auth_command returns OK but the session reverts to default — verified empirically.

Reproduction:
  ACL SETUSER alice on '>pw' '~k*' '+get'
  (on a fresh connection)
  AUTH alice pw      -> OK  (both)
  ACL WHOAMI         -> vendored: NOPERM ('acl|whoami' denied for alice)
                     -> fr:       'default'  (session reverted)

  With CONFIG SET requirepass <some>:
  AUTH alice pw      -> OK  (both)
  ACL WHOAMI         -> NOPERM (both)  ✓

So fr's switch only sticks when auth_required() is true. Likely cause: a downstream refresh_authentication_for_server call between frames silently resets authenticated_user back to default when auth_state.auth_required() is false.

Impact:
  ACL command/key/channel enforcement (frankenredis-7ymk5) only kicks in for non-default users. So without requirepass, ACL restrictions effectively cannot be applied in fr.

Out of scope: this bead is the follow-up to frankenredis-7ymk5 — that bead removed the script_nesting_level gate from the ACL enforcement path; this bead fixes the session-bridge logic that prevents AUTH from switching users in the first place.

Test plan:
  - Connect with default credentials (no requirepass).
  - Run ACL SETUSER alice on '>pw' '+get'.
  - On a fresh connection: AUTH alice pw, then ACL WHOAMI must return 'alice' (or NOPERM on vendored, depending on alice's ACL).
  - SET k1 v on alice's session must return NOPERM.
  - On the same default-user session that ran SETUSER: ACL WHOAMI must remain 'default'.

title: COMMAND INFO emits subcommands in alphabetical order; vendored uses declaration order
description:
COMMAND INFO for parent commands with subcommands (OBJECT, FUNCTION, ACL, CLIENT, CLUSTER, etc.) emits the subcommand list in alphabetical order. Vendored Redis 7.2.4 emits them in source-declaration order (the order they appear in commands/*.json).

Reproduction:
  COMMAND INFO object
    vendored order: freq, refcount, idletime, encoding, help
    fr order:       encoding, freq, help, idletime, refcount   (alphabetical)

  COMMAND INFO function
    vendored order: list, load, flush, dump, kill, delete, restore, help, stats
    fr order:       delete, dump, flush, help, kill, list, load, restore, stats

The subcommand metadata itself (arity, flags, key specs) matches between fr and vendored — only the iteration order differs.

Scope: low-impact (cosmetic), but a real parity gap for clients that parse COMMAND INFO output positionally. Fix would require preserving each parent's subcommand declaration order in COMMAND_TABLE or a side table.

Probe sweep #117 (COMMAND INFO object/function, inline).
close_reason: completed

title: [perf] fr-persist RDB exact capacity preplanning
description: Profile-backed follow-up to rejected frankenredis-3gonq on the pass49 multidb RDB harness. Existing perf report artifacts/optimization/icywolf-perf-20260605-pass49-rdb-streaming-crc/baseline-perf-report.txt shows encode_rdb_internal 44.67%, rdb_encode_length 14.04%, rdb_encode_string 12.33%, memmove 6.13% on --dbs 4096 --entries-per-db 2 --iters 500; 3gonq rejected DB bucketing and named the next deeper primitive as RDB size preplanning/exact capacity reservation plus compact length-string emission. One lever: compute a deterministic conservative exact-ish output capacity before encode_rdb_internal appends bytes, reserve Vec capacity once, preserve the existing global stable (db,key) ordering and all emitted bytes/checksum. Baseline with rch-built fr-persist-rdb-multidb harness before edits; golden RDB sha256 must match exactly; FP/RNG unchanged; prove with fr-persist tests/fmt/clippy; keep only if paired hyperfine Score>=2.0.
close_reason: Rejected: RDB capacity preplanning preserved golden SHA 9c0b4109f8ece11500fd11e9d559c1c70cade75ca87a60ff78b890e9fc627e95 but paired hyperfine regressed baseline 170.4 ms +/- 6.0 ms vs candidate 175.3 ms +/- 8.8 ms; source hunk removed. Next primitive: fused inline RDB length/string writer.
