title: Lua parser error wording uses Rust-style debug output instead of upstream 'X expected near Y'
description:
fr's Lua parser emits Rust-debug-style error messages like
"expected LParen, got End", "unexpected token in expression: Then",
"expected name after local, got Nil". Vendored Lua 5.1 uses
"'(' expected near 'end'", "unexpected symbol near 'then'",
"'<name>' expected near 'nil'". This is purely a wording difference
but affects any test/tool that pattern-matches parser diagnostics.

Probed cases (against vendored Redis 7.2.4 / fr on :16380/:16480):

  EVAL "function f end" 0
    vendored: '(' expected near 'end'
    fr:       expected LParen, got End

  EVAL "if then end" 0
    vendored: unexpected symbol near 'then'
    fr:       unexpected token in expression: Then

  EVAL "if true do end" 0
    vendored: 'then' expected near 'do'
    fr:       expected Then, got Do

  EVAL "local nil = 1" 0
    vendored: '<name>' expected near 'nil'
    fr:       expected name after local, got Nil

  EVAL "function 1() end" 0
    vendored: '<name>' expected near '1'
    fr:       expected function name, got Number(1.0)

  EVAL "repeat end" 0
    vendored: 'until' expected near 'end'
    fr:       expected Until, got End

  EVAL "::label::" 0
    vendored: unexpected symbol near ':'
    fr:       unexpected token in expression: Colon

  EVAL "[ " 0
    vendored: unexpected symbol near '['
    fr:       unexpected token in expression: LBracket

  EVAL "elseif x then return end" 0
    vendored: '<eof>' expected near 'elseif'
    fr:       unexpected token: ElseIf

  EVAL "do" 0
    vendored: 'end' expected near '<eof>'
    fr:       expected End, got Eof

  EVAL "break" 0
    vendored: no loop to break near '<eof>'
    fr:       (silently accepted at parse time)

Fix locus: crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs Parser impl.

Strategy:
1. Update Parser::expect at line 1334 to format
   "<token_display(expected)>'? expected near '<token_display(tok)>'".
   For keyword/symbol expected tokens use single quotes around the
   display form ('=' expected near '<eof>'). For Token::Name and
   anything not a literal keyword/punct, use '<name>' as the
   expected slot.
2. Replace "expected name in for, got X" / "expected function name,
   got X" / "expected name after local, got X" / "expected name, got
   X" / "expected field name" / "expected method name" / "expected
   parameter name" / "expected name after '.'" — all become
   "'<name>' expected near '<token_display(X)>'".
3. Replace "unexpected token in expression: X" at line 1854 with
   "unexpected symbol near '<token_display(X)>'".
4. Replace "unexpected token: {:?}" at lines 2419/4711/8484 with
   "'<eof>' expected near '<token_display(t)>'".
5. Add a parse-time loop-scope tracker so `break` outside a loop
   raises "no loop to break near '<eof>'".

Test plan: pin all ~12 cases above plus regressions on valid code
that parses cleanly.

Priority 3: pure wording — doesn't change semantics, but valuable for
any tool that pattern-matches parser errors (some Redis libraries do
this for richer client-side diagnostics).
close_reason: Parser error wording in crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs now matches vendored Lua 5.1: (1) Parser::expect rewritten to use new parser_expected_near helper producing 'TOK expected near GOT' with single-quoted token-display forms; (2) all 10 'expected name in for/function name/after local/after dot/field/method/parameter name' sites route through parser_name_expected_near producing '<name>' expected near 'GOT'; (3) parse_expr's unexpected-token fallback now says 'unexpected symbol near X' instead of 'unexpected token in expression: X'; (4) the three top-of-chunk 'unexpected token' sites now say ''<eof>' expected near X'; (5) added parser-level loop_depth counter (incremented on while/repeat/for entry) so Stmt::Break raises 'no loop to break near GOT' when outside a loop, matching lparser.c::breakstat. Regression test lua_parser_errors_use_upstream_wording_i0h24 pins 18 error cases plus 3 break-in-loop regressions. Live diff vs vendored on :16380 clean across 21 probes (5 deeper parser-strategy diffs remain: bare 1 or { or (garbage) as a statement, and return-with-extra-tokens — those need parser path reshaping rather than wording-only fixes and are out of scope here).

title: Event loop integration (fr-eventloop wired to fr-server)
close_reason: Wired fr-eventloop tick planning, accept path validation, read path validation, and active expiry into TCP server

title: COMMAND DOCS BITCOUNT/BITPOS return generic placeholders
description: fr's COMMAND DOCS for BITCOUNT and BITPOS emits fallback args (key + arg2 string) instead of upstream's nested block layouts. BITCOUNT_Args has key + range block(start, end, unit oneof BYTE/BIT). BITPOS_Args has key + bit + range block(start + end-unit-block(end + unit oneof BYTE/BIT)). Source: legacy_redis_code/redis/src/commands.def BITCOUNT_Args / BITPOS_Args. Add entries in upstream_command_docs_arguments() at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:15249.
close_reason: Added explicit bitcount/bitpos entries in upstream_command_docs_arguments mirroring commands.def. BITCOUNT: key + range block(start, end, unit oneof BYTE/BIT). BITPOS: key + bit + range block(start + end-unit-block(end + unit oneof BYTE/BIT)). diff <(vendored DOCS X) <(fr DOCS X) byte-equivalent for both. Pinned with 2 new tests in command_docs_bit_range.rs. Workspace clippy clean.

title: Lua builtin bad-argument errors ignore current_invocation_name (still emit hardcoded fn name)
description:
Lua 5.1's luaL_argerror reads the function name from the active C
closure's debug info. When the closure is invoked indirectly via
pcall/xpcall, lua_getinfo("n.name") returns nil, so the error reads
"bad argument #N to '?' (...)" with no source-location prefix.
fr's setmetatable/getmetatable arms already match this via
format_builtin_argerror, which consults current_invocation_name.
But the free-function helpers lua_bad_table_arg / lua_bad_number_arg
(and the wrappers lua_table_arg / lua_required_integer_arg /
lua_optional_integer_arg) hardcode the builtin's literal name and
always emit the user_script:1: prefix. So when called via pcall the
error wrongly carries the prefix and the builtin's internal name.

Probed against vendored Redis 7.2.4 (:16380):

  local ok,e=pcall(ipairs, nil); return tostring(e)
    vendored: bad argument #1 to '?' (table expected, got nil)
    fr:       user_script:1: bad argument #1 to 'ipairs' (table expected, got nil)

  local ok,e=pcall(pairs, nil); return tostring(e)
    vendored: bad argument #1 to '?' (table expected, got nil)
    fr:       user_script:1: bad argument #1 to 'pairs' (table expected, got nil)

  local ok,e=pcall(next, nil); return tostring(e)
    vendored: bad argument #1 to '?' (table expected, got nil)
    fr:       user_script:1: bad argument #1 to 'next' (table expected, got nil)

  Also affects: pcall(table.insert,...), pcall(table.remove,...),
  pcall(unpack,...), pcall(rawset,...), pcall(rawequal,...),
  pcall(math.floor,'abc'), pcall(math.abs,'abc')

Fix locus: crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs.

1. Convert lua_bad_table_arg / lua_bad_number_arg to use the same
   pattern as format_builtin_argerror: when current_invocation_name
   is Some, prefix with user_script:1: and use the AST name; when
   None, use '?' and no prefix. The simplest signature change is
   adding `inv_name: Option<&str>` as the leading parameter,
   replacing the existing `function: &str`.

2. Update the lua_table_arg / lua_required_integer_arg /
   lua_optional_integer_arg wrappers to thread inv_name through.

3. At each callsite, replace the hardcoded function-name literal with
   self.current_invocation_name.as_deref(). The internal name is no
   longer needed — current_invocation_name carries the AST name when
   the builtin was called directly (which equals the internal name
   for unaliased calls) and is None when called indirectly.

4. Handlers that hand-roll the error string with format!("bad argument
   #N to 'X' (...)") — e.g. math.floor, math.abs, rawset's nil-key
   check, rawequal — should be routed through format_builtin_argerror
   instead.

Test plan: pin each of the divergent probes above plus regressions
where the builtin is called via AST (the prefix and name should stay).
close_reason: Threaded inv_name: Option<&str> through the bad-arg helpers in crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs. lua_bad_table_arg / lua_bad_number_arg / lua_table_arg / lua_required_integer_arg / lua_optional_integer_arg / lua_check_number / lua_check_string / lua_check_table now consult the caller-provided invocation name: Some → 'user_script:1: bad argument #N to <NAME>'; None → 'bad argument #N to ?' (no prefix). Callers pass self.current_invocation_name.as_deref(), which pcall correctly clears for indirect dispatch. Updated 55+ callsites via sed; the hand-rolled errors in rawset and rawequal now route through format_builtin_argerror for the same treatment. Regression test lua_builtin_argerror_drops_prefix_for_indirect_pcall_i18ug pins 11 cases including the AST-direct regression. Live diff vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 on :16380 clean across 17 probes.

title: Sentinel failover reconfiguration should not wait on S_DOWN replicas
description: Upstream Redis 7.2.4 sentinelFailoverDetectEnd skips replicas flagged SRI_S_DOWN when counting not_reconfigured slaves, so a failover can finish once all reachable non-promoted replicas are reconfigured. fr-sentinel::failover currently seeds FailoverContext::slaves_to_reconfig with every non-promoted replica during FailoverEvent::SlaveSelected and is_reconfiguration_complete waits for every key in that list, so an S_DOWN replica can keep completion false forever. Acceptance: selected failover context excludes S_DOWN replicas from required reconfiguration targets while still excluding the promoted replica, with regression coverage.
close_reason: Excluded S_DOWN replicas from failover reconfiguration targets like Redis sentinelFailoverDetectEnd

title: [perf] Attack LPUSH packed-list front-insert residual
description: Profile-backed child of frankenredis-ohsk5. Current post-SADD sweep (2026-06-13) shows LPUSH as the next residual: Redis 966183 req/s vs FrankenRedis 961538 req/s at P16/C50/n200k, redis/fr ~1.00x with FR slightly slower. Fresh pass must baseline LPUSH via rch-built release-perf, prove golden RESP/DUMP parity, test exactly one list/storage/event-loop lever, keep only Score>=2.0, then re-profile.
close_reason: Evidence-only closeout: fresh rch-built current-main LPUSH baseline did not show an eligible vs-upstream gap. FR hyperfine P16/C50/n1M was 1.015s +/- 0.030s; Redis same harness was 1.240s +/- 0.139s. Visible n1M throughput samples tied/noise (FR 1.028806M req/s, Redis 1.044932M req/s), and fresh n200k sweep routed the next real residual to INCR (Redis 766283 req/s vs FR 591716 req/s). No production source lever attempted.

title: [perf] fr-runtime::queue_client_tracking_invalidations:4330 — client_list_sessions() allocated per write with zero tracking clients
description: Profiling (gdb, MIXED p16): queue_client_tracking_invalidations present in 13% of samples. It early-returns only on keys.is_empty(); for any write command it then builds observed_keys (filter over client_tracking_observed_keys) AND calls self.client_list_sessions() (allocates a sessions collection) and iterates them — even when NO client has tracking enabled (the default / this RESP2 bench). LEVER: add a cheap O(1) guard — skip the whole body when client_tracking_observed_keys.is_empty() AND there are no bcast-tracking clients. The clean way is to maintain a server-level counter of tracking clients (à la Redis server.tracking_clients), updated in the CLIENT TRACKING on/off paths, and gate on (observed_keys empty && tracking_clients==0). Multi-site (enable/disable + this call site) so NOT a single-line lever; needs its own isomorphism proof (golden + tests + a CLIENT TRACKING BCAST integration test) and quiet-host measurement (vibu6).
notes: Reopened to correct stale accepted closeout: no i2v99 runtime code remains in the final tree for this pass.
close_reason: Rejected / zero-change: the final tree contains no i2v99 runtime counter or invalidation guard code, so no optimization was shipped for this bead. Prior candidate evidence was not kept in the final diff; tracker corrected to avoid claiming a code change that is absent.

title: LPUSHX/RPUSHX missing LFU frequency bump
description: LPUSHX and RPUSHX modify existing lists but don't bump LFU counter. Should call bump_lfu_freq when pushing to existing list.
close_reason: done

title: SENTINEL instance flags should mirror upstream ordering and full flag set
description: Upstream Redis 7.2.4 addReplySentinelRedisInstance emits flags in this order: s_down,o_down,master,slave,sentinel,disconnected,master_down,failover_in_progress,promoted,reconf_sent,reconf_inprog,reconf_done,force_failover,script_kill_sent,master_reboot. fr-sentinel currently emits master/slave/sentinel first and omits disconnected plus promoted/reconf/force/script/master_reboot flags, so SENTINEL MASTER/MASTERS/REPLICAS/SENTINELS expose divergent flag strings. Scope: crates/fr-sentinel/src/commands.rs; mirror upstream order and include the full modeled flag set plus link.disconnected.
close_reason: Mirrored upstream SENTINEL instance flag ordering and included disconnected/reconfig/promotion flags.

title: fr-command::client_trackinginfo_frame returns flat Array even in RESP3 — upstream emits a Map per addReplyMap
description:
/deadlock-finder-and-fixer differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 (HELLO 3):

  CLIENT TRACKINGINFO (RESP3, with HELLO 3 / -3 flag)
    upstream: 'flags off / redirect -1 / prefixes' (Map shape — redis-cli renders k v pairs)
    fr      : 'flags / off / redirect / -1 / prefixes / ' (flat Array)
  CLIENT TRACKINGINFO (RESP2)
    upstream + fr both flat Array — match.

Source: crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:13871-13885 — client_trackinginfo_frame always builds RespFrame::Array regardless of dispatch_client_ctx.resp_protocol_version.

Compare upstream networking.c::trackingInfoCommand which calls addReplyMapLen(c, 3) so the wire shape is a Map under RESP3 (just like HGETALL). fr already has the right pattern in hgetall (lib.rs:3008-3032) — reads dispatch_client_ctx.resp_protocol_version and emits Map under RESP3, flat Array under RESP2.

Fix: extend client_trackinginfo_frame to take the protocol version, return RespFrame::Map under RESP3 with three (key, value) pairs:
  ('flags' → Array of flag strings)
  ('redirect' → Integer)
  ('prefixes' → Array of bulk strings)
and the existing flat Array under RESP2. Update the fr-runtime call site at lib.rs:9397 to pass self.session.resp_protocol_version.

Severity P1 because this is a wire-protocol divergence visible to any RESP3 client (Redis Insight, redis-cli -3, lettuce, redis-rs RESP3 mode) for a frequently-introspected subcommand.
close_reason: Extended client_trackinginfo_frame to take resp_protocol_version. Returns RespFrame::Map under RESP3 (mirroring upstream networking.c::trackingInfoCommand's addReplyMapLen(c, 3)) and the existing flat Array under RESP2. Updated runtime call site (fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:9397) + fr-command call site to pass session.resp_protocol_version / dispatch_client_ctx.resp_protocol_version. Updated 3 prop test sites to pass 2 (RESP2 default). Two new pin tests: client_trackinginfo_under_resp3_returns_map_shape (HELLO 3 then asserts Map with 3 entries + correct keys + redirect=-1) and client_trackinginfo_under_resp2_stays_flat_array. clippy clean.

title: Align DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE runtime test with Redis atoi behavior
description: cargo test --workspace exposed a stale fr-runtime admin_commands_test expectation: DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE 2 was expected to error, but vendored Redis debug.c uses atoi and existing core_debug fixtures expect OK for nonzero values. Update the runtime integration test to assert the upstream-compatible OK/toggle behavior.

title: Enable mimalloc/jemalloc by default in fr-server release build (system allocator costs ~27pts parity + 70x p99 tail)
description: Gauntlet round-7 perf finding. Controlled re-baseline on a quiet rch worker (release-perf profile) shows the memory allocator is the DOMINANT perf lever: default system allocator = 36.5% geomean parity vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 with pathological p99 tails (lpop 53ms, set 3.5ms); switching to --features mimalloc = 63.6% geomean and p99 tails collapse ~70x (lpop 0.74ms, set 0.86ms). Vendored redis uses jemalloc. This explains why the README's published 71-83% parity didn't reproduce in the gauntlet's default-release baseline. RECOMMEND: (a) make the fr-server release build enable mimalloc (or jemalloc) by default [default=[] in crates/fr-server/Cargo.toml features], OR (b) document prominently that published parity numbers require --features mimalloc/jemalloc. Evidence: gauntlet workspace .bench-history/remote/. Relates to frankenredis-vibu6.
close_reason: done

The harness already exposes protocol/replay runners, FR-P2C packet-family suites, live Redis diff entry points, and broad smoke coverage. Update the Differential conformance harness row to match the implemented/tested surface without overstating always-on live-oracle coverage.

title: perf(fr-store): ZCOUNT O(range) score_bound_count instead of O(log n) rank-diff (~3.3x slower even warm)
description: Same class as 7uonw (ZLEXCOUNT, shipped). ZCOUNT 20000 80000 on a 100k zset = fr 6072 vs redis 19867 req/s (~3.3x), and stays slow even with a WARM treap because SortedSet::score_bound_count does full.ordered.range((lower,upper)).filter().count() = O(range). Redis subtracts two skiplist ranks = O(log n). Fix: when rank_tree is warm, compute count via rank-diff of the score bounds — but score bounds (ScoreBound min/max, inclusive/exclusive + +inf/-inf) are trickier than the single-score lex bounds: need rank_of(min_for_score/max_for_score sentinels or boundary ScoreMembers) honoring exclusive bounds. Fall back to scan when cold. crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs ~1276. Pattern proven in 7uonw.
close_reason: Shipped 66b7492e8 (rebased 49ef631d2): O(log n) rank-diff ZCOUNT on warm zsets via order-statistic treap, mirroring ZLEXCOUNT (7uonw). 3.99x (4955->19763 req/s on 100k). Byte-exact vs redis (72-combo differential sha256 aadb00b8...) + warm/cold/brute isomorphism test (incl ±inf/exclusive/inverted); 591 fr-store tests pass incl corrupted-sentinel guard (tree.len()==dict.len()).

title: BITFIELD_RO rejects write subcommand before validating args + skips no-op WRONGTYPE check
description: redis bitops.c::bitfieldGeneric validates EVERY op's args (type/offset/value/OVERFLOW keyword) in a full pass BEFORE rejecting BITFIELD_RO for a SET/INCRBY, and type-checks the key in the read-only branch even with no GET op. fr's bitfield_ro_cmd short-circuited on the first SET/INCRBY -> returned 'BITFIELD_RO only supports the GET subcommand' where redis returns 'Invalid bitfield type' / 'value is not an integer' / 'Invalid OVERFLOW type'; and BITFIELD_RO on a wrong-type key with no op returned empty instead of WRONGTYPE. Found via new bitfield_fuzz.py (6-12 diffs/seed). Fix: mirror bitfield_cmd's validate-all-then-act pass in fr-command.
close_reason: Fixed in b59399522 (fr-command bitfield_ro_cmd): now runs the full arg-validation pass (type/offset/value/OVERFLOW) first, returns the BITFIELD_RO write rejection only after, then peek-type-checks the key (WRONGTYPE) and executes GETs — mirroring the writable bitfield_cmd (bitfieldorder). Differential 0 diffs across 6 seeds x 600 (new bitfield_fuzz.py); targeted cases (invalid type/value/overflow on write op, later-op type error, no-op wrongtype) byte-exact; valid-arg writes still rejected. unit test bfro-order; fr-command 1137 tests pass, clippy clean.

title: fr-conformance debug_object_reports_live_metadata fixture pinned to bulk_contains_all but fr emits SimpleString after gmqk1
description:
Tick finding via /testing-conformance-harnesses on the long-standing single-failure in fr-conformance core_server.

CONTEXT (frankenredis-gmqk1, already shipped): DEBUG OBJECT now emits RespFrame::SimpleString matching upstream addReplyStatusFormat at debug.c, instead of BulkString. The wire shape is correct.

ISSUE (crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/core_server.json::debug_object_reports_live_metadata):
  expect.kind = 'bulk_contains_all' — a matcher that only succeeds against RespFrame::BulkString(Some(_)). The matcher implementation at fr-conformance/src/lib.rs lines 3063-3068 explicitly returns false for SimpleString.

Result: fr-conformance core_server has been failing on this single case for the entire post-gmqk1 window, blocking the suite from going green.

REPRO:
  cargo test -p fr-conformance conformance_core_server
  → FAILED with 'debug_object_reports_live_metadata: expected BulkString contains_all [...], actual SimpleString("Value at:0x0 refcount:1 encoding:embstr ...")'

Severity: [LOW]. Test infrastructure gap, not a server-side defect. Blocks the conformance suite from being green.

FIX:
  Add a new ExpectedFrame variant 'simple_contains_all' that matches against RespFrame::SimpleString the same way bulk_contains_all matches BulkString. Update the debug_object fixture to use the new matcher. Also extend the existing test coverage in fr-conformance/src/lib.rs.

Alternative considered: broadening BulkContainsAll to also accept SimpleString. Rejected — the matcher kind name encodes the wire shape and clients shouldn't conflate the two.
close_reason:
Added two new ExpectedFrame matcher variants to fr-conformance to bridge the gap between bulk-string fixtures and the post-gmqk1 SimpleString DEBUG OBJECT replies. fr-conformance is now 193/193 green for the first time since gmqk1 landed.

CHANGES:
- crates/fr-conformance/src/lib.rs: Added two new ExpectedFrame variants:
  - SimpleContainsAll { value: Vec<String> } — mirror of BulkContainsAll for SimpleString frames; substring match on the status payload.
  - AnySimple — mirror of AnyBulk; matches any RespFrame::SimpleString.
  Both wired into expected_to_frame and frame_matches_expected.
- crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/core_server.json: debug_object_reports_live_metadata flipped from bulk_contains_all → simple_contains_all.
- crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/core_debug.json: 16 DEBUG OBJECT fixtures (case-insensitive argv match) flipped from any_bulk → any_simple. The fixtures targeting OTHER DEBUG subcommands (e.g. JMAP, RELOAD, SLEEP) keep any_bulk because those handlers still emit BulkString.
- New regression test expected_frame_simple_contains_all_matches_simple_payload_substrings pins the matcher's positive and negative cases (also asserts a BulkString is rejected).

Verification:
- cargo test -p fr-conformance → 193/193 pass (was 191/193 with two failing core_server / core_debug tests).
- cargo clippy -p fr-conformance --lib --no-deps -- -D warnings → clean.

[LOW] severity. Test infrastructure gap, not a server-side defect — but it's been blocking the conformance suite from being green since gmqk1 shipped.

title: DEBUG CONFIG-REWRITE-FORCE-ALL returns OK; vendored returns 'ERR CONFIG-REWRITE-FORCE-ALL failed: Bad address' when no config file loaded
close_reason: DEBUG CONFIG-REWRITE-FORCE-ALL now emits 'ERR CONFIG-REWRITE-FORCE-ALL failed: Bad address' instead of OK, mirroring upstream debug.c::debugCommand line 962-967 which calls rewriteConfig(server.configfile, 1) directly and addReplyErrorFormat with strerror(errno). When server.configfile is NULL — always true for fr's purely-in-memory config — rewriteConfig hits EFAULT and strerror(EFAULT) is 'Bad address'. Note this DIFFERS from the regular CONFIG REWRITE handler which checks for NULL configfile first; DEBUG bypasses that check. Verified via differential probe — fr now byte-equal vendored. Updated the existing pin test debug_config_rewrite_force_all_drop_cluster_packet_filter_client_eviction_match_upstream to assert the error reply.

title: GEORADIUS/GEOSEARCH/GEOSEARCHSTORE must type-check key (WRONGTYPE) before parsing shape/unit/radius/count options
description: Upstream geo.c georadiusGeneric/geoSearchCommand do lookupKeyRead + checkType(OBJ_ZSET) before extracting coords/radius/unit and the option tail, so a wrong-type key yields WRONGTYPE ahead of any 'need numeric radius'/'unsupported unit'/'radius cannot be negative'/'height or width cannot be negative' option error. fr parsed options first. GEORADIUSBYMEMBER/GEOPOS/GEOHASH/GEODIST already type-check early. Found via rich_option_fuzz.py. Missing key still falls through to option parse (matches upstream NULL-zobj path). Fix: early peek_value_type WRONGTYPE check (None/ZSet pass; GEOSEARCHSTORE checks source argv[2]).

One lever only: in record_ops_sec_sample, call estimate_memory_usage_bytes only if read_rss_bytes() fails, preserving INFO/MEMORY semantics because observe_memory_sample already consumes RSS when available and logical estimate remains the fallback. See artifacts/optimization/frankenredis-a971j/PROOF.md.

title: table.getn silently returns 0 for non-table args (vendored errors)
description:
Upstream Lua 5.1 ltablib.c::getn calls luaL_checktype(L,1,LUA_TTABLE)
which raises 'bad argument #1 (table expected, got TYPE)' for any
non-table arg (including missing/nil). fr's table.getn handler
returns 0 silently when the value isn't a table.

Repro:
  EVAL "local ok,e = pcall(table.getn, nil); return tostring(ok)..':'..tostring(e)" 0
    vendored: false:bad argument #1 to '?' (table expected, got nil)
    fr:       true:0

Same pattern affects table.getn() (no args), table.getn('abc'),
table.getn(42), etc.

table.maxn already validates correctly via lua_check_table — apply
the same routing to table.getn at fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:7161.
close_reason: completed
