Paired 1M baseline 1.818s +/-0.061 vs candidate 1.825s +/-0.161 (baseline 1.00x +/-0.09 faster); reversed candidate 1.283s +/-0.020 vs baseline 1.246s +/-0.027 (baseline 1.03x +/-0.03 faster). Golden transcript sha256 7d8b2be372ee1d89095e4c8e1864d5e62505812e8118e527a1a5296fb8df906b matched; focused fr-store parity test and candidate release-perf build passed via rch. Source hunk not shipped.

title: Sentinel automatic failover should honor restart cooldown
description: Upstream Redis 7.2.4 sentinelStartFailoverIfNeeded refuses to start a new automatic failover while mstime() - failover_start_time is less than failover_timeout*2. fr-sentinel::failover::should_start_failover only checks ODOWN/state/leader and can allow immediate repeated failover attempts after a prior start. Acceptance: automatic failover is suppressed before the upstream cooldown boundary, permitted at the exact boundary, and only permits when the caller is leader or FORCE_FAILOVER is set.
close_reason: Implemented in 587d0b1

title: fr-command WAIT silently accepts extra args (upstream arity is exact 3) and lacks the noscript guard (CMD_NOSCRIPT) so Lua scripts can call WAIT
description:
## Symptom
\`WAIT 0 0 extra\` via dispatch_argv accepts the extra arg silently and returns 0; upstream rejects with the table-level wrong-arity error (commands.def declares WAIT arity=3 exact).

\`redis.call('WAIT', 0, 0)\` from Lua via dispatch_argv returns 0; upstream blocks the call with 'This Redis command is not allowed from script' (CMD_NOSCRIPT flag set in commands.def line 10777-equivalent for WAIT — see legacy_redis_code/redis/src/commands.def).

## Differential probe
Upstream commands.def declares WAIT with arity=3 (exact, positive value) and the CMD_NOSCRIPT flag. Upstream replication.c::waitCommand at line 3529 doesn't need to re-check arity because the table-level arity check fires first; CMD_NOSCRIPT is enforced in the script context before the handler runs.

fr-command's wait_cmd at fr-command/src/lib.rs:15468-15486:
- Uses 'argv.len() < 3' (allows extras silently).
- Has no script_nesting_level guard (other admin/replication-adjacent handlers like WAITAOF, FAILOVER, CLUSTER subcommands all guard via 'if store.script_nesting_level >= 1 { return Err(script_noscript_command_error()); }').

## Fix
1. Change wait_cmd signature: '(argv: &[Vec<u8>]) → (argv, store: &Store)'.
2. Update dispatch_argv call site at lib.rs:1383 to pass store.
3. Add 'if store.script_nesting_level >= 1' guard mirroring WAITAOF's pattern at lib.rs:7843.
4. Tighten arity to 'argv.len() != 3' so trailing args trigger WrongArity.
5. Existing test wait_clamps_negative_numreplicas_and_uses_timeout_specific_errors continues to pass; add a new test wait_rejects_script_context_and_extra_args covering both new branches.

## Out of scope
- WAIT-on-replica check ('WAIT cannot be used with replica instances...') requires replication state which Store doesn't expose; fr-runtime would need its own check (and already returns 0 in standalone).
- The 0-replicas always-returns-0 stub is tracked separately under project_wait_commands_stub.
close_reason: fr-command WAIT now enforces argv.len() == 3 (was < 3, silently accepting extras) and guards on store.script_nesting_level >= 1 to mirror upstream's CMD_NOSCRIPT flag — same noscript-guard pattern WAITAOF and the cluster admin handlers already use. wait_cmd takes &Store now; dispatch_argv call site updated. New test wait_rejects_script_context_and_extra_args pins both branches: extra args → WrongArity('WAIT'), Lua context → SCRIPT_NOSCRIPT_ERROR.

title: MOVE bumps the dirty counter twice (copy+del) instead of once
description: Found via differential dirty-counter audit vs vendored 7.2.4. MOVE key db is implemented as store.copy() + store.del(), each of which bumps self.dirty, so a successful MOVE bumps dirty by 2; upstream db.c::moveCommand does server.dirty++ exactly once. Diverged rdb_changes_since_last_save + auto-save threshold. Two code paths: fr-runtime::handle_move_command (network dispatch) and fr-command::move_cmd (AOF replay / Lua redis.call). Both normalized to dirty = before+1. Verified vs vendored: MOVE success dirty 1, no-op (dest exists) dirty 0; key transfer + move_from/move_to keyevents unchanged. 399 fr-runtime + 1082 fr-command tests pass; regression test added.
close_reason: FIXED + shipped (commit 9b5a6749a). Both MOVE paths (fr-runtime::handle_move_command network dispatch + fr-command::move_cmd AOF/Lua) now capture dirty before copy+del and normalize to +1, matching upstream moveCommand's single server.dirty++. Verified vs vendored 7.2.4: MOVE success dirty +1, no-op (dest exists) +0; key transfer + move_from/move_to keyevents unchanged. 399 fr-runtime + 1082 fr-command tests pass; regression test added.

title: COMMAND INFO SETEX/PSETEX/SETNX/SETRANGE emit wrong keyspec flags
description: Four single-key write commands have wrong COMMAND INFO keyspec flags in fr because the heuristic-bucket fallback runs without an override: SETEX/PSETEX emit 'RW access' (should be 'OW update'), SETNX emits 'RW' (should be 'OW insert'), SETRANGE emits 'RW access' (should be 'RW update'). Vendored commands.def declares: SETEX_Keyspecs/PSETEX_Keyspecs as CMD_KEY_OW|CMD_KEY_UPDATE, SETNX_Keyspecs as CMD_KEY_OW|CMD_KEY_INSERT, SETRANGE_Keyspecs as CMD_KEY_RW|CMD_KEY_UPDATE. Fix: add these four names to the override branch at fr-command/src/lib.rs:14846+ alongside the existing 'mset'/'msetnx'/'hset'/'zadd' entries. Note: GETKEYSANDFLAGS already emits the right flags via the separate handler at lib.rs:912-922; only COMMAND INFO's keyspec emission needs the override.
close_reason: COMMAND INFO SETEX/PSETEX/SETNX/SETRANGE now emit correct keyspec flags matching upstream commands.def. Added overrides at fr-command/src/lib.rs:14891+: 'setex'|'psetex' grouped with 'mset' under ['OW','update']; 'setnx' grouped with 'msetnx' under ['OW','insert']; 'setrange' under ['RW','update']. Live differential probe vs vendored: all 4 commands now byte-exact (diff empty). Tests: fr-command 910 pass. Full COMMAND INFO sweep across all 370 commands turned up 34 total divergences -- the other 30 are filed as separate beads to keep change diffs reviewable.

title: CLIENT TRACKING: FLUSHALL/FLUSHDB must send invalidate(null) push to tracking clients
description:
Per-key invalidations work, but FLUSHALL/FLUSHDB (and SYNC/ASYNC variants) must send each RESP3 tracking client an 'invalidate' push with a NULL payload (>2 $10 invalidate _) meaning 'evict your entire cache'. fr sends nothing, so a tracking client keeps stale cached entries after a flush. Oracle 7.2.4 sends >2
$10
invalidate
_
. Found via differential CLIENT TRACKING invalidation probe (8/9 scenarios matched; flushall_invalidates_null diverged).
close_reason: Fixed: FLUSHALL/FLUSHDB now emit an 'invalidate' push with a NULL payload (>2 $10 invalidate _) to every tracking client. Added queue_client_tracking_flush_invalidation (runtime), FLUSHALL runtime intercept mirroring fr-command flushall, empty-keys=null-flush sentinel in the frame encoder (fr-command + fr-conformance), and RESP3-aware pubsub delivery (encode_into_resp3) so the null renders as _\r\n. Byte-exact vs vendored 7.2.4 for FLUSHDB + FLUSHALL SYNC. Found via differential CLIENT TRACKING probe (8/9 matched, flushall_invalidates_null diverged). 1087 fr-runtime + 194 fr-conformance + fr-command + fr-server tests pass, clippy clean.

title: fr-protocol parse_frame_internal: chained RESP3 attribute wrappers can stack-overflow the parser — xfxtd fix removed depth budget without adding a per-chain cap
description:
REVIEW MODE finding (cross-reviewing peer commit b658bf8 / xfxtd):

  --- a/crates/fr-protocol/src/lib.rs (before xfxtd)
  +++ b/crates/fr-protocol/src/lib.rs (after xfxtd)
       b'|' => {
  -        let (_attr, consumed) = parse_resp3_map(input, next, depth + 1, config)?;
  -        parse_frame_internal(input, consumed, depth + 1, config)
  +        let (_attr, consumed) = parse_resp3_map(input, next, depth, config)?;
  +        parse_frame_internal(input, consumed, depth, config)
       }

The motive was correct (attribute is a transparent prefix; double-bumping the depth was over-conservative — the test xfxtd shipped pins parsing 1-deep arrays under max_recursion_depth=1). But removing the bump entirely leaves NO upper bound on chained attribute prefixes.

Pathological input:
  '|1\\r\\n+k\\r\\n+v\\r\\n' × N + real-frame
Each '|' triggers a recursive parse_frame_internal call at the SAME depth (no budget consumed). The Rust call stack grows linearly with N. parse_frame_internal is a hot path on the server side handling untrusted client bytes.

Severity: MEDIUM as remote-DoS. With max_recursion_depth=128 (default) the depth check at line 205 fires for nested CONTAINERS but not for sequential attribute wrappers. Default 8 MiB Rust thread stack ÷ ~100 bytes/frame ≈ 80K nested-attr depth before stack overflow. With small frames (15-byte attribute prefix above) ~80K bytes of attacker input suffices to crash the parser.

Reachability: any wire-protocol entry point that parses RESP3 client input with allow_resp3=true. The default ParserConfig has allow_resp3=false, so the impact is gated on TLS/HELLO 3 paths; still reachable via HELLO 3 from any authenticated client.

Fix: introduce a separate attribute-chain counter that's bounded independently of the recursion depth. Bump it each time the '|' branch is taken; reject when exceeding a tight cap (8 — well above any legit pattern). Preserve the existing test (xfxtd's max_recursion_depth=1 case still passes since chain_depth bumps by 1 and starts at 0).
close_reason: Added RESP3_ATTRIBUTE_CHAIN_LIMIT = 8 — separate cap on consecutive '|...' attribute prefixes. parse_frame_internal gains a fourth param (attr_chain_depth) that bumps only on the attribute branch and is reset to 0 when descending into independent child frames (parse_array elements, parse_resp3_map k/v pairs). Preserves xfxtd's depth-transparent behavior for the legitimate single-attribute case while bounding the chain-of-attrs DoS surface. Two new pin tests: chain-of-16 rejected with RecursionLimitExceeded, chain-of-7 still parses to the wrapped SimpleString. All 61 fr-protocol tests pass; clippy clean.

title: MED: tcp_sentinel_failover_integration CONFIG REWRITE errors without --config argument
description:
## Summary

`fr-server/tests/tcp_e2e_test.rs::tcp_sentinel_failover_integration` spawns replicas via `spawn_frankenredis(port, Some(primary_port))` (line 383, no --config arg), then asserts `CONFIG REWRITE` returns `SimpleString("OK")`. Actual reply is `Error("ERR The server is running without a config file")` — legitimate per upstream Redis semantics.

Exposed after frankenredis-ea1j cleared the workspace clippy gate; previously blocked by fr-command fail-fast.

## Fix direction

Switch the sentinel-failover test's `spawn_frankenredis` calls to a variant that writes a minimal tmp `redis.conf` (`appendonly no`, `dbfilename dump.rdb`, `bind 127.0.0.1`) and passes `--config <tmp>/redis.conf`. The test harness already has `spawn_frankenredis_with_config` at tests/tcp_e2e_test.rs:393 — extend it so the caller can add the `--replicaof` override for the two replicas without losing the config file.

Alternative: relax the CONFIG REWRITE assertion to accept either "OK" or the no-config error, but that weakens the conformance of this suite. Prefer the spawn-with-config approach.

Sibling test at line 2408 has the same issue and should land in the same fix.

## Not a stub

Our CONFIG REWRITE path is correct per upstream — upstream returns the same error when invoked without a config file.

## Labels

fr-server, tcp-e2e, config-rewrite, sentinel, test-env
close_reason: Fixed in c6e3090 — sentinel-failover test now spawns replicas with a tmp redis.conf so CONFIG REWRITE has a target file.

title: [perf] large-value GET write-side output path below Redis at >=64KiB
description: Successor split from frankenredis-largeval-bigbulk-zerocopy-qesp3 after SET-side closeout. Fresh gate artifact artifacts/optimization/coralox-pass200/large_value_current_20260614_1846.txt (sha256 3447f9b05141294fa596c6e1eb805ae6f2a8733d0e7fed7be0e01a7079a35b5c) shows SET mostly fixed but GET remains below Redis: 64KiB 0.76x, 256KiB 0.43x, 1MiB 0.54x. Do not repeat direct socket-write, threshold-only scatter-gather, or SET chunk-size tuning without a new profile row. First profile the current GET output path; candidate families: output-buffer ownership, vectored write of bulk header/value/trailer, per-client large-bulk send cursor, or copy-eliding response buffer layout. Acceptance: one safe-Rust lever, raw RESP golden SET/GET sha256 vs Redis, rch crate-scoped check/clippy/tests, before/after large_value_perf_gate; keep only Score>=2.0.

title: Runtime does not yet consume Lua script propagation masks
description:
Evidence: crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs and crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs now record per-command Lua propagation masks for redis.set_repl(REPL_NONE/REPL_AOF/REPL_REPLICA/REPL_ALL), but fr-runtime still records top-level EVAL/FCALL writes without consulting that metadata.

Gap: selective script propagation is now modeled in command/store state, but runtime backlog/AOF capture still needs to consume those records so replica backlog and script-side propagation control diverge less from Redis.

Real impl path: thread store.script_propagation_records through the runtime EVAL/FCALL capture path in crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs and decide how to route AOF-vs-replica destinations against the existing persistence/backlog model.
close_reason: Runtime now consumes Lua script propagation records

title: [feat] Wire write_aof_manifest_dir into the runtime AOF-write path (emit appendonlydir)
description: fr-persist::write_aof_manifest_dir (aofw1) writes a redis-7 appendonlydir (manifest + base.rdb + incr.aof), the inverse of read_aof_manifest_dir and byte-compatible with redis (verified manifest format + round-trip). It is not yet called by the runtime: fr still WRITES a single-file AOF. To complete fr->redis AOF interop, wire it into the AOF subsystem — BGREWRITEAOF / persist_snapshot_to_disk should emit the appendonlydir layout (base = store_to_rdb_entries via encode_rdb_with_functions + registered FUNCTION libs; incr = aof_records tail) and the incremental flush should append to the current incr.aof, advancing manifest seq on rewrite. Subtle: flush-cursor + multi-file interactions (see ol9tz). fr-runtime + fr-server. Foundation + round-trip test shipped.
close_reason: Wired write_aof_manifest_dir into the runtime AOF write path (commit ebda5e686). rewrite_aof_manifest() emits redis-7 appendonlydir (manifest + <base>.<seq>.base.rdb via encode_rdb_with_functions + FUNCTION libs + <base>.<seq>.incr.aof) from BGREWRITEAOF/SAVE/BGSAVE/DEBUG RELOAD; flush_aof_to_disk appends to the current incr; load_aof resolves the <path>.manifest sibling and continues the seq. LIVE INTEROP VERIFIED: vendored redis-server 7.2.4 loads fr's appendonlydir (base.2.rdb 3 keys + incr.2.aof, base_key DELeted). 421 fr-runtime lib tests pass; added bgrewriteaof_emits_redis_appendonlydir_manifest_layout. Residual: no history auto-gc (stale lower-seq files linger; manifest references only current).

title: Lua parser accepts suffix (./[/:/() on literal primaries (number/string/bool/nil)
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:

  return 1[2]          v=parser: '<eof>' expected near '['     fr=runtime: attempt to index a number value
  return 1.0[2]        v=parser: '<eof>' expected near '['     fr=runtime: attempt to index a number value
  return true[1]       v=parser: '<eof>' expected near '['     fr=runtime: attempt to index a boolean value
  return nil[1]        v=parser: '<eof>' expected near '['     fr=runtime: attempt to index a nil value
  return 'a'[1]        v=parser: '<eof>' expected near '['     fr=(empty output - runtime nil)
  return true.foo      v=parser: '<eof>' expected near '.'     fr=runtime: attempt to index a boolean value
  return nil.foo       v=parser: '<eof>' expected near '.'     fr=runtime: attempt to index a nil value
  return 1()           v=parser: '<eof>' expected near '('     fr=runtime: attempt to call a number value
  return true()        v=parser: '<eof>' expected near '('     fr=runtime: attempt to call a boolean value
  return nil()         v=parser: '<eof>' expected near '('     fr=runtime: attempt to call a nil value
  return 'a'()         v=parser: '<eof>' expected near '('     fr=runtime: attempt to call a string value
  return 'abc':upper() v=parser: '<eof>' expected near ':'     fr=ABC                                          (!!)

Upstream Lua 5.1 grammar (lparser.c::primaryexp + suffixedexp):
  prefixexp ::= var | functioncall | `(' exp `)'
  primaryexp -> Name | `(' expr `)'    (these are the ONLY suffixable primaries)
  simpleexp -> NUMBER | STRING | NIL | TRUE | FALSE | ... | constructor | FUNCTION body
  (simpleexp values are NOT routed through suffixedexp.)

fr's parse_primary at lua_eval.rs ~line 2017 conflates simpleexp (literals) with prefixexp, so parse_suffixed_expr accepts any literal as a suffix base. Worst case: `return 'abc':upper()` actually RUNS on fr and returns 'ABC' — semantically permissive but a real divergence.

Fix: split the parser so suffix loop only runs when the primary was Name or paren-wrapped expression. Bare literals exit immediately and bubble up to the top-level chunk check, which then emits `'<eof>' expected near '<suffix-token>'` via the existing parse_block-after-return check.

NOTE: the existing fr behavior of `'abc':upper()` returning 'ABC' suggests fr's string metatable has __index='string', which is the canonical Lua 5.1 behavior — but upstream rejects it at parse time because 'abc' is a simple expr, not a prefix expr.
close_reason: Split parse_primary into a new parse_primary_with_kind helper that returns the expression plus a 'suffixable' boolean. Only Name and parenthesized expressions match upstream's primaryexp production and can carry suffixes; everything else (Number/String/Bool/Nil/table/function/varargs) is simpleexp and exits the suffix loop immediately. Combined with the parse_block-after-return change (frankenredis-yunl8), trailing suffix tokens on literals now surface at the chunk-level <eof>-expected check. Regression test lua_parser_rejects_suffix_on_literal_primaries_oee7k pins 12 invalid cases + 5 sanity-check valid cases. Full release test suite (1011 tests) still passes. Live-diff vs vendored 16380: 15/15 cases match byte-for-byte.

title: SYNC reply has spurious protocol error: 'got \r as reply type byte'
description:
Differential probe vs vendored redis 7.2.4:

  vendored SYNC:
    Full resync with master, discarding 313 bytes of bulk transfer...
    Full resync done. Logging commands from master.
    Entering replica output mode...
    (then PING commands from master loop)

  fr SYNC:
    Full resync with master, discarding 370 bytes of bulk transfer...
    Full resync done. Logging commands from master.
    Error: Protocol error, got "\r" as reply type byte
    Entering replica output mode...

After the RDB bulk transfer completes, fr emits a bare CRLF that
redis-cli (when used as a debug replica via SYNC) interprets as
the start of a malformed reply. The two messages get out of order
and redis-cli reports a protocol error before entering output
mode.

Severity: P4 deferred — SYNC is replica-only protocol, real
replicas accept arbitrary subsequent bytes. The 'protocol error'
is a redis-cli pretty-printing artifact when used as a debug
client. Actual replica connections still function. Real fix
requires auditing fr's post-RDB-transfer output framing to
match upstream's exact byte sequence.
close_reason: completed

title: fr-runtime: CLIENT LIST ID rejects 0/negative client IDs as 'Invalid client ID' instead of empty match
description:
Upstream networking.c::clientCommand parses CLIENT LIST ID values via getLongLongFromObjectOrReply with the 'Invalid client ID' wording — only non-numeric input errors. Numeric values that don't match any live client (including 0 and negative) silently filter out: lookupClientByID returns NULL and the reply is empty.

fr-runtime's handle_client_list strictly required parsed_id > 0, returning 'ERR Invalid client ID' for 0 / negative ids.

Differential probe vs vendored redis 7.2.4:
  CLIENT LIST ID 0    → fr Invalid, redis empty
  CLIENT LIST ID -1   → fr Invalid, redis empty
  CLIENT LIST ID abc  → both Invalid (regression-protected)

title: fr-persist: fuzz_aof_decoder corpus has only happy-path seeds; no smoke test, no truncated/malformed inputs
description:
fuzz/corpus/fuzz_aof_decoder/ ships 14 corpus seeds, all valid AOF records (set_cmd, get_cmd, hset_cmd, xadd_cmd, multi_sequence, etc.). decode_aof_stream's interesting failure modes — truncated arrays mid-record, oversized array-length declarations, CRLF inside a bulk payload, non-RESP prefix bytes, recursion-depth bombs — have zero corpus coverage so libfuzzer must discover them from scratch.

Additionally fr-persist has no equivalent of fr-protocol's fuzz_corpus_smoke.rs, so corpus regressions (e.g. a future cleanup that empties the corpus dir) wouldn't show up under regular `cargo test`.

Plan in this diff:

1. Add 8 hostile-input seeds covering the AOF decoder's main error
   surfaces:
     malformed_array_len_overflow      *9999999999\r\n... (over AOF_MAX_ARRAY_LEN cap)
     malformed_bulk_len_overflow       $9999999999\r\n... (over AOF_MAX_BULK_LEN cap)
     truncated_array_header            *3\r\n  (no body)
     truncated_bulk_header             $5\r\n  (no body)
     truncated_bulk_body               $5\r\nab  (premature EOF)
     missing_crlf_after_bulk_len       $3\r\nfoo  (no trailing \r\n)
     non_resp_prefix                   ?bad-prefix

   size >= 14.

new seeds present.

Both pass; clippy clean. Sister to cwx6 (fr-protocol fuzz_resp_parser corpus).

title: [perf] Close the ~2x vs-redis pipelined (P16) per-command CPU gap
description:
Measured (release, mimalloc, taskset-pinned, P16/300k): fr is ~1.6-2.0x SLOWER than vendored redis 7.2.4 in pipelined mode where syscalls amortize and per-command CPU dominates — GET 1.96x, SET 2.01x, INCR 1.95x, ZADD 1.80x, MSET 1.82x, HSET 1.56x, LPUSH 1.62x, SADD 1.67x. (Single-client P1 gap is only ~1.1-1.2x = IO-bound.) This is the headline vs-upstream perf gap.

perf profile (pipelined GET) is FLAT — per-command overhead spread across many small costs, not one hotspot. Identified levers:
- command_table_index SipHash lookup (~3% CPU) — FIXED first step: switched to foldhash (frankenredis-cmdidxhash). It is also called MULTIPLE times per command (command_key_indexes, command_key_references, dispatch, ACL) each re-hashing + re-lowercasing into a [u8;64] — compute the index ONCE per dispatch and thread it through (bigger structural win).
- per-command ClientSession::clone (1.33%) at fr-runtime ~L5382: every command clones the full session into client_sessions (only read by CLIENT LIST of OTHER clients). Update lazily / store lighter snapshot.
- SplitWhitespace ~2.35%, from_utf8 ~1.0%, acl_permission_error_for_argv ~1.13% per command.
- The structural endpoint: per-command alloc->0 + fewer metadata lookups + zero-copy reply writing (RespFrame->output buffer; frozen 5r1ry). IcyWolf owns the borrowed-parser half. Target: close to <=1.1x (Score~2.0).

comments.author: SlateOtter
comments.text: Fresh symbol-resolved profile (b9ca5f5fd scripts/profile_hot_path.sh: --release with strip=false+debug=1 retrieves a symboled binary; profiling was blocked before this). SET -P16 ~700k req/s, fr-code self-time: drop_glue::<fr_store::Value> ~17% (free of OLD value on every overwrite — concrete byte-exact lever: reuse+truncate the existing Vec<u8> when string overwrites string instead of free+alloc, also kills canonical_string_value_from_slice ~8%); memcmp ~9% (hashmap key probe); clock_gettime+Instant::now+elapsed ~6-7% (multiple time reads/cmd); estimate_entry_memory_usage_bytes ~1%/write (eager->lazy). Profile FLAT (max ~17%) -> 2x needs batched-dispatch rewrite; drop_glue value-buffer-reuse is the biggest single concrete lever.

comments.author: SlateOtter
comments.text: PREMISE NOW STALE — gap measured CLOSED (SlateOtter, scripts/perf_gap_dashboard.sh @5c64e29eb). redis-benchmark -P16 -c50 best-of-3, back-to-back same host (contended), fr=DEBUG-SYMBOLED binary (pessimistic lower bound). redis/fr ratio (<1.0=fr faster): set 0.75x, get 0.83x, incr 0.98x, lpush 0.96x, rpush 1.02x, sadd 0.99x, hset 1.13x, zadd 0.89x, spop 0.90x. fr is parity-or-FASTER on 7/9 (GET ~1.2x, SET ~1.3x faster). The '1.6-2.0x slower' headline is gone. ONLY residual outlier: HSET 1.13x (one narrow per-command lever, ~13% not 2x). Recommend: re-baseline on a quiet host with the SHIPPED (stripped) release for publishable absolute Score, then this bead can likely be reframed to 'HSET residual' or closed. Last self-time profile (b9ca5f5fd): drop_glue::<Value> ~17% on SET overwrite is the value-buffer-reuse lever if pushing further.

comments.author: SlateOtter
comments.text: HSET residual partly addressed (be61bc3d2): CommandHistogramTracker::record_canonical_with_kind allocated a String per non-get/set command via entry(command.to_string()); now get_mut-first, alloc only on first occurrence. Profile (HSET) showed the histogram path ~7% self-time; get/set were fast-pathed (why they already beat redis, HSET didn't). Byte-exact (identical INFO commandstats, 596 fr-store tests). Isolated before/after ~1.03x HSET (flips to fr-faster), but end-to-end is noise-dominated on the contended host. DEEPER HSET-specific residual still open: IndexMap::insert_full ~9.7% self — fr stores small hashes as IndexMap<Vec,Vec> (3 allocs per new 1-field hash) vs redis listpack (1 packed alloc); a packed small-hash representation is the next lever but is a large change (listpack packing was a 0.93x REGRESSION for lists, so measure carefully). Re-baseline on a quiet host.

comments.author: SlateOtter
comments.text: Alloc-elimination floor-work cont'd (4f35e79b9): internal_entry get-or-insert now borrows the key + takes a lazy FnOnce default, so the common existing-key collection write (repeated HSET/SADD/ZADD) allocates NOTHING (was: eager key.to_vec() + boxed empty collection, both thrown away on key-exists; insert cloned key twice). All 6 sites. Byte-exact (596 tests, clippy clean). Like the histogram fix (be61bc3d2), the end-to-end delta is BELOW this contended host's noise floor (~±1-3%); the eliminated allocs are <1% of per-op cost. PIVOT per NO-CEILING: the alloc-micro-lever well is dry (each is real floor-work but sub-noise). The remaining HSET-specific residual is structural: IndexMap::insert_full ~9.7% self — fr stores small hashes/sets/zsets as IndexMap/Box (heap structure + per-element Vecs) where redis uses a single packed listpack. NEXT BIG SWING = a packed/inline small-collection representation (succinct data structure; alien-graveyard memory-layout primitive), target the ~9.7% insert_full + the per-element allocs. CAVEAT: large + risky (list packing was a 0.93x regression), and UNMEASURABLE on this noisy host — needs a quiet-host baseline + taskset pinning first. Recommend reframing ohsk5 to 'small-collection packed repr' or closing (gap is already closed: fr parity-or-faster everywhere).

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
PROFILE + BENCH EVIDENCE (perf, paranoid=1, release symbol-bearing binary via profile_hot_path.sh; P16 c50 r100k 1M req):
  cmd    fr_rps     redis_rps   ratio
  get    1011122    997009      1.01x  (parity+)
  set    801925     809717      0.99x  (parity)
  incr   749625     813008      0.92x  (fr -8%)
  lpush  912409     946074      0.96x  (fr -4%)
  sadd   830565     885740      0.94x  (fr -6%)
  hset   757576     767460      0.99x  (parity)
The '~2x pipelined gap' in this bead's title is GONE — fr is at parity on get/set/hset and within 4-8% on incr/sadd/lpush. No 2x gap remains anywhere.

SET P16 perf-record self-time (DSO=frankenredis): canonical_string_value_from_slice 23.7% (THE hot spot) but it is MANDATORY/IRREDUCIBLE work — disasm confirms it = parse_i64 int-encoding check (redis-correct tryObjectEncoding) + copy value bytes into inline SmallStr (required; read buffer is reused) + Value construct. Other self-time: keyspace foldhash+memcmp ~14%, drop_if_expired callees ~12% (mostly clock reads: clock_gettime 2.7% + Instant::now 3.0% + elapsed 2.3% ~= 8% — candidate: cache mstime once per event-loop iteration like redis server.mstime, ~1.09x ceiling), socket write ~8%. NO single lever clears Score>=2.0 (largest is ~1.3x and the work is mandatory). The residual incr/sadd/lpush gaps are per-command bookkeeping (this bead's domain). Closing them is sub-2.0 micro-work, not a 2x algorithmic swing; the only 2x-class lever left is architectural (batched IO / event-loop restructure), blocked by writer-pool owned-bytes + io_uring needing unsafe (forbidden).

title: [perf] HSET packed-hash single-probe overwrite path
description: Profile-backed pass89 child for the standard request CPU gap. Current-main RCH build /tmp/codex-fr-pass89-current-target/release-perf/frankenredis (sha256 aacdb33dec1c69d267c9b984584e93ba59a3e74dd5153e933ed1c8940d3a24e8) shows standard P16 sweep HSET as the largest current Redis gap: fr 511717.53 ops/sec vs Redis 730918.21 ops/sec, ratio 0.7001, p99 10679us vs 2031us. Follow-up HSET P16/1M profile: 596739.56 ops/sec, p99 2557us, zero lost samples. Flat profile top user symbols: Store::internal_entry 10.79%, Runtime::refresh_store_runtime_info_context 6.13%, PackedStrMap::insert 4.65%, foldhash Vec key hash 4.22%, vdso time 2.96%, PackedStrMap::locate 2.26%, parse_command_args_borrowed_into 1.74%. Code inspection: Store::hset does drop_if_expired, entries.contains_key for LFU, internal_entry does another contains_key and get_mut, then HashFieldMap::insert does contains_key plus PackedStrMap::insert/locate scans to decide is_new and overwrite. Alien-graveyard route: data-structure probe-path/layout primitive (§7.7 Swiss tables / §7.2 cache-oblivious data structures contracts): profile probe paths and allocation churn, then remove redundant probes without changing observable ordering. One lever only: collapse HSET existing-key and packed-map existing-field overwrite to a single entry/field probe where possible, preserving hash insertion order, LFU/touch/write dirty counters, one-way hash encoding promotion, field TTL semantics, digest stale behavior, error ordering, and RESP replies. Baseline via hyperfine/RCH before edit; prove with golden HSET/HGET/HGETALL/OBJECT ENCODING/DEBUG DIGEST transcript sha256 and focused fr-store tests; rebench HSET P16 with reversed confirmation. Keep only Score>=2.0; otherwise remove source hunk and record rejection.
close_reason: Rejected: HSET packed-hash single-probe candidate failed Score>=2.0. Baseline hyperfine HSET P16/300k 594.5ms +/- 26.2ms; candidate 614.1ms +/- 37.7ms; paired samples neutral/slightly worse (baseline 567788.21 ops/sec, candidate 567447.29 ops/sec). Golden transcript unchanged sha256 5bfc6446d8456bf1a2610a02eda03a88a3786b58bb11606bf9f98346931fa773; RCH cargo test -p fr-store hset passed. Source hunk removed; evidence under artifacts/optimization/frankenredis-ohsk5.1/. Next route: re-profile and attack zero-copy RESP/command packet or arena/slab batching, not packed-HSET micro-probes.

title: [perf] Bypass full integer canonicalization for nonnumeric SET payloads
description: Pass113 profile-backed target after direct borrowed SET parser keep. Fresh post-profile SET P16/C50/3M on c3d13bfda shows fr_store::canonical_string_value_from_slice 5.99% self, Store::set_plain_borrowed 1.34%, process_buffered_frames 1.17%, execute_plain_set_borrowed 0.91%, plain_borrowed_default_key_write_allows 0.91%, foldhash hash_one<&[u8]> 0.78%. Workload uses fr-bench default nonnumeric x... SET payloads with decimal suffixes, so the measured top row is wasted Redis integer-encoding detection before copying the string. Candidate primitive: algebraic normalization/prefilter for canonical i64 shape so values that cannot be Redis integer encodings go directly to SmallStr::from_slice while numeric, empty, leading-zero, negative, overflow, object-encoding, digest, ordering, expiry, FP, and RNG semantics stay byte-identical. Graveyard route: §7.7 metadata/probe discipline and §7.9 allocation discipline; do not repeat pass110 positive short integer parser micro-family, pass111 static OK reply, or pass112 direct SET packet parser. Baseline via rch/hyperfine first; keep only Score>=2.0 with golden sha256 proof.

title: [perf] HSET packed-hash single-locate insert API
description: Profile-backed pass90 child after frankenredis-ohsk5.1 rejection. Current HSET P16 profile from artifacts/optimization/frankenredis-pass89-reprofile/current shows Store::internal_entry 10.79% flat, PackedStrMap::insert 4.65%, foldhash Vec-key hashing 4.22%, PackedStrMap::locate 2.26%, with HSET broad sweep at fr 511717.53 ops/sec vs Redis 730918.21 ops/sec. Code inspection shows the deeper primitive remains: HashFieldMap::insert on Packed first scans with p.contains_key for promotion and then scans again in PackedStrMap::insert; the HSET call site also scans to compute is_new. One structural lever only: add a HashFieldMap/PackedStrMap insert API that returns the new-field bit while locating a packed field once and preserving insertion order, overwrite position, promotion thresholds, one-way hashtable promotion, digest/touch/LFU behavior, HSET integer replies, OBJECT ENCODING, DEBUG DIGEST, and wrongtype ordering. Baseline via hyperfine before edit; prove with byte-exact HSET transcript sha256 and focused fr-store tests; keep only if Score>=2.0.

Prior single-probe internal_entries_insert, lazy expiry, runtime-info deferral, ACL/metadata gates, and parser scratch levers were rejected. One lever: switch fr-server's event-loop HashMap/HashSet surfaces (client/token/write/blocked/paused/deferred registries and helpers) from std SipHash to foldhash::quality, preserving per-client FIFO and command reply bytes while reducing hot token/map hashing. Requires rch baseline/rebench, golden sha256, crate-scoped check/clippy/tests, Score>=2.0.
close_reason: Rejected foldhash event-loop token maps: isolated baseline/candidate at c85f25527 preserved byte-exact RESP golden sha256 cd37cbcdc1c44b04bcd11c2644c6ed0233cbc87eca53c9edfab159e9d8a748f3, but paired SET P16/300k tied (baseline 0.5304s +/-0.0145, candidate 0.5290s +/-0.0189, 1.00x +/-0.05) and reversed SET P16/1M tied (candidate 1.6703s +/-0.0469, baseline 1.6732s +/-0.1010, 1.00x +/-0.07). Score <2.0; production hunk removed. Security note: foldhash would also reduce HashDoS resistance for client-controlled blocked-wake keys, so no keep without clear perf win. Evidence retained under artifacts/optimization/frankenredis-ohsk5.6/. Next route: stop event-loop hasher swaps and attack a deeper reply/output batching or keyspace layout primitive only after fresh profile support.

title: [perf] Profile PING_INLINE epoll-vs-io_uring reactor frontier
description:
Pass192 evidence (`frankenredis-ohsk5.59`) confirmed only one fresh-server residual: PING_INLINE P16/C50/n500k Redis 1,018,329 req/s vs FrankenRedis 854,700 req/s, redis/fr 1.19x. GDB sampled the main thread in epoll_wait and writer workers parked; strace on PING_INLINE n300k showed sendto=18,751 and recvfrom=18,802, already roughly one syscall per P16 batch. Do not repeat parser, command-packet, list-push, or INCR micro-levers without a new code hotspot.

Goal: establish whether a safe-Rust, feature-gated io_uring/reactor wedge can beat the current epoll/syscall frontier while preserving serial RESP order. Baseline current main via RCH release-perf first; capture PING_INLINE/PING_MBULK/GET adjacency numbers and syscall counts. Only attempt one implementation lever if feasibility evidence names a concrete source surface with Score >= 2.0.

Proof requirements: inline PING golden SHA256, mixed PING/SET/GET serial-order replay, connection close/error replay, epoll fallback on unsupported io_uring or queue exhaustion, no changed default behavior unless gated. Graveyard mapping: io_uring batching (§15.8), region/slab buffer ownership (§5.10). Target: at least 1.10x faster PING_INLINE vs current FR with no PING_MBULK/GET regression.
close_reason: Evidence-only closeout: current-main PING_INLINE residual is 1.103x while PING_MBULK/GET are fr-faster; strace shows batch-shaped send/recv and no Score>=2.0 Rust source lever.

title: [perf] Pass194 current-main cold-command sweep after borrow fast paths
description: After pass193 closed the PING_INLINE reactor lane evidence-only and current main gained the BITPOS no-range borrow fast path, acquire a fresh current-main P16/C50 sweep focused on recent borrow/cold-command surfaces plus a standard adjacency row. Baseline via RCH release-perf first from clean detached HEAD. Commands to include: PING_INLINE/PING_MBULK/GET for adjacency, and MEMORY USAGE, OBJECT ENCODING/REFCOUNT, BITCOUNT, BITPOS, DBSIZE, COMMAND COUNT, HSTRLEN, XLEN, EXPIRETIME/PEXPIRETIME if supported by the local harness. Only attempt one source lever if a fresh gap is >=1.20x and syscall/GDB evidence names a concrete parser/runtime/store/output surface with Score >=2.0. Otherwise close evidence-only and route to the next alien primitive. Required proof for any lever: raw RESP golden sha256, commandstats/keyspace parity where applicable, crate-scoped RCH gates, paired re-benchmark, then re-profile.
close_reason: Kept exact uppercase inline PING fast path: PING_INLINE +20.2-22.0% same-worker paired/reversed; Redis golden TCP replay matched; fmt/check/unit/clippy gates passed.

title: [perf] Pass199 current P16 residual and parser/event profile
description: Fresh current-main profile after OBJECT REFCOUNT keep and SCARD/HSTRLEN/OBJECT ENCODING residual invalidation. Measure standard and extended P16/C50 throughput against vendored Redis using the current release-perf binary, then select a non-overlapping parser/event/store target only if the measured redis/fr gap and profile evidence can score >=2.0. No source edits without baseline, golden SHA256, isomorphism proof, and re-benchmark.

description: Current pass211 custom C-client sweep on pushed main c9e67f54c found ZCARD as the clearest unowned residual: redis 1086956 req/s, fr 970873 req/s, Redis/fr 1.12x. Existing PlainCardinalityCmd fast path is present, but the b'Z' dispatch bucket checks ZADD/ZINCRBY/ZPOP recognizers before ZCARD. Test one lever: hoist borrowed_plain_cardinality_args ahead of those Z write/pop recognizers. Preserve exact ZCARD replies, wrongtype/missing behavior, keyspace stats, LFU/LRU touches, and do not regress adjacent ZADD in paired confirmation.

Current custom sweep suggested ZCARD Redis/fr 1.12x, but focused rch-built current-main baseline measured Redis 1006711 req/s vs FrankenRedis 1102941 req/s (Redis/fr 0.913x, FR faster). No source edit made; do not hoist ZCARD without fresh focused evidence.

title: [perf] GET borrowed bulk reply avoids Value::string_owned clone
description:
Pass 96 profile-backed target from current main 40d6fde22. Baseline artifacts: artifacts/optimization/boldfalcon-pass96-current-20260609/baseline-set-get-hset-p16-300k-hyperfine.json and baseline-hset-p16-300k-hyperfine.json. GET P16/300k baseline: 547.2 ms +/- 42.9 ms, 10 runs. Fresh GET P16/1M perf profile: Value::string_owned 7.95% self, __memmove_avx_unaligned_erms 6.08%, Runtime::refresh_store_runtime_info_context 4.41%, Store::get 2.80%, parse_command_args_borrowed_into 1.69%. Prior event-loop foldhash maps pass95 was proof-clean but bench-tied and rejected; do not repeat hasher swaps. Prior HSET mutation capsules and SET expiry/index guards also rejected.

One lever only: implement a borrowed GET bulk-reply fast path that preserves the canonical GET semantics while avoiding cloning the stored string into RespFrame::BulkString before response encoding. Preserve nil/wrongtype replies, lazy expiry behavior, LFU/touch semantics, commandstats/slowlog/latency/accounting, output limit behavior, RESP2/RESP3 bytes, ordering, tie-breaking, floating-point/RNG invariants. Required proof: byte-identical golden transcript sha256 for GET hit/miss/wrongtype/RESP3 null plus crate-scoped tests/checks/clippy, then same-worker or directly comparable hyperfine re-benchmark. Keep only if Impact x Confidence / Effort >= 2.0.
close_reason: Completed Pass 96: kept profile-backed GET borrowed bulk reply. Baseline current main 40d6fde22 GET P16/300k 0.5472s +/- 0.0429; profile showed Value::string_owned 7.95% and __memmove 6.08%. Lever adds borrowed bulk-slice encoder, Store::get_string_bytes, Runtime::execute_plain_get_borrowed_into, and server FastEncodedReply for strict borrowed GET only. Golden raw TCP transcript hit/miss/wrongtype/empty/binary/integer/RESP3 nil matched baseline exactly, sha256 bf011f8135d36ad1b738535223a228243a24a52b8a87365951754d85680cca7a. Paired GET P16/300k baseline 0.5467s +/- 0.0238, candidate 0.5138s +/- 0.0198 (1.06x +/- 0.06). Paired GET P16/1M baseline 1.484s +/- 0.038, candidate 1.454s +/- 0.029 (1.02x +/- 0.03). Score 2.25, keep. Validation: crate-scoped check/clippy/fmt/focused tests green; fr-conformance library/live-oracle passed, package binary fingerprint failure confirmed pre-existing on clean baseline; UBS nonzero only broad pre-existing inventories with embedded gates clean. Post-change profile removes Value::string_owned; next target shifted Store::get_string_bytes / runtime-info refresh / key lookup.

title: [perf] Lazy client-memory aggregate refresh for MEMORY/INFO
description: Profile-backed narrow lever under frankenredis-ohsk5. Repeated P16 profiles show Runtime::refresh_client_memory_aggregates in the hot path (recent post102 SET P16/1M: 0.75%; prior GET/SET profiles up to ~1.5%). Baseline current origin/main first, then test one lever: mark client memory aggregates dirty on session add/update/remove and recompute only when MEMORY STATS or INFO reads consume them. Proof obligations: MEMORY STATS clients.normal/clients.slaves and INFO memory must observe current values after connect/update/remove; no command ordering/reply/tie/RNG/FP changes; golden RESP sha unchanged. Keep only if paired/reversed hyperfine clears Score>=2.0; otherwise remove hunk and close rejected.
close_reason: Rejected: clmemlazy preserved golden RESP sha 88e0947c283e4236deab7ed211d7dc5179f98c16017177d00e2ed5e09dbcf7c8 but failed the keep gate. SET P16/300k paired candidate only 1.02x +/- 0.07; reversed 1.32x +/- 0.25 was too noisy; SET P16/1M confirmation baseline 1.375s +/- 0.022s vs candidate 1.422s +/- 0.066s, baseline 1.03x +/- 0.05 faster. Source hunk removed; evidence in artifacts/optimization/frankenredis-clmemlazy/.

title: [perf] SET value/key path after writer-pool shift
description: Post-pass109 writer-pool reprofile on SET/P16/C50/3M: throughput 1178056.8112407476 ops/sec, p50 637us, p95 972us, p99 1417us, 14809 perf samples, 0 lost. Main user-space self hotspots: fr_store::canonical_string_value_from_slice 3.27%, Store::set_plain_borrowed 3.04%, foldhash::quality::RandomState::hash_one::<&[u8]> 2.11%, ServerState::run_active_expire_cycle 1.61%, Runtime::plain_borrowed_default_key_write_allows 1.34%. Scope: crates/fr-sentinel only; emit bulk-string integer values and update debug tests.

title: fr-command: ZADD with odd score/member tail emits wrong-arity instead of syntax error

Upstream t_zset.c::zaddGenericCommand emits 'ERR syntax error' instead. Caught by ad-hoc differential probe (e.g. ZADD z BAD 1 m: fr 'wrong arity', redis 'syntax error').

title: Fix ACL admin parity gaps exposed by live Redis harness
description: The new core_acl live differential harness exposed remaining mismatches versus vendored Redis in ACL LIST default-user serialization, ACL GETUSER map shape/details, ACL CAT categories and unknown-category wording, ACL subcommand wrong-arity wording, ACL GENPASS extra-args wording, and a stateful ACL USERS lowercase sequence. Use the new ACL live harness in smoke.rs as the oracle and preserve its stable passing subset.

title: Implement SENTINEL CONFIG GET/SET parity
description: Redis 7.2.4 sentinel.c exposes SENTINEL CONFIG GET/SET for global Sentinel options. fr-sentinel dispatch has no CONFIG subcommand, so Sentinel clients cannot read/update resolve-hostnames, announce-hostnames, announce-ip, announce-port, sentinel-user, sentinel-pass, or loglevel with upstream-shaped validation and duplicate handling. Scope: crates/fr-sentinel only.
close_reason: Completed

title: BLMPOP silently skips wrongtype keys instead of returning WRONGTYPE
description:
fr's BLMPOP swallows StoreError::WrongType during its per-key iteration via , treating a wrongtype key as if it were empty/missing. Vendored Redis 7.2.4 returns WRONGTYPE immediately, matching LMPOP's behavior.

Root cause: fr-command/src/lib.rs:22667-22670 in blmpop():

  let llen = match store.llen(key, now_ms) {
      Ok(n) => n,
      Err(_) => continue,    // <-- swallows WrongType
  };

LMPOP at lib.rs:12881 correctly fires WRONGTYPE:

  Err(StoreError::WrongType) => {
      return Err(CommandError::Store(StoreError::WrongType));
  }

Upstream t_list.c::lmpopGenericCommand (used by both LMPOP and BLMPOP via blmpopGenericCommand) calls checkType(c,o,OBJ_LIST) on each key and returns WRONGTYPE on the first wrongtype encounter.

Probe sweep #65 (probe130.sh) confirmed the bug class. The siblings all work correctly:
  vendored BLPOP strk 0.001   -> WRONGTYPE   (matches fr)
  vendored BRPOP strk 0.001   -> WRONGTYPE   (matches fr)
  vendored BLMOVE strk dst LEFT RIGHT 0.001 -> WRONGTYPE   (matches fr)
  vendored BZPOPMIN strk 0.001 -> WRONGTYPE   (matches fr)
  vendored BZMPOP 0.001 1 strk MIN -> WRONGTYPE   (matches fr)
  vendored BLMPOP 0.001 1 strk LEFT -> WRONGTYPE   (fr returns empty <-- BUG)

Fix plan:
- In blmpop() change the Err(_) => continue arm to mirror LMPOP's StoreError::WrongType handling, returning the error.
- Add regression test pinning BLMPOP <string-key> with a wrongtype to WRONGTYPE.
close_reason: Fixed in iteration 98: replaced BLMPOP's blanket 'Err(_) => continue' with explicit StoreError::WrongType handling that returns WRONGTYPE immediately, mirroring LMPOP's existing pattern at lib.rs:12881. Upstream t_list.c::lmpopGenericCommand (shared by both LMPOP and BLMPOP) returns WRONGTYPE on the first wrongtype hit during key iteration. Live-diff: BLMPOP 0.001 1 strk LEFT and BLMPOP 0.001 2 nokey strk LEFT now both return WRONGTYPE. Regression test blmpop_returns_wrongtype_for_non_list_key_oks10 pins single-wrongtype and nokey-then-wrongtype cases. Full 1023-test suite passes.

title: BITPOS on empty existing string returns 0 instead of -1 for bit=0
description:
Differential probe vs vendored redis 7.2.4:

  SET k ""
  BITPOS k 0   → vendored: -1, fr: 0     ← divergence
  BITPOS k 1   → vendored: -1, fr: -1    (matches)
  BITPOS k 0 0 → vendored: -1, fr: 0     ← divergence
  BITPOS k 0 0 -1     → vendored: -1, fr: 0  ← divergence
  BITPOS k 0 0 -1 BIT → vendored: -1, fr: 0  ← divergence

Missing-key path is fine (vendored=0, fr=0).

Root cause: crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs:3355-3357 has an explicit
early-return for bytes.is_empty() that mirrors the missing-key
return value (bit ? -1 : 0). Upstream bitposCommand does NOT
short-circuit on empty existing strings — it falls through to
range normalization (start=0, end=strlen-1=-1) where start>end
naturally produces -1 for both bit=0 and bit=1.

Fix: delete the bytes.is_empty() short-circuit. The existing
range normalization (s_idx=0, e_idx=-1 for None/None) already
hits the s_idx > e_idx → -1 path. Verified by tracing each
modifier combination.

Add regression test pinning all four divergent variants.
close_reason: fr-store::bitpos no longer short-circuits on empty existing strings; range normalization (s_idx=0 > e_idx=-1) returns -1 naturally for both bit values and all unit/range combinations. Verified against vendored 7.2.4 across 11 BITPOS variants (existing-empty, missing-key, non-empty BIT/BYTE).

title: AOF durability: no incremental append, writes lost on restart
description: fr with appendonly=yes never writes commands to the AOF file during normal operation. write_aof_file is only called on SAVE/BGSAVE/BGREWRITEAOF (full snapshot via store.to_aof_commands). Between rewrites, every write command is captured into the in-memory aof_records buffer but NEVER flushed to disk -> all writes since the last snapshot are lost on restart. Repro: start fr --aof appendonly.aof; SET k1/k2/k3; the AOF file is never even created. FIX: incrementally append newly-captured aof_records to the AOF file once per event-loop iteration, fsync per appendfsync policy (always/everysec/no), reset the flush cursor on snapshot rewrite/load.
close_reason: Implemented incremental AOF append: flush_aof_to_disk appends newly-captured aof_records to the on-disk AOF file once per event-loop iteration, fsync per appendfsync policy (always/everysec/no). Cursor (aof_disk_flushed_records) anchored at aof_records.len() by every full rewrite (SAVE/BGREWRITEAOF/load) and 0 on FullResync clear; forked BGSAVE child now writes RDB-only (write_aof=false) to avoid parent-cursor desync. Verified e2e: writes now persist without SAVE, post-BGREWRITEAOF writes persist, SIGKILL+restart preserves all 7 keys. 2 new unit tests + 404 fr-runtime/all fr-server tests pass, clippy clean.

title: Add metamorphic property tests for advanced ZSET ops
description: Add comprehensive metamorphic tests for fr-store ZSET commands: ZPOPMIN, ZPOPMAX, ZRANK, ZREVRANK using proptest.

description:
Narrowed slice of br-frankenredis-3jrv. No RDB / AOF / HGETEX-HGETDEL yet — those are part 4b (RDB) and part 4c (AOF + HGETEX/HGETDEL).

Scope:

- Tests per axis.

Command-side keyspace events (HEXPIRE → hexpire etc.) and DEBUG OBJECT expired_fields deferred to a future bead alongside RDB + AOF (3jrv remains open).

title: Implement real MONITOR command streaming

title: Sentinel leader election should require configured quorum
description: Upstream Redis 7.2.4 sentinelGetLeader only returns a winner when the candidate has both an absolute majority of known Sentinels and at least master->quorum votes. fr-sentinel::consensus::evaluate_leader_election only computes majority from sentinel_count and can elect a leader when quorum is higher than majority. Acceptance: leader election uses max(majority, configured quorum) as votes_needed and refuses a majority winner below quorum.
close_reason: Implemented in d75b62d

title: HLL PFMERGE/PFCOUNT cardinality mismatch

title: fr-persist: parse_aof_manifest rejects blank lines and # comments; upstream Redis 7.2 aof.c accepts them
close_reason: Completed: parse_aof_manifest now skips Redis-style blank and # comment lines; valid_comments_blank fuzz corpus seed is classified as valid.

title: redis.set_repl and redis.setresp reject non-integer/non-numeric args instead of lua_tonumber-coercing
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:

  EVAL 'return redis.set_repl("abc")' 0
    vendored: (nil)  — silently treats as 0, accepted (0 & ~PROPAGATE_AOF|REPL = 0)
    fr:       ERR Invalid replication flags ...

  EVAL 'return redis.set_repl(1.5)' 0
    vendored: (nil)  — lua_tonumber truncates to 1 (PROPAGATE_AOF), accepted
    fr:       ERR Invalid replication flags ...

  EVAL 'return redis.set_repl(nil)' 0
    vendored: (nil)  — lua_tonumber returns 0, accepted
    fr:       ERR Invalid replication flags ...

  EVAL 'return redis.setresp(2.5)' 0
    vendored: (nil)  — lua_tonumber truncates to 2, accepted
    fr:       ERR RESP version must be 2 or 3

Root cause: upstream script_lua.c::luaRedisSetReplCommand and luaSetRespCommand both call lua_tonumber, which silently coerces non-numeric arguments to 0 and truncates floats to int via the int cast. The bit-mask / value check then accepts 0 (set_repl) or rejects (setresp where 0 != 2 and 0 != 3 — but 2.5 truncates to 2 so it IS accepted).

fr's handlers reject explicitly:
  - non-numeric args (LuaValue::to_number returns None for nil/bool/table) → silently 0 upstream
  - fractional inputs (1.5, 2.5) → silently truncate upstream

Vendored-correct behavior (probe confirmed):
  set_repl: bad-arg rejection only fires for numeric arg whose int truncation has bits outside PROPAGATE_AOF|REPL (e.g., -1, 99)
  setresp:  bad-arg rejection only fires when int(val) is neither 2 nor 3 (e.g., setresp(5), setresp('abc') → truncates to 0 → rejects)

Fix: in both fr handlers, coerce via to_number().unwrap_or(0.0) and cast as i64 (truncating floats), then apply the bit-mask / value check on the truncated integer.
close_reason:
Reworked redis.set_repl and redis.setresp at fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs (~line 4395, 4416) to coerce arguments via lua_tonumber semantics. Both now use args[0].to_number().unwrap_or(0.0) (matches Lua 5.1's lua_tonumber which returns 0 for nil/bool/table/non-numeric-string) and then truncate to i64 via 'as i64' cast (matches C's implicit int conversion which truncates floats toward zero). The bit-mask (set_repl) and 2-or-3 (setresp) checks then apply on the truncated integer.

Result: set_repl('abc'), set_repl(nil), set_repl({}), set_repl(true), set_repl(1.5), set_repl(0.5) — all silently accepted (coerce to 0, no bits set). set_repl(99/-1/12345) — still rejected (bits outside PROPAGATE_AOF|REPL). setresp(2.5) — accepted (truncates to 2). setresp('abc'/nil/{}/0/5) — rejected (0/5 not in {2,3}).

Regression test redis_set_repl_and_setresp_coerce_via_lua_tonumber_op1r0 covers 7 set_repl silent-accept cases, 1 set_repl reject case, 1 setresp truncate-accept case, 3 setresp reject cases. Live diff vs vendored 7.2.4 matches on 21 probed variants.

title: ZINCRBY missing LFU frequency bump for existing zset
description: ZINCRBY modifies existing sorted set but doesn't bump LFU counter. zincrby_with_options should call bump_lfu_freq when key already exists.
close_reason: done

title: fr-command::copy_cmd rejects every non-zero DB target — Lua redis.call('COPY', src, dst, 'DB', N) and AOF replay can never copy across databases
description:
/mock-code-finder: continuing the dispatch_argv-vs-fr-runtime audit (w9yzb MOVE, j22p8 SELECT, rdz52 FLUSHDB). COPY has the same architectural drift.

Source: crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:18078-18127 — copy_cmd:

  if arg.eq_ignore_ascii_case("DB") {
      ...
      let db = parse_i64_arg(&argv[i + 1])?;
      if db != 0 {
          return Err(CommandError::Custom("ERR DB index is out of range".to_string()));
      }
      ...
  }
  ...
  let copied = store.copy(source, destination, replace, now_ms)...;

Two bugs:
1. Hard-coded 'db != 0' check rejects EVERY non-zero target db, regardless of store.database_count. Upstream supports cross-db COPY for any valid db index.
2. Uses raw source/dest bytes — no encode_db_key namespacing — so even the source key is read from the unprefixed (db 0) namespace, ignoring dispatch_client_ctx.db_index.

Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 via Lua:
  EVAL 'return redis.call("COPY", "foo", "foodst", "DB", "1")' 0
    upstream: 1 (key copied to db 1; db 1 GET foodst -> bar)
    fr      : ERR DB index is out of range script: ...

Reachability of broken path (same as MOVE/SELECT/FLUSHDB):
- Lua redis.call('COPY')
- AOF replay (fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:2262 routes through dispatch_argv)
- MULTI/EXEC COPY

Fix: rewrite copy_cmd to mirror fr-runtime::handle_copy_command at lib.rs:10309+ — encode keys with encode_db_key(source_db, &source) / encode_db_key(target_db, &destination), validate target_db < store.database_count, handle the source==destination check on the encoded keys (cross-db COPY of same logical key IS allowed). Preserve REPLACE / DB option parsing. source_db comes from store.dispatch_client_ctx.db_index.
close_reason: Rewrote fr-command::copy_cmd to mirror fr-runtime::handle_copy_command — parses target_db against store.database_count (instead of hard-coded 'db != 0' rejection), encodes source/dest via fr_store::encode_db_key with source_db read from store.dispatch_client_ctx.db_index, and only flags source==destination when BOTH the keys AND the DBs match. Lua redis.call('COPY', src, dst, 'DB', N), AOF replay, and MULTI/EXEC now copy across databases on the dispatch_argv path. 4 new tests pin: cross-db payload transfer + source preservation, out-of-range db rejection without source mutation, cross-db same-name COPY succeeds, same-key/same-db with non-zero DB option still emits the upstream error. clippy clean.

title: CONFIG SET hash/zset-max-listpack-value treated as memory-value + set-max-listpack-value recognized
description: Upstream config.c declares hash-max-listpack-value, zset-max-listpack-value (and their ziplist aliases) as MEMORY_CONFIG so they accept suffix forms (64K, 1m, ...) and report 'argument must be a memory value' on parse failure. fr was treating them as plain integers with the integer-out-of-range / couldnt-be-parsed wording. set-max-listpack-value was missing from the recognized set entirely. Fix: split the encoding-threshold block into MEMORY_CONFIG vs INTEGER_CONFIG paths and add set-max-listpack-value to the integer set. Negative values now also report 'argument must be a memory value' for the memory-value fields, matching upstream's memtoull behaviour

title: fr-conformance core_scripting fixtures pin pre-q27hu pcall/xpcall error format — should include 'user_script:1: ' prefix to match upstream
description:
/testing-conformance-harnesses re-run after recent peer commits surfaced 5 failures, of which 3 are tied to the q27hu Lua error source-level fix:

  conformance_core_scripting fails on:
    eval_pcall_catches_error
      expected: 'boom'
      actual:   'user_script:1: boom'
    eval_xpcall_error_with_handler
      expected: 'caught: boom'
      actual:   'caught: user_script:1: boom'
    eval_xpcall_with_handler
      expected: 'handled: boom'
      actual:   'handled: user_script:1: boom'

q27hu (peer 19a0a83) correctly aligned fr's error() builtin to upstream Lua's source-level prefix behavior — error('boom') now returns 'user_script:1: boom' (matching live oracle). The 3 fixtures pinned the pre-q27hu plain message and now diverge from both fr AND upstream.

Verified upstream behavior via differential probe earlier:
  EVAL \"return error('boom')\" 0
    upstream: ERR user_script:1: boom script: ..., on @user_script:1.

Fix: update the 3 expected values in core_scripting.json to include the upstream prefix.

Note: the other 4 conformance failures (live_redis_stream_dump_restore / xtrim_limit / xautoclaim / function_dump_restore) appear unrelated to q27hu — likely live-oracle harness flakiness or pre-existing red. Left for separate triage.
close_reason: Updated 3 fixture cases in core_scripting.json (eval_pcall_catches_error, eval_xpcall_error_with_handler, eval_xpcall_with_handler) to include the upstream 'user_script:1: ' prefix that error() now emits per q27hu. Verified upstream alignment via differential probe earlier in the session: EVAL returns 'ERR user_script:1: boom ...' on vendored 7.2.4. conformance_core_scripting now passes (was failing on 3 cases). The 4th case at line 3655 ('eval_pcall_catches_error' second variant) doesn't reference the error message text — checks the if-ok branch returning literal 'caught' — so untouched.

title: [perf] Lazy logical memory estimate during RSS sampling
description:
Profile-backed pass31 target for /extreme-software-optimization.

Fresh pass30 profile after rejected integer-canonicality lever: GDB samples during SET pipeline=16 hit estimate_entry_memory_usage_bytes / estimate_string_value_memory_usage_bytes under Store::record_ops_sec_sample. Code currently computes let used_memory = estimate_memory_usage_bytes(); then read_rss_bytes().unwrap_or(used_memory), so the O(N) logical estimate and string classification work run even when procfs RSS succeeds and the logical value is discarded.

One lever: make the logical estimate a lazy fallback for RSS failure, preserving the same RSS behavior and same fallback value when procfs is unavailable. This is a sample-gated stats-refresh primitive, not another integer parsing micro-tweak.

Proof requirements:
- rch-built current baseline before edit.
- Golden MEMORY USAGE / INFO memory / stats trace sha256 proving observable replies unchanged.
- Isomorphism: command ordering/tie-breaking unchanged; RSS-success path observes same RSS sample; RSS-failure fallback computes same logical estimate when needed; FP/RNG N/A.
- Re-benchmark via rch; keep only if Score >=2.0, otherwise remove source and record rejection.
close_reason: Rejected after rch-built A/B: baseline direct 281823.65 ops/sec vs candidate 264329.94; long paired hyperfine baseline 6.884s vs candidate 7.034s. Source removed; behavior proof sha256 31eb164a64364842b2fc660a3dc7377f54a81eb313aa5219a04f63ca6366c977 and normalized INFO/stat sha256 ddc8952d287835a0df686a80a77c17d965b1011139b68d9f02d8d79e9308a7cc.

title: fr-command BITCOUNT error precedence: type check + arg-parse order diverges from upstream 7.2.4
description:
REVIEW MODE Tick 14 finding via /porting-to-rust differential probe vs legacy_redis_code/redis/src/bitops.c::bitcountCommand.

UPSTREAM ORDER (bitops.c lines 805-854):
  1. lookupKeyReadOrReply with shared.czero — missing key returns Integer(0)
  2. checkType(OBJ_STRING) — non-string key returns WRONGTYPE
  3. argc 4|5: parse start (argv[2]), parse end (argv[3]), [argc 5: check BIT|BYTE on argv[4]]
  4. argc 2: whole string
  5. else: shared.syntaxerr

FR ORDER (crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs::bitcount lines 9783-9819):
  1. argv.len() < 2 → WrongArity
  2. store.exists(key) → missing returns Integer(0)
  3. argv.len() match: 5 → check unit FIRST then parse start/end
  4. store.bitcount(...) → returns WRONGTYPE only here

DIVERGENCES:
  A. WRONGTYPE precedence: For BITCOUNT listkey BAD BAD BADUNIT, fr returns SyntaxError; upstream returns WRONGTYPE.
  B. Unit-check order in argc==5: For BITCOUNT key BAD BAD BADUNIT, fr returns 'ERR syntax error' (unit check fires first); upstream returns 'ERR value is not an integer or out of range' (start parses first).

Severity: [LOW]. Visible only when input is malformed in multiple ways simultaneously. Wire-format conformance gap.

FIX:
  1. Use store.key_type() pattern (matches BITPOS lines 9846-9850) to short-circuit on missing AND fail with WRONGTYPE on non-string BEFORE arg parsing.
  2. In argc==5 branch, parse start/end first, then check BIT|BYTE.
close_reason:
Reordered bitcount() to match upstream bitops.c precedence:

1. Added store.key_type() check at the top (mirroring BITPOS lines 9846-9850) so missing → Integer(0) and non-string → WRONGTYPE BEFORE any argv parsing. Replaced the prior store.exists() short-circuit which only handled missing keys.
2. In the argv.len() == 5 branch, parse start (argv[2]) and end (argv[3]) BEFORE checking the BIT|BYTE modifier — upstream bitops.c lines 815-830 do this order, so a malformed integer now takes precedence over a bad unit.

Two new regression tests pin both invariants:
- bitcount_wrongtype_wins_over_arg_parse_errors — BITCOUNT lst BAD BAD BADUNIT now returns WRONGTYPE instead of SyntaxError.
- bitcount_invalid_start_beats_invalid_unit — BITCOUNT k BAD 0 BADUNIT now returns 'ERR value is not an integer or out of range' instead of 'ERR syntax error'.

Verification:
- cargo test -p fr-command --lib bitcount → 8/8 pass.
- cargo clippy -p fr-command --lib --no-deps -- -D warnings → clean.

[LOW] severity — wire-format conformance gap, only visible when input is malformed in multiple ways simultaneously.

title: CRITICAL: ZRANGESTORE z2 z 0 -1 BYSCORE panics fr-server (BTreeMap range start > end)
description:
Calling 'ZRANGESTORE dest src 0 -1 BYSCORE' against fr-server triggers a panic that closes the connection (and presumably tears down the worker thread):

  thread 'main' panicked at .../btree/search.rs:121:21:
  range start is greater than range end in BTreeMap

This happens because the BYSCORE flag interprets the third+fourth args as score bounds; passing 0 and -1 yields start=0, end=-1, and the BTreeMap::range() call inside the score-range walker panics rather than returning an empty range.

Acceptance: 'ZRANGESTORE dest src 0 -1 BYSCORE' returns Integer(0) (empty range, dest unchanged) byte-for-byte matching vendored redis 7.2.4. Same fix should cover ZRANGE BYSCORE / ZRANGEBYSCORE with start > end: those already return empty arrays without panicking, but the underlying BTreeMap walker for ZRANGESTORE may take a different path that's not guarded.

Reference: ad-hoc differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored redis 7.2.4 (port 16701) caught the panic. Affects ANY caller that hits start > end on the BYSCORE/BYLEX-backed BTreeMap walker for ZRANGESTORE.

Severity rationale: fr-server panic + connection close from a single client command is a denial-of-service vector. Promoting to P1.
close_reason: Added command regression for inverted ZRANGESTORE BYSCORE panic path; fr-command/fr-runtime tests pass via rch

title: INFO 'commandstats' section missing; fr tracks histograms but emits no cmdstat_<cmd> lines
description:
fr-store's CommandHistogramTracker tracks per-command call counts (used by INFO Latencystats). fr-runtime's handle_info_command at crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:10733+ orders sections as Server/Clients/Memory/Persistence/Stats/Replication/CPU/Modules/Errorstats/Latencystats/Cluster/Keyspace, omitting upstream's Commandstats section (server.c::genRedisInfoString:6168-6173, format defined at server.c::genRedisInfoStringCommandStats:5329-5353).

Repro vs vendored 7.2.4:
  fr> INFO commandstats     -> empty bulk string
  redis> INFO commandstats  -> '# Commandstats\r\ncmdstat_set:calls=N,usec=0,usec_per_call=0.00,rejected_calls=0,failed_calls=0\r\n...'

Fix: add handle_info_commandstats_section emitting '# Commandstats' followed by 'cmdstat_<lowercased_name>:calls=<N>,usec=0,usec_per_call=0.00,rejected_calls=0,failed_calls=0' per histogram, slotted between modules and errorstats per upstream order. usec/rejected/failed unmaintained; emit 0 stubs to keep the field-set parseable by clients.

title: ZSCAN missing LFU frequency bump
description: ZSCAN reads from existing zset but doesn't bump LFU counter. Should call bump_lfu_freq when scanning zset.
close_reason: done

title: SORT BY <const> (dontsort) + LIMIT materializes whole list then truncates — O(n) not O(limit)
description: SORT key BY <constant-pattern> (dontsort) loads the ENTIRE collection via store.sort_elements then applies LIMIT by truncate — O(n) load + O(n) drop. Redis windows the natural-order load for a list (O(limit)). Differential (50k list): SORT mylist BY nosort LIMIT 0 10 = oracle 52us vs fr 1235us (23.8x); with GET 26.2x. Output already byte-exact (8 curated cases match) — only the cost is wrong. Fix: for dontsort + LIST + real LIMIT, load only the natural-order window via store.lrange (which now uses iter_from chunk-seek, 3r9lz) handling ASC/DESC, instead of sort_elements. Sets/zsets/no-limit keep the full path. Real sort (BY pattern with *) is 2.4x (separate, softer).
close_reason: Fixed: SORT BY <const> (dontsort) + real LIMIT over a LIST now loads only the natural-order window via the chunk-seeking lrange (O(limit)) instead of materializing the whole list then truncating (O(n)). Byte-identical: 14 curated + 2880 sort_differ combos + STORE cases all byte-exact vs redis 7.2.4. SORT mylist BY nosort LIMIT 0 10 (50k): 837us->44us (19x), parity with redis 46us. golden 93e2585a; fr-command lib 1143/0.

title: perf: exact four-pair MSET packet parser
description: br ready/open work was empty or already claimed by swarm-mates; deepen borrowed command fast-path coverage with an exact canonical four-pair MSET packet parser. Add focused parser coverage and run crate cargo check only; batch tests remain pending by instruction.

title: FAILOVER TO host PORT — port accepts any signed long, not just u16
description:
Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 shows fr's FAILOVER TO
parser rejects ports outside u16 with the int-out-of-range wording,
where vendored accepts any signed long-long. The port value is
stored but not otherwise validated by upstream — only the parse must
succeed.

Examples:

  FAILOVER TO host 99999
    vendored: ERR FAILOVER requires connected replicas.
    fr:       ERR value is not an integer or out of range

  FAILOVER TO host -1
    vendored: ERR FAILOVER requires connected replicas.
    fr:       ERR value is not an integer or out of range

  FAILOVER TO host 0
    both: ERR FAILOVER requires connected replicas.

  FAILOVER TO host 9223372036854775808
    both: ERR value is not an integer or out of range

  FAILOVER TO host 99999 abc
    vendored: ERR syntax error           (abc is unknown trailing option)
    fr:       ERR value is not an integer or out of range  (port rejected first)

Fix locus: crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs::failover_cmd, the `TO`
branch. Currently casts the parsed i64 to u16 via try_from. Replace
with a plain parse_i64_arg and store the value as i64 (or drop it
entirely — the port field is unused).

Test plan: pin "FAILOVER TO host 99999" → connected-replicas error,
"FAILOVER TO host -1" → same, "FAILOVER TO host 99999 abc" → syntax
error, "FAILOVER TO host 9223372036854775808" → int-out-of-range.
close_reason: fixed

title: Sentinel master role mismatch should drive subjective down
description: Upstream Redis 7.2.4 sentinelCheckSubjectivelyDown marks a monitored master S_DOWN when it reports role:slave for longer than down_after_period + sentinel_info_period*2. fr-sentinel currently records role_reported and exposes check_role_mismatch, but evaluate_instance_health does not turn the sustained master-as-slave report into S_DOWN, so a misconfigured/promoted master can remain subjectively up with fresh PONGs. Acceptance: a master reporting Role::Slave is marked S_DOWN only after the upstream grace window, and remains up at the exact boundary.
close_reason: Implemented in ef64c1a

title: PING in subscribe mode emits [pong, msg] array + PING table arity -1
description: (1) fr's PING command returned the standard '+PONG' (or +'msg' bulk) reply even when the client was in subscribe mode; upstream networking.c::pingCommand emits a 2-element array ['pong', msg] in subscribe-mode RESP2 (2) PING was registered with table-level arity 1 (exact); upstream commands.def declares arity -1 (1+) with the handler checking argc<=2 internally. Without arity -1 fix, the runtime's command_arity_ok gate blocked the subscribe-mode interception for 'PING msg'

title: perf(fr-store): O(log n+k) ZREMRANGEBYLEX, was O(n) full scan
description: ZREMRANGEBYLEX found the elements to remove via iter_asc().filter(lex_in_range) — a FULL O(n) scan of the zset — while the read path ZRANGEBYLEX already uses the indexed lex_range_asc (BTreeMap range, O(log n+k)) and zremrangebyscore uses score_bound_members. On a 50k single-score zset, ZREMRANGEBYLEX of 100 elements ran 2045 rps vs redis 32967 (16x slower). Fix: use zs.lex_range_asc(min,max) (isomorphic — equal-score walks the BTreeMap range, multi-score falls back to the identical iter+filter). 2045->37500 rps (18.3x), now 1.14x faster than redis. Parity: removed-count + remaining-set byte-identical to redis across 8 ranges incl edge bounds; random_command_differ 0 div; 585 fr-store tests + clippy clean.
close_reason: Shipped: lex_range_asc indexed range; 2045->37500 rps (18.3x), beats redis; isomorphic (8 ranges byte-identical) + differ 0 div + 585 tests

title: INCRBYFLOAT (and HINCRBYFLOAT) lose precision at large magnitudes — fr uses f64; vendored uses long double
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 16380:
  SET counter 100
  INCRBY counter -9223372036854775808  → both: -9223372036854775707
  INCRBYFLOAT counter 1.5
    vendored: -9223372036854775705.5  (preserves the .5 fractional)
    fr      : -9223372036854776000    (lost both the .5 AND last 3 digits)

Root cause: upstream t_string.c::incrbyfloatCommand uses 'long double' (80-bit extended precision on x86_64) for the running value:
  long double incr, value;
  ...
  if (getLongDoubleFromObjectOrReply(c, o, &value, NULL) ...)

fr's INCRBYFLOAT uses Rust's f64 (64-bit IEEE 754), which has only ~15-17 decimal digits of precision. At magnitudes near 9e18 (the operand here), f64 can't even represent integers spaced by 1, let alone the .5 fractional offset.

Likely affected commands (need verify): INCRBYFLOAT, HINCRBYFLOAT, GEOADD (which encodes lat/lon to float), ZADD INCR with large running scores, ZINCRBY.

Fix scope: switch fr's INCRBYFLOAT/HINCRBYFLOAT from f64 to a long-double-equivalent crate (rug ≈ MPFR binding, or num-bigfloat). This is a dependency-changing task — file as P3 since it requires product judgment about adding a heavyweight numeric dep, and the divergence only manifests at near-overflow magnitudes that real workloads rarely hit.

Alternative: document the limitation in README and add a clamping check that surfaces 'value is not a representable IEEE 754 double' for operands >= 2^53 (similar to how Redis currently surfaces NaN/Infinity).
close_reason: Preserved INCRBYFLOAT/HINCRBYFLOAT decimal text precision for simple finite decimal operands near i64 magnitudes, with f64 fallback for hex/exponent paths

title: Lua struct stdlib not exposed (sandbox surface)
description:
fr's Lua sandbox exposes neither the `struct` library nor any equivalent. Vendored Redis 7.2.4 ships lua-struct (struct.pack / struct.unpack / struct.size) and scripts that use it for binary packing fail on fr with the generic protected-global error.

Reproduction:
  EVAL 'return type(struct)' 0
    vendored: table
    fr:       ERR user_script:1: Script attempted to access nonexistent global variable 'struct' ...
  EVAL 'return struct.pack("i", 100)' 0
    vendored: <4-byte binary>
    fr:       (same protected-global error)

struct format string covers: native byte order/alignment (`<`, `>`, `=`, `!`), integer/uint sizes (b/B/h/H/i/I/l/L), floats (f/d), packed strings (`sN`, `cN`, `cs`), and pad/skip (`x`/`X`/`s`). Full grammar in https://www.lua.org/wiki/StructurePacking and the lua-struct C source vendored under `legacy_redis_code/redis/deps/lua/src/lua_struct.c`.

Existing companion beads:
- frankenredis-7gtvz (cmsgpack — also missing)
- frankenredis-2wn7j (newproxy — also missing)

Fix plan (substantial):
1. Add a `struct` global table to the Lua sandbox via the same mechanism that exposes `redis`, `cjson`, `bit` (likely fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs sandbox init).
2. Implement pack/unpack/size with the documented format grammar.
3. Pin against vendored across the format grammar (signed/unsigned, endianness, alignment, packed strings).

Probe sweep #82 (sandbox visibility, inline).

Not scoped for this iteration.
close_reason: done

title: Lua table.concat must read raw signed integer keys
description: Lua 5.1/Redis table.concat uses lua_rawgeti for every integer in the requested range. FrankenRedis currently indexes only the dense array vector, so table.concat(t, ',', -1, 1) or table.concat(t, ',', 0, 1) treats existing hash-backed integer slots as nil and raises an invalid-value error. Fix concat to use raw table lookup for signed integer ranges and add focused coverage.
close_reason: Completed: table.concat now uses raw Lua integer key lookup across signed start/end ranges, preserving t[-1], t[0], dense, and hash-backed integer slots before validating string/number values. Verified with rch cargo fmt -p fr-command -- --check, rch cargo test -p fr-command table_concat_reads_raw_signed_integer_keys_ozc36 -- --nocapture, rch cargo check -p fr-command, and UBS on changed files (UBS reports pre-existing file-wide lua_eval.rs inventory; fmt/clippy/check/test-build clean).

title: fr-server: parse_blocking_deadline edge cases (-0, 0e100, subnormal) lack focused regression coverage
description:
fr-server's parse_blocking_deadline (BLPOP/BRPOP/BLMPOP/etc. timeout handling) has unit tests for finite/non-finite/negative/fractional/out-of-range cases but is missing focused coverage for three edge cases that are easy to silently regress:

1. `-0` (negative zero literal): in IEEE-754, `-0.0 == 0.0` is true and `-0.0 < 0.0` is false. The current implementation correctly treats this as the zero-timeout 'block-forever' case (returns Some(u64::MAX)) but no test pins that. A future refactor that uses sign-bit checks (e.g. `signum() < 0.0`) would silently break negative-zero handling.

2. `0e100` and `0.0e0`: scientific-notation zeros parse to 0.0; should also map to Some(u64::MAX), not a far-future deadline. Subtle f64-parse behavior worth pinning.

3. `1e-300` and `1e-323` (subnormal/underflow positives): timeout_secs * 1000.0 still underflows to a non-zero positive, ceil rounds up to 1ms, deadline = now_ms + 1. Behavior is correct but worth pinning as a "no panic / no None" assertion to catch a future refactor that breaks subnormal handling.

These are deadlock-adjacent because parse_blocking_deadline is on the critical path for every BL* command: a regression that silently returns a wrong deadline (e.g. 'forever' when client requested 1ms, or vice-versa) yields a hung client without any error surface.

Acceptance: a single test that pins all three categories. Add it to the existing parse_blocking_deadline test cluster in crates/fr-server/src/main.rs.

Reference: /deadlock-finder-and-fixer audit on 2026-05-04.
notes: Added parse_blocking_deadline_handles_signed_zero_and_subnormals test in crates/fr-server/src/main.rs. Pins -0/-0.0/-0e0 → Some(u64::MAX), 0e100/0.0e0/0.0e-100 → Some(u64::MAX), and 1e-300/1e-323/1e-200/0.000000000001 → bounded future deadline (never block-forever, never None). Closes the regression-coverage gap that would let a future refactor of the timeout parser silently swap 'block forever' and 'timeout immediately' on edge inputs.

title: RESTORE of integer set 15-20x slow: GenericSet build plus from_index_set conversion
description: PROFILE-BACKED pipelined RESTORE perf record: RESTORE of an intset-encoded set was 15-20x slower than redis 7.2.4 (superlinear: 5.6x/15.5x/19.6x for 100/400/512 members = O(n squared)). perf: PackedStrSet::insert 28.6 percent + CompactFieldMap lookup/insert 26 percent. ROOT CAUSE: restore_key RDB_TYPE_SET_INTSET arm inserted each decoded member into a GenericSet hash table then converted via from_index_set; the equivalent RDB-load path (apply_rdb_entries_to_store to sadd) hit the per-member sorted-Vec insert. FIX: parse the canonical i64 members + sort + dedup straight into SetValue::Int (restore_key); plus SetValue::try_bulk_unique_ints fast path in sadd for the RDB-load path. Byte-exact: re-DUMP sha256 identical to redis incl i64::MIN/MAX/negatives, encoding stays intset; 634 fr-store tests, dump_restore_fuzz, rdb_cross_compat all pass. BENCH pipelined P16: RESTORE intset 19.6x to 3.89x = 5.0x speedup (is400 5.5x). Residual = decode to_string + RESTORE command overhead.
close_reason: Fixed in 1f28f6fb9: restore_key + sadd build SetValue::Int directly from i64s (sort+dedup) instead of GenericSet+from_index_set. RESTORE intset 19.6x->3.89x (5.0x speedup); byte-exact (re-DUMP identical, 634 tests + dump_restore_fuzz + rdb_cross_compat pass).
