title: fr-command SLOWLOG GET parses count argument twice — once for validation, once for value extraction
close_reason:
Refactored slowlog_cmd to single-pass parsing.

CHANGE (crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs around lines 15399-15414):
Replaced the two-pass `if let Some(count_arg) = argv.get(2) { parse_i64_arg(count_arg)?; ... }` blocks with a single `match argv.get(2)` that parses once, validates `parsed < -1`, then derives the usize count from the same parsed value.

Verification:
- `cargo test -p fr-command --lib slowlog` — 3/3 pass (slowlog_get_accepts_minus_one_and_rejects_invalid_shapes, slowlog_dispatch_uses_store_backed_entries, slowlog_len_and_reset_reject_extra_arguments).
- `cargo clippy -p fr-command --lib --no-deps -- -D warnings` — clean.

[LOW] severity — performance only, no behavioral change.

title: XINFO CONSUMERS: 'inactive' field should be -1 for consumers never seen, not idle_ms
description: fr's XINFO CONSUMERS reports both 'idle' and 'inactive' incorrectly for consumers without pending entries. Two related bugs: (1) idle field: fr computes idle_ms from the MAX last_delivered_ms across the consumer's pending entries (crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs:8804-8815); if there are no pending entries, falls back to 0. Vendored Redis 7.2.4 stores consumer->seen_time set at consumer creation (XGROUP CREATECONSUMER calls streamLookupConsumer with create=1 which sets seen_time = mstime()) and computes idle = mstime() - seen_time. So a fresh consumer never seen XREADGROUP shows idle = ~tens of ms in vendored vs 0 in fr. (2) inactive field: crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:5977 reuses idle_ms for inactive, but vendored separates them. Vendored initializes consumer->active_time = -1 in streamCreateConsumer; XINFO CONSUMERS emits inactive = mstime() - active_time when active_time > 0 else -1. XREADGROUP updates active_time. For a freshly-created consumer: vendored inactive = -1, fr inactive = 0. Reproduce: XADD s '*' f v; XGROUP CREATE s g 0; XGROUP CREATECONSUMER s g c1; XINFO CONSUMERS s g -> vendored: idle:<small>,inactive:-1; fr: idle:0,inactive:0. Fix needs a struct for per-consumer metadata in fr-store (currently consumers are just BTreeSet<Vec<u8>> names): introduce StreamConsumerState { seen_time_ms, active_time_ms (Option<u64> = None for never-active) }, replace BTreeSet with BTreeMap<Vec<u8>, StreamConsumerState>, update XGROUP CREATECONSUMER to seed seen_time, update XREADGROUP/XCLAIM/XACK to refresh seen_time AND active_time, update xinfo_consumers to return both fields as a 4-tuple instead of (name, pending, idle_ms). Discovered during REVIEW PHASE differential-probe sweep.
close_reason: XINFO CONSUMERS now reports 'inactive' = -1 for consumers that have never received an active event (XREADGROUP/XCLAIM/XAUTOCLAIM), matching upstream Redis 7.2.4. Implementation: (1) Added StreamConsumerMetadata struct in fr-store with seen_time_ms (u64) and active_time_ms (Option<u64>); (2) Added consumer_metadata: BTreeMap<Vec<u8>, StreamConsumerMetadata> field to StreamGroup alongside the existing consumers BTreeSet (additive — no breaking changes to dump/restore format); (3) Plumbed updates through XREADGROUP, XCLAIM, XAUTOCLAIM (all set seen_time and active_time to now_ms), XGROUP CREATECONSUMER (sets seen_time only, active_time stays None), XGROUP DELCONSUMER (removes the metadata entry); (4) Added StreamConsumerInfoEx 4-tuple type (name, pending, idle_ms, inactive_ms_or_neg_one) and migrated xinfo_consumers + stream_consumer_info_to_frame + stream_full_consumer_info_to_frame to use it; (5) inactive emits -1 when active_time_ms is None, otherwise now_ms - active_time_ms; idle uses seen_time_ms when known, falling back to last-pending-delivery for RDB-restored consumers; (6) Updated XINFO STREAM FULL active-time field accordingly: -1 when never active, else absolute mstime reconstructed from now_ms - inactive_ms. Verified: 336 fr-store + 910 fr-command + 372 fr-runtime tests pass; conformance_core_stream fixture updated to expect inactive:-1 for freshly-created consumers (3 fixture values flipped from 0 to -1).

title: Stream RAM ~6.9x: BTreeMap<StreamId, Vec<StreamField>> scattered Vecs vs redis listpack-packed
description: PROFILE-BACKED (2026-06-16, fresh-process RSS): 200 streams x 1000 entries x 3 fields: redis +9.2MB (~46 B/entry... ~46KB/stream) vs fr +63.5MB (~318KB/stream) = ~6.9x RAM — the BIGGEST gap in the project. fr stores StreamEntries=BTreeMap<StreamId, Vec<StreamField>> where StreamField=(Vec<u8>,Vec<u8>): each entry is a scattered Vec of (Vec<u8>,Vec<u8>) field-value pairs (24-byte Vec header + heap block per field AND per value). redis packs stream entries into listpacks in a rax. LEVER (proven compact-arena pattern, cf CompactFieldMap/CompactStrSet): replace Vec<StreamField> with a PackedStreamFields type — one contiguous Vec<u8> per entry holding [flen varint][field][vlen varint][value] pairs in order (NO dedup, order-preserved — stream fields are an ordered list, may repeat). Eliminates ~6 allocs/entry + the per-field Vec headers. Build via from_pairs(Vec<StreamField>), read via iter()->(&[u8],&[u8]). Touch points: XADD build + XRANGE/XREAD/XINFO/XAUTOCLAIM read + DUMP/RESTORE/RDB encode-decode + DEBUG DIGEST. lib.rs (CoralOx-WIP-contended) — use selective staging. Byte-exact: field order + DUMP/DIGEST content unchanged. Target: stream RAM 6.9x -> ~1x.

title: ZCOUNT/ZLEXCOUNT O(n) on cold large zset — never warm the rank treap
description: score_bound_count/lex_count have an O(log n) rank-diff path (i5896/7uonw) but only fire when the order-statistic treap is already warm. The treap is built lazily ONLY by ZRANK/ZRANGE-by-index, so a ZADD+ZCOUNT workload (rate limiters, event-window counting) never warms it and pays an O(range) BTreeMap scan every call. Measured: ZCOUNT [1,500000] on a ~500k zset = 4190us (fr) vs 34us (redis 7.2.4) = ~123x. Fix: adaptive maybe_warm_rank_tree_for_count mirroring maybe_warm_rank_tree_for_index (warm large sets after repeated count hits); rank-diff path already isomorphism-tested.
close_reason: Shipped: adaptive maybe_warm_rank_tree_for_count warms the rank treap on repeated ZCOUNT/ZLEXCOUNT over large zsets -> O(log n) rank-diff path. ZCOUNT 3706->39us (95x), ZLEXCOUNT 1279->39us (33x), both at parity with redis 7.2.4; byte-exact (10 ZCOUNT + 5 ZLEXCOUNT cases oracle==new==old, golden sha256 9b0f1060)

title: GEOSEARCH near-global radius at the antimeridian: fr vs redis cell-coverage/inclusion diverges (pathological)
description:
Found by /tmp/geo_edge_probe.py (antimeridian/antipodal boundary differential). At NEAR-GLOBAL radii (>=~20000 km) with members at/near lon=+-180, fr's geohash neighbor-cell range scan (7hg0r, 21500496c) includes/orders members differently from redis 7.2.4.

REPRO (isolated, byte-exact distance agreement): GEOADD g 180 0 p180 ; GEOADD g 0 0 origin ; GEODIST g origin p180 km => 20020.7336 (both). GEOSEARCH g FROMLONLAT 0 0 BYRADIUS 20021 km ASC => redis [origin] (OMITS p180 though 20020.73 <= 20021), fr [origin, p180] (includes the in-radius point). At BYRADIUS 20000 with both (180,0) and (-180,0) present, redis asymmetrically returns the (-180,0) antipode but not (180,0) — both ~20020.73 km, both actually OUTSIDE 20000 — so redis is itself inconsistent at the antipode/antimeridian boundary.

ASSESSMENT: WONTFIX-leaning. (1) Only manifests at near-global radii (>=~20000 km); MODERATE-radius antimeridian queries (<=~100 km across lon=180) are byte-exact fr==redis (verified). (2) redis itself is inconsistent here (includes one antipodal point, not the symmetric other; omits an in-radius point) — there is no clean correct order/set to match. (3) A separate equal-distance TIE-ORDER divergence in GEOSEARCH ASC (e.g. symmetric east/west swapped) is redis qsort-arbitrary (deterministic per-process but no defined secondary sort key) — same unstable-order WONTFIX class as dict-order cases. Strict-mode parity would require replicating redis's antimeridian-non-wrapping geohashGetNeighbors coverage gap + qsort tie outcome. Filed for the record so this edge is not re-chased; moderate-radius GEO is byte-exact.
close_reason: WONTFIX: only two divergence classes, both non-bugs — (1) antimeridian-crossing where redis's neighbor-cell scan misses cross-dateline points and fr is MORE correct (replicating it = reproducing a redis limitation), (2) equal-distance tie order which is redis qsort-arbitrary (no defined contract). Realistic distinct non-dateline data byte-exact. Regression-locked by geo_boundary_gate.py (d6da31140)

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
REFINED + BROADENED (scripts/geo_boundary_gate.py): the divergence is the ANTIMERIDIAN-CROSSING case in general, NOT just near-global radius. Whenever a GEOSEARCH/GEORADIUS region straddles lon=+-180 and members exist on the other side of the dateline, redis's geohash neighbor-cell scan does NOT cross the antimeridian and MISSES those members, while fr's scan finds them. Confirmed at MODERATE radius: frommember near180(180,84.9) bybox 1000km -> redis [near180], fr [near180, negfar(-179.9,84.9)] (negfar is ~1km away across the dateline, genuinely in-box). So fr is MORE CORRECT here; redis has a documented antimeridian limitation. Replicating it would mean reproducing a redis bug.

Two WONTFIX classes total: (1) antimeridian-crossing coverage (fr more correct; redis misses cross-dateline points); (2) equal-distance TIE ORDER (redis sort_gp_asc returns 0 for equal dist -> qsort-arbitrary, no defined contract; fr's deterministic order differs). Realistic distinct, non-dateline data is byte-exact (poles/cluster sets + geo_differ 20 seeds PASS). Regression-locked by geo_boundary_gate.py (poles+cluster strict; antimeridian/tie/near-global tracked as known-pb7zp). Resolution: WONTFIX (fr-more-correct + undefined-order).

title: perf(fr-store): zset skiplist member single-storage — FullSortedSet dedup dict+ordered via Arc<[u8]> (1.63x RSS measured)
description:
PROFILE-BACKED (scripts/zset_memory_profile.py, fresh processes, 500 keys x 800 small ~12B members = 400k members): fr 63.25 MB data-RSS (166 B/member) vs redis 7.2.4 38.71 MB (101 B/member) = 1.63x. (used_memory does NOT show this — it is a faithful redis-MODEL estimate by design; the gap is only in actual process RSS. Reused processes report bogus ~0 delta due to mimalloc retaining freed pages — measure fresh.)

ROOT CAUSE: FullSortedSet (crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs ~595-2200) stores every member TWICE:
  - dict: IndexMap<Vec<u8>, f64>          (member -> score)
  - ordered: BTreeMap<ScoreMember, ()>    where ScoreMember.member = MemberPart::Actual(Vec<u8>)
Each copy is a 24-byte Vec header + a separate heap block (allocator-rounded). redis keeps ONE sds per member, shared between its dict and skiplist node. Hash and Set are ALREADY compacted (Packed listpack + inline HashFieldBytes/SetMember in packed_set.rs); zset is the LAST un-compacted collection — completes the trilogy.

LEVER: share each member as a single Arc<[u8]> between dict and ordered (Arc, NOT Rc, to preserve any Send bound on Value; Arc<[u8]> clone = cheap atomic inc, ZADD is not the hottest path). One allocation per member, two refs (16B fat-ptr each) instead of two 24B Vec headers + two heap blocks. Est. ~60->~41 MB (toward redis 38.7). ~100 access sites, concentrated: dict {len,get,get_mut,insert,swap_remove}, ordered {len,insert,remove,keys,first/last_key_value}, ScoreMember::actual (15 call sites), MemberPart::Actual.

PRECHECK before implementing: confirm rank_tree (ZRankTreap) indexes POSITIONS over `ordered` (not member-byte copies) — if it duplicates members it is a third copy to fold in. Also PackedZSet (small zsets) is already compact; only FullSortedSet (skiplist, >128 entries) is affected.

ISOMORPHISM (absolute): byte-exact across ZADD(all flags)/ZSCORE/ZMSCORE/ZRANGE/ZRANGEBYSCORE/ZRANGEBYLEX/ZREVRANGE*/ZRANK/ZREVRANK/ZREM/ZREMRANGEBY*/ZPOPMIN/ZPOPMAX/ZINCRBY/ZRANDMEMBER/ZCOUNT/ZLEXCOUNT/ZRANGESTORE/ZUNION*/ZINTER*/ZDIFF* + DUMP/RESTORE + DEBUG DIGEST. Tie-breaking (member lex order on equal score) MUST be preserved exactly.

HOW TO SHIP SAFELY: fr-store/lib.rs is CONTESTED (CoralOx large-SET WIP uncommitted + p8dd2 assignee). Do it in an ISOLATED git worktree from clean HEAD (no entanglement with the shared-tree WIP), prove there (full fr-store zset suite via rch + zset differential fuzzer vs oracle + re-run zset_memory_profile.py for the RSS drop), then land. Distinct from closed p8dd2 (that was the used_memory-estimate concern + hash, both resolved); this is the zset-specific actual-RSS lever.
close_reason: Rejected after profile-backed implementation attempts: Arc<[u8]>, inline/heap key, and Box<[u8]> zset member-key representations topped out at 58.29 MB vs 59.46 MB baseline (1.02x best, below Score>=2.0). No source change kept; see artifacts/optimization/coralox-pass218-peni2-zset-single-storage/rejection-report.md. Deeper structural follow-up filed.

title: CLUSTER SET-CONFIG-EPOCH blanket-returns cluster_disabled_error even when cluster is enabled
description: Upstream cluster.c::clusterCommand:6420+ for CLUSTER SET-CONFIG-EPOCH validates the epoch arg and emits one of: 'Invalid config epoch specified: <epoch>' (negative), 'The user can assign a config epoch only when the node does not know any other node.' (peer dict size > 1), or 'Node config epoch is already non-zero' (configEpoch != 0); on first-call success it sets configEpoch and replies OK. fr's cluster_cmd at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:8429 unconditionally returns cluster_disabled_error after the arity gate — meaning even when cluster_enabled is true, the subcommand never advances past the disabled-error path. The existing test acknowledges this as a follow-up. Tighten to: cluster_enabled gate first, then parse epoch (InvalidInteger on non-numeric), then 'Invalid config epoch specified: <n>' for negative, else OK reply (we don't model configEpoch state separately so we can't reproduce the 'already non-zero' branch, but the negative-epoch and happy-path arms align with upstream and make the stub usable for cluster mode tests).
close_reason: fixed

title: COMMAND_TABLE has PFDEBUG and PSYNC arities inverted vs upstream commands.def
description:
Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4:
  COMMAND INFO PFDEBUG -> upstream: 3   fr: -3
  COMMAND INFO PSYNC   -> upstream: -3  fr: 3

Upstream commands.def:
  MAKE_CMD("pfdebug",...,pfdebugCommand,3,...) -- arity=3 (exact: subcommand + key)
  MAKE_CMD("psync",...,syncCommand,-3,...)     -- arity=-3 (3 or more; replication options)

fr's COMMAND_TABLE entries had the two declarations swapped relative
to upstream:
  ('pfdebug', -3, ...)  -- declared variadic, but handler enforces argv.len() != 3
  ('psync',    3, ...)  -- declared exact-3, but handler accepts argv.len() < 3 (rejects)
                          -- and PSYNC tests already cover trailing args

Handler logic was already aligned with upstream; only the
COMMAND_TABLE metadata was stale. `COMMAND INFO PFDEBUG` and
`COMMAND INFO PSYNC` therefore returned the wrong arity
field, diverging from `redis-cli COMMAND INFO ...` against
vendored 7.2.4.

Fix: swap the two arities so they match upstream commands.def
(pfdebug=3, psync=-3) and pin a regression test
(command_info_arity_for_pfdebug_and_psync_matches_upstream).

title: CONFORMANCE: redis sets parity matrix

title: CONFIG SET min-slaves-max-lag accepts negative values — vendored 7.2.4 rejects with [0, 2147483647] bound message
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 16380:
  CONFIG SET min-slaves-max-lag -1
    vendored: ERR CONFIG SET failed (possibly related to argument 'min-slaves-max-lag') - argument must be between 0 and 2147483647 inclusive
    fr      : OK

Upstream config.c declares min-slaves-max-lag (alias of min-replicas-max-lag) as INTEGER_CONFIG with inclusive range [0, INT_MAX]. fr's existing min-replicas-max-lag handler at lib.rs:8135 emits 'Invalid argument' on negative values rather than the table-level bound wording — same template miss as xqgra. Fix: route through config_set_failed with the bound wording. Same likely applies to min-slaves-to-write / min-replicas-to-write — verify and bundle.
close_reason: Bundled fix for the entire min-{replicas,slaves}-{to-write,max-lag} family. Changes in fr-runtime's handle_config_set: (1) merged min-replicas-to-write and min-slaves-to-write into a single branch (same applies to *-max-lag); (2) replaced the non-standard 'Invalid argument' wording with config_set_failed's table-level 'argument must be between 0 and 2147483647 inclusive' / 'argument couldn't be parsed into an integer'; (3) added explicit i32::MAX upper bound (was implicitly accepting any non-negative i64); (4) GET round-trips the user-supplied alias key (CONFIG SET min-slaves-max-lag stores under 'min-slaves-max-lag', SET min-replicas-max-lag stores under 'min-replicas-max-lag', so each alias's GET reflects its own SET). Pinned via config_set_min_replicas_slaves_family_validates_range_and_aliases which exercises -1 (bound err), 'abc' (parse err), 0 and 42 (accepted) for all four names. Live differential probe vs vendored 16380 confirms identical bound-error wording for all four. Workspace clippy clean under -D warnings.

title: RESP3: null replies emit $-1/*-1 instead of _ (global; redis 7.2 uses _ for all nulls under HELLO 3)
notes:
IMPL ANALYSIS (do NOT rush — needs full conformance+perf run): redis 7.2 emits _ for ALL nulls under HELLO 3; fr emits $-1 (bulk) / *-1 (array). Verified: GET miss RESP3 ven=_
 fr=$-1
. Approach: add RespFrame::Null encoding _
 in fr-protocol, then recursively promote BulkString(None)/Array(None)->Null at the COMMAND-RESPONSE encode site in fr-server/main.rs ONLY when resp_protocol_version==3. CRITICAL COUPLING: the should_block check (~main.rs:1446) compares response==RespFrame::Array(None)/BulkString(None) to decide blocking — promotion MUST happen on a transformed copy at encode time, AFTER block/suppress logic, never mutating the response used for block detection, or BLPOP/BRPOP/etc. break. Also: parser maps _ ->BulkString(None) (lib.rs:481), so a roundtrip test parse(encode(Null))==Null would fail unless parser also maps _ ->Null (check fr_p2c_002_u010_null_semantics_parity). 12 encode_into sites in main.rs; only the command-reply ones (~1434/1474) + blocked-timeout (~2583) carry nulls. Differ masks this (normalizes nil) — use raw byte probes.
close_reason: Fixed: added RespFrame::encode_into_resp3 (emits _ for null bulk/array/map/set, recurses into containers for nested nulls) and selected it at all client command-reply + blocked-reply encode sites in fr-server when resp_protocol_version==3. Frames are never mutated, so block-detection comparisons against BulkString(None)/Array(None) are unaffected (BLPOP/BRPOP verified). RESP2 wire unchanged (569JNRXghkl1/*-1); RESP3 now emits _ for every null. Verified byte-exact vs vendored 7.2.4: GET/HGET/LPOP/ZSCORE/SPOP/GETDEL/LINDEX/RANDOMKEY/LPOP-count misses, nested MGET nulls, XPENDING empty summary, BLPOP timeout. Updated the one e2e test that read a RESP3 null with a RESP2-only parser. fr-protocol+fr-server+fr-conformance suites green.

title: [perf] Borrowed fast path for GETDEL write form
description: Split from frankenredis-8ap9a after plain ZRANGE was shipped. Profile-backed deep-pipeline row: GETDEL 0.428x vs Redis, missing borrowed write fast path. Scope to GETDEL key; mirror write-gated borrowed commands, preserve delete propagation, dirty/keyspace-event behavior, nil/missing and WRONGTYPE replies, and golden transcript SHA proof.
close_reason: Already completed by origin/main 26491cd30 perf(fr-runtime): borrowed write fast path for GETDEL. Commit evidence: GETDEL 0.43x -> 1.06x vs Redis deep-pipeline, golden sha256 884857b8e3dac4ae964d6ce10e3eaef212171424adde33a5c44a8ed9b9c61051, focused isomorphism tests.

title: ZPOPMIN/ZPOPMAX with count argument do not bump dirty (data-loss class)
description: Store::zpopmin_count / zpopmax_count remove members and handle empty-key deletion + digest, but never bump self.dirty (unlike the no-count zpopmin/zpopmax which add 1). So 'ZPOPMIN z N' / 'ZPOPMAX z N' (any explicit count, incl count 1) are invisible to RDB/AOF persistence, replication, AND keyspace notifications -- data-loss + replica-divergence. Confirmed vs vendored 7.2.4: ZPOPMIN z 5 on a 3-elem set -> oracle rdb_changes_delta=3, fr=0; no keyevent fired (oracle zpopmin+del). Upstream t_zset.c::genericZpopCommand does server.dirty += popped. Fix: bump self.dirty by result.len() in both count methods. Found via keyspace-event differential probe. Same class as SPOP(bbutt)/ZRANGESTORE(bhd3u)/LPUSHX(maggg).
close_reason: Fixed: Store::zpopmin_count/zpopmax_count now bump self.dirty by result.len() (the popped count), mirroring the no-count zpopmin/zpopmax and upstream genericZpopCommand. ZPOPMIN/ZPOPMAX with any count are now visible to RDB/AOF persistence, replication, and keyspace notifications. Verified vs vendored 7.2.4: dirty deltas match (3/3/1/1) and the zpopmin+del keyevents now fire. 480 fr-store tests pass; regression test added.

title: SENTINEL active monitoring incomplete: master runid/flags/ping-times stay empty (no INFO ping loop)
description: Found alongside w6nhk (NobleRiver 2026-06-08): fr in --sentinel mode registers a master via SENTINEL MONITOR but does not actively connect/PING it, so SENTINEL MASTER reports runid='', flags='master,disconnected', last-ok-ping-reply/info-refresh/role-reported-time=0, vs redis-sentinel which pings the master and fills these. Also SENTINEL MYID returns a fixed placeholder (0123...4567) instead of a per-instance random run-id (non-deterministic; can't be byte-exact but should at least be unique). Structure/reply-format already byte-exact (w6nhk). This is the deeper active-monitoring loop in fr-sentinel — bigger feature.
close_reason: done

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: MYID sub-part FIXED (aa8bdbd15, NobleRiver 2026-06-08): SentinelState::new() no longer hardcodes a shared '0123...4567' myid — now generates a unique 40-hex run id per instance (SplitMix64 over SystemTime nanos + atomic seq, pure safe std). This also unblocks multi-sentinel discovery (self-hello filtering in discovery.rs keyed on myid was collapsing all peers into 'self'). Verified: 2 live instances distinct/stable/40-hex; new test; 165 fr-sentinel tests; sentinel_differ still byte-exact. STILL OPEN: the active-monitoring loop itself — fr-server must drive outbound PING/INFO connections to the monitored master on a tick and call the (already-implemented + unit-tested) fr-sentinel health.rs/discovery.rs fns (record_pong/record_info_response/apply_info_to_instance/discover_replicas_from_info) so runid/flags(disconnected->master)/last-ok-ping/info-refresh/role-reported fill in. That wiring (outbound conns in the mio loop + scheduling) is the remaining bulk.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: ACTIVE MONITORING LOOP LANDED (51475d810, NobleRiver 2026-06-08). fr-server now drives a 1s Sentinel tick: blocking PING+INFO each monitored master, folding via new Store/Runtime pass-throughs into the (already unit-tested) fr-sentinel health/discovery fns. LIVE VERIFIED vs redis 7.2.4 (master+replica): SENTINEL MASTER runid == master's actual INFO run_id (differential proof), flags 'master' (was 'master,disconnected') matching a real redis-sentinel, role-reported/role-reported-time populate, replica discovered (num-slaves=1, SENTINEL SLAVES lists it). Combined with the MYID fix (aa8bdbd15), the 'empty disconnected master forever' bug is RESOLVED. 165 fr-sentinel + 482 fr-runtime + fr-store/fr-server tests pass; sentinel_differ byte-exact. RESIDUAL (minor, fr-command-locked): the per-tick ms-ago delta fields (last-ok-ping-reply/info-refresh) render against state.previous_time so read ~0 within the 1s tick vs redis's exact sub-second value — fixing needs dispatch_sentinel_command (fr-command) to take a real now_ms. Closing: the missing INFO ping loop + empty runid/flags (the bead's substance) are done.

title: LTRIM emits no keyspace 'ltrim' event when the trim removes nothing (no-op)
description: Upstream ltrimCommand fires NOTIFY_LIST 'ltrim' (and signalModifiedKey) UNCONDITIONALLY for a valid LTRIM on an existing list, even when start/stop cover the whole list so nothing is removed (server.dirty += 0). fr ties keyspace events to dirty>0 via the post-command dispatcher, so a no-op LTRIM emitted no 'ltrim' event. Repro vs redis 7.2.4: RPUSH M a b; LTRIM M 0 5 -> redis fires 'ltrim', fr fired nothing. Trim-some and trim-empty already matched. Found via scripts/keyspace_notif_differ.py. Fix: Store::ltrim self-fires 'ltrim' for the removed==0 non-empty case (the dirty-gated dispatcher still covers removed>0; no double-fire).
notes: Fixed: Store::ltrim fires NOTIFY_LIST 'ltrim' for the no-op (removed==0, non-empty) case via decode_db_key, after the entry borrow ends. Verified vs redis: noop/some/empty all match (noop now ['ltrim'], some ['ltrim'] single-fire, empty ['ltrim','del']). keyspace_notif_differ seeds 42/314/1234 clean. Harness has occasional async-drain flakiness (not a parity bug).

title: XREAD/XREADGROUP emit Unbalanced error instead of syntax error when STREAMS has no following args
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:

  XREAD COUNT 5 STREAMS
    vendored: ERR syntax error
    fr:       ERR Unbalanced 'xread' list of streams: for each stream key an ID or '$' must be specified.

  XREAD BLOCK 10 STREAMS
    vendored: ERR syntax error
    fr:       ERR Unbalanced 'xread' list of streams: ...

  XREADGROUP GROUP g c COUNT 5 STREAMS
    vendored: ERR syntax error
    fr:       ERR Unbalanced 'xreadgroup' list of streams: ...

  XREADGROUP GROUP g c COUNT 5 NOACK STREAMS
    vendored: ERR syntax error
    fr:       ERR Unbalanced 'xreadgroup' list of streams: ...

Root cause: upstream t_stream.c::xreadCommand uses '!strcasecmp(o,"STREAMS") && moreargs' as the STREAMS-keyword match. When STREAMS is the last argument (moreargs==0), the && short-circuits, so the keyword doesn't match and the for-loop's terminal 'else' branch fires addReplyErrorObject(c, shared.syntaxerr).

fr's xread() / xreadgroup() accept STREAMS regardless of trailing arg count, then fall into the 'Unbalanced' branch when tail < 2. The Unbalanced wording is reserved upstream for odd-count tails (e.g. STREAMS k id1 id2 id3) — the empty-tail case should be the generic 'ERR syntax error'.

Fix: in both xread() and xreadgroup() at fr-command/src/lib.rs (lines ~6677 and ~6783), require at least one arg after the STREAMS keyword for it to match (return SyntaxError otherwise). The existing odd-count branch then correctly handles tail==1 / tail==3 / etc with the Unbalanced wording.
close_reason: Fixed in xread() and xreadgroup() at fr-command/src/lib.rs: after matching the STREAMS keyword, both functions now check 'idx + 1 >= argv.len()' and return SyntaxError if there are no following args. Matches upstream t_stream.c::xreadCommand's '!strcasecmp(o,"STREAMS") && moreargs' short-circuit. The odd-tail 'Unbalanced ... list of streams' path is preserved for tails of 1, 3, 5, etc. Regression test xread_xreadgroup_streams_with_empty_tail_returns_syntax_error_pnkd6 covers 6 empty-tail variants (XREAD with COUNT, BLOCK, BLOCK+COUNT; XREADGROUP with COUNT, COUNT+NOACK, BLOCK) plus 2 unbalanced-tail control cases. Live diff vs vendored 7.2.4 matches on all 9 probed variants.

title: XREADGROUP '>' re-delivery bumps PEL delivery count when redis keeps it
description: After group last-delivered is reset below an entry already in a consumer's PEL, a '>' read re-delivers it; fr bumps the PEL delivery_count (e.g. 1->2) but vendored redis 7.2.4 keeps it. Found via stream_xinfo_differ.py seed 1234 iter 2193 (XREADGROUP GROUP g1 c2 COUNT 1 STREAMS s2 > -> entry 25-0 c2 delivery: redis 1, fr 2). Independent of lpiwh/lag fixes. Root-cause in Store::xreadgroup NewEntries path (line ~11563 pending_entry.deliveries bump) vs redis streamReplyWithRange '>' handling of already-PEL entries (t_stream.c ~1756-1771).
notes: Fixed: fr '>' (NewEntries) delivery path set deliveries += 1; redis streamReplyWithRange creates a fresh NACK (delivery_count=1) and on reassignment (XGROUP SETID lowered the group ID so '>' re-serves a pending entry) RESETS delivery_count=1 (t_stream.c ~1768), never bumps. Changed fr to set deliveries=1 in the '>' path. Verified vs redis 7.2.4 minimal repro + stream_xinfo_differ.py clean: seed 1234 x5000 + seeds 7/42/999/2026 x2500 each, zero divergence.

title: CLIENT TRACKING: a read that returns WRONGTYPE does not register the key for invalidation
description:
Found alongside the COPY over-invalidation fix (6c9609729). When a tracked client
reads a key with the WRONG type (e.g. GET on a hash/list), redis still records the
key in the tracking table (trackingRememberKeys keys off the command's key specs /
lookupKeyRead, not the reply), so a later write to that key invalidates the client.
fr only records tracking keys in the Ok branch of the dispatch
(record_client_tracking_keys at fr-runtime ~10767), so a command returning a
WRONGTYPE error skips registration and the client is never invalidated.

Differential vs redis 7.2.4 (RESP3 CLIENT TRACKING ON):
  HSET h f 1; GET h (-> WRONGTYPE); HSET h f 2
  redis: invalidate push [h]   fr: no push

Reads via type-correct commands (HGET/LRANGE/SMEMBERS/ZRANGE) track correctly; only
the wrongtype-error read path misses registration. Fix: record client-tracking keys
for read commands based on their key specs regardless of whether the reply is an
error (mirroring upstream trackingRememberKeys), being careful not to register for
early rejections that never accessed a key (wrong arity, unknown command). Scope:
fr-runtime dispatch. Observability/cache-coherence only; data replies unaffected.
close_reason: Fixed in bcd191c1f: the dispatch outer Err arm now records client-tracking keys when the error is StoreError::WrongType and the command is a read (post-access registration, matching upstream trackingRememberKeys in afterCommand). Validation errors (wrong arity/syntax) access no key and stay unregistered. Byte-exact vs redis 7.2.4 (GET-on-hash / LRANGE-on-string / STRLEN-on-hash now register -> later write invalidates). 18-scenario tracking differ still passes; unit test wrongtype_read_registers_client_tracking_key. 495 fr-runtime tests pass, clippy clean.

title: XGROUP DELCONSUMER of a no-pending consumer does not bump dirty (gated on pending count, not consumer existence) — not persisted/replicated, no keyevent
close_reason: Fixed: Store::xgroup_delconsumer now bumps dirty when the consumer actually EXISTED (BTreeSet::remove returned true), not when removed_pending>0. Deleting a consumer with no pending entries was silently not persisted/replicated and fired no keyevent. Non-existent consumer stays a no-op. Byte-exact vs vendored 7.2.4 (xgroup-delconsumer keyevent fires for no-pending + with-pending, none for nonexistent).

title: Lua cjson encode/decode edge cases: sparse arrays, boolean keys, decode arg-validation
description:
Several cjson divergences vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 found via probe:

1. Sparse-array encoding: a table with integer keys 1..N where N exceeds
   the array part triggers vendored to render as an array with explicit
   nulls. fr falls back to an object map.

   EVAL "return cjson.encode({1,nil,3})" 0
     vendored: [1,null,3]
     fr:       {"1":1,"3":3}

   EVAL "local x={1}; x[4]='gap'; return cjson.encode(x)" 0
     vendored: [1,null,null,"gap"]
     fr:       {"1":1,"4":"gap"}

2. Boolean-key rejection: vendored raises when a table key is bool/
   table/function. fr stringifies the bool to "true"/"false".

   EVAL "return cjson.encode({[true]=1})" 0
     vendored: ERR Cannot serialise boolean: table key must be a number
               or string
     fr:       {"true":1}

3. decode() bad-arg checks missing:

   EVAL "return cjson.decode()" 0
     vendored: ERR bad argument #1 to 'decode' (expected 1 argument)
     fr:       (returns nil silently)

   EVAL "return cjson.decode(nil)" 0
     vendored: ERR bad argument #1 to 'decode' (string expected, got nil)
     fr:       ERR invalid JSON: nil

   EVAL "return cjson.decode('')" 0
     vendored: ERR Expected value but found T_END at character 1
     fr:       (returns nil silently)

4. cjson.null sentinel: vendored exposes cjson.null as a userdata that
   round-trips to JSON null. Decoding 'null' returns this sentinel, so
   type(cjson.decode('null')) == 'userdata'. fr returns LuaValue::Nil
   which has type 'nil'. (Deferred — needs userdata support; the rest
   of this bead is in-scope.)

Fix locus: crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs

- lua_value_to_json: detect "sparse array" tables (those with the
  longest contiguous-from-1 integer-key prefix, with the rest going as
  null padding up to max numeric key) — render those as array-with-
  nulls. Reject boolean/table/function keys with the upstream wording.
- cjson.decode arm: check arity and string type before parsing.

Test plan: pin sparse-array round-trips, boolean-key rejection, and
the three decode arg-validation cases.
close_reason: lua_value_to_json in crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs now mirrors lua_cjson.c's array-detection algorithm: (1) reject bool/table/function keys with 'Cannot serialise TYPE: table key must be a number or string'; (2) when ALL keys are positive integers, apply the upstream sparse-safe=10 / sparse-ratio=2 threshold — pass → render as array padding gaps with null; fail → raise 'Cannot serialise table: excessively sparse array' (matching encode_sparse_convert=false default); (3) mixed/string-keyed tables fall through to the object form unchanged. cjson.decode now arg-validates strictly: zero args raises 'expected 1 argument', non-string-non-number raises 'string expected, got TYPE', empty/whitespace string raises 'Expected value but found T_END at character 1'. Regression test lua_cjson_sparse_array_and_decode_args_pt4d4 pins 9 cases. Live diff vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 on :16380 clean across 15 probes.

title: fr-conformance: pubsub_unknown_subcommand fixture case ran in subscribed state, expected non-subscribed wording
description:
core_pubsub.json had pubsub_unknown_subcommand at position 23, after subscribe_for_pubsub_test (case 12, SUBSCRIBE ch1) and pubsub_numpat_with_pattern (case 14, PSUBSCRIBE alert.*) entered subscribe mode. fr correctly returned upstream-aligned 'ERR Can\'t execute \'pubsub|nosuchsub\': only (P|S)SUBSCRIBE / (P|S)UNSUBSCRIBE / PING / QUIT / RESET are allowed in this context' (matches processCommand's allow-list at server.c:4067-4082; PUBSUB-the-command is not in upstream's allow list). The case was intended to pin the unknown-subcommand wording from a non-subscribed connection ('ERR unknown subcommand \'NOSUCHSUB\'. Try PUBSUB HELP.').

Fix: move the case to position 12 — between pubsub_numpat_zero and subscribe_for_pubsub_test — so the unknown-subcommand path runs before any subscribe state lands. Adding cleanup after the case would have broken later cases (spublish_no_subscribers, cleanup_unsubscribe_all) that depend on lingering subscriptions.

title: fr-store: hash field TTL needs golden-artifact pin against Redis 7.4 spec since vendored Redis 7.2.4 oracle returns 'unknown command'
description:
fr-store implements the Redis 7.4 hash-field TTL family (HEXPIRE/HPEXPIRE/HEXPIREAT/HPEXPIREAT, HTTL/HPTTL/HEXPIRETIME/HPEXPIRETIME, HPERSIST). Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4:

  HEXPIRE h 60 FIELDS 1 f
    upstream: ERR unknown command 'HEXPIRE' ...
    fr      : 1

This is a feature fr ships ahead of the vendored oracle, not a divergence — but it means the live conformance harness can't validate the response codes against upstream. The existing inline tests cover the matrix (hexpire_sets_ttl_and_returns_applied_code, hexpire_nx_xx_gt_lt_flag_matrix_matches_upstream_codes, etc.) but a documented golden anchored to the Redis 7.4 spec response codes makes future regressions and refactor drift visible without touching the live-oracle path.

Acceptance: a conformance/golden test pins the documented Redis 7.4 return codes for HEXPIRE applied/already-set/no-ttl/in-the-past/missing-field cases. (Upstream: HEXPIRE returns Array of N integers — 1 applied, 2 deleted-due-to-past, 0 NX-XX-GT-LT-skipped, -2 no-such-field; HTTL returns -1 no-ttl, -2 no-field, positive seconds remaining.)

Ship a regression test in this same diff.

Reference: /testing-conformance-harnesses + /porting-to-rust audit on 2026-05-04 — note that this resolves a stale gap previously documented in agent project memory under hash_field_ttl_gap; the implementation has landed and tests exist, the gap is documentary.
notes: Added hash_field_ttl_chained_hexpire_httl_hpersist_round_trip_golden in crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs. Pins the full Redis 7.4 spec response codes for a chained scenario: HEXPIRE applies (1) and reports -2 for missing field; HTTL surfaces the live remaining seconds; HEXPIRE NX skips when TTL set ([0]); XX applies ([1]); LT applies/skips on the proposed-vs-current comparison; GT applies when proposed > current; HPERSIST reports persisted (1)/no-such-field (-2); final HTTL returns -1 for fields with no TTL. fr-conformance can't validate this against vendored Redis 7.2.4 (HEXPIRE returns 'unknown command'), so this golden anchors the contract directly to the 7.4 spec.

title: perf(dispatch): borrowed fast-path for write commands (SET-options/SETNX/INCR/DECR/APPEND/SETEX)
description: h8gbc CLOSED: 13 READ commands now on the borrowed runtime fast-path (GET/HGET/MGET/EXISTS/STRLEN/GETRANGE/HMGET/SISMEMBER/HEXISTS/LLEN/SCARD/LINDEX/ZSCORE), each ~1.4-2x. Plain SET already fast-pathed (execute_plain_set_borrowed). NEXT TIER = the hottest remaining WRITES (writes are harder: they mutate, dirty++, may propagate to AOF/replica, fire keyspace notifications — but the fast-path predicate plain_borrowed_default_key_read_allows ALREADY disables on aof_path.is_some()/replicas/notify/tracking, so in the conservative default state a plain write is safe to fast-path like SET is). Candidates, one per commit, same recipe: SETNX (set-if-absent, Integer), APPEND (Integer new-len), INCR/DECR/INCRBY/DECRBY (Integer, parse), SETEX/PSETEX (with TTL — careful: expiry propagation), GETSET/GETDEL (return old). MUST mirror SET's existing borrowed write predicate (can_execute_plain_set_borrowed) NOT the read one — writes need the dirty/propagation bookkeeping the SET path already does. Study execute_plain_set_borrowed + record_plain_set_borrowed_metrics as the template. Golden-prove byte-identical incl keyspace-notification + dirty-count parity (use a differ that also checks INFO keyspace/rdb_changes_since_last_save). DEEPER alt (NO-CEILING): instead of per-command, generalize the borrowed dispatch so ALL commands operate on borrowed argv without the copy_borrowed_argv_into_scratch owned-Vec materialization (zero-copy dispatch end-to-end) — bigger structural swing, ~all-command alloc reduction.
notes: APPEND borrowed write fast-path IMPLEMENTED + PROVEN but commit-BLOCKED: OrangeMouse reserved fr-runtime mid-turn (was empty at start). Respected the lock (no GUARD_MODE=warn override). Patches saved (apply clean on HEAD, ZERO leak symbols): /data/projects/.scratch/handoffs/append_q0qym_fr-runtime_NobleRiver.patch (3 hunks: plain_append_owned_argv + can/execute/record_plain_append_borrowed + 2 parity tests) + append_q0qym_fr-server_NobleRiver.patch (2 hunks: borrowed_plain_append_args matcher + dispatch branch) + append_fastpath_golden.py. PROOF: 2 fr-runtime parity tests pass (fresh compile); golden candidate==baseline==oracle byte-exact d38be4e3 (436B) over create/grow/empty/numeric/wrongtype/binary/SELECT1/MULTI-EXEC. Bench inconclusive (host load 28-54); mechanism-identical to DECR (3.3x last turn). LAND when fr-runtime frees: git apply both patches + cp golden, then commit. NOTE: had to clean up heavy shared-worktree contamination (OrangeMouse's get_read_value/BulkStringInline GET-refactor interleaved in fr-runtime/fr-server/fr-protocol/fr-store/lua_eval); restored their WIP (now write_buf_start cursor, compiles). REMAINING q0qym: SETNX/GETSET/GETDEL/SETEX.

Reversed P16/1M candidate 6.376s +/- 0.669s vs baseline 8.025s +/- 0.212s, 1.26x +/- 0.14 faster. Golden candidate==baseline==Redis oracle sha256 d38be4e317ec2f452852b91575d1a7fb906f7a4357af9a80d9ac1451595aeb71. Remaining write fast paths split to frankenredis-6tsou.

title: fr-command COMMAND DOCS reply emits flat Array under RESP3 — upstream wraps in addReplyMapLen for both outer envelope and per-arg entries
description:
REVIEW MODE finding extending the i40x2/e4njz/udl3y/asvh1/5d04d/ndz0j/5h86s/cz712/ec2rf RESP3-Map-shape audit cluster.

Source:
  - fr-command/src/lib.rs:12876 — outer COMMAND DOCS reply: Ok(RespFrame::Array(Some(docs)))
  - fr-command/src/lib.rs:13192 — per-command entry returned as RespFrame::Array(Some(entry))

Upstream server.c::commandDocsCommand wraps the outer reply in addReplyMapLen and each per-command entry in addReplyMapLen (per the metadata fields summary/since/group/complexity/history/arguments).

The outer reply is a Map of {command_name → command_doc} pairs; each command_doc is itself a Map. Both are flat Arrays in fr regardless of session protocol version.

Reachable from any client running 'COMMAND DOCS' under HELLO 3.

Fix: thread store.dispatch_client_ctx.resp_protocol_version through command_cmd → command_docs_entry. Outer envelope branches: RESP3 → RespFrame::Map of (name, doc); RESP2 → flat Array. Inner per-command doc envelope branches: RESP3 → RespFrame::Map of (key, value); RESP2 → flat Array.

(Per-argument inner shape is a separate even-deeper bug — fr's args are oversimplified flat 4/6-elem Arrays; upstream emits ~7-entry Map per arg with display_text/key_spec_index/token/etc. That deeper feature gap is filed separately and will be ignored for this cycle's tick.)
close_reason: Threaded store.dispatch_client_ctx.resp_protocol_version through command_cmd → command_docs_entry. Outer COMMAND DOCS reply now branches: RESP3 → RespFrame::Map of (command_name, doc_map) pairs; RESP2 → existing flat alternating Array. Each per-command entry similarly branches: RESP3 → RespFrame::Map of (key, value) pairs (mirroring upstream server.c::commandDocsCommand's addReplyMapLen); RESP2 → existing flat alternating Array. Two new pin tests: command_docs_under_resp3_returns_outer_and_inner_maps (asserts outer Map → inner Map → 'summary' first key) and command_docs_under_resp2_stays_flat_outer_and_inner_array. All 5 existing command_docs tests still pass; clippy clean. Tenth in the RESP3-Map-shape audit cluster (i40x2/e4njz/udl3y/asvh1/5d04d/ndz0j/5h86s/cz712/ec2rf/q11am).

Repro: compare vendored redis-server redis-cli --raw EVAL "return error('boom')" 0 and fr-command eval_cmd/eval_script on the same script. Fix in same tick: implement Lua error level handling for level 0 vs default and add regression coverage.

title: ZRANGEBYLEX/ZREVRANGEBYLEX deep LIMIT offset O(offset) — same treap-select fix as yozwx
description: Sibling of yozwx (ZRANGEBYSCORE deep-offset, e5ea9dd9a). The lex range-limited paths (lib.rs ~1277-1300, lex_bound_range_limited or equivalent) also apply .skip(offset) on the ordered BTreeMap = O(offset). Uniform-score zsets: ZRANGEBYLEX zl - + LIMIT 450000 10 = oracle ~1.1ms vs fr ~3.5ms (3.2x). Same fix: for a single-score (uniform) zset, rank == member lex rank (the lex_count 7uonw path already relies on this), so rank_of(lex bound) + select jumps to the start in O(take*log n). Gate to deep offset + warm treap + adaptive warming. Byte-exact.
close_reason: Fixed (sibling of yozwx): ZRANGEBYLEX/ZREVRANGEBYLEX/ZRANGE-BYLEX deep LIMIT offset jumps to the start rank via treap rank_of(lex bounds, same as lex_count 7uonw)+select, O(take*log n), instead of O(offset) BTreeMap walk. Gated to deep offset + adaptive warming; cold/shallow fall back to scan. ZRANGEBYLEX LIMIT 450000 10 (500k uniform): 3274us->70us (46x), 11x faster than redis. Byte-exact: 12 curated (ASC/REV/inclusive/exclusive/edges) + lex_range_window isomorphism test + zset_differ 4000 iters. golden 21a1d803.

title: WAITAOF numlocal=1 immediately under appendfsync=everysec (vs redis 0-then-1) — intentional synchronous-fsync-visibility divergence
description: Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4: after a write under appendfsync=everysec, redis WAITAOF reports numlocal=0 (the ~1s background fsync hasn't run; becomes 1 later) while fr reports numlocal=1 immediately. Root: Runtime::capture_aof_record advances replication_ack_state.local_fsync_offset=primary_offset inline whenever local_waitaof_fsync_tracks_primary_offset() (aof enabled + appendfsync!=No), modelling the AOF append as durably persisted at write time. This is fr's documented synchronous-fsync-visibility design: fr's AOF is state-snapshot based (persist_snapshot_to_disk/to_aof_commands), not append-per-write, so there is no real per-second background fsync to lag behind. The e2e test tcp_waitaof_appendfsync_no_keeps_local_ack_visible_across_bgrewriteaof explicitly documents legacy WAITAOF fsync timing as kernel-timing-dependent and deliberately does NOT equality-compare fr vs legacy for these replies. Matching redis would require modelling everysec's 1s fsync cadence (time-gated cron advance + blocked-WAITAOF re-eval), contradicting the design and risking the WAITAOF blocking tests. Error paths are correct (numlocal>0 with appendonly off errors; replica rejected). Recommend WONTFIX. See memory project_waitaof_everysec_synchronous.
close_reason: WONTFIX — intentional synchronous-fsync-visibility divergence. fr's AOF is state-snapshot based (not append-per-write), so there is no per-second background fsync to lag; reporting numlocal=1 at write time is the deliberate design (see e2e tcp_waitaof_appendfsync_no_keeps_local_ack_visible_across_bgrewriteaof comment treating legacy timing as kernel-dependent and not equality-comparing it). Matching redis's everysec lag would require modelling a 1s fsync cadence + blocked-WAITAOF re-eval, contradicting the design and risking existing WAITAOF tests. Documented in memory project_waitaof_everysec_synchronous; filter WAITAOF-under-everysec from differential probes.

title: Sentinel hello processing should advance epochs without address changes
description: Upstream Redis 7.2.4 sentinelProcessHelloMessage updates sentinel.current_epoch whenever a received hello advertises a larger current_epoch, and updates master->config_epoch when the hello's master_config_epoch is newer even if the master address is unchanged. fr-sentinel process_hello_message only returns UpdateMasterAddr when a newer config epoch also changes the address, so same-address epoch bumps and peer current_epoch advancement are lost. Acceptance: applying a valid hello advances SentinelState.current_epoch from hello.current_epoch, updates master.config_epoch for newer same-address hello messages, and preserves existing sentinel discovery/update behavior.
close_reason: Applied hello current_epoch and master config_epoch updates during Sentinel discovery

title: [perf] Allocation-free integer string memory estimation
description:
Profile-backed pass30 target after fresh current-HEAD profiling.

Initial hypothesis was recv/parser-side work because SET pipeline=16 strace still showed recvfrom 62,550 calls with 31,250 WouldBlock errors. Local mio 1.2.0 source confirms epoll uses EPOLLET, so read-until-WouldBlock is required and the old one-read shortcut is not a safe lever.

Fresh GDB sampling during the same workload pointed instead at fr-store memory estimation: samples hit is_int_encoded_string -> i64::to_string -> String push/copy through estimate_string_value_memory_usage_bytes / estimate_entry_memory_usage_bytes. Current code parses a UTF-8 integer and then allocates n.to_string() solely to check canonical integer encoding.

One lever: replace is_int_encoded_string's parse + allocating to_string canonicality check with a byte-level canonical signed i64 parser/check that preserves exact accepted forms.

Proof requirements:
- rch-built baseline already recorded: direct 293429.18 ops/sec, p99 4723 us; hyperfine 1.612s +/-0.038s.
- Golden MEMORY USAGE / SET / GET trace sha256 for canonical ints, non-canonical ints, plus signs, negative zero, overflow, non-UTF8/non-integer strings.
- Isomorphism: same integer-encoding classification, same memory-usage replies, same command order; FP/RNG N/A.
- Re-benchmark via rch; keep only if Score >=2.0, otherwise remove source and record rejection.
close_reason: Measured allocation-free integer memory-estimate candidate: direct 299429.99 -> 297971.21 ops/sec and paired hyperfine 1.590s -> 1.635s, so rejected below Score>=2.0; source removed; golden sha256 95bf66c334f215e45952c101db3ebeced83ee83074f1654aca19f1c6a7bb04e9.

title: Lua cjson.decode strict-parser rewrite (rejects trailing commas, missing values, non-string keys)
description:
fr's json_to_lua_value is a permissive string-matcher: it accepts
malformed JSON that vendored Redis's lua-cjson decoder rejects. Probed
divergences:

  EVAL "return cjson.decode('[1,2,]')" 0
    vendored: Expected value but found T_ARR_END at character 6
    fr:       (returns {1,2} — silently accepts trailing comma)

  EVAL "return cjson.decode('{\"a\":1,}')" 0
    vendored: Expected object key string but found T_OBJ_END at character 8
    fr:       (returns empty table — silently accepts trailing comma)

  EVAL "return cjson.decode('{1:2}')" 0
    vendored: Expected object key string but found T_NUMBER at character 2
    fr:       (returns 2 — non-string keys silently accepted)

  EVAL "return cjson.decode('{\"a\":}')" 0
    vendored: Expected value but found T_OBJ_END at character 6
    fr:       (returns empty)

  EVAL "return cjson.decode('[1,2 3]')" 0
    vendored: Expected comma or array end but found T_NUMBER at character 6
    fr:       invalid JSON: 2 3

  EVAL "return cjson.decode('\"abc')" 0
    vendored: Expected value but found unexpected end of string at character 5
    fr:       invalid JSON: "abc

  EVAL "return cjson.decode('abc')" 0
    vendored: Expected value but found invalid token at character 1
    fr:       invalid JSON: abc

Fix locus: crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs — replace the current
permissive matcher with a tokenizer-driven recursive-descent parser
that produces upstream-shaped errors with character offsets.

This is a substantial rewrite (~200 lines). Priority 4 — affects
parity but only for malformed JSON which real apps shouldn't send.

Test plan: pin each of the 7 cases above with upstream wording, plus
the existing positive cases (the cjson_sparse_array_and_decode_args
test) must continue to pass.
close_reason: done

title: fr-command: HSCAN/SSCAN/ZSCAN parse trailing args before checking key existence, leaking syntax errors for missing keys
description:
Upstream t_hash.c::hscanCommand (and the SSCAN/ZSCAN siblings) lookup the key via lookupKeyReadOrReply *before* delegating to scanGenericCommand, so missing keys reply with shared.emptyscan ([0, []]) regardless of any unknown trailing arguments. fr-command parsed the args first, so for example HSCAN nokey 0 NOVALUES extra returned 'ERR syntax error' even though the key didn't exist.

Differential probe vs vendored redis 7.2.4:
  fr:    ERR syntax error
  redis: 0  (empty scan)

Same divergence on SSCAN/ZSCAN. Wrong-type keys still emit WRONGTYPE; existing keys with bad args still emit syntax error.

Fix: reorder hscan/sscan/zscan to check key_type first — return empty_scan_reply() for missing key, WRONGTYPE for wrong type — before parse_scan_args.
