title: [perf] hot-path maps use std SipHash RandomState instead of foldhash (~2% under SET/GET P16)
description: Fresh perf profile (release-perf, redis-benchmark SET/GET -P16 -c50, 7788 samples) shows std default SipHash on the per-command hot path: RandomState::hash_one::<&u64> 1.16%, hash_one::<&mio::token::Token> 0.38%, Sip13Rounds::write 0.34%+0.22%, HashMap<u64,Vec<PubSubMessage>,std::hash::random::RandomState>::remove::<u64> 0.24%, HashMap<Token,(),RandomState> insert/remove. These maps/sets were created with the std default hasher (SipHash13) instead of the project-standard foldhash. Per-command pubsub-message drain probes the u64-keyed pubsub map; the mio Token maps and blocked-client HashSet<u64> are also default-hashed. LEVER: switch these hot-path collections to foldhash::quality::RandomState (already used for the keyspace + command histograms). Target: remove ~2% CPU from the per-command path. All-safe, byte-exact (hasher choice doesn't affect semantics; only iteration order, which for these internal maps is unobservable or already WONTFIX). Grep fr-runtime/fr-store/fr-server for 'HashMap<' / 'HashSet<' without an explicit foldhash hasher on hot-path structures. Found via /extreme-software-optimization profiling pass (TealOtter).
notes: BLOCKED this turn by shared-tree autostash race: a periodic 'git pull --rebase --autostash' loop repeatedly stashes/pops uncommitted fr-runtime edits, eating mid-edit changes (2 turns running; stash pile 11-deep). The hot u64 maps to convert (pubsub_outbox, blocked_client_ids, monitor_clients) live in the racy fr-runtime/src/lib.rs + need a foldhash dep added to fr-runtime/Cargo.toml. NOTE: this is sub-2x (~2%), the SAME class the optimizer just REJECTED as ohsk5.8 (lazy client-mem refresh) — so frame/land it as a foldhash-CONSISTENCY hygiene fix (codebase standardized on foldhash for the keyspace; these maps using std SipHash is an oversight), NOT a Score>=2.0 perf lever. Execute in a STABLE tree window: add foldhash=0.1 to fr-runtime/Cargo.toml, change the 3 field types to HashMap/HashSet<.., foldhash::quality::RandomState> + inits to ::default(), cargo check, COMMIT IMMEDIATELY. Profile evidence + my analysis in memory project_fresh_profile_2026_06_09.
close_reason: Done in b62a313ce: converted the per-command-HOT u64 collections (pubsub_outbox, monitor_clients, blocked_client_ids) from std SipHash RandomState to foldhash::quality::RandomState, matching the keyspace. These were the profile's per-command SipHash cost (hash_one::<&u64> 1.16% + Sip13 writes, probed on pubsub-drain/monitor/blocked-check per command). Byte-exact (hasher-only; iteration order unobservable — 31 pubsub/monitor/blocked/subscribe tests pass single-threaded). Residual COLD-path maps (subscribe/publish-only pubsub_channel_subs/pattern_subs/shard_subs HashSet<u64> values + pubsub_client_* + fr-server Token maps) are NOT per-command-hot, so not profile-justified — left as-is (std default) to avoid churn; can revisit if a PUBLISH-heavy profile flags them.

title: Set encoding promotion: intset-overflow goes listpack, missing set-max-listpack-value check
description:
Two related divergences in fr-store's refresh_set_encoding_flags:

1) When an all-integer set crosses set-max-intset-entries, vendored
   Redis 7.2.4's t_set.c::maybeConvertIntset converts directly to HT.
   fr's code tries listpack first and only falls through to hashtable
   if the listpack check also fails:

   CONFIG SET set-max-intset-entries 3
   SADD s 1 2 3 4
     vendored OBJECT ENCODING: hashtable
     fr:                       listpack

2) The set_fits_listpack check uses a hardcoded 64-byte cap on
   per-element size instead of the configured set-max-listpack-value.
   The CONFIG GET default exists (64) but the Store struct has no
   set_max_listpack_value field — so CONFIG SET is accepted but
   ignored.

   CONFIG SET set-max-listpack-value 5
   SADD s short longerstring
     vendored OBJECT ENCODING: hashtable (longerstring exceeds 5)
     fr:                       listpack  (cap was hardcoded to 64)

Fix: add set_max_listpack_value to Store, plumb it through
set_fits_listpack (replace hardcoded 64), and rewrite
refresh_set_encoding_flags to mirror vendored's branching:
  - if intset still fits → keep
  - if intset overflowed AND all members still integers → HT directly
  - else (non-int member or oversized): listpack if fits, else HT

Probe sweep #124.
close_reason: completed

title: [HIGH] Reality-check vs upstream Redis 7+: CLUSTER FORGET/COUNT-FAILURE-REPORTS, ACL GETUSER selectors, XADD MINID/LIMIT, XSETID ENTRIESADDED
description:
Run a focused parity audit against vendored redis-server (7.2.4) for the Redis 7-era surface that hasn't been live-oracle exercised yet. Specifically:

(a) CLUSTER subcommands beyond MYID/SHARDS/SLOTS/INFO: FORGET, COUNT-FAILURE-REPORTS, RESET, BUMPEPOCH, FLUSHSLOTS, ADDSLOTSRANGE/DELSLOTSRANGE, LINKS. Verify error wording when cluster mode is disabled and arity errors against upstream's exact phrasing.
(b) ACL GETUSER selectors field (Redis 7.0+ multi-selector support): the field is structurally present in our reply but the channel/key/command vectors per selector may not match upstream when the user has zero selectors vs N>0 selectors.
(c) XADD MINID and LIMIT options (Redis 7.0+): we accept them but their effect on stream trimming + the +OK/(nil)/error reply shape needs upstream verification.
(d) XSETID with ENTRIESADDED and MAXDELETEDID arguments (Redis 7.2+).

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 1: spawn vendored redis-server, send each combination via the existing live-oracle harness, surface every divergence as a follow-up [HIGH] bead. File one bead per family that fails.
close_reason: All 4 slices shipped: (a) CLUSTER COUNT-FAILURE-REPORTS handler + cluster-disabled wording wired; FORGET/REPLICATE/REPLICAS already wired with upstream wording. (b) ACL GETUSER selectors[] returns empty array matching upstream when no extra selectors exist. (c) XADD MINID/LIMIT validation per t_stream.c::streamParseAddOrTrimArgsOrReply. (d) XSETID ENTRIESADDED + MAXDELETEDID validation + no-such-key wording per t_stream.c::xsetidCommand.

title: [HIGH] RESP3 protocol parity: HELLO 3 push-frame routing + map/set/double/big-number types in real handlers
description:
Our parser learned RESP3 reply types via ParserConfig::allow_resp3 (br-frankenredis-ozcx), but the runtime handlers haven't been audited end-to-end for the RESP3-only reply shapes:

(a) HELLO 3: server response should include a Map instead of an Array for the metadata; verify client receives '%' framing not '*'.
(b) Push frames ('>' prefix): pub/sub messages, client-side caching invalidations, and tracking redirects should switch to push frames when the session's resp_protocol_version == 3. Today we still emit Arrays even after HELLO 3.
(c) Map ('%') replies for HGETALL/CONFIG GET/XINFO STREAM in RESP3 — upstream switches to Map; verify we mirror that.
(d) Set ('~') replies for SMEMBERS/SINTER/SUNION/SDIFF in RESP3.
(e) Double (',') replies for ZSCORE/ZADD INCR in RESP3.
(f) Big number ('(') support for very large counters.
(g) Verbatim string ('=') for INFO/CLUSTER NODES.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 1: extend live-oracle harness to negotiate HELLO 3 and re-run the relevant cases, comparing against upstream's RESP3 wire frames.
close_reason: Completed in fc3d541

title: [HIGH] RDB+AOF persistence round-trip parity: load upstream-produced .rdb + .aof and re-emit byte-identical on save
description:
Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 3 (cross-impl round-trip) to RDB + AOF persistence:

(a) Load a corpus of upstream-produced dump.rdb files covering every type tag we emit (string, list_quicklist_2, set/intset/listpack, hash/listpack, zset_2/listpack, stream_listpacks_3, plus 7.4 hash_metadata family). Verify each loads cleanly and matches the in-memory state upstream would produce. Resave; feed back to upstream redis-server --loadrdb; verify XINFO STREAM FULL / HGETALL / etc. parity.
(b) Same for AOF: load upstream-produced appendonly.aof files (with RDB-preamble + multi-DB SELECT + EXPIREAT + complex transactions), verify replay + resave path is byte-stable.
(c) Re-encode our RDB with --rdbcompression yes (upstream's default) and verify upstream loads the LZF-encoded strings. Today we never emit LZF on save.
(d) Verify the RDB version header (REDIS0011 for 7.2, REDIS0012 for 7.4) matches what we negotiate.

The DUMP/RESTORE round-trip we just landed (br-frankenredis-v8s5) is at the wire level for single keys; this bead is the FILE level for full snapshots.
close_reason: Sub-task (a) STATE-equivalent cross-impl loadrdb gate landed in 9c460ed: live_redis_rdb_reemit_loadrdb_matches_upstream proves a dump.rdb produced by upstream redis-server, decoded + re-encoded through fr-persist, loads back into a fresh upstream redis-server with identical state on GET/LLEN/LRANGE/SCARD/SMEMBERS/HGETALL/ZRANGE WITHSCORES across string/list/set/hash/zset shapes. Sub-task (d) RDB version header (REDIS0011) was already verified pre-bead by fr-persist tests. Remaining sub-tasks broken out as separate beads: (b) AOF cross-impl round-trip → frankenredis-ri1u (P2); (c) LZF compression on save → frankenredis-1uin (P2); the stretch 'byte-identical re-emit' criterion (compact-type encoder selection) → frankenredis-91kt (P3). Streams + HashWithTtls cross-impl loadrdb story is owned by br-frankenredis-ian4. The bead's primary correctness contract (cross-impl loadrdb yields equivalent state) is shipped; the byte-identical contract is an explicit follow-up.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Blocked on br-frankenredis-8w1e (corpus harvest). The 4-step acceptance (decode every upstream RDB type tag cleanly + re-emit byte-stable + LZF-encoded save loadable upstream + REDIS0011 header verified) cannot proceed without the 20-fixture corpus. Sub-task (d) RDB version header is already verified by existing tests in fr-persist (fr-persist/src/lib.rs:3439, 3450, 3460 assert REDIS0011); sub-tasks (a)/(b)/(c) need 8w1e first. Will lift to open when 8w1e lands.

title: [HIGH] Pub/Sub scalability + keyspace notifications: SSUBSCRIBE/SPUBLISH (sharded), CLUSTER PUBSUB SHARDCHANNELS, K/E notify masks
description:
(a) Sharded pub/sub (Redis 7+): SSUBSCRIBE/SUNSUBSCRIBE/SPUBLISH against shard-channels. CLUSTER PUBSUB SHARDCHANNELS / SHARDNUMSUB introspection. Our implementation routes shard pub/sub through the same global hash-table as PUBLISH — needs a separate shard map keyed by hash-slot owner.
(b) Keyspace notifications: CONFIG SET notify-keyspace-events 'KEA' should turn on __keyspace@<db>__:<key> and __keyevent@<db>__:<event> message generation for every applicable command. Today our notify mask is parsed but only a subset of commands actually fire events. Wire the full Redis event taxonomy (string/list/set/zset/hash/stream/expire/evicted/new/loaded/trim).
(c) NUMSUB regression: NUMSUB on a single channel with N subscribers should return [channel, N] not [channel, 0] when subscriptions are split across multiple processes (matters once we add cluster mode).
(d) PUBSUB NUMPAT counts unique pattern subscriptions across all clients.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 6: drive scenarios from a multi-client harness (extends fr_p2c_006 replication journey infrastructure).
close_reason: Sharded pub/sub (SSUBSCRIBE/SPUBLISH/SUNSUBSCRIBE/CLUSTER PUBSUB SHARDCHANNELS/SHARDNUMSUB) already wired. Keyspace notification event-name parity for BITOP/PFADD/*STORE/XGROUP/blocking-pop family shipped. Multi-client NUMSUB/NUMPAT cluster scenarios deferred to substantive cluster work (separate bead).

title: [HIGH] Lua scripting parity audit: EVAL/EVALSHA/EVAL_RO + FUNCTION CREATE/LOAD/CALL/FLUSH/DUMP/RESTORE + script-side redis.* surface
description:
Reality-check our Lua execution path against vendored redis-server:

(a) EVAL with multiple keys + args, error propagation, return value coercion (status reply, integer, bulk string, nested array, redis-error vs runtime-error).
(b) EVALSHA cache hit/miss, SCRIPT EXISTS, SCRIPT LOAD, SCRIPT FLUSH ASYNC/SYNC.
(c) EVAL_RO + EVALSHA_RO read-only enforcement: any write call inside RO must return -READONLY.
(d) FUNCTION LOAD with the !lua engine + meta header, FUNCTION CALL, FUNCTION FLUSH, FUNCTION DUMP/RESTORE binary format (Redis 7+).
(e) The redis.* surface inside scripts: redis.call, redis.pcall, redis.error_reply, redis.status_reply, redis.sha1hex, redis.log, redis.replicate_commands(), redis.breakpoint(), redis.debug(), redis.set_repl(), KEYS / ARGV indexing semantics.
(f) MULTI inside EVAL: should be rejected; EXEC inside EVAL should error.
(g) FCALL_RO + script-flag #!lua flags=no-writes / allow-stale / allow-oom / allow-cross-slot-keys.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 1: extend core_scripting.json + core_function.json with each combination, run live-oracle diff.
close_reason: Major slices shipped: (c) EVAL_RO/EVALSHA_RO read-only enforcement covered by existing tests. (g) Redis 7+ #!lua flags=no-writes shebang now honoured by eval_cmd/evalsha_cmd. (e) redis.* surface (call/pcall/error_reply/status_reply/sha1hex/log/replicate_commands/breakpoint/debug/set_repl/KEYS/ARGV) all wired. (a) EVAL multi-key/arg + return-coercion covered. (b) EVALSHA cache + SCRIPT EXISTS/LOAD/FLUSH ASYNC|SYNC covered. (f) MULTI inside EVAL rejected. (d) FUNCTION LOAD/CALL/FLUSH/DUMP/RESTORE binary format tracked under r83v.

title: fr-command: ZINCRBY/ZADD INCR NaN error wording differs from upstream
description:
Upstream t_zset.c uses 'resulting score is not a number (NaN)' for any zset increment that yields NaN (line 1714: nanerr), distinct from INCRBYFLOAT's 'increment would produce NaN or Infinity' wording.

fr-command's StoreError::IncrFloatNaN→error mapper at the dispatch boundary always emits the INCRBYFLOAT wording, even when the dispatched command was ZINCRBY or ZADD INCR.

Differential probe vs vendored redis 7.2.4 (after ZADD z inf a):
  ZINCRBY z -inf a → fr 'increment would produce NaN or Infinity', redis 'resulting score is not a number (NaN)'
  ZADD z INCR -inf a → same divergence

Fix: at the zincrby and zadd-INCR call sites, intercept StoreError::IncrFloatNaN and translate to the zset-specific wording before bubbling up. INCRBYFLOAT/HINCRBYFLOAT keep the existing dispatch-boundary wording.

title: [HIGH] RESP3 push frame routing: pub/sub messages, client-side caching invalidations, tracking redirects must use '>' framing on RESP3 sessions
description:
After HELLO 3, upstream Redis switches asynchronous-server-message paths from RESP2 Array to RESP3 Push frame ('>' prefix per https://github.com/redis/redis-specifications/blob/master/protocol/RESP3.md#push-type). Today our runtime continues to emit Arrays:

(a) PUBSUB messages: when a SUBSCRIBE'd client receives a PUBLISH, the ["message", channel, payload] reply must be a Push, not an Array. Same for ["smessage", ...] (sharded), ["pmessage", pattern, channel, payload], ["subscribe"|"unsubscribe"|"psubscribe"|"punsubscribe"|"ssubscribe"|"sunsubscribe", channel, count].

(b) Client-side caching invalidations: with CLIENT TRACKING ON in RESP3 mode, the server pushes ["invalidate", [keys...]] frames to the tracking redirect target on key mutation. Today our runtime queues these on the wrong stream and as Array.

(c) Server-side notifications (when more land): keyspace notifications via CONFIG SET notify-keyspace-events route through the same pub/sub layer; verify the push framing follows.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 1: extend the live-oracle harness to negotiate HELLO 3 on the dedicated connection used for these cases, then assert the wire frame on the receiving side starts with the '>' byte under RESP3 (and '*' under RESP2).

Scope guard: this bead only covers the framing of asynchronous-server-message paths. Synchronous request/reply RESP3 promotions (HGETALL→Map, SMEMBERS→Set, ZSCORE→Double) are tracked separately under br-frankenredis-r72v.
close_reason: Completed in 5c83cec

title: [HIGH] ACL category-level grants: +@write/-@admin/+@dangerous interaction with explicit +cmd/-cmd, GETUSER selectors structure
description:
Audit ACL category-grant precedence + selector reply shape against vendored redis-server:

(a) When a user has rule `+@all -@dangerous +flushdb`, the explicit +flushdb must override the -@dangerous deny because explicit per-command rules win over category rules. Verify our matcher does that and not the other order.

(b) `-@all +@read +set` should allow SET (explicit grant beats -@all category) and reject everything else not in @read. Verify GET/SET/DEL.

(c) GETUSER selectors[] field (Redis 7.0+ multi-selector support): the user can have N selectors each with their own keys/channels/commands; reply shape is an array of selector maps. Today we emit an empty array; verify what upstream emits for a user with zero selectors (single empty-or-omitted shape) vs ≥1 selectors (array of selector dicts).

(d) ACL SETUSER `(channel-pattern command-list)` syntax: parses a selector. Verify our SETUSER accepts the `(...)` selector group and adds it to the user's selectors[] vector; reject malformed selectors with upstream wording.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 1: extend core_acl.json with these cases and run live-oracle diff.
close_reason: Slice (a) +@all -@dangerous +flushdb override pinned via acl_explicit_allow_overrides_category_deny test. Slices (b) -@all +@read +set / category precedence already covered by acl_per_command_override_category_deny + acl_per_command_allow_specific_commands. Slice (c) GETUSER selectors[] returns empty array which matches upstream when no extra selectors exist (the 1-selector root is folded into top-level commands/keys/channels per upstream listLength(u->selectors)-1). Slice (d) SETUSER (channel-pattern command-list) selector group syntax remains a feature gap; tracked as follow-up.

title: [HIGH] XAUTOCLAIM semantics: cursor cycling, JUSTID, COUNT, MINIDLETIME, deleted-entries reply slot (Redis 7+)
description:
XAUTOCLAIM is the Redis 7.0+ replacement for XCLAIM in idempotent retry loops. Reply shape:
  [next_cursor_id, claimed_entries_array, deleted_entries_array]

Verify against vendored redis-server:

(a) Cursor semantics: the returned next_cursor_id is the next ID to scan from on the next XAUTOCLAIM invocation; '0-0' means the iteration is complete.

(b) JUSTID option: returns just IDs (no field/value pairs).

(c) COUNT N: limits the batch.

(d) MINIDLETIME ms: only claim entries whose idle time >= ms.

(e) Deleted entries: when a pending entry was XDEL'd, it appears in the third reply slot (id-only, never the second slot).

(f) Group/consumer creation: if the consumer doesn't exist, it is created automatically (parity with XCLAIM).

(g) Error wording: NOGROUP for missing group, wrong-arity wording for short argv.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 1: extend core_stream.json with XAUTOCLAIM cases (with PEL setup, idle-time advancement, JUSTID toggles, deleted-entry intermixing) and run live-oracle diff.
close_reason: XDEL no longer cleans PEL eagerly + XAUTOCLAIM count budget covers deleted entries per upstream t_stream.c. New live_redis_xautoclaim_matches_upstream cross-impl test pins cursor cycling + COUNT + JUSTID + MINIDLETIME against vendored 7.2.4. core_stream live oracle 100% STRICT.

title: [HIGH] FUNCTION DUMP/RESTORE binary format (Redis 7+): library serialization, FLUSH ASYNC|SYNC, REPLACE flag
description:
Redis 7+ adds FUNCTION DUMP / FUNCTION RESTORE for library transfer between servers. Format:
  payload = type_byte(1) + library_blob + version_u16_le + crc64_le
The library_blob is a length-prefixed engine-specific (lua) bytestream containing:
  [meta_header (#!lua name=<lib> [flags=...]) + function_count + per-function (name, body, flags, sha1)]

Verify against vendored redis-server:

(a) FUNCTION DUMP returns a BulkString with the documented envelope; CRC64 must match Redis crc64.

(b) FUNCTION RESTORE accepts our DUMP output (round-trip), and we accept upstream's DUMP output. Cross-impl pattern as with v8s5 STRING/STREAM.

(c) FUNCTION RESTORE with REPLACE replaces an existing library; without REPLACE on an existing library yields the upstream error.

(d) FUNCTION RESTORE with FLUSH first clears the function space then loads.

(e) FUNCTION FLUSH ASYNC|SYNC accepts both modifiers; default is SYNC. Wrong modifier returns upstream syntax error.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 3 (cross-impl round-trip): extend core_function.json with DUMP/RESTORE cases, plus a new live_redis_function_dump_restore_roundtrip_matches_upstream test mirroring the STRING/STREAM/container round-trip gates.
close_reason: FUNCTION DUMP envelope shipped (RDB version 11 + CRC64 footer matching upstream functions.c::functionDumpCommand). FLUSH ASYNC|SYNC + REPLACE/APPEND/FLUSH policies + KILL NOTBUSY all wired with upstream wording. Full upstream body-format (RDB-style library serialization) is v8s5-class work tracked separately as a follow-up bead.

title: [HIGH] CLUSTER shard map negotiation: SHARDS reply slot ranges + node addr/port/id, MEET/FORGET edge cases, COUNT-FAILURE-REPORTS shape
description:
Even with cluster mode disabled, several CLUSTER subcommands need their reply shape verified against vendored redis-server (which we can spawn as a single-node cluster via cluster-enabled yes + a minimal nodes.conf).

(a) CLUSTER SHARDS reply: an array of shard maps. Each map has {slots: [start, end, ...], nodes: [{id, port, ip, role, replication-offset, health}]}. We model this as a flat array of pairs but the per-node sub-map shape needs verification.

(b) CLUSTER MEET <ip> <port> [<bus-port>]: rejects malformed ip/port with upstream wording. Verify error message for bus-port out of range, ip parse failure.

(c) CLUSTER FORGET <node-id>: error wording when node-id is unknown vs. when node is the current node ('I tried hard but I can't forget myself...').

(d) CLUSTER COUNT-FAILURE-REPORTS <node-id>: returns Integer 0 for unknown node (Redis 7+ behavior; earlier versions errored). Today we error.

(e) CLUSTER LINKS reply: array of link maps with direction, peer-id, send-buffer-allocated, etc.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 1 + a single-node cluster bring-up step in the live-oracle harness.
close_reason: Completed in 8356caa

title: [HIGH] FUNCTION DUMP body upstream-compat: RDB-style library serialization + cross-impl round-trip via vendored redis-server
description:
Follow-up to br-frankenredis-r83v which shipped the upstream-compat envelope (version+CRC64 footer). The body is still FrankenRedis-private:

Current body: [count:4LE] [for each: name_len:4LE name code_len:4LE code engine_len:4LE engine]

Upstream body (functions.c::functionDumpCommand): [count:rdb-len] [for each: rdb-string name; rdb-string engine; rdb-string description (or null marker); rdb-string code]. Upstream uses rdbSaveLen + rdbSaveRawString helpers that carry over from the regular RDB encoder.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 3 (cross-impl round-trip):
  1. Seed our store via FUNCTION LOAD, DUMP via store.function_dump, send 'FUNCTION RESTORE <bytes>' to vendored redis-server, verify FUNCTION LIST on the oracle returns the same library set.
  2. Reverse: FUNCTION LOAD on oracle, oracle FUNCTION DUMP, feed into store.function_restore, verify Store::function_list returns the same set.
  3. Add live_redis_function_dump_restore_roundtrip_matches_upstream test mirroring the STRING/STREAM/container round-trip gates landed under v8s5.

Deliverables:
  * Replace the count+name+code+engine fixed-width encoding with rdb_encode_length + rdb_encode_string per upstream.
  * Wrap each library with the upstream sub-frame structure: name | engine | description (or null) | code.
  * Update function_restore to parse via rdb_decode_length + rdb_decode_string.
  * Add the cross-impl conformance test.

Acceptance: cross-impl round-trip succeeds for libraries with multi-byte UTF-8 names, empty descriptions, multi-line code blocks, and the empty-libraries edge case.
close_reason: Implemented upstream FUNCTION2 body format and cross-impl roundtrip

title: [perf] Stream runtime RDB snapshots without entry Vec materialization
description: Follow-up after frankenredis-nx6lv rejected. Evidence from runtime snapshot harness (16 DBs x 512 keys, 100 snapshots): baseline golden SHA 8fa3e640031a3ed17ae06dcfc6ae0ff6814e7af9d3e63bbb4464f6868d215a19; ordered snapshot candidate preserved bytes exactly but paired hyperfine regressed baseline 198.4 ms +/- 12.7 vs candidate 207.5 ms +/- 14.0, baseline 1.05x +/- 0.10 faster. Do not retry the same ordered-entry/no-sort lever. Next primitive must re-profile runtime RDB snapshot phases and attack a fundamentally different dataflow: stream store values directly into an RDB sink with incremental CRC and no RdbEntry Vec/reference-sort materialization, or prove a deeper bottleneck first. Baseline first via rch harness; one lever; exact golden RDB SHA; preserve DB/key ordering, expiry side effects, stream/group ordering, zset f64 bytes, function/aux order, and no RNG/FP drift; keep only Score>=2.0 and target >=1.25x.
close_reason: Kept string-only runtime RDB snapshot fast path: 16 DB x 512 keys x 1000 iters baseline 1.75245281494s +/- 0.23670690921, candidate 0.88165349964s +/- 0.04683373744, 1.99x faster; golden RDB sha256 unchanged 8fa3e640031a3ed17ae06dcfc6ae0ff6814e7af9d3e63bbb4464f6868d215a19; fr-persist/fr-store/fr-runtime focused tests, crate-scoped check/clippy, and fr-conformance passed via rch.

title: Repair LuaTable hash access regression under strict clippy
close_reason: Already fixed: LuaTable hash→string_hash/other_hash migration completed with hash_pairs() and hash_is_empty() helpers in commit 29f6f2c

title: CLIENT NO-TOUCH ON does not suppress LFU OBJECT FREQ bump (only LRU idle-time suppressed)
redis.log error wording missing 'ERR ' prefix in pcall-caught values
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:

  EVAL 'local ok,err = pcall(redis.log, "bad", "m"); return tostring(err)' 0
    vendored: ERR First argument must be a number (log level).
    fr:       First argument must be a number (log level).

  EVAL 'local ok,err = pcall(redis.log, -1, "m"); return tostring(err)' 0
    vendored: ERR Invalid debug level.
    fr:       Invalid debug level.

  EVAL 'local ok,err = pcall(redis.log, 99, "m"); return tostring(err)' 0
    vendored: ERR Invalid debug level.
    fr:       Invalid debug level.

Same pattern as frankenredis-f5rgn (redis.sha1hex). Upstream's luaPushError stores the error string with the 'ERR ' code prefix included in the error table's err field. pcall sees the string with the prefix verbatim. fr's redis.log handler stores the bare wording.

Direct-call path likely matches because eval_script_error_reply auto-prepends 'ERR ' for unprefixed bodies — but pcall-caught wording diverges.

Fix: change the two error strings in redis.log at fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs (~line 4418, 4426) to include 'ERR ' prefix. The direct-call wrapper recognises the prefix via error_has_resp_code_prefix and does not double-add (same mechanism as f5rgn).
close_reason: Added 'ERR ' prefix to all three error strings in redis.log handler at fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs (~line 4408): arity error, non-numeric level, and out-of-range level. Same pattern as frankenredis-f5rgn (redis.sha1hex): upstream's luaPushError stores 'ERR ...' in the err table's err field, so pcall sees the prefix verbatim while the direct-call wrapper (eval_script_error_reply) recognises the prefix via error_has_resp_code_prefix and does not double-add. Regression test redis_log_error_carries_err_prefix_r9f5y covers 6 pcall variants (arity, non-numeric, negative, 99, table, nil). Live diff vs vendored 7.2.4 matches on 11 probed variants (6 pcall + 4 direct + 1 valid-call regression).

title: redis parity: RESET and QUIT should reject script context
description: Vendored Redis marks RESET and QUIT as NOSCRIPT in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/commands/reset.json and quit.json, but the local handlers at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:13933 and crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:8683 return success without any script_nesting_level guard. Add Redis-compatible NOSCRIPT rejection after arity validation and cover both commands from Lua.
close_reason: Completed

title: redis parity: SUBSCRIBE/UNSUBSCRIBE/PSUBSCRIBE/PUNSUBSCRIBE/SSUBSCRIBE/SUNSUBSCRIBE should reject script context
description: Vendored Redis marks SUBSCRIBE, UNSUBSCRIBE, PSUBSCRIBE, PUNSUBSCRIBE, SSUBSCRIBE, and SUNSUBSCRIBE as NOSCRIPT in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/commands/subscribe.json, unsubscribe.json, psubscribe.json, punsubscribe.json, ssubscribe.json, and sunsubscribe.json. The handlers at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:12025, 12051, 12100, 12124, 6507, and 6532 have no script_nesting_level guard today. Add Redis-compatible NOSCRIPT handling for these subscription-mode commands and cover the single- and multi-argument forms from Lua.
close_reason: Completed

title: Add live Redis conformance tests for HASH commands
notes: core_hash_live_redis_matches_runtime exists

title: [perf] Range-index argv parser arena for borrowed dispatch
description: Profile-backed pass 60 target from frankenredis-1k5km/pass59: GET P16/1M profile still shows borrowed parser/dispatch/store path with Store::get 10.62%, Runtime::refresh_store_runtime_info_context 9.12%, hash_one 4.79%, execute_plain_get_borrowed 4.51%, and residual parse_command_args_borrowed_into cost. Exact GET parser stub was rejected (score 0.75). Next deeper primitive: replace the fresh Vec<&[u8]> borrowed-argv materialization with a range-index argv parser/reusable arena that records byte ranges and reconstructs borrowed slices only at dispatch boundaries, preserving RESP ordering, errors, fallback, and reply bytes. Scope: parser/server borrowed dispatch plumbing only; do not touch NobleRiver-owned write fast-path bead frankenredis-q0qym.
close_reason: Rejected: range-index argv parser failed Score>=2.0; paired GET P16/300k baseline 407.3ms +/- 11.8 vs candidate 408.7ms +/- 18.4, reversed 1M baseline 1.327s +/- 0.029 vs candidate 2.339s +/- 1.126. Golden sha256 matched; source removed.

title: Lua method-style C-builtin call uses 'calling X on bad self' wording
description: When a C-builtin (like select) is invoked with method-style colon syntax 't:s(...)' which expands to 't.s(t, ...)' (passing the table as first arg), Lua 5.1 detects this and emits 'user_script:1: calling \'s\' on bad self (number expected, got table)' instead of the standard 'bad argument #1' template. The check fires when the receiver type doesn't match what the builtin expects for arg #1. fr's format_builtin_argerror always uses the bad-argument template. Edge case — would require detecting method-style invocation in call_function_with_callee and threading that into the formatter. Discovered while implementing frankenredis-557p3.
close_reason: completed

title: List listpack byte-size off by ~14 bytes vs redis lpBytes at the 8192 boundary
description: After the +11 fix (frankenredis frankenredis-el0qc), fr's listpack size via encode_listpack_strings still slightly over-counts vs redis's actual lpBytes for certain mixed-element lists, causing premature quicklist conversion right at the 8192 boundary. Concrete repro (encoding_differ seed 99 iter 1121): a 1311-element list (534 len-1, 680 len-2 incl 690 int-like, ~78 60-70-byte strings; sum elem bytes 6557, identical contents both servers) → redis OBJECT ENCODING=listpack, fr=quicklist. Textbook lpBytes formula computes 8206 (>8192) yet redis stays listpack, so redis's real lpBytes is <=8192 — fr/formula over-counts by ~14 bytes. Need byte-level diff of fr encode_listpack_strings output vs redis's stored listpack (DEBUG LISTPACK / RESTORE a known listpack) to find the per-entry overcount (candidate: backlen sizing or int-encoding width for a specific value range). Uniform-element conversion points (65-byte->121, big+small->222) are byte-exact, so it's a mix/boundary precision issue.
notes: FIXED (f7fa23fdf): listpack→quicklist now decided at ADD time on RAW element lengths, once per command, sticky w/ AUTO shrink hysteresis (faithful t_list.c listTypeTryConvertListpack). ListValue tracks lp_bytes (O(1), byte-exact vs encode_listpack_strings) + forced_quicklist; note_command_grow makes the per-command decision; Store grow handlers (LPUSH/RPUSH/LPUSHX/RPUSHX/LINSERT/LSET/LMOVE/RPOPLPUSH) snapshot lp_before + raw_add. encoding_differ list-encoding check RE-ENABLED: 9 seeds×3000 iters vs redis 7.2.4 zero divergence incl seed-99 iter-1121 repro. 539 lib tests + list_differ clean.

title: Direct CLIENT TRACKING REDIRECT accepts nonexistent client id
description: Runtime CLIENT TRACKING validates REDIRECT target IDs against active client sessions and returns ERR The client ID you want redirect to does not exist for missing targets, including TRACKING OFF REDIRECT. Direct fr-command dispatch has a single synthetic client but applies the parsed tracking state without checking requested.redirect, so impossible redirect IDs are accepted. Validate against the synthetic dispatch client id and pin ON/OFF REDIRECT behavior.

title: perf: RDB SAVE serialize is 1.68x slower than redis at scale
description: At-scale heavy-op benchmarking (1M-key DB, ~632k live string keys) found SAVE (synchronous RDB serialize) ~1.68x slower than vendored redis 7.2.4: redis 0.283s vs fr 0.475s, same dataset. KEYS * (other gap from the same sweep) is FIXED (7cdda9ff2, now 1.13x faster than redis). Everything else measured (GET/SET hot path, compute reads SORT/BITCOUNT/LRANGE/ZRANGE/HGETALL, -P1 latency, 3M-key scale) is parity-or-faster. Candidate levers in fr-persist RDB encoder: per-key alloc/clone churn in encode loop; length/varint encoding; buffer growth vs single preallocated buffer; RDB_TYPE_STRING fast path for the common string case (CRC64 is already slice-by-8, not the bottleneck). Profile fr SAVE with the symbol binary under a populated keyspace. Repro: redis-benchmark -t set -n 1000000 -r 1000000 then time SAVE; harness /tmp/heavy_ops.sh.
close_reason: Closed: gap closed to parity (0181314b7). Root cause was persist_snapshot_to_disk (SAVE/BGSAVE-to-disk) being the one RDB writer still using store_to_rdb_entries — cloning the whole keyspace into an owned Vec<RdbEntry>. Wired it through the pre-existing borrowed string-only fast path (try_encode_string_only_rdb_snapshot, already used by AOF-base + in-memory snapshot), zero RdbEntry/RdbValue allocation, transparent fallback to the owned encoder for non-string values. Byte-identical (golden sha256 all-string 2-DB w/ TTL = 3ac2f141). Bench (1M keys, interleaved best-of-6): 0.3391s -> 0.2312s = 1.47x, now 0.98x vs redis 0.2271s (was ~1.5x slower -> PARITY). Residual = the sorted-iteration re-lookup, irreducible given fr's dual HashMap+BTreeSet keyspace + deterministic-sorted-RDB requirement; documented in comments. Two earlier levers (iterate-entries+sort; both regressed) ruled out and recorded in bead history.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
SlateOtter (cc): Profiled (symbol build, 1M-key DB, perf cycles:u). Hot path: get_value_and_expiry 34.84% (per-key entries.get RE-LOOKUP), __memcmp 30% (hashmap key-verify inside that get + the cheap pre-sorted encode sort), __memmove 16% (key+value clones), encode_rdb_internal only 6.47%. Baseline this box: fr 0.335s vs redis 0.221s = 1.51x.

ROOT CAUSE = the dual keyspace structure. SAVE walks the sorted ordered_keys BTreeSet (gives sorted order cheaply) then re-probes the entries HashMap per key for the value. The re-lookup IS the cost, but it is irreducible: (a) RDB must be sorted-deterministic because entries' foldhash seed is random per-process (unsorted/insertion order => non-deterministic RDB => breaks replication/DEBUG-RELOAD idempotency + golden byte-tests); (b) the value lives only in the HashMap, ordered_keys is keys-only.

TRIED + REVERTED: iterate entries directly (kill re-lookup) then let encode_rdb_internal sort. REGRESSED to 0.41s (0.82x) — a full O(n log n) sort of 632k unsorted entries is ~12M key-memcmps vs the 1.26M memcmps of (re-lookup + verify-already-sorted). The sort is ~10x more comparisons than the re-lookup it removes. So sorted-iteration+re-lookup is genuinely the better structure here.

REMAINING LEVER (not done — large/risky, ~1.2-1.5x est, only ties redis): direct borrowed encode keeping sorted iteration — eliminate the all_keys() clone + the intermediate Vec<RdbEntry> value clones + the second sort by emitting bytes straight from borrowed &Value. Blocked by: fr-persist does NOT depend on fr-store (can't read fr_store::Value), so the encoder loop must move to fr-runtime with pub-ified rdb_encode_* helpers, AND it must handle the physical-vs-logical db-order mismatch (db0 unprefixed sorts AFTER db>0 \0-prefixed keys, so ordered_keys is NOT db-grouped — needs the decoded-(db,key) sort that encode_rdb_internal already does). Keeps the 55% re-lookup, so caps ~1.5x. The only path to >2x / beat-redis is an architectural unification (single ordered map holding values) which regresses O(1) point GET/SET. Recommend: leave open as architectural; not a clean within-the-hour isomorphic Score>=2.0 lever.

title: CONFORMANCE: redis scripting parity matrix
description: Wire core_scripting.json fixture through the live_redis_diff harness via a new live_redis_core_scripting_matches_runtime test, mirroring the existing live_redis_core_errors_matches_runtime pattern. Covers: EVAL/EVALSHA/SCRIPT LOAD/SCRIPT EXISTS/SCRIPT FLUSH. For every divergence file a [HIGH] review bead and mark the case XFAIL. Acceptance: test passes under STRICT with all divergences explicitly XFAIL'd.
close_reason: Live-oracle wiring already present and passing 100% under FR_CONFORMANCE_LIVE_ORACLE_STRICT=1.

title: fr-command::flushdb wipes ALL databases instead of selected one — Lua redis.call('FLUSHDB') and AOF replay destroy data outside the active DB
description:
/modes-of-reasoning-project-analysis: continuing the dispatch_argv-vs-fr-runtime audit pattern that surfaced w9yzb (MOVE) and j22p8 (SELECT). FLUSHDB has the same architectural drift.

Source: crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:2912-2918
  fn flushdb(...) {
      ...
      store.flushdb();   // ← Store::flushdb() does self.entries.clear() — clears EVERYTHING
      Ok(SimpleString('OK'))
  }

Store::flushdb() at fr-store/src/lib.rs:3792-3811 clears entries, ordered_keys, hash_field_expires, etc. across ALL databases — i.e. it has FLUSHALL semantics despite the name. The proper per-DB primitive is Store::flush_database(db) at line 3832.

fr-runtime::handle_flushdb_command at fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:10203 correctly calls store.flush_database(self.session.selected_db). The dispatch_argv path does not.

Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 via Lua:

  -n 1 SET k1 v1; SET k0 v0   (k0 in db 0, k1 in db 1)
  -n 1 EVAL "return redis.call('FLUSHDB')" 0
    upstream: clears db 1 only; k0 in db 0 still v0
    fr      : clears EVERYTHING; k0 gone too

Reachability:
- Lua redis.call('FLUSHDB')
- AOF replay of a FLUSHDB record (fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:2262 routes through dispatch_argv)
- MULTI/EXEC FLUSHDB

Severity P1 because this is silent destructive data loss on commit/replay paths — reloading from AOF after a per-DB FLUSHDB would wipe out unrelated DBs' contents.

Fix: change fr-command::flushdb to call store.flush_database(store.dispatch_client_ctx.db_index) instead of store.flushdb(). fr-command::flushall keeps store.flushdb() (which is the all-DBs primitive). Add unit tests pinning the per-DB scope on the dispatch_argv path.
close_reason: fr-command::flushdb now calls store.flush_database(store.dispatch_client_ctx.db_index) — per-DB scope matching upstream and fr-runtime::handle_flushdb_command. fr-command::flushall keeps store.flushdb() (the all-DBs primitive). Two new tests pin: (1) FLUSHDB through dispatch_argv with db_index=1 wipes db 1 only, leaves db 0 + db 2 intact; (2) FLUSHALL with db_index=1 wipes every db. clippy clean; existing flushdb_command + arity tests still pass.

title: redis.call silently coerces booleans to '1'/'0' and uses per-type error wordings instead of upstream's single 'Lua redis lib command arguments must be strings or integers' message
description:
Upstream src/script_lua.c::luaArgsToRedisArgv calls lua_tolstring on each redis.call argument; lua_tolstring returns NULL for nil / boolean / table / function / thread / userdata, and the loop bails with a single unified error: 'Lua redis lib command arguments must be strings or integers'.

fr's LuaValue::to_redis_arg had two divergences:
  1. SILENT BOOL COERCION: LuaValue::Bool(true) returned b'1', Bool(false) returned b'0', so `redis.call('SET', k, true)` silently set the value to the string '1' on fr, while vendored errored.
  2. PER-TYPE ERROR WORDINGS: nil/table/function/thread each had their own 'Lua X can't be used as a Redis argument' wording, while upstream emits the unified wording.

Repro vs vendored 7.2.4:
  > EVAL "return redis.call('SET', 'k', true)" 0
    16380> (error) ERR Lua redis lib command arguments must be strings or integers
    16381 (pre-fix)> OK  (silent coercion)
  > EVAL "return redis.call('SET', 'k', {})" 0
    16380> (error) ERR Lua redis lib command arguments must be strings or integers
    16381 (pre-fix)> (error) ERR Lua table can't be used as a Redis argument
  > EVAL "return redis.call('SET', 'k', nil)" 0
    16380> (error) ERR Lua redis lib command arguments must be strings or integers
    16381 (pre-fix)> (error) ERR Lua nil can't be used as a Redis argument

Fix: collapse to_redis_arg's match to two accept arms (Number, String) and a single `_ => Err(...)` arm with upstream's exact wording. The boolean coercion was particularly dangerous because it silently changed user-script behavior — a fix that pinned this earlier would have caught a class of bugs where scripts assumed bool was rejected.
acceptance_criteria: 1. redis.call('SET', k, true) returns the upstream error (not OK). 2. redis.call('SET', k, false) returns the same error. 3. redis.call('SET', k, nil) / {} / function() end / multi-element table all return the unified wording. 4. redis.call('SET', k, 12345) still round-trips the integer correctly via GET. 5. redis.call('SET', k, 'hello') still round-trips the string correctly. 6. The Number formatting path (integer vs %.17g-equivalent for fractional) is unchanged.

title: investigate: stream XINFO GROUPS/entries-read/lag drift vs redis after XDEL/XTRIM/XACK sequences
description: Watermark-aware randomized differential fuzz (after XSETID fix t9qwi) still shows digest divergences whose visible symptom is XGROUP CREATE <g> $ or XSETID ... ENTRIESADDED returning +OK on both but leaving divergent XINFO state. The reply matches; the divergence is in XINFO GROUPS (group entries-read/lag) or a stream watermark field beyond length/last-generated-id/max-deleted/entries-added, after sequences mixing XADD/XDEL/XTRIM MINID/XACK/XREADGROUP. Likely group entries-read or stream entries-added accounting under tombstones (cf h3vkq lag/entries-read tombstone work). Reproduce with a digest that diffs the FULL XINFO GROUPS (entries-read, lag) + recorded-first-entry-id, root-cause the first command that drifts entries-read/lag, fix in fr-store stream accounting. Reusable harness: scripts-style python fuzzer w/ watermark digest in [[feedback_randomized_differential_fuzz]].
notes: PROGRESS (commit pending): two concrete fixes landed for this surface — (1) XINFO empty-stream group lag now returns nil when last-delivered-id is AHEAD of the last-generated-id watermark (e.g. after XSETID lowered it), else 0; previously always 0 (fr-command stream_full_group_lag_frame now takes last_generated_id). (2) type-21 RDB/DUMP stream encoder rewritten to group entries into stream-node-max-bytes/entries macro-nodes exactly like upstream streamAppendItem (per-node master entry + delta+SAMEFIELDS members), with byte-exact listpack integer encoding (lpEncodeIntegerGetType all 6 buckets) and canonical lpStringToInt64 field/value int-packing — was one-listpack-per-entry, never byte-exact. Proven byte-for-byte vs vendored redis 7.2.4 on a 250-entry split-forcing stream w/ consumer group (new golden test golden_stream_type21_byte_exact_vs_vendored_redis_724 + fixture).\n\nRESIDUALS still open under this bead: (a) fr-native XDEL streams lose max-deleted-entry-id on persist -> spun out to frankenredis-fplrm (P2). (b) the original fuzzer-found LIVE XINFO GROUPS entries-read/lag digest divergence after mixed XADD/XDEL/XTRIM MINID/XACK/XREADGROUP is NOT yet root-caused — re-run the watermark-digest fuzzer after fplrm lands.
close_reason: Completed current stream XINFO/RDB slice: XINFO GROUPS/FULL lag now uses the last-generated-id watermark for empty streams, and type-21 stream RDB encoding now packs live entries into upstream-style listpack macro-nodes. Verified: cargo fmt --check -p fr-command -p fr-persist; rch cargo check -p fr-command -p fr-persist --all-targets; rch cargo clippy -p fr-command -p fr-persist --all-targets -- -D warnings; rch cargo test -p fr-command stream_full_group_lag_frame_matches_redis_estimable_cases -- --nocapture; rch cargo test -p fr-persist rdb_stream -- --nocapture. UBS ran on touched Rust files and reported existing broad inventory, with fmt/clippy/check/test clean. Residual fr-native max-deleted persistence gap tracked as frankenredis-fplrm.

title: core_replication conformance: isolate REPLCONF/run_fixture sessions so shared client_id doesn't inherit replica state
description:
run_fixture used a shared session for all cases; REPLCONF listening-port/ip-address/capa call ensure_replica(self.session.client_id), which marks the shared session as a replica via replication_runtime_state.replicas. Subsequent REPLICAOF/SLAVEOF cases on the same session were then rejected with "Command is not valid when client is a replica." matching upstream's per-connection gate (replication.c::replicaofCommand).

Three coupled changes:
1. live_oracle_case_uses_dedicated_connection now classifies REPLCONF as needing a dedicated session (was already covered for the live-oracle differential path; now consistent on both paths).
2. with_isolated_session allocates a unique client_id from a u64::MAX - counter and runs cleanup_disconnected_client(isolated_id) before restoring the saved session, so per-client residue (replicas map, pubsub tables, client_sessions) doesn't leak.
3. run_fixture's loop body now branches on live_oracle_case_uses_dedicated_connection and dispatches via with_isolated_session for those commands, mirroring the live-oracle path.

Verified: rch exec -- cargo test -p fr-conformance replication = 3 passed, 0 failed (was 34 failures previously).

title: fr-command Lua: redis.REPL_AOF and REPL_SLAVE constants swapped from upstream
description:
Upstream server.h: PROPAGATE_AOF=1, PROPAGATE_REPL=2. script_lua.c exports redis.REPL_AOF=PROPAGATE_AOF, REPL_SLAVE=REPL_REPLICA=PROPAGATE_REPL. fr had AOF=2 and SLAVE/REPLICA=1 swapped, plus fr-store's SCRIPT_PROPAGATE_AOF/SCRIPT_PROPAGATE_REPLICA used the same swap so redis.set_repl(redis.REPL_AOF) toggled the replica bit instead of the AOF bit.

Fix: swap SCRIPT_PROPAGATE_AOF (now 0b0001) and SCRIPT_PROPAGATE_REPLICA (now 0b0010); swap REPL_AOF (now 1.0) and REPL_SLAVE/REPL_REPLICA (now 2.0); update eval_tracks_script_propagation_targets_across_set_repl_changes to expect REPL_REPLICA + REPL_SLAVE = 4.
close_reason: Fixed

title: [perf] Response segment queue for SET OK socket batching
description:
Profile-backed pass29 target for /extreme-software-optimization.

Evidence: pass28 clean current profile on SET pipeline=16 still dominated by the response/socket cluster: sendto 31,250 calls / 48.63%, recvfrom 62,550 calls / 35.23%, epoll_wait 1,968 calls / 14.31%. GDB also sampled fr_protocol::RespFrame::encode_into via fr_server::encode_client_reply on the SET +OK response stream in pass27. A direct +OK encoder micro-lever failed TCP keep gate, so this pass must attack the response model, not another encode_into branch.

One lever: add an ordered response segment queue for the common RESP2 SimpleString("OK") reply and flush static +OK segments with safe Rust write_vectored. Preserve ordering by materializing pending OK segments before any non-OK bytes are appended. Do not change read-drain semantics or per-client event ordering.

Proof requirements:
- Baseline via rch-built fr-server/fr-bench before source edit.
- Golden mixed transcript sha256 covering SET OK, GET bulk, INCR integer, errors, PING, QUIT, and mixed SET/GET pipeline ordering.
- Isomorphism: same per-client response order, same close semantics, same partial-write behavior; FP/RNG N/A.
- Keep only if Score >=2.0 by paired hyperfine/direct benchmarks; otherwise remove source and record rejection.
close_reason: Measured response segment queue candidate: direct 287244.61 -> 232391.03 ops/sec and paired hyperfine 1.660s -> 1.772s, so rejected below Score>=2.0; source removed; golden5 sha256 514f74a29375f5a12274a3712dbe212a679488837cc46771446846c7ab5543e6.

title: RESTORE-ASKING command missing from fr COMMAND_TABLE / dispatcher (cluster slot-migration alias of RESTORE)
description:
RESOLVED 2026-05-08.

Aliased RESTORE-ASKING to CommandId::Restore in classify_command
(14-char length branch); registered the dedicated COMMAND_TABLE
entry with arity=-4, flags='write denyoom asking',
key_spec=(1,1,1); extended command_info_key_specs() to emit
OW+update key_flags for the RESTORE/RESTORE-ASKING pair (vendored
uses these instead of the catch-all RW+access). The name-based
extract_keys_for_argv() and CMD_NOSCRIPT loop also recognise
RESTORE-ASKING. The handler/is_write_command/AOF replay paths
pick it up automatically through the CommandId alias.

Pinned via command_info_restore_asking_matches_upstream_metadata —
asserts name, arity=-4, flags=[write,denyoom,asking],
first/last/step=1, is_write_command(true), classify_command alias
of RESTORE, and ACL categories @keyspace/@write/@dangerous.

Closes the previously-flagged internal inconsistency between
ACL CAT keyspace (already listed restore-asking) and COMMAND
INFO restore-asking (previously returned nil).

title: perf: exact six-key MGET packet parser
description: br ready was empty; continue closing the borrowed command hot-path coverage gap with an exact canonical six-key MGET packet parser. Add focused parser coverage and run crate cargo check only; batch tests remain pending by instruction.

title: perf(runtime): skip runtime-info refresh in default borrowed fast paths
description: Profile-backed Pass 57 target after cod-pass56 GET lookup fusion rejection. Current GET P16 server profile shows refresh_store_runtime_info_context at 10.99% plus clock/time overhead under borrowed GET. Every borrowed read/write fast path calls refresh_store_runtime_info_context even after plain_borrowed_default_key_read_allows/plain_borrowed_default_key_write_allows has proven the default state: db0, no tracking observers/bcast/recording, no maxmemory, no AOF path, no replicas, no keyspace notifications, no monitor clients, no scripts, no blocked clients. In that state refresh_store_runtime_info_context writes the same zero/default tracking/runtime context and iterates empty/default structures per command. Acceptance: one lever only, preferably a guarded fast-path skip or cached-default context update that preserves INFO-visible fields when any disabling state is active. Must prove ordering/tie/floating/RNG N/A, INFO tracking/maxmemory/RDB/AOF/backlog fields byte-identical for default and non-default states, golden TCP sha256 identical, rch crate-scoped check/clippy/tests pass, and paired/reversed hyperfine Score>=2.0. Do not touch NobleRiver's per-command write fast-path bead except for unavoidable shared helper call-site wiring; coordinate with reservations.
close_reason: Rejected: runtime-info refresh skip did not clear Score>=2.0; 300k paired/reversed regressed and 1M normal was within noise. Proof in artifacts/optimization/frankenredis-rf7sh/PROOF.md

title: redis parity: function restore flush mode
close_reason: Completed

title: redis parity: CONFIG RESETSTAT should reset command/error stats like Redis
description: Vendored Redis configResetStatCommand in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/config.c resets server stats, command table stats, and error table stats, but local config_cmd only calls Store::reset_info_stats at crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs. Bring CONFIG RESETSTAT to Redis parity by resetting the command/error accounting surfaces exposed by INFO and related introspection, not just the current store-local counters.

title: redis parity: CONFIG REWRITE should error without config file and surface rewrite failures
description: Vendored Redis configRewriteCommand in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/config.c returns 'The server is running without a config file' when no config file is loaded and surfaces rewrite OS failures, but local config_cmd in crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs unconditionally returns OK for CONFIG REWRITE. Implement Redis-compatible CONFIG REWRITE parity, including the no-config-file error path and failure surface, and update the stale core_config/core_server conformance expectations that currently accept OK.
close_reason: Completed

title: [HIGH] r73v sub-task (b): AOF cross-impl round-trip parity
description: Follow-up to br-frankenredis-r73v: apply the same cross-impl Pattern 3 round-trip approach to AOF as 9c460ed did for RDB. Spawn upstream oracle_a with --appendonly yes; populate shapes; run BGREWRITEAOF (or AOF auto-trigger); capture appendonly.aof.* under <dir>/appendonlydir/; decode via fr_persist::decode_aof_replay_stream; re-encode via fr_persist::encode_aof_stream; drop into a fresh appendonlydir; spawn oracle_b with --appendonly yes pointed at that dir; verify in-memory state parity. Cover: RDB-preamble + RESP tail (manifest format), multi-DB SELECT, EXPIREAT timing, MULTI/EXEC transaction blocks. Acceptance: BGREWRITEAOF-then-load round-trip is state-equivalent across implementations.
close_reason: Completed: AOF cross-impl conformance now verifies post-rewrite incremental SELECT, EXPIREAT/PEXPIREAT, and MULTI/EXEC replay parity.

title: Align replication offsets with byte-stream semantics
description: Replication currently advances primary/ack offsets by one per AOF record, while PSYNC backlog replay and replica propagation operate on RESP byte streams. This misstates ROLE/INFO replication offsets and risks incorrect partial-resync boundaries once command sizes vary. Update offset accounting and backlog slicing to use encoded stream byte offsets, then add tests covering ROLE/INFO and partial-resync boundaries.
close_reason: Implemented and verified

title: COMMAND GETKEYSANDFLAGS write/insert/metadata-only flag parity
description: Final round of GETKEYSANDFLAGS parity: HSET/HMSET/ZADD/XADD = RW/update; HSETNX/LPUSH/RPUSH/LPUSHX/RPUSHX/SADD = RW/insert; XDEL/XTRIM = RW/delete; XLEN/LLEN/HLEN/SCARD/ZCARD/STRLEN/TYPE/OBJECT = RO (no 'access' since they only read metadata, not the value). Added KEY_FLAGS_RW_UPDATE and KEY_FLAGS_RO constants and per-command branches

title: master_repl_offset advances even with no replicas / backlog (vendored gates on slaves+backlog+AOF)
description:
Vendored Redis 7.2.4 replication.c::replicationFeedSlaves short-circuits ALL master_repl_offset advances when:
  server.repl_backlog == NULL && listLength(slaves) == 0 (and AOF disabled)

In that state, the only increment per command is a single +1 (for AOF fsync bookkeeping, only when AOF is enabled). Otherwise the offset stays put.

fr's Runtime::capture_aof_record unconditionally advances primary_offset by the AOF-encoded command length on every replication-eligible command, so:

  vendored after fresh boot + 5 SETs (no replicas, AOF off):
    master_repl_offset:2 (boot-time only)
  fr same scenario:
    master_repl_offset:2590

Fix scope:
1. Add a  flag to ReplicationRuntimeState
2. In capture_aof_record, skip primary_offset advance when:
     connected_replicas == 0
     AND !backlog_ever_activated
     AND !aof_enabled
3. Flip backlog_ever_activated to true on first replica registration (PSYNC handshake)
4. Update replication_info_reports_byte_offsets test to seed a replica or enable AOF first

Pairs with the cosmetic display fix in frankenredis-f82ny. Probe sweep #125.
close_reason: completed

title: Sentinel INFO parser should enforce Redis 40-byte run_id handling
description: Upstream Redis 7.2.4 sentinelRefreshInstanceInfo only processes run_id lines with at least 40 bytes after 'run_id:' and stores exactly the first 40 bytes via sdsnewlen(l+7,40). fr-sentinel::health::parse_info_response currently accepts short run_id values and preserves overlong values verbatim, which can diverge in Sentinel instance identity and promotion tie-breaks. Acceptance: run_id parsing ignores short values and truncates longer values to Redis's 40-byte runid field.
close_reason: Completed Redis-compatible Sentinel run_id parsing

title: Implement MIGRATE with full key transfer semantics
close_reason: MIGRATE fully implemented: parses all options (COPY/REPLACE/AUTH/AUTH2/KEYS), validates KEYS with empty key requirement, establishes real TCP connection with timeout, returns NOKEY for empty key list, IOERR for connection failures

title: [code-review] CONFIG SET list-compress-depth is validated as boolean
description: Review of recent CONFIG SET static validation found list-compress-depth in the boolean fallback even though vendored Redis 7.2 config.c registers it with createIntConfig(... MODIFIABLE_CONFIG, 0, INT_MAX ...). Repro: CONFIG SET list-compress-depth 1 should return OK and CONFIG GET list-compress-depth should report 1; current runtime returns ERR argument must be 'yes' or 'no'. Acceptance: accept non-negative integer values, reject negative/non-integer values with integer CONFIG SET wording, and add core_config/live conformance coverage.
close_reason: Completed: list-compress-depth now follows upstream non-negative integer CONFIG SET semantics with runtime and live-oracle config coverage

title: Unknown commands tracked in slowlog/commandstats/latency (upstream skips them)
description:
Vendored Redis 7.2.4 src/server.c::processCommand rejects unknown commands via commandCheckExistence BEFORE call() is invoked. As a result, unknown commands never update slowlog, INFO commandstats, or the latency histogram — they only bump INFO errorstats (errorstat_ERR).

fr's runtime dispatches unknown commands through the normal pipeline, which records all three: a cmdstat_<unknown> entry in INFO commandstats, an entry in SLOWLOG GET, and a latency sample.

Reproduction:
  CONFIG RESETSTAT; CONFIG SET slowlog-log-slower-than 0; SLOWLOG RESET
  BOGUSCMD
  INFO commandstats | grep -i bogus
    vendored: (empty)
    fr:       cmdstat_boguscmd:calls=1,usec=...
  SLOWLOG GET 5
    vendored: (only the SLOWLOG RESET row)
    fr:       BOGUSCMD row + SLOWLOG RESET row

Impact:
  - Monitoring agents that snapshot commandstats see synthetic command names appearing after typos/probes.
  - SLOWLOG fills with garbage entries for unknown commands.
  - Capacity-planning tools see inflated total_commands_processed though upstream counts unknown commands only as rejections.

Fix location: crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs line ~5230 — the post-dispatch recording block. When the dispatch result is Err(CommandError::UnknownCommand{..}), skip record_slowlog, record_latency_sample, and record_command_histogram_outcome. The errorstats path (record_command_rejected or its counterpart) should remain.

Test plan:
  - Pin INFO commandstats does not contain cmdstat_<unknown> after dispatching BOGUSCMD.
  - Pin SLOWLOG GET does not list the unknown command.
  - Pin INFO errorstats still bumps errorstat_ERR.

title: [perf] RESP3 tracking push path is transport-bound under enabled tracking profile
description: Pass 9 profile target for RESP3 CLIENT TRACKING redirected invalidation pushes. rch hyperfine baseline for 2,000 BCAST REDIRECT invalidations: mean 255.7 ms +/- 10.6 ms. rch strace for same workload: sendto 37.18%, epoll_wait 17.48%, epoll_ctl 16.79%, recvfrom 10.79%; subagent GDB sampling over 250,000 redirected SET invalidations found 8/27 samples in fr-server try_flush, 1/27 in queue_client_tracking_invalidations client_list_sessions, and none in RESP3 push conversion. Candidate narrower client_list_sessions lever scored 1.33 and was rejected; no source change justified.

title: CONFIG GET port returns hardcoded 6379 instead of actual listening port
description:
fr's CONFIG GET port always returns the static default (6379) rather than the actual port the server is listening on:

$ /usr/bin/redis-cli -p 16480 CONFIG GET port
1) port
2) 6379  # WRONG — server is listening on 16480

This breaks downstream tooling that uses CONFIG GET to discover the listening port. Same root cause likely affects CLIENT INFO laddr (also showing 127.0.0.1:6379 regardless of actual port).

The runtime DOES store the right value — set_server_port at fr-server/src/main.rs:665 sets self.server.store.server_port to the parsed --port. But handle_config_get at fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:7763 iterates CONFIG_STATIC_PARAMS which has ('port', '6379') hardcoded, and 'port' is not in the dynamic-skip list.

Fix: add 'port' to the skip list and emit a dynamic entry above using self.server.store.server_port. Same pattern as the existing dynamic emission for enable-debug-command. Also fix CLIENT INFO laddr to use the live port (it already does — but the bug there might be elsewhere; investigate).
close_reason: Added dynamic port emission in handle_config_get, mirroring the enable-debug-command pattern. Reads self.server.store.server_port (set via runtime.set_server_port at startup). port added to static-loop skip list to avoid double-emit. Pin test verifies both custom and default port. CLIENT INFO laddr already used the same live source — verified working with rebuilt binary. Commit e40f791.

title: fr-command: XAUTOCLAIM error wording divergences (min-idle-time, COUNT validation)
description:
Three wording divergences in XAUTOCLAIM vs vendored redis 7.2.4 t_stream.c::xautoclaimCommand:

- min-idle-time non-numeric: fr 'value is not an integer or out of range' → upstream 'Invalid min-idle-time argument for XAUTOCLAIM'.
- min-idle-time negative: fr InvalidInteger error → upstream silently clamps to 0.
- COUNT non-numeric or <=0: fr 'value is not an integer or out of range' / InvalidInteger → upstream 'COUNT must be > 0'.
- COUNT (no value): fr WrongArity → upstream SyntaxError (already SyntaxError after this fix).

Fix:
- Map min-idle-time parse failure to the dedicated wording.
- Silently clamp negative min-idle-time to 0 (mirrors ).
- Custom 'COUNT must be > 0' for both unparseable and <=0 COUNT values.
- Update xclaim_and_xautoclaim_validation_and_nogroup_errors to expect the new COUNT wording.

title: fr-runtime CLIENT SETINFO validation error hardcodes uppercase attr; upstream preserves input case
description: Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4: `CLIENT SETINFO lib-name 'a b'` returns `ERR lib-name cannot contain spaces, newlines or special characters.` (input case preserved). Upstream networking.c::clientSetinfoCommand:2935-2952 forwards `attr = c->argv[2]->ptr` verbatim through `addReplyErrorFormat("%s cannot contain ...", attr)`. fr-runtime's handle_client_command::SETINFO branch hardcoded the canonical uppercase 'LIB-NAME'/'LIB-VER' wording, so any probe with mixed or lowercase input echoed back the wrong case. This is the same defect that bead frankenredis-e2xii closed for the fr-command dispatch path; the runtime path was missed.
acceptance_criteria: CLIENT SETINFO's validation error must echo argv[2] verbatim (mirroring upstream's `%s` substitution), regardless of whether the attr is `lib-name`, `Lib-Name`, `LIB-NAME`, `lib-ver`, `Lib-Ver`, or `LIB-VER`. Same for any future variant accepted by the case-insensitive matcher.
close_reason: Replaced the two hardcoded 'LIB-NAME'/'LIB-VER' literals in fr-runtime/src/lib.rs::handle_client_command's SETINFO branch with `format!("ERR {attr} cannot contain ...")` so the user-supplied attr is echoed verbatim. Also collapsed the redundant `eq_ignore_ascii_case("lib-name")` arm (eq_ignore_ascii_case already handles case). Pinned with client_setinfo_validation_error_preserves_input_attribute_case covering 6 case variants (3 for lib-name, 3 for lib-ver).

title: fr-command: XCLAIM error wording divergences (min-idle-time, IDLE/TIME/RETRYCOUNT, Unrecognized option)
description:
Multiple wording divergences in XCLAIM vs vendored redis 7.2.4 t_stream.c::xclaimCommand:

- min-idle-time non-numeric: fr 'value is not an integer or out of range' → upstream 'Invalid min-idle-time argument for XCLAIM' (also: upstream silently clamps negative to 0 instead of erroring).
- IDLE/TIME/RETRYCOUNT non-numeric or negative: fr 'value is not an integer or out of range' → upstream 'Invalid <NAME> option argument for XCLAIM'.
- IDLE/RETRYCOUNT/LASTID without value (last token): fr 'wrong number of arguments' → upstream 'Unrecognized XCLAIM option <NAME>' (the && moreargs guard means the option name itself isn't recognized when no value follows).
- Unknown option token: fr 'Invalid stream ID specified as stream command argument' (because the ID-parser fired first) → upstream 'Unrecognized XCLAIM option <NAME>' (parser breaks on first unparseable ID and continues to options).
- Generic syntax error fallthrough: fr SyntaxError → upstream 'Unrecognized XCLAIM option <NAME>'.

Fix:
- Switch the ID-collection loop to break on the first unparseable token (mirrors upstream's NULL-client streamParseStrictIDOrReply).
- Drop the empty-IDs-vector wrong-arity check; let the option parser surface 'Unrecognized XCLAIM option …' for that case.
- Per-option errors threaded with the upstream wording.
- Silently clamp negative min-idle-time to 0.

Do not edit source unless allocation counts or stack evidence scale with request count and identify a specific command-packet/parser/reply allocation target. Close evidence-only if allocation stats do not name a scoreable row.

No source lever attempted.

title: fr-command LCS DP-table oversize error wording is invented; upstream emits 'Insufficient memory, transient memory for LCS exceeds proto-max-bulk-len'
description:
REVIEW MODE Tick 14 finding via /testing-conformance-harnesses sweep on LCS error wording vs vendored t_string.c::lcsCommand.

UPSTREAM ERRORS (t_string.c lines 749, 792, 812, 818):
  1. 'The specified keys must contain string values' — wrong type
  2. 'String too long for LCS' — sdslen(a) or sdslen(b) >= UINT32_MAX-1
  3. 'Insufficient memory, transient memory for LCS exceeds proto-max-bulk-len' — DP table > proto_max_bulk_len
  4. 'Insufficient memory, failed allocating transient memory for LCS' — ztrymalloc returned NULL

FR CURRENT (crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs lines 11400-11403, 11445-11448):
  'ERR The strings are too long to compute the LCS without LEN flag.'

This wording is invented — it appears nowhere in upstream Redis source. Both compute_lcs (plain LCS reply) and compute_lcs_matches (IDX mode) gate their DP allocations through `m.saturating_mul(n) > LCS_MAX_DP_SIZE` (16M entries) and emit the invented message on overflow. fr also caps 8× lower than upstream (proto_max_bulk_len = 512 MB → 128M u32 entries).

The wording change is the lowest-risk part — bringing fr's reply in line with the upstream-documented error a client would scrape for. The cap-policy change (16M → 128M entries) is a separate decision; deferring.

Severity: [LOW]. Wire-format conformance gap. A client suite that triggers the LCS oversize path would see a string fr invented instead of upstream's documented message.

FIX:
  Replace 'ERR The strings are too long to compute the LCS without LEN flag.' with 'ERR Insufficient memory, transient memory for LCS exceeds proto-max-bulk-len' in both compute_lcs and compute_lcs_matches.
close_reason:
Replaced fr's invented LCS oversize error wording with upstream's documented message at t_string.c:812.

CHANGE (crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs):
Both compute_lcs (plain LCS) and compute_lcs_matches (IDX mode) emitted 'ERR The strings are too long to compute the LCS without LEN flag.' on m*n > LCS_MAX_DP_SIZE — a wording fr invented. Now emit 'ERR Insufficient memory, transient memory for LCS exceeds proto-max-bulk-len', matching upstream addReplyError at t_string.c:812.

Regression test lcs_oversize_emits_upstream_proto_max_bulk_len_wording exercises both paths via dispatch_argv with 4097-byte strings (m*n = 16,785,409 > LCS_MAX_DP_SIZE=16,777,216). The early bail-out fires before DP allocation, so the test stays cheap.

The cap-policy gap (fr 16M entries vs upstream 128M entries from proto_max_bulk_len/sizeof(uint32_t)) is deferred — that's a memory-policy decision, not an error-message bug.

Verification:
- cargo test -p fr-command --lib lcs → 6/6 pass.
- cargo clippy -p fr-command --lib --no-deps -- -D warnings → clean.

[LOW] severity. Wire-format conformance gap.

title: run_fixture: reset shared session replica state per-case instead of swapping sessions; restores fr_p2c_004/scripting/smoke fixtures broken by replconfisolation
description:
b0aa553 (replconfisolation) used with_isolated_session for ALL dedicated-connection commands in run_fixture (HELLO/AUTH/CLIENT/SELECT/RESET/REPLCONF/...) to fix core_replication. That broke fr_p2c_004_e2e_contract_smoke, smoke_report_is_stable, core_scripting_live_redis_matches_runtime, core_acl_conformance, core_client_conformance, core_connection_conformance, core_copy_conformance, core_server_conformance — fixtures that rely on shared session state across cases (or that simply did not need session swapping).

Replaced the per-case session swap with a narrow per-case Runtime::cleanup_disconnected_client(shared_id) which only drops the shared client's replica tag (and monitor/tracking state) from server-shared maps before each case. REPLCONF listening-port/ip-address/capa still tag the shared session as a replica during the case, but the tag is cleared at the head of the NEXT case so the subsequent REPLICAOF/SLAVEOF works.

Net effect: smoke failures drop from 8 to 3 (the 3 remaining are HELLO state pollution / wording divergence in fr_p2c_004 / scripting / smoke that pre-date this commit).

Verified: rch exec -- cargo test -p fr-conformance --test smoke replication = 3 passed, 0 failed.

title: [perf] Demand-refresh runtime INFO aggregates after pass28 profile
description:
Profile-backed pass28 target for /extreme-software-optimization.

Baseline/profile evidence captured before source edits on clean HEAD 3bc563e52:
- SET pipeline=16, 500k requests, 50 clients: direct 276,738.05 ops/sec, p50 2627 us, p95 4159 us, p99 5083 us.
- Hyperfine mean 1.7025s +/- 0.0255s over 8 runs.
- Server strace under same workload: sendto 31,250 calls / 48.63%, recvfrom 62,550 calls / 35.23% with drain-to-EAGAIN correctness preserved, epoll_wait 1,968 calls / 14.31%.
- GDB samples hit Runtime::refresh_store_runtime_info_context at crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:4860 from ordinary SET dispatch, plus frame_to_argv and socket send/recv frames.

One lever: make the runtime aggregate refresh demand-driven for INFO/reporting visibility instead of running before every ordinary command. Preserve Redis-visible freshness by refreshing before INFO dispatch; ordinary SET/GET paths do not observe stat_tracking_*, background persistence flags, or mem_replication_backlog.

Proof requirements:
- Golden RESP sha256 for SET/GET/INFO stats/INFO memory/INFO persistence/client tracking sequence.
- Isomorphism: command ordering, tie-breaking, partial I/O ordering unchanged; FP/RNG N/A.
- rch crate-scoped fmt/check/clippy/tests only.
- Keep only if before/after benchmark score >= 2.0; otherwise remove source and record rejection.

Candidate demand-refreshed runtime INFO aggregates only for INFO commands, but the longer SET pipeline=16 tie-breaker regressed: baseline 7.693s +/-0.190s vs candidate 7.835s +/-0.131s on 2M requests. Source hunk removed; final tree retains no runtime source change. Rejection artifacts: artifacts/optimization/icywolf-perf-20260603-pass28-runtime-info-refresh/.

title: HIGH: hash field TTL family (HEXPIRE/HPEXPIRE/HEXPIREAT/HPEXPIREAT/HTTL/HPTTL/HEXPIRETIME/HPEXPIRETIME/HPERSIST) not implemented
description:
## Summary

Per-field hash TTL shipped in upstream Redis 7.4 (Aug 2024) as a complete command family. `grep -rn HEXPIRE\|HPEXPIRE\|HPERSIST\|HTTL\|HPTTL crates/fr-command/src crates/fr-store/src` returns zero hits on `main` (HEAD eb6b915). Our project memory confirms: \"Redis 7.4 hash field TTL commands (HEXPIRE, HTTL, etc.) not implemented\".

Impact: any 7.4-negotiating client that relies on hash-level expiration (session stores, caches with per-entry TTLs inside a user hash, leaderboards with expiring bonuses) will get `ERR unknown command` and fall back to non-expiring behavior — a correctness gap against the Legacy Behavioral Oracle contract spelled out in AGENTS.md FrankenRedis section.

## Surface to implement (strict-mode)

Nine commands + HGETEX/HGETDEL 7.4 flags:

| Command | Arity | Semantic |
|---|---|---|
| HEXPIRE key seconds [NX\|XX\|GT\|LT] FIELDS numfields field ... | 6+ | set per-field TTL (seconds) |
| HPEXPIRE key ms [NX\|XX\|GT\|LT] FIELDS numfields ... | 6+ | set per-field TTL (ms) |
| HEXPIREAT key unix-seconds [NX\|XX\|GT\|LT] FIELDS numfields ... | 6+ | absolute seconds |
| HPEXPIREAT key unix-ms [NX\|XX\|GT\|LT] FIELDS numfields ... | 6+ | absolute ms |
| HTTL key FIELDS numfields field ... | 4+ | seconds remaining per field |
| HPTTL key FIELDS numfields field ... | 4+ | ms remaining |
| HEXPIRETIME key FIELDS numfields ... | 4+ | absolute expiry seconds |
| HPEXPIRETIME key FIELDS numfields ... | 4+ | absolute ms |
| HPERSIST key FIELDS numfields ... | 4+ | drop TTL on fields |

Reply shape: array of integers with upstream-exact codes (-2 field missing, -1 no TTL, 0 condition blocked by NX/XX/GT/LT, 1 applied, 2 applied-and-deleted-due-to-past-expiry).

Also affects:
- HGETEX key [EX seconds\|PX ms\|EXAT unix\|PXAT unix-ms\|PERSIST] FIELDS numfields ...
- HGETDEL key FIELDS numfields ...
- OBJECT ENCODING: switch to listpack_ex / hashtable_ex when any per-field TTL is present.
- DEBUG OBJECT: expose expired_fields counter.
- RDB: upstream adds hash-metadata types 20/21 (hashtable) and 22/23 (listpack_ex). fr-persist does not know these tags; importing a 7.4 RDB with any TTL-bearing hash fails today.
- AOF: new inline propagation forms for HEXPIRE/HPEXPIRE; AOF replay must synthesize them for a mixed-TTL hash.
- Keyspace events: hexpire, hpexpire, hexpireat, hpexpireat, hpersist, hexpired (lazy delete) need entries in the fr-command keyspace-notification matrix.

## Applying /testing-conformance-harnesses

- Fixture capture from live 7.4 oracle: cross-product of (TTL variant x NX/XX/GT/LT x expired/present/missing x listpack/hashtable encoding boundary).
- Round-trip gate in fr-persist: write a TTL-bearing hash to RDB, reload, compare field-TTL vector byte-for-byte; write to AOF, replay, same.
- Metamorphic invariants in fr-store:
  - HSET of an existing field preserves TTL if no flag given; clears only with the KEEPTTL-style flag where specified.
  - HDEL of the last TTL-bearing field restores encoding to the non-_ex variant.
  - HPERSIST then HTTL returns -1 for that field.
  - Aggregate TTL over expired fields triggers lazy delete exactly once; memory accounting stays monotonic.
- XFAIL suite in fr-conformance/DISCREPANCIES.md until commands land.

## Suggested decomposition (as follow-up beads)

1. fr-store: per-field TTL storage + listpack_ex / hashtable_ex encoding
2. fr-command: the nine commands + HGETEX/HGETDEL flags + error wording
3. fr-persist: RDB types 20-23 + AOF propagation
4. fr-runtime: keyspace-event matrix + DEBUG OBJECT fields

## Non-goals

- 7.4 stream consumer-group-level TTL (XGROUP SETID ... ENTRIESREAD) — separate.
- 7.4 CLUSTER FORGET quorum — separate.

## Acceptance

- cargo test -p fr-command --lib covers all NX/XX/GT/LT x missing/present x before/after expiry cells.
- fr-conformance live-oracle diff against legacy_redis_code/redis (rebuilt to 7.4) scores >= 0.95 on hash-TTL fixtures.
- RDB produced by upstream 7.4 with per-field TTLs parses cleanly; our RDB of the same reloads under upstream.

## Labels

fr-command, fr-store, fr-expire, hash-field-ttl, redis-7_4, conformance-gap
close_reason: All six sub-beads landed (wwz3, c0z9, b8ut, omff, 7jhg, t2nh, 25re, 4bao, th7q). Hash field TTL family fully in place end-to-end: storage primitives → 9 commands + HGETEX/HGETDEL → lazy expiry on reads → OBJECT ENCODING listpack_ex/hashtable_ex → keyspace events (hexpire/hpexpire/hexpireat/hpexpireat/hpersist/hexpired) → DEBUG OBJECT hexpired_fields → AOF propagation with HPEXPIREAT synthesis → RDB type 21 roundtrip. Upstream byte-compat RDB types 22/23 is a separate interop bead.

title: HMGET should bump LFU access frequency for existing hashes

Store::hmget currently returns values/nulls without bumping LFU, so HMGET on an existing hash leaves OBJECT FREQ unchanged. Acceptance: under allkeys-lfu, HMGET against an existing hash preserves result ordering/null semantics and increments the hash key LFU frequency.

title: [perf] fr-server::process_buffered_frames:1404 — guard per-command frame_to_argv pause-check

Isomorphism: golden RESP wire sha256 identical before/after (7e92abc5...), 46 fr-server tests pass; paused path unchanged (is_command_paused re-checks is_client_paused internally). Measured (interleaved paired A/B, drift-cancelled, contended host load~47): GET p1 +2.9% median (14/16 pairs, sign p~0.002); MIXED p16 +4.2% median (11/12 pairs, p~0.003). Evidence: tests/artifacts/perf/run_20260601T232249Z_58605c812/OPTIMIZATION_01_pause_argv_guard.md. Absolute throughput still contention-depressed (see vibu6).

Golden RESP wire sha256 identical (7e92abc5...), 46 fr-server tests pass; interleaved paired A/B (drift-cancelled, host load~47): GET p1 +2.9% (14/16 pairs, sign p~0.002), MIXED p16 +4.2% (11/12, p~0.003). Score 15. Evidence: tests/artifacts/perf/run_20260601T232249Z_58605c812/OPTIMIZATION_01_pause_argv_guard.md
