title: redis parity: BGREWRITEAOF should reject script context
description: Vendored Redis marks BGREWRITEAOF as NOSCRIPT in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/commands/bgrewriteaof.json, but local bgrewriteaof_cmd at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:12049 returns a success string without any script_nesting_level guard. Add Redis-compatible NOSCRIPT handling after arity validation and cover redis.call('BGREWRITEAOF') from Lua.
close_reason: Completed

title: Replace MODULE stub with real module introspection semantics
close_reason: Implemented MODULE introspection and verified

title: FUNCTION/FCALL (Redis Functions 7.0) is a non-registering stub: LOAD doesn't persist, LIST/STATS/DUMP wrong
description: Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4: FUNCTION LOAD returns the lib name but does NOT register the library — FUNCTION LIST returns [] afterward, FUNCTION STATS reports 0 libraries_count/functions_count, FUNCTION DUMP returns a near-empty payload (vs the real serialized library), and re-loading an existing lib returns the name instead of erroring 'Library already exists'. FCALL on a registered function won't dispatch. Redis 7.2.4 fully supports Functions (redis.register_function, FCALL/FCALL_RO, library management, function flags like no-writes). This is a substantial feature implementation (Lua library registration in lua_eval, function table, FCALL dispatch, FUNCTION DUMP/RESTORE serialization, flags). Filed for scoping; not a quick fix. Found alongside frankenredis-zf7uq.
close_reason: RESOLVED — re-verified against current main: FUNCTION/FCALL is fully functional and byte-exact vs vendored 7.2.4. Confirmed working: FUNCTION LOAD (positional + table-form register_function, flags, description, REPLACE, all error wording incl missing-name/bad-engine/register-outside/dup-name), FCALL + FCALL_RO (incl write-flag rejection), FUNCTION LIST/LIST WITHCODE/LIST LIBRARYNAME, FUNCTION STATS (multi-lib counts), FUNCTION DELETE/FLUSH, FUNCTION DUMP + RESTORE (cross-server restore succeeds BOTH directions — format compatible). Original 'stub' report was a stale-binary artifact. ONLY residual: function-iteration ORDER within a library in LIST/DUMP differs (oracle uses dict hash-seed order e.g. bbb,aaa,ccc; fr uses insertion order) — unmatchable per-instance random siphash seed, WONTFIX, same class as SCAN cursor / hashtable-DUMP order; cross-restore is order-independent so it is harmless. No code change needed.

title: Implement CLIENT GETREDIRECTID and CLIENT HELP subcommands
close_reason: Added CLIENT GETREDIR, CLIENT TRACKINGINFO, CLIENT UNBLOCK, and CLIENT HELP subcommands

title: fr-store: GETRANGE with fully-negative range returns empty instead of clamped slice
description:
fr-store::getrange at crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs:2290 only clamps the START offset to 0 after the negative-offset normalization, leaving END negative when both inputs are negative AND large in magnitude. The 's > e' guard then wrongly returns empty. Upstream t_string.c::getrangeCommand clamps both.

Repro:
  SET k 'Xbcdef' (len 6)
  GETRANGE k -100 -90:
    fr returns ''
    upstream returns 'X' (clamped to [0..0])

Fix: clamp  to 0 the same way  is clamped.
close_reason: GETRANGE with fully-negative offsets now clamps both start and end to 0, returning the upstream-aligned clamped slice instead of empty. Verified across 10 negative-offset edge cases against vendored redis 7.2.4. Shipped in f69d42e.

title: RESTORE should reject empty aggregate RDB payloads like upstream
description: Upstream Redis 7.2.4 rdbLoadObject jumps to emptykey for zero-length list/set/hash/zset and compact aggregate encodings; RESTORE then returns Bad data format. fr-store::restore_key currently accepts several empty aggregate dump bodies (base list/set/hash/zset, quicklist/listpack/intset variants), creating empty keys that upstream would reject. Acceptance: restore_key rejects empty aggregate RDB payloads while preserving empty string RESTORE, with focused regression tests.
close_reason: Added empty aggregate checks for all RDB types - base list/set/hash/zset and compact encodings

title: [perf] Batch packed-list RPUSH append for current P16 residual
description: Pass160 profile-backed target: current-main perf_gap_dashboard P16/c50/n300k best-of-5 shows RPUSH as the largest remaining direct residual (redis 980392 req/s, fr 931677 req/s, Redis/FR 1.05x), with SPOP 1.03x and LPUSH 1.01x. Prior passes rejected per-value materialization and exact RPUSH parser micro-levers, so this child must use a structural packed-list/listpack batch append primitive: baseline first, one source lever, preserve ListValue front-to-back VecDeque isomorphism, Redis listpack/quicklist hysteresis, reply/order semantics, RNG/floating-point untouched, golden TCP transcript sha256 unchanged, rch crate-scoped tests/build, paired and longer RPUSH benchmark. Keep only if Score>=2.0.
close_reason: Rejected: RPUSH missing-key single-list constructor showed a short 300k paired win (328.1ms +/- 8.9ms -> 312.8ms +/- 6.4ms, 1.05x +/- 0.04) but the longer P16/c50/n1M confirmation reversed (baseline 1.006s +/- 0.032s, candidate 1.023s +/- 0.037s; baseline 1.02x +/- 0.05 faster). Source hunk removed; evidence in artifacts/optimization/frankenredis-coralox-pass160/.

title: REPLICAOF should reject when issued by replica connections (match Redis CLIENT_SLAVE guard)

title: fr-command: small zset error wording divergences (ZRANGEBYSCORE BAD-BOUND, ZUNIONSTORE WEIGHTS BAD, ZINTERCARD LIMIT BAD)
description:
Three small wording divergences from upstream Redis 7.2:
  - ZRANGEBYSCORE BAD-BOUND 5: fr 'value is not a valid float', redis 'min or max is not a float'
  - ZUNIONSTORE dst 1 z WEIGHTS BAD: fr 'value is not an integer or out of range', redis 'weight value is not a float'
  - ZINTERCARD 1 z LIMIT -1 / BAD: fr 'value is not an integer or out of range', redis 'LIMIT can't be negative'

All cosmetic but clients that match on error wording will trip. Fix at the parser: replace the generic float/int error with the upstream wording for these specific contexts.

title: fr-command: NOGROUP error wording differs from upstream (XPENDING/XAUTOCLAIM)
description:
XPENDING / XAUTOCLAIM emit 'NOGROUP No such consumer group <g> for key name <k>' when the consumer group is missing. Upstream Redis 7.2 emits 'NOGROUP No such key <k> or consumer group <g>' (note the 'No such KEY ... or consumer group' wording, used for both missing-key and missing-group cases — see legacy_redis_code/redis/src/t_stream.c).

Acceptance: error text byte-equal to upstream. Both 'XPENDING nokey g' and 'XAUTOCLAIM s g c 0 0-0' should emit the new wording. Reference: ad-hoc differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 (port 16701). Fix: search-replace the old error wording in fr-command (likely a const).
close_reason: Added XPENDING/XAUTOCLAIM missing-group NOGROUP wording regression; fr-command tests pass via rch

title: fr-command MEMORY STATS returns flat Array even in RESP3 — same shape divergence as i40x2/e4njz
description:
/mock-code-finder follow-up to i40x2 (CLIENT TRACKINGINFO) and e4njz (ACL GETUSER):

  MEMORY STATS (RESP3, with HELLO 3 / -3)
    upstream: Map shape — 'peak.allocated 3105808 / total.allocated 3105784 / ...'
    fr      : flat Array — every key and value on its own RESP element

Source: crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:15165-15253 — memory_cmd's STATS branch unconditionally builds RespFrame::Array. The TODO comment at line 15172 already documents the expected dual-shape behavior:

  // Upstream object.c::memoryCommand emits a RESP map (or
  // RESP2 alternating-key/value array) with 27 fixed fields
  // plus per-db sub-maps.

But the implementation never branches on store.dispatch_client_ctx.resp_protocol_version.

The pattern fr already uses correctly elsewhere (HGETALL, CLIENT TRACKINGINFO via i40x2, ACL GETUSER via e4njz) is to emit RespFrame::Map under RESP3 with (key, value) pairs and the existing flat Array under RESP2.

Fix: walk the accumulated items in 2-strides, pair them up into (key, value) tuples for RESP3 Map, keep the flat Array path for RESP2. The db.0 sub-map (a single key with an Array of its own) becomes one Map entry whose value is the inner Array.

Severity P1 because MEMORY STATS is a frequently-polled introspection command for monitoring stacks; under RESP3 the wire shape is wrong.
close_reason: fr-command MEMORY STATS now branches on store.dispatch_client_ctx.resp_protocol_version: emits RespFrame::Map under RESP3 by walking the accumulated items vector in 2-strides into (key, value) pairs; RESP2 stays the flat Array. Two new pin tests: memory_stats_under_resp3_returns_map_shape (asserts Map with first 3 keys = peak.allocated/total.allocated/startup.allocated) and memory_stats_under_resp2_stays_flat_array (asserts >=40-element flat array, every-other-element key). Existing 13 memory subcommand tests still pass; clippy clean. Same pattern as i40x2 (TRACKINGINFO) and e4njz (ACL GETUSER).

title: fr-store: list_max_listpack_value forces quicklist for any 65+ byte element, diverges from upstream byte-budget-only logic
description:
Sister bug to frankenredis-llry. After removing the spurious entries-count gate, fr-store's object_encoding for Value::List still has a per-value size cap:

  if self.list_fits_legacy_listpack_size(l)
      && l.iter().all(|v| v.len() <= self.list_max_listpack_value)

`list_max_listpack_value` defaults to 64; the field is wired through CONFIG GET/SET as `list-max-listpack-value`, but this config is not present in upstream Redis 7.2 (it's an fr invention, mirroring `list-max-listpack-entries`).

Differential probe vs vendored redis 7.2.4:

  RPUSH l <65-byte value>   → upstream: listpack    fr: quicklist
  RPUSH l <100-byte value>  → upstream: listpack    fr: quicklist
  RPUSH l <8000-byte value> → upstream: listpack    fr: quicklist  (still under 8 KiB budget)
  RPUSH l <9000-byte value> → upstream: quicklist  fr: quicklist  (byte budget exceeded)

Upstream t_list.c::listTypeTryConversion uses only the byte-budget check; there is no separate per-value cap.

Fix: drop the `l.iter().all(|v| v.len() <= self.list_max_listpack_value)` check from object_encoding. Keep the field/config as a benign no-op for compatibility with existing CONFIG SET callers (mirrors the llry treatment of list-max-listpack-entries).

Acceptance: a list with one 8000-byte element reports OBJECT ENCODING == "listpack" matching vendored redis 7.2.4. Existing test list_encoding_quicklist_when_any_value_exceeds_64_bytes is updated to match upstream byte-budget semantics.

Reference: differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored redis 7.2.4 (port 16701) on 2026-05-04 while extending llry coverage.
notes:
Removed the per-element value-size cap from object_encoding's list branch. Now relies solely on the byte-budget check via list_fits_legacy_listpack_size, matching upstream t_list.c::listTypeTryConversion. The fr-only list-max-listpack-value config remains read/write through CONFIG SET as a benign no-op (mirrors llry's handling of list-max-listpack-entries).

Existing test list_encoding_quicklist_when_any_value_exceeds_64_bytes was renamed to list_encoding_listpack_for_values_above_legacy_per_value_cap and inverted to assert listpack — pinning the new upstream-aligned behavior. Differential probe vs vendored redis 7.2.4 confirmed: 65/100/8000-byte single-element lists report listpack on both fr and upstream.

title: Sentinel IS-MASTER-DOWN-BY-ADDR epoch parse error should match Redis
description: Upstream Redis 7.2.4 sentinelCommand parses IS-MASTER-DOWN-BY-ADDR current-epoch with getLongLongFromObjectOrReply(..., NULL), which returns the default ERR value is not an integer or out of range for malformed epochs. fr-sentinel currently returns ERR Invalid current epoch, diverging from Redis' observable error string. Acceptance: malformed current-epoch reports Redis' default integer error while existing negative-epoch wrapping behavior remains unchanged.
close_reason: Completed Sentinel IS-MASTER-DOWN-BY-ADDR epoch error parity fix

title: perf(fr-store): ZADD member-sharing between score-map and rank-treap (kill the per-update member dup-alloc)
description: ZADD on a saturated zset (redis-benchmark -t zadd -r: ~75% dup members with random NEW scores) hits the Full/skiplist treap-update path. fr's rank treap is keyed by (score,member) SEPARATE from the member->score map, so every score update must (a) remove the old (score,member) treap node and (b) alloc a FRESH member copy for the new ScoreMember treap key — the member bytes are duplicated between the map key and each treap node key. redis's skiplist reuses the existing member sds on zslUpdateScore (no member realloc). This is why the runtime->store double member alloc (pair[1].to_vec() then member.clone()) can't be cleanly eliminated and ZADD sits ~1.04x vs redis (compute+alloc bound, lowest-throughput hot cmd ~404k/s). LEVER (architectural, not micro): share member bytes between the map and treap — e.g. Rc<[u8]>/Arc<[u8]> member, or key the treap by member-id with score as satellite, so a score update reuses the member and only moves the node. Validate with interleaved before/after A/B (single dashboard runs are too noisy on this shared host — see project_perf_collection_write_gap memory). Pattern proven on SADD (c1d7904b9) + HSET (56241ff6a) via borrowed insert; ZADD needs the structure change first.
close_reason: Shipped member-alloc reduction (get_mut score-update + owned-member move): eliminates 2 of ~4 per-dup-update member allocs, byte-identical, ~1.05x median (8/11 reps). Full Arc-shared-member redesign deferred (trades read-indirection).

title: EPIC: Implement real cross-process replication (PSYNC full-resync + partial-resync + live streaming)
description:
# Replication Epic

## Why This Is Critical
FEATURE_PARITY.md claims "parity_green" for replication, but this is false. The state machine logic (665 lines in fr-repl) is tested in isolation, but:
- REPLICAOF returns "OK" without actually connecting to a primary
- No bytes ever flow between two FrankenRedis processes
- Cannot replicate from a real Redis primary
- A real Redis replica cannot connect to FrankenRedis as primary
- Zero cross-process replication tests exist

The PSYNC decision logic, backlog window math, and handshake FSM are correct in isolation — the gap is the actual TCP client that connects, negotiates, receives snapshots, and streams commands.

## What Success Looks Like
1. `frankenredis --replicaof host port` connects to a primary (Redis or FrankenRedis), completes PSYNC handshake, receives RDB snapshot, applies it, and enters live streaming mode
2. A second FrankenRedis instance can connect as a replica to a FrankenRedis primary
3. Writes to the primary appear on the replica within bounded latency
4. Partial resync works after brief disconnection (backlog covers the gap)
5. An integration test demonstrates this end-to-end

## Architecture
The existing fr-repl state machine and fr-server replica sync code form the skeleton. The missing pieces are:
1. Real TCP client connection in REPLICAOF handler (currently a no-op)
2. Backlog buffer that actually stores propagated commands
3. Command propagation: after each write command, serialize and push to replica write buffers
4. Snapshot streaming: encode current store as RDB and send to new replicas
5. Live command streaming: send write commands to connected replicas in real-time

## Non-Goals for V1
- Multi-master / cluster replication
- Sentinel integration
- Diskless replication (can always go through RDB file)
close_reason: All sub-tasks complete. Verified: REPLICAOF TCP client works (1v8b), RDB snapshot streaming works (de0t), backlog buffer exists via aof_records (iz1j), command propagation works (1ng7), cross-process E2E test proves FR-to-FR replication end-to-end (34b8), FEATURE_PARITY updated (8xmq). Replication status remains in_progress pending REPLICAOF runtime command fix and replica-of-replica chains.

title: [HIGH] BITCOUNT wrong-arity + BITFIELD OVERFLOW wording drift
description: Surfaced by live_redis_core_bitmap_matches_runtime: bitcount_wrong_arity_too_many — upstream quietly returns Integer(0) for BITCOUNT with too-many args (undocumented leniency), ours returns 'ERR syntax error'. bitfield_overflow_invalid — ours: 'ERR Invalid OVERFLOW type (should be one of WRAP, SAT, FAIL)' vs upstream 'ERR Invalid OVERFLOW type specified'. Fix in fr-command BITCOUNT + BITFIELD OVERFLOW parsers.
close_reason: Validated existing BITCOUNT precedence and BITFIELD OVERFLOW wording parity; core_bitmap live oracle passes

title: CLUSTER subcommand wrong-arity check fires before cluster_enabled gate; upstream gates first
description: Upstream cluster.c::clusterCommand starts with 'if (server.cluster_enabled == 0) { addReplyError(c,"This instance has cluster support disabled"); return; }' — meaning the cluster_enabled gate fires BEFORE any subcommand-level arity / option validation. fr's cluster_cmd runs per-arm checks like 'if argv.len() != X { return wrong_arity }; if !cluster_enabled { return cluster_disabled }', so calls like 'CLUSTER MYID extra' against a non-cluster instance return wrong-arity instead of cluster-disabled. Add a top-of-function cluster_enabled gate after the basic argv.len() < 2 check (which mirrors upstream's table-level CLUSTER arity = -2 check), so every subcommand path consistently emits cluster-disabled when cluster mode is off. Last tick (frankenredis-53ati) plugged the unknown-subcommand fallthrough; this generalises to all per-arm paths.
close_reason: wontfix

title: Implement COMMAND LIST FILTERBY MODULE/ACLCAT/PATTERN
close_reason: Implemented COMMAND LIST FILTERBY MODULE/ACLCAT/PATTERN. MODULE returns empty (no modules), ACLCAT uses commands_in_acl_category, PATTERN uses glob_match

title: XACK looks up key/group before validating IDs
description: fr-command xack_cmd parsed all IDs first (rejecting 'abc' with 'Invalid stream ID specified as stream command argument'). Upstream t_stream.c::xackCommand calls lookupKeyRead and streamLookupCG before validating IDs and short-circuits with 0 when either is missing - so 'XACK missing-key group abc' returns 0 not an error. Now uses xpending_summary as a non-mutating existence probe before parsing IDs

title: Keyspace RAM ~5.4x: hashbrown + 3-4 Arc-keyed side-indices vs redis custom dict (native SCAN cursor + random-bucket)
description:
PROFILE 2026-06-16 (fresh-process RSS, 1M small string keys key:%08d/val:%08d, --rdb noexist to dodge leftover dump.rdb, exact PIDs via ps): fr +467MB vs redis +87MB = ~5.4x (467 vs 87 bytes/key). fr's own used_memory MODEL=72MB << actual RSS 467MB (model omits index overhead) — confirms the ~395 bytes/key of STRUCTURAL overhead is in the index structures, not logical data. THIS IS THE BIGGEST GAP IN THE PROJECT: affects EVERY workload (every DB has keys), unlike streams (one type).

ROOT CAUSE: fr keyspace = HashMap<Arc<[u8]>, Entry, foldhash> (hashbrown) which lacks redis dict's two native capabilities — (1) reverse-binary cursor SCAN over the table itself, (2) O(1) random-bucket sampling. fr compensates with 3-4 FULL-KEYSPACE side indices, each storing an Arc<[u8]> (16B fat ptr) + structure overhead per key: ordered_keys: BTreeSet<Arc> (SCAN/KEYS determinism); random_key_slots: Vec<Vec<Arc>> per-db + random_key_positions: HashMap<Arc,(usize,usize)> (RANDOMKEY O(1) sample + O(1) swap-remove); volatile_keys: BTreeSet<Arc> (expiry sampling). redis maintains only the main dict + db->expires(volatile only) = ZERO general side indices. Plus fat Entry (~80B: value40 + expires8 + last_access8 + lfu_last8 + mod_count8 + lfu_freq/7bools8) amplified by hashbrown 2x table over-alloc at 1M (just past 2^20 boundary).

OVERHEAD BREAKDOWN (~per key, 2x table at 1M): entries table ~184 (Arc16 + Entry80 + ctrl)x2; random_key_positions ~66 (Arc16+16+ctrl)x2; random_key_slots ~24; ordered_keys ~20; Arc alloc header ~16. redis ~87 total.

BIG LEVER (multi-day, alien-artifact): build a redis-dict-class open-addressing table in 100% safe Rust supporting NATIVE reverse-binary cursor SCAN + O(1) random-bucket sampling, so it REPLACES entries+ordered_keys+random_key_slots+random_key_positions with ONE structure. Target 5.4x -> ~2x. (#![forbid(unsafe_code)] stays; foldhash-seeded.)

INTERIM LEVERS (stackable, each universal): (1) last_access_ms u64->u32 SECONDS (redis LRU clock is seconds/24-bit; OBJECT IDLETIME already reports seconds so BYTE-EXACT) -8B/Entry; (2) random_key_positions HashMap -> u32 slot index stored in Entry's freed space (eliminate the ~66B/key map) ~-66MB; (3) Entry de-bloat: move expires_at_ms to a volatile-only side dict (redis db->expires) -8B for all-persistent; lfu_last_touch_min u64->u16 (redis 16-bit). NOTE bool-packing is USELESS (Rust auto-reorders the 7 bools+lfu_freq into one 8B slot already; struct rounds to 8-align). value Box<[u8]> REJECTED (APPEND becomes O(n^2) without Vec geometric growth).

PROOF STANDARD: golden RANDOMKEY/SCAN/DEL/OBJECT IDLETIME byte-exact vs redis 7.2.4 + fresh-process RSS re-measure (1M key probe). Guarded by existing tests random_key_index_arc_shared_consistent_and_fewer_allocs_x4tgs + randomkey_in_db_uses_positional_index.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: SHIPPED interim lever #1 (46aadf2f8): eliminated random_key_positions HashMap -> Entry.random_slot:u32. Keyspace RAM 5.36x->4.49x (478.6MB->396.7MB @1M keys, -81.9MB/17.4% cut), byte-exact (SCAN/RANDOMKEY/DBSIZE/OBJECT FREQ+IDLETIME incl post-RELOAD slot rebuild). Entry 72B->80B (+8) net win since the ~66B/key map is gone. REMAINING: (a) lfu_last_touch_min u64->u32 to reclaim the 8B — BLOCKED short-term: CoralOx actively rewriting set_plain_owned (2 lfu assign sites) so the field shrink would break their uncommitted WIP build. (b) expires_at_ms to a volatile-only side dict. STRUCTURAL (multi-day): redis-dict-class table w/ native cursor-SCAN + random-bucket sampling to drop ordered_keys+random_key_slots (target ~2x). Keep OPEN.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: STRUCTURAL FIX step-1 SHIPPED (9186a4a0b): KeyDict — redis-dict-class chaining hash table in 100% safe Rust (crates/fr-store/src/keyspace_dict.rs). Native reverse-binary cursor SCAN (dictScan contract: no present-throughout key missed across table doubling, TESTED) + O(1) random_sample (Lemire multiply-reduce off high bits, robust to weak LCG low bits). Owns keys once as Box<[u8]> (no Arc header/sharing). Near-drop-in API (get/get_mut/contains_key/insert/remove/len/clear/iter/keys). 6 tests incl the scan-no-miss-across-growth invariant; 633 fr-store tests pass; clippy clean. NOT yet wired (#[allow(dead_code)]). STEP-2 (the big wiring, multi-day): swap KeyDict in for entries: HashMap<Arc<[u8]>,Entry>, route SCAN via KeyDict::scan + RANDOMKEY via random_sample, DELETE ordered_keys + random_key_slots + the Arc-sharing. entries API surface to mirror: get_mut(149)/contains_key(126)/get(89)/len(6)/keys(3)/is_empty(3)/remove(1 via internal_entries_remove)/iter(1)/insert(1 via internal_entries_insert)/clear(1). Target keyspace 4.49x->~2x. Keep OPEN.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: WIRING SCOPE (step-2) re-assessed 2026-06-16: the global-KeyDict-with-db-filter approach AVOIDS the per-db-dict refactor (keep db-encoded keys; db-filter SCAN/RANDOMKEY/KEYS results; db0 dominates so rejection-sampling is fine). BUT the crux blocker is SCAN: fr SCAN walks SORTED ordered_keys via a custom key-position cursor + scan_cache/db_scan_cache LRU resume (43 ordered_keys sites, 2 scan fns scan()/scan_in_db()). KeyDict::scan is reverse-binary HASH-order. Switching = (a) changes SCAN order from sorted->hash (redis-faithful: redis SCAN is unordered, so this IS more correct, but it's a behavior change vs fr's current); (b) must REWRITE test 32939 (asserts SCAN==sorted ordered_keys) + check/regen conformance fixture core_scan.json for order-sensitivity; (c) rip out scan_cache/db_scan_cache (cursor scheme changes). This is the multi-day core. Other wiring is mechanical: entries HashMap<Arc,Entry>->KeyDict<Entry> (get/get_mut/contains_key ~360 sites unchanged), insert Arc->Box, RANDOMKEY->random_sample+db-filter (drop random_key_slots+Entry.random_slot), keep volatile_keys (own Box copies). Target 4.49x->~2x. CoralOx WIP now LIGHT (3 clippy hunks, disjoint from keyspace machinery) so contention is low. NEXT BIG SWING.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: CRITICAL CONSTRAINT found 2026-06-16: fr's SCAN is DELIBERATELY sorted-order with INDEX cursors, and this is ENCODED in conformance fixture core_scan.json (~10 order-sensitive cases, e.g. 'SCAN 0 COUNT 2' -> cursor="2" + lexicographically-first 2 keys; 'SCAN 0 COUNT 100' -> keys in sorted order) + test 32939 (asserts SCAN==sorted ordered_keys). This is NOT redis-parity (redis SCAN = hash-order + reverse-binary cursor), so it's an FR-DESIGN CHOICE (deterministic/testable SCAN). The KeyDict structural fix REQUIRES hash-order reverse-binary SCAN -> would REVERSE this design + force regenerating the conformance fixture + rewriting the test. That makes the 4.49x->2x lever a SCAN-SEMANTICS DESIGN DECISION (determinism vs RAM), not a clean unilateral optimization. The ordered_keys BTreeSet (~20B/key) is the COST of deterministic sorted SCAN. Recommend: keyspace RAM win needs EITHER (a) design sign-off to switch SCAN to hash-order (then KeyDict wiring proceeds, multi-day), OR (b) a keep-sorted-SCAN approach (much smaller win: Entry shrink via lfu-u32 [blocked by CoralOx proximity] / expires-side-dict; can't drop ordered_keys). Reframes the gap: partly a deliberate design tradeoff, not purely unoptimized.

title: XADD: invalid ID validated before field-count and after fall-through LIMIT
description: fr-command xadd: (1) LIMIT consumed token even without trailing value, returning generic syntax error; upstream t_stream.c::streamParseAddOrTrimArgsOrReply only consumes LIMIT when (i+1) < remaining, otherwise the token falls through and becomes the candidate stream ID (2) explicit-ID validation happened after field-count check, so an invalid ID like 'LIMIT' yielded WrongArity instead of upstream's 'Invalid stream ID specified as stream command argument' from streamParseStrictIDOrReply

title: [perf] fr-server::process_buffered_frames:1413 — frame.clone() deep-copies every command before execute
description: Profiling (gdb, MIXED p16, 80 samples; allocator=58% of on-CPU). In process_buffered_frames: 'let response = runtime.execute_frame_with_unix_time_us(frame.clone(), ts, ts_us); let parsed_frame = frame;'. The frame.clone() deep-copies the entire parsed RespFrame (every bulk arg Vec<u8>) on EVERY command because execute_frame consumes the frame by value while parsed_frame is still needed afterward for is_quit_frame/should_block/blocking-state checks. This is the 2nd-largest per-command allocation after the (now-fixed) frame_to_argv pause copy (ryfqt). LEVER: restructure so the post-execute checks (is_quit_frame, should_block, try_build_blocked_state) operate on a borrowed reference or on cheaply-extracted fields BEFORE moving the frame into execute, eliminating the clone. Requires care: execute_frame takes ownership; options include (a) have execute return the needed metadata, or (b) compute is_quit/blocking predicates from &frame before the call. Verify with golden RESP sha256 + cargo test -p fr-server, measure on a QUIET host (vibu6). Expected impact: comparable to ryfqt (+2-4% throughput).
close_reason: Rejected: borrowed Runtime execute candidate removed frame.clone but paired hyperfine was slower (GET p1 919.7ms baseline vs 936.3ms after; MIXED p16 351.8ms baseline vs 354.4ms after). No code kept.

title: fr-command: CLUSTER ADDSLOTSRANGE odd-args + SET-CONFIG-EPOCH missing-args reverse upstream's check ordering
description:
Upstream cluster.c::clusterCommand checks server.cluster_enabled FIRST (replying 'This instance has cluster support disabled'), then dispatches to subcommand handlers which can do further validation. The table-level commands.def arity check fires BEFORE clusterCommand though.

Two ordering issues:
1. CLUSTER ADDSLOTSRANGE 1 2 3 (argc=5): commands.def arity = -4 passes; in standalone mode upstream emits 'cluster support disabled' (the multiple-of-2 pair check is inside the subcommand handler, after the cluster gate). fr was running the pair check at the top and emitting wrong-arity instead.
2. CLUSTER SET-CONFIG-EPOCH (argc=2): commands.def arity = 3 fails table-level; upstream emits 'wrong number of arguments for cluster|set-config-epoch command'. fr emitted 'cluster support disabled' regardless.

Fix: split the ADDSLOTSRANGE/DELSLOTSRANGE arity check (only argc<4 triggers wrong-arity early; the multiple-of-2 check now runs after the cluster_enabled gate). For SET-CONFIG-EPOCH, validate argc==3 before the cluster-disabled error.

title: XINFO lag wrong when group caught up to last-generated-id but last live entry is older
description: fr stream_full_group_lag_frame used the last LIVE entry as the stream's last id, so a group whose last-delivered-id == last-generated-id watermark (but > last live entry, e.g. after the last entry was XDEL'd or XSETID raised the watermark) wrongly returned lag=nil instead of 0. Upstream streamReplyWithCGLag/streamEstimateDistanceFromFirstEverEntry compare against s->last_id = the last-GENERATED-id watermark; id==last_id -> entries_added -> lag 0. Found via stream_xinfo_differ.py seed 1234 iter 1748 (XGROUP SETID s2 g2 $ -> redis lag 0, fr nil). Fix: rewrite the frame as a faithful port using the watermark.
notes: Rewrote stream_full_group_lag_frame + new stream_estimate_distance_from_first_ever as a faithful port of redis streamReplyWithCGLag, using last_generated_id watermark as s->last_id (fallback to last live entry only for synthetic callers). Unit test matches_redis_estimable_cases still green; g2 caught-up case now lag 0; conformance_core_stream green.

title: Implement COMMAND GETKEYSANDFLAGS live command support
close_reason: Implemented COMMAND GETKEYSANDFLAGS and added parity tests

title: DEBUG OBJECT/SDSLEN/LISTPACK/QUICKLIST/HTSTATS-KEY read db0 regardless of SELECTed DB
description:
FOUND 2026-06-17 probing multi-DB introspection (same vein as DEBUG DIGEST multi-DB fix 18401a770). The five key-bearing DEBUG subcommands looked up the RAW arg key, which only matches db0 (fr stores keys DB-namespaced via encode_db_key). REPRO: SELECT 2; SET onlydb2 hello; (and SET onlydb2 DB0DECOY in db0); DEBUG OBJECT onlydb2 -> fr reports serializedlength of the DB0 decoy, not db2's value; for a db2-only key DEBUG OBJECT/QUICKLIST/HTSTATS-KEY return 'no such key'/'not a quicklist'/'not a hash table' (read empty db0 slot). DEBUG SDSLEN read db0's value length too.
ROOT CAUSE: crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs DEBUG dispatch — 5 sites used  raw. FIX: namespace via fr_store::encode_db_key(store.dispatch_client_ctx.db_index, &argv[2]) for the value lookup (same pattern as the 0667f DIGEST-VALUE fix); DEBUG SDSLEN additionally keeps key_sds_len = argv[2].len() (LOGICAL key the client passed, matching redis's per-DB sds key) while looking the value up by the physical key. ISOMORPHIC for db0 (logical==physical). Verified: all 5 byte-exact in db2 + isomorphic db0, 1148 fr-command unit tests, parity_suite 36/36, new regression gate debug_multidb_key_gate.py. NOTE: DEBUG HTSTATS-KEY emits canned 'No stats available' (fr can't expose redis dict bucket stats — pre-existing WONTFIX stub); the gate checks key-resolution for it, not exact content. fr-command (clean crate).
close_reason: Fixed in f397852b4: all 5 key-bearing DEBUG subcommands (OBJECT/SDSLEN/LISTPACK/QUICKLIST/HTSTATS-KEY) now namespace argv[2] to the client's SELECTed DB via encode_db_key; SDSLEN keeps the logical key length for key_sds_len. Isomorphic db0, byte-exact db2. Verified: 1148 unit tests, parity_suite 36/36, new gate debug_multidb_key_gate.py.

title: Sentinel disconnected health should use last available reply
description: Upstream Redis 7.2.4 sentinelCheckSubjectivelyDown computes elapsed as mstime() - link->last_avail_time when an instance is disconnected and no ping is pending. fr-sentinel evaluate_instance_health currently uses last_pong_time in the disconnected path, so an instance with recent valid non-PONG activity but an older PONG can be marked S_DOWN too early. Acceptance: disconnected instances are not marked down until now - last_avail_time exceeds down_after_period, while stale last_avail_time still marks S_DOWN.
close_reason: Implemented in d6813ee

title: Lua string.char error lacks 'user_script:1:' source prefix
description:
Vendored Redis 7.2.4 emits Lua argument errors via luaL_argerror, which prepends "user_script:1: " (the source-location prefix) before the argument template. fr's string.char handler returned the bare wording.

Reproduction:
  EVAL "return string.char(256)" 0
    vendored: ERR user_script:1: bad argument #1 to 'char' (invalid value)
    fr:       ERR bad argument #1 to 'char' (invalid value)
  EVAL "return string.char(-1)" 0
    vendored: ERR user_script:1: bad argument #1 to 'char' (invalid value)
    fr:       ERR bad argument #1 to 'char' (invalid value)

Impact: low — wording-only, but client error pattern matching that checks for the source-location prefix would mis-treat fr's reply. Same shape of fix already applied to other builtins (string.format, etc.).

Test plan: pin EVAL "return string.char(256)" / "return string.char(-1)" returning the user_script-prefixed error.

title: fr-conformance fixtures: 3 cases pin behaviors that don't exist in upstream Redis 7.2.4 (swapdb_non_integer, zscan_novalues_*) — fr matches upstream, fixtures are wrong
description:
/testing-conformance-harnesses audit:

  rch exec -- cargo test -p fr-conformance --lib conformance_core_scan conformance_core_connection
  → 3 failures

Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 (port 16701):

  SWAPDB abc 0
    upstream: ERR invalid first DB index
    fr      : ERR invalid first DB index   (matches)
    fixture : ERR value is not an integer or out of range  (WRONG)

  ZSCAN myz 0 NOVALUES
    upstream: ERR syntax error  (NOVALUES is a 7.4 HSCAN-only flag)
    fr      : ERR syntax error  (matches)
    fixture : expected NOVALUES output  (WRONG — pins nonexistent feature)

  ZSCAN myz 0 MATCH member1 NOVALUES
    upstream: ERR syntax error
    fr      : ERR syntax error
    fixture : expected NOVALUES output  (WRONG)

Fix: update the three core_scan.json + core_connection.json fixture entries to reflect actual upstream 7.2.4 behavior. fr's runtime is correct; the fixtures drifted.
close_reason: Updated 3 fixture cases to match upstream Redis 7.2.4 actual behavior: swapdb_non_integer expects 'ERR invalid first DB index' (the bespoke db.c wording); zscan_novalues_returns_members_only and zscan_novalues_with_match renamed to *_rejected_as_syntax_error and expect 'ERR syntax error' since NOVALUES is HSCAN-only and 7.4-only. Conformance scan + connection tests now green; fr's runtime was correct, the fixtures had drifted.

title: BITOP missing LFU frequency tracking on source keys
description:
BITOP reads multiple source string keys but was not bumping LFU frequency counters for accessed source keys.

Fix: Changed from entries.get() to entries.get_mut() with bump_lfu_freq and touch() calls for each source key.
close_reason: fixed

title: Lua pattern errors keep user_script:1: prefix in pcall(string.find/match) shape
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 — pcall(string.find, …) with bad pattern:

  pcall(string.find,'abc','(')
    vendored: unfinished capture
    fr:       user_script:1: unfinished capture

  pcall(string.find,'abc','[')
    vendored: malformed pattern (missing ']')
    fr:       user_script:1: malformed pattern (missing ']')

  pcall(string.find,'abc','%')
    vendored: malformed pattern (ends with '%')
    fr:       user_script:1: malformed pattern (ends with '%')

  pcall(string.match,'abc','(')
    vendored: unfinished capture
    fr:       user_script:1: unfinished capture

Same dual-shape pattern as frankenredis-fllxr (string.format) and frankenredis-ymb2q (string.char + math.min/max). Pattern errors in `lua_pattern_validate` (fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs ~line 7370) hardcode the "user_script:1: " prefix and must drop it when the host C-builtin is invoked directly by pcall.

Direct calls already match — vendored emits "ERR user_script:1: unfinished capture script: ... on @user_script:1." and fr matches byte-for-byte because the wrapper sees the bare "user_script:1: …" and prepends "ERR ". Only the pcall shape diverges.

Fix: thread `inv_name: Option<&str>` through lua_pattern_validate and route the prefix through a conditional helper. Callers (string.find/match/gmatch/gsub) already have access to self.current_invocation_name.as_deref().

NOTE: orthogonal vendored quirk discovered while probing — `gsub('abc','(','x')` returns 'xaxbxcx' (lazy validation) and gmatch returns a function without raising. fr eagerly errors. Deferring as a separate behavioral divergence (track later if it becomes load-bearing).
close_reason: Threaded inv_name (Option<&str>) through lua_pattern_validate by renaming to lua_pattern_validate_named and removing the old wrapper. All 4 callers (string.find/match/gmatch/gsub) now pass self.current_invocation_name.as_deref() so pattern errors drop the user_script:1: prefix when invoked via pcall(C-builtin). Regression test lua_pattern_errors_pcall_shape_drops_prefix_uqnq6 pins 5 pcall shapes + direct-call regression + lua-wrapped-pcall regression. Existing test lua_pattern_validate_catches_malformed_patterns_vfv8s still passes. Live-diff vs vendored 16380: 5/5 pcall + 2/2 direct cases match.

title: conformance core_replication: psync_returns_fullresync expects all-zero replication ID
description: Pre-existing fr-conformance failure in tests::conformance_core_replication (visible on main pre-40v6f session). All 6 PSYNC variants (psync_returns_fullresync, psync_with_replid_returns_fullresync, psync_with_full_replid, psync_with_zero_offset, psync_case_insensitive, psync_wrong_arity_too_many) expect 'FULLRESYNC 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000 0' but fr returns 'FULLRESYNC <real-40-char-hex-replid> 0'. The fixture assumes an unset/zero replication ID at fresh-boot; fr's TCP server initializes a random replid eagerly at startup. Two options: (a) update fixtures to allow any 40-char hex replid (preferred — vendored Redis behavior is also to generate a random replid on first start, the fixture is overly strict); (b) defer replid generation until first PSYNC handshake. Recommend (a) — extend conformance harness to support replid placeholder match like '{ANY_HEX:40}'. Reproduce: cargo test -p fr-conformance --lib conformance_core_replication. Surfaced during REVIEW PHASE.
close_reason: Added new ExpectKind 'SimplePattern { value }' to fr-conformance harness supporting {hex40} and {int} placeholders. Updated all 7 FULLRESYNC fixture entries in crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/core_replication.json from 'simple: FULLRESYNC 0000...0000 0' to 'simple_pattern: FULLRESYNC {hex40} 0' (and similar for the 0...0001 / sync_alias variant). This lets fr's randomly-generated 40-char replid (per upstream replication.c::initServerConfig -> getRandomHexChars(server.replid, CONFIG_RUN_ID_SIZE)) satisfy the fixture while still validating the FULLRESYNC structure and offset. Implementation: simple_pattern_matches(pattern, actual) walks pattern and actual in lockstep, consuming exactly 40 hex chars at each {hex40} marker and an optionally-signed integer at each {int} marker. Verified: cargo test -p fr-conformance --lib conformance_core_replication passes; full fr-conformance suite drops to 1 remaining failure (conformance_core_geo, separately tracked as frankenredis-15x10).

title: used_memory estimate ~parity (0.87x hashtables=kv015); real gap=fr actual collection RAM ~2x redis (compact-storage lever, NOT alloc/RSS)
description:
MEASURED (clean, used_memory vs /proc RSS, 100 hashes x 1000 fields = hashtable encoding):
  redis: used_memory +6.4MB, RSS +5.6MB (used_memory tracks RSS, accurate)
  fr   : used_memory +4.2MB, RSS +7.8MB (used_memory UNDER-reports actual by ~46%)
Small-string keys are ~ok (fr 71 B/key vs redis 98), but COLLECTION container overhead (hashbrown bucket arrays, Vec capacity slack, Entry/Arc headers, server buffers) is NOT counted by fr-store estimate_memory_usage_bytes (lib.rs:18497), which just sums per-entry data-size estimates.

IMPACT (why P2, not cosmetic): classify_maxmemory_pressure (lib.rs:8286, the EVICTION decision) uses estimate_memory_usage_bytes as the metric vs maxmemory_bytes. Under-reporting ~46% means fr EVICTS LATE — a collection-heavy server with maxmemory set will use ~up to 2x the configured limit before evicting, risking real OOM. redis uses accurate zmalloc tracking, evicts on time. Also INFO used_memory under-reports (monitoring, clients, used_memory_peak).

FIX (correct, redis-model = count requested bytes; keeps fr 100% safe-Rust): add an external counting global allocator wrapping mimalloc (e.g. the 'cap' crate Cap<MiMalloc> or stats_alloc — unsafe is encapsulated in the dep, fr crates stay #![forbid(unsafe_code)]). fr-server reads GLOBAL.allocated() and pushes it into the Store each memory-sample tick via a new Store::set_allocator_live_bytes(n); estimate_memory_usage_bytes returns that live count when set (production) and falls back to the estimate when 0 (unit tests, no global allocator) -> minimal ripple, no signature changes, unit tests unaffected. Verify: re-measure used_memory ~= RSS; maxmemory eviction triggers at the limit (not 2x); bench_vs_redis no regression (atomic per-alloc only on allocating ops, fast path is alloc-free, same cost redis pays); used_memory_peak/RSS fields. Caveat: ensure the count is FRESH at eviction time (push before the maxmemory check, not just periodically).
notes:
CORRECTION — original premise was FLAWED (measured fr's redis-MODEL estimate vs fr's ACTUAL RSS, which are different targets). CORRECT comparison (default estimate binary vs redis, identical 100x1000-field hashtable load): fr used_memory=5.61MB vs redis used_memory=6.43MB = 0.87x — only ~13% under, NOT 46%/2x. estimate_memory_usage_bytes (lib.rs:21641) DELIBERATELY models redis memory (REDIS_DICT_ENTRY_BYTES / REDIS_OBJECT_OVERHEAD / sds alloc) for used_memory + MEMORY USAGE PARITY, and it is a good model (~13% off on hashtables = the KNOWN architectural dict-bucket-rehash-state divergence, kv015 / project_intset_memory_usage_overreport, documented WONTFIX). So maxmemory eviction is ~PARITY with redis (within ~13%, not 2x). 

stats_alloc AND RSS approaches were BOTH WRONG-TARGET: they measure fr's ACTUAL heap, not redis's model — wiring them into used_memory would BREAK redis-parity (report ~2x redis), on top of stats_alloc's ~7% perf cost and RSS's lags-frees-over-evict. DISMISSED.

REMAINING REAL gap (reframed, separate concern): fr's ACTUAL heap for hashtables is ~2.17x redis (Rust HashMap<Vec<u8>,Vec<u8>> ~140 B/field vs redis dict+sds ~64 B/field; small STRING keys fr is BETTER, 71 vs 98 B). Consequence: estimate-based maxmemory keeps fr at parity with redis's logical limit, but fr's ACTUAL RAM reaches ~2x -> OOM risk for hashtable-heavy maxmemory deployments. Fixing it WITHOUT breaking eviction-parity requires COMPACT collection storage (reduce fr's per-field actual overhead to ~redis's) — a major Value::Hash/Set/SortedSet inner-representation change (arena/inline small fields / compact map), NOT an allocator or RSS swap. That is the genuine lever (memory efficiency), big + careful. Downgraded P2->P4: the parity/eviction-timing is fine (~13%); the actual-RAM-2x is the real but architectural collection-storage lever.
close_reason: Kept large-hash inline field/value storage: fresh 100x1000 hash RSS delta improved 9684 KiB -> 8196 KiB (1.18x), HSET/HGET timing neutral-to-positive, golden transcript sha256 892cf80f502d65c105bd47a8683eee2d855951aae44d49ba4e4579abf53ac1bd, Redis packed-collection probe passed.

title: RESTORE option/TTL validation order matches upstream cluster.c::restoreCommand

title: Keyspace notification: SMOVE fires 'smove'x2 (should be srem on src + sadd on dst); LMOVE/RPOPLPUSH wrong per-key events
notes:
EXACT upstream events + ORDERING (verified vs 7.2.4; del-on-empty position differs per family — NOT a simple name swap):
- SMOVE a b m: srem(a), [del(a) if a empties], sadd(b)  => src-first, del BETWEEN src and dst
- RPOPLPUSH a b / BRPOPLPUSH: lpush(b), rpop(a), [del(a)]  => DST-first, del AFTER both
- LMOVE a b FROM TO / BLMOVE: <push>(b), <pop>(a), [del(a)]  where pop=LEFT?lpop:rpop, push=LEFT?lpush:rpush. DST-first.
  LMOVE L R -> rpush(b),lpop(a) ; R L -> lpush(b),rpop(a) ; L L -> lpush(b),lpop(a) ; R R -> rpush(b),rpop(a)
IMPL: add a move-pair special case in fr-runtime emission block (like RENAME's rename_from/rename_to). For SMOVE fire srem on cmd_keys[0], then the g0crt del-on-empty for src, then sadd on cmd_keys[1] — so SMOVE del must be emitted in the src step, BEFORE the dst event, not in the generic g0crt trailing loop. For list-moves fire dst push first, then src pop, then del. Currently g0crt (commit 88a45a6a8) fires a trailing 'del' for these (mispositioned for SMOVE) and the primary is wrong ('smove'x2 / 'lpush'x2) — exclude SMOVE/LMOVE/RPOPLPUSH/BLMOVE/BRPOPLPUSH from the generic primary+del path and handle them in the dedicated move-pair branch. P3 cosmetic (data correct, only keyevent names/order wrong).
close_reason: List-move family done: LMOVE/BLMOVE/RPOPLPUSH/BRPOPLPUSH now fire push(dst)+pop(src)+[del] in upstream order via a dedicated runtime branch + list_move_events helper; byte-exact vs vendored 7.2.4 across all FROM/TO combos, self-moves, and src-empties. SMOVE split into frankenredis-4ayjf (needs store-level emission for its conditional sadd — runtime can't see the handler add-signal).

title: perf: adaptive int-set intersection (linear-merge for similar sizes) — 18.7x SINTER of two large intsets
description: SetValue::retain_intersect int-int path used a.retain(|n| b.binary_search(n)) = O(k log N) per-element binary search. For two similar-size sorted intsets a linear two-pointer merge is O(k+N). Added intersect_sorted_i64 with ratio-adaptive dispatch (GALLOP_RATIO=32): linear merge when sizes similar (18.7x on 100k INT 100k, the SINTER-of-two-large-intsets case), full-array cache-warm binary search when skewed (1.02x, no regression). Powers SINTER/SINTERSTORE. Byte-identical output (6000 random + edge cases vs binary-search reference, golden fnv 0xb3e1ccf38291f53d). Graveyard primitive: adaptive set intersection.
close_reason: Shipped d79e5e6f9: adaptive int-set intersection. retain_intersect int-int now dispatches by size ratio (GALLOP_RATIO=32) — linear merge O(k+N) for similar sizes (18.73x on 100k INT 100k), cache-warm full-array binary search for skewed (1.02x, no regression). Byte-identical output proven (6000 random + edge cases, golden FNV 0xb3e1ccf38291f53d); 570 fr-store tests pass.

title: [code-review] CONFIG SET int fields accept values above INT_MAX
description: Review of recent CONFIG validation commits found the shared static integer branch validates list-compress-depth, lfu-log-factor, and tcp-keepalive as >=0 but does not enforce the upstream INT_MAX upper bound. Repro with vendored Redis 7.2.4: CONFIG SET list-compress-depth 2147483648, CONFIG SET lfu-log-factor 2147483648, and CONFIG SET tcp-keepalive 2147483648 each return ERR CONFIG SET failed ... argument must be between 0 and 2147483647 inclusive. Current fr-runtime accepts these values and CONFIG GET echoes them. Acceptance: enforce 0..=2147483647 for the shared branch, add runtime/conformance coverage for over-max cases, and keep live Redis differential cases in core_config.
close_reason: Enforced INT_MAX upper bound for CONFIG SET integer fields and added live Redis conformance cases

title: CONFIG SET save accepts '0 0' (and other zero-second pairs); upstream rejects with 'Invalid save parameters'
close_reason: CONFIG SET save validation now mirrors upstream config.c::setConfigSaveOption per-position rule: even-indexed seconds must be >= 1, odd-indexed changes must be >= 0. Pre-fix, fr accepted '0 0', '0 100', etc. as long as all parts were >= 0, which let through pairs that have a meaningless 0-second window. Verified via differential probe — fr now rejects the invalid forms with the same 'Invalid save parameters' wording as vendored, and still accepts the valid '3600 1', '3600 0', empty (clear), and multi-pair forms. Pin: config_set_save_enforces_seconds_at_least_one_per_upstream.

title: fr-command: parse_f64_arg silently accepts overflow-to-infinity (e.g. '1e500') instead of upstream's ERANGE rejection
description:
Upstream util.c::string2d uses strtod and rejects values where errno == ERANGE and *dp is HUGE_VAL/-HUGE_VAL. Rust's parse::<f64> silently saturates '1e500' to f64::INFINITY without indicating overflow, so fr-command's parse_f64_arg accepted such inputs as valid scores.

Differential probe vs vendored redis 7.2.4:
  ZADD z 1e500 a  → fr OK (stores +inf), redis 'value is not a valid float'
  ZADD z -1e500 a → same with -inf

Fix: after parse::<f64>, if the value is ±infinity, only accept if the original text is an explicit infinity literal ('inf', '+inf', '-inf', 'infinity', case-insensitive). Overflow-to-infinity is rejected.

Existing '+inf' / '-inf' / large-but-in-range values (1e308) remain valid.

title: LMOVE missing LFU frequency bump

title: perf(fr-store): residual full-zset structural storage after Arc member sharing
description: PROFILE-BACKED follow-up after c4417d55e landed shared Arc<[u8]> full-zset member storage for frankenredis-peni2. CoralOx independent pass218 measurements on the same zset RSS workload (fresh processes, 500 keys x 800 small members) found baseline Redis 37.79 MB / 99 B-member and FrankenRedis 59.46 MB / 156 B-member. Tested member-key representation variants before the upstream landing: Arc<[u8]> shared dict+ordered+treap member keys (58.29 MB, 153 B-member, 1.55x), inline/heap key enum (59.49 MB, 156 B-member, 1.57x), and Box<[u8]> keys (58.30 MB, 153 B-member, 1.55x). These artifacts indicate the next residual gap is dominated by FullSortedSet structural overhead (BTreeMap<ScoreMember, ()> nodes + IndexMap table/node overhead), not just member bytes. Target a structurally different safe-Rust ordered zset primitive that preserves score/member lex ordering, ZRANDMEMBER distribution contract, DUMP/RESTORE/DEBUG DIGEST, and all Z* command replies. Start from artifacts/optimization/coralox-pass218-peni2-zset-single-storage/rejection-report.md and rejected-zset-member-key-representation.diff, plus upstream artifact artifacts/optimization/coralox-pass218-zset-member-single-storage/summary.md.
close_reason: Kept compact full-zset ordered-index lever: RSS 58.23 MB -> 47.27 MB on 500x800 zset profile; golden SHA 236d95a41c7c140172773e3d382dbaf63aab44e3de1e759c3ceb9632dfec02c5; zset differ 8000 seed 150219 passed.

title: Lua base library: rawset/rawequal arg validation, rawlen exposure, error level prefix, loadstring wording, collectgarbage option
description:
Eight Lua base-library divergences uncovered after the lua_stdlib_builtins audit:

1. **rawlen exposure**: fr exposes a global 'rawlen' function. Vendored Redis ships Lua 5.1 (rawlen was added in Lua 5.2), so scripts get 'Script attempted to access nonexistent global variable rawlen' on vendored. fr's bonus implementation breaks parity — and worse, scripts written against fr will fail in production Redis. Need to either gate it behind a flag or remove it from the protected-env globals snapshot.

2. **rawset({}, 1) missing value arg**: vendored errors 'user_script:1: bad argument #3 to rawset (value expected)'; fr silently returns nil. luaL_checkany(L, 3) is the upstream contract.

3. **rawset({}, nil, 1) nil table key**: vendored errors 'table index is nil' (no user_script:1 prefix — this comes from lua_rawset which uses a different code path); fr silently succeeds and stores under nil key.

4. **rawequal() / rawequal(1) missing args**: vendored errors 'user_script:1: bad argument #N to rawequal (value expected)'; fr returns 1 for no-arg and nothing for 1-arg call (luaL_checkany contract).

5. **rawset() no-args wording**: fr emits 'bad argument #1 to rawset (table expected, got nil)' missing the user_script:1: prefix AND saying 'got nil' instead of 'got no value' for the missing-arg case. Compare to other recently-fixed Lua builtins (frankenredis-3osi6 pattern).

6. **error('x', 5) high-level position**: vendored emits 'ERR x' (no source location, since level=5 is beyond the call stack); fr always emits 'ERR user_script:1: x'. Upstream's luaB_error implementation only prepends source info when the requested level is in scope.

7. **loadstring() / loadstring(nil) missing wording prefix**: fr emits 'bad argument #1 to loadstring (string expected, got nil)' missing the user_script:1: prefix. The no-arg call also says 'got nil' instead of 'got no value'.

8. **collectgarbage('unknown')**: vendored errors 'user_script:1: bad argument #1 to collectgarbage (invalid option unknown)'; fr silently returns 0.

Out of scope:
- error({}) crashes vendored 7.2.4 with a segfault — that's an upstream bug. fr emits 'ERR table: 0x...' which is better, though the pointer leak is ugly. NOT matching the crash.
- collectgarbage('count') returns differing magic numbers (38 vs 32). Both are 'reasonable' stubs since fr has no real GC and vendored's count is a moving target. Not worth pinning.

Test plan:
  - rawlen as a global is undefined (matches vendored's 'Script attempted to access nonexistent global variable').
  - rawset / rawequal / loadstring / collectgarbage error wordings match vendored verbatim with the user_script:1: prefix.
  - error('x', 5) returns 'ERR x ...' without the source prefix.
  - error('x') and error('x', 1) and error('x', 0) still work (current behavior).
  - Server stays alive after every error case.
close_reason:
Fixed 8 Lua base-library divergences:

1. rawlen exposure: REMOVED from the protected-env globals snapshot. rawlen was added in Lua 5.2; vendored Redis 7.2.4 ships Lua 5.1 and emits 'Script attempted to access nonexistent global variable rawlen'. fr now mirrors that. (Dispatch handler is kept for any internal callers but unbound to scripts.)

2. rawset({}, 1): now raises 'user_script:1: bad argument #3 to rawset (value expected)' per luaL_checkany(L, 3).

3. rawset({}, nil, 1): now raises plain 'table index is nil' (no user_script:1 prefix — vendored emits this from lua_rawset deep in the VM, not from the library wrapper). Also added the symmetric 'table index is NaN' guard.

4. rawset no-arg / wrong-type wording: now 'user_script:1: bad argument #1 to rawset (table expected, got no value | got <type>)' replacing the old prefix-less, type-name-collapsed wording.

5. rawequal / rawequal(1): now raises 'user_script:1: bad argument #N to rawequal (value expected)' for missing args per luaL_checkany(L, 1) and (L, 2).

6. loadstring() / loadstring(nil): now raises 'user_script:1: bad argument #1 to loadstring (string expected, got no value | got nil)' with the prefix.

7. collectgarbage('unknown'): now raises 'user_script:1: bad argument #1 to collectgarbage (invalid option "unknown")' rather than silently returning 0. Documented option set: collect / stop / restart / step / setpause / setstepmul / count.

8. error('x', level) level semantics: prefix only when level == 1 (the EVAL top-level frame). level==0, level >=2, level<0 now emit 'ERR x' without the source-location prefix, matching upstream luaL_where's stack-walk.

Live diff vs vendored 7.2.4 (port 16380): 18/18 probe cases match.

Out of scope: error({}) crashes vendored 7.2.4 with a segfault — upstream bug. fr emits 'ERR table: 0x...' which is safer but leaks an Rc pointer. Not matching the crash.

Pinned by lua_base_lib_parity_uyj7c (1 rawlen check + 8 missing-arg/wording cases + 6 error-level cases + 3 happy-path regressions + post-error liveness check via the same Store).

All 940 fr-command lib tests pass.

title: cargo fmt gate: normalize fr-runtime/fr-server formatting drift
description: Filed during frankenredis-8ap9a landing. cargo fmt -p fr-runtime -p fr-server -- --check currently requests broad whole-file rewrites across pre-existing fr-runtime/fr-server formatting drift; keep separate from one-lever perf commits. Do not mix with benchmarked optimization changes.
close_reason: DONE (30a050172): fr-runtime cargo fmt drift normalized (78 sites, pure rustfmt, cargo check clean). fr-runtime --check now passes.

title: perf(dispatch): extend borrowed runtime fast-path to MGET/HGET/GETRANGE/EXISTS/STRLEN
description: cod's 409a70f8f added a conservative BORROWED runtime fast-path for exact-multibulk GET (1.37x, Score 10): the command enters a fast path BEFORE generic argv materialization/dispatch, with full fallback on any of auth/ACL, non-db0, CLIENT NO-TOUCH, transaction, subscription, pause, maxmemory, AOF, replica, blocked-client, notification, tracking, monitor, script states. NEXT SWING: replicate the SAME machinery for the next-hottest READ commands — MGET, HGET, GETRANGE, EXISTS, STRLEN, TYPE — each can serve borrowed bytes from storage into the reply and skip argv Vec materialization + generic dispatch. Target ~1.3-1.5x each (same class as GET). RISK: parity is ABSOLUTE — the disabling-condition set is correctness-critical (a missed condition silently breaks notifications/AOF/repl/tracking). Mirror cod's exact fallback predicate; prove per cod's rigor (RCH check+clippy+fmt, focused parity test, raw-TCP golden input+output sha256 cmp before/after, hyperfine paired bench P16/1M). ONE command per commit; reuse the GET fast-path gating helper. Key metadata now gated by scripts/command_getkeys_gate.py. Lower-confidence sibling: COMMAND_TABLE flags are a space-separated &str re-split per call (get_command_flags().split_whitespace(), cod profile SplitWhitespace 2.95%) — precompute a u32 bitset for O(1) branchless tests, but FIRST verify a universal per-command consumer exists (sites found so far are slowlog/ACL-cat/keyspec, not universal) else rejected micro-lever.
notes: EXISTS DONE+shipped (41aa1b621). 1.84x/1.67x (paired+reversed -P16 EXISTS a, candidate ~750-780k vs baseline ~425-449k rps, p50 1.6ms->0.75ms). CRITICAL: EXISTS uses Store::exists_no_touch (NOT exists) — it's a no-touch metadata query (fz457), verified OBJECT IDLETIME unchanged candidate==baseline. Counts duplicates, any value type, never errors. Golden byte-identical candidate==baseline==redis7.2.4 (291B, sha 4b85cdc2...). STATUS: GET(cod)+HGET+MGET+EXISTS shipped. REMAINING (one/commit): GETRANGE (4-arg, store.getrange@4572, WRONGTYPE error path like HGET, returns BulkString substring; empty-string on missing key), STRLEN (2-arg, store.strlen@4375, Integer len, WRONGTYPE, 0 on missing). After both, consider closing uz39v. LESSON: don't put OBJECT IDLETIME in golden-vs-oracle (LRU-clock artifact, fr:1 vs redis:big — pre-existing, not our bug; use it only for candidate==baseline no-touch checks).
close_reason: COMPLETE: borrowed runtime fast-path now covers all targeted reads — GET(cod 409a70f8f) + HGET(0685c8c29) + MGET(d94646968) + EXISTS(41aa1b621) + STRLEN(82d2803bf) + GETRANGE(25ec4b37e). Each ~1.3-1.8x P16 (HGET 1.5x, MGET 1.78x, EXISTS 1.8x, STRLEN 1.6x, GETRANGE 1.4x), all golden-sha256 byte-identical candidate==baseline==redis7.2.4 across hit/miss/wrongtype/edge + all disabling conditions. Shared plain_borrowed_default_key_read_allows() predicate prevents drift. GETRANGE defers non-integer args to generic for exact error-stat parity. Future extension targets (new bead if pursued): HMGET, LINDEX, LLEN, SCARD, ZSCORE, HEXISTS, SISMEMBER — all single/multi-key reads amenable to the same recipe.

title: DEBUG OBJECT emits 7.4-only 'hexpired_fields:N' suffix even though target is Redis 7.2.4 — should be omitted entirely
description:
Vendored Redis 7.2.4 emits:
  Value at:%p refcount:%d encoding:%s serializedlength:%zu lru:%d lru_seconds_idle:%llu<extra>
where <extra> is empty unless QUICKLIST.

fr emits an additional ' hexpired_fields:N' suffix on every DEBUG OBJECT reply — added by br-frankenredis-25re for Redis 7.4 alignment, but the project's parity baseline is 7.2.4 (README + INFO server.redis_version + legacy_redis_code/redis/src/version.h:'7.2.4').

Differential probe vs vendored 16380:
  vendored: Value at:0x... refcount:1 encoding:embstr serializedlength:6 lru:N lru_seconds_idle:0
  fr      : Value at:0x0 refcount:1 encoding:embstr serializedlength:49 lru:N lru_seconds_idle:0 hexpired_fields:0

Fix:
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:18329-18348 → drop hexpired_fields field from format string and the no-longer-needed hash_field_expired_count() call.
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:24516-24564 → remove debug_object_reports_hexpired_fields_counter test (it pinned a 7.4-only field).

Note: the underlying hash_field_expired_count counter is still useful for HEXPIRE telemetry — only the DEBUG OBJECT suffix is wrong.
close_reason: Dropped 7.4-only ' hexpired_fields:N' suffix from DEBUG OBJECT (crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:18329-18348). Vendored Redis 7.2.4 debug.c:649-654 ends the reply at lru_seconds_idle (followed only by quicklist-specific fields); the suffix was added by br-frankenredis-25re for Redis 7.4 alignment but the project's parity baseline is 7.2.4. Live differential probe vs vendored 16380 confirms parity restored. Replaced the now-defunct debug_object_reports_hexpired_fields_counter test (which pinned the 7.4-only field) with debug_object_omits_redis_7_4_hexpired_fields_suffix_for_7_2_4_parity (asserts the suffix is absent and the line ends in lru_seconds_idle). Adjusted debug_object_emits_lru_clock_and_idle_seconds_as_separate_fields to anchor with ends_with(' lru_seconds_idle:5') instead of trailing-whitespace contains() — the former relied on the hexpired_fields suffix providing the trailing space. fr-command lib tests: 4 debug_object cases pass; 4 pre-existing stream/MIGRATE failures unrelated to this change confirmed via git-stash re-run.

title: perf(fr-command): Lua full slot-resolved locals after compact frame keep
description: Follow-up to frankenredis-iror0 pass143. Compact Vec local frames kept with 1.37-1.41x loop1000 and 1.28x table200 gains, but remaining Redis gap requires the deeper primitive described in iror0: parse-time resolution mapping Expr::Name to (scope_depth, slot_index), Scope to slot-indexed LuaCell vectors, and runtime local access to O(1) slot indexing. Preserve Lua 5.1 semantics: lexical shadowing, local function recursion cell predeclare, local x=x initializer reads outer x, numeric-for fresh cells for closure captures, varargs, _ENV/_G/global fallback, error/accessor labels, RNG and floating-point behavior. Baseline from artifacts/optimization/frankenredis-iror0/pass143/. Do not repeat HashMap/scope-allocation micro-levers.
close_reason: Kept full Lua slot-resolved locals: loop1000 1.081852151s -> 1.009675558s paired, 1.0737x reversed; table200 0.721912404s -> 0.680783219s paired, 1.1003x reversed; golden sha256 522a3ab10859dcc45592ec5a323f1bc0f40b81db1662124aacb7a6d3e7bed005; RCH fmt/check/clippy/lua tests passed.

title: CONFIG HELP via TCP server returns BulkString frames + custom wording — vendored emits SimpleString frames with upstream's single-pattern/single-directive wording
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4 (CONFIG HELP through TCP):

  127.0.0.1:16380> CONFIG HELP
   1) CONFIG <subcommand> [<arg> [value] [opt] ...]. Subcommands are:
   2) GET <pattern>
   3)     Return parameters matching the glob-like <pattern> and their values.
   ...
  (each line is a SimpleString)

  127.0.0.1:16480> CONFIG HELP
   1) "CONFIG <subcommand> ..."
   2) "GET <pattern> [<pattern> ...]"
   3) "    Return configuration parameters matching the specified patterns."
   ...
  (each line is a BulkString — note the double quotes that --no-raw renders for bulk strings)

Two divergences:

(1) Frame type: vendored uses addReplyHelp which emits SimpleString frames; fr-runtime emits BulkString frames.

(2) Wording: vendored mirrors config.c::configHelpCommand (lines 3368-3382):
  GET <pattern>           — singular form
  SET <directive> <value> — singular form, uses 'directive' word
  RESETSTAT — reset by 'the INFO command'
  REWRITE   — 'Rewrite the configuration file.' (no 'with the current in-memory settings')

fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:7187 config_help_lines() uses bulk() (BulkString) and a custom wording that mentions multi-arg forms ('GET <pattern> [<pattern> ...]', 'SET <parameter> <value> [<parameter> <value> ...]'). The multi-arg forms ARE supported at runtime (Redis 7+), but upstream's HELP text still shows the single-arg form.

A SECOND impl already exists in fr-command/src/lib.rs:15203 with the correct SimpleString frames + upstream wording (closes frankenredis-kuthf), but the runtime TCP path bypasses the fr-command dispatch_argv route for CONFIG HELP and hits the runtime's own copy.

Fix: rewrite fr-runtime::config_help_lines() to mirror the fr-command version (SimpleString + upstream wording line-for-line). Optionally collapse to a single shared source if a sane place exists.

Acceptance: CONFIG HELP through TCP byte-equal vendored 7.2.4.
close_reason: fr-runtime::config_help_lines() now mirrors upstream config.c::configHelpCommand line-for-line: SimpleString frames (was BulkString — addReplyHelp emits status replies, not bulk strings) and singular wording (GET <pattern>, SET <directive> <value>, etc.) instead of fr's previous custom multi-arg phrasing. Verified via differential probe — CONFIG HELP through TCP now byte-equal vendored 7.2.4. Pin: config_help_frames_are_simple_strings_matching_upstream_wording.

title: [perf] Re-test large SET direct-read chunk with corrected gate

Earlier 64KiB->256KiB direct-read chunk trial was rejected using a Python-process hyperfine harness that included client batch allocation and masked server throughput. Re-test the same one-line direct read batch-size lever using the repo internal timed large-value gate / fr-bench before escalating to heavier parser IO restructuring. Preserve semantics: only max bytes per nonblocking read into owned SET staging changes.

title: Add live Redis conformance tests for connection commands

title: fr-runtime::feed_monitors allocates a heap String per non-printable byte in MONITOR output — O(N) extra allocs per byte for binary payloads
description:
/profiling-software-performance audit:

  crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:5280
    line.push_str(&format!(\"\\x{b:02x}\"));

This call sits inside the per-byte loop that builds the MONITOR text line for every command propagation. For each non-printable byte (anything outside [32..=126]), it heap-allocates a 4-char String via format!() just to push 4 ASCII bytes into 'line', then drops the String.

Per-byte cost when MONITOR is active and the argv contains a binary payload (RDB chunk, hash with binary field, MIGRATE serialised dump, etc.):
  - 1 String allocation (small allocator overhead)
  - 1 'String->&str' deref + copy
  - 1 dealloc

A single MIGRATE of a 1MB binary key fired through MONITOR would do roughly 1M extra heap allocations on top of the already-byte-by-byte loop. With multiple MONITOR clients attached (each gets its own line.clone() at line 5292), the regression compounds linearly.

Fix: use the std::fmt::Write trait in a write!() — same write target ('line: String'), zero intermediate allocation. The 'use std::fmt::Write as _' pattern from frankenredis-tvn6n / mec2q has the rest of the codebase using this idiom already.

Optional follow-up: the byte-by-byte loop itself could be vectorised but that's a separate scope.
close_reason: Replaced line.push_str(&format!('\\x{b:02x}')) inside fr-runtime::feed_monitors per-byte loop with write!(line, '\\x{b:02x}') using the existing 'use std::fmt::Write as _' import. Eliminates per-byte intermediate String alloc for non-printable bytes — matters for MONITOR streams carrying binary payloads (RDB chunks, MIGRATE dumps, hash binary fields) where most bytes hit that branch. 4 monitor unit tests pass; clippy clean. Same pattern as mec2q/tvn6n's INFO renderer cleanup.

title: MULTI/EXEC over-wraps single-effect transactions (spurious MULTI/EXEC in repl+AOF vs redis bare)
Lua cjson.null sentinel needs userdata (currently returns LuaValue::Nil)
description:
Lua-bundled cjson distinguishes JSON null (returned as cjson.null, a
userdata sentinel) from absent/Lua nil. type(cjson.decode('null')) is
'userdata' in vendored Redis 7.2.4. fr returns LuaValue::Nil, so
type(...) is 'nil'.

Implementing this would require adding LuaValue::Userdata or at least
a sentinel singleton table tagged with metatable __type='userdata'.
Either way it touches fr's type system. Defer until userdata story is
clearer — real apps generally treat null as nil-equivalent so impact
is small.
close_reason: done

title: string.sub silently defaults j arg when non-numeric (vendored errors)
description:
Same pattern as frankenredis-izta5 but for string.sub's third arg
('j', the end position). Upstream luaB_sub uses luaL_optinteger
which raises 'bad argument #3 (number expected, got TYPE)' for
non-number-convertible values. fr previously coerced via to_number()
and silently defaulted to -1 (full-length), masking the error.

Repro:
  EVAL "local ok,e = pcall(string.sub,'hello',1,'bad'); return tostring(ok)..':'..tostring(e)" 0
    vendored: false:bad argument #3 to '?' (number expected, got string)
    fr:       true:hello

  EVAL "local ok,e = pcall(string.sub,'hello',1,{}); return tostring(ok)..':'..tostring(e)" 0
    vendored: false:bad argument #3 to '?' (number expected, got table)
    fr:       true:hello

Numeric and numeric-string j inputs still work; nil/missing still
defaults to -1.

Fix at fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:6463: replace
  args.get(2).and_then(|v| v.to_number()).unwrap_or(-1.0) as i64
with
  lua_optional_integer_arg(inv, 3, args.get(2), -1)?
close_reason: completed

title: Sentinel CONFIG SET should accept mixed-case option names
description: Upstream Redis 7.2.4 sentinelConfigSetCommand validates SENTINEL CONFIG SET options with stringSetDictType, a case-insensitive dictionary, then applies options with strcasecmp. fr-sentinel::commands::canonical_sentinel_config_key currently requires exact lowercase keys and the test sentinel_config_set_rejects_mixed_case_option_names encodes the opposite behavior. Acceptance: CONFIG SET option lookup and duplicate detection are case-insensitive, mixed-case valid options apply atomically, and replies still emit canonical lowercase names.
