title: GEOSEARCHSTORE accepts WITHCOORD/WITHDIST/WITHHASH instead of rejecting
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:

  GEOSEARCHSTORE dst geo FROMMEMBER P BYRADIUS 100 km WITHCOORD
    vendored: ERR GEOSEARCHSTORE is not compatible with WITHDIST, WITHHASH and WITHCOORD options
    fr:       1 (silently accepted)

  GEOSEARCHSTORE dst geo FROMMEMBER P BYRADIUS 100 km WITHDIST  → same divergence
  GEOSEARCHSTORE dst geo FROMMEMBER P BYRADIUS 100 km WITHHASH  → same divergence
  GEOSEARCHSTORE dst geo FROMMEMBER P BYRADIUS 100 km STOREDIST WITHCOORD → same divergence

Root cause: upstream src/geo.c::georadiusGeneric reaches a guard like:
    if (storekey && (withdist || withhash || withcoord)) {
        addReplyError(c, "GEOSEARCHSTORE is not compatible with WITHDIST, WITHHASH and WITHCOORD options");
        return;
    }
for the GEOSEARCHSTORE dispatch path. fr's geosearchstore (fr-command/src/lib.rs ~line 5496) and/or the shared geo_search core (line 4808) silently accept these flags, which on the GEOSEARCHSTORE path are dropped during the store step — leaving the destination key correctly populated but bypassing the upstream syntax check.

Fix: in geosearchstore() / the shared core path that handles GeoSearchStore variant, after parsing options, return ERR with the exact upstream wording if any of withcoord/withdist/withhash are set. Plain GEOSEARCHSTORE without those flags and GEOSEARCHSTORE with STOREDIST alone must continue to succeed (probe confirmed both accept in vendored).
close_reason: Added WITHCOORD/WITHDIST/WITHHASH rejection in geosearchstore() at fr-command/src/lib.rs after the parse_geo_search_flags call (line ~5660). Now emits 'ERR GEOSEARCHSTORE is not compatible with WITHDIST, WITHHASH and WITHCOORD options' matching upstream src/geo.c::georadiusGeneric's GEOSEARCHSTORE-path guard. Regression test geosearchstore_rejects_with_options_w2whf covers each WITH* flag individually, STOREDIST+WITH* combo, plus regression checks that plain GEOSEARCHSTORE and STOREDIST alone still succeed. Live diff vs vendored 7.2.4 matches on all 8 probed variants.

title: Implement FAILOVER with full standalone validation
close_reason: FAILOVER fully implemented: parses TO/FORCE/ABORT/TIMEOUT arguments with proper validation, returns correct errors (no failover in progress, requires replicas, etc.)

title: Lua select(0, ...) error names 'select' instead of '?'
description: fr's select() emits 'bad argument #1 to \'select\' (index out of range)' for select(0, ...). Vendored emits 'bad argument #1 to \'?\' (index out of range)' — Lua 5.1 uses '?' when the C function lacks a syntactic name in the debug info (which select always lacks). Minor wording fix. Discovered during REVIEW PHASE.
close_reason: Tightened select() bad-argument handling in lua_eval.rs to match vendored Lua 5.1 for the DIRECT-call case (which is what real scripts use). Now emits 'user_script:1: bad argument #1 to \'select\' (number expected, got TYPE)' for non-coercible args and 'user_script:1: bad argument #1 to \'select\' (index out of range)' for NaN/zero/<-argcount. Added NaN→0 and ±inf saturation in the integer cast so NaN trips 'index out of range' identically. Fractional indices truncate toward zero (1.5→1) — verified vs vendored. The original bead description claimed vendored always emits '?' but live probes show that's only the PCALL-via-callback path; direct calls use 'select'. fr's call_builtin can't distinguish those two contexts without call-site plumbing, so the pcall-callback divergence is filed as follow-up frankenredis-pdkqj (similar pattern to md71j for accessor context). Verified live diff vs vendored 7.2.4 port 16380: 9/9 direct-call cases match. Pinned by lua_select_bad_argument_errors_match_upstream_wording covering 8 sub-cases. All 925 fr-command lib tests pass.

title: [HIGH] CLIENT INFO / CLIENT ID / HELLO id drift + CLIENT SETINFO/NO-EVICT/NO-TOUCH/CACHING/PAUSE wording parity
description: Surfaced by live_redis_core_client_matches_runtime (12 divergences after CLIENT REPLY OFF XFAIL landed): (a) CLIENT INFO and CLIENT ID differ because upstream's client_id increments per TCP connection whereas our test harness always sees id=1 on the single harness client — fixture was captured against a live Python-redis client with id=4. (b) HELLO id field similarly drifts (ours=3 vs fixture=6, or ours=6 vs fixture=9). (c) CLIENT NO-EVICT / NO-TOUCH invalid-arg emits 'argument must be on or off' vs upstream 'ERR syntax error'. (d) CLIENT CACHING YES/NO without tracking emits our verbose multi-mode message vs upstream's 'is only valid when tracking is enabled in OPTIN/OPTOUT mode'. (e) CLIENT SETINFO LIB-NAME/LIB-VER with space emits our SETNAME-pattern message vs upstream 'LIB-NAME cannot contain spaces, newlines or special characters'. (f) CLIENT PAUSE -ms accepted (returns OK) vs upstream 'ERR timeout is negative'. Fix: align error wording in fr-command CLIENT subcommand parsers; canonicalize CLIENT INFO / ID / HELLO id drift in live_oracle_frames_match.
close_reason: Fully shipped: live_redis_core_client_matches_runtime passes 124/124 against vendored oracle. Sub-tasks (a) CLIENT INFO/ID and (b) HELLO id drift covered by live_oracle_client_id_drift_matches / live_oracle_hello_id_drift_matches / live_oracle_client_info_drift_matches canonicalizers in crates/fr-conformance/src/lib.rs. Sub-tasks (c)-(f) wording aligned (CLIENT NO-EVICT/NO-TOUCH/CACHING/SETINFO/PAUSE) per commits 160b9e5, 8b0dfb9, deec7b8, 865eb1f.

title: SENTINEL: RESP3 map not downconverted to array for RESP2 + wrong arity/unknown-subcommand wording
description: Differential probe (NobleRiver 2026-06-08) of fr in --sentinel mode vs redis-sentinel 7.2.4. 3 bugs: (1) SENTINEL MASTER/MASTERS/SLAVES/SENTINELS return a RESP3 Map (%N) even on a RESP2 connection — a protocol violation; redis's addReplyMapLen emits a flat *2N array in RESP2. (2) Known-subcommand wrong-arity error used 'sentinel master' (space) vs redis 'sentinel|master' (pipe canonical fullname). (3) Unknown subcommand used the long 'unknown subcommand or wrong number of arguments for X' vs redis's short 'unknown subcommand X' (commandCheckExistence, like CLIENT/CONFIG). Fix: downconvert sentinel reply for RESP2 in fr-command sentinel_cmd (reuse lua RESP3->RESP2 converter); fr-sentinel wrong_arity pipe-ifies + subcommand_syntax_error short form. fr-command + fr-sentinel clean crates.
close_reason: Fixed in fb9a8f245: (1) sentinel_cmd (fr-command) downconverts the RESP3 map reply to a flat array for RESP2 clients (reuses lua RESP3->RESP2 converter, now pub(crate)); RESP2 SENTINEL MASTER now *40 (was %20), RESP3 stays %20. (2) fr-sentinel wrong_arity pipe-qualifies 'sentinel|master'; (3) subcommand_syntax_error uses upstream short 'unknown subcommand X' form. Verified vs redis-sentinel 7.2.4: 13 error cases byte-exact, MASTER/MASTERS structure now array in RESP2. fr-command 1129 + fr-sentinel 164 green. Residual: runtime ping-state + MYID non-determinism (separate).

title: GEOSEARCH unknown shape keyword surfaces wrong-arity error instead of syntax error
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:
GEOSEARCH geo FROMLONLAT 15 37 BYSHAPE 200 km
ven: ERR syntax error
fr : ERR wrong number of arguments for 'geosearch' command

Per upstream geo.c::geosearchGenericCommand, an unknown shape keyword (anything other than BYRADIUS or BYBOX) takes the SyntaxError branch via addReplyErrorObject(c, shared.syntaxerr). fr surfaces a misleading 'wrong number of arguments' instead, presumably because the BYSHAPE token consumes the next arg slot when scanning options and the post-loop arity check then fires.

Fix: ensure the GEOSEARCH option-token loop emits SyntaxError on any unknown keyword before the trailing arity check, matching upstream addReplyErrorObject(c, shared.syntaxerr) on the shape branch.
close_reason: Reordered GEOSEARCH option parsing so trailing-flags loop (parse_geo_search_flags) runs BEFORE the has_center/has_shape arity check. Unknown shape keywords (BYSHAPE etc.) now route through the existing SyntaxError else-branch in the flags loop, matching upstream geo.c::geoSearchGeneric. Pinned via geosearch_unknown_shape_keyword_surfaces_syntax_error_per_upstream.

title: [perf] Specialize borrowed RESP command length parsing
description: Profile-backed pass 3 target after frankenredis-ds9o7. Borrowed parser final profile: parse_command_frame_borrowed 73.96% self, with the old owned parse_bulk/drop costs removed; remaining hot path still uses generic parse_i64_strict for array and bulk lengths on every command argument. Lever: add a borrowed-command-only strict RESP length parser that preserves -1/null, leading-zero rejection, max-length errors, incomplete behavior, and owned-parser error parity while avoiding signed i64 overflow machinery on the hot borrowed multibulk path. Baseline first with RCH-built ds9o7 parser harness MODE=borrowed-command FIELDS=16 VALUE_SIZE=64 ITERATIONS=200000, then one lever, golden argv sha256, RCH fr-protocol tests/clippy/fmt/UBS, keep only if Score >= 2.0.
close_reason: Rejected: borrowed-command baseline 136.5 ms +/- 5.6 ms vs specialized length parser 149.8 ms +/- 5.5 ms in paired hyperfine, so candidate regressed 1.10x and failed Score>=2.0. Code reverted. Next deeper primitive: caller-reused argv buffer/runtime wiring or CRLF scan primitive, not more signed-length micro-specialization.

title: [perf] Pass217 event-loop tail and batched I/O profile

Only create/claim a source child if a fresh below-gate row identifies a concrete event-loop, batched I/O, parser, or output-ownership target likely to score >=2.0; otherwise close evidence-only and route deeper.
close_reason: Evidence-only closeout: current origin/main RCH build at 773a3a47 profiled event-loop/tail route. Throughput stayed above 0.9x gate across SET/GET/INCR/MIXED P1/P4/P16 and PING P1/P4/P16; lowest SET P1 ops=0.950x, PING inline P16=0.983x. P99 tails were favorable on all matrix rows. Focused SET/P1 repeated confirmation invalidated the p999 outlier: median ops=0.982x, p99=0.828x, p999=0.912x. Raw RESP golden matched sha256 79529d5aa52e35c347df6f0b5f618fe1c0595dcac4000ed167bd8b5a72773280. No source lever attempted; no-op isomorphism preserved ordering/tie/FP/RNG/expiry/persistence/replication/reply bytes. Artifacts: artifacts/optimization/coralox-pass217-eventloop-tail-profile/.

title: RESP protocol parser error messages + edge cases diverge from redis
description: 9 divergences found via raw-socket protocol probe vs vendored 7.2.4: (1) fr emits generic 'Protocol error: invalid frame' where redis has specific 'invalid multibulk length' (*xyz, *huge), 'invalid bulk length' ($abc, $huge, $-1 arg), "expected '$', got '+'" (non-bulk multibulk element); (2) *-1 and *0 multibulk should be silently skipped (no-op, process next command) but fr errors 'invalid command frame'; (3) fr accepts +PING inside *1 multibulk (returns PONG) instead of rejecting; (4) inline 'PING"' should be 'unbalanced quotes' but fr treats as unknown command. Highest-value: exact error-message parity + *<=0 no-op behavior. Found via protocol_error_differ probe.
close_reason: Fixed 6/9: protocol error-message parity (fr-server now renders the specific RespParseError via Display instead of hardcoded 'invalid frame'; fr-protocol BulkLengthTooLarge/MultibulkLengthTooLarge messages aligned to redis's 'invalid bulk/multibulk length') AND *0/*-1 empty/null multibulk now silently skipped as a no-op (matching networking.c) instead of erroring. Verified byte-exact vs vendored for $abc/$huge/*xyz/*huge/*-1/*0. Remaining 3 (non-$ multibulk element / $-1 arg / inline trailing-quote = command-element validation) split to follow-up bead. New tests tcp_protocol_errors_use_upstream_wording + tcp_empty_and_null_multibulk_are_skipped; fr-server+fr-protocol tests pass, clippy clean.

title: Sentinel tilt should trigger on any backward clock step
description: Upstream Redis 7.2.4 sentinelCheckTiltCondition enters TILT if delta < 0 or delta > sentinel_tilt_trigger, and refreshes tilt_start_time whenever that condition is observed. fr-sentinel::SentinelState::check_tilt uses now.abs_diff(previous_time), so a small backward clock step below the trigger does not enter TILT and an anomaly during existing TILT does not refresh the period. Acceptance: any backward now < previous_time enters/refreshes TILT, forward deltas above tilt_trigger still enter/refresh, and normal elapsed tilt period exits only after a non-anomalous tick.
close_reason: Implemented Sentinel tilt parity: backward clock steps and oversized forward deltas enter/refresh tilt; remote fr-sentinel fmt/check/test/clippy and UBS passed.

title: fr-command::move_cmd is a stub returning Integer(0) — Lua redis.call('MOVE') and AOF replay route through this dead path, silently lying about cross-db transfers
description:
/mock-code-finder audit:

  crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:16560 — '// Stub — in production this would trigger graceful shutdown'
  crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:16574 — '// In single-namespace mode, all DBs share the same store, so MOVE cannot succeed'

The MOVE comment is WRONG. fr's storage engine namespaces keys per-DB via fr_store::encode_db_key, and fr-runtime::handle_move_command at lib.rs:10309 implements the cross-DB transfer correctly.

Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 via Lua (which routes through dispatch_argv → fr-command::move_cmd, NOT fr-runtime::handle_move_command):

  EVAL 'return redis.call("MOVE", "foo", 1)' 0
    upstream: 1, foo gone from db 0, foo in db 1
    fr      : 0, foo still in db 0, db 1 empty   (silently fails)

  EVAL 'return redis.call("MOVE", "foo", 999)' 0
    upstream: ERR DB index is out of range script: ...
    fr      : ERR out of range   (wrong wording)

Other affected paths:
- AOF replay (fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:2262) routes through dispatch_argv too. AOF'd MOVE records would silently no-op on replay.
- MULTI/EXEC routes through the same dispatch path.

Range check is also wrong: hardcoded 0..16 instead of store.database_count.

Fix: rewrite fr-command::move_cmd to mirror fr-runtime::handle_move_command — parse target_db, validate against database_count, reject same-db, then use fr_store::encode_db_key + Store::copy + Store::del. source_db comes from store.dispatch_client_ctx.db_index.
close_reason: Rewrote fr-command::move_cmd to mirror fr-runtime::handle_move_command — parses target_db as i64, validates against store.database_count, rejects same-db with upstream wording, encodes source/dest keys via fr_store::encode_db_key, then Store::copy + Store::del. source_db is read from store.dispatch_client_ctx.db_index. Added 3 new unit tests covering same-db rejection, cross-DB transfer success, and out-of-range wording. Old test move_same_db that pinned the buggy Integer(0) reply replaced. Lua redis.call('MOVE') and AOF replay now route through the corrected handler.

title: Implement SENTINEL SET master-reboot-down-after-period parity

title: WAIT incorrectly fires noscript guard from EVAL/FCALL — upstream commands.def has flags=0 (no CMD_NOSCRIPT)
description: Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4: `redis.call('WAIT', '0', '1000')` inside EVAL returns Integer(0) — vendored allows WAIT from scripts. fr's wait_cmd at fr-command/src/lib.rs:16005 had a script_nesting_level guard that fired the noscript reply, diverging from upstream. The original o7kay change introduced this guard with a comment claiming CMD_NOSCRIPT was set in commands.def — but checking commands.def line 10689 confirms WAIT has flags=0 (zero), distinct from the adjacent WAITAOF entry on line 10690 which does carry CMD_NOSCRIPT. The existing test pin wait_rejects_script_context_and_extra_args asserted the wrong noscript wording.
close_reason: Removed the noscript guard in wait_cmd. Updated comment to reference commands.def evidence and contrast with WAITAOF. Renamed and rewrote the existing test pin to wait_rejects_extra_args_and_runs_inside_script to assert the upstream-correct behavior: trailing args still surface WrongArity, but a scripted call returns Integer(0). 837 fr-command (4 pre-existing stream failures), 330/330 fr-store.

title: DB-index commands report wrong error for >i32 values (COPY/MOVE/SELECT/SWAPDB)
description:
Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4:

Upstream parses the DB index argument via getIntFromObjectOrReply
(int = i32), which has two failure modes:
  - value doesn't fit i32 → "value is out of range, value must
    between -2147483648 and 2147483647"
  - value fits i32 but is outside [0, dbnum) → command-specific
    "DB index is out of range" / "invalid first DB index" / etc.

fr's parse_db_index_arg uses parse_i64_arg and then checks
[0, dbnum). Everything outside that range — including values that
don't fit i32 — returns the per-command DB-index wording.

Affected commands:

  COPY src dst DB 99999999999    (and 2147483648, etc.)
    vendored: ERR value is out of range, value must between -2147483648 and 2147483647
    fr:       ERR DB index is out of range

  MOVE k 99999999999
    vendored: ERR value is out of range, value must between -2147483648 and 2147483647
    fr:       ERR DB index is out of range

  SELECT 99999999999
    vendored: ERR value is out of range, value must between -2147483648 and 2147483647
    fr:       ERR DB index is out of range

  SWAPDB 99999999999 0
    vendored: ERR invalid first DB index
    fr:       ERR DB index is out of range

  SWAPDB 0 99999999999
    vendored: ERR invalid second DB index
    fr:       ERR DB index is out of range

In-range-but-too-large values (e.g. DB 99 with dbnum=16) match
on both — only the i32-overflow path diverges.

Note SWAPDB uses a different per-arg wording ("invalid first DB
index" / "invalid second DB index") because upstream's
getLongFromObjectOrReply for SWAPDB passes that custom message in
both the parse-fail and i32-overflow path. COPY/MOVE/SELECT use
the generic i32-range wording for overflow.

Fix locus: crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs::parse_db_index_arg, the
shared helper. Add a parse-time i32 narrowing step that emits the
appropriate overflow wording before the [0, dbnum) range check.
SWAPDB's call sites need a separate signature variant since their
overflow wording diverges from the range-overflow wording.

Test plan: pin each command for {>i32, ==i32::MAX+1, in-range
but >=dbnum, valid, negative} variants.
close_reason: fixed

title: DEBUG OBJECT omits quicklist ql_nodes/ql_avg_node/ql_listpack_max/ql_compressed/ql_uncompressed_size fields

title: fuzz_client_tracking: crash on empty input
description:
The client tracking fuzzer crashes with an empty input [].

The crash hash da39a3ee5e6b4b0d3255bfef95601890afd80709 is the SHA-1 of an empty string.

Code analysis shows the fuzzer should return early when parsing fails:
- fuzz_raw_client_tracking() calls argv_from_raw() which returns empty vec
- parse_client_tracking_state() should return Err for empty argv
- Function should return early without crashing

Needs investigation to find the actual crash location - possibly a panic in library code.

Artifact: fuzz/artifacts/fuzz_client_tracking/crash-da39a3ee5e6b4b0d3255bfef95601890afd80709
close_reason: harness bugs - fixed error message constants and validation logic to match upstream

title: fr-command DEBUG missing STRUCTSIZE / LISTPACK / QUICKLIST subcommands
description:
Sibling defects to frankenredis-l2zqy / r2l7c / 53n6u / byt3l (DEBUG-subcommand-missing family). Three more subcommands upstream debug.c::debugCommand exposes that fr-command currently routes to the catch-all 'ERR unknown subcommand or wrong number of arguments...' branch:

- DEBUG STRUCTSIZE at debug.c:878-888 — argc==2; returns a BulkString with C-internals struct sizes:
    bits:N robj:N dictentry:N sdshdr5:N sdshdr8:N sdshdr16:N sdshdr32:N sdshdr64:N
  Tooling sometimes parses bits:64 to detect arch. fr has no equivalent C structs; surface upstream-typical values so the format stays parseable.

- DEBUG LISTPACK <key> at debug.c:680-691 — argc==3; lookup key. Returns:
    ERR no such key                          if missing
    ERR Not a listpack encoded object.       if encoding != listpack
    +Listpack structure printed on stdout    otherwise
  Upstream prints the structure to stdout via lpRepr; fr can skip the print and return the canned status.

- DEBUG QUICKLIST <key> [full] at debug.c:692-706 — argc==3 or 4; lookup key. Returns:
    ERR no such key                          if missing
    ERR Not a quicklist encoded object.      if encoding != quicklist
    +Quicklist structure printed on stdout   otherwise
  The optional  argument (0/1) toggles verbose output upstream; fr can accept-and-ignore.

fr's object_encoding() helper already classifies values as 'listpack' / 'quicklist' (and others), so the encoding gate maps cleanly.

Acceptance: the three subcommand arms validate arity, look up the key, return the upstream-canonical error text on missing/wrong-encoding, and return the upstream-canonical success SimpleString otherwise. STRUCTSIZE returns a BulkString with the 8 fields. Wrong-arity surfaces the existing 'unknown subcommand or wrong number of arguments for X. Try DEBUG HELP.' shape used by the rest of the family. Tests stay green; clippy clean.
close_reason: done

title: SENTINEL MASTER/SLAVES/SENTINELS replies missing many upstream fields
description:
Redis 7.2.4 addReplySentinelRedisInstance emits many fields that fr-sentinel instance_to_info_array omits:

Always emitted:
- link-pending-commands (ri->link->pending_commands)
- link-refcount (ri->link->refcount)
- last-ping-sent (mstime() - ri->link->act_ping_time)
- last-ok-ping-reply (mstime() - ri->link->last_avail_time)
- last-ping-reply (mstime() - ri->link->last_pong_time)

Conditional:
- s-down-time (when S_DOWN: mstime() - ri->s_down_since_time)
- o-down-time (when O_DOWN: mstime() - ri->o_down_since_time)
- failover-state (when FAILOVER_IN_PROGRESS)

Masters/Slaves:
- info-refresh (mstime() - ri->info_refresh)
- role-reported (master/slave string)
- role-reported-time (mstime() - ri->role_reported_time)

Masters only:
- config-epoch

Slaves only:
- master-link-down-time
- master-link-status (ok/err)
- master-host
- master-port
- slave-priority
- slave-repl-offset
- replica-announced

Scope: crates/fr-sentinel only; add these fields to instance_to_info_array.
close_reason: Completed Sentinel instance info field parity: added upstream missing fields and validated fr-sentinel check/test/clippy via rch plus UBS.

title: XTRIM error messages diverge for missing-threshold, duplicate-strategy, and over-long argv
description:
Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 surfaces three XTRIM
error-message divergences. Upstream
streamParseAddOrTrimArgsOrReply iterates over argv token-by-token,
tracking a trim_strategy flag. fr's XTRIM handler hard-codes
positions and caps argv.len() at 7, producing the wrong wording for:

1. XTRIM s MAXLEN '~'
   vendored: ERR value is not an integer or out of range
   fr:       ERR syntax error
   (upstream tries to parse argv[i+1] for the threshold after
   consuming ~; with no threshold it falls into the int-parse error.)

2. XTRIM s MINID '~'
   vendored: ERR Invalid stream ID specified as stream command argument
   fr:       ERR syntax error
   (same as 1, but MINID parses as a stream ID, so the parse error
   reflects that type.)

3. XTRIM s MAXLEN 5 MINID 0
   XTRIM s MINID 0 MAXLEN 5
   XTRIM s MAXLEN 5 MAXLEN 3
   XTRIM s MAXLEN '~' 5 MINID 0
   XTRIM s MAXLEN '~' 5 LIMIT 5 MINID 0
   vendored: ERR syntax error, MAXLEN and MINID options at the same time are not compatible
   fr:       ERR syntax error (or "wrong number of arguments" for argc>7)
   (upstream's loop sees the second strategy token and trips the
   trim_strategy != NONE guard. Two MAXLENs also trigger the same
   message — upstream doesn't distinguish "duplicate" from "both".)

Fix locus: crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs::xtrim. Replace the
positional parser with a token-iteration loop that:
  - tracks trim_strategy (None / MAXLEN / MINID) and rejects a
    second strategy with the incompat wording,
  - parses the threshold type-specifically (int for MAXLEN, stream
    ID for MINID) so the parse error reflects the upstream wording
    on missing threshold,
  - removes the argv.len() <= 7 cap so `MAXLEN ~ 5 LIMIT 5 MINID 0`
    can detect the second strategy.

XADD already matches upstream wording for these cases — it uses a
different parser path. This bead only covers XTRIM.

Test plan: pin all 5 wording variants in a regression test plus the
existing positive paths (MAXLEN 5, MINID 0, MAXLEN ~ 5 LIMIT 5).
close_reason: fixed

title: SENTINEL SET down-after should propagate to replicas and sentinels
description: Upstream Redis 7.2.4 sentinelSetCommand calls sentinelPropagateDownAfterPeriod after changing a master's down-after-milliseconds, updating the master's known replicas and sentinels to the same down_after_period. fr-sentinel currently changes only the master, leaving SENTINEL REPLICAS/SENTINELS instance info divergent after SET. Acceptance: SENTINEL SET <master> down-after-milliseconds <ms> updates the master, all known replicas, and all known sentinels, with regression coverage.

title: redis parity: ACL SAVE round-trip should persist sanitize-payload marker
description: The runtime test acl_save_and_load_round_trip_persists_users at crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:17477 is red because it still expects ACL SAVE output without sanitize-payload, while local serialization emits it and both core_acl.json and upstream redis/tests/unit/acl.tcl treat sanitize-payload as the default flag. Reconcile the runtime round-trip expectation and any related save/load assertions so ACL SAVE and ACL LOAD match Redis default sanitize-payload behavior.
close_reason: Completed

title: Lua string escape: unrecognized \X should drop backslash (Lua 5.1.5 semantics)
description:
Lua 5.1.5's llex.c::read_string handles unrecognized escape sequences (e.g. \x, since Lua 5.1 has no \xNN hex escape) by saving the char *after* the backslash and advancing. Vendored Redis ships this Lua, so:

  EVAL 'return string.len("\x00\x01\xff")' 0
    vendored → 9   (becomes literal "x00x01xff")
    fr        → 12 (kept the backslashes verbatim)
  EVAL 'return string.byte("\xff")' 0
    vendored → 120 (ASCII 'x')
    fr        → 92  (ASCII '\\')

Same script produces a different SHA via redis.sha1hex when bytes are involved, breaking Lua scripts that rely on Lua 5.1's lenient escape behavior.

Probe sweep #122.
close_reason: completed

title: resp3_reply_type_gate: extend with complex nested RESP3 maps (XINFO STREAM/GROUPS, XPENDING, COMMAND INFO/DOCS, FUNCTION STATS/LIST)

title: Add golden artifact tests for fr-sentinel info parsers

title: MEMORY STATS: overhead.total hardcoded to 0 (should sum prior fields)
description: fr-command/src/lib.rs:18586 hardcodes 'overhead.total' to 0 in MEMORY STATS output. Upstream object.c::getMemoryOverheadData computes mh->overhead_total as the sum of mh->startup_allocated + mh->repl_backlog + mh->clients_slaves + mh->clients_normal + mh->cluster_links + mh->aof_buffer + mh->lua_caches + mh->functions_caches + mh->total_db_overheads_per_db_keys. Vendored reports overhead.total=868408 while fr reports 0 -- this is a derived field that just needs to be computed from values fr already has after zfu61 wired up clients.normal/slaves. Implementation: replace pair('overhead.total', 0) with a running sum collected during the iteration of the prior pair() emissions, or compute explicitly: stat_clients_normal_mem_bytes + stat_clients_replica_mem_bytes + scripts_memory_bytes + functions_memory_bytes + (sum over dbs of main_bytes + expires_bytes). Note that startup.allocated/replication.backlog/cluster.links/aof.buffer are still 0 so those won't contribute -- the sum will be dominated by client buffers + db hashtable overhead. Acceptance: overhead.total emits a non-zero value matching the sum of the populated component fields.
close_reason: MEMORY STATS overhead.total now reports the real sum of overhead components instead of hardcoded 0. Implementation: captured the same component values used for the prior pair() emissions in local i64 bindings (startup_allocated, replication_backlog, clients_slaves, clients_normal, cluster_links, aof_buffer, lua_caches, functions_caches), summed them into a mutable overhead_total accumulator before the emit loop, then in the per-db iteration also accumulated main_bytes + expires_bytes (the dictMemUsage approximation) into overhead_total. The trailing 'overhead.total' row now emits this real sum. Live differential probe with 2 keys: fr 'overhead.total: 560' = clients.normal 432 + 2 keys * 64B/dict-entry = 560 (correct). Vendored reports overhead.total=868408 which is dominated by its 865864 startup.allocated baseline -- fr doesn't track startup.allocated yet (separate gap). The relation between fr's overhead.total and its component fields is now correct and consistent with upstream object.c::getMemoryOverheadData's mh->overhead_total formula. Tests: fr-command 910 pass.

title: Skip maxmemory eviction loop for read commands
description: Maxmemory enforcement currently runs eviction loop for all commands, including reads. This can evict keys on GET and overwrites last eviction info. Redis enforces maxmemory only on write-ish commands. Fix by skipping eviction loop when command does not advance replication offset; add regression test.
close_reason: Skip maxmemory eviction loop for read commands; add regression test

title: fr-command: MEMORY unknown-subcommand wording uses verbose form instead of upstream's shorter 'unknown subcommand X' reply
description:
Vendored Redis 7.2.4 emits the simpler wording for an unknown MEMORY subcommand, preserving the user-typed casing:
  ERR unknown subcommand '<input>'. Try MEMORY HELP.

fr-command emitted the verbose form 'Unknown subcommand or wrong number of arguments for '<lowercase>'. Try MEMORY HELP.', and lowercased the subcommand token.

Differential probe vs vendored redis 7.2.4:
  MEMORY NOSUCH → fr 'Unknown subcommand or wrong number of arguments for nosuch …', redis 'unknown subcommand NOSUCH …'
  MEMORY nosuch → same divergence with 'nosuch' token

Fix: use 'unknown subcommand '<sub_str>'. Try MEMORY HELP.' with the original argv[1] casing preserved.

title: DUMP/RDB LZF compression diverges from redis lzf_c.c (HLOG, hash, post-match rehash) — non-byte-exact compressed strings
description: DUMP of an HLL (and any string >20B that compresses) diverges byte-for-byte from vendored redis 7.2.4: oracle stored the 33B HLL raw (0x21 len prefix) while fr LZF-compressed it (0xc3 marker, 33->29B). RESTORE is interchangeable (functional OK) but the wire/RDB bytes differ. Root cause: fr-persist::lzf_compress (line 1842) is NOT a faithful port of deps/lzf/lzf_c.c VERY_FAST path: (1) fr HLOG=14 vs redis HLOG=16; (2) fr uses Knuth multiplicative hash vs redis liblzf VERY_FAST IDX = ((h>>8)-h*5)&0xFFFF; (3) fr omits the VERY_FAST post-match rehash (redis: after a match, --ip;--ip; reinsert 2 htab entries). Different hashing+rehash => different match-finding => different (valid) compressed bytes AND different compress-vs-raw decisions (redis only LZF if output fits in len-4). The existing all-'x' test (z4tsz) passes because identical trigrams collide regardless of hash. Fix: port lzf_c.c VERY_FAST exactly + add a random-input differential test (DUMP vs oracle byte-exact). Also: stream DUMP encoding diverges (oracle 101B vs fr 132B) — separate. Found by cc-opus.
close_reason: VERIFIED RESOLVED (cc-opus). fr lzf_compress is byte-exact with vendored redis 7.2.4: differential DUMP sweep across ~4200 random + periodic short/repetitive strings (len 1-4000, alphabets 1-5 chars, RLE + periodic patterns) = 0 divergences. Line-by-line code review vs legacy_redis_code/redis/src/lzf_c.c confirms the active VERY_FAST config (FRST/NEXT/IDX, HLOG=16), match-acceptance, maxlen, the 16-unroll match-length loop, ip-advance by full match, and the two-position post-match rehash all match. The residual 3 short-repeat edges from the 4b113f7c7-era note are fixed. DUMP/RDB LZF parity complete.

title: assert() second arg: nil should use default msg; non-string/number should luaL_argerror
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:

  assert(false, nil)             v=assertion failed!                                      fr=nil
  assert(nil, nil)               v=assertion failed!                                      fr=nil
  assert(nil, true)              v=bad argument #2 to '?' (string expected, got boolean)  fr=true
  assert(false)                  both: assertion failed!                                  ✓
  assert(nil, 42)                both: 42                                                 ✓
  assert(nil, 'custom')          both: custom                                             ✓

Upstream luaB_assert (lbaselib.c):
  if (!lua_toboolean(L, 1))
    return luaL_error(L, "%s", luaL_optstring(L, 2, "assertion failed!"));

`luaL_optstring(L, 2, "assertion failed!")` returns the default when arg 2 is nil OR absent, accepts string/number via lua_tolstring coercion, and rejects other types with "bad argument #2 (string expected, got TYPE)".

fr's assert at lua_eval.rs ~line 5104 calls `args.get(1).map(|a| to_display_string(a)).unwrap_or("assertion failed!")` — this:
  1. Treats explicit nil as the message (rendering "nil") instead of treating it as missing.
  2. Accepts bool/table/etc. via to_display_string instead of erroring.

Fix:
- Treat None and LuaValue::Nil as "use default".
- For LuaValue::Str / LuaValue::Number: coerce to byte string.
- For other types: raise "bad argument #2 to '?' (string expected, got TYPE)" via lua_format_argerror (so pcall(assert,…) and direct calls both render correctly).

The pcall-shape for the error uses lua_format_argerror with name "assert"; pcall caller emits `'?'` (already handled).
close_reason: Rewrote assert arg-2 handling to match upstream luaL_optstring: None/Nil → 'assertion failed!' default; Str/Number → coerce to byte string (with integer fast-path); other types → lua_format_argerror('assert', 2, 'string expected, got TYPE') which honors the dual direct/pcall shape. Regression test assert_second_arg_uses_default_for_nil_and_argerror_for_bad_type_wmu03 pins 4 default-msg cases + 2 pass-through cases + 3 bad-type cases + 2 truthy regressions. Full release test suite (1014 tests) still passes. Live-diff vs vendored 16380: 8/8 cases match.

title: SENTINEL PENDING-SCRIPTS should report running jobs with run-time
description: Upstream Redis 7.2.4 sentinelPendingScriptsCommand emits a five-field map per script job. For jobs flagged SENTINEL_SCRIPT_RUNNING it returns flags=running, pid=<pid>, and run-time=<mstime()-start_time>; scheduled jobs return flags=scheduled, pid=0, and run-delay. fr-sentinel ScriptJob has only path/args/retry_count, so cmd_pending_scripts always reports scheduled and run-delay=0. Scope: crates/fr-sentinel; model running job metadata and add dispatch regression coverage for the running branch while preserving scheduled replies.
close_reason: Modeled SENTINEL PENDING-SCRIPTS running job metadata and added dispatch coverage for run-time plus scheduled run-delay.

title: [perf] fr-protocol to_bytes should reserve exact encoded length
description: Profile-backed fr-protocol encoder workload: RespFrame::to_bytes on an 8-field HGETALL-like bulk-array response, 10,000,000 iterations via rch hyperfine baseline mean 3.241 s +/- 0.044 s. GDB profile during 30,000,000 encodes collected 40 samples: to_bytes/encode_into in 28/40, extend_from_slice in 25/40, memcpy in 12/40, RawVec allocation/reallocation frames in 4/40 and free/drop glue present. The rejected one-byte-prefix Vec::push lever regressed to 3.306 s and was not retained. Acceptance: make to_bytes reserve exact encoded length before encode_into; preserve every encoded byte and ordering with baseline/candidate golden sha256; run rch fr-protocol fmt/check/tests/clippy; rebenchmark same harness and keep only if score >=2.0.
close_reason: Completed: exact to_bytes encoded-length reservation. rch hyperfine encode workload improved 3.241s +/-0.044s to 2.345s +/-0.052s (1.382x, 27.66% lower mean). Golden to_bytes vs encode_into sha256 matched 4f705e7ccd2a8358261488889f285c75ba0b03a6051aa85501c29b8eedd50770. rch fmt/check/golden/full fr-protocol tests/clippy passed; UBS touched-file scan completed with existing inventory.

title: fr-command Lua: redis.error_reply/status_reply/sha1hex/redis.call accept invalid arg shapes silently or use wrong wording
description:
Four wording/behavior divergences in fr's Lua redis.* builtins vs vendored redis 7.2.4 script_lua.c:

1. redis.error_reply(<non-string>) / redis.status_reply(<non-string>): upstream returns the table {err='ERR wrong number or type of arguments'} as a *value* (luaPushError + return 1, not luaError), so the resulting RESP error has no 'script: <sha>' suffix. fr converted any value to display string and built an error table from it (e.g. error_reply(123) → 'ERR 123').

2. redis.call(): upstream emits 'Please specify at least one argument for this redis lib call' when no command name is provided. fr emitted 'wrong number of arguments for redis.call'.

3. redis.sha1hex(): upstream emits 'wrong number of arguments' when the function is called with no args. fr returned the hex of the empty string (da39a3ee...).

Fix:
- error_reply / status_reply: return the wrong-number-or-type-of-arguments error as a {err=...} table value (not as an Err) so the script-context wrapper doesn't fire.
- error_reply: pre-pend '-' to the user-supplied body before passing to lua_format_error_reply_payload (mirrors luaRedisErrorReplyCommand). This preserves the existing test cases pinning user-supplied codes (NOAUTH, custom, …) which were already correct.
- redis.call: change the no-args wording to 'Please specify at least one argument for this redis lib call'.
- redis.sha1hex: error on no args.

title: omnibus parity suite emits phantom FAILs: config contamination between gates + edge_sweep2 PTTL 1ms jitter
close_reason: Fixed both phantom-FAIL sources. (1) run_parity_differs.sh now snapshots the as-started baseline of the commonly-mutated configs (encoding *-max-listpack-* family incl list=-2/hash-entries=512, notify-keyspace-events, maxmemory[-policy]) from both servers once and restores them + FLUSHALL before EVERY gate, so a config-mutating gate (encoding_config_boundary etc.) no longer contaminates later gates (geo/hash/fuzz_untrodden saw phantom OBJECT ENCODING/reply diffs). (2) edge_sweep2_differ.py tolerates (P)TTL/(P)EXPIRETIME us-ms jitter (PTTL 100000 vs 99999 on fresh SET PX) like the other gates. Validated: omnibus 3 phantoms now PASS; edge_sweep2 5/5.

title: [perf] Thread single command metadata packet through dispatch
description: Profile-backed follow-up after frankenredis-ferss rejection. Pass75 SETEX/PSETEX P16/1M profile still shows repeated command metadata/hash work as a class: RandomState::hash_one::<&[u8]> 8.46% flat, command_table_index 1.74% flat / 3.21% children, command_key_indexes 1.19% flat / 2.26% children, acl_command_selectors_for_argv 0.48% flat / 2.02% children, plus dispatch/classification/propagation rows. The fixed-key command_key_indexes fallback replacement alone was rejected at ~1.00x, so do not retry that micro-lever. One deeper safe-Rust lever only: compute a per-frame command metadata packet once (canonical command id/name, table index or custom key-spec marker, arity result, key indexes/references, ACL selector metadata as appropriate) and thread it through key extraction, ACL checks, dispatch/classification, and propagation rewrite so repeated lowercasing/hash/table scans are eliminated as a class. Preserve all bespoke key-spec branches, duplicate first-occurrence behavior, invalid UTF-8/unknown command errors, pubsub channel ACL separation, command ordering, transaction semantics, propagation bytes, and SETEX/PSETEX golden sha256. Baseline first with rch release-perf + hyperfine on SETEX/PSETEX P16/1M; keep only with same-worker Score>=2.0.
notes: Superseding final optimization ledger: later Pass77 tested client-tracking observed-key foldhash map against fresh SETEX/PSETEX P16/1M profile; golden sha256 dc3d47345c58e9839e6aa57875e4b3473379bc218bcc240c5b45907f8cb00dd7 matched; paired baseline 4.6065499663s +/- 0.0332700525s vs candidate 4.6382182554s +/- 0.1686028856s; reversed baseline 4.5613431148s +/- 0.0631943326s vs candidate 4.5728807854s +/- 0.0369083723s; Score=0, no source retained. Proof: artifacts/optimization/frankenredis-wooin/PROOF.md. Next route: zero-copy/batched command packet, not hash micro-family.
close_reason: Rejected all-access ACL metadata fast path: SETEX/PSETEX P16/1M paired hyperfine baseline 4.76140013354s +/- 0.07191982313, candidate 4.91062918114s +/- 0.24149283718; golden sha256 matched dc3d47345c58e9839e6aa57875e4b3473379bc218bcc240c5b45907f8cb00dd7; no source retained. Proof: artifacts/optimization/frankenredis-wooin/PROOF.md

title: fr-command: XGROUP CREATE missing ENTRIESREAD support, wrong shape wording
description:
Upstream xgroupCommand parses XGROUP CREATE as:
  XGROUP CREATE key groupname id|$ [MKSTREAM] [ENTRIESREAD entries-read]
i.e. MKSTREAM and ENTRIESREAD are independent option tokens that may appear in either order.

For invalid shapes (argc < 5 or unrecognised options token), upstream calls addReplySubcommandSyntaxError producing 'unknown subcommand or wrong number of arguments for CREATE. Try XGROUP HELP.'. The ENTRIESREAD value is parsed via getLongLongFromObjectOrReply with 'value is not an integer or out of range' for non-numeric.

fr-command:
- Strictly required argc ∈ {5, 6} so any ENTRIESREAD form was wrong-arity rejected.
- Emitted 'ERR syntax error' / 'wrong number of arguments for xgroup|create command' instead of the addReplySubcommandSyntaxError wording.

Differential probe vs vendored redis 7.2.4 byte-matches all six divergent cases after fix:
  XGROUP CREATE s g 0 ENTRIESREAD 5
  XGROUP CREATE s g 0 ENTRIESREAD abc
  XGROUP CREATE s g 0 ENTRIESREAD
  XGROUP CREATE s g 0 ENTRIESREAD 5 EXTRA
  XGROUP CREATE s g 0 EXTRANOSUCH
  XGROUP CREATE s g 0 MKSTREAM ENTRIESREAD 10

title: Stale unit tests after LASTSAVE-seed and CONFIG-RESETSTAT-preserves-slowlog changes
description:
Two unit tests were left broken by prior commits 76024ac (LASTSAVE seeds the entry counter at boot) and 18ddb91 (CONFIG RESETSTAT preserves slowlog entries):

- lastsave_returns_timestamp: asserts initial LASTSAVE == 0, but Store::new() now stamps last_save_time_sec to SystemTime::now()
- config_resetstat_clears_info_stats_counters: asserts slowlog_len() == 0 after CONFIG RESETSTAT, but the new behavior preserves slowlog entries (matches upstream)

Update both tests to mirror the new contract.
close_reason: Updated lastsave_returns_timestamp to capture boot timestamp (matches frankenredis-30hub) and config_resetstat_clears_info_stats_counters to assert slowlog survives RESETSTAT (matches frankenredis-0vneu / upstream). Bundled into commit 528877d.

title: fr-runtime: fr_p2c_008_u001 fast-expire command-path test fails in rch
close_reason: Added fast active-expire cycle on command path and stabilized runtime test expectations

title: Master does not propagate DEL/UNLINK on key expiry (active + lazy) -> replication-stream/AOF lacks expiry deletes, breaks fr-master<->redis-replica
description: Upstream propagates a DEL (or UNLINK when lazyfree-lazy-expire=yes) to replicas + AOF for every key it expires, via both the active-expire cycle and lazy expiry on access (db.c::deleteExpiredKeyAndPropagate / propagateDeletion). fr expires keys (store active_expire_cycle + drop_if_expired) and fires the 'expired' keyspace event, but captures NO DEL for propagation. Confirmed via fr master->replica MONITOR: oracle replica stream shows [SET ek v PXAT <ms>, DEL ek] on expiry; fr shows only [SET ek v PXAT <ms>] (no DEL). fr 'works' only because its replica runs the same active-expire cycle and self-expires — but real redis replicas do NOT expire independently (they serve logically-expired keys as nil and wait for the master's DEL), so an fr master with a redis replica would leak/diverge stale keys (SCAN/DBSIZE/RANDOMKEY see them). Also the AOF lacks the explicit expiry DEL. Fix: have the store's active-expire cycle + lazy drop_if_expired record evicted (db, logical-key) pairs, and the runtime capture_aof_record a DEL (UNLINK if lazyfree-lazy-expire) per evicted key, ordered after the triggering command. Likely a store eviction-queue drained by the runtime (mirrors the keyspace-notification queue but NOT gated on notify-keyspace-events).
close_reason: Active-expire-cycle DEL/UNLINK propagation shipped (commit 6f9ca7ad7) — this was the observed divergence (key set + reaped by the cycle with no access; oracle propagated DEL, fr propagated nothing). Now the master propagates DEL (UNLINK if lazyfree-lazy-expire) per reaped key, in the key's own db (SELECT emitted), gated on master role so replicas don't synthesize their own. Verified end-to-end multi-db; regression test active_expire_cycle_propagates_del_for_reaped_keys. The LAZY-on-access path is tracked as a separate follow-up bead (needs an in-dispatch hook with strict before-command ordering).

title: fr-command SRANDMEMBER/HRANDFIELD/ZRANDMEMBER negative count i64::MIN causes DoS — loops 9.2 quintillion times; upstream rejects via getRangeLongFromObjectOrReply
description:
REVIEW MODE Tick 14 finding via /testing-fuzzing skill (audited the *RANDMEMBER family count-validation paths against upstream).

LOCATION (crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs):
  - srandmember_count line 6001:
      let abs_count = count.unsigned_abs() as usize;
      let mut result = Vec::with_capacity(abs_count.min(1024));
      for _ in 0..abs_count { ... }   // <-- unbounded loop
  - hrandfield_count line 4607: same pattern
  - zrandmember_count line 7302: same pattern

UPSTREAM (legacy_redis_code/redis/src/t_zset.c::zrandmemberCommand line 4370, plus identical guards in t_set.c::srandmemberCommand and t_hash.c::hrandfieldCommand):

  if (getRangeLongFromObjectOrReply(c, c->argv[2],
                                    -LONG_MAX, LONG_MAX,
                                    &l, NULL) != C_OK) return;

object.c::getRangeLongFromObjectOrReply emits "value is out of range, value must between -9223372036854775807 and 9223372036854775807" when value is outside [-LONG_MAX, LONG_MAX]. Since LONG_MAX on 64-bit Linux = i64::MAX = 9223372036854775807, the only i64 value rejected is i64::MIN = -9223372036854775808.

fr's parse_i64_arg accepts any i64 including i64::MIN. Then count.unsigned_abs() returns 9223372036854775808u64 which casts to ~9.2 quintillion as usize on 64-bit systems. The for loop runs ~10^18 iterations — server hangs (not OOM since Vec capacity is capped at 1024, but the loop and clone calls progress glacially).

Reproducer:
  redis-cli> SADD s a b c
  redis-cli> SRANDMEMBER s -9223372036854775808
  upstream: ERR value is out of range, value must between -9223372036854775807 and 9223372036854775807
  fr (current): server hangs

Severity: [HIGH]. Public DoS. Any client with read access to a set / hash / sorted-set can hang the server. Affects SRANDMEMBER, HRANDFIELD, ZRANDMEMBER.

Fix:
1. Add a range-check helper parse_i64_arg_long_range(arg) that returns the upstream error format when value is i64::MIN (out of [-LONG_MAX, LONG_MAX] = [-i64::MAX, i64::MAX]).
2. Use it in srandmember, hrandfield, zrandmember handlers.
3. Add regression tests covering each command.
close_reason: fr-command SRANDMEMBER / HRANDFIELD / ZRANDMEMBER count argument now matches upstream getRangeLongFromObjectOrReply([-LONG_MAX, LONG_MAX]). New parse_i64_arg_long_range helper rejects i64::MIN (the only i64 value outside [-i64::MAX, i64::MAX] = [-LONG_MAX, LONG_MAX] on 64-bit Linux) with the upstream error wording 'ERR value is out of range, value must between -9223372036854775807 and 9223372036854775807'. Wired into all 3 *RANDMEMBER handlers. Prior behavior: i64::MIN passed as a negative count would call .unsigned_abs() returning 9.2*10^18, then loop that many iterations in srandmember_count / hrandfield_count / zrandmember_count — server hangs. Public DoS — any client with read access to a set/hash/sorted-set could trigger. New regression test randmember_family_rejects_i64_min_count_to_prevent_dos pins all three commands rejecting i64::MIN and accepting -1 as in-range. Discovered via /testing-fuzzing skill — fuzzed the count parameter validation paths against upstream's getRangeLongFromObjectOrReply guard. All fr-command lib tests pass; clippy clean. Thirty-second concrete fix this session, second via /testing-fuzzing (after z1h45 inline-quote fix). [HIGH] severity.

title: Reject HSCAN NOVALUES for Redis 7.2 parity
description: Conformance discovery via /testing-conformance-harnesses: live_redis_core_scan_matches_runtime XFAILs hscan_novalues_returns_keys_only and hscan_novalues_with_match because HSCAN NOVALUES is Redis 7.4-only while the vendored oracle is Redis 7.2.4. FrankenRedis currently accepts HSCAN ... NOVALUES, leaking a future feature against the V1 7.2.x parity baseline. Fix scope: reject NOVALUES in HSCAN with the same syntax-error path as vendored Redis 7.2.4, update core_scan fixtures to pin syntax errors, and remove both HSCAN NOVALUES entries from the live-oracle XFAIL list. Acceptance: targeted live oracle cases byte-match vendored Redis 7.2.4 and live_redis_core_scan_matches_runtime no longer filters HSCAN NOVALUES.
close_reason: Updated core_scan HSCAN NOVALUES fixtures to expect Redis 7.2.4 syntax errors and removed them from live scan XFAIL. live_redis_core_scan_matches_runtime now runs 107 cases with 107 passed against vendored Redis.

title: RDB stream save used fr-private type 15 (not Redis-loadable); enable upstream-compatible type-21 (STREAM_LISTPACKS_3) by default
description: fr's RDB file SAVE encoded streams as fr-private RDB_TYPE_STREAM=15, which upstream Redis cannot load (breaks fr->redis RDB interchange / migration). fr ALREADY had a Redis-compatible STREAM_LISTPACKS_3 (type 21) encoder (rdb_stream::encode_upstream_stream_listpacks3) gated behind the 'upstream-stream-rdb' feature, but the feature was off by default and the 5 stream round-trip tests failed (they compared decoded-with-transient-RdbStreamMetadata to original-without). The encoder is correct (data round-trips; the metadata is only stashed at the RDB layer for lossless re-encode and is never read by the live store, so no stale-bytes/data-loss risk). Fix: make 'upstream-stream-rdb' a default feature in fr-persist; normalize the transient stream metadata in the round-trip tests (strip_stream_metadata helper + fuzz normalizer). Verified end-to-end: fr SAVE a stream (entries + consumer group + PEL) -> vendored redis-server 7.2.4 loads it cleanly (XRANGE/XINFO GROUPS/XPENDING all intact, 'Done loading RDB, keys loaded: 2'); fr->fr round-trip also intact. Residual: streams that can't be losslessly encoded as type 21 (no watermark / out-of-order entries / complex consumer groups per can_encode_upstream_stream_losslessly) still fall back to type 15 (fr-loadable, not redis-loadable) - rare.
close_reason: Fixed + shipped (commit bed78e62f). fr now SAVEs streams as Redis-compatible STREAM_LISTPACKS_3 (type 21) by default (upstream-stream-rdb feature now default in fr-persist). Verified end-to-end: fr SAVE a stream with consumer group + PEL -> vendored redis-server 7.2.4 loads it cleanly (XRANGE/XINFO GROUPS/XPENDING intact); fr->fr round-trip also intact. The encoder was already correct; just feature-gated off + the round-trip tests needed the transient RdbStreamMetadata normalized away (strip_stream_metadata). fr-persist 190 + fr-runtime 541 pass. Residual: non-losslessly-encodable streams still fall back to type 15 (rare).

title: fr-server CLI defaults to hardened mode despite strict-mode contract
description: Review-mode finding: README and RuntimePolicy::default document strict as the default compatibility mode, but crates/fr-server/src/main.rs initializes mode_str to hardened and help text advertises hardened as default. This changes no-flag server startup semantics away from byte-exact Redis parity.
close_reason: Fixed fr-server no-flag startup to use strict by default and covered help-text default-mode documentation; validated with rch cargo fmt/check/test/clippy for fr-server.

title: Reconcile contradiction register with runtime persistence and replication integration
close_reason: Reconciled contradiction register with runtime persistence and replication integration

title: CLIENT HELP in fr-runtime returns short BulkString-based form, missing all per-subcommand description lines vs vendored 7.2.4
description:
Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 (16380):

  CLIENT HELP
    fr (RESP2)  : 18 BulkString lines, no descriptions
    ven (RESP2) : 51 SimpleString lines with per-subcommand description (CACHING, GETREDIR, GETNAME, ID, INFO, KILL <ip:port>, KILL <option> <value>..., LIST [options ...], UNPAUSE, PAUSE, REPLY, SETNAME, SETINFO with sub-bullets, UNBLOCK, TRACKING, TRACKINGINFO, NO-EVICT, NO-TOUCH, HELP)

Source: crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:10145 — emits BulkString-wrapped short form. The descriptive SimpleString form already exists in crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:15915 (cmd-table dispatch path). The fr-runtime path was added before the longer help block and never updated.

Fix: replace the fr-runtime CLIENT HELP block with the same SimpleString sequence used in fr-command, so both dispatch paths emit identical wire bytes. Field-by-field byte parity vs vendored 7.2.4 is the goal.
close_reason:
Replaced fr-runtime's stale 18-line BulkString-based CLIENT HELP block with the 54-line SimpleString descriptive form that matches upstream networking.c::clientCommand HELP path verbatim. Both dispatch paths (fr-command::client_cmd and fr-runtime's handle_client_command) now emit identical wire bytes.

Verified byte-for-byte parity vs vendored 7.2.4 (16380): diff CLIENT HELP returns no output, both emit 54 lines. Pinned via client_help_emits_simplestring_descriptive_form_per_upstream which asserts:
  - exactly 54 items
  - all SimpleString frames (addReplyStatusFormat parity)
  - header text exact match
  - CACHING description line at index 2
  - HELP description as the final line

888/888 fr-runtime+fr-command lib tests pass. Workspace clippy clean under -D warnings. Pre-existing conformance live-oracle failures (geo, scan, xtrim, scripting) unrelated to this bead.

title: [EPIC] INFO and CLIENT LIST Accuracy
description:
## Overview

Several INFO sections and CLIENT LIST output contain hardcoded or missing dynamic values. While some were fixed (hz, maxclients, executable, version), several remain.

## Remaining Issues

1. CLIENT LIST addr field: Always reports 127.0.0.1:0 instead of actual client peer address. Fixing this requires tracking peer addresses per-client in the server's session management.

2. CLIENT LIST laddr field: Partially fixed (port now dynamic) but IP is still hardcoded to 127.0.0.1.

3. INFO replication repl_backlog_size: Hardcoded to 1048576 in fr-command. Should read from runtime's actual backlog size.

4. XINFO STREAM radix-tree metrics: Hardcoded to 1/0. These require internal stream-node accounting that doesn't exist yet. Lower priority since the radix-tree is a Redis implementation detail, not a user-facing metric.

## Architecture for Client Addresses

To fix CLIENT LIST properly:
1. When accept_connections creates a new ClientConnection, store the peer addr from TcpStream::peer_addr()
2. Add a peer_addr field to ClientSession
3. In CLIENT LIST/INFO, read the stored peer addr
4. This also enables CLIENT KILL by addr filtering
close_reason: Epic acknowledged — children tasks now unblocked for implementation

title: Reject non-finite blocking timeouts consistently
close_reason: Completed

title: rv89 part 1: hash field TTL storage primitives (fr-store)
description:
First slice of br-frankenredis-rv89. Add per-field TTL storage to Store without changing Value::Hash or the hash-read paths:

- New Store field: `hash_field_expires: BTreeMap<(Vec<u8>, Vec<u8>), u64>` (abs-epoch-ms).
- New Store methods:
  - `hash_field_set_abs_expiry(key, field, expires_at_ms) -> HashFieldTtlSet` with NX/XX/GT/LT semantics.
  - `hash_field_ttl_ms(key, field, now_ms) -> HashFieldTtl` returning Missing/NoTtl/Remaining(ms)/Expired.
  - `hash_field_persist(key, field) -> HashFieldPersistResult`.
  - `drop_hash_field_if_expired(key, field, now_ms)` (internal).
  - `clear_hash_field_ttls_on_hdel` hook for existing HDEL sites (wired in part 3).
- Unit tests in fr-store tests/ for: set-get-persist roundtrip; NX/XX/GT/LT; expired-field detection; key-deletion removes all field TTLs.

No fr-command commands yet (part 2). No encoding / RDB changes yet (part 4). No command-integration (part 3).
close_reason: Landed in f700569: per-field TTL storage on Store with NX/XX/GT/LT-aware setter, unit-aware HTTL/HEXPIRETIME readers, HPERSIST semantics, and HDEL/whole-key cleanup hooks. 16 unit tests pass; fr-store overall 280/0. Downstream parts 2/3/4 (c0z9, b8ut, 3jrv) consume these primitives.

title: getkeys_flags_differ: cover COPY DB key extraction variants
description: Fallback cod-b quality bead because br ready is empty. COMMAND GETKEYS/GETKEYSANDFLAGS differential coverage currently probes COPY same-DB and COPY REPLACE only, but not COPY with DB <n> or DB <n> REPLACE. Add no-mock live-oracle probes for those cross-DB key extraction variants.

title: Accept legacy RDB_TYPE_LIST_QUICKLIST RESTORE payloads
