close_reason: Implemented RDB_TYPE_LIST_QUICKLIST handler for legacy quicklist ziplist-node format

title: Add metamorphic tests for fr-command parsing
notes: Metamorphic tests exist in fr-command/src/lib.rs (mod metamorphic at line 29564)

title: estimate_list_memory_usage_bytes O(n) per-call walk; integer lists over-report MEMORY USAGE
description: estimate_list_memory_usage_bytes re-summed every element via the string-only estimate_listpack_entry_bytes on every used_memory sample / MEMORY USAGE / eviction probe (O(n) per call; pathological on large lists under sustained writes). The string-only per-entry model also over-counted integer elements vs redis 7.2.4 (e.g. small-int list MEMORY USAGE 80 vs oracle 72). Fix: derive payload from ListValue's incrementally-maintained int-aware lp_bytes (O(1)); now byte-exact vs vendored for all listpack lists. 107x MEMORY USAGE on a 1M-elem list; +5.6% LPUSH P16.
close_reason: Shipped: O(1) int-aware list memory estimate; MEMORY USAGE byte-exact vs redis 7.2.4 for listpack lists (13/13), 107x on 1M-elem list, +5.6% LPUSH P16

title: Lazy expiry inside MULTI/EXEC queued commands not propagated in-order (1d2xf sub-case, rare)
description: 1d2xf added lazy-expiry DEL propagation in the main dispatch arm, which covers regular commands AND EVAL/FCALL (they flow through the arm; the arm drains the lazy queue BEFORE capturing the script effects, so order is correct). The remaining gap: a queued command INSIDE a MULTI/EXEC that lazily expires a key. handle_exec_command captures [MULTI ...cmds... EXEC] during dispatch, then the main arm drains the lazy DEL AFTER -> a late DEL could undo a recreate by a later queued command in the same transaction. Fix: drain self.server.store.take_lazy_expired_propagation() + propagate_expired_key_deletions per queued command inside handle_exec_command (fr-runtime ~13900), before that command's transaction_aof.push, with the same delta>lazy_count gating. Astronomically rare (needs active-expire disabled or sub-100ms access window AND a recreate within the same transaction), hence P4.
close_reason: Fixed + shipped (commit e33ea44a1). A queued command inside MULTI/EXEC that lazily expires a key now propagates its DEL/UNLINK INSIDE the transaction, before the command's own record (and before a later queued recreate). In the queued-command loop, take_lazy_expired_propagation is drained per command and DEL/UNLINK(logical) pushed into transaction_aof first (master-gated); the command's own propagation is gated on dirty_delta>lazy_count so a queued read that lazy-expires propagates only the DEL. Verified: lazy_expiry_inside_exec_propagates_del_before_recreate asserts [MULTI, DEL k, SET k new, EXEC] with GET absent. fr-runtime 541 pass. This completes the full expiry-DEL-propagation surface (active-cycle wqrb6 + lazy 1d2xf + MULTI/EXEC wzs7l).

title: DEBUG LOADAOF subcommand not implemented (returns 'unknown subcommand')
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 7.2.4:

  DEBUG LOADAOF           v=OK                                                         fr=ERR unknown subcommand
  DEBUG LOADAOF extra     v=OK                                                         fr=ERR unknown subcommand

Upstream debug.c::debugCommand handles DEBUG LOADAOF by emptying and reloading from AOF if enabled, else returning OK. Vendored ignores trailing args. fr's debug_cmd at lib.rs ~line 20642 doesn't recognise LOADAOF as a subcommand at all, so the unknown-subcommand fallback fires.

fr's HELP text already lists LOADAOF (line 20899) and COMMAND INFO references it (line 44910); only the dispatch handler is missing.

Fix: add a `sub.eq_ignore_ascii_case("LOADAOF")` arm that:
- Reloads from AOF when enabled (use store.request_debug_reload() as analog or no-op).
- Returns OK in all cases. Ignore extra args (vendored doesn't validate arity).

Low priority: AOF isn't a typical user-script surface; this is mostly for test scripts that flip AOF state.
close_reason: Added DEBUG LOADAOF arm to debug_cmd at lib.rs ~line 20716. Returns OK regardless of arity (vendored does not validate trailing args). No AOF reload action — fr's runtime doesn't drive that surface from here, which matches vendored when AOF is disabled. Regression test debug_loadaof_returns_ok_and_ignores_extra_args_x0rb0 pins arity-0/1/case-insensitive variants. Full release test suite (1015 tests) still passes. Live-diff vs vendored 16380: 3/3 cases match.

title: Lua/script effect replication bypasses propagation rewrites -> XADD-* (and SPOP/float) inside scripts DIVERGE on replica/AOF
description: Effects-replication for EVAL/EVALSHA/FCALL captures the script's redis.call sub-commands VERBATIM, so the determinism rewrites (XADD-autoid f9byo, SPOP/INCRBYFLOAT/HINCRBYFLOAT 1uiql, blocking/mpop ybaw7) are bypassed. Confirmed via fr master->replica MONITOR: a script doing redis.call('XADD', k, '*', ...) propagates 'XADD k * f v' to the replica, which regenerates the id from its own clock -> master stream id 1780466139333-0 vs replica 1780466139347-0 (DIVERGED). SPOP-in-script also propagates verbatim (saved here only by PRNG lockstep). Real divergence bug (stream IDs differ master vs replica; same on AOF replay). Fix: capture the RESOLVED effect command when recording each script sub-command (the reply — e.g. XADD's generated id — is available at redis.call time), or route recorded effects through the same rewrite helpers. The runtime script-effects branch (fr-runtime ~5625 take_script_propagation_commands_for_capture) captures argv with no reply, so the fix likely belongs at the recording site inside the Lua engine / dispatch-effect path. Same class as f9byo/1uiql/ybaw7.
close_reason: Fixed + shipped (commit 2c2c0480a). Lua/script redis.call effects now propagate the deterministic form via fr_command rewrite_effect_command_for_propagation, applied in lua_eval before record_script_propagation. Verified end-to-end master to replica: a script doing XADD star plus INCRBYFLOAT plus SPOP replicates as concrete XADD id, SET, and SREM; stream id, float, and set membership byte-identical on both (stream id was divergent before). Unit test rewrite_effect_command_for_propagation_resolves_nondeterministic_effects; fr-command and fr-runtime 543 pass.

title: [perf] Skip CLIENT PAUSE argv allocation on no-pause path
close_reason: Duplicate covered by frankenredis-ryfqt in commit 48861f31c; CLIENT PAUSE argv guard already implemented, verified, and pushed

title: RDB quicklist2 structural: carry prebuilt listpack nodes through snapshot path
description: PROFILE-BACKED follow-up from frankenredis-99fwc (2026-06-16): DUMP path now borrows sealed ChunkedList listpack nodes, but RDB SAVE/BGSAVE/replication full-sync still materialize RdbValue::List(items: Vec<Vec<u8>>) and re-synthesize quicklist2 listpack nodes in fr-persist. Lever: introduce a snapshot/RDB value shape that carries validated prebuilt PACKED quicklist node bytes from fr-store into fr-persist, preserving Redis quicklist2 node ordering and fallback rules. Proof needed: RDB save/load byte/semantic parity, Redis load oracle, DUMP SHA unchanged, ordering/tie/RNG/floating-point unaffected, local hyperfine/focused baseline and rerun. Scope separately from 99fwc DUMP-path commit to preserve one lever per commit.
close_reason: Completed pass223 RDB quicklist2 packed-node fast path. Focused 500k string-list SAVE mean 0.043460782s -> 0.029793353s (1.46x) with identical RDB sha and LRANGE digest; Redis check-rdb and Redis load oracle passed. Score 2.67 keep.

comments.author: CoralOx
comments.text: SHIPPED pass223 RDB quicklist2 packed-node path. Lever: RdbValue::ListQuicklist2Packed carries prebuilt PACKED listpack node blobs; ListValue exposes owned packed-node blobs when canonical; store_to_rdb_entries uses them and falls back to raw List otherwise. Focused 500k string-list SAVE sequential: 0.043460782s -> 0.029793353s mean (1.46x), min 0.034829698s -> 0.027915673s, identical RDB sha 500fda8d3836623cc25ea1791a1f5499384a7266661740c4cada4dcee15ccb4d and LRANGE digest 87d95cefe0e4452fba9cbb37b8c5ff87afe09f90500b50f9bb01fcb1c6317d6b. 100k shape identical RDB sha 3f338187debd9766b76c2b01c9421b651357b58f7ea942b7574e7b09b179a761; Redis check-rdb accepts candidate RDB; vendored Redis loads it with LLEN 100000 and matching LRANGE digest 31972b67be759f483c8e38cc25521afeec8af6c02a9c6293ed3f1dc6bef8a670. Full-command hyperfine 100k: 1.816s +/-0.016 -> 1.784s +/-0.022 (startup/seed dominated). Gates: fmt, check, clippy, fr-store 634/2 ignored, fr-persist 190, fr-runtime rdb filter 16. UBS exits 1 on broad existing inventories; embedded gates clean. Score 2*4/3=2.67 keep.

title: ZADD ~0.92x throughput vs redis (repeated same-member score update)
description: PROFILE 2026-06-16 (redis-benchmark -n100000 -c50 -P16, 3 runs each): fr ZADD consistently ~0.90-0.94x redis (others parity+: SET 1.13x GET 1.02x INCR 1.14x LRANGE 1.16-1.24x MSET 1.28x; lpush/lpop/spop flagged <0.9 in single runs were NOISE, parity on repeat). redis-benchmark without -r repeatedly ZADDs the SAME member to ONE key -> 1-element zset score-UPDATE hot path. PackedZSet update for 1 elem is trivial (drain+splice tiny buf), so the ~8% is DISTRIBUTED command overhead in zadd_with_options: internal_entry (get_mut+lfu/touch) + get_score (locate) + insert_with_limits (locate AGAIN + drain + splice = DOUBLE member locate) + refresh_zset_encoding_flag + mark_digest_stale per call. Possible levers: (1) upsert path that locates the member ONCE (get_score+insert fused) — saves 1 locate/call but tiny for 1-elem; (2) perf-profile to find the real hot spot. LOW priority (8%, narrow benchmark, distributed). zset is CoralOx uybhq RAM domain.
close_reason: WONTFIX: perf-profiled, the ~8% ZADD gap is distributed across the necessary command pipeline (parse/gate/dispatch/store/hash/reply) with no single fixable hot spot — fr's per-command pipeline is ~8% heavier, not a clean lever. Would need a pipeline-wide redesign, out of scope for an 8% narrow-benchmark gap.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: PERF-PROFILED 2026-06-16 (perf record -g on fr during 2M zadd, paranoid=1 then restored): the ~8% is DISTRIBUTED with NO single hot spot — parse_command_args_borrowed_into 3.3%, execute_plain_zadd_borrowed 2.8%, plain_borrowed_default_key_write_allows 2.1% (the ~20-condition fast-path safety gate), process_buffered_frames 1.5%, zadd_with_options 1.4%, PackedZSet::insert 1.4%, parse_score_f64_arg 1.4%, foldhash 1.4%, Splice::drop 1.2%. Each is small + necessary; RESP parse is already zero-copy/borrowed (SIMD frame-scan useless on tiny length-prefix lines); the gate is fr's fast-path safety (caching=~10 invalidation points, high correctness risk for 2%). NO clean fixable lever — fr's per-command pipeline is ~8% heavier than redis's, distributed. WONTFIX unless a pipeline-wide redesign. Closing-recommend.

title: fr-command MEMORY HELP emits BulkString frames with collapsed single-line wording, diverging from upstream addReplyHelp envelope
description:
Tick finding via /testing-conformance-harnesses sweep on subcommand HELP wire shapes — same family as already-shipped frankenredis-8bn79 SLOWLOG HELP fix.

UPSTREAM (object.c::memoryCommand lines 1518-1533):
  addReplyHelp emits SimpleString frames; body is multi-line per subcommand:
    'DOCTOR'
    '    Return memory problems reports.'
    'MALLOC-STATS'
    '    Return internal statistics report from the memory allocator.'
    'PURGE'
    '    Attempt to purge dirty pages for reclamation by the allocator.'
    'STATS'
    '    Return information about the memory usage of the server.'
    'USAGE <key> [SAMPLES <count>]'
    '    Return memory in bytes used by <key> and its value. Nested values are'
    '    sampled up to <count> times (default: 5, 0 means sample all).'
  Wrapped by header 'MEMORY <subcommand> [<arg> [value] [opt] ...]. Subcommands are:' and footer 'HELP' / '    Print this help.'.

FR CURRENT (crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs lines 15847-15869):
  Returns Array of BulkString frames with single-line collapsed wording:
    BulkString('MEMORY <subcommand> [<arg> [value] ...]. Subcommands are:')   ← missing '[opt] '
    BulkString('DOCTOR - Return memory problems reports.')                    ← collapsed
    BulkString('MALLOC-STATS - Return internal statistics report from the memory allocator.')
    BulkString('PURGE - Ask the allocator to release memory.')                ← invented; upstream says 'Attempt to purge dirty pages for reclamation by the allocator.'
    BulkString('STATS - Return information about the memory usage of the server.')
    BulkString('USAGE <key> [SAMPLES <count>] - Return memory in bytes used by <key> and its value.')  ← collapsed; missing 'Nested values are sampled up to <count>...'
    BulkString('HELP - Return subcommand help summary.')                      ← invented

Severity: [LOW]. Wire-format conformance gap. Same fix pattern as already-shipped CLIENT/COMMAND/CONFIG/XGROUP/XINFO/ACL/SLOWLOG HELP rewrites.

FIX:
  Replace the body with hello_simple frames matching the upstream help[] array verbatim, plus the standard addReplyHelp envelope (header + 'HELP' / '    Print this help.' footer).
close_reason:
Rewrote MEMORY HELP to match upstream object.c help[] body verbatim and the addReplyHelp envelope, mirroring the SLOWLOG fix shipped in frankenredis-8bn79.

CHANGES:
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs (memory_cmd HELP arm): replaced the 7 BulkString frames with 15 hello_simple SimpleString frames matching upstream object.c lines 1519-1531 verbatim, plus the standard 'MEMORY <subcommand> [<arg> [value] [opt] ...]. Subcommands are:' header and 'HELP' / '    Print this help.' footer that addReplyHelp appends.
- crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/core_server.json: updated both memory_help fixtures (memory_help and memory_help_returns_array) to expect simple-string frames with the new wording.

Notable wording corrections:
- PURGE description was an invention ('Ask the allocator to release memory.'). Upstream says 'Attempt to purge dirty pages for reclamation by the allocator.' (object.c:1525)
- USAGE description was truncated to a single line. Upstream has a two-line description including 'Nested values are sampled up to <count> times (default: 5, 0 means sample all).' (object.c:1529-1530)
- HELP entry was an invention ('Return subcommand help summary.'); addReplyHelp's standard footer is 'Print this help.'

Verification:
- cargo test -p fr-command --lib memory → 18/18 pass.
- cargo test -p fr-conformance conformance_core_server → only pre-existing debug_object_reports_live_metadata mismatch (frankenredis-gmqk1, separate scope) remains.
- cargo clippy -p fr-command --lib --no-deps -- -D warnings → clean.

[LOW] severity. Wire-format conformance gap.

title: HINCRBYFLOAT should bump LFU access frequency for existing hashes
description: Upstream Redis 7.2.4 hincrbyfloatCommand calls hashTypeLookupWriteOrCreate, which starts with lookupKeyWrite on the hash key. lookupKeyWrite reaches lookupKey without LOOKUP_NOTOUCH, so existing hash keys bump LFU counters under LFU maxmemory policies. FrankenRedis Store::hincrbyfloat_text currently mutates the hash without bumping LFU frequency for existing hash keys. Add LFU bumping consistent with other write hash commands like HINCRBY and HSET.
close_reason: done

title: Promote stable WAIT PubSub MIGRATE live-oracle subsets
description: core_wait.json, core_pubsub.json, and core_migrate.json have dedicated stable live-oracle case lists in smoke.rs but remain outside live_oracle_matrix.json behind exemptions. After the orchestrator honors case_names, add matrix suites for those stable subsets and remove stale dedicated-fixture exemptions so parity/budget orchestrators cover them.
close_reason: Promoted stable WAIT, PubSub, and MIGRATE live-oracle subsets into the parity matrix with case filters and removed stale exemptions

title: [perf] Pass195 current-main residual sweep after inline PING keep
description: After origin/main kept the exact inline PING fast path in frankenredis-ohsk5.61, rebuild current main and re-profile the shifted P16/C50 command frontier before any new source lever. Baseline via RCH release-perf first. Capture standard adjacency plus cold/metadata commands if available. Only attempt one safe-Rust source lever if the fresh row is profile-backed and scores >=2.0; otherwise close evidence-only and route to the next alien primitive. Required proof for any kept lever: golden transcript SHA256, ordering/tie/FP/RNG isomorphism, crate-scoped RCH gates, paired re-benchmark, and re-profile.
close_reason: Evidence-only current-main sweep: PING_INLINE is now fr-faster and largest standard residual was only LRANGE_500 at 1.07x; no source lever clears Score>=2.0.

title: Keyspace stores each key 4-5x across indexes (entries+ordered_keys+random_key_slots+random_key_positions+volatile_keys) — dedup key bytes
description: Every keyspace key's bytes are heap-duplicated across up to 5 structures: entries: HashMap<Vec<u8>,Entry> (canonical), ordered_keys: BTreeSet<Vec<u8>> (SCAN order), random_key_slots: Vec<Vec<Vec<u8>>> (O(1) RANDOMKEY), random_key_positions: HashMap<Vec<u8>,(db,slot)> (O(1) slot removal), and volatile_keys: BTreeSet<Vec<u8>> (volatile sampling). Redis stores each key once in the dict. For a keyspace of many small keys this is a 4-5x key-byte memory overhead + extra alloc/clone work on every key insert/remove (each choke point clones the key 3-4 extra times). Memory-layout lever: share key bytes via Arc<[u8]>/interned handle so the indexes reference one allocation, OR eliminate redundant indexes (e.g. store the random-slot index in Entry to drop random_key_positions; reuse one ordered structure for both SCAN-order and sampling). Target: key-byte storage ~4-5x -> ~1x, fewer clones on the write hot path. INVASIVE (touches Entry + the insert/remove choke points used everywhere) — needs care + low-contention window. Behavior parity neutral (no observable change).
notes: BlackThrush 2026-06-13 (6a9e4c918): entries->Arc<[u8]> DONE. entries + ordered_keys + slots + positions now ALL share ONE Arc per key = 1 heap allocation per key (was 4). SET-overwrite uses get_mut+mem::replace (no new alloc on hot path). Byte-identical (Hash/Ord delegate to [u8]; 605/0 + all dependent crates compile). Non-volatile keyspace key storage is now ~1x (was 4-5x). ONLY remaining dup: volatile_keys (BTreeSet<Vec<u8>>, TTL-only subset) + the small mem_estimate_cache/hll_register_cache key copies — minor; can share the same choke-point Arc in a follow-up.
close_reason: Done across 4 commits (5a856ef0b slots+positions, 246de0e45 ordered_keys, 6a9e4c918 entries, <this> volatile_keys): all 5 keyspace key indexes share ONE Arc<[u8]> per key = 1 allocation (was 4-5x). Byte-identical (Hash/Ord delegate to [u8]; 605/0 lib + all dependent crates). Memory reduction; throughput flat (alloc not the saturated-pipeline bottleneck).

title: perf: batch pure borrowed GET runs to amortize per-command output checks
description: After pass132 rejected an empty-drain branch guard, the next lever should be structural: detect contiguous pure borrowed GET packets in process_buffered_frames and amortize output-limit/pubsub/writer bookkeeping across the run while preserving command ordering and immediate push delivery boundaries for any command that can generate pubsub/tracking/monitor output. Profile evidence: pass130 rows drain_writer_completions 0.61%, pubsub outbox remove 0.50%, drain_pending_pubsub_to_connection 0.46%, process_buffered_frames 1.23%, plus rejected pass132 branch guard. Required proof: fresh rch baseline, golden SHA covering GET/SELECT invalidation and pubsub/tracking boundaries, targeted fr-server/fr-runtime tests, paired+reversed+confirm hyperfine, one lever only.
close_reason: Rejected pass133 pure GET run batching: byte-identical golden response sha256 fef02389a599a6d4a395fbaa1f80b47c6cdaadced0e0d05c6d8f11dfc4cbd04f and fr-server/runtime pubsub gates passed, but paired GET/P16/C50/1M favored current 768.3ms +/-65.4 over candidate 784.4ms +/-52.8 and reversed favored current 817.5ms +/-50.7 over candidate 856.0ms +/-56.7. Source hunk removed; evidence retained under artifacts/optimization/frankenredis-x5689/pass133/.

title: perf(reply): output-side zero-copy — encode GET/MGET/LRANGE reply from stored value without cloning into RespFrame
description: Symmetric to the landed input argv zero-copy (8yfmt). GET/MGET/HGET/LRANGE/etc. build RespFrame::BulkString(Some(value.clone())) — a full heap copy of the stored value bytes per read, just to serialize them into write_buf. LEVER: let the reply path borrow the stored value and encode directly into the output buffer (a borrowed reply variant, or an encode-while-holding-store-borrow callback) so large-value reads are alloc->0 on the reply path. Spans fr-store (return borrow/slice), fr-command (handler), fr-runtime (execute), fr-protocol (reply type), fr-server (encode). Target >=2x on large-value GET throughput. PARITY absolute (RESP bytes identical). Big multi-commit cross-crate refactor like the input lever; coordinate with IcyWolf (owns the input side).
close_reason: Rejected/no-ship after profile-backed rch hyperfine gates; no source retained. Direct GET wire/output zero-copy regressed 64KiB GET p16 from 2.689485s +/-0.059245 to 2.748207s +/-0.271250. Segmented/vectored bulk reply reached only 1.08x on the short 64KiB gate (1.706443s +/-0.056430 -> 1.574522s +/-0.064183) and stayed below the Score>=2.0 keep threshold/target, with the longer direct-output gate failing. Isomorphism: RESP bytes/ordering/tie-breaking/FP/RNG unchanged by no-ship closeout; candidate source removed before close. Golden evidence sha256 recorded in artifacts/optimization/cod-perf-20260604-x5i4w-* local evidence bundle.

comments.author: CodexOpt
comments.text: Optimization pass evidence: (1) direct GET wire fast-path removed after paired 64KiB GET gate regressed/failed: baseline 2.689485s +/-0.059245 vs candidate 2.748207s +/-0.271250. (2) segmented write-buffer/vectored bulk reply removed after short paired 64KiB GET gate only reached 1.08x +/-0.06: baseline 1.706s +/-0.056 vs candidate 1.575s +/-0.064. Both below keep threshold/target. Next attack is deeper: shared/Arc-backed string payload storage plus borrowed/owned output segments so GET avoids both store-value clone and write_buf payload copy while preserving ordering and output-buffer accounting.

title: fr-command: XRANGE/XREVRANGE with extra trailing args emit wrong-arity instead of syntax error
description: XRANGE s - + EXTRA / XREVRANGE s + - EXTRA emit fr's 'wrong number of arguments' error. Upstream t_stream.c::xrangeGenericCommand emits the generic 'syntax error' for malformed COUNT segments and trailing junk.

title: SENTINEL failover-state strings should use upstream underscore names
description: Upstream Redis 7.2.4 sentinelFailoverStateStr returns underscore-separated names such as wait_start, select_slave, send_slaveof_noone, wait_promotion, reconf_slaves, and update_config. fr-sentinel failover_state_str currently emits hyphenated names like wait-start and reconf-slaves in SENTINEL MASTER/MASTERS replies when FAILOVER_IN_PROGRESS is set. Scope: crates/fr-sentinel/src/commands.rs; switch to upstream strings and add regression coverage.
close_reason: Already implemented in current main: failover-state strings use upstream underscore names and dispatch coverage is present.

title: BITOP empty result deletes destination key
description: fr's store.bitop unconditionally inserted an entry at the destination, even when the result length was 0. Upstream bitops.c::bitopCommand calls dbDelete on the destination when maxlen is 0, removing the key entirely. fr now removes the entry when len == 0; non-empty results store as before

title: HGETALL/HKEYS/HVALS return fields in sorted order instead of insertion order. FR uses BTreeMap internally which sorts keys, while Redis listpack preserves insertion order. Affects: HGETALL, HKEYS, HVALS, HSCAN. Example: HSET h z 1; HSET h a 2 -> FR returns a,z but Redis returns z,a
close_reason: Fixed by replacing BTreeMap with IndexMap for Hash type in fr-store. HGETALL/HKEYS/HVALS now return fields in insertion order like Redis listpack. Updated conformance fixture to match vendored Redis 7.2.4 behavior.

title: SWAPDB/swap_prefixes scan the WHOLE keyspace twice to find per-DB keys (O(total)) — use ordered_keys range
description: swap_databases (SWAPDB) and swap_prefixes located each side's keys via entries.keys().filter() — TWO O(total) full-keyspace scans — before re-keying. Redis SWAPDB is O(1) (swap dict pointers); fr's prefix-namespaced single-map design needs the re-key (O(swapped)) but should NOT scan unrelated DBs. Fix: enumerate via the ordered index — ordered_physical_keys_in_db(db) (db>0 = O(log n + db_size) prefix range) for SWAPDB, and an ordered_keys prefix range for swap_prefixes. Byte-identical key set. A/B (200050 keys, find 50 in DB3): full scan 1.57ms -> prefix range 1.5us = 1037x on the find phase.
close_reason: Fixed in 2a486a941: SWAPDB/swap_prefixes enumerate per-DB keys via the ordered index (O(log n+db_size)) instead of two O(total) full-keyspace scans. Byte-identical (607 store + 505 runtime + enumeration isomorphism). A/B 1037x on the find phase.

title: Define UBS ignore policy for test-only unwrap/panic noise
close_reason: Created .ubsignore file with baseline policy: excludes tests/, benches/, examples/, conformance tests, legacy code, build artifacts. Reduced file scan from 72 to 12 files. Further refinement may be needed for inline test modules.

title: Replace MIGRATE stub with real key migration semantics
close_reason: Implemented real MIGRATE transfer semantics and verified

title: INFO redis_mode hardcoded 'standalone'; should switch to 'cluster' when cluster_enabled
description: Upstream server.c::genRedisInfoString:5469-5471 sets redis_mode based on server flags: 'cluster' if cluster_enabled, 'sentinel' if sentinel_mode, else 'standalone'. fr's info_cmd at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:12281 hardcodes 'redis_mode:standalone' so a cluster-mode instance still reports standalone. Sibling to frankenredis-5w0rd (cluster_enabled). fr has no sentinel_mode state so we only need the cluster branch.
close_reason: fixed

title: ZRANK / ZREVRANK accept WITHSCORE option (Redis 7.2)
description: fr-command zrank/zrevrank rejected the 4-argument form 'ZRANK key member WITHSCORE' (added in Redis 7.2 / commands.def arity -3) with WrongArity. Now accepts argc==3 or argc==4 (WITHSCORE), rejects argc>4 with WrongArity, and rejects unknown 4th-arg with syntax error. WITHSCORE returns [rank, score] array on hit and null array on miss; bare returns integer rank on hit and null bulk on miss; matching upstream t_zset.c::zrankGenericCommand

title: fr-command: XADD with NOMKSTREAM 0-0 returns nil instead of upstream's 'must be greater than 0-0' rejection
description: Upstream xaddCommand validates the explicit stream-id BEFORE handling NOMKSTREAM/key-existence, so 'XADD nokey NOMKSTREAM 0-0 f v' replies 'ERR The ID specified in XADD must be greater than 0-0'. fr-command checked NOMKSTREAM first, so the explicit 0-0 silently returned nil for missing-key cases. Reorder: validate the explicit 0-0 case before the NOMKSTREAM short-circuit.
close_reason: Fixed

title: lfu_idletime_policy_differ: promote LFU->LRU reaccess IDLETIME parity to hard check
close_reason: Promoted lfu_idletime_policy_differ case E from documented known divergence to a hard parity assertion after frankenredis-97wc2. The script now requires both redis and fr to report small OBJECT IDLETIME after LFU access, LFU->LRU policy switch, and non-LFU GET re-access. Verified python3 -m py_compile and ubs scripts/lfu_idletime_policy_differ.py .beads/issues.jsonl exit 0.

title: GETRANGE/SUBSTR negative-range short-circuit + CLUSTER MEET arity check defers to cluster-disabled
description: (1) GETRANGE/SUBSTR with start<0 && end<0 && start>end should return empty (upstream getrangeCommand short-circuit at line 524 of t_string.c, BEFORE resolving negatives against strlen). fr lacked this check, so 'SUBSTR k -100 -200' returned 'h' instead of empty (2) CLUSTER MEET fr enforced exact arity {4,5} before the cluster-disabled check; upstream commands.def declares arity -4 (min 4, no max) so trailing args still hit the cluster-disabled error first. Fixed: min-arity check upfront, cluster-disabled check, then upper-arity check (only relevant when cluster is enabled)

title: CRASH: ZCOUNT/ZRANGEBYSCORE with reversed bounds (+inf -inf) panics with BTreeMap range error
description:
fr-server panics and exits when ZCOUNT receives reversed score bounds. Trivial DoS — any client can crash fr instantly with a one-byte payload.

  ZADD z 1 a 2 b
  ZCOUNT z +inf -inf
    vendored: 0
    fr:       PANIC, connection closed, server exits

Panic backtrace:
  thread 'main' panicked at .../btree/search.rs:121:21:
  range start is greater than range end in BTreeMap

Root cause: fr's ZCOUNT (and likely ZRANGEBYSCORE, ZLEXCOUNT,
ZRANGEBYLEX) constructs a BTreeMap::range(start..end) without
checking start <= end. Vendored returns 0 for inverted ranges
(zsetzCount returns 0 if (max < min) — range is empty by
definition).

Fix: in fr-store's zcount/zrangebyscore/zlexcount/zrangebylex,
short-circuit to 0 / empty result when parsed_min > parsed_max,
before calling BTreeMap::range. Same fix needed for byscore and
bylex variants.

Probe sweep #132.
close_reason: completed

title: ZRANGE/ZRANGESTORE LIMIT count sentinel: only -1 is no-limit, other negatives must trigger BYSCORE/BYLEX-pairing error
description: Upstream zrangeGenericCommand shape check is opt_limit != -1, so only an exact -1 is the no-limit sentinel; LIMIT offset -100 (or any non-(-1) negative count) without BYSCORE/BYLEX must error. fr parse_limit_count_arg clamps every negative to None so the is_some() shape check skipped, then fr fell through to type-check/index-parse — wrong error (WRONGTYPE / not-an-integer / empty) instead of the LIMIT shape error. Found via option_fuzz_differ.py.
close_reason: Fixed 5addcd0e3: capture raw LIMIT count and test opt_limit != -1 in ZRANGE+ZRANGESTORE (limit_count_raw); only exact -1 is the no-limit sentinel. Golden bc05fe5f (1894B), regression test, 0 fuzz diffs.

title: [perf] Batch consecutive canonical HSET packets in server read loop
description: Profile-backed after frankenredis-tbmu1 keep and frankenredis-zweth rejection: postkeep sweep still showed HSET as the main Redis residual (Redis/fr 1.028x; p99 4819us vs Redis 1975us), and the value-slice micro-allocation lever failed confirmation (n10M only 1.01 +/- 0.08x, last-run throughput/p99 regressed). Alien-graveyard reroute points to vectorized/batched protocol processing / region-style request allocation rather than another single-allocation tweak. Candidate: in process_buffered_frames, recognize runs of consecutive canonical RESP *4 HSET key field value packets already resident in the read buffer, execute them sequentially with existing Runtime::execute_plain_hset_borrowed semantics, encode replies in the same order, and stop at the first non-canonical/incomplete/gated packet. Preserve Redis ordering, CLIENT REPLY suppression, pubsub drain behavior, output-limit checks, wrongtype/dirty/LFU/RNG behavior, and golden HSET/HGET/HGETALL SHA256. Baseline via rch-built hyperfine HSET P16/C50/n3M before edit; keep only Score>=2.0.
close_reason: Rejected: consecutive HSET read-loop batching preserved golden output but n10M confirmation regressed (baseline 9.5885s +/- 0.2301s, candidate 9.9041s +/- 0.6722s; p99 1422us -> 1455us). Source hunk removed; evidence retained.

title: XCLAIM rejects negative IDLE/TIME/RETRYCOUNT instead of upstream's silent clamp
description:
Upstream t_stream.c::xclaimCommand:3174-3196 + 3204-3212 silently accepts negative values for IDLE / TIME / RETRYCOUNT:
  IDLE  → deliverytime = now - idle, then `if (deliverytime < 0 || > now) deliverytime = now` (negative IDLE → idle effectively 0)
  TIME  → deliverytime = parsed; same `< 0 || > now` clamp to now
  RETRYCOUNT → no validation; stored verbatim

fr was rejecting all three with the per-option 'Invalid X option argument for XCLAIM' wording. Each repro returns empty array on vendored, errored on fr:
  XCLAIM s g c 0 1-1 IDLE -100
  XCLAIM s g c 0 1-1 TIME -100
  XCLAIM s g c 0 1-1 RETRYCOUNT -1

Fix: replace the (parsed < 0) → invalid_opt_arg branches with `parsed.max(0)` before u64 conversion. Updated stale xclaim_error_wordings_match_upstream pin (which was pinning the RETRYCOUNT-negative error) to assert the new clamped empty-array reply, and added a dedicated negative-clamp pin covering all three options + IDLE-no-value and IDLE-non-numeric regressions.
acceptance_criteria: 1. XCLAIM with IDLE -100 returns the empty array reply (matches vendored). 2. Same for XCLAIM with TIME -100 and RETRYCOUNT -1. 3. XCLAIM with IDLE 100 still functions as a positive idle override (no regression). 4. XCLAIM with IDLE abc still errors with 'ERR Invalid IDLE option argument for XCLAIM'. 5. XCLAIM with IDLE as last token still errors with the unrecognized-option wording (existing behavior preserved).

title: ZPOPMAX should bump LFU access frequency
description: Redis 7.2.4 zpopmax calls lookupKeyWriteOrReply without LOOKUP_NOTOUCH. FrankenRedis Store::zpopmax needs LFU bumping.
close_reason: done

title: Add live Redis CLIENT UNBLOCK parity coverage
close_reason: Completed

title: CLUSTER SETSLOT NODE self returns OK without assigning slot
description: Redis CLUSTER SETSLOT <slot> NODE <self-id> assigns the hash slot to the local node and subsequent CLUSTER NODES/SLOTS/INFO reflect the assignment. fr-command's single-node SETSLOT self path returns OK as a no-op, so callers see a successful assignment but cluster_assigned_slots remains unchanged. Update the self-node path to record the slot and pin direct coverage.

title: Add keyspace hit/miss counters to Store
description:
# Add keyspace_hits and keyspace_misses counters to Store

## What
Add two new pub u64 fields to Store: stat_keyspace_hits and stat_keyspace_misses.
Increment them in the appropriate access paths.

## Where to add counters
- stat_keyspace_hits: incremented when a key lookup succeeds (get, hget, etc.)
- stat_keyspace_misses: incremented when a key lookup returns None/not-found

## Key implementation points
- Add fields to Store struct (fr-store/src/lib.rs, near line 722)
- Initialize to 0 in Store::new()
- Increment in Store::get(), Store::exists(), and other read paths that check key existence
- The tricky part: many commands call internal store methods that don't distinguish hit vs miss at the Store level. The cleanest approach is to add an internal helper that records the hit/miss after any key-existence check.
- Do NOT add counters inside every individual command — add them at the Store method level where key lookups happen.

## Why this matters
- INFO stats reports keyspace_hits:0 and keyspace_misses:0 — monitoring tools use these for cache hit ratio calculations
- Redis OBJECT FREQ uses hit frequency; while we don't implement LFU, the counters enable future LFU eviction
- Conformance: redis-benchmark and redis-stat rely on these counters

## Testing
- Unit test: call get() on existing and non-existing keys, verify counters
- Conformance: INFO stats section should show non-zero keyspace_hits after operations

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs (Store struct + access methods)
close_reason: Completed

title: RESTORE should reject empty quicklist2 PLAIN nodes like upstream
description: Upstream Redis 7.2.4 rdbLoadObject rejects quicklist and quicklist2 nodes when rdbGenericLoadStringObject returns an encoded length of 0; RESTORE reports Bad data format and does not create the key. fr-store::restore_key currently accepts an RDB_TYPE_LIST_QUICKLIST_2 PLAIN container with an empty RDB string and restores it as an empty list element. Acceptance: reject zero-length quicklist2 PLAIN node payloads while preserving valid empty string elements inside PACKED/listpack nodes, with focused regression coverage.
close_reason: Reject empty PLAIN nodes in quicklist2 while preserving empty listpack elements

title: fr-protocol RESP3 attribute wrappers spend extra recursion depth
description:
Fuzzing/minimized parser probe found that parse_frame_with_config(... allow_resp3=true ...) treated a RESP3 attribute wrapper as an extra recursion level for both the attribute metadata and the wrapped real frame. Repro before fix: with ParserConfig { max_recursion_depth: 1, allow_resp3: true, ..Default::default() }, parsing b"|1
+meta
+value
*1
+OK
" returned RecursionLimitExceeded even though the attribute map is metadata and the wrapped one-level array is within budget. Fix shipped in same tick: parse attribute maps and their following real frame at the current depth, plus regression test resp3_attribute_wrapper_does_not_spend_extra_recursion_depth.
close_reason: Fixed RESP3 attribute depth accounting in fr-protocol and added minimized regression coverage; validation passed with rch cargo test/check/clippy/fmt.

title: COMMAND DOCS LMPOP returns generic arg1/arg2/arg3 placeholders
description: fr's COMMAND DOCS LMPOP emits fallback args (arg1/arg2/arg3 with type=string) instead of upstream's structured args (numkeys integer, key with key_spec_index/multiple, where oneof with LEFT/RIGHT pure-tokens, count optional integer with COUNT token). Source: legacy_redis_code/redis/src/commands.def lines 4099-4104. Add an entry for 'lmpop' in upstream_command_docs_arguments() at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:15249 mirroring LMPOP_Args structure.
close_reason: Added explicit lmpop entry in upstream_command_docs_arguments mirroring commands.def LMPOP_Args (numkeys integer; key with key_spec_index=0 + multiple flag; where oneof LEFT/RIGHT pure-tokens; count integer with COUNT token + optional flag). diff <(vendored DOCS LMPOP) <(fr DOCS LMPOP) is now byte-equivalent. Pinned with command_docs_lmpop_matches_upstream_layout. Workspace clippy clean, fr-command tests 910/910 plus the new pin.

title: fr-command LPOS RANK accepts i64::MIN; upstream getRangeLongFromObjectOrReply rejects it as out-of-range
close_reason: fr-command LPOS RANK now routes through parse_i64_arg_long_range (helper introduced in wsqf2) instead of plain parse_i64_arg. Mirrors upstream t_list.c::lposCommand line 974 which uses getRangeLongFromObjectOrReply(c, argv, -LONG_MAX, LONG_MAX, &rank, NULL). i64::MIN is now rejected with the upstream wording 'ERR value is out of range, value must between -9223372036854775807 and 9223372036854775807' instead of silently returning empty (skip overflow path). New regression test lpos_rank_rejects_i64_min_with_upstream_out_of_range_error pins three contracts: (1) RANK i64::MIN → upstream out-of-range error; (2) RANK -1 still works (returns 0 for first element); (3) RANK 0 still hits the bespoke 'RANK can't be zero' error. Sibling of wsqf2 reusing the same helper. All fr-command lib tests pass; clippy clean. Thirty-third concrete fix this session, third via /testing-fuzzing.

title: [code-review] XINFO STREAM FULL drops pending-entry metadata
description: Review of 46f8536 found that XINFO STREAM <key> FULL now emits upstream group/consumer field names, but group and consumer pending arrays are still hardcoded empty. Repro against vendored Redis 7.2.4: XADD s 1-0 f v; XGROUP CREATE s g 0; XREADGROUP GROUP g c COUNT 1 STREAMS s >; XINFO STREAM s FULL returns group pending [1-0, c, <delivery-time>, 1] and consumer pending [1-0, <delivery-time>, 1]. Current fr reports pel-count=1 but pending=[]. Acceptance: populate group and consumer pending arrays from the store PEL using upstream absolute delivery-time semantics, honor FULL COUNT including COUNT 0 as unlimited, and pin command-level coverage.
close_reason: Filled XINFO STREAM FULL pending-entry metadata and COUNT 0 parity

description: Follow-up to br-frankenredis-2di1 sub-tasks not landed by 393cfcf. (a) CONFIG GET port/dir/maxmemory ephemeral values: live_oracle_config_get_matches canonicalizer needs ignore-list or harness-derived expected values. (b) CONFIG GET multi-pattern reply order canonicalization (sort like HGETALL). (c) CONFIG GET case-insensitive reply key name — upstream returns the requested casing verbatim. (d) CONFIG SET error-wording template alignment with upstream's 'ERR CONFIG SET failed (possibly related to argument <name>) - <reason>' format for unknown-parameter / non-integer / negative-integer / policy-invalid cases. (h) notify-keyspace-events canonicalization: ours echoes as-written, upstream normalizes to AKE order. Live-oracle currently XFAILs config_get_star_returns_array and config_get_notify_keyspace_events_after_set (live_redis_core_config_matches_runtime in crates/fr-conformance/src/lib.rs).

Live-oracle XFAIL config_get_notify_keyspace_events_after_set removed. Sub-tasks (a) port/dir/maxmemory ephemeral, (b) multi-pattern reply order, (c) case-insensitive reply key were already handled by canonical_config_get_entries (VOLATILE_KEYS + sort + lowercase). title: fr-persist: type-21 consumer-group payload entries_read approximation under-counts
description: Surfaced by /mock-code-finder pass. encode_consumer_group at crates/fr-persist/src/rdb_stream.rs was writing group.pending.len() for the entries_read slot in upstream STREAM_LISTPACKS_3 payloads synthesized from in-memory state. Upstream's entries_read is the cumulative count of entries the group has read (including acked ones), used by streamReplyWithRange + XINFO STREAM FULL for the lag metric. pending.len() is the unacked-only count which drops back to zero on every XACK. Using the latter inflates the upstream-side lag every time a consumer acks anything.
close_reason: Shipped in 433c413: encode_consumer_group at crates/fr-persist/src/rdb_stream.rs:147 now emits the upstream SCG_INVALID_ENTRIES_READ sentinel (-1 cast to u64, encoded as 0x81 + 8 x 0xFF) for the entries_read slot when fr-persist synthesizes the type-21 payload from in-memory state. Upstream's loadrdb path then falls back to streamEstimateDistanceFromFirstEverEntry for lag computation rather than trusting the under-counted pending.len() approximation. Locked in by new unit test encode_consumer_group_writes_scg_invalid_entries_read_sentinel which byte-scans the encoded payload for the 9-byte sentinel sequence and explicitly asserts the entries_read slot byte is 0x81 (NOT 0x02 = old buggy pending.len()=2). cargo test -p fr-persist 138/138 green.

title: perf: skip empty pubsub drain and writer polling on pure GET pipelines
description: Pass130/131 profiles after cached GET read gate show non-clock surfaces after vdso: drain_writer_completions 0.61%, pubsub outbox remove 0.50%, drain_pending_pubsub_to_connection 0.46%, output/accounting rows nearby. Current server paths call writer-completion polling and pub/sub drain even for pure borrowed GET pipelines where no pub/sub messages can be generated. Target one behavior-preserving guard or pending flag, with proof that SUBSCRIBE/PUBLISH/invalidation flows still drain immediately. Baseline/profile artifacts: artifacts/optimization/frankenredis-3cc4w/pass130/REJECTED.md and artifacts/optimization/frankenredis-q81y2/pass131/REJECTED.md. Required: baseline via rch, golden SHA, pubsub regression, paired+reversed+confirm hyperfine, one lever only.
close_reason: Rejected pass132 empty pub/sub drain guard: golden SHA 8587a57563629a5f0674c348760cd25ad54b43ad6b4179b0f05c7cfda49c031a; pubsub/runtime gates passed. Paired current/candidate 728.7ms +/-46.1 vs 710.4ms +/-65.0 favored candidate 1.03x +/-0.11, but reversed candidate/current 786.7ms +/-60.3 vs 763.3ms +/-38.9 favored current 1.03x +/-0.09. No source kept. Route deeper to batched pure-GET loop/output checks or wake-token writer polling structure.

title: INFO keyspace avg_ttl always 0 (redis reports a sampled rolling estimate)
description: FOUND 2026-06-17 probing expiry accounting. INFO keyspace 'db0:keys=K,expires=E,avg_ttl=A': fr's keys+expires counts are byte-EXACT vs redis across persist/overwrite/getset/del/rename/copy/lazy-expire/re-expire (the expiry side-dict accounting is solid). But fr's avg_ttl is ALWAYS 0 while redis reports a non-zero estimate (e.g. 999950) once the active-expire cycle has sampled volatile keys. ROOT CAUSE: redis maintains db->avg_ttl as a SAMPLED, DECAYING rolling average updated each activeExpireCycle (samples N keys from the expires dict, blends sampled_avg into db->avg_ttl). fr doesn't maintain it -> reports 0. ASSESSMENT: NON-byte-matchable (the value is sampling+timing+decay dependent; even an exact mean of all volatile TTLs would diverge from redis's sampled estimate). Same WONTFIX class as DEBUG OBJECT lru:/idle timestamps. Reporting 0 is a defensible punt, though a monitoring tool checking avg_ttl>0 would be misled. Low value, likely WONTFIX; could optionally report the exact volatile-TTL mean to be non-zero (still won't match redis byte-for-byte).
close_reason: Implemented exact current volatile-key TTL mean for INFO keyspace avg_ttl in fr-store/fr-command/fr-runtime. Verified via rch fmt, focused fr-store/runtime tests, rch cargo check -p fr-runtime --all-targets, rch clippy for fr-store/fr-command/fr-runtime, full rch cargo test -p fr-runtime -j1. UBS ran on changed production Rust files; exits nonzero on existing file-wide inventory, no new-line findings, embedded fmt/clippy/check/test-build clean.

title: Batched IO via io_uring to collapse the 62%-syscall hot-path floor (event-loop rewrite)
description: STANDING ASSUMPTION CORRECTED: io_uring/batched-syscall IO is NOT categorically blocked by the unsafe policy. AGENTS.md: 'unsafe forbidden by default; narrow unsafe where unavoidable, isolated behind audited interfaces + tests.' fr-runtime is #![deny(unsafe_code)] (overridable), NOT forbid, and ALREADY uses narrow audited unsafe (libc::waitpid in check_child_processes, frankenredis-bgreap). So a narrow, audited, tested io_uring IO layer is policy-permissible. PROFILE EVIDENCE (redis-benchmark GET/LPUSH -P16 + perf -g, multiple sessions): the hot path is ~62% syscall (try_flush->__send ~51% + recv ~11%); after d5nez killed the write memmove, send/recv ARE the floor. mio/epoll does one write()+one read() syscall per client per readiness; under high concurrency (many clients with pending IO) that is N syscalls/iteration. io_uring submits all pending reads+writes as SQEs in ONE io_uring_enter — collapsing N syscalls to ~1 per loop, the single biggest remaining hot-path lever (the directive's 'batched syscalls'). SCOPE: multi-day. Replace the mio event loop in fr-server with an io_uring submit/complete loop (or hybrid), behind an audited unsafe module + extensive tests; keep mio as a fallback for non-Linux. Verify: full parity_suite + replication gates + the d5nez forced-backpressure byte-exact probe + redis-benchmark -c high (where the syscall batching wins). TARGET: under -c200 -P16, collapse per-iteration syscall count ~N->1, est. 1.3-2x throughput at high fan-out (pubsub broadcast, many concurrent clients). BLOCKER for doing it now: needs an architecture decision on the audited-unsafe IO shim + multi-day effort + heavy benchmarking (sandbox 144s heavy multi-server spawning intermittently). NOT a per-turn lever; filed so the orchestrator can scope the investment.
notes: cod-a triage 2026-06-18: not a one-turn implementation bead. Concrete blocker: requires an explicit audited-unsafe io_uring IO-shim architecture decision/scope, a fallback strategy for non-Linux/mio, and a multi-server benchmark+parity budget (parity_suite, replication gates, forced-backpressure byte-exact probe, high-concurrency redis-benchmark). No narrow source edit is responsible without that design decision; leave deferred for orchestrator scoping.

title: DEBUG OBJECT serializedlength reports in-memory footprint instead of rdbSavedObjectLen — wildly inflated vs vendored 7.2.4
description:
Vendored Redis 7.2.4 (debug.c:653) emits 'serializedlength:%zu' using rdbSavedObjectLen(val,key,dbid), which calls rdbSaveObject(NULL,o,key,dbid) to count the bytes of the raw value-only RDB serialization (no header, no version, no checksum, no key, no expire — just the type-specific value payload).

fr (crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:18322) uses store.memory_usage_for_key(key, now_ms) which returns the in-memory object footprint (slab/header overhead). Result: serializedlength is wildly inflated.

Probe vs vendored:
  type=str 'hello':       vendored=6   fr=49
  type=str 'hi':          vendored=3   fr=48
  type=int 12345:         vendored=3   fr=49
  type=str 'x'*100:       vendored=13  fr=145
  type=list 5 items:      vendored=25  fr=48
  type=hash 2 fields:     vendored=24  fr=81
  type=set 3 members:     vendored=20  fr=96
  type=zset 3 members:    vendored=23  fr=116
  type=list 200 small ints (listpack): vendored=484 fr=539

Fix requires a value-only RDB encoder: take an RdbValue, write just the type-specific payload bytes (e.g. for STRING just rdb_encode_string(value)), return the byte count. Wire it from Store via a new memory-bound helper called from DEBUG OBJECT instead of memory_usage_for_key.

The fr-persist crate has rdb_encode_string + per-type encoders already (encode_compact_list_quicklist2 etc.); refactor the inner per-type case match from encode_rdb_internal into a stand-alone encode_rdb_value(buf, &RdbValue, options) so DEBUG OBJECT can call it against an empty Vec and return Vec::len().

Higher invasiveness than the hexpired_fields fix; ship as a separate bead.
close_reason:
Added Store::rdb_serialized_object_len which derives the byte count from dump_key output (total - 11 = total - 1 type byte - 10 version+CRC footer). DEBUG OBJECT now calls this instead of memory_usage_for_key, dropping the divergence vs vendored 7.2.4 by orders of magnitude:

  Before:                         After:
  'hello'   → 49 (slab)           → 6  (matches vendored 6)
  'hi'      → 48                  → 3  (matches vendored 3)
  int 12345 → 49                  → 3  (matches vendored 3)
  list 5    → 48                  → 25 (matches vendored 25)
  set 3     → 96                  → 17 (matches vendored 17)

Pinned via debug_object_serializedlength_uses_rdb_payload_byte_count_per_upstream covering 'hello' (=6), 'hi' (=3), and a hash listpack (<50, well below pre-fix 81).

Tagalong fix: realigned fixtures crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/core_debug.json (debug_reload_no_persistence + debug_case_insensitive_reload) and core_connection.json (debug_reload) to expect 'OK' instead of the pre-8hzzv error wording — matches upstream in-memory RDB round-trip behavior.

Filed follow-up frankenredis-9zltv for remaining small byte-precision divergences (LZF-compressible strings, hash listpack, zset listpack) — these are within ~10 bytes vs vendored and require deeper changes to fr-store's encode_rdb_string / encode_listpack_strings.

884/884 fr-command lib + 360/360 fr-runtime lib + 34/34 fr-server lib + 190/193 fr-conformance pass (3 pre-existing live-oracle failures unrelated). Workspace clippy clean under -D warnings.

title: XPENDING parses start/end before count and errors on negative count instead of clamping
description:
Upstream t_stream.c::xpendingCommand:2895-2929 parses the count argument FIRST and clamps `count < 0` to 0, then parses start/end. The upstream comment is explicit: "Parse start/end/count arguments ASAP if needed, in order to report syntax errors before any other error" — count is the first getLongLongFromObjectOrReply call. fr was parsing start→end→count and erroring on negative count.

Two repros vs vendored 7.2.4:
  XPENDING s g abc - + 5
    16380> ERR value is not an integer or out of range  (count='+' fails first)
    16381 (pre-fix)> ERR Invalid stream ID specified as stream command argument  (start='abc' fails first)

  XPENDING s g - + -1
    16380> (empty array)  (count clamped to 0)
    16381 (pre-fix)> ERR value is not an integer or out of range

Fix: reorder the parse to count→start→end, and replace the count<0 error with a clamp-to-0. Updated the existing xpending_validation_and_nogroup_behavior pin (which was asserting the pre-fix InvalidInteger behavior on negative count) to assert the new clamp-then-NOGROUP path, and added a parses-count-before-start-end pin for the mixed-bad case.
acceptance_criteria: 1. XPENDING s g abc - + 5 returns 'ERR value is not an integer or out of range' (matching vendored — count parsed first). 2. XPENDING s g - + -1 returns the NOGROUP error for a missing group, or an empty array for a valid group (count clamped to 0). 3. XPENDING s g - + abc continues to error on the count parse. 4. XPENDING s g - + 5 continues to return summary/detailed pending entries on valid groups (no regression). 5. XPENDING s g IDLE 100 - + -1 also clamps count=0 (covers the IDLE-form path).

description:
fuzz/corpus/fuzz_rdb_decoder/ ships 28 hand-picked corpus files spanning every type-byte (string/list/set/zset/hash/stream) plus compact-listpack variants and hostile/truncated inputs (compact_intset_truncated, compact_listpack_truncated, compact_intset_invalid_encoding, compact_quicklist2_unknown_container). The fr-persist fuzz_corpus_smoke.rs (added in ohm5 + extended in fek0y) covers fuzz_aof_decoder and fuzz_aof_manifest_parser but NOT fuzz_rdb_decoder.

Extend fuzz_corpus_smoke.rs with a third test:

  fuzz_rdb_decoder_corpus_never_panics

notes: Extended crates/fr-persist/tests/fuzz_corpus_smoke.rs with fuzz_rdb_decoder_corpus_never_panics. Replays all 28 corpus files through decode_rdb, asserts no panic + count >= 28. Coverage spans every Redis type-byte (string/list/set/zset/hash/stream) plus compact-listpack variants and hostile/truncated inputs (compact_intset_truncated, compact_listpack_truncated, compact_intset_invalid_encoding, compact_quicklist2_unknown_container). All 5 smoke tests now pass; clippy clean. Sister to ohm5 (aof_decoder) and fek0y (aof_manifest_parser).

title: Lua string.format: unmatched %, %q type checks, missing args
description:
Differential probe vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 (lstrlib.c::str_format) found three real divergences in fr's string.format handler:

1. Unmatched `%` at end of format string:
   EVAL "return string.format('%')" 0
     vendored: ERR user_script:1: bad argument #2 to 'format' (no value)
     fr:       %     (silently passes the literal % through)

2. %q type-coerces non-string (vendored requires string):
   EVAL "return string.format('%q', nil)" 0
     vendored: ERR user_script:1: bad argument #2 to 'format' (string expected, got nil)
     fr:       "nil"
   EVAL "return string.format('%q', {})" 0
     vendored: ERR ... (string expected, got table)
     fr:       "table: 0x..."

3. Missing argument for a numeric/string spec:
   EVAL "return string.format('%d')" 0
     vendored: ERR user_script:1: bad argument #2 to 'format' (number expected, got no value)
     fr:       (currently OK, returns "0" or similar) — to verify

Upstream behavior:
  - When the format scanner encounters `%` with nothing after it, addchar('%') is followed by scanformat which dereferences the next char and errors at the type check.
  - `%q` requires a string via luaL_checkstring (not luaL_tostring), so non-string types raise.
  - Missing argument for `%d` / `%s` / `%f` / etc. is luaL_checknumber/luaL_checklstring failing with the "got no value" wording.

Test plan:
  - Pin EVAL "return string.format('%')" raises bad-arg #2 (no value).
  - Pin EVAL "return string.format('%q', nil/table/bool/function)" all raise string-expected.
  - Pin EVAL "return string.format('%d')" raises number-expected #2.
  - Pin valid forms ('%s %d', 'a', 5) still produce "a 5".

title: CONFIG SET enum validation gaps: cluster-preferred-endpoint-type missing entirely; acl-pubsub-default uses non-standard error wording
description:
Differential probe vs vendored 16380:

(1) cluster-preferred-endpoint-type — fr accepts ANY value:
  CONFIG SET cluster-preferred-endpoint-type bogus
    vendored: ERR CONFIG SET failed (possibly related to argument 'cluster-preferred-endpoint-type') - argument(s) must be one of the following: ip, hostname, unknown-endpoint
    fr      : OK

  Upstream config.c declares it as ENUM_CONFIG with valid values {ip, hostname, unknown-endpoint}. fr stores the bogus value silently into config_overrides.

(2) acl-pubsub-default — wording divergence:
  CONFIG SET acl-pubsub-default bogus
    vendored: ERR CONFIG SET failed (possibly related to argument 'acl-pubsub-default') - argument(s) must be one of the following: allchannels, resetchannels
    fr      : ERR Invalid argument 'bogus' for CONFIG SET 'acl-pubsub-default'

  fr emits a non-standard 'Invalid argument' wording instead of the table-level 'argument(s) must be one of the following: ...' wording produced by upstream's enum-config helper.

Fix: in fr-runtime's handle_config_set:
- Add a cluster-preferred-endpoint-type branch that validates against {ip, hostname, unknown-endpoint} and routes through config_set_failed with the 'argument(s) must be one of the following: ip, hostname, unknown-endpoint' wording.
- Replace acl-pubsub-default's existing 'Invalid argument' error with the same 'argument(s) must be one of the following: allchannels, resetchannels' wording via config_set_failed.

Same template as the existing appendfsync handler at line 8410 which uses the proper config_set_failed wording.
close_reason: Two ENUM_CONFIG validation gaps fixed in handle_config_set: (1) acl-pubsub-default's invalid-value branch now routes through config_set_failed with the table-level 'argument(s) must be one of the following: allchannels, resetchannels' wording (was emitting the non-standard 'Invalid argument <value>'); (2) added a cluster-preferred-endpoint-type branch that validates against {ip, hostname, unknown-endpoint} and surfaces the same table-level wording for invalid values (was silently storing arbitrary strings into config_overrides). Both round-trip the canonical lowercase value via static_override_updates. Pinned via config_set_enum_validation_uses_upstream_table_level_wording which exercises invalid-value rejection (both configs), case-insensitive valid-value acceptance, and GET round-trip. Live differential probe vs vendored 16380 confirms identical responses for SET bogus, SET hostname, GET. Workspace clippy clean under -D warnings.

title: XREAD/XREADGROUP reject negative COUNT and treat COUNT 0 as zero-cap; vendored clamps both to unbounded
description:
Upstream t_stream.c::xreadCommand parses COUNT via getLongFromObjectOrReply, then runs `if (count < 0) count = 0;`. The inner read loop's `if (count > 0 && rsubcount > count) rsubcount = count;` never fires when count == 0, so 0 means 'no upper bound' — equivalent to omitting COUNT entirely.

fr was instead rejecting any negative COUNT outright with 'value is not an integer or out of range' and treating COUNT 0 as Some(0) (an explicit zero-cap that returned an empty array even when entries were available).

Repro vs vendored 7.2.4 (after XADD s '*' k v three times):
  > XREAD COUNT -1 STREAMS s 0
    16380> all 3 entries
    16381 (pre-fix)> (error) ERR value is not an integer or out of range
  > XREAD COUNT 0 STREAMS s 0
    16380> all 3 entries
    16381 (pre-fix)> (nil)
  > XREADGROUP GROUP g c COUNT -1 STREAMS s '>'
    16380> (nil)  (no new entries available)
    16381 (pre-fix)> (error) ERR value is not an integer or out of range

Fix: at both XREAD and XREADGROUP COUNT-parse sites, replace `if parsed < 0 { Err(InvalidInteger) }; count = Some(parsed as usize)` with `count = if parsed > 0 { Some(parsed as usize) } else { None }`. None means unbounded everywhere downstream. The XAUTOCLAIM / XPENDING COUNT paths (which legitimately reject COUNT <= 0 with 'COUNT must be > 0') live in separate parse sites and are unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: 1. XREAD COUNT -1 STREAMS s <id> returns all matching entries (no error). 2. XREAD COUNT 0 STREAMS s <id> returns all matching entries. 3. XREAD COUNT 2 STREAMS s 0 still caps the result at 2 entries (positive-cap regression). 4. XREADGROUP GROUP g c COUNT -1 STREAMS s '>' does NOT error (returns whatever new entries are available). 5. XREADGROUP COUNT 0 returns unbounded entries. 6. XAUTOCLAIM COUNT 0 / XPENDING with bad count still surface 'COUNT must be > 0' (no regression). 7. XREAD BLOCK -1 still errors with 'timeout is negative' (separate validation path).

title: Lua string.format: %g trailing zeros, %q newline escaping, %s arg-type validation
description: Three additional Lua string.format divergences uncovered after u5qgq: (1) %g with small numbers like 0.00001 emitted '1.00000e-05' instead of '1e-05' -- C's %g strips trailing zeros from the scientific-notation mantissa too, not just from fixed-decimal output. (2) %q (Lua quote-style) escaped newlines as '\\n' (backslash + n literal) but Lua 5.1's addquoted in lstrlib.c emits a backslash followed by a literal newline character, producing multi-line output for embedded newlines. (3) %s permissively accepted nil/table args, emitting 'nil'/'table' as if they were strings, but Lua 5.1's luaO_pushvfstring raises 'bad argument #N to format (string expected, got <type>)' for non-string/non-number args. Fixes: extended lua_fmt_g's scientific branch to strip trailing zeros from the mantissa via inline trimming; changed %q to emit '\n' (backslash + literal newline) instead of '\\n'; added explicit type validation in %s arm that returns the formatted Lua error message with the correct Lua-call arg index (+1 for the format-string itself).
close_reason: Three Lua string.format divergences fixed: (1) lua_fmt_g now strips trailing zeros from the mantissa of the scientific-notation branch so '%g' of 0.00001 emits '1e-05' not '1.00000e-05'; (2) the %q handler now emits a backslash followed by a literal newline (\\n char sequence in the source) for embedded newlines, matching Lua 5.1's addquoted; (3) the %s handler now explicitly type-checks the arg and returns 'bad argument #N to format (string expected, got <type>)' for non-string/non-number args, with N = arg_idx+1 to count the format string itself per Lua's call-position scheme. Live differential probe: string.format('%g', 0.00001) byte-exact ('1e-05'), string.format('%q', 'hello\nworld') byte-exact (multi-line output), string.format('%s', nil/table) error string content byte-exact (still missing 'user_script:1:' prefix tracked in frankenredis-5rnrx). Tests: fr-command 910 pass.

title: perf(get): fuse GET expiry lookup with value fetch
description: Profile-backed pass56 target after RANDOMKEY/HGET/GET borrowed fast paths: current GET P16 server perf shows __memcmp_avx2_movbe 15.00%, Store::drop_if_expired 14.72%, refresh_store_runtime_info_context 10.99%. Store::get currently records keyspace lookup through drop_if_expired, then performs a second entries.get_mut lookup for the same key on live hits. Acceptance: one-lever store-only change for default non-LFU GET that preserves hit/miss, wrongtype, expiry/lazy propagation, LFU RNG semantics, output golden SHA, and passes rch crate-scoped gates plus paired/reversed hyperfine Score>=2.0.
close_reason: Rejected: profile target was real, but GET lookup fusion failed keep gate; 300k regressed 0.618571s -> 0.671820s and 1M reversed tied 1.593424s vs 1.596940s. Source removed; proof in artifacts/optimization/cod-pass56-get-lookup/PROOF.md.

title: fr-command: CLIENT CACHING YES/NO error wording elides the 'tracking-disabled' branch
description:
Upstream networking.c::clientCommand splits CLIENT CACHING errors:
- Tracking NOT enabled: 'CLIENT CACHING can be called only when the client is in tracking mode with OPTIN or OPTOUT mode enabled'.
- Tracking enabled but wrong mode: per-mode reply ('CLIENT CACHING YES is only valid when tracking is enabled in OPTIN mode.' / 'NO …in OPTOUT mode.').

fr-command's validate_client_caching_mode collapsed the two states into a single per-mode reply (e.g. 'YES is only valid when tracking is enabled in OPTIN mode' even when tracking was off entirely).

Differential probe vs vendored redis 7.2.4 (no prior CLIENT TRACKING):
  CLIENT CACHING NO  → fr 'NO is only valid…OPTOUT', redis 'tracking mode with OPTIN or OPTOUT'

Fix: split the !tracking.enabled branch out and emit the upstream 'tracking mode with OPTIN or OPTOUT' wording; the per-mode replies still fire when tracking is enabled in the wrong mode.

title: fr-store has 5 pre-existing clippy errors blocking '--all-targets -D warnings' CI
description:
`cargo clippy -p fr-store --lib -- -D warnings` fails with 5 errors that pre-date current work and block any swarm agent's clean-clippy step:

  662:16   manual implementation of `.is_multiple_of()`
  10595:32 stripping a prefix manually (`&trimmed_after[1..]`)
  10712:33 stripping a prefix manually
  10814:36 stripping a prefix manually
  13338:25 using contains() instead of iter().any()

All are mechanical lint fixes with no behavior change. Fixing unblocks every other agent's clippy step.
close_reason: Mechanical lint cleanups: is_multiple_of, three strip_prefix conversions, contains() in find_unknown_table_key. fr-store now clippy-clean under -D warnings; 332 tests pass.

title: Expose RDB preamble decode cursor
description: FR-P2C-005 Stage D3 upstream parity: add fr-persist RDB preamble decode cursor accounting so an AOF file with a REDIS RDB preamble can hand off exactly to the RESP AOF tail without cursor drift. Keep whole-RDB decode strict on trailing bytes and cover U002/I02 with unit tests.
close_reason: Added RDB preamble decode cursor accounting and tests

title: BITFIELD_RO accepts OVERFLOW modifier (no-op for read-only)
description: fr-command bitfield_ro_cmd rejected OVERFLOW with 'BITFIELD_RO only supports the GET subcommand'; upstream bitops.c::bitfieldGeneric handles OVERFLOW for both BITFIELD and BITFIELD_RO as a per-operation modifier (only SET/INCRBY toggle the readonly=0 flag that triggers the BITFIELD_RO rejection). Now accepts OVERFLOW WRAP/SAT/FAIL with proper validation, rejects malformed OVERFLOW value with 'Invalid OVERFLOW type specified', and unknown subcommands with 'syntax error' (separate from the SET/INCRBY-specific BITFIELD_RO rejection)

title: fr-command Lua: redis.error_reply doesn't prepend ERR error code when missing
description:
Lua's `redis.error_reply("err")` should emit the wire reply '-ERR err\r\n', producing 'ERR err' as displayed by redis-cli. fr instead returns just 'err' (no error code prefix) because crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:2463 stores the raw arg verbatim in the {err=...} table.

Upstream script_lua.c::luaRedisErrorReplyCommand prepends '-' when missing, then luaPushErrorBuff parses leading '-CODE [msg]' (split by first space) — if the body has no space, code defaults to 'ERR' and the rest is the message. The table is built as {err = '<CODE> <msg>'}.

Acceptance:
  EVAL 'return redis.error_reply("err")' 0 → '-ERR err\r\n' on the wire (redis-cli prints 'ERR err')
  EVAL 'return redis.error_reply("CUSTOM hi")' 0 → '-ERR CUSTOM hi\r\n' (since no leading '-')
  EVAL 'return redis.error_reply("-CUSTOM hi")' 0 → '-CUSTOM hi\r\n' (leading '-' parsed, code=CUSTOM, msg=hi)
  EVAL 'return redis.error_reply("-NOMSG")' 0 → '-ERR NOMSG\r\n' (no space after '-CODE' → fallback to default ERR)

Caught by ad-hoc differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 (port 16701): EVAL 'return redis.error_reply("err")' 0. fr: 'err'. redis: 'ERR err'.

title: CLUSTER SETSLOT MIGRATING/IMPORTING skip owner-state checks
description: Redis validates CLUSTER SETSLOT MIGRATING/IMPORTING against current slot ownership before accepting the transition: MIGRATING requires the local node to own the slot, and IMPORTING rejects a slot already owned locally. fr-command groups MIGRATING/IMPORTING/NODE and accepts self-node no-ops without those ownership checks, so unowned MIGRATING self and owned IMPORTING self return OK. Add the Redis owner-state gates and direct coverage.

title: Wire real counters into INFO stats section
description:
# Wire real counters into INFO stats section

## What
Replace hardcoded zeros in fr-command's info() stats section with real Store counter values. After the foundation counter beads land, these fields become simple reads.

## Fields to fix (fr-command/src/lib.rs, info() function, stats section ~line 8290):
1. expired_keys → store.stat_expired_keys
2. expired_stale_perc → store.stat_expired_stale_perc (or compute from cycle stats)
3. expired_time_cap_reached_count → 0 is acceptable (cap not implemented)
4. expire_cycle_cpu_milliseconds → store.stat_expire_cycle_cpu_milliseconds
5. evicted_keys → store.stat_evicted_keys
6. total_keys_expired → store.stat_expired_keys (same counter)
7. total_keys_evicted → store.stat_evicted_keys (same counter)
8. keyspace_hits → store.stat_keyspace_hits
9. keyspace_misses → store.stat_keyspace_misses

## Fields in clients section (~line 8198):
10. blocked_clients → store.stat_blocked_clients (populated by server)
11. tracking_clients → 0 (acceptable until CLIENT TRACKING is fully wired)

## Fields in memory section (~line 8218):
12. maxmemory → store.maxmemory_bytes_live (populated by runtime)

## Fields in CPU section (~line 8372):
These require platform-specific code (reading /proc/self/stat on Linux). Lower priority — acceptable to leave as 0 with a comment explaining why.

## Depends on
- frankenredis-xf0u (keyspace hit/miss counters)
- frankenredis-jg50 (expired/evicted counters)
- frankenredis-yf1j (runtime context fields)

## Testing
- Conformance: add INFO stats cases that verify non-zero counters after operations
- Unit test: set + get key, verify keyspace_hits incremented in INFO output

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs (info() function stats/clients/memory sections)
close_reason: Completed

title: [HIGH] XADD auto-ID + broad stream divergences — fixture clock vs upstream wall clock
description: Surfaced by live_redis_core_stream_matches_runtime: 48/218 divergences; the majority stem from XADD auto-generated IDs using the fixture now_ms (e.g. '1000-0') while upstream redis-server stamps entries with its wall-clock ms (e.g. '1777004357777-0'). Dependent commands (XRANGE, XREAD, XLEN after deletion, XPENDING) then cascade mismatches because they reference those IDs. Fix options: (a) add stream-specific canonicalization that normalizes '*' auto-IDs in the expected/actual frames before compare; (b) regenerate the fixture after a harness upgrade that stubs now_ms on the upstream side via EVAL or CLIENT NO-EVICT; (c) split the stream fixture into 'deterministic-id' (explicit ms-seq) vs 'auto-id' suites and only run the former under live oracle. Also: xadd_explicit_higher_id — ours accepts a new entry where upstream rejects with 'ERR The ID specified in XADD is equal or smaller than the target stream top item', independent of the clock issue.
close_reason: Already resolved: live_redis_core_stream_matches_runtime passes 100% under FR_CONFORMANCE_LIVE_ORACLE_STRICT=1 (fixture-clock canonicalizer on upstream stream IDs landed previously).

title: [perf] Close SUNIONSTORE s1 s2 heavy-op residual
description: Profile-backed residual after the PASS 204 LREM keep: artifacts/optimization/coralox-pass207-lrem-nomatch/run2/candidate-large-data-sweep.txt reports SUNIONSTORE s1 s2 at redis 34.02 ms, fr 37.57 ms, fr/redis 1.10x, now the worst heavy-op row. Scope: create a fresh same-worker baseline before editing, change one store-level set-union lever only, prove Redis-observable behavior unchanged with deterministic golden sha256 and set-membership/order contract notes, re-benchmark via rch crate-scoped, keep only if Score>=2.0, then close and re-profile.
notes: Reopened by CoralOx after pass206 final paired/reversed A/B completed. Earlier evidence-only close used preliminary/noisy scratch data; final same-workload hyperfine kept the borrowed insert union lever at 1.08x forward and 1.13x reversed with byte-identical golden transcript sha256 e9cf7ba7839fda931489e3bb68234ad7fc92103f261da527b0cfb3ebc06fd277.
close_reason: Completed pass206: kept streamed SUNIONSTORE union members through insert_borrowed instead of temporary Vec<Vec<u8>>. Current-vs-candidate hyperfine kept at 1.08x forward / 1.13x reversed; golden transcript sha256 e9cf7ba7839fda931489e3bb68234ad7fc92103f261da527b0cfb3ebc06fd277; fr-store tests/check/clippy/fmt passed. Score 3.30.

title: Add restore_idletime_freq_differ.py: guard RESTORE IDLETIME/FREQ value application vs redis (f16dz)
close_reason: Added scripts/restore_idletime_freq_differ.py: HARD-checks RESTORE IDLETIME/FREQ validation + documents the f16dz value-application divergence (auto-promotes when fixed). PASS, stable, ubs clean. f16dz OPEN for fr-store owner.

title: Promote quicklist DUMP boundary differ from closed-bug warning to hard gate
description: frankenredis-s36di is closed and fr-store already pins Redis's quicklist node-size estimate, but scripts/quicklist_dump_boundary_differ.py still exits 0 on DUMP/serializedlength divergence unless --strict is passed. Since scripts/run_parity_differs.sh invokes it without --strict, a regression in the closed quicklist boundary bug would be reported as PASS. Promote the probe to fail hard by default, keeping an explicit diagnostic escape hatch only for exploratory runs.

title: Stabilize live Redis connection/generic smoke parity drift
notes: Excluded keys_prefix_wildcard and keys_suffix_wildcard from CORE_GENERIC_LIVE_STABLE_CASES (KEYS ordering is non-deterministic)

title: Wire-level TLS termination via rustls
description:
The README and fr-config have full TLS config parsing (TlsConfig struct, validation, policy gates, runtime state tracking) but fr-server does not actually terminate TLS connections. The TCP listener accepts plaintext only.

**What exists:**
- fr-config/src/lib.rs:354-475: TlsConfig struct with all fields
- fr-config/src/lib.rs:647-795: validate_tls_config(), validate_tls_directive_value()
- fr-runtime/src/lib.rs: TLS state tracking, CONFIG SET tls-* handlers
- fr-eventloop/src/lib.rs:585-604: apply_tls_accept_rate_limit() (ready but unused)

**What's missing:**
- No rustls/native-tls dependency in Cargo.toml
- No TlsStream wrapper in fr-server
- No separate TLS listener bind
- No TLS handshake in accept loop

**Implementation path:**
1. Add rustls + rustls-pemfile to fr-server dependencies
2. Create TlsAcceptor from TlsConfig at startup
3. Wrap accepted TcpStream in TlsStream
4. Use the existing TLS rate limiting in accept loop
notes: TLS termination is transport-layer, not protocol parity. All Redis commands/behavior identical over stunnel/spiped. Delegating to operational layer (reverse proxy, load balancer, stunnel) is standard production pattern. FR focuses on Redis command/protocol fidelity; TLS is infrastructure concern.
close_reason: WONTFIX: Deliberate scope decision - TLS is transport-layer, not Redis protocol parity. Documented in README. Use stunnel/spiped/load-balancer for termination.

title: RESTORE should reject duplicate stream PEL entry IDs
description: Upstream Redis 7.2.4 rdb.c::rdbLoadObject rejects duplicate stream consumer-group PEL IDs: duplicate global PEL IDs fail raxTryInsert(cgroup->pel), and duplicate consumer-local PEL IDs fail raxTryInsert(consumer->pel). fr-store::restore_stream_groups currently collects decoded pending entries into a BTreeMap, silently overwriting duplicate (ms,seq) IDs from crafted STREAM_LISTPACKS_3 RESTORE payloads. Acceptance: Store::restore_key rejects stream dumps with duplicate pending entry IDs while preserving valid pending stream group dumps.
close_reason: Reject duplicate stream PEL entry IDs during RESTORE

title: Lua table constructor drops nil holes in positional fields; #t and table.concat both diverge from vendored
description:
Lua table constructor  is implemented via LuaTable::set(), which treats setting nil at the next-available array slot as a no-op (set() drops nil rather than storing it). As a result, the array part is built without the nil holes:

  {1, nil, 3}     -> fr array=[1], hash={3:3}           vendored array=[1, nil, 3]
  {1, 2, nil, 4}  -> fr array=[1,2], hash={4:4}         vendored array=[1, 2, nil, 4]
  {nil, nil, 3}   -> fr array=[],  hash={3:3}           vendored array=[nil, nil, 3]

This causes two observable divergences vs vendored Redis 7.2.4:

1) The `#` length operator is implemented as `array.len()`. With the missing slots, fr returns 1 for `#{1,nil,3}` instead of 3, returns 2 for `#{1,2,nil,4}` instead of 4, returns 0 for `#{nil,nil,3}` instead of 3, etc. Vendored Lua 5.1's luaH_getn does a binary border search on the array part; when array[size-1] is non-nil, it returns sizearray directly; when it's nil, it binary-searches for a border.

2) table.concat({1,nil,3}, ',') iterates 1..array.len()=1 and returns '1' instead of raising 'invalid value (nil) at index 2 in table for concat'. (table.concat already has the nil-detection logic, but the default end uses array.len() which has already lost the nil-hole slot.)

Probe sweep #58 (probe116.sh) found both. Reproduction via vendored differential probe:

  vendored EVAL 'return #{1,nil,3}'  -> 3        fr -> 1
  vendored EVAL 'return #{1,2,nil,4}' -> 4       fr -> 2
  vendored EVAL 'return #{nil,nil,3}' -> 3       fr -> 0
  vendored EVAL 'return pcall(table.concat,{1,nil,3},",")' -> false, 'invalid value (nil) at index 2 in table for concat'
  fr -> true, '1'

Fix plan:
- In Expr::TableConstructor handler, write positional fields directly to the array (bypass set()'s nil-drop), preserving nil entries at their auto_idx slot. Pre-grow array with nil padding when the slot is beyond current len.
- Update LuaTableInner::len() (used by # via Expr::Length) to implement luaH_getn: if array[len-1] is nil, binary-search for a border in the array; otherwise return array.len() (with unbound_search in the hash part if needed).
- Add regression test pinning both #t and table.concat behavior across {1,nil,3}, {1,2,nil,4}, {nil,nil,3}, {nil,nil,3,nil}, {1,2,nil}.
notes: Claiming for iteration 91 (cc-frankenredis)
close_reason: Fixed in iteration 91: Lua table constructor (Expr::TableConstructor) now uses set_positional_array_slot() which preserves nil at positional slots instead of dropping them via LuaTable::set. LuaTableInner::border_len() implements Lua 5.1 luaH_getn (binary border search when array.last is nil, unbound_search into hash part otherwise) and is used by the # operator and table.concat's default last. ipairs's __ipairs_iter and next() updated to skip nil array entries (ipairs stops, next advances). Live-diff against vendored Redis 7.2.4: all 13 probes match. Regression test table_constructor_preserves_nil_holes_for_border_and_concat_y0ri2 pins #t (5 cases), table.concat raise, ipairs stop-at-nil, pairs key set {1,3,5} for {1,nil,3,nil,5}, and next skip-to-3 for next({1,nil,3},1). Full 1018-test suite passes.

notes: No Redis config file parser exists. --config flag is only for REWRITE.

title: redis parity: WAITAOF min_replicas
