title: Investigate severe throughput gap vs Redis baselines
description:
# Severe Performance Gap Against Redis Baseline

## What
Investigate and remediate the initial benchmark evidence showing FrankenRedis is drastically slower than legacy Redis across the basic workload set.

## Evidence
From `baselines/` captured on 2026-04-07:

- `SET`: FrankenRedis `1240.93 ops/sec` vs Redis `94402.47 ops/sec` (`1.31%` of Redis throughput)
- `GET`: FrankenRedis `1204.65 ops/sec` vs Redis `91142.35 ops/sec` (`1.32%` of Redis throughput)
- `MIXED`: FrankenRedis `1159.23 ops/sec` vs Redis `96834.08 ops/sec` (`1.20%` of Redis throughput)
- `INCR`: FrankenRedis `1176.59 ops/sec` vs Redis `95183.76 ops/sec` (`1.24%` of Redis throughput)
- `PIPELINE16`: FrankenRedis `1221.37 ops/sec` vs Redis `860900.42 ops/sec` (`0.14%` of Redis throughput)

Latency evidence is equally severe:
- `SET` p99: `58,847us` vs Redis `1,022us`
- `GET` p99: `51,647us` vs Redis `1,038us`
- `PIPELINE16` p50/p99: `693,759us / 857,087us` vs Redis `759us / 1,817us`

## Why This Matters
The `frankenredis-gess` bead explicitly required at least `50%` of legacy Redis throughput for the basic SET/GET baselines. The recorded baselines are far below that threshold, and pipeline depth does not improve throughput at all, which strongly suggests a structural bottleneck in the server/event-loop/runtime path.

## Investigation Targets
1. Profile FrankenRedis under the `set`, `get`, and `pipeline16` workloads.
2. Determine whether the dominant bottleneck is in:
   - `fr-server` event loop / socket IO
   - `fr-runtime::execute_frame` dispatch path
   - RESP parsing / encoding overhead
   - store keyspace representation / DB namespacing
   - per-command bookkeeping (`INFO` stats, slowlog, latency tracker, evidence logging)
3. Produce a benchmark delta report after the first optimization lever.
4. Preserve behavior with conformance evidence before and after each change.

## Exit Criteria
- Root-cause profile captured and checked in under `artifacts/benchmark/` or `artifacts/optimization/`
- First targeted optimization landed with before/after benchmark delta
- Follow-up bead(s) created for remaining bottlenecks if the full gap is not closed in one pass

title: [HIGH] HGETALL field iteration order differs from upstream (fixture captures upstream ordering)
description: Surfaced by live_redis_core_hash_matches_runtime case hgetall_result: upstream returns [name,alice,age,30] (insertion order), ours returns [age,30,name,alice]. HGETALL order is technically unspecified in Redis, but the fixture was captured from upstream. Either canonicalize in the harness (sort pairs by field) or switch our hash backend to insertion-order for parity with small-hash listpack encoding. Fix in fr-store Hash::entries iteration or conformance harness comparator.
close_reason: Already shipped: live_oracle_hgetall_field_order_matches canonicalizer at crates/fr-conformance/src/lib.rs:2573 sorts pairs by field bytes before compare; live_redis_core_hash_matches_runtime passes 137/137 (verified against vendored oracle). HGETALL field-order divergence is canonicalized in the harness; no code change needed for this gap.

title: perf: ZSCAN O(N^2/batch) -> O(N) via order-statistic treap resume (index_slice_asc) — 38x when treap built
description: zscan resumed with zs.iter_asc().skip(start) = O(start) (BTreeMap iter has no O(1) nth, unlike IndexMap-backed HSCAN/SSCAN whose slice skip is already O(1)), making a full ZSCAN O(N^2/batch). Wired ZSCAN to zs.index_slice_asc(start,batch_size) which jumps via the oyhl7 order-statistic treap select(start)+range = O(log N + batch) WHEN the treap is built (common for ranked zsets); cold zsets fall back to the same iter_asc().skip = identical output + position cursor, zero regression. 38.18x on a 20k zset full scan @ COUNT10 with treap built (241ms->6.3ms). Byte-identical: ZSCAN full == iter_asc-filtered reference, cold AND hot, over 60 random x COUNT/pattern, golden 0xbc1a8d4b00abb681; 580 fr-store + 11 fr-command scan tests pass. NOTE: HSCAN/SSCAN were investigated + found ALREADY O(N) (IndexMap/IndexSet iter().skip forwards nth to slice = O(1)) — not a target.
close_reason: Shipped 28270ebf0: ZSCAN uses index_slice_asc (treap select+range, O(log N+batch)) when rank tree built, falls back to iter_asc().skip otherwise = identical output/cursor. 38.18x at 20k full scan with treap. Byte-identical cold+hot over 60 random, golden 0xbc1a8d4b00abb681; 580 fr-store + 11 fr-command scan pass. HSCAN/SSCAN found already-O(N) (IndexMap slice skip), not targets.

title: Sentinel instance replies should use RESP3 map frames like Redis
description: Vendored Redis 7.2.4 addReplySentinelRedisInstance uses addReplyMapLen, so after HELLO 3 SENTINEL MASTER alpha starts with %20 and list commands contain map entries. fr-sentinel currently returns a flat RespFrame::Array for instance_to_info_array, so RESP3 clients see an array instead of a map. Acceptance: instance_to_info_array returns RespFrame::Map with the same fields/order, and MASTER/MASTERS/REPLICAS/SLAVES/SENTINELS tests consume map entries while field values remain unchanged.
close_reason: done

title: ZRANGEBYLEX/ZREVRANGEBYLEX silently accept WITHSCORES instead of upstream BYLEX-incompatibility error
description:
Upstream t_zset.c::genericZrangebyscoreCommand guards `if (withscores && zlex)` and emits 'syntax error, WITHSCORES not supported in combination with BYLEX'. fr was reusing parse_zrangebyscore_opts (which silently accepts WITHSCORES) and discarding the flag for the legacy ZRANGEBYLEX / ZREVRANGEBYLEX paths, so a stray WITHSCORES returned bare elements without scores instead of erroring. The newer ZRANGE BYLEX path already rejected this case correctly; the regression was scoped to the two legacy commands.

Repro vs vendored 7.2.4:
  ZADD z 1 a 2 b 3 c
  ZRANGEBYLEX z '[a' '[z' WITHSCORES
    16380> ERR syntax error, WITHSCORES not supported in combination with BYLEX
    16381 (pre-fix)> 1) "a"  2) "b"  3) "c"

Fix: inspect the withscores flag returned from parse_zrangebyscore_opts in both handlers and surface the upstream wording verbatim when set.
acceptance_criteria: 1. ZRANGEBYLEX with WITHSCORES returns 'ERR syntax error, WITHSCORES not supported in combination with BYLEX'. 2. ZREVRANGEBYLEX with WITHSCORES returns the same wording. 3. ZRANGEBYLEX without WITHSCORES still returns the bare element list (regression on happy path). 4. ZRANGEBYLEX with LIMIT still honors the offset/count window (regression on existing parse_zrangebyscore_opts behavior).

title: XAUTOCLAIM validates COUNT (>0, overflow) before type-checking the key
description: XAUTOCLAIM key grp cons min-idle start COUNT <i64max> on a wrong-type key: redis returns 'ERR COUNT must be > 0' (COUNT parse/overflow validation runs before checkType), fr returns WRONGTYPE (type-check first). redis getRangeLongFromObjectOrReply / count*attempts_factor overflow makes huge COUNT fail the >0 check. Niche overflow+order edge. Found via rich_option_fuzz.py. Verify exact redis order in xautoclaimCommand before fixing.
close_reason: Fixed 22da46b16: XAUTOCLAIM COUNT range-checked to [1, i64::MAX/16] (= LONG_MAX/max(sizeof(streamID)=16,attempts_factor=10)) with 'COUNT must be > 0' wording, in the option loop before the type-check — matching getRangeLongFromObjectOrReply at t_stream.c:3367 preceding checkType at :3380. fr only checked <=0 before. Golden e28f6a08 (4145B), regression test, 8 xautoclaim tests + clippy clean.

title: Sentinel INFO parser should gate slave fields on role like Redis
description: Upstream Redis 7.2.4 sentinelRefreshInstanceInfo processes master_host, master_port, master_link_status, slave_priority, and slave_repl_offset only inside the role == SRI_SLAVE branch as it scans INFO lines. fr-sentinel::health::parse_info_response accepts those fields regardless of the observed role and also maps master_repl_offset into the replica offset field. Acceptance: slave-specific INFO fields are only captured after a role:slave line has been observed, master INFO does not populate slave_repl_offset from master_repl_offset, and existing normal slave parsing still works.
close_reason: Completed Redis-compatible Sentinel INFO role-gated parser parity

title: ZPOPMIN count parse failure surfaces 'value is not an integer or out of range' instead of upstream's unified 'value is out of range, must be positive'
description:
Upstream t_zset.c::genericZpopCommand uses
  getRangeLongFromObjectOrReply(c, c->argv[2], 0, LONG_MAX, &count,
                                'value is out of range, must be positive')
so non-numeric, negative, and out-of-range counts all surface the same message.

fr's ZPOPMIN was calling parse_i64_arg directly: a non-numeric count returned the generic 'ERR value is not an integer or out of range', while a negative count returned the upstream wording (via an explicit `count < 0` branch). ZPOPMAX (which routed through the local parse_zpop_count helper) was already correct. The asymmetry between ZPOPMIN and ZPOPMAX wordings is purely an oversight.

Repro vs vendored 7.2.4:
  > ZPOPMIN z abc
    16380> (error) ERR value is out of range, must be positive
    16381 (pre-fix)> (error) ERR value is not an integer or out of range
  > ZPOPMIN z 1.5
    16380> (error) ERR value is out of range, must be positive
    16381 (pre-fix)> (error) ERR value is not an integer or out of range
  > ZPOPMAX z abc
    16380> (error) ERR value is out of range, must be positive
    16381> (error) ERR value is out of range, must be positive  ← already correct

Fix: route ZPOPMIN's count parse through parse_zpop_count (already used by ZPOPMAX). The count == 0 short-circuit is preserved inline for the explicit empty-array reply.
acceptance_criteria: 1. ZPOPMIN z abc returns 'value is out of range, must be positive'. 2. Same for ZPOPMIN z 1.5 and any non-integer input. 3. ZPOPMIN z -1 still returns the same wording (no regression). 4. ZPOPMIN z 1 still pops one (member, score) pair. 5. ZPOPMIN z 0 still returns the empty array (special-case preserved). 6. ZPOPMAX wording unchanged (already correct).

title: Fix fr-server help output to document --bind
close_reason: Completed

title: perf: ZRANDMEMBER with count is 1.3x slower than redis (O(log n) treap select vs O(1) dict-random)
description:
Heavy-op sweep (single 100k-element zset, small-reply ops): ZRANDMEMBER is the ONLY command found slower than vendored redis 7.2.4 — everything else (SRANDMEMBER 2x, HRANDFIELD 1.8x, LPOS-deep 5x, ZRANK/ZSCORE/ZRANGEBYSCORE/ZRANGEBYLEX all fr-faster) is parity-or-faster.

Scaling (req/s, 20k ops, 100k zset, redis-benchmark): count=1 fr=56980 redis=54347 (fr FASTER); count=10 fr=43196 redis=40650 (fr faster); count=100 fr=11370 redis=15048 (1.32x SLOWER); count=1000 fr=1875 redis=2219 (1.18x SLOWER). Crossover ~count=100: per-pick cost fr ~0.5us vs redis ~0.43us.

ROOT CAUSE: fr's zrandmember_count draws random indices (next_rand % len, distinct via HashSet for positive count) then maps each index to a member via SortedSet::members_at_indices -> ZRankTreap::select (O(log n) per pick) because the Full zset has no O(1) nth. Redis ZRANDMEMBER-with-count picks via the skiplist's dict (dictGetFairRandomKey, O(1) per pick). So fr is O(count*log n) vs redis O(count); the log n (~17 for 100k) is the gap.

LEVER (est ~1.8x for count>=100, flips slower->faster, BEATS redis): give the Full zset O(1) random-member access. FullSortedSet.dict is currently HashMap<Vec<u8>,f64,foldhash> (member->score, iteration order documented as never observed). indexmap IS already a fr-store dep. Change dict to IndexMap and add a dict-order pick path used ONLY by zrandmember_count (dict.get_index(rand % len), O(1)); keep members_at_indices (score-order, hard-pinned by members_at_indices_isomorphic_and_faster_zrnd1) for nothing-or-tests. 15 .dict sites (3 remove -> swap_remove since order unobserved).

PROOF NOTE: ZRANDMEMBER is a RANDOM command — byte-exact golden does NOT apply. Picking by dict-order index instead of score-order index changes WHICH members are returned for a given RNG state, but both are uniform distinct samples (contract preserved: count, distinctness for +count, repeats for -count, valid members/scores). Differ harness already treats ZRANDMEMBER as shape-only random. So proof must be: (a) contract/distribution check, (b) ALL non-random zset ops (ZADD/ZSCORE/ZREM/ZRANGE/ZPOP/...) byte-exact under the IndexMap dict (verify zset_differ + fr-store zset tests), (c) NO ZADD/ZSCORE hot-path regression from IndexMap (re-bench perf_gap_dashboard), (d) update fr's zrandmember output-pinning unit tests to contract checks. Repro: build 100k zset, redis-benchmark -t/-q ZRANDMEMBER key 100.
close_reason: Closed: gap flipped to fr-faster (630a4cbe0). Made FullSortedSet.dict an IndexMap and added random_members_at_indices using dict.get_index (O(1) per pick) instead of the O(log n) treap select (or O(n) iter_asc fallback). Contract-equivalent for the random ZRANDMEMBER reply (uniform distinct sample, count/repeats preserved). Verified: zset_differ vs redis 4000 iters zero divergence (all non-random ops byte-exact under IndexMap dict); ZRANDMEMBER contract checks pass; 596 fr-store + 7 fr-command tests pass. Bench (100k zset): count=100 redis=15416 -> fr 34843 (2.26x faster, was 1.32x slower); count=1000 4.5x faster; -100 2.05x faster. ZADD/ZSCORE 1.01x (no hot-path regression).

title: redis parity: LPUSH variadic element ordering
close_reason: Superseded by COMMAND INFO parity work

title: ZRANGE/ZRANGESTORE LIMIT rejects negative offset before BYSCORE/BYLEX shape check, masking upstream's verbose error and empty-result paths
description:
Upstream t_zset.c::zrangeGenericCommand parses LIMIT offset via getLongFromObjectOrReply, which accepts any long (including negatives), and only runs the BYSCORE/BYLEX-pairing validation AFTER the parse loop completes. With a negative offset, genericZrangebyscoreCommand short-circuits to an empty result because `if (offset >= ll) goto cleanup` never fires for offset<0 but `if (limit == 0) break` does fire on the clamped count.

fr was calling parse_u64_arg on the LIMIT offset in both zrange and zrangestore_cmd, so any negative value failed with 'value is not an integer or out of range' before the dependency check could run.

Repro vs vendored 7.2.4:
  > ZRANGE z 0 -1 LIMIT -1 0
    16380> (error) ERR syntax error, LIMIT is only supported in combination with either BYSCORE or BYLEX
    16381 (pre-fix)> (error) ERR value is not an integer or out of range
  > ZRANGE z 0 +inf BYSCORE LIMIT -1 0
    16380> (empty array)
    16381 (pre-fix)> (error) ERR value is not an integer or out of range
  > ZRANGESTORE dst z 0 -1 LIMIT -1 0
    16380> (error) ERR syntax error, LIMIT is only supported in combination with either BYSCORE or BYLEX
    16381 (pre-fix)> (error) ERR value is not an integer or out of range

Fix: switch both LIMIT-offset parses to parse_limit_offset_arg (returns usize::MAX on negative input). With usize::MAX as the .skip() offset, the BYSCORE branch produces the empty result upstream produces, while the post-loop dependency check still wins for the no-BYSCORE-no-BYLEX path.
acceptance_criteria: 1. ZRANGE z 0 -1 LIMIT -1 0 (no shape) returns the verbose 'LIMIT is only supported in combination with either BYSCORE or BYLEX' error matching vendored. 2. ZRANGE z 0 +inf BYSCORE LIMIT -1 0 returns an empty array. 3. ZRANGESTORE dst z 0 -1 LIMIT -1 0 returns the same shape-error wording. 4. ZRANGESTORE dst z 0 +inf BYSCORE LIMIT -1 0 returns Integer(0) and deletes any pre-existing dst. 5. Positive LIMIT 0 5 without BYSCORE/BYLEX still surfaces the same dependency-check error (no regression on existing pin). 6. Other ZRANGE-family commands that don't share this code path remain untouched.

title: zset-listpack score MEMORY USAGE undercounts (missing listpack entry encoding+backlen)
description: estimate_listpack_score_bytes returns only the score's digit/string LENGTH, but a zset listpack stores the score as a full listpack entry: integer-looking scores get listpack integer encoding (1..9 bytes by magnitude), others a string entry, plus a 1-byte backlen. Undercount skews MEMORY USAGE near jemalloc size-class boundaries (n=3,5,8 members -> redis 80/88/104 vs fr 72/80/96, -8). Found by /tmp/memusage_sweep.py. Sibling of intset-memusage-width.
close_reason: Fixed 6bb1319a9: estimate_listpack_score_bytes now models the score as a full listpack entry (integer encoding 1..9B by magnitude or string entry, + backlen) via redis_score_to_string. n=3/5/8: fr 72/80/96->80/88/104; 1e20: fr 88->72. Unit test + 596 fr-store tests + live sweep 25/25 + cluster_admin gate 62/62 + clippy clean.

title: ZUNIONSTORE/ZINTERSTORE with negative numkeys returns generic out-of-range instead of upstream's input-key wording
description:
Upstream t_zset.c::zunionInterDiffGenericCommand gates on `setnum < 1` after a successful getLongFromObjectOrReply, so a NEGATIVE numkeys count surfaces the same dedicated 'at least 1 input key is needed for <cmd> command' wording as numkeys=0. fr was splitting the check into a (numkeys < 0) → InvalidInteger arm plus a separate (numkeys == 0) → input-key arm, diverging from vendored 7.2.4 only on the negative branch. Repro: 'ZUNIONSTORE dst -1 nonex' → vendored says 'ERR at least 1 input key is needed for ...zunionstore... command'; fr (pre-fix) said 'ERR value is not an integer or out of range'. Same regression on ZINTERSTORE; ZDIFFSTORE / ZINTER / ZUNION / ZDIFF / ZINTERCARD were already using `<= 0` correctly.

Fix: collapse the two-arm guard into a single `< 1` branch in both handlers, preserving the per-command wording. Add a test pin alongside the existing zero-count case.
acceptance_criteria: 1. ZUNIONSTORE dst -1 <key> returns 'ERR at least 1 input key is needed for 'zunionstore' command'. 2. ZINTERSTORE dst -1 <key> returns 'ERR at least 1 input key is needed for 'zinterstore' command'. 3. Numkeys = 0 path continues to surface the same wording (no regression). 4. Numkeys >= 1 happy path continues to write to the destination set and reply with the integer count (no regression on the read side).

title: fr-command/lua: yield inside for/while/if/function-call silently skips iterations on resume — bw15's PC tracking only handles outer-stmt yields
close_reason: Added nested_exec_stmts_depth counter on LuaState. exec_stmts bumps it on entry / decrements on exit (with manual fallthrough so the counter stays balanced even on Err). resume_coroutine resets the counter to 0 at the coroutine boundary (saving/restoring the previous value) so a yield from the body's outer-stmt level reads as depth 0. coroutine.yield checks: if depth > 0 (yield from inside for/while/repeat/if-then bodies, function-call bodies, pcall/xpcall lambdas), return the upstream Lua 5.1 wording 'attempt to yield across metamethod/C-call boundary' instead of letting bw15's outer-stmt PC tracking silently drop iterations on the next resume. Two new pin tests: yield-inside-for-loop now errors (was silently dropping iterations), yield-inside-pcall errors and pcall catches it. Existing 7 coroutine tests still pass; clippy clean.

title: Fix LTRIM and LRANGE index normalization bugs
description: LTRIM and LRANGE incorrectly handle stop indices that are less than start or less than -len, returning or keeping the first element instead of an empty list.
close_reason: Fixed LTRIM and LRANGE: clamp normalized indices before comparison to prevent negative-to-usize wrapping when indices are out of range

title: SET expire-option validation order: conflict/overwrite must beat invalid value (syntax error precedence)
description: Found via SET-option differential fuzz vs redis 7.2.4. fr's SET parser validated each EX/PX/EXAT/PXAT value INLINE during the option scan, so: (1) SET k v EX 0 PX 100 (conflicting kinds) returned 'invalid expire time' instead of redis's 'syntax error' (conflict must win); (2) SET k v EX 0 EX 10 (repeated SAME kind, last-wins) returned 'invalid expire time' instead of OK (the overwritten EX 0 must not be validated); (3) SET k v EX abc PX 100 returned 'not an integer' instead of 'syntax error'. Upstream parseExtendedStringArgumentsOrReply scans options WITHOUT validating the value (only different kinds conflict; same kind repeats last-wins) then validates the FINAL value once, after the scan, before NX/XX gating. Fixed in fr-command set(): store raw expire value during scan, validate after. 6000-op fuzz now 0 diffs; golden sha256 byte-exact.
close_reason: Fixed d2903d83f: fr-command set() now defers expire-value validation to after the option scan (only conflicting expire KINDS error during scan; same kind last-wins; final value validated once before NX/XX gate). Matches redis parseExtendedStringArgumentsOrReply. SET-option fuzz 0 diffs/6000 (was 12+); golden sha256 byte-exact; regression test added. SET fast-path unaffected (plain 3-arg bypasses options parser).

title: [perf] HSET packed hash borrow value slice through overwrite path
description: Profile-backed after frankenredis-tbmu1 keep: postkeep P16/C50/n300k sweep reduced HSET throughput gap to Redis/fr 1.028x, but HSET p99 remains 4819us vs Redis 1975us. Historical profiles for ohsk5/gu5nf identify allocator pressure and per-command allocation as first-class residuals. Current runtime still calls hset_borrowed(key, field, pair[1].to_vec(), now_ms), allocating a value Vec before the packed map can overwrite the existing field. Candidate: add one borrowed-value path from Runtime::execute_plain_hset_borrowed -> Store -> HashFieldMap/PackedStrMap so packed duplicate overwrites copy directly from &[u8] into the packed buffer and allocate value bytes only for hashtable/promoted cases requiring owned storage. Preserve ordering, field position, HGETALL order, LFU/RNG bookkeeping, wrongtype behavior, dirty/modification counters, and golden SHA256. Baseline via rch-built hyperfine HSET P16/C50/n3M; keep only Score>=2.0.
close_reason: Rejected: HSET borrowed-value slice path golden matched but n10M confirmation was only 1.01 +/- 0.08x faster and last-run throughput/p99 regressed; production hunk removed.

title: FUNCTION LOAD: present-but-empty name= returns wrong error (name-not-given vs charset/length)
close_reason: Fixed b00c32fc8: FUNCTION LOAD now gates 'Library name was not given' on !name_seen (absent token) and folds present-but-empty/whitespace name= into the charset+length error, matching upstream functionsVerifyName. Byte-exact vs redis 7.2.4 across empty/whitespace/absent/bad-charset/valid name cases. fr-store function_load suite 19/19 + new regression test. Note: FUNCTION LIST within-library order is dict-hash order (WONTFIX, like KEYS/SCAN); FUNCTION LOAD not surfacing Lua compile syntax errors is a separate deeper gap (fr text-scans register_function instead of executing the chunk; engine-specific wording, CoralOx Lua domain).

title: Sentinel launcher binary (--sentinel flag)
description:
The fr-sentinel crate is fully implemented with working state machine, SENTINEL command dispatch, discovery/health/consensus/failover modules. Tests prove it works when Store::sentinel_mode is enabled.

**What exists:**
- crates/fr-sentinel/: 2,807 lines of working code
- SENTINEL command dispatch wired via sentinel_cmd() in fr-command
- Store::sentinel_mode flag (default false)
- Full test coverage

**What's missing:**
- No --sentinel CLI flag in fr-server argument parser
- No sentinel.conf file parsing (optional)
- No separate fr-sentinel binary

**Implementation path (trivial):**
1. Add --sentinel flag to fr-server argument parser
2. When flag present, set store.sentinel_mode = true before starting runtime
3. Optionally: add sentinel.conf parsing or separate binary target
close_reason: Implemented --sentinel CLI flag in fr-server

title: SENTINEL sentinel info should report zero last-hello timestamp like upstream
description: Upstream Redis 7.2.4 addReplySentinelRedisInstance emits last-hello-message for Sentinel instances as mstime() - ri->last_hello_time with no zero special-case. fr-sentinel currently returns 0 when SentinelRedisInstance.last_hello_time is 0, which diverges for SENTINEL SENTINELS output on newly-created or missing-hello sentinel entries. Acceptance: sentinel instance info arrays report now_ms - last_hello_time for sentinels, including last_hello_time=0, with a regression test.
close_reason: Aligned Sentinel last-hello-message with upstream elapsed-time behavior for zero timestamps.

title: XADD returns max ID instead of error when stream ID exhausted
close_reason: Fixed: next_auto_stream_id now returns Option and propagates None when IDs exhausted. The error message matches Redis 7.2.4: 'The stream has exhausted the last possible ID, unable to add more items'. Also fixed the partial auto-id (ms-*) path to use checked_add instead of saturating_add.

title: CONFIG SET port / bind live listener rebind (MODIFIABLE in redis 7.2.4, fr wrongly rejects as immutable)
description:
Differential probe vs vendored redis 7.2.4: CONFIG SET port and CONFIG SET bind are MODIFIABLE_CONFIG in redis (config.c: createIntConfig port 0-65535 with updatePort apply hook; bind with updateBindAddr). redis rebinds the listening socket SYNCHRONOUSLY and IMMEDIATELY (verified: after 'CONFIG SET port 16398' on the :16399 conn, :16399 stops accepting and :16398 starts; the issuing connection and all existing client connections SURVIVE — only the LISTENER socket moves). On bind failure redis returns 'ERR CONFIG SET failed (possibly related to argument port) - Unable to listen on this port. Check server logs.' and keeps the old listener.

fr currently lists port+bind in the handle_config_set immutable set (~lib.rs:14173) and returns 'can't set immutable config' for BOTH — a real parity gap (only divergence left in the 195-param config_set_validation_differ sweep, currently allowlisted as KNOWN_ISSUE 'port').

IMPLEMENTATION SKETCH (safe, isolated to the LISTENER token):
- fr-runtime ServerState: add pending_port_change: Option<u16> (mirror pending_client_kills) + a stored bind_addr so the CONFIG SET handler can test-bind.
- handle_config_set: remove port (and bind) from immutable list; add a port handler that int-parses [0,65535] (range/parse wording like other INTEGER_CONFIG), then std::net::TcpListener::bind(bind_addr:port) as a test (matches redis changeListener error path) — Err -> 'Unable to listen on this port. Check server logs.'; Ok -> drop test listener, store port via set_server_port, set pending_port_change, return +OK.
- fr-server event loop: pass bind_addr into runtime at startup; after command processing drain take_pending_port_change() and rebind: bind NEW mio TcpListener FIRST, register with LISTENER token, then deregister+drop the old (bind-new-before-drop-old keeps the server reachable if the rare TOCTOU bind fails). Existing client connections (other tokens) untouched.
- Tests: unit test the runtime handler (range/parse errors + OK + pending signal set); live differential — CONFIG SET port N, reconnect on N (works), old port refused, existing conn survives, CONFIG GET port reflects N, occupied-port -> 'Unable to listen' error.
- Then flip config_set_validation_differ.py KNOWN_ISSUES (drop 'port') so the gate goes fully green.

bind (multi-addr updateBindAddr) is a follow-on; port is the primary case.

title: DECRBY i64::MIN reports 'decrement would overflow' bespoke wording
description: fr's decrby returned the generic 'increment or decrement would overflow' when the increment was i64::MIN (whose negation overflows). Upstream t_string.c::decrbyCommand emits the bespoke 'decrement would overflow' wording before delegating to incrDecrCommand for that one specific case (which is the only path where the negation itself overflows; all other overflows hit the generic 'increment or decrement would overflow' from incrDecrCommand)

title: SENTINEL CONFIG GET should return map-shaped replies
description: Redis 7.2.4 sentinelConfigGetCommand uses setDeferredMapLen for SENTINEL CONFIG GET, so matched config keys are returned as map entries. fr-sentinel currently returns a flat Array [key, value, ...], diverging from the RESP3/map-shaped command surface already used for SENTINEL DEBUG. Scope: crates/fr-sentinel only.
close_reason: Completed
